aaron.notes: Ἀαρών (Heb. אַהֲרוֹן), indecl. (in FlJ, -ῶνος), Aaron (Exo.4:14, al.): Luk.1:5, Act.7:40, Heb.5:4, 7:11, 9:4.† (AS)

abaddon.notes: Ἀβαδδών (Heb. אֲבַדּוֹן, destruction; LXX, ἀπώλεια, only in Wisdom Lit., of the place of the ruined dead: Job.26:6, 28:22, 31:12, Psa.88:12, Pro.15:11*), indecl.; in NT, Abaddon, the angel of the Abyss: Rev.9:11.† (AS)

abares.notes: αβαρής, -ές (βαρός), without weight; metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word) not burdensome: 2Co.11:9.† (AS)

abba.notes: Ἀββά (T, -ᾶ), indecl. (Aram, אַבָּא, emphatic form of אַב = Heb. אָב, father), used in the phrase Ἀ. ὁ παήρ, Abba, Father (see Swete on Mk, I.with): Mrk.14:36, Rom.8:15, Gal.4:6.† (AS)

abel.notes: Ἄβελ, (WH, Ἅ-), ὁ, indecl. (הֶבֶל), Abel (Gen.4:2-10): Heb.11:4, 12:24; αἵμα Ἄ., Mat.23:35, Luk.11:51.† (AS)

abia.notes: Ἀβιά (Heb. אֲבִיָּה, אֲבִיָּהוּ), ὁ, indecl. (in FlJ, Ἀβίας, -α), Abia, Abijah. __1. Son of Rehoboam (3Ki.14:1): Mat.1:7. __2. A priest of the line of Eleazar (1Ch.24:3, 10): Luk.1:5. † (AS)

abiathar.notes: Ἀβιάθαρ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. אֶבְיָתָר), Abiathar (1Ki.21:1): Mrk.2:26.† (AS)

abilene.notes: Ἀβειληνή (T, Rec. Ἀβι-), -ῆς, ἡ, (sc. χώρα), Abilene, a district in the Anti-Lebanon: Luk.3:1.† (AS)

abioud.notes: Ἀβιούδ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. אֲבִיָּהוּד), Abiud, Abihud: Mat.1:13.† (AS)

abraam.notes: Ἀβραάμ (Heb. אַבְרָהָם), ὁ, indecl. (in FlJ, Ἄβραμος, -ου; MM, VGT, see word), Abraham (Gen.17:5 al.): Mat.1:1, 2 al. (AS)

abussos.notes: ἄ-βυσσος, -ον (Ion. βυσσός = βυθός), __1. in cl., boundless, bottomless (e.g. ἄ. πέλαγος, ἄ. πλοῦτος Æsch.). __2. [In LXX (for תְּהוֹם, Gen.1:2, al.; exc. Isa.44:27, Job.41:22 for צוּלָה, מְצוּלָה, Job.36:16 for…

achaia.notes: Ἀχαία (T, Ἀχαΐα), -ας, ἡ (Bl., § 46, 11) Achaia, the Roman province: Act.18:12, 27 19:21 Rom.15:26, 1Co.16:15, 2Co.1:1 9:2 11:10, 1Th.1:7-8.† (AS)

acharistos.notes: ἀχάριστος, -ον (χαρίζομαι), [in LXX: Wis.16:29, Sir.29:17, 25, 4Ma.9:10 * ;] __(a) ungracious, unpleasing; __(b) ungrateful, thankless: Luk.6:35, 2Ti.3:2.† (AS)

achaz.notes: Ἄχαζ (WH, Ἄχας), ὁ (Heb. אָחָז), Ahaz: Mat.1:9.† (AS)

acheiropoietos.notes: ἀ-χειρο-ποίητος, -ον (χειροποίητος) not made by hands: Mrk.14:58, 2Co.5:1; metaphorically, περιτομή ἀ. (i.e. spiritual ), Col.2:11 (MM, see word).† (AS)

achlus.notes: ἀχλύς, -ύος, ἡ [in Aq.: Eze.12:7; Sm.: Job.3:5 * ;] a mist, esp. a dimness of the eyes: Act.13:11 (see Tr., Syn., § with).† (AS)

achreioo.notes: ἀχρεόω (Rec. -ειόω, Polyb., LXX), -ῶ (ἄχρεος = ἀχρεῖος) [in LXX (-ειόω) for אָלַח ni., etc. ;] to make useless, unprofitable: pass., Rom.3:12 (LXX).† (AS)

achreios.notes: ἀ-χρεῖος, -ον (χρεῖος, useful), [in LXX: 2Ki.6:22 (שָׁפָל), Ep. Jer.17:1-27 * ;] useless, unprofitable: Mat.25:30, Luk.17:10 (MM, see word).† (AS)

achrestos.notes: ά-χρηατος, -ον [in LXX: Hos.8:8 (חֵפֶץ אַיִן), Wis.2:11, Sir.16:1, al. ;] useless; unserviceable: opposite to εὔχρηστος, Phm 11.† (AS)

achuron.notes: ἄχυρον, -ον, τό [in LXX chiefly for תֶּבֶן ;] chaff: Mat.3:12, Luk.3:17.† (AS)

adam.notes: Ἀδάμ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. אָדָם), Adam: Luk.3:38, Rom.5:14, 1Co.15:22,45, 1Ti.2:13,14 Ju 14. Christ ὁ ἔσχατος Ἀ., 1Co.15:45.† (AS)

adapanos.notes: ἀδάπανος, -ον (δαπάνη), without expense, free of charge: 1Co.9:18.† (AS)

adelos.notes: ἄ-δηλος, -ον (δῆλος), [in LXX: Ps 50 (51):6 (בַּטֻחוֹת) ;] __1. unseen, unobserved, not manifest (Ps, l.with): Luk.11:44. __2. uncertain, indistinct: 1Co.14:8. † (AS)

adelphe.notes: ἀδελφή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀδελφός), [in LXX for אָהוֹת ;] a sister: Mat.19:29, al.; metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word), of a member of the Christian community: Rom.16:1, 1Co.7:15 Jas.21:15, al. (AS)

adelphos.notes: ἀδελφός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀ- copul., δελφύς, womb), in cl., a brother, born of the same parent or parents. [In LXX (Hort, Ja., 102f.), for אָח ;] __1. lit. of a brother (Gen.4:2, al.). __2. Of a neighbour (Lev.19:17). __3. Of…

adelphotes.notes: ἀδελφότης, -ητος, ἡ (ἀδελφός), [in LXX: 1Ma.12:10,17, 4Mac .5 * ;] __1. abstract, brotherhood, brotherly affection (LXX). __2. Concrete, the brotherhood, the Christian community: 1Pe.2:17 5:9 (MM, VGT, see word). † (AS)

ademoneo.notes: ἀδημονέω, -ῶ (on the derivation, see MM, VGT, see word), [in Aq.: Job.18:20; Sm.: Psa.60 (61):3 115:2 (116:11), Ecc.7:17(16), Eze.3:15 * ;] to be troubled, distressed (MM, l.with): Mat.26:37, Mrk.14:33, Php.2:26.† (AS)

adiakritos.notes: ἀ-διά-κριτος, -ον (διακρίνω), [in LXX: Pro.25:1 * ;] __1. not to be parted, mixed, undistinguishable (cf. Pr, l.with, and see Lft., Ignat. Eph., §3). __2. without uncertainty (Hort, in l., but see MM, VGT, see word):…

adialeiptos.notes: ἀδιάλειπτος, -ον (διαλείπω), unremitting, incessant: Rom.9:2, 2Ti.1:3. (For exx., see MM, VGT, see word) † (AS)

adikema.notes: ἀδίκημα, -τος, τό (ἀδικέω), [in LXX for עָוֹן, פֶּשַׁע, etc. ;] a wrong, injury, misdeed (MM, VGT, see word): Act.18:14 24:20, Rev.18:5.† (AS)

adikeo.notes: ἀδικέω, -ῶ (ἄδικος), [in LXX for עשׁק, עוה, etc. ;] __1. intrans., to be ἄδικος, do wrong, act wickedly or criminally: Act.25:11, 1Co.6:8, 2Co.7:12, Col.3:25, Rev.22:11; to do hurt, Rev.9:19. __2. Trans. __(a) to do…

adikia.notes: ἀδικία, -ας, ἡ (ἄδικος), [in LXX for עָוֹן, פֶּשַׁע, עָוֶל, etc. ;] __1. injustice: Luk.18:6, Rom.9:14. __2. unrighteousness, iniquity: Jhn.7:18, Act.8:23, Rom.1:18, 29 2:8 6:13, 2Ti.2:19, 1Jn.1:9 5:17; opposite to…

adikos.notes: ἄδικος, -ον (δίκη), [in LXX for שׁקר, עול, etc. ;] __1. unjust: Rom.3:5, Heb.6:10. __2. unrighteous, wicked: Luk.16:11 18:11, Act.24:15, 1Co.6:1,9 1Pe.3:18; opposite to δίκαιος, Mat.5:45; to εὐσεβής, 2Pe.2:9; to…

ado.notes: ᾄδω (Attic form of Ion. and poët. ἀείδω), [in LXX chiefly for שִׁיר ;] to sing, __(a) intrans., with dative (MM, VGT, see word), of praise to God: Eph.5:19, Col.3:16 ; __(b) trans., with cogn. accusative: ᾠδὴν,…

adokimos.notes: ἀ-δόκιμος, -ον, [in LXX: Pro.25:4, Isa.1:22 (סִיג) * ;] __1. of things (prop. of metals: LXX, ll. with), not standing the test, rejected: γῆ, Heb.6:8. __2. Of persons, rejected after testing, reprobate: Rom.1:28,…

adolos.notes: ἄδολος, -ον, __1. guileless (Pind., Thuc). __2. Of liquids (Æsch., Eur., and late prose writers), genuine, pure (in π. and in MGr. of wine, also of corn: MM, VGT, see word; Milligan, NTD, 77): of milk,…

adramuttenos.notes: Ἁδραμυντηνός (T, Rec. Ἀδραμυττηνός), -ή,-όν, of Adramyttium, a seaport of Mysia: Act.27:2.† (AS)

adrias.notes: Ἁδρίας (T, Rec. Ἀδρίας), -ου, ὁ, the Adriatic sea, in later Greek usage extended to take in all the waters between Greece and Italy: Act.27:27.† (AS)

adunateo.notes: ἀδυνατέω, -ῶ (ἀδύνατος), [in LXX: Deu.17:8, Zec.8:6 (פּלא ni.), Job.42:2 (בּצר ni.), al. ;] __1. Abbott-Smith omits this number to be unable (cl., Philo; π., see MM, VGT, see word). __2. In LXX and NT (Kennedy,…

adunatos.notes: ἀ-δύνατος, -ον, [in LXX for אֶבְיוֹן, דַּל, etc. ;] __1. of persons, unable, powerless: Act.14:8; figuratively Rom.15:1 (MM, VGT, see word). __2. Of things, impossible: Mat.19:26, Mrk.10:27, Luk.18:27, Rom.8:3,…

aei.notes: ἀεί, adv., [in LXX: Isa.42:14 (מֵעוֹלָם) 51:13 (תָּמִיד), Ps 94(95):10, al. ;] ever; __1. of continuous time, unceasingly, perpetually: Act.7:51, 2 Co.4.11; 6:10, Tit.1:12, Heb.3:10. __2. Of successive occurrences,…

aer.notes: ἀήρ, ἀέρος, ὁ, [in LXX: 2Ki.22:12 (= Psa.17(18):11 שׁחק), Wi .8 ;] in Hom., Hes., the lower air which surrounds the earth, as opposite to the purer αἰθήρ of the higher regions; generally, air (MM, VGT, see word):…

aetos.notes: ἀετός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX for נֶשֶׁר ;] an eagle: Rev.4:7 8:13 (Rec. ἀγγέλου) 12:14. Where carrion is referred to, ἀ. is probably a vulture (cf. Job.39:30, Pro.30:17): Mat.24:28, Luk.17:37 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

agalliao.notes: ἀγαλλιάω, -ῶ, Hellenistic form of cl. ἀγάλλω, to glorify, mid. -ομαι, to exult in; [in LXX (most frequently in Pss.) chiefly for גיל, רנן pi. ;] to exult, rejoice greatly: before ἐπί, with dative, Luk.1:47; with…

agalliasis.notes: ἀγαλλίασις, -εως, ἡ (ἀγαλλιάω), [in LXX (most frequently in Pss. and often coupled with εὐφροσύνη, as Ps 44(45):15) chiefly for גִּיל ;] exultation, exuberant joy: Luk.1:44, Act.2:46, Heb.1:9, Ju 24; χαρὰ καὶ ἀ.,…

agamos.notes: ἄ-γαμος, -ον [in LXX: 4Ma.16:9 * ;] unmarried: 1Co.7:8, 32; fem. (= cl. ἄνανδρος), ib. 11, 34.† (AS)

aganakteo.notes: ἀγανακτέω, -ῶ (ἄγαν, much, ἄχομαι, to grieve), [in LXX: Wis.5:22, 12:27, Da TH Bel 28, 4Ma.4:21 * ;] to be indignant: Mat.21:15, 26:8, Mrk.10:14, 14:4; before περί, Mat.20:24, Mrk.10:41; before ὅτι, Luk.13:14 (see…

aganaktesis.notes: ἀγανάκτησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀγανακτέω), [in LXX: Est.18:3 א 1 * ;] indignation: 2Co.7:11 (see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

agapao.notes: ἀγαπάω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for אהב ;] to love, to feel and exhibit esteem and goodwill to a person, to prize and delight in a thing. __1. Of human affection, to men: τ. πλησίον, Mat.5:43; τ. ἐχθρούς, ib. 44; to…

agape.notes: ἀγάπη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for אַהֲבָה, which is also rendered by ἀγάπησις and φιλία ;] love, goodwill, esteem. Outside of bibl. and eccl. books, there is no clear instance (with Deiss., LAE, 18:4, 70:2, cf. the same…

agapetos.notes: ἀγαπητός, -ή, -όν (ἀγαπάω), [in LXX chiefly for יָתִיד, יָדִיד ;] beloved (see M, Pr., 221); __(a) by God: of Christ, Mat.3:17; of men, Rom.1:7; __(b) by Christians, of one another: 1Co.4:14; frequently as form of…

agar.notes: Ἅγαρ (Rec. Ἄ-), ἡ, indecl. (in FlJ, Ἀγάρα, -ης, Heb. הָגָר), Hagar (Gen.16): Gal.4:24,25.† (AS)

agathoergeo.notes: ἀγαθοεργέω, -ῶ, to do good, show kindness: 1 Ti 6:18 (Cremer, 8).† ἀγαθουργέω, -ῶ, contracted form (rare, see WH, App., 145) of ἀγαθοερ- (which see), to do good: Act.14:17.† (AS)

agathopoieo.notes: ἀγαθὀ-ποιέω, -ῶ (= cl. ἀγαθὸν ποιεῖν, εὐεργετεῖν), [in LXX: Num.10:32, Jdg.17:13 A, Zep.1:12 (יטב hi.), Tob.12:13 B, 1Ma.11:33, 2Ma.1:2 * ;] to do good; __(a) univ.: 1Pe.2:15, 20, 3:6, 17, 3Jo.11; __(b) for another's…

agathopoios.notes: ἀγαθοποιός, -όν = cl. ἀγαθουργός, [in LXX, of a woman who deals pleasantly in order to corrupt, Sir.42:14 * ;] doing well, acting rightly (Plut.): 1Pe.2:14 (Cremer, 8; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

agathos.notes: ἀγαθός, -ή, -όν, [in LXX chiefly for טוֹב ;] in general, good, in physical and in moral sense, used of persons, things, acts, conditions, etc., applied to that which is regarded as "perfect in its kind, so as to…

agathosune.notes: ἀγαθωσύνη (on the termination, see: ἁγιότης, and cf . WH, App., 152; MM, VGT, see word), -ης, ἡ (ἀγαθός), [in LXX for טוֹבָה, טוֹב, טוּב, only in Heb. bks. ;] goodness (representing "the kindlier, as δικαιοσύνη, the…

agele.notes: ἀγέλη, -ης, ἡ (ἄγω), [in LXX chiefly for עֵ֫דֶר ;] a herd: Mat.8:30-32, Mrk.5:11, 13, Luk.8:32,33.† (AS)

agenealogetos.notes: ἀγενεαλόγητος, -ον (γενεαλογέω), without genealogy, i.e. without recorded pedigree (cf. Neh.7:64): Heb.7:3 (Cremer, 152; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

agenes.notes: ἀγενής, -ές (γένος), __1. unborn (Plat.); __2. of no family, ignoble, base (opposite to ἀγαθός, Soph., Fr., 105): opposite to εὐγενής, 1Co.1:28 (for exx. from π., see MM, VGT, see word). † (AS)

agkale.notes: ἀγκάλη,-ης, ἡ (ἄγκος, a bend), [in LXX for אַצִּיל, חֵיק ;] the bent arm: Luk.2:28 (cf. ἐναγκαλίζομαι).† (AS)

agkistron.notes: ἄγκιστρον, -ου, τό (ἄγκος, a bend), [in LXX חַכָּה, etc. ;] a fish-hook: Mat.17:27.† (AS)

agkura.notes: ἄγκυρα, -ας, ἡ (ἄγκος, a bend), [in Sm.: Jer.52:18 * ;] an anchor: Act.27:29, 30 40 ; figuratively (MM, VGT, see word), Heb.6:19.† (AS)

agnaphos.notes: ἄγναφος, -ον (= ἄγναπτος, γνάπτω, late form of κνάπτω, to card wool), uncarded, undressed, i.e. new (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.92:6, Mrk.2:21.† (AS)

agnoema.notes: ἀγνόημα, -τός, τό (ἀγνοέω), [in LXX: Gen.43:12 (מִשְׁגֶּה), Tob.3:3, Jdth.5:20, Sir.23:2 51:19, 1Ma.13:39 * ;] a sin of ignorance (so in π.; see MM, VGT, see word): Heb.9:7.† (AS)

agnoeo.notes: ἀγνοέω, -ῶ (> ἁγνίζω), [in LXX for שָׁגָה, שָׁגַג, אָשַׁם, etc. ;] __1. to be ignorant, not to know: absol., Ι Ti 1:13, Heb.5:2; with accusative, Act.13:27 17:23, Rom.10:3, 2Co.2:11; ἐν οἷς, 2Pe.2:12; before ὅτι,…

agnoia.notes: ἄγνοια, -ας, ἡ (ἀγνοέω), [in LXX chiefly for אָשָׁם, שְׁגָגָה ;] ignorance : Act.3:17 17:30, Eph.4:18 (with sense of wilful blindness; cf. MM, VGT, see word), 1Pe.1:14.† (AS)

agnosia.notes: ἀγνωσία, -ας, ἡ (γινώσκω), [in LXX: Job.35:16 (בִּבְלִי־דַּעַת), Wis.13:1, 3Ma.5:27 * ;] ignorance (opposite to γνῶσις): 1Co.15:34, 1Pe.2:15 (see Hort in l.; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

agnostos.notes: ἄγνωστος, -ον (γινώσκω), [in LXX: Wis.11:18 18:3, 2Ma.1:19 2:7 (Cremer, 157) * ;] unknown: Act.17:23 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

ago.notes: ἄγω, [in LXX for בּוא hi., לקח, נהג, etc. ;] __1. to lead, bring, carry: with ace, before ἐπί, εἰς, ἕως, πρός and simple dative; metaphorically, to lead, guide, impel: Jhn.10:16 Rom.2:4, Heb.2:10, 2Ti.3:6, al. __2.…

agoge.notes: ἀγωγή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἄγω), [in LXX: Est.2:20 10:3, 2Ma.4:16 6:8 11:24, 3Ma.4:10 * ;] __1. a carrying away. __2. a leading, guiding; metaphorically, training; hence, from the expression ἀ. τοῦ βίου, absol., conduct, way of…

agon.notes: ἀγών, -ῶνος, ὁ (ἄγω), [in LXX: Isa.7:13 (לאה), Est.4:17, Wis.4:2 10:12, II Mac.6, 4Mac .5 * ;] __1. a gathering, esp. for games. __2. a place of assembly. __3. a contest, struggle, trial; metaphorically (MM, VGT, see…

agonia.notes: ἀγωνία, -ας, ἡ (ἀγών), [in LXX: 2Ma.3:14,16 15:19 * ;] __1. a contest, wrestling (Eur., Xen.). __2. Of the mind, great fear, agony, anguish (Dem., Arist.): Luk.22:44 (cf . Field, Notes, 77 f. ; Abbott, Essays, 101 f.…

agonizomai.notes: ἀγωνίζομαι (ἀγών), [in LXX: Da TH 6:14 (שׂוּם בָּל), Sir.4:28, I, II, 4Mac .5 * ;] __1. to contend for a prize: 1Co.9:25, __2. to fight, struggle, strive: Jhn.18:36; metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word). Col.1:29 4:12,…

agora.notes: ἀγορά, -ας, ἡ (ἀγείρω, to bring together) [in LXX for עִזָּבוֹן, שׁוּק ;] __1. an assembly (Hom., Xen., al.). __2. a place of assembly, a public place or forum, a market-place (Hom., Thuc, al.; LXX): Mat.11:16 20:2…

agoraios.notes: ἀγοραῖος, -ον (ἀγορά), __1. frequenting the ἀγορά , a lounger in the ἀγορά (Xen., al.): Act.17:5. __2. In late writers (Strab., al.), proper to the ἀγορά : ἀγοραῖοι (sc. ἥμεραι) ἀγονται (cf. Lat. conventus agere),…

agorazo.notes: ἀγοράζω (ἀγορά), [in LXX chiefly for שׁבר, קנה ;] __1. to frequent the ἀγορά (Hdt., al.). __2. to buy in the market, purchase (Xen., al.; LXX; in π. very common in deeds of sale, see MM, VGT, see word): absol.,…

agra.notes: ἄγρα, -ας, ἡ (ἄγω), __1. the chase, a hunting or catching: Luk.5:4. __2. that which is taken, a catch: of fish, Luk.5:9. † (AS)

agrammatos.notes: ἀγραυλέω, -ῶ (ἀγραυλος, dwelling in the field; ἀγρός, αὐλή), to live in the fields: Luk.2:8.† (AS)

agrauleo.notes: to dwell in the field , (New Testament) (ML)

agreuo.notes: ἀγρεύω (ἄγρα), [in LXX: Job.10:16, Pro.5:22 6:25,26, Hos.5:2 (לקח ni., שׁחט, etc.)* ;] to catch or take by hunting or fishing; metaphorically, Mrk.12:13.† (AS)

agrielaios.notes: ἀγρι-έλαιος, -ον __1. of the wild olive (Anth.). __2. As subst., the wild olive: Rom.11:17, 24 (CGT, in 1.; MM, VGT, see word). † (AS)

agrios.notes: ἄγριος, -α, -ον (ἀγρός), [in LXX for שָׂדֶה, etc. ;] __1. living in fields, wild: μέλι. Mat.3:4, Mrk.1:6. __2. savage, fierce: Ju 13. (Cf. usage in π. of a malignant wound; MM, VGT, see word) † (AS)

agrippas.notes: Ἀγρίππας, -α (Bl., §7, 2), ὁ Agrippa (II): Act.25:13, 22, 23, 24, 26 26:1, 2, 19, 27, 28, 32. (For Agrippa I, see: Ἡρῴδης, 3.) † (AS)

agros.notes: ἀγρός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for שָׂדֶה ]; __1. a field: Mat.6:28, al. __2. the country: Mrk.15:21, al.; pl., country places, farms: Mrk.5:14 6:36 56, Luk.8:34 9:12. __3. = χωρίον a piece of ground: Mrk.10:29,…

agrupneo.notes: ἀγρυπνέω, -ῶ (ἄγρυπνος, seeking sleep ; ἀγρεύω, ὕπνος), [in LXX chiefly for שׁקד ;] to be sleepless, wakeful (Theogn., Xen., al.); metaphorically (LXX) = cl. ἐγρήγορα, to be watchful, vigilant: Mrk.13:33, Luk.21:36,…

agrupnia.notes: ἀγρυπνία, -ας, ἡ (see supr.), [in LXX: Si .9, 2Ma.2:26 * ;] sleeplessness, watching: 2Co.6:5 11:27. (Plat., Hdt.; for exx. in π., see MM, VGT, see word) † (AS)

ahima.notes: αἷμα, ατος, τό, [in LXX for דָּם ;] blood. __1. In the ordinary sense: Mrk.5:25, Luk.8:43,44 22:44, Jhn.19:34, Act.15:20,29 21:25, Rev.8:7,8 11:6 16:3,4,6 19:13. __2. In special senses: __(a) of generation, origin,…

ahimatekchusia.notes: αἱματεκχυσία, -ας, ἡ (αἷμα, ἐκ, χέω), shedding of blood (Eccl.; Cremer, 71): Heb.9:22.† (AS)

ahimorroeo.notes: αἱμορροέω, (αἷμα, ῥέω), [in LXX: Lev.15:33 (דָּוֶה) * ;] to lose blood, suffer from a flow of blood (Hipp.): Mat.9:20.† (AS)

ahireo.notes: αἱρέω, [in LXX for אמר hi., בּחר, etc. ;] to take; Mid., -ομαι (M, Pr., 158 f.; MM, VGT, see word), to choose: Php.1:22, 2Th.2:13, Heb.11:25. (Cf. ἀν-, ἀφ-, δι-, ἐξ-, καθ-, περι-, προ-αιρέω.) † (AS)

ahiresis.notes: αἵρεσις, -εως, ἡ (αἱρέω, -ομαι), [in LXX for נְדָבָה,] __1. capture. __2. choosing, choice (see MM, VGT, see word). __3. that which is chosen, hence, opinion; esp. a peculiar opinion, heresy: 1Co.11:19, Gal.5:20,…

ahiretikos.notes: αἱρετικός, -ή, όν (αἱρέομαι), __1. capable of choosing (Plat.). __2. causing division, heretical, factious (Cremer, 614): as subst., Tit.3:10. † (AS)

ahiretizo.notes: αἱρετίζω, [in LXX chiefly for בּחר (see Cremer, 615) ;] = αἱρέομαι (Hipp., Inscr.), to choose: Mat.12:18 (LXX, ἀντιλήμψομαι).† (AS)

aichmalosia.notes: αἰχμαλωσία -ας, ἡ (αἰχμάλωτος), [in LXX chiefly for שְׁבִי, גּוֹלָה ;] captivity (Diod., al.): Rev.13:10; pl., abstr. for concr., = αἰχμάλωτοι, Eph.4:8 (LXX).† (AS)

aichmaloteuo.notes: αἰχμαλωτεύω (αἰχμάλωτος), [in LXX chiefly for שׁבה ;] = αἰχμαλωτίζω, which see, to lead captive: Eph.4:8 (LXX).† (AS)

aichmalotizo.notes: αἰχμαλωτίζω (αἰχμάλωτος), [in LXX chiefly for שׁבה ;] in late writers = cl. αἰχμάλωτον ποιῶ (ἄγω), to take or lead captive: before εἰς, Luk.21:24 (cf. Tob.1:10); metaphorically, Rom.7:23, 2Co.10:5, 2Ti.3:6.† (AS)

aichmalotos.notes: αἰχμάλωτος, -ον (αἰχμή, a spear, ἁλίσκομαι, to be taken), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁבָה, גּוֹלָה ;] captive: Luk.4:18 (LXX).† (AS)

aidios.notes: ἀΐδιος, -ον (ἀεί), [in LXX: Wis.7:26, 4Ma.10:15 * ;] everlasting (frequently in Inscr.; MM, VGT, see word): Rom.1:20, Ju 6.† SYN.: αἰώνιος, also frequently in Inscr. (Deiss., BS, 363 J. The etymological distinction…

aidos.notes: αἰδώς (-όος), -οῦς, ἡ, [in LXX: 3Ma.1:19 4:5 * ;] a sense of shame, modesty: 1Ti.2:9 (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word).† SYN.: αἰσχύνη (see Thayer, 14; Tr., Syn., §xix; Cremer, 611 f.; CGT on I Ti, l.with). (AS)

aigeios.notes: αἴγειος (WH, -γιος), -α, -ον (αἶξ, a goat), [in LXX for עֵז ;] of a goat: Heb.11:37 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

aigialos.notes: αἰγιαλός, -οῦ, ὁ (on the derivation, see Boisacq, see word), [in LXX: Jdg.5:17 (חוֹף), Sir.24:14 * ;] the sea-shore, beach (cf. Field, Notes, 146; DCG, i, 175 f.; MM, VGT, see word): Mat.13:2,48 Jhn.21:4, Act.21:5…

aiguptos.notes: Αἴγυπτος, ου, ἡ, Egypt: Mat.2:13-15,19, Act.2:10 7:9-39, Heb.3:16 11:26,27; γῆ Αἴ., Act.7:40 13:17, Heb.8:9, Ju 5 (cf. Exo.5:12, al.); ἡ Αἴ., Act.7:11; figuratively, of Jerusalem as hostile to God, Rev.11:8.† (AS)

aineo.notes: αἰνέω, -ῶ (αἶνος), poët., Ion. and late prose (MM, VGT, see word) for cl. ἐπαινέω, [in LXX chiefly for הלל, ידה ;] to praise: with accusative, τ. θεόν, Luk.2:13,20 19:37 24:53, Act.2:47 3:8,9, Rom.15:11; with dative,…

ainesis.notes: αἴνεσις, -εως, ἡ (αἰνέω), [in LXX chiefly for תּוֹדָה, תְּהִלָּה ;] praise (Eccl.): θυσία αἰνέσεως (Lev.7:12, זֶבַח תּוֹדָה), Heb.13:15.† (AS)

ainigma.notes: αἴνιγμα, -ατος, τό (αἰνίσσομαι, to speak in riddles; αἰνός = δεινός, dread, strange), [in LXX for חִידָה, Num.12:8 and always exc. Deu.28:37 (שַׁמָּה) ;] a dark saying, riddle: 1Co.13:12 (cf. Nu, l.with).† (AS)

ainon.notes: Αἰνών, ἡ, indecl. (cf. Heb.עַיִן, a spring), Aenon: Jhn.3:23.† (AS)

ainos.notes: αἶνος, ου, ὁ, [in LXX for הלל pi, עֹז ;] poët, and Ion., __1. = μῦθος, a tale (Horn., al.). __2. = Att. ἔπαινος, praise (Horn., al.): Mat.21:16 (LXX), Luk.18:43. __3. In π., a decree (MM, VGT, see word). † (AS)

aion.notes: αἰών, -ῶνος, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for עַד ,עוֹלָם ;] __1. in cl., like Lat. aevum (LS, MM, VGT, see word), a space of time, as, a lifetime, generation, period of history, an indefinitely long period; in NT of an…

aionios.notes: αἰώνιος, -ον (as usual in Attic), also -α, -ον. 2Th.2:16, Heb.9:12; (αἰών), [in LXX chiefly for עוֹלָם ;] age-long, eternal, __(a) of that which is without either beginning or end: Rom.16:26, Heb.9:14; __(b) of that…

aiphnidios.notes: αἰφνίδιος (in Lk, l.with, ἐφν- WH; see M, Pr., 35), -ον (αἴφνης = ἄφνως, suddenly), [in LXX: Wis.17:15, 2Ma.14:17, 3Ma.3:24 * ;] sudden, unexpected: Luk.21:34, 1Th.5:3.† (AS)

airo.notes: αἴρω, [in LXX chiefly for נשׂא, also for לקח, etc. ;] __1. to raise, take up, lift or draw up: Jhn.8:59 11:41, Act.27:17, al. __2. to bear, carry: Mat.4:6 16:24, al. __3. to bear or take away, carry off, remove:…

aischrokerdes.notes: αἰσχροκερδής, -ες (αἰσχρός, κέρδος), greedy of base gains: 1Ti.3:3,8 Abbot-Smith omits 1Ti.3:3, Tit.1:7.† (AS)

aischrokerdos.notes: αἰσχροκερδῶς, adv., from eagerness for base gain: 1Pe.5:2 (here only).† (AS)

aischrologia.notes: αἰσχρολογία, -ας, ἡ (αἰσχρός, λέγω), abusive language, abuse (Lft., ICC, in l.; MM, VGT, see word): Col.3:8.† (AS)

aischros.notes: αἰσχρός, -ά, όν (αἶσχος, shame, disgrace), [in LXX: Gen.41:3 ff. (רַע, רֹעַ), Jdth.12:12, al. ;] base, shameful: 1Co.11:6 14:35, Eph.5:12, Tit.1:11 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

aischune.notes: αἰσχύνη, -ης, ἡ (αἶσχος, shame, disgrace), [in LXX chiefly for בּשֶׁת ;] shame (MM, VGT, see word): subjectively, Luk.14:19, 2Co.4:2; objectively, Php.3:19, Heb.12:2; as something to be ashamed of, Rev.3:18; pl.…

aischuno.notes: αἰσχύνω (αἶσχος, shame), [in LXX chiefly for בּוּשׁ ;] __1. to disfigure (Hom.). __2. to dishonour (Pro.29:15). __3. to make ashamed (Sir.13:7). Pass., to be put to shame, be ashamed: 2Co.10:8; Php.1:20, 1Pe.4:16,…

aisthanomai.notes: αἰσθάνομαι, [in LXX for בּין, חפז, ידע ;] to perceive: withaccusative of thing(s) (Bl., §36, 5; MM, VGT, see word), Luk.9:45 (Cremer, 619 f.).† (AS)

aisthesis.notes: αἴσθησις, -εως, ἡ (αἰσθάνομαι), [in LXX chiefly for דַּעַת ;] perception (MM, VGT, see word): Php.1:9.† SYN.: ἐπίγνωσις, which see (cf. Cremer, 620). (AS)

aistheterion.notes: αἰσθητήριον, -ου, τό (αἰσθάνομαι), [in LXX: Jer.4:19 (קִיר), 4Ma.2:22 * ;] sense, organ of perception: Heb.5:14 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

aitema.notes: αἴτημα, -ατος, τό (αἰτέω), [in LXX chiefly for שְׁאֵלָה ;] that which has been asked for, a petition, request: Luk.23:24, Php.4:6, Jhn.5:15.† SYN.: see: δέησις. (AS)

aiteo.notes: αἰτέω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for שׁאל ;] to ask, request: absol., Mat.7:7, Jas.1:6; with accusative of person(s), Mat.5:42, Luk.6:30; with accusative of thing(s), before ἀπό, Mat.20:20, 1Jn.5:15; id. before παρά,…

aithiops.notes: .*Αἰθίοψ, -οπος, ὁ (αἴθω, to burn, ὤψ, face; i.e. swarthy), [in LXX for כּוּשׁ ;] Ethiopian: Act.8:27.† (AS)

aitia.notes: αἰτία, -ας, ἡ, [in LXX: Gen.4:13 (עָוֹן), Pro.28:17 (עָשַׁק), and frequently in Wi, II, III Mac ;] __1. cause, reason, occasion, case: Mat.19:3, Luk.8:47, Act.10:21 22:24 28:20, 2Ti.1:6,12, Tit.1:13, Heb.2:11 ; εἰ…

aitiama.notes: αἰτίωμα (Rec. αἰτίαμα, the usual form; see MM, VGT, see word), -τος, τό (αἰτιάομαι, αἰτία), a charge, accusation: Act.25:7.† (AS)

aitios.notes: αἴτιος, -α, ὁ (αἰτία), [in LXX: 1Ki.22:22 (סבב) Dan LXX Bel 41, TH ib. 42, Su 53, 2Ma.4:47 13:4, 4Ma.1:11 * ;] __1. causative of, responsible for; as subst., ὁ αἰ., the cause, author: Heb.5:9; τὸ αἰ., the cause,…

akaireo.notes: ἀκαιρέομαι, -οῦμαι (ἄκαιρος, unseasonable), to have no opportunity (opposite to εὐκαιρέω): Php.4:10.† (AS)

akairos.notes: ἀκαίρως, adv. (ἄκαιρος, unseasonable), [in LXX: Si 35 (32):4 * ;] out of season, unseasonably: opposite to εὐκαίρως (which see), 2Ti.4:2 (cf. Cremer, 740; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

akakos.notes: ἄκακος, -ον, [in LXX for פְּתִי, תָּם, etc. ;] __(a) as in cl. (Æsch., Plat., al.), of persons, simple, guileless: Rom.16:18, Heb.7:26 (cf. Cremer, 327); __(b) of things, undamaged (? MM, VGT, see word). † (AS)

akantha.notes: ἄκανθα, -ης, ἡ (ἀκή, a point), [in LXX chiefly for קוֹץ, also for סִיר, שַׁיִת, etc. ;] a prickly plant, thorn, brier; in NT always pl.: Mat.7:16 13:7,22 27:29, Mrk.4:7,18, Luk.6:44 8:7,14, Jhn.19:2, Heb.6:8 (see MM,…

akanthinos.notes: ἀκάνθινος, -ον (ἄκανθα), [in LXX: Isa.34:13 (סיר) * ;] __1. of thorns: Mrk.15:17, Jhn.19:5. __2. of acantha-wood (Hdt.; π. ap. MM, VGT, see word). † (AS)

akarpos.notes: ἄ-καρπος, -ον, [in LXX: Jer.2:6 (צַלְמָוֶת), Wis.15:4, 4Ma.16:7 ;] unfruitful, barren: figuratively, Mat.13:22, Mrk.4:19, 1Co.14:14, Eph.5:11, Tit.3:14, 2Pe.1:8, Ju 12.† (AS)

akatagnostos.notes: ἀ-κατά-γνωστος, -ον (καταγινώσκω), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:47 ;] not open to just rebuke, irreprehensible: Tit.2:8 (see Cremer, 676; and for other exx., MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

akatakaluptos.notes: ἀ-κατα-κάλυπτος, -ον (κατακαλύπτω), [in LXX: Lev.13:45 A (פָּרוּע) * ;] uncovered, unveiled: 1Co.11:5, 13.† (AS)

akatakritos.notes: ἀ-κατά-κριτος, -ον (κατακρίνω), __1. uncondemned (EV): Act.16:37, 22:25. __2. = cl. ἄκριτος, without trial, not yet tried (MM, VGT, see word) Ac, ll. cc. † (AS)

akatapaustos.notes: ἀκατάπαυστος, -ον (καταπαύω), that cannot cease, not to be restrained: with genitive of thing(s), 2Pe.2:14, T, Tr. txt.† (AS)

akatastasia.notes: ἀκαταστασία, -ας, ἡ (ἀκατάστατος), [in LXX: Pro.26:28 (מִדְחֶה), Tob.4:13 * ;] instability (MM, VGT, see word); hence, confusion, tumult: 1Co.14:33, Jas.3:16; pl. (Bl., §32, 6), Luk.21:9, 2Co.6:5, 12:20 (Polyb., al.;…

akatastatos.notes: ἀ-κατά-στατος, -ον (καθίστημι), [in LXX: Isa.54:11 (סֹעֲרָה) ;] unsettled, unstable: Jas.1:8, 3:8.† (AS)

akatharsia.notes: ἀκαθαρσία, -ας, ἡ (ἀκάθαρτος), [in LXX chiefly for טֻמְאָה, טָמֵא ;] uncleanness, impurity, __(a) physical (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.23:27; __(b) moral: Rom.1:24 6:19, 2Co.12:21, Gal.5:19, Eph.4:19 5:3, Col.3:5,…

akathartos.notes: ἀκάθαρτος, -ον (ἀ- neg., καθαίρω), [in LXX chiefly for טָמֵא ;] unclean, impure; __(a) physically (LS, MM, VGT, see word); __(b) ceremonially: Act.10:14,28 11:8, 1Co.7:14, 2Co.6:17, Rev.18:2; __(with) morally:…

akeldama.notes: § Akeldama dama (Proper name) (traditionally located south of the valley of Hinnom), "field of blood"

akeraios.notes: ἀκέραιος, -ον (κεράννυμι), [in LXX: Est.8:13 * ;] unmixed, pure, hence, metaphorically (cf. MM, VGT, see word), guileless, simple: Mat.10:16, Rom.16:19, Php.2:15.† SYN.: ἄδολος, ἄκακος, ἁπλοῦς (cf. Ellic. on Phl.,…

aklines.notes: ἀκλινής, -ές (κλίνω), [in LXX: 4Ma.6:7 17:3 * ;] unbending, firm: metaphorically (MM, VGT, see word). Heb.10:23.† (AS)

akmazo.notes: ἀκμάζω (ἀκμήν), [in LXX: 4Ma.2:3 * ;] to be at the prime; of produce of the ground, to be ripe (Thuc.): Rev.14:18 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

akmen.notes: ἀκμήν, accusative of ἀκμή, a point, used as adv., at the present point of time, even now, even yet: Mat.15:16.† (AS)

akoe.notes: ἀκοή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀκούω), [in LXX: Exo.15:26, al. for שׁמע, its parts and derivatives, exc. Deu.11:22 (שׁמר) ;] __1. hearing, the sense of hearing: 1Co.12:17, 2Pe.2:8; "Hebraic dative," ἀκοῇ ακούειν (frequently in LXX;…

akoloutheo.notes: ἀκολουθέω, -ῶ (ἀκόλουθος, following; ἀ- cop., κέλευθος, poët., away), [in LXX chiefly for הָלַךְ ;] to accompany, follow: Mat.4:25, al. Metaphorical, of discipleship: Mat.9:9, Mrk.9:38, Jhn.12:26, al. Absol.:…

akolutos.notes: ἀκωλύτως adv. (κωλύω), [in Sm.: Job.34:31 * ;] without hindrance (so frequently in legal documents; MM, VGT, see word): Act.28:31.† (AS)

akon.notes: ἀκων (Attic contr. for ἀέκων), -ουα, -ον (ἀ- neg., ἅκων, willing), [in LXX: Job.14:17, 2Ma.11:12 * ;] unwilling: 1Co.9:17.† (AS)

akouo.notes: ἀκούω, [in LXX chiefly for שׁמע ] to hear, listen, attend, perceive by hearing, comprehend by hearing. __1. Intrans.: Mrk.4:3 7:37, Jas.2:5, Rev.2:7, al.; τ. ὠσίν, Mat.13:15 (LXX); with cogn. dative, ακοῇ ἀ. (see:…

akrasia.notes: ἀκρασία, -ας, ἡ (ἀκρατής, which see), [in LXX: 1Ma.6:26 * ;] in Arist. and later writers = ἀκράτεια (Lft., Notes, 222 f.), want of power, hence want of self-control, incontinence: Mat.23:25, 1Co.7:5.† (AS)

akrates.notes: ἀκρατής, -ές (κράτος), [in LXX: Pro.27:20 * ;] __(a) powerless, impotent; __(b) in moral sense, lacking self-control, incontinent: 2Ti.3:3.† (AS)

akratos.notes: ἄκρατος, -ον (κεράννυμι), [in LXX: Psa.75:8 (חֲמַר), Jer.25:15 (חֵמָה), 3Ma.5:2 * ;] unmixed, pure: οἶνος, Rev.14:10.† (AS)

akribeia.notes: ἀκρίβεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀκριβής), [in LXX: Dan LXX TH 7:16 (יַצִּיב), Wis.12:21, Sir.16:25 42:4 * ;] exactness, precision (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word): Act.22:3.† (AS)

akribestatos.notes: ἀκριβής, -ές [in LXX: Dan LXX 2:45 6:12 (יַצִּיב) Dan.4:25, Est.4:5, Sir.18:29 19:25 31:24 32:3 * ;] exact, precise, careful, of things and persons: superl., Act.26:5.† (AS)

akriboo.notes: ἀκριβόω, -ῶ (ἀκριβής), [in Aq.: Isa.30:8 49:16 * ;] to enquire with exactness, learn carefully: Mat.2:7, 16 (for similar ex., see MM, VGT, sv.).† (AS)

akribos.notes: ἀκριβῶς adv. (ἀκριβής), [in LXX: Deu.19:18 (יָטַב), Da TH 7:19 (יְצֵב), Eze.39:14, Wis.19:18, Sir.18:29 ;] with exactness, carefully: Mat.2:8, Luk.1:3, Act.18:25, Eph.5:15, 1Th.5:2 (M, Th., in l). Compar.,…

akris.notes: ἀκρίς, -ίδος, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for אַרְבֶּה, also for חָגָב, etc. ;] a locust: Mat.3:4, Mrk.1:6, Rev.9:3, 7.† (AS)

akroaterion.notes: ἀκροατήριον, -ου, τό (ἀκροάομαι, to listen) a place of audience: Act.25:23 (Plut.).† (AS)

akroates.notes: ἀκροατής, οῦ, ὁ (see supr.) [in LXX: Isa.3:3 (לַחַשׁ), Sir.3:29 ;] a hearer: Rom.2:13, Jas.1:22, 23, 25.† (AS)

akrobustia.notes: ἀκροβυστία, -ας, ή (perh. an Alexandrian form of cl. ἀκροποσθία; cf. MM, VGT, see word) [in LXX for עׇרְלָה ;] the prepuce, foreskin (LXX), hence abstr., uncircumcision: Act.11:3, Rom.2:25-27 3:30 4:10-12,…

akrogoniaios.notes: ἀκρο-γωνιαῖος, -αία, -αῖον (ἄκροι, γωνία, an angle) [in LXX: Isa.28:16 (פִּנָּה) * ;] = Attic γωνιαῖος (frequently in Inscr.; MM, VGT, see word ἀ.), at the extreme angle: ὁ ἀ., the corner foundation stone, Eph.2:20,…

akron.notes: ἄκρος, -α, -ον [in LXX for קֶצַח, בֹּהֶן, etc. ;] highest, extreme; as subst., τὸ ἄ., the top, extremity: Mrk.13:27, Luk.16:24, Heb.11:21; pl. (cf. MM, VGT, see word), Mat.24:31.† (AS)

akrothinion.notes: ἀκροθίνιον, -ου, τό (ἄκρος, θίς, a heap), prop., the top of a heap, hence, in pl., __1. first-fruits (Xen.; MM, VGT, see word). __2. In war, the choicest spoils (cf. Hdt., viii, 121 f.): Heb.7:4.† (AS)

akulas.notes: Ἀκύλας, -ου (and -α; MM, VGT, see word), ὁ (Lat.), Aquila: Act.18:2, 18 18:26, Rom.16:3, 1Co.16:19, 2Ti.4:19.† (AS)

akuroo.notes: ἀκυρόω, -ῶ (κῦρος, authority), [in LXX: 1Es.6:32 Est.6:1-34, 2Ma.6:1-31 * ;] to revoke, invalidate (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.15:6, Mrk.7:13, Gal.3:17 (Plut.).† (AS)

alabastron.notes: ἀλάβαστρον, -ου, τό (also -ος, ὁ, ἡ; colloq. and κοινή for ἀλάβαστος), [in LXX: 4Ki.21:13 (צַלַּחַת)* ;] a box of alabaster (ἀλαβαστίτης) for ointment: Mat.26:7, Mrk.14:3, Luk.7:37 (see DCG, i, 41b; MM, VGT, see…

alalazo.notes: ἀλαλάζω (onomat. from the battle-cry ἀλαλά), [in LXX chiefly for רוּעַ hi., יָלַל ;] prop, to raise a war-cry, shout with triumph or joy; rarely of grief, to wail: Mrk.5:38 (cf. Jer.4:8); of a cymbal, ἀλαλάζον (RV.…

alaletos.notes: ἀ-λάλητος, -ον (λαλέω), inexpressible, not to be uttered: Rom.8:26.† (AS)

alalos.notes: ἄ-λαλος, -ον (λάλος, talkative), [in LXX: Psa.31:18 (אָלַם ni.) Psa.38:13 (אִלֵּם)* ;] dumb, speechless: Mrk.7:37 9:17, 25.† (AS)

alazon.notes: ἀλαζών, -όνος, ὁ, ἡ (ἄλη, wandering) [in LXX: Job.28:8 (שַׁחַץ) Hab.2:5 (יָהִיר), Pro.21:24 (לוּץ)* ;] prop, a vagabond, hence, an impostor, a boaster: Rom.1:30, 2Ti.3:2.† SYN.: ὐβριστής, ὑπερήφανος (see Tr., Syn., §…

alazoneia.notes: ἀλαζονία(Rec. -εία, the earlier form), -ας, ἡ (ἀλαζών), [in LXX: Wis.5:8 17:7, 2; 4Ma.5:1-38 * ;] the character of an ἀλαζών, boastfulness, vainglory, vaunting: Jas.4:16 (Mayor, in l), 1Jn.2:16.† (AS)

aleipho.notes: ἀλείφω (cf. λίπος, oil), [in LXX: Gen.31:13, Exo.40:15, Num.3:3 (מָשַׁח), Eze.13:10 ff. (טוּחַ), Rut.3:3, 2Ki.12:20 14:2, 4Ki.4:2, 2Ch.28:15, Mic.6:15, Dan LXX TH Dan.10:3 (סוּךְ), Est.2:12, Jdth.16:8 * ;] to anoint,…

alektor.notes: ἀλέκτωρ, -ορος, ὁ (poët. form of ἀλεκτρυών; see MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX: Pro.30:31) (זַרְזִיר; BDB, Lex., 267)* ;] a cock: Mat.26:34, Mrk.14:30, 68 14:72 Luk.22:34, 60-61, Jhn.13:38 18:27.† (AS)

alektorophonia.notes: ἀλεκτοροφωνία, -ας, ἡ (ἀλέκτωρ, φωνή), cock-crowing, i.e. the third watch in the night: Mrk.13:35.† (AS)

aletheia.notes: ἀλήθεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀληθής), [in LXX chiefly for אֱמֶת (on which, see Cremer, 627f.), אֱמוּנָה ;] truth (see DB, iv, 818f.). __1. Objectively, "the reality lying at the basis of an appearance; the manifested, veritable…

alethes.notes: ἀληθής, -ές (λήθω = λανθάνω, hence primarily, unconcealed, manifest; hence, actual, real), [in LXX for אֶמֶת, etc. ;] __(a) of things, true, conforming to reality: Jhn.4:18 5:31-32 6:55 (= ἀληθινός, which see)…

aletheuo.notes: ἀληθεύω (ἀληθής), [in LXX: Gen.20:16 (יָכַח) Gen.42:16 (אֶמֶת), Pro.21:3 (מִשְׁפָּט), Isa.44:26 (שָׁלַם), Sir.34:4 * ;] to speak the truth (R, mg., deal truly; Field, Notes, 192): Gal.4:16, Eph.4:15.† (AS)

alethinos.notes: ἀληθινός, -ή, -όν (ἀληθής), [in LXX for אֶמֶת ;] true, in the sense of real, ideal, genuine: Luk.16:11 Jhn.1:9 4:23, 37 6:32 7:28 8:16 15:1 17:3 19:35, 1Th.1:9, Heb.8:2 9:24 10:22, 1Jn.2:8 Jn 5:20, Rev.3:7, 14 6:10…

aletho.notes: ἀλήθω (κοινή form of the Attic ἀλέω), [in LXX for טָחַן ;] to grind: Mat.24:41, Luk.17:35.† (AS)

alethos.notes: ἀληθῶς adv. (ἀληθής). [in LXX (Jer.28:6, Psa.58:1, al.) chiefly for אָמֵן and cogn. forms ;] truly, surely: Mat.14:33 26:73 Mrk.14:70 15:39, Luk.9:27 12:44 21:3, Jhn.1:48 4:42 6:14 7:26, 40 8:31 17:8 Act.12:11,…

aleuron.notes: ἄλευρον, -ου, τό (ἀλεύω, to grind), [in LXX for קֶמַח, Num.5:15, al. ;] real: Mat.13:33, Luk.13:21.† (AS)

alexandrinos.notes: Ἀλεξανδρινός (Rec. -δρῖνο; see Kühner3, II, 296), -ή, -όν, Alexan­drian: Act.27:6 28:11.† (AS)

alexandros.notes: Ἀλέξανδρος, -ου, ὁ, Alexander __1. Son of Simon of Cyrene: Mrk.15:21 __2. A kinsman of the High Priest: Act.4:6. __3. A certain Jew: Act.19:33. __4. A coppersmith: 1Ti.1:20. __5. Perh. = 4 (see Ellic. on I Ti,…

alisgema.notes: ἀλίσγημα, -τος, τό (late ἀλισγέω, to pollute) pollution: Act.15:20.† (AS)

allachothen.notes: ἀλλαχόθεν adv. (ἀλλος), [in LXX: 4Ma.1:7 * ;] = ἀλλοθεν (seeMM, VGT, see word) from another place: Jhn.10:1.† (AS)

allasso.notes: ἀλλάσσω (ἄλλος), [in LXX chiefly for חָלַף, מוּר hi., etc. ;] __1. to change: Act.6:14, Gal.4:20. __2. to transform: 1Co.15:51-52, Heb.1:12. __3. to exchange: with accusative, before ἐν (= בְּ, Psa.106:20) instead of…

allegoreo.notes: ἀλληγορέω, -ῶ (ἄλλος, ἀγορεύω) to speak allegorically (Cremer, 96 ff.): Gal.4:24.† (AS)

allelon.notes: ἀλλήλων (genitive pl.), dative -οις, -αις, accusative -ους, -ας, -α (no nom.), recipr. pron. (ἄλλος), of one another, mutually: Mat.25:32, Mrk.4:41, Jhn.13:22, al. (AS)

allelouia.notes: ἁλληλουιά (Rec. ἀλληλούϊα; Heb. הָלַל יָהּ, praise the Lord), [in LXX in the titles of certain Psa (104 (105), al.), and at the end of Psa.150:1-6; also Tob.13:18, 3Ma.7:13 ;] hallelujah, alleluia: Rev.19:1, 3-4…

allogenes.notes: ἀλλογενής, -ές (ἄλλος, γένος), [in LXX chiefly for זוּר, נֵכָר ;] of another race, a foreigner ( = ἀλλόφυλος; Cremer, 150; MM, VGT, see word): Luk.17:18.† (AS)

allophulos.notes: ἀλλόφυλος, -ον (ἄλλος, φῦλον, a tribe), [in LXX chiefly for פְּלֶשֶׁת ;] foreign, of another race (MM, VGT, see word); as opposite to a Jew, a Gentile: Act.10:28.† (AS)

allos.notes: ἄλλος, -η, -ο, (cf. Lat. alius, Eng. else), [in LXX for אַחֵר ,אֶחָד, etc. ;] other, another: absol., Mat.20:3, al.; ἄ. δέ, 1Co.3:10 12:8; pl., Mrk.6:15; attached to a noun, Mat.2:12 4:21, al.; with art., ὁ ἄ., the…

allotriepiskopos.notes: ἀλλοτρι-επίσκοπος (Rec. ἀλλοτριοεπ-), -ου, ὁ one who meddles in things alien to his calling: 1Pe.4:15 (see ICC, in l; Deiss., BS, 224-4; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

allotrios.notes: ἀλλότριος, -α, -ον (ἄλλος), [in LXX for זוּר, נֵכָר, אַחֵר ;] __1. be-longing to another, not one's own (opposite to ἴδιος): Luk.16:12, Rom.14:4 15:20 (Field, Notes, 165 f.), 2Co.10:15-16, 1Ti.5:22, Heb.9:25. __2.…

aloao.notes: ἀλοάω, -ῶ (ἅλως, see: ἅλων; and cf. MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX chiefly for דּוּשׁ ;] to thresh: 1Co.9:9 9:13; 1Ti.5:18.† (AS)

aloe.notes: ἀλόη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Sng.4:14 א (אֲהָלִים)* ;] the aloe, aloes (the powder of a fragrant wood): Jhn.19:39.† (AS)

alogos.notes: ἄ-λογος, -ον [in LXX: Exo.6:12 (שָׂפָה עָרֵל), Num.6:12 (נָפַל), Job.11:12, Wis.11:15-16, 4Ma.3:1-21 * ;] __1. without reason, irrational: ζῷα, 2Pe.2:12, Ju 10. __2. contrary to reason: Act.25:27 (see MM, VGT, see…

alopex.notes: ἀλώπηξ, -εκος, ἡ [in LXX for שׁוּעָל ;] a fox: Mat.8:20, Luk.9:58; metaphorically, of Herod, Luk.13:32.† (AS)

alpha.notes: Α, α, ἄλφα (which see), τό, indecl., alpha, the first letter of the Greek alphabet. As a numeral, ά = 1, α = 1000. As a prefix, it appears to have at least two and perhaps three distinct senses: __1. ἀ- (before a…

alphaios.notes: Ἀλφαῖος (WH, Ἀλ-), -ου, ὁ (Aram. חַלְפַּי) Alphæus. __1. Father of Levi: Mrk.2:14. __2. Father of James: Mat.10:3, Mrk.3:13, Luk.6:15, Act.1:13.† (AS)

amachos.notes: ἄμαχος, -ον (μάχη); __1. invincible (frequently in cl.). __2. abstaining from fighting, non-combatant (Xen.). Metaphorical (cf. MM, VGT, see word), not contentious: 1Ti.3:3, Tit.3:2.† (AS)

amao.notes: ἀμάω, -ῶ (in cl. chiefly poët.), to reap: Jas.5:4.† (AS)

amarantos.notes: ἀμάραντος, -ον (μαραίνομαι), [in LXX: Wis.6:12 (σοφία)* ;] un­fading (whence ὁ ἀ., the amaranth, an unfading flower): 1Pe.1:4 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

amathes.notes: ἀμαθής, -ές (μανθάνω), [in Sm.: Psa.49:11 * ;] unlearned, ignorant: 2Pe.3:16 (on the rareness of this word, see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

ameleo.notes: ἀμελέω, -ῶ (μέλει), [in LXX: Jer.4:17 (מָרָה) Jer.31:32 (בַּעַל), Wis.3:10, 2Ma.4:14 * ;] __(a) absol., to be careless, not to care: Mat.22:5; __(b) with genitive, to be careless of, to neglect: 1Ti.4:14, Heb.2:3 8:9…

amemptos.notes: ἄ-μεμπτος, -ον (μέμφομσι), [in LXX chiefly for תָּם ;] blameless, free from fault (in π. of a marriage-contract; M, Th., I, 3:13; cf. MM, VGT, see word): Luk.1:6, Php.2:15 3:6, 1Th.3:13 (WH, mg., -ως) Heb.8:7.† SYN.:…

amen.notes: ἀμήν, indecl. (Heb. אָמֵן, verbal adj. fr. אמן, to prop. ni., be firm), [in LXX: 1Ch.16:36, I Est.9:46, Neh.5:13 8:6, Tob.8:8 14:15, 3Ma.7:23, 4Ma.18:24 (elsewhere ''א is rendered ἀληθινός, Isa.65:16; ἀληθῶς, Je 35…

amerimnos.notes: ἀμέριμνος, -ον (μέριμνα), [in LXX: Wis.6:15 7:23 * ;] free from anxiety or care: Mat.28:14, 1Co.7:32 (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

ametameletos.notes: ἀ-μεταμέλητος, -ον (μεταμέλομαι), not repented of, unregretted: Rom.11:29, 2Co.7:10.† (AS)

ametanoetos.notes: ἀμετανόητος, -ον (μετανοέω) __1. impenitent: Rom.2:5 __2. = ἀμεταμέλητος (π., Philo, al.; see Deiss., BS, 257; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

ametathetos.notes: ἀ-μετάθετος, -ον (μετατίθημι), [in LXX: 3Ma.5:1 3Mac 5:12 * ;] immutable: Heb.6:18; as subat., τὸ ἀ., immutability, Heb.6:17 (see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

amethustos.notes: ἀμέθυστος, -ου, ἡ (accusative to Plut., ἀ- μεθύω, being regarded as an antidote against drunkenness) [in LXX: Exo.28:19 36:19 39:12 Eze.28:13 * ;] amethyst, a purple quartz: Rev.21:20.† (AS)

ametor.notes: ἀμήτωρ, -ορος, ὁ, ἡ (μήτηρ), without a mother (frequently in Gk. writers of the gods): ἀπάτωρ ἀ. of one without recorded genealogy, Heb.7:3 (cf. MM, VGT, see word) (AS)

ametros.notes: ἄμετρος, -ον (μέτρον), without measure: adverbially, εἰς τὰ ἄ., excessively, 2Co.10:13 10:15† (AS)

amiantos.notes: ἀ-μίαντος, -ον (μιαίνω), [in LXX: Wis.3:13 4:2 8:20, 2Ma.14:36 2Mac 15:34 * ;] undefiled, free from contamination (in π., of αἰθήρ; MM, VGT, see word): Heb.7:26 13:4, 1Pe.1:4, Jas.1:27.† SYN.: ἄμωμος, ἄσπιλος…

aminadab.notes: Ἀμιναδάβ, ὁ indecl. (Heb עַמִּינָדָב), Amminadab: Mat.1:4, Luk.3:33 (WH om.).† (AS)

ammos.notes: ἄμμος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for חוֹל ;] sand, sandy ground: Mat.7:26, Rom.9:27, Heb.11:12, Rev.18:1, Rev.20:8.† (AS)

amnos.notes: ἀμνός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for כֶּבֶשׂ ;] a lamb: figuratively, of Christ (DCG, ii, 620b), Jhn.1:29, 36, Act.8:32" (LXX), 1Pe.1:19 (cf. ἀρνίον; Cremer, 102, 635).† (AS)

amometos.notes: ἀ-μώμητος, -ου (μωμάομαι), blameless: 2Pe.3:14.† SYN.: ἄμεμπτος (which see), ἀνέγκλητος, ἀνεπίλημπτος (AS)

amomos.notes: ἄ-μωμος, -ον (μῶμος, which see), [in LXX chiefly for תָּמִים ]; of sacrificial victims, without blemish: of Christ, Heb.9:14, 1Pe.1:19; etbically, unblemished, faultless: Eph.1:4 5:27, Php.2:15, Col.1:22, Ju 24,…

amos.notes: Ἀμώς, ὁ indecl. (Heb. אָמוֹץ, Isa.1:1; עָמוֹס, Amo.1:1; אָמוֹן, 4Ki.21:18 ff. B) __1. as in 2Ki, Lc. B (Α. Ἀμμών; Jos., Ἀμμών, "Αμωσσς), Amon: Mat.1:10. __2. Amos: Luk.3:25.† (AS)

ampelon.notes: ἀμπελών, -ῶνος, ὁ (ἄμπελος), [in LXX for כֶּרֶם ;] a vineyard: Mat.20:1 ff. Mat.21:28 ff, Luk.13:6 20:9 ff., 1Co.9:7. Æschin., 49, 13; Diod., al.; see MM, VGT, see word; LS, see word ἀμπελουργεῖον.) (AS)

ampelos.notes: ἄμπελος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX for גֶּפֶן ;] vine: Mat.26:29, Mrk.14:25, Luk.22:18 Jas.3:12; figuratively, of Christ, Jhn.15:1, 4-5; of his enemies (on the usage here, see MM, VGT, see word): Rev.14:18-19.† (AS)

ampelourgos.notes: ἀμπελουργός, -οῦ, ὁ, ἡ [in LXX for כֹּרֵם ;] a vine dresser: Luk.13:7.† (AS)

amphiblestron.notes: ἀμφίβληστρον, -ου, τό (ἀμφιβάλλω), [in LXX chiefly for חֵרֶם ;] something thrown around, as a garment; spec., a casting-net: Mat.4:18.† (AS)

amphiennumi.notes: ἀμφιέννυμι (ἕννυμι, to clothe), to clothe: Mat.6:30 11:8, Luk.7:25 (cf. ἀμφιάζω).† (AS)

amphipolis.notes: Ἀμφίπολις, -εως, ἡ Amphipolis, in Macedonia, so called because the river Strymon flowed around it: Act.17:1.† (AS)

amphodon.notes: ἄμφοδον, -ου, το (ἀμφί ὁδός), [in LXX for אַרְמוֹן (Jer.17:27 30:10 49:27)* ;] prop., a road around anything (RV, the open street): Mrk.11:4, Act.19:28, WH, mg.† (AS)

amphoteros.notes: ἀμφότεροι, -αι, -α (replaces ἄμφω in κοινή, V. M, Pr., 57; used of more than two, Pr., 80; MM, VGT, see word), both of two: Mat.9:17, al. (AS)

amplias.notes: Ἀμπλιᾶτος (T, -ίατος; Rec. Ἀμπλιᾶς; see MM, VGT, 5.see), -ου, ὁ Ampliatus: Rom.1:6 8:8.† (AS)

amuno.notes: ἀμύνω [in LXX (mid.): Jos.10:13 (נָקַם), נָקַם), Psa.118:10-12 מוּל hi.), Isa.59:16 (יָשַׁע hi.), Wis.11:3, al. ;] to ward off, etc. Mid. __(a) to defend oneself against; __(b) to requite; __(with) = act., to defend,…

an.notes: ἄν, conditional particle, which cannot usually be separately translated in English, its force depending on the constructions which contain it (see further, LS, see word; WM, §xlii; M, Pr., 165 ff.; MM, VGT, see…

anabaino.notes: ἀναβαίνω [in LXX chiefly for עָלָה ;] to go up, ascend, __(a) of persons: ἐπί συκομωρέαν, Luk.19:4; εἰς τ. πλοῖον, Mrk.6:51; εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα, Mat.20:17; εἰς τ. ἱερόν, with inf. (M, Pr., 205), Luk.18:10; with mention…

anaballo.notes: ἀνα-βάλλω [in LXX: Psa.78:21 89:38 (עָבַר), 1Ki.28:14, Psa.104:2 (עָטָה) ;] to defer, put off (MM, VGT, see word): mid., Act.24:22.† (AS)

anabibazo.notes: ἀνα-βιβάζω (causal of ἀναβαίνω), [in LXX chiefly for עָלָה hi., also for רָכַב hi., etc. ;] to make go up, draw up, as a ship (Xen.): σαγήνην, Mat.13:48 (metaphorically, MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anablepo.notes: ἀνα-βλέπω [in LXX chiefly for נָשָׂא ;] __1. to look up: Mrk.8:24, al.; before εἰς, Mat.14:19, al. (Xen., Plat.). __2. to recover sight (Plat., Aristoph.; cf. MM, VGT, see word): Mat.11:5, Jhn.9:11, al (AS)

anablepsis.notes: ἀνά-βλεψις, -εως, ή (ἀναβλέπω), [in LXX: Isa.61:1 (פְּקַח־קוֹחַ)* ;] recovery of sight: Luk.4:18 (Lxx).† (AS)

anaboao.notes: ἀνα-βοάω, -ῶ [in LXX for צָעַק, זָעַק, קָרָא, etc. ;] to cry out: Mat.27:46 (WH, ἐβόησεν; see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anabole.notes: ἀναβολή, - ῆς, ἡ (ά αβάλλω), [in LXX for בּנף, etc. ;] delay: Act.25:17 (for exx. of other meanings, see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anachoreo.notes: ἀνα-χωρέω, -ῶ [in LXX for בָּרַח, נוּם, etc. ;] __1. to go back. __2. to withdraw: Mat.9:24; frequently in sense of avoiding danger (MM, VGT, see word), Mat.2:12 (but see Thayer), Mat.2:13-14, 22 4:12 12:15 14:13…

anachusis.notes: ἀνά-χυσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναχέω, to pour out), a pouring out, overflowing, excess: metaphorically, 1Pe.4:4 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anadechomai.notes: ἀνα-δέχομαι [in LXX: 2Ma.6:19 2Mac 8:36 * ;] __1. to assume, undertake (in π. frequently as legal term: MM, VGT, see word): ἐπαγγελίας, Heb.11:17. __2. = cl. ὑποδέχομαι, to receive: of guests, Act.28:7.† (AS)

anadeiknumi.notes: ἀνα-δείκνυμι [in LXX: Hab.3:2 (יָדַע), Dan LXX 1:11 (מָנָה), Dan.1:20 (מָצָא), 1Esdra.6.2, 3Mac.9 * ;] __1. to lift up and show, show forth, declare (cf. 2Ma.2:8, see MM, VGT, see word): Act.1:24. __2. to consecrate,…

anadeixis.notes: ἀνά-δειξις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναδείκνυμι), [in LXX: Sir.43:6 * ;] a sheaving forth, announcement: Luk.1:80.† (AS)

anadidomi.notes: ἀνα-δίδωμι [in LXX: Sir.1:22, 2Ma.13:15 * ;] __1. to give forth, send up, as of plants (Hdt., al.). __2. to give up, yield, hand over (MM, VGT, see word): Act.23:33.† (AS)

anagennao.notes: ἀνα-γεννάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Sir prol.17 א* (ΑΒאc παρα-)* ;] to beget again: metaphorically, of spiritual birth, 1Pe.1:3 1:23 (cf. Cremer, 147; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anaginosko.notes: ἀνα-γινώσκω (Attic ἀναγιγν-), [in LXX chiefly for קָרָא ;] __1. to know certainly, know again, recognize. __2. Of written characters, to read: Mat.24:15, Mrk.13:14, Act.15:31 23:34, Eph.3:4; with accusative of…

anagkaios.notes: ἀναγκαῖος, -αῖα, -αῖον (ἀνάγκη), [in LXX: Est.8:13, Wis.16:3, Sir prol.22, 2Ma.4:23 2Mac 9:21, 4Ma.1:2 * ;] __1. necessary: Act.13:46, 1Co.12:22, 2Co.9:5, Php.2:25, Tit.3:14, Heb.8:3; comp. -αιότερον, Php.1:24. __2.…

anagkastos.notes: ἀναγκαστῶς adv., necessarily or by constraint: opposite to ἑκουσίως, 1Pe.5:2 (rare).† (AS)

anagkazo.notes: ἀναγκάζω (ἀνάγκη), [in LXX: Pro.6:7 (שֹׁטֵר), I Est.3:24, 1Ma.2:25, al. ;] to necessitate, compel by force or persuasion, constrain: with accusative, 2Co.12:11; id. with inf., Mat.14:22, Mrk.6:45, Luk.14:23,…

anagke.notes: ἀνάγκη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for מָצוֹק, צַר ;] __1. necessity: ἔχειν ἀ-, with inf., to be compelled, Luk.14:18 23:17 (Rec., R, mg.), 1Co.7:37, Ju 3, Heb.7:27; ἐξ ἀ., κατ᾽ ἀ., of necessity, 2Co.9:7, Heb.7:12, Phm…

anagnorizo.notes: ἀνα-γνωρίζω [in LXX: Gen.45:1 (יָדַע hith.)* ;] to recognize: Act.7:13 (WH, txt., ἐγνωρίσθη).† (AS)

anagnosis.notes: ἀνά-γνωσις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX: Neh.8:8 (מִקְרָא), I Est.9:48, Sir prol. 9, 13 * ;] __1. recognition (Hdt.). __2. reading (Plat., al.): of the public reading of Scripture (Milligan, NTD, 173, 210 f.): Act.13:15,…

anago.notes: ἀν-άγω [in LXX chiefly for עָלָה hi. ;] to lead or bring up: before είς, with accusative loc., Mat.4:1, Luk.2:22 4:5 (WH om. είς, κ.τ.λ.), Act.9:39 16:34; of raising the dead (cl.), ἐκ νεκρῶν, Rom.10:7, Heb.13:20; to…

anaideia.notes: ἀναιδία (Rec. -εία, as in cl.), -ας, ἡ (αἰδώς), [in LXX: Sir.25:22 * ;] shamelessness, importunity: Luk.11:8 (for exx. from π., see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anaireo.notes: ἀν-αιρέω, -ῶ [in LXX for הָרַג hi., מוּת hi., נָכָה hi., etc. ;] __1. to take up: mid., Act.7:21. __2. to take away, make an end of, destroy (for late exx. of various senses, see MM, VGT, see word); __(a) of things…

anairesis.notes: ἀν-αίρεσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναιρέω), [in LXX: Num.11:15 (הָרַג), Jdg.15:17 (רָמָה), Jdth.15:4, 2Ma.5:13 * ;] __1. a taking up or away (Thuc.). __2. a destroying, slaying, murder (Field, Notes, 116; MM, VGT, see word):…

anaitios.notes: ἀν-αίτιος, -ον (αἰτία), [in LXX: Deu.19:10, 13 21:8-9 (נָקִי), Dan LXX TH Sus1:62, always of αἷμα (of. MM, VGT, see word)* ;] guiltless, innocent: Mat.12:5, 7.† (AS)

anakainizo.notes: ἀνα-καινίζω (καινός), [in LXX: 2Ch.15:8, Psa.103:5 104:30, La 5:21 (חָדַשׁ pi., hith)., Psa.39:2 (עָכַר ni.), 1Ma.6:9 * ;] to renew: Heb.6:6 (Isocr., Plut.).† (AS)

anakainoo.notes: ἀνα-καινόω, -ῶ = ἀνακαινίζω (cf. MM, VGT, see word), to make new: 2Co.4:16, Col.3:10 (see Cremer, 323).† (AS)

anakainosis.notes: ἀνακαίνωσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀνακαινόω), renewal: Rom.12:2, Tit.3:5 (Cremer, 324; MM, VGT, see word).† SYN.: παλινγενεσία, in NT, new birth, of which ἀ. is the conse­quent renewal or renovation, in which man as well as God…

anakalupto.notes: ἀνα-καλύπτω [in LXX chiefly for גָּלָה ni., pi. ;] to unveil: metaphorically of removing hindrance to perception of spiritual things, 2Co.3:14 3:18.† (AS)

anakampto.notes: ἀνα-κάμπτω [in LXX: 1Ch.19:5, Jer.3:1, al. (שׁוּב), Jer.15:5 (סוּר) ;] __1. trans., to bend or turn back. __2. Intrans., to return: Mat.2:12, Act.18:21, Heb.11:15; metaphorically (cf. MM, VGT, see word), Luk.10:6.† (AS)

anakathizo.notes: ἀνα-καθ-ίζω (see: καθίζω); __1. trans., to set up. __2. Intrans., to sit up: Luk.7:15 (WH, mg., ἐκάθισεν), Act.9:40 (frequently in medical writings: MM, VGT, see word). † (AS)

anakeimai.notes: ἀνά-κειμαι [in LXX: I Est.4:10, Tob.9:6 א * ;] __1. in cl., as pass. of ἀνατίθημ, to be laid up, laid: Mrk.5:40 Rec. __2. In late writers (cf. MM, VGT, see word) = κεῖσθαι, κατακεῖσθαι, to recline at table:…

anakephalaioomai.notes: ἀνα-κεφαλαιόω, ῶ (see: κεφαλαιόω) [in Th., Al.: Psa.72:20 * ;] to sum up, gather up, present as a whole: mid., Rom.13:9, Eph.1:10 (on WH. see Lft., Notes, 321 L; AR, in l; Cremer, 354, 748).† (AS)

anaklino.notes: ἀνα-κλίνω [in LXX: 3Ma.5:16 * ;] to lay upon, lean against, hence, __(a) to lay down: Luk.2:7; __(b) to make to recline: Mrk.6:39, WH, mg., Luk.12:37. Pass., to lie back, recline: Mat.8:11 14:19, Luk.13:29.† SYN.:…

anakrazo.notes: ἀνα-κράζω [in LXX for קָרָא, etc. ;] to cry out, shout: Mrk.1:23 6:49, Luk.4:33 8:28 23:18.† (AS)

anakrino.notes: ἀνα-κρίνω [in LXX: 1Ki.20:12 (חָקַר), Dan LXX Su 1:13, LXX, TH Su 1:48, 51 * ;] to examine, investigate, question (Lft., Notes, 181 f.): Act.17:11, 1Co.2:14-15 4:3-4 9:3 10:25 10:27 14:24; in forensic sense (MM, VGT,…

anakrisis.notes: ἀνά-κρισις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX: 3Ma.7:5 * ;] an examination: spec. of legal preliminary investigation, Act.25:26 (see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anakupto.notes: άνα-κύπτω [in LXX: Job.10:15 (נָשָׂא רֹאשׁ), Dan LXX, Su 1:35 * ;] to lift oneself up; __(a) bodily; Luk.13:11, Jhn.8:7, 10; __(b) mentally, to be elated: Luk.21:28 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

analambano.notes: ἀνα-λαμβάνω [in LXX chiefly for נָשָׂא, also for לָקַח, etc. ;] __1. to take up, raise: Mrk.16:19, Act.1:2, 11 1:22 10:16, 1Ti.3:16. __2. to take up, take to oneself: Act.7:43 20:13-14 23:31, Eph.6:13, 16, 2Ti.4:11…

analepsis.notes: ἀνά-λημψις, -εως, ἡ (κοινή form of ἀνάληψις; see Th., Gr., 108 f.), a taking up: Luk.9:51 (MM, VGT, see word).† ἀνά-ληψις, -εως, ἡ, Rec. for ἀνάλημψις, which see (AS)

analisko.notes: ἀν-αλίσκω (on the etymology, see MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX chiefly for אָכַל also for כָּלָה, etc. ;] __1. to expend. __2. to consume, destroy: Luk.9:54, Gal.5:15, 2Th.2:8, Rec. WH, mg.† (AS)

analogia.notes: ἀναλογία, -ας, ἡ (λόγος), [in Al.: Lev.27:1-34"18 * ;] proportion (MM, VGT, see word): Rom.12:6 (cf. Cremer, 397).† (AS)

analogizomai.notes: ἀνα-λογίζομαι [in LXX: Wis.17:13 א, 2Ma.12:43 A, 3Ma.7:7 * ;] to consider: Heb.12:3 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

analos.notes: ἄναλος, -ον (ἅλς), [in Aq.: Eze.13:10-11, 15 22:28 * ;] saltless, insipid: Mrk.9:50.† (AS)

analuo.notes: ἀνα-λύω [in LXX: I Est.3:3, Tob.2:9, Jdth.13:1, Sir.3:15, Wis.3:1-19 2:1-24,3 Mac.10 * ;] __1. to unloose. __2. to unloose for departure, depart (MM, VGT, see word): from life, Php.1:23 __3. to return, Luk.12:36.† (AS)

analusis.notes: ἀνά-λυσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναλύω), a loosing, e.g. of a vessel from its moorings, hence, departure: from life, 2Ti.4:6.† (AS)

anamartetos.notes: ἀναμάρτητος, -ον (ἁμαρτεῖν), [in LXX: Deu.29:19 (18) 2Ma.8:4 2Mac 12:42 * ;] __1. without missing, unerring (Xen.). __2. In moral sense, faultless (Plat.), without sin: Jhn.8:7 (see Cremer, 102, 634; MM, VGT, see…

anameno.notes: ἀνα-μένω [in LXX for קָוָה pi. ;] to await "one whose coming is expected, perhaps with the added idea of patience and confidence" with accusative, 1Th.1:10 (see M, Th., in l; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anamimnesko.notes: ἀνα-μιμνήσκω [in LXX for זָכַר hi. ;] to remind, call to one's remembrance: with accusative of thing(s), 1Co.4:17; with inf., 2Ti.1:6. Pass., to remember, call to mind: Mrk.11:21 14:72, 2Co.7:15, Heb.10:32.† (AS)

anamnesis.notes: ἀνάμνησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναμιμνήσκω), [in LXX: Psa.38:1-22 70:1-5 title. (זָכַר hi.), Lev.24:7 (אַזְכָּרָה), Num.10:10 (זִכְרוֹן), Wis.16:6 * ;] remembrance: εἰς τ. ἐμὴν ἀ., Luk.22:19 (WH om.), 1Co.11:24-25; ἀ. ἁμαρτιῶν,…

ananeoomai.notes: ἀνα-νεόω, -ῶ (νέος), [in LXX: Job.33:24, Est.3:13, 1; Ezr.3.1-13, 4Ma.8:1-29 * ;] to renew: pass., Eph.4:23 (see Cremer, 428; MM, VGT, see word)† (AS)

ananepho.notes: ἀνα-νήφω to return to soberness: metaphorically, 2Ti.2:26 (of. ἐκνήφω).† (AS)

anangello.notes: ἀν-αγγέλλω [in LXX chiefly for נָגַד hi ;] __1. to bring back word, report (Æsch., Thuc., al.): Jhn.5:15 (WH, εἶπεν), Act.14:27 15:4, 2Co.7:7 __2. Later, = ἀπαγγέλλω (MM, VGT, see word), to announce, declare (LXX;…

ananias.notes: Ἀνανίας (WH, Ἁναν-), -α, ὁ (Heb. חֲנַנְיָה), Ananias; __1. of Jerusalem: Act.5:1, 3 5:5. __2. Of Damascus: Act.9:10-13, 17 22:12. __3. High Priest: Act.23:2 24:1.† (AS)

anantirretos.notes: ἀν-αντί-ρητος (Τ, -ρρητος, -ον (ῥητός, spoken), [in Sm.: Job.11:2 33:13 * ;] not to be contradicted, undeniable: Act.19:36 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anapauo.notes: ἀνα-παύω, [in LXX for fourteen different words, chiefly נוּחַ, also רָבַץ, שָׁאַן, etc. ;] to give intermission from labour, to give rest, refresh: Mat.11:28, 1Co.16:18, Phm 20; pass., Phm 7, 2Co.7:13. Mid., to take…

anapausis.notes: ἀνά-παυσις, -εως, ή (ἀνάπαύω) [in LXX chiefly for נוּחַ and its derivatives, שַׁבָּת and its cognates (Ex, Le) ;] cessation, rest, refreshment: Mat.11:26 12:43, Luk.11:24, Rev.4:8 14:11.† SYN.: ἄνεσις (lit, the…

anapeitho.notes: ἀνα-πείθω [in LXX: Jer.29:8 (נָשָׁא hi.), 1Ma.1:11 * ;] to per­suade, incite: Act.18:13 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anapempo.notes: ἀνα-πέμπω __1. to send up, __(a) to a higher place (Æsch., Plat., al.); __(b) to a higher authority (Deiss., BS, 229; MM, VGT, see word; cf. also Field, Notes, 140): Luk.23:7, 15, Act.25:21. __2. to send back…

anaperos.notes: ἀνά-πηρος (WH, -ειρος; see Field, Notes, 67), -ον (πηρός, maimed) [in LXX: Tob.14:2 א, 2Ma.8:24 * ;] maimed, crippled: Luk.14:13, 21.† (AS)

anaphaino.notes: ἀνα-φαίνομαι [in LXX for צָדַק hi., גָּלַשׁ ;] to bring to light, make to appear: ἀναφάναντες τ. Κύπρον, i.e. having sighted C.: Act.21:3 WH; pass., to appear, be made manifest: Luk.19:11 (AS)

anaphero.notes: ἀνα-φέρω [in LXX chiefly for עָלָה hi., also for קָטַר hi., etc. ;] __1. to carry or lead up: with accusative of person(s), Mat.17:1, Mrk.9:2; pass., Luk.24:51 (WH, rejects, R, mg. omits); ἀ. τ. ἁμαρτίας ἐπί τ. ξύλον…

anaphoneo.notes: ἀνα-φωνέω, -ῶ [in LXX for שָׁמַע hi., זָכַר hi. ;] to cry out, exclaim: Luk.1:42 (Arist., al.).† (AS)

anapipto.notes: ἀνα-πίπτω [in LXX: Gen.49:9 (כָּרַע) Tob.2:1 7:8, Jdth.12:10, Sir.25:18 32:2, Da TH Sus 1:37 * ;] __1. (cl.) to fall back. __2. In late writers = ἀνα-κλίνομαι, to recline for a repast (MM, VGT, see word): at table,…

anapleroo.notes: ἀνα-πληρόω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for מָלֵא, Lev.12:6, al.; also שָׁלַם (Gen.15:16, 3Ki.7:51, Isa.60:20), etc. ;] __1. to fill up, make full (in π. of com­pleting contracts and making up rent; cf. MM, VGT, see word):…

anapologetos.notes: ἀναπολόγητος, -ον (ἀπολογεόμαι), without excuse, inexcusable (in Polyb., al., as a forensic term; see Lft., Notes, 252): Rom.1:20 2:1.† (AS)

anapsucho.notes: ἀνα-ψύχω [in LXX for נָפַשׁ ni., חָיָה, etc. (frequently in sense of revive, refresh oneself) ;] to refresh: with accusative of person(s), 2Ti.1:16 (MM, VGT, see word; Cremer, 588).† (AS)

anapsuxis.notes: ἀνά-ψυξις, -εως, ἡ (ἀναψύχω), [in LXX: Exo.8:15 (רְוָחָה)* ;] a refreshing: Act.3:19.† (AS)

anapto.notes: ἀν-άπτω [in LXX chiefly for יָצָא ;] to kindle: Luk.12:49, Jas.3:5 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anaptusso.notes: ἀνα-πτύσσω [in LXX for פָּרַשׂ,etc. ;] to unroll: τ. βιβλίον, Luk.4:17 (WH, R, ἀνοίξας).† (AS)

anaseio.notes: ἀνα-σείω [in Aq.: 1Ki.26:19, Job.2:3; Aq., Sm.: Isa.36:18 * ;] __1. to shake out, shake back, move to and fro (Thuc., a1.). __2. In late writers (Diod., al.; see MM, VGT, see word), to stir up; metaphorically, to…

anaskeuazo.notes: ἀνα-σκευάζω (σκεῦος, a vessel), prop, to pack up baggage, hence, to dismantle, ravage, destroy; metaphorically, to unsettle, subvert (MM, VGT, see word): ψυχάς, Act.15:24.† (AS)

anaspao.notes: ἀνα-σπάω, -ῶ [in LXX for לָקַח, עָלָה hi. ;] to draw up: Luk.14:5, Act.11:10 (in π. of pulling up barley; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anastasis.notes: ἀνά-στασις, -εως, ή (ἀνίστημι), [in LXX: Zep.3:8 (קוּם), La 3:63 (קִימָה), קִימָה), Psa.66:1-20 title., Dan LXX 11:20, 2Ma.7:14 2Mac 12:43 * ;] __1. a raising up, awakening, rising (in Inscr. of the erection of a…

anastatoo.notes: ἀνα-στατόω, ῶ (ἀνάστατος, driven from home; ἀνίστημι), [in LXX: Dan.7:23 (דּוּשׁ;* also in Aq., and in π. (see Deiss., LAE, 80 f.; MM, VGT, see word),] to stir up, excite, unsettle: with accusative; __(a) to tumult…

anastauroo.notes: ἀνα-σταυρόω __1. to impale (Hdt.). __2. to raise on a cross, crucify (Polyb., al.). __3. to crucify again: Heb.6:6 (see Westc., in l).† (AS)

anastenazo.notes: ἀνα-στενάζω [in LXX: La 1:4 (אָנַח ni.), Sir.25:17), Da TH Sus 1:22, 2Ma.6:30 * ;] to sigh deeply: Mrk.8:12.† (AS)

anastrepho.notes: ἀνα-στρέφω [in LXX chiefly for שׁוּב ;] __1. to overturn: Jhn.2:15. __2. to turn back, return: Act.5:22 15:16. __3. to turn hither and thither; pass., to turn oneself about, sojourn, dwell: Mat.17:22 Rec.;…

anastrophe.notes: ἀνα-στροφή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀναστρέφομαι) [in LXX: Tob.4:14, 2Ma.5:8 2Mac 6:23 * ;] __1. a turning down or back, a wheeling about (Soph., Thuc., al.). __2. In late writers (Polyb., al.; see: ἀναστρέφω, and cf. Hort on…

anatasso.notes: ἀνα-τάσσομαι [in LXX only as seel. (Ald.) in Ecc.2:20 ;] to arrange in order, bring together from memory (Blass., Phil. Gosp., 14 ff.; MM, VGT, see word): Luk.1:1.† (AS)

anatello.notes: ἀνα-τέλλω [in LXX for צָמַח, פָּרַח, זָרַח, etc. ;] __1. trans., to cause to rise: Mat.5:45. __2. Intrans., to rise: φῶς, Mat.4:16 (= Isa.9:1); ὁ ἥλιος, Mat.13:6, Mrk.4:6 16:2, Jas.1:11; νεφέλη, Luk.12:54; φωσφόρος,…

anathallo.notes: ἀνα-θάλλω (θάλλω, to flourish), [in LXX: Psa.28:7 (עָלַז), Eze.17:24 (פָּרַח hi.), Hos.8:9, Wis.4:4, Sir.5:1-15 * ;] to revive: Php.4:10 (of MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anathema.notes: ἀνάθεμα, -τος, τό (ἀνατίθημι), Hellenistic for Attic ἀνάθημα (Bl., § 27, 2); __1. prop. = τὸ ἀνατιθέμένον, that which is laid by to be kept, a votive offering (as ἀνάθημα in 2Ma.2:13, Luk.21:5—where LT read -θεμα,…

anathematizo.notes: ἀνα-θεματίζω (ἀνίθεμα), [in LXX chiefly for חָרַם hi. (Num.21:2, 1Ki.15:3, al.), 1Ma.5:5 ;] to devote to destruction, declare or invoke ana­thema: absol., Mrk.14:71; ἐαντόν, to bind oneself under a curse: Act.23:12,…

anatheoreo.notes: ἀνα-θεωρέω, -ῶ to observe carefully, consider well: Act.17:23, Heb.13:7 (Diod., al.).† (AS)

anatithemi.notes: ἀνα-τίθημι [in LXX chiefly for חָרַם (Cremer, 546) ;] to lay upon, set up, etc. Mid. -εμαι, in late writers (Plut., al.; see also MM, VGT, see word), to set forth, declare: Act.25:14, Gal.2:2.† (AS)

anatole.notes: ἀνατολή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀνατέλλω), [in LXX chiefly for מִזְרָח קָדִים ;] __1. a rising: of light, Luk.1:78. __2. the sun-rising, the east (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.2:2, 9, Rev.21:13; ἀ. ἡλίου, Rev.7:2 16:12 (WΗ, p1.); pl.,…

anatrepho.notes: ἀνα-τρέφω [in LXX: Wis.7:4 B, 4Ma.10:2 4Mac 11:15 א* ;] to nurse up, nourish, educate, bring up: Luk.4:16, WΗ, mg., Act.7:20-21 22:3.† (AS)

anatrepo.notes: ἀνα-τρέπω [in LXX for דָּחָה, הָדַף, etc. ;] to overturn, destroy: Jhn.2:15 WH, txt.; metaphorically, to subvert (MM, VGT, see word): 2Ti.2:18, Tit.1:11.† (AS)

anaxios.notes: ἀν-άξιος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ἄξιος), [in LXX: Jer.15:19 א2 (זָלַל), Est.8:13, Sir.25:8 * ;] unworthy: with genitive, 1Co.6:2 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anazao.notes: ἀνα-ζάω, -ῶ [in Al.: Gen.45:27 * ;] to live again, regain life (cf. cl. ἀναβιόω; Cremer, 722; and for other exx., v MM, VGT, see word): metaphorically of moral revival, Luk.15:24 (WH, mg., ἔζησεν); of sin, Rom.7:9.† (AS)

anazeteo.notes: ἀνα-ζητέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Job.3:4 (דָּרַשׁ), Job.10:6 (בָּקַשׁ pi.), 2Ma.13:21 * ;] to look for or seek carefully ("specially of searching for human beings, with an implication of difficulty": MM, VGT, see word):…

anazonnumi.notes: ἀνα-ζώννυμι [in LXX: Jdg.18:16, Pro.31:17 (חָגַר)* ;] to gird up: figuratively, τ. ὀσφύας τ. διανοίας, 1Pe.1:13.† (AS)

anazopureo.notes: ἀνα-ζωπυργέω, -ῶ (ζωός, πῦρ), [in LXX: 1Ma.13:7 * ;] to kindle afresh: metaphorically, 2Ti.1:6 (for vernac. exx., see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

andrapodistes.notes: ἀνδραποδιστής, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀνδράποδον, a slave, captured in war) a slave-dealer, kidnapper: 1Ti.1:10 (see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

andreas.notes: Ἀνδρέας, -ου, ὁ Andrew, the Apostle: Mat.4:18 10:2, Mrk.1:16, 29 3:18 13:3, Luk.6:14, Jhn.1:41, 45 6:8 12:22, Act.1:13.† (AS)

andrizo.notes: ἀνδρίζω [in LXX for חָזַק אָמַץ (Jos.1:6 ff., 1Ch.22:19, al.; in 2Ki.10:12, Psa.27:14 31:24 combined with κρατιοῦσθαι, as in 1Co, l.with) ;] to make a man of. Mid., to play the man (cf. MM, VGT, see word):…

androphonos.notes: ἀνδρο-φόνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: 2Ma.9:28 * ;] a man-slayer: 1Ti.1:9 (cf. φονεύς, and see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anecho.notes: ἀν-έχω [in LXX chiefly for אָפַק hithp. ;] to hold up; in NT always mid., to bear with, endure: in cl. most frequently with accusative, but in NT with genitive of person(s), Mat.17:17, Mrk.9:19, Luk.9:41, 2Co.11:1…

anegkletos.notes: ἀν-έγκλητος, -ον (ἀ-, ἐγκαλέω), [in LXX: 3Ma.5:31 * ;] not to be called to account, unreprovable: 1Co.1:8, Col.1:22, 1Ti.3:10, Tit.1:6-7.† SYN.: ἄμεμπτος, ἀνεπίλημπτος (see Tr., Syn., § ciii; Cremer, 742; MM, VGT,…

anekdiegetos.notes: ἀν-εκδιήγητος, -ον (ἀ-, ἐκδιηγέομαι) inexpressible: 2Co.9:15 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anekleiptos.notes: ἀνέκλειπτος, -ον (ἀ-, ἐκλείπω), unfailing: Luk.12:33 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

aneko.notes: ἀν-ήκω (ἀνά, ἥκω), [in LXX: Jos.23:14 (בּוֹא), 1Ki.27:8, Sir. prol.10, 1, 2 Mac6* ;] prop., to have come up to; in later writers, impers. it is due, it is befitting: in ethical sense (MM, VGT, see word), Eph.5:4,…

anektos.notes: αν-εκτός, -όν (also in late Gk. -ή, -όν; ἀνέχομαι), tolerable: compar., -ότερος, Mat.10:15 11:22, 24, Luk.10:12, 14 † (AS)

aneleemon.notes: ἀν-ελεήμων, -ον (-ἀ, ἐλεήμων), [in LXX for אַכְזָר ;] without mercy: Rom.1:13.† (AS)

anemeros.notes: ἀν-ήμερος, -ον (ἀ-, ἥμερο ), not tame, savage (MM, VGT, see word): 2Ti.3:3.† (AS)

anemizomai.notes: ἀνεμίζω = Attic ἀνεμόω (ἄνεμος); pass., to be driven by the wind: Jas.1:6.† (AS)

anendektos.notes: ἀν-ένδεκτος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ἔνδεκτος; ἐνδέχομαι), impossible, inadmissible: Luk.17:1.† (AS)

anepaischuntos.notes: ἀν-επ-αίσχυντος, -ον (ἐπαισχύνομαι) not to be put to shame: 2Ti.2:15.† (AS)

anepileptos.notes: ἀν-επί-λημπτος (Rec. -λμπτος; Bl., § 6, 8), -ον (ἀ- ἐπιλαμβάνω) without reproach: 1Ti.3:2 5:7 6:14.† SYN.: ἀμεμπτος, ἀνέγκλητος. It is stronger than these, for it implies not only that the man is of good report, but…

anepsios.notes: ἀνεψιός, -οῦ, ὁ (cf. Lat. nepos), [in LXX: Num.36:11 ( בֵּן דּוֹד), Tob.7:2 9:6 א * ;] a cousin: Col.4:10 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

aner.notes: ἀνήρ, ἀνδρός, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for אִישׁ, frequently אֱנוֹשׁ, also אָדָם, etc. ;] a man, Lat. vir. __1. As opposite to a woman, Act.8:12, 1Ti.2:12; as a husband, Mat.1:16, Jhn.4:16, Rom.7:2, Tit.1:6. __2. As…

anerchomai.notes: ἀν-έρχομαι [in LXX: 3Ki.13:12 (הָלַךְ)* ;] to go up: Jhn.6:3, Gal.1:17-18 (cf. ἐπανέρχ-; and on its use of "going up " to the capital, MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anesis.notes: ἄνεσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀνίημι), [in LXX: 2Es.4.22 (שָׁלוּ), 2Ch.23:15, I Est.4:62, Wis.13:13, Sir.15:20 26:10 * ;] a loosening, relaxation: Act.24:23 (RV, indulgence; cf. MM, VGT, see word); by St. Paul, opposite to…

anetazo.notes: ἀν-ετάζω (ἀνά, ἐτάζω, to examine; see MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX: Jdg.6:29 (דָּרַשׁ), Est.2:23 (בָּקַשׁ), Da TH Su 1:14 * ;] to examine judicially: Act.22:24, 29..† (AS)

anethon.notes: anise, dill , (Aristophanes Comicus); ionic ἄννησον or ἄνησον (Herdotus Historicus); aeolic ἄννητον, or ἄνητον, (Sapho). (ML)

aneurisko.notes: ἀν-ευρίσκω (ἀνά, εὑρίσκω), [in LXX: 4Ma.3:14 * ;] to find out by search, discover (see Field, Notes, 47 f.): Luk.2:16, Act.21:4.† (AS)

aneuthetos.notes: ἀν-εύ-θετος, -ον (see MM, VGT, see word) not well placed, not fit: Act.27:12.† (AS)

anexereunetos.notes: ἀνεξεραύνητος (Rec. -εύνητος, as in Attic; M, Pr., 46), -ον (ερευνάω), [in Sm. (-ευ-): Pr., 25:3 * ;] unsearchable: Rom.11:33.† (AS)

anexichniastos.notes: ἀνεξιχνίαστος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ἐξιχνιάζω, to track out; ἴχνος) [in LXX: Job.5:9 9:10 34:24 (חֵקֶר אַיִן)* ;] that cannot be traced out: Rom.11:33, Eph.3:8 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anexikakos.notes: ἀνεξί-κακος, -ον (fut., ἀνέξομαι, κακός) patiently forbearing (cf. ἀνεξικακία, Wis.2:19; and see MM, VGT, see word): 2Ti.2:24.† (AS)

angareuo.notes: ἀγγαρεύω (from the Persian; cf. Vg. angiare, and the Heb. אִגֶרֶת; on the orthogr., see Bl., §6, 1; M, Pr., 46), to impress into public service, employ a courier; hence, to compel to perform a service (prob. common…

angeion.notes: ἀγγεῖον, -ου, τό (ἄγγος), [in LXX chiefly for כְּלִי ;] a vessel (see MM, VGT, see word): Mat.25:4.† (AS)

angelia.notes: ἀγγελία, -ας, ἡ (ἄγγελος), [in LXX chiefly for שְׁמוּעָה ;] a message: 1Jo.1:5, 3:11 (Cremer, 18; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

angelos.notes: ἄγγελος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for מַלְאָךְ ;] __1. a messenger, one sent: Mat.11:10, Jas.22:25. __2. As in LXX, in the special sense of angel, a spiritual, heavenly being, attendant upon God and employed as his…

aniemi.notes: ἀν-ίημι (ἀνά, ἵημι), [in LXX for רָפָה, נָשָׂא, etc. ;] __1. to send up, produce, to send back. __2. to let go, leave without support: Heb.13:5 (cf. Deu.31:6; Hom., Iliad., ii, 71). __3. to relax, loosen (see Field,…

anileos.notes: ἀν-έλεος, -ον (Attic ἀνηλεής, ἀνελεήμων; MM, VGT, see word), merciless: Jas.2:13.† (AS)

aniptos.notes: ἄνιπτος, -ον (ἀ. neg., νίπτω), unwashed: Mat.15:20, Mrk.7:5 (5 Rec).† (AS)

anistemi.notes: ἀνίστημι (ἀνά, ἵστημι), [in LXX chiefly for קוּם ;] __1. causal, in fut. and 1 aor. act., with accusative, to raise up: Act.9:41; from death, Jhn.6:39, Act.2:32; to raise up, cause to be born or appear: Mat.22:24,…

anna.notes: Ἄννα, -ας, ἡ (Heb. חָנָה), Anna, a prophetess: Luk.2:36.† (AS)

annas.notes: Ἄννας, -α (FlJ, Ἄνανος, -ου), ὁ (Heb. חָנַן), Annas, the high priest: Luk.3:2, Jhn.18:13-24, Act.4:6.† (AS)

ano.notes: ἄνω adv (ἀνά), __(a) up, upwards: Jhn.11:41, Heb.12:15; __(b) above (opposite to κάτω): Act_2:19; with art. ἡ, Gal.4:26, Php.3:14; τά, Jhn.8:23, Col.3:1-2; ἕως ἄ. (up to the brim), Jhn.2:7 (Cremer, 106; MM, VGT, see…

anoche.notes: ἀνοχή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀνέχω, -ομαι), [in LXX: 1Ma.12:25 (RV, respite)* ;] __1. in cl., a holding back, delaying (MM, VGT, see word). __2. forbearance, delay of punishment: Rom.2:4 3:26.† SYN.: μακροθυμία, ὑπομονή. ἀ.,…

anoetos.notes: ἀ-νόητος, -ον (ἀ- neg., νοητός; νοέω), [in LXX: Pro.17:28 (אֱוִיל), Sir.42:8, al. ;] __1. not thought on, not understood (Hom., Plat.). __2. not understanding, foolish (Hdt., al., LXX): Luk.24:25, Rom.1:14, Gal.3:1,…

anogeon.notes: ἀνάγαιον (Rec. ἀνώγεον; on the form, see Rutherford, NPhr., 357 f.; MM, VGT, see word), -ου, τό (ἀνά, γῆ), an upper room: Mrk.14:15, Luk.22:12.† SYN.: ὑπερῷον (AS)

anoia.notes: ἀνοια, -ας, ἡ (ἄ-νοος, without understanding), [in LXX: Pro.14:8, 2215 (אִוֶּלֶת), Wis.15:18, al. ;] folly, foolishness: 2Ti.3:9; expressed in violent rage (cf. Plat., Tim., 86B): Luk.6:11.† (AS)

anoigo.notes: ἀν-οίγω (ἀνά, οἴγω = οἴγνυμι) [in LXX chiefly for פָּתַח ;] to open; __1. trans., with accusative; a door or gate, Act.5:19 12:14, Rev.4:1; pass., Act.12:10 16:26-27; metaphorically of opportunity or welcome,…

anoikodomeo.notes: ἀν-οικο-δομέω, -ῶ [in LXX for בָּנָה, גָּדַר ;] to build. again, rebuild (MM, VGT, see word): Act.15:16.† (AS)

anoixis.notes: ἄνοιξις, -εως, ἡ (ἀνοίγω), an opening (in MGr., springtime): ἐν ἀ., as often as I open, Eph.6:19.† (AS)

anomia.notes: ἀνομία, -ας, ἡ (ἄνομος), [in LXX for עָוֺן, פָּשַׁע, פֶּשַׁע, תּוֹעֵבַה, רֶשַׁע, etc. ;] lawlessness, iniquity: Mat.7:23 13:41 23:28 24:12, Rom.6:19, 2Co.6:14, 2Th.2:3 2:7, Tit.2:14, Heb.1:9, 1Jn.3:4; in pl. (as LXX,…

anomos.notes: ἄ-νομος, -ον (ἀ. neg., νόμος), [in LXX for עָוֺן, פָּשַׁע, רֶשַׁע, etc. ;] __1. lawless, wicked: Mrk.15:28, Luk.22:37, Act.2:23, 1Ti.1:9, 2Pe.2:8; ὁ ἄ., 2Th.2:8 (= ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῆς ἀνομίας, 2Th.2:3). __2. without law…

anopheles.notes: ἀν-ωφελής, -ές (ἀ- neg., ὄφελος), [in LXX: Isa.44:10 (יָעַל בִּלְתִּי) Jer.2:8 (יָעַל לֹא), Pro.28:3, Wis.1:11 * ;] unprofitable: Tit.3:9; neut. as subst., unprofitableness: Heb.7:18.† (AS)

anorthoo.notes: ἀν-ορθόω, -ῶ (ἀνά, ὀρθόω, to set straight, set up), [in LXX chiefly for כּוּן hi. ;] to set upright or straight again, restore: of persons, Luk.13:13, Heb.12:12; of things, σκηνήν, Act.15:16 (MM, VGT, see word;…

anosios.notes: ἀν-όσιος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ὅσιος), [in LXX: Eze.22:9 (זִמָּה, Wis.12:4, 2Ma.7:34 2Mac 8:32, 3Ma.2:2 3Mac 5:7, 4Ma.12:11 * ;] unholy, profane (Cremer, 464): 1Ti.1:9, 2Ti.3:2 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anoteros.notes: ἀνώτερος, α, -ον [in LXX: Neh.3:25 (עֶלְיוֹן), Eze.41:7 (מַעַל), Tob.8:3 * ;] only in neut., as adv. (cf. ἐξώτερος), __(a) of motion, higher: Luk.14:10; __(b) of rest, above, before: Heb.10:8.† (AS)

anothen.notes: ἄνωθεν adv (ἄνω), __(a) from above: ἀπὸ ἄ., Mat.27:51, Mrk.15:38; ἐκ τῶν ἄ., Jhn.19:28; meaning, from heaven: Jhn.3:31 19:11, Jas.1:17 3:15, 17 . __(b) from the first, from the beginning: Luk.1:3, Act.26:5; whence…

antagonizomai.notes: ἀντ-αγωνίζομαι depon., [in LXX: 4Ma.17:14 * ;] to struggle against: before πρός, with accusative, Heb.12:4.† (AS)

antallagma.notes: ἀντ-άλλαγμα, -τος, τό (ἀντί, ἄλλαγμα; ἀλλάσσω) [in LXX chiefly for מְחִיר ;] an exchange, the price received as an equivalent for an article of commerce: Mat.16:26, Mrk.8:37 (cf. Sir.26:14; and see Swete, Mk.,…

antanapleroo.notes: ἀντ-ανα-πληρόω, -ῶ (ἀντί, ἀναπληρόω), to fill up in turn: Col.1:24 (see Lft., in l; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

antapodidomi.notes: ἀντ-απο-δίδωμι (ἀντί ἀποδίδωμι), [in LXX for שָׁלַם pi., גָּמַל, שׁוּב hi., etc. ;] to give back as an equivalent, recompense, requital (the ἀντί ex­pressing the idea of full, complete return; see Lft., Notes, 46);…

antapodoma.notes: ἀντ-από-δομα, -τος, τό (ἀνταποδίδωμι) [in LXX chiefly for גְּמוּל ;] ( = cl. -δοσις, which see) requital; __(a) in favourable sense: Luk.14:12; __(b) in unfavourable sense: Rom.11:9.† (AS)

antapodosis.notes: ἀντ-από-δοσις, -εως, ἡ (see supr.), [in LXX chiefly for גְּמוּל, שִׁלּוּם ;] recompense: Col.3:24 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

antapokrinomai.notes: ἀντ-απο-κρίνομαι (ἀντί, ἀποκρίνω), [in LXX: Jdg.5:29, Job.16:9 (8) Job.32:12 (עָנָה)* ;] to answer again, reply against: before πρός, with accusative of thing(s), Luk.14:6; with dative of person(s), Rom.9:20.† (AS)

antecho.notes: ἀντ-ὲχω (ἀντί, ἔχω), [in LXX for חָזַק hi., etc. ;] __1. trans., to hold against. __2. Intrans., to withstand. Mid. 1. in cl., to hold out against. __3. (cf. MM, VGT, see word), to hold firmly to, cleave to: with…

anthistemi.notes: ἀνθ-ίοτημι (ἀντί, ἵστημι), [in LXX for עָמַד, יָצַב, etc. ;] __1. in pres., impf., fut. and 1 aor. act., causal, to set against. __2. In mid, and pass., also pf. and 2 aor. act., to withstand, resist, oppose: with…

anthomologeomai.notes: ἀνθ-ομολογέομαι, -οῦμαι (ἀντί, ὁμολογέομαι) [in LXX: Psa.79:13 (יָדָה), Dan LXX 4:34 (שְׁבַח), I Est.8:91, Sir.20:2, 3Ma.6:33 * ;] __1. to make a mutual agreement (Dem., Polyb.). __2. to acknowledge fully, confess…

anthos.notes: ἄνθος, -εος, τό [in LXX for צִיץ, etc. ;] a flower: Jas.1:10-11. 1Pe.1:24" (LXX) .† (AS)

anthrakia.notes: ἀνθρακιά, -ᾶς, ἡ (ἄνθραξ), [in LXX: Sir.11:32, 4Ma.9:20 * ;] a heap of burning coals: Jhn.18:18 21:9.† (AS)

anthrax.notes: ἄνθραξ, -ακος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for גֶּחֶל ;] coal, charcoal: . ἄ. πυρός, a burning coal, Rom.12:20.† (AS)

anthropareskos.notes: ἀνθρωπ-άρεσκος, -ον (ἄνθρωπος, ἄρεσκος, pleasing), [in LXX: Psa.53:5 * ;] studying to please men: Eph.6:6, Col.3:22 (Cremer, 642; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anthropinos.notes: ἀνθρώπινος, -η, ον (ἄνθρωπος), [in LXX for אָדָם, אֱנוֹשׁ ;] human, belonging to man: χεῖρες, Act.17:25; σοφία, 1Co.2:13; φύσις, Jas.3:7; κτίσις, 1Pe.2:13 (MM, VGT, see word); ἀ. ἡμέρα, opposite to ἡ ἡμ. (1Co.3:13,…

anthropoktonos.notes: ἀνθρωποκτόνος, -ον (κτείνω, to kill) a murderer, manslayer (Eur.; see MM, VGT, see word): Jhn.8:44, 1Jn.3:16.† SYN.: φονεύς, ἀνδροφόνος (see Tr., Syn, § 83) (AS)

anthropos.notes: ἄνθρωπος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for אִישׁ ,אָדָם, also for אֱנוֹשׁ, etc. ;] man: __1. generically, a human being, male or female (Lat. homo): Jhn.16:21; with art., Mat.4:4 12:35, Mrk.2:27, Jhn.2:25, Rom.7:1, al;…

anthupatos.notes: ἀνθύπατος -ου, ὁ (ἀντί, ὕπατος, altern. for ὑπέρτατος), supreme, a consul, one acting in place of a consul, a proconsul, the administrator of a senatorial province (cf. ἡγεμών, and see MM, VGT, 44): Act.13:7-8, 12…

antiballo.notes: ἀντι-βάλλω [in LXX: 2Ma.11:13 * ;] to throw in turn, exchange: metaphorically, λόγους (cf. Lat. conferre sermons; see Field, Notes, 81), Luk.24:17.† (AS)

antichristos.notes: άντι-χριατος, -ον, ό Antichrist, "one who assuming the guise of Christ opposes Christ" (Westc., Epp. Jo., 70): 1Jn.2:18 Jn 2:22 Jn 4:3, 1Jn.1:7 pl. 1Jn.2:18 (cf. ψευδόχριστος, and see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

antidiatithemi.notes: ἀντι-δια-τίθημι in mid, to place oneself in opposition, oppose: 2Ti.2:25 (EV; but see Field, Notes, 215 f.; cf. MM, VGT, see word) (AS)

antidikos.notes: ἀντίδικος, -ον (δίκη), [in LXX for רִיב ;] as subst., an opponent in a lawsuit, adversary: Mat.5:25, Luk.12:58 18:3, 1Pe.5:8 (Cremer, 696; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

antikathistemi.notes: ἀντι-καθ-ίστημι [in LXX: Deu.31:21 (עָנָה), Jos.5:7, Mic.2:8 (קוּם) * ;] __1. causal in Pres. imperfect fut. and 1 aor.; to replace, oppose. __2. Intrans. in pass. and 2 aor. act.; __(a) to supersede; __(b) to…

antikeimai.notes: ἀντί-κειμαι [in LXX for אָיַב, צוּר, שָׂטַן, etc. ;] __1. to lie opposite to. __2. to oppose, withstand, resist: with dative, Luk.13:17 21:15, Gal.5:17, 1Ti.1:10; as participial subst. (ὁ) ἀντικείμενος, 1Co.16:9,…

antikru.notes: ἄντικρυς (Tr. -ύς, Rec. ἀντικρύ), adv. (ἀντί) [in LXX: Neh.12:8 (נֶגֶד), 3Ma.5:16 * ;] in cl., outright; in κοινή (= cl. καταντικρύ), over against: Act.20:15 (see Bl., § 5, 4; 40, 7; Rutherford, NPhr., 500 f.; MM,…

antilambano.notes: ἀντι-λαμβάνω [frequently in LXX for חָזַק hi., סָמַךְ, etc ;] to take instead of or in turn. Mid., with genitive, to take hold of; __(a) of persons, to help (see MM, VGT, see word): Luk.1:54, Act.20:35; __(b) of…

antilego.notes: ἀντι-λέγω [in LXX: Hos.4:4 (רִיב hi.), Isa.50:5 (סוּג ni.) Isa.22:22 65:2, Sir.4:25, 3Ma.2:28, 4Ma.4:7 4Mac 8:2 * ;] cοntradict, oppose, resist (see Field, Notes, 106; MM, VGT, see word); absol.: Act.28:19,…

antilepsis.notes: ἀντί-λημψις (Rec. -ληψις; see MM, VGT see word; M, Pr., 56), -εως, ἡ (ἀντιλαμβάνομαι) [in LXX for עֹז, זְרוֹעַ, etc., frequently in Psa.2:1-12,3Mac.; frequently also in π. in petitions to the Ptolemies in sense of…

antilogia.notes: ἀντιλογία, -ας ἡ (ἀντιλέγω), [in LXX chiefly for רִיב ;] gain-saying, strife (the latter sense being found in π.; see MM, VGT, see word; cf. Field, Notes, 106): Heb.6:16 7:7 12:3, Ju 11.† (AS)

antiloidoreo.notes: to rail at or abuse in turn , (New Testament); Mid., with accusative rei, (Lucian) (ML)

antilutron.notes: ἀντί-λυτρον, -ου, τό [in Al.: Psa.49:9 * ;] a ransom: 1Ti.2:6 (see CGT, in l; and cf. λύτρον).† (AS)

antimetreo.notes: ἀντι-μετρέω, -ῶ to measure in return: Luk.6:38 (WH, mg., μετρέω).† (AS)

antimisthia.notes: ἀντιμισθία, -ας, ἡ (ἀντίμισθος, for a reward) a reward, requital: in good sense, 2Co.6:13; in bad sense, Rom.1:27 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

antiocheus.notes: Ἀντιοχεύς, -έως, ὁ a citizen of Antioch, an Antiochian: Act.6:5.† (AS)

antiparerchomai.notes: ἀντι-παρ-έρχομαι [in LXX: Wis.16:10 * ;] to pass by opposite to: Luk.10:31-32 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

antipas.notes: Ἀντίπας (T, Ἀντείπας), -α (in some MSS. it appears to be indecl.; but see M, Pro.12:1-28; it is abbrev. from Ἀντίπατρος), ὁ, Antipas: Rev.2:13.† (AS)

antipatris.notes: Ἀντιπατρίς, -ίδος, ἡ Antipatris, bet. Joppa and Caesarea: Act.23:31.† (AS)

antipipto.notes: ἀντι-πίπτω [in LXX: Exo.26:5 (קָבַל), Exo.26:17 (שׁוּב), Num.27:14 (מְרִיבָה) Job.23:13 (שָׁלַב hi.) * ;] __1. to fall against or upon (Arist., Polyb.). __2. to strive against, resist (Arist.): with dative, Act.7:51 (AS)

antistrateuomai.notes: ἀντι-στρατεύομαι to make war against: with dative, Rom.7:23.† (AS)

antitasso.notes: ἀντι-τάσσω (Att..., -ττω) [in LXX for לוּץ hi., נָשָׂא, etc. ;] to range on battle against; mid., to set oneself against, resist: absol., Act.18:6; with dative, Rom.13:2, Jas.4:6 5:6, 1Pe.5:5 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

antitupos.notes: ἀντί-τυπος, -ον (see: τύπος), [in LXX: Est.3:13 A * ;] __1. active striking back; metaphorically, resisting, adverse. __2. Pass. struck back; metaphorically, corres­ponding to (MM, VGT, see word); __(a) as impression…

antlema.notes: ἄντλημα, -τος, τό (ἀντλέω), __(a) prop., what is drawn (Diosc.); __(b) a vessel to draw with, a bucket (Plut.; see Abbott, Essays, 88): Jhn.4:11.† (AS)

antleo.notes: ἀντλέω, -ῶ (ἄντλος, bilge-water in a hold), [in LXX for שָׁאַב, etc. ;] __1. prop., to bale out. __2. Generally, to draw water: absol., Jhn.2:8 4:15; ὕδωρ, Jhn.2:9 4:7 (on its use of the water made wine, see DCG, ii,…

antophthalmeo.notes: ἀντοφθαλμέω, -ῶ (ἀντί, ὀφθαλμός), [in LXX: Wis.12:14 * ;] to look in the face, look straight at (Polyb.). Metaphorical, to face, withstand (Wis, l.with, Polyb.): with dative, ἀ. τ. ἀνέμῳ, as nautical term, to beat up…

anudros.notes: ἄνυδρος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ὕδωρ) [in LXX for צִיָּה, יְשִׁימוֹן (γῆ ἄ), etc. ;] waterless: τόποι, Mat.12:43, Luk.11:24; πηγαί, 2Pe.2:17; νεφέλαι, Ju 12 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anupokritos.notes: ἀν-υπόκριτος, -ον (ά- neg., ὑποκρίνομαι) [in LXX: Wis.5:18 18:16 * ;] unfeigned: Rom.12:9, 2Co.6:6, 1Ti.1:5, 2Ti.1:5, Jas.3:17, 1Pe.1:22 (Cremer, 380; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

anupotaktos.notes: ἀνυπότακτος, -ον (ά- neg., ὑποτάσσω) [in Sm.: 1Ki.2:12 10:27 (for LXX, λοιμός, בֵּן בְּלִיַּעַל)* ;] of things, not subject to rule: Heb.2:8; of persons, unruly: 1Ti.1:9, Tit.1:6, 10 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

aoratos.notes: ἀόρατος, -ον (ὁράω), [in LXX: Gen.1:2 (תֹּהוּ), Isa.45:3 (מִסְתָּר), 2Ma.9:5 * ;] unseen, invisible: Rom.1:20, Col.1:15-16, 1Ti.1:17, Heb.11:27.† (AS)

apagcho.notes: ἀπ-άγχω (ἄγχω, to press, strangle), [in LXX: 2Ki.17:23 (חָנַק), Tob.3:10 * ;] to strangle; mid., to hang oneself (or, to choke; see M, Pr., 155): Mat.27:5.† (AS)

apago.notes: ἀπ-άγω [in LXX for נָהַג, הָלַךְ hi., etc. ;] to lead away: Luk.13:15, Act.23:17 24:7 (R, mg.), 1Co.12:2; esp. of leading to trial (so as law term in Attic), prison and death (MM, VGT, see word): Mat.26:57 27:2, 31,…

apaideutos.notes: ἀ-παίδευτος, -ον (παιδεύω), [in LXX for כְּסִיל, etc., chiefly in Wis. lit. ;] uninstructed, ignorant: 2Ti.2:23.† (AS)

apairo.notes: ἀπ-αίρω [in LXX chiefly for נָסַע ;] to lift off, hence, to take away; pass.: Mat.9:15, Mrk.2:20, Luk.5:35.† (AS)

apaiteo.notes: ἀπ-αιτέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Deu.15:2-3, al. (נָגַשׂ), Sir.20:15, Wis.15:8, al. ;] to ask back, demand back: Luk.6:30 12:20 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

apalgeo.notes: ἀπ-αλγέω, -ῶ __1. prop., to cease to feel pain for (Thuc., ii, 61). __2. In late Gk. __(a) to despair (Polyb., i, 35, 5); __(b) to become callous, reckless (Polyb., xvi, 12, 7; MM, VGT, see word): Eph.4:19.† (AS)
