apallasso.notes: ἀπ-αλλάσσω [in LXX for סוּר hi., etc. ;] to remove, release: Heb.2:15; pass., to depart: Act.19:12; in legal sense (MM, VGT, see word), before ἀπό, with genitive of person(s), to be quit of: Luk.12:58 (Cremer, 90,…

apallotrioo.notes: ἀπ-αλλοτριόω, -ῶ [in LXX for זוּר, H5110 etc. ;] to alienate, estrange; pass.: Eph.2:12 4:18, Col.1:21 (MM, VGT, see word; Cremer, 95, 633).† (AS)

apangello.notes: ἀπ-αγγέλλω [in LXX chiefly for נָגַד hi. ;] of a messenger, speaker, or writer, to report, announce, declare: with accusative of thing(s), Act.4:23, al.; with dative of person(s), Mat.2:8, al.; before ὃτι, Luk.18:37;…

apantao.notes: ἀπαντάω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for פָּגַע ;] __1. to go to meet. __2. to meet; with dative: Mrk.14:13, Luk.17:12 (WH, mg., ὑπ-; in Rec. frequently as see1. for ὑπ-, which see).† (AS)

apantesis.notes: ἀπάντησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀπαντάω), [in LXX chiefly for קָרָא ;] usually with seel., ὑπ-; a meeting; εἰς ἀ., with genitive or dative, to meet: Mat.25:6 27:32 (WH, txt., omits), Act.28:15, 1Th.4:17 (see M, Th., in l; M,…

aparabatos.notes: ἀ-παρά-βατος, -ον (παραβαίνω), inviolable, and so unchangeable: Heb.7:24 (see Westc., in l; Cremer, 653; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

aparche.notes: ἀπ-αρχή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀπάρχομαι, to make a beginning in sacrifice, offer first fruits) [in LXX chiefly for תְּרוּמָה, רֵאשִׁית ;] __1. the beginning of a sacrifice. __2. first fruits: τοῦ φυράματος (cf. Num.15:20),…

aparneomai.notes: ἀπ-αρνέομαι (-οῦμαι) depon., [in LXX: Isa.31:7 (מָאַס) * ;] to deny, i.e. to refuse to recognize, to ignore: with accusative, of oneself (DCG, ii, 598 f.), Mat.16:24, Mrk.8:34 (MM, VGT, see word), Luk.9:23 (WH, mg.,…

apartismos.notes: ἀπαρτισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀπαρτίζω, to finish), completion: Luk.14:28 (cf. MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

apatao.notes: ἀπατάω, -ῶ (ἀπάτη), [in LXX for פָּתָה, נָשָׁא hi., etc. ;] to deceive: with accusative, Jas.1:26; with accusative of person(s), dative of thing(s), Eph.5:6; pass., 1Ti.2:14 (on its infrequency in late writers, see…

apate.notes: ἀπάτη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Ecc.9:6 א (no Heb. equiv.), Jdth.9:3, 10 9:13 16:8, 4Ma.18:8 * ;] deceit, deceitfulness: Col.2:8; τοῦ πλούτου, Mat.13:22, Mrk.4:19 (MM, VGT, see word); τῆς ἀδικίας, 2Th.2:10; τῆς ἁμαρτίας,…

apator.notes: ἀπάτωρ, -ορος, ὁ, ὴ (ἀ- neg., πατήρ) __1. fatherless. __2. without father (MM, VGT, see word), i.e., with no recorded genealogy: Heb.7:3.† (AS)

apaugasma.notes: ἀπ-αύγασμα, -τος, τό (αὐγή, brightness, whence ἀπαυγάζω, to radiate or reflect) [in LXX: Wis.7:26 * ;] of light beaming from a luminous body, radiance, effulgence: Heb.1:3.† (AS)

apecho.notes: ἀπ-έχω [in LXX chiefly for רָחַק ;] __1. trans., __(a) to hold back, keep off; __(b) to have in full, to have received (on the " punctiliar " force of the compound, see M, Pr., 109, 247): with accusative, Mat.6:2, 5…

apeile.notes: ἀπειλή, -ῆ, ἡ [in LXX for גְּעָרָה, etc. ;] threatening, threat: Act.4:29 9:1, Eph.6:9.† (AS)

apeileo.notes: ἀπειλέω, -ῶ (ἀπειλή) [in LXX: Nam.1:4 (גָּעַר), Isa.66:14 (זָעַם), Sir.19:17, al. ;] to threaten: 1Pe.2:23; mid., Act.4:17 (see MM, VGT, see word, and cf. προσαπειλέω).† (AS)

apeimi.notes: ἄπ-ειμι (εἰμί, sum) [in LXX for כָּחַד ni., נָדַח ni. ;] to be absent: 1Co.5:3, 2Co.10:1 10:11 13:2 13:10 Php.1:27, Col.2:5.† (AS)

apeipon.notes: ἀπ-εῖπον, 2 aor. without present in use [in LXX for מָאַס, אָמַר, etc. ;] __1. to tell out. __2. to forbid (3Ki.11:2). __3. to renounce: 1 aor. mid. (WH, App., 164; MM, VGT, see word), 2Co.4:2.† (AS)

apeirastos.notes: απείραστος, -ον (πειράζω for cl. ἀπείρητος, πειράω), untempted, untried, without experience: Jas.1:13 (see Hort, in l; MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

apeiros.notes: ἄπειρος, -ον (ἀ- neg., πεῖρα, trial), [in LXX: Num.14:23, Zec.11:15 (אֱוִלִי) Jer.2:6 (עֲרָבָה) * ;] without experience of: with genitive of thing(s), Heb.5:13 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

apeitheia.notes: ἀπείθεια (WH, -θία, exc. Heb.11:1-40. with), -ας, ἡ (ἀπειθής), [in LXX 4Ma.8:9 4Mac 8:18 4Mac 12:4 * ;] disobedience (MM, VGT, see word): Rom.11:30, 32, Heb.4:6, 11; υἱοὶ τῆς ἀ. (genitive of definition, see M, Pr.,…

apeitheo.notes: ἀπειθέω, -ῶ (ἀπειθής), [in LXX for מָרָה, סָרַר, etc. ;] as in cl. (MM, VGT, see word); to disobey, be disobedient: absol., Act.14:2 19:9, Rom.10:21 11:31 15:31, Heb.3:18 11:31, 1Pe.3:20; with dative, Jhn.3:36,…

apeithes.notes: ἀπειθής, -ές (πείθομαι), [in LXX for מַרְאָה, מְרִיא, סָרַר ;] disobedient: absol., Luk.1:17, Tit.1:16 3:3; with dative, Act.26:19, Rom.1:30, 2Ti.3:2.† (AS)

apekdechomai.notes: ἀπ-εκ-δέχομαι depon., to await or expect eagerly (Lft., Notes, 149; MM, VGT, see word): absol., 1Pe.3:20; C. accusative of thing(s), Rom.8:19, 23 8:25, 1Co.1:7, Gal.5:5; with accusative of person(s), Php.3:20,…

apekduomai.notes: ἀπ-εκ-δύω to strip off clothes or arms; mid., to strip off from oneself: Col.3:9; to strip, despoil (mid, for act., ICC, in l; but cf. Lft., Ellic.), Col.2:15.† (AS)

apekdusis.notes: ἀπ-έκ-δυσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀπεκδύω) a putting or stripping off: Col.2:11 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

apelauno.notes: ἀπ-ελαύνω [in LXX for נָצַל, שָׁלַח ;] to drive away: Act.18:16 (MM, see word).† (AS)

apelegmos.notes: ἀπ-ελεγμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀπελέγχω, to convict, refute), refutation, disrepute: ἐλθεῖν εἰς ἀ., Act.19:27 (not elsewhere; see MM, see word).† (AS)

apeleutheros.notes: ἀπ-ελεύθερος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ a freedman: τ. κυρίου (MM, see word), 1Co.7:22.† (AS)

apelles.notes: Ἀπελλῆς, -οῦ, accusative -ῆν (MM, see word), ὁ Apelles: Rom.16:10.† (AS)

apelpizo.notes: ἀπ-ελπίζω [in LXX: Isa.29:19 (אֶבְיוֹן), Jdth.9:11, Est.4:17, Sir.22:21 27:21, 2Ma.9:18 * ;] __1. to give up in despair, despair of (Polyb., Diod., LXX). __2. to hope to receive from or in return (Field, Notes, 59;…

aperantos.notes: ἀπέραντος, -ον (περαίνω, to complete, finish) [in LXX: Job.36:26 (חֵקֶר אַיִן) 3Mac.20 * ;] endless, interminable: 1Ti.1:4.† (AS)

aperchomai.notes: ἀπέρχομαι, [in LXX chiefly for הלךְ ;] __1. to go away, depart (also, in late writers, with "perfective" force, to arrive at a destination, the thought being carried on to the goal; M, Pr., 111f., 247; MM, see word);…

aperispastos.notes: ἀπεριστπάστως adv. (περισπάω the adj. occurs in Wis.16:11 Sir.41:1); without distraction: 1Co.7:35.† (AS)

aperitmetos.notes: ἀ-περί-τμητος, -ον (περιτέμνω), [in LXX chiefly for עָרֵל ;] uncir­cumcised; metaphorically (τ.) καρδίαις (cf. Jer.9:26, al.): Act.7:51 (ἄσημος, found in π., appears to have been the word used by Greek-speaking…

aphaireo.notes: ἀφ-αιρέω, -ῶ [in LXX (Cremer, 615 f.) for סוּר, עָבַר, H7311 כָּרַת, etc. (35 words in all) ;] to take from, take away, take off: with accusative, τὸ ὠτίον, Mat.26:51, Mrk.14:47 (ὠτάριον, WH), Luk.22:50 (οὖς);…

aphanes.notes: ἀφανής, -ές (φαίνω), [in LXX: Neh.4:8(2), Job.24:20, Sir.20:30 41:14, 2Ma.3:34 * ;] unseen, hidden: Heb.4:13.† (AS)

aphanismos.notes: ἀφανισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀφανίζω), [in LXX chiefly for שַׁמָּה, שְׁמָמָה ;] vanishing: Heb.8:13.† (AS)

aphanizo.notes: ἀφανίζω (ἀφανής), [in LXX for שָׁמֵם ni., שָׁמַד hi., etc. ;] __1. to make unseen, hide from sight (Xen., a1.). __2. Later (MM, see word) __(a) to destroy: Mat.6:19-20; __(b) to disfigure: Mat.6:16. Pass., to vanish:…

aphantos.notes: ἄφαντος, -ον (φαίνομαι), poët. and late prose (MM, see word) invisible, hidden: Luk.24:31.† (AS)

apheidia.notes: ἀφειδία (L, -εία), -ας, ἡ (ἀφειδής, unsparing); __1. extravagance. __2. unsparing treatment, severity: Col.2:23.† (AS)

aphelotes.notes: ἀφελότης, -ητος, ἡ = cl. ἀφέλεια (see MM, see word), simplicity: Act.2:46† (AS)

aphesis.notes: ἄφεσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀφίημι), [in LXX for יוֹבֵל, דְּרוֹר, etc. (see Deiss., BS, 98 ff.; MM, see word) ;] __1. dismissal, release: Luk.4:18. __2. Metaphorical, of sins (never in LXX), pardon, remission of penalty:…

aphiemi.notes: ἀφ-ίημι, [in LXX for נשׂא, נוח hi., נתן, סלח ni., עזב, etc. ;] __1. to send forth, send away, let go: of divorce (DB, iii, 274a), τ. γυναῖκα (Hdt.), 1Co.7:11-13; of death, τ. πνεῦμα (Gen.35:18, Hdt., al.), Mat.27:50;…

aphikneomai.notes: ἀφ-ικνέομαι, -οῦμαι [in LXX for בּוֹא, etc. ;] perfective of ἱκνέομαι, to come (M, Pr., 247), to arrive at, come to, reach: metaphorically (MM, see word), Rom.16:19.† (AS)

aphilagathos.notes: ἀ-φιλ-άγαθος, -ον without love of good: 2Ti.3:3 (not elsewhere in Gk. lit., but see MM, see word).† (AS)

aphilarguros.notes: ἀ-φιλ-άργυρος, -ον without love of money, not avaricious; 1Ti.3:3, Heb.13:5. (For other instances, see MM, see word)† (AS)

aphistemi.notes: ἀφ-ίστημι [in LXX for סוּר, מָעַל, etc. (41 words in all) ;] __1. trans. in pres., impf., fut., 1 aor., to put away, lead away; metaphorically, to move to revolt: Act.5:37. __2. Intrans. in pf., plpf., 2 aor., to…

aphixis.notes: ἄφ-ιξις, -εως, ἡ (ἀφικνέομαι), [in LXX: 3Ma.7:18 * ;] in cl. usually, arrival; rarely, departure: Act.20:29 (so in π., cf. MM, see word; M, Pro.26:1-28, n.).† (AS)

aphno.notes: ἄφνω adv., [in LXX for פִּתְאוֹם ;] suddenly: Act.2:2 16:26 28:6.† (AS)

aphobos.notes: ἀφόβως adv. (φόβος), [in LXX: Pro.1:33 (פַּחַד), Wis.17:4 * ;] without fear: Luk.1:74, Php.1:14, 1Co.16:10, Ju 12.† (AS)

aphomoioo.notes: ἀφ-ομοιόω, -ῶ [in LXX: Wis.13:14, Ep. Jer 1:5, 63, 71* ;] to make like: pass., Heb.7:3.† (AS)

aphonos.notes: ἀφωνος, -ον (φωνή) [in LXX: Isa.53:7 (עָלַם ni.), Wis.4:19, 2Ma.3:29 * ;] dumb, speechless: Act.8:32 (LXX); of idols (MM, see word), 1Co.12:2; of beasts, 2Pe.2:19; τοσαῦτα γένη φωνῶν καὶ οὐδὲν ἄ.—so many kinds of…

aphorao.notes: ἀφ-οράω, -ῶ [in LXX: 4Ma.17:10 (είς θεόν)* ;] __(a) to look away from all else at, fix one's gaze upon: metaphorically (MM, see word), Heb.12:2; __(b) simply, to see: ἀφίδω (see: ἀπεῖδον, and Lft., Phi., in l; MM,…

aphorizo.notes: ἀφ-ορίζω [in LXX (Cremer, 805 f.) for בָּדַל hi., סָגַר hi., נוּף hi., רוּם hi etc. ;] __(a) to mark off by boundaries from, separate from: with accusative, Act.19:9, Gal.2:12; id. before ἐκ (ἀπό), Mat.13:49 25:32…

aphorme.notes: ἀφ-ορμή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Eze.5:7 (הָמוֹן), Pro.9:9, 3Ma.3:2 * ;] prop., a starting point; in war, a base of operations; metaphorically, an occasion, incentive, opportunity (MM, see word): 2Co.11:12, Gal.5:13; ἀφ.…

aphrizo.notes: ἀφρίζω (ἀφρός), to foam at the mouth: Mrk.9:18, 20.† (AS)

aphron.notes: ἄφρων, -ον genitive -ονος (φρήν), [in LXX for כְּסִיל, נָבָל, etc. ;] without reason, senseless, foolish, expressing "want of mental sanity and sobriety, a reckless and inconsiderate habit of mind" (Hort; cf. MM, see…

aphros.notes: foam , of the sea, (Iliad by Homer): of an angry lion, foam, froth , (Iliad by Homer); ἀπ᾽ ἀνθρώπων ἀφρόν frothy blood , (Aeschulus Tragicus) (ML)

aphrosune.notes: ἀφροσύνη, -ης, ἡ (ἄφρων), [in LXX for אִוֶּלֶת, נְבָלָה, etc. ;] foolishness: Mrk.7:22, 2Co.11:1 11:17 11:21.† (AS)

aphtharsia.notes: ἀφθαρσία, -ας, ἡ (ἄφθαρτος), [in LXX: Wis.2:23 6:19, 4Ma.9:22 4Mac 17:12 * ;] incorruptibility, immortality: Rom.2:7, 1Co.15:42 15:50 15:53-54, 2Ti.1:10; ἀγαπώντων . . . ἐν ἀ., Eph.6:24 (see AR, in l).† ἀ-φθορία,…

aphthartos.notes: ἄ-φθαρτος, -ον (φθείρω), [in LXX: Wis.12:1 18:4 * ;] imperishable, immortal; __(a) of things: 1Co.9:25, 1Pe.1:4 1:23 3:4; __(b) of persons: of men, 1Co.15:52; of God, Rom.1:23, 1Ti.1:17 (MM, see word).† (AS)

aphupnoo.notes: ἀφ-υπιόω, -ῶ (ὑπνόω, to put to sleep), [in Al.: Gen.28:11 * ;] __1. to awake from sleep (Anth.). __2. = cl. καθυπνόω (MM, see word), to fall asleep: Luk.8:23.† (AS)

apisteo.notes: ἀπιστέω, -ῶ (ἄπιστος), [in LXX: Wis.1:2 10:7 12:17 18:13, Sir.1:27, 2Ma.8:13 * ;] __1. to disbelieve, be faithless: Mrk.16:11, 16, Luk.24:11, 41, Act.28:24, 1Pe.2:7; so prob. also Rom.3:3, 2Ti.2:13 (ICC, CGT, in ll;…

apistia.notes: ἀπιστία, -ας, ἡ (ἄπιστος), [in LXX: Wis.14:25, 4Ma.12:4 * ;] want of faith, unbelief: Mat.13:58, Mrk.6:6 9:24 16:14, Rom.3:3 (but see: ἀπιστέω) Rom.4:20 11:20, 23, 1Ti.1:13, Heb.3:12, 19 (DCG, ii, 775a; Cremer,…

apistos.notes: ἄ-πιστος, -ον (ἀ- neg., πιστός) [in LXX: Pro.17:6 28:25, Isa.17:10 * ;] __(a) of things, incredible: Act.26:8; __(b) of persons, without faith or trust, unbelieving: Mat.17:17, Mrk.9:19, Luk.9:41 12:46, Jhn.20:27,…

apobaino.notes: ἀπο-βαίνω [in LXX for הָיָה, etc. ;] to step off, disembark: Luk.5:2, Jhn.21:9; metaphorically, of events, to issue, turn out (Field, Notes, 74): Luk.21:13, Php.1:19 (MM, see word).† (AS)

apoballo.notes: ἀπο-βάλλω [in LXX: Isa.1:30 (נָבֵל) and elsew. without Heb. equiv. ;] to throw off: Mrk.10:50; metaphorically, to lose, let go (Field, Notes, 231 f.; MM, see word): Heb.10:35.† (AS)

apoblepo.notes: ἀπο-βλέπω [in LXX for פָּנָה, etc. ;] to look away from all else at one object; hence, to look steadfastly: Heb.11:26 (cf. ἀφοράω).† (AS)

apobletos.notes: ἀπό-βλητος, -ον (ἀποβάλλω), [in Aq.: Lev.7:18, al.; Sm.: Hos.9:3 (טָמֵא) ;] to be thrown away, rejected: 1Ti.4:4 (Hom., Plut.).† (AS)

apobole.notes: ἀπο-βολή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀποβάλλω); __1. a throwing away, rejection: opposite to πρόσλημψις, Rom.11:15. __2. a losing, loss: Act.27:22.† (AS)

apochoreo.notes: ἀπο-χωρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Jer.46:5 (סוּג), 2Ma.4:33, 3Ma.2:33 * ;] to go away, depart, withdraw: before ἀπό, Mat.7:23, Luk.9:39, Act.13:13 (absol., Luk.20:20, Tr., mg.).† (AS)

apochorizo.notes: ἀπο-χωρίζω [in LXX: Eze.43:21 (מִפְקָד)* ;] to separate, part asunder: pass., Rev.6:14; reflexively, to separate oneself: Act.15:39.† (AS)

apochresis.notes: ἀπό-χρησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποχράομαι, to use to the full, abuse) abuse, misuse: Col.2:22 (MM, see word).† (AS)

apodechomai.notes: ἀπο-δέχομαι [in LXX: Tob.7:17, Jdth.13:13; Jdt.13.1, 4Mac.10:1-21 * ;] to accept gladly, welcome, receive: Luk.8:40 9:11, Act.18:27 21:17 28:30; metaphorically, with accusative of thing(s), Act.2:41 24:3 (MM, see…

apodeiknumi.notes: ἀπο-δείκνυμι [in LXX: Est.2:9 (רָאָה), al. ;] __1. to bring out, show forth, exhibit (Lft., Notes, 200; ICC, in l): 1Co.4:9. __2. to declare, show: Act.2:22 __3. to prove: Act.25:7. __4. As frequently in late Gk., to…

apodeixis.notes: ἀπό-δειξις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποδείκνυμι) [in LXX: 3Ma.4:20, 4Ma.3:19 4Mac 13:10 * ;] __1. a showing off. __2. As used by Gk. philosophers, demonstration, certain proof: 1Co.2:4 (see ICC, in l; MM, see word; Lft., Notes,…

apodekatoo.notes: ἀπο-δεκατοω, [in LXX for עָשַׂר in both senses foll., e.g. (1) Gen.28:22 (2) 1Ki.8:15 ;] __1. with accusative of thing(s), to tithe, pay a tenth of: Mat.23:23, Luk.11:42. __2. C. accusative of person(s), to exact…

apodemeo.notes: ἀπο-δημέω, -ῶ (ἀπόδημος), [in LXX: Eze.19:3 A * ;] to be or go abroad (M, Pr., 130, 2): Mat.21:33 25:14-15, Mrk.12:1, Luk.15:13 20:9.† (AS)

apodemos.notes: ἀπό-δημος, -ον gone abroad (RV, sojourning in another country): Mrk.13:34.† (AS)

apodidomi.notes: ἀπο-δίδωμι [in LXX for מָכַר, שׁוּב hi., נָתַן, שָׁלֵם pi., etc. ;] to give up or back, restore, return: Mat.27:58, Luk.4:20 9:42 19:8; esp. of wages, debts, oaths, etc. (MM, see word), to render what is due, to pay…

apodiorizo.notes: ἀπο-δι-ορίζω (διορίζω, ἅρος, a limit), to mark off, hence metaphorically to make separations: Ju 19 (Cremer, 806).† (AS)

apodoche.notes: ἀπο-δοχή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀποδέχομαι), acceptance, approbation (Field, Notes, 203): 1Ti.1:15 4:9 (Cremer, 686; MM, see word).† (AS)

apodokimazo.notes: ἀπο-δοκιμάζω [in LXX: Psa.118:22, al. (מָאַס) ;] to reject: Mat.21:42 (LXX), Mrk.8:31 12:10" (LXX) Luk.9:22 17:25 20:17" (LXX), Heb.12:17, 1Pe.2:4 2:7" (LXX) (Cremer, 701; MM, see word).† (AS)

apogenomenos.notes: ἀπο-γίνομαι (cl. -γίγν-) [in LXX: Da TH Dan.2:1 * ;] __1. to be away, removed from. __2. to depart life, to die (MM, see word): τ. ἀμαρτίαις, i.e. with ref. to sins, 1Pe.2:24 (Cremer, 149, 668).† (AS)

apographe.notes: ἀπο-γραφή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀπογράφω) [in LXX: Dan.10:21 (כָּתָב), I Est.8:30, AB, 2Ma.2:1, 3Ma.2:32 3Mac 4:15 3Mac 4:17 3Mac 7:22 * ;] __1. a written copy. __2. As law term, a deposition (Demos.). __3. In late writers, a…

apographo.notes: ἀπο-γράφω [in LXX: Jdg.8:14, Pro.22:20 (כָּתַב), I Est.8:30, 3Ma.2:29 3Mac 4:14 3Mac 6:34 3Mac 6:38 * ;] __1. to write out, copy. __2. to enrol; mid., to enrol oneself: Luk.2:1 (M, Pr., 162; but. see ICC, in l),…

apokalupsis.notes: ἀπο-κάλυψις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποκαλύπτω), [in LXX: 1Ki.20:30 (עֶרְוָה), Sir.11:27 22:22 42:1 * ;] an uncovering, laying bare (Plut.). Metaphorical, a reveal­ing, revelation: a disclosure of divine truth, or a manifestion…

apokalupto.notes: ἀπο-καλύπτω [in LXX chiefly for גָּלָה ] __1. in general sense (cl.), to reveal, uncover, disclose, __(a) of things: Mat.10:26, Luk.2:35 12:2, 1Co.3:13; __(b) of persons: pass., Christ, Luk.17:30; Antichrist, 2Th.2:3…

apokaradokia.notes: ἀπο-καραδοκία, -ας, ἡ (ἀποκαραδοκέω, used by Aq. in Psa.37:7 for חוּל; ἀπό, κάρα the head, δοκέω, in Ion., to watch), to watch with outstretched head, watch anxiously (Polyb., π.; see Deiss., LAE, 374.5, 377 f.),…

apokatallasso.notes: ἀπο-κατ-αλλάσσω (cf. καταλλάσσω: ἀπό here signifies completely, see Lft., Col., l.with; Ellic., Eph., l.with; but also Mey., Eph., l.with), to reconcile completely: Eph.2:16, Col.1:20-21.† (AS)

apokatastasis.notes: ἀπο-κατά-στασις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποκαθίστημι), restoration: Act.3:21 (in τ. of repairs and restorations of temples, see MM, see word).† (AS)

apokathistemi.notes: ἀπο-καθ-ίστημι (ἀποκαθιστάω, Mrk.9:12, Rec., -ιστάνω, LTTr.; cf. Act.1:6; -κατιστάνω, WH), [in LXX chiefly for שׁוּב ;] __1. to restore, i.e. to a former condition: of health, Mat.12:13, Mrk.3:5 8:25, Luk.6:10; of…

apokeimai.notes: ἀπό-κειμαι [in LXX: Gen.49:10 (שִׁילֹה), Job.38:23 (חָשַׂךְ), 2Ma.12:45, 4Ma.8:11 * ;] to be laid up, in store, laid away: Luk.19:20. Metaphorical, C. dative of person(s), to be reserved (Dem., Plat.; and see MM, see…

apokephalizo.notes: ἀπο-κεφαλίζω (ἀπό, κεφαλή), [in LXX: Psa.151:7 * ;] to behead: Mat.14:10, Mrk.6:16, 28 Luk.9:9.† (AS)

apokleio.notes: ἀπο-κλείω [in LXX chiefly for סָגַר ;] to shut fast: Luk.13:25.† (AS)

apokopto.notes: ἀπο-κόπτω [in LXX for קָצַץ, כָּרַת, etc. ;] to cut off: Mrk.9:43, 45, Jhn.18:10, 26 Act.27:32. Mid., to mutilate oneself, have oneself mutilated: Gal.5:12 (cf. Deu.23:1 LXX; and see Cremer, 751; MM, see word).† (AS)

apokrima.notes: ἀπό-κριμα, -τος, τό (ἀποκρίνω); __1. prop., a judicial sentence: 2Co.1:9, R, mg. __2. an answer (see Thayer, see word): 2Co.1:9, R, txt. (In FlJ, Ant., xiv, 10, 6, of a rescript of the Senate; in Inscr. of an…

apokrino.notes: ἀποκρίνω, ἀποκρίνομαι, [in LXX chiefly for ענה ;] in cl., __1. to separate, distinguish. __2. to choose. Mid., to answer: Mat.27:12, Mrk.14:61, Luk.3:16 23:9, Jhn.5:17, 19 Act.3:12. In late Gk. the pass, also is used…

apokrisis.notes: ἀπό-κρισις, -εως, ἡ (ἀποκρίνομαι), [in LXX for דָּבָר, etc. ;] an answering, an answer: Luk.2:47 20:26, Jhn.1:22 19:9.† (AS)

apokruphos.notes: ἀπόκρυφος, -ον (ἀποκρύπτω), [in LXX Chiefly for סֵתֶר ;] hidden: Mrk.4:22, Luk.8:17, Col.2:3 (see Lft., in l; MM, see word).† (AS)

apokrupto.notes: ἀπο-κρύπτω [in LXX chiefly for סָתַר ;] to hide, conceal, keep secret: with accusative, Luk.10:21; pass., 1Co.2:7, Eph.3:9, Col.1:26 (MM, see word).† (AS)

apokteino.notes: ἀπο-κτείνω (also in late forms -κτέννω, Mat.10:28, a1., LTTr., -κτεννύω, Mrk.12:5, WH), [in LXX for הָרַג, מוּת ;] to kill: Mat.14:5, al.; before instr. ἐν (which see), Eph.2:16, Rev.2:23, al. Metaphorical: Rom.7:11;…

apokueo.notes: ἀπο-κυέω (Rec. -κύω), -ῶ (ἀπό, κυέω or κύω, to be pregnant), [in LXX: 4Ma.15:17 * ;] prop., "the medical word for birth as the close of pregnancy" (Hort, Ja., 26 f.). In κοιν, "an ordinary syn. of τίκτω, but…

apokulio.notes: ἀπο-κυλίω (see: κυλίω), [in LXX: Gen.29:3, 8 29:10 (גָּלַל) Jdth.13:9 * ;] to roll away: Mat.28:2, Mrk.16:3, Luk.24:2.† (AS)

apolambano.notes: ἀπο-λαμβάνω [in LXX: Num.34:14 (לָקַח (Deu.25:5 A, Isa.5:17, 2Ma.4:46 2Mac 6:21 2Mac 8:6, 4Ma.18:23 * ;] __1. to receive from another; absol., to receive as one's due: Luk.16:25 18:30 (seel. λάβῃ) Luk.23:41,…

apolausis.notes: ἀπόλαυσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀπολαύω, to take of, enjoy a thing) [in LXX: 3Ma.7:16 * ;] enjoyment: 1Ti.6:17, Heb.11:26 (for late exx., see MM, see word).† (AS)

apoleia.notes: ἀπώλεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀπόλλυμι), [in LXX (Cremer, 797) for אָבַד, אֵיד, etc. ;] destruction, waste, loss, perishing (in π., of money, see MM, see word): Mat.26:8, Mrk.14:4, Act.8:20, Rom.9:22, 1Ti.6:9, 2Pe.2:1; in special…

apoleicho.notes: ἀπο-λείχω (for ἐπιλ-, which see), to lick up: Luk.16:21, Rec.† (AS)

apoleipo.notes: ἀπο-λείπω [in LXX for H2308 יָתַר, etc. ;] __1. to leave, leave behind (in π. a term. techn. in wills; see MM, see word): 2Ti.4:13 4:20, Tit.1:5; pass., to be reserved, remain: Heb.4:6, 9 10:26. __2. to desert,…

apollonia.notes: Ἀπολλωνία, -ας, ἡ Apollonia, a city of Macedonia: Act.17:1.† (AS)

apollos.notes: Ἀπολλώς, -ώ, ὁ (perh. contr. from Ἀπολλώνιος, Act.18:24 D) Apollos: Act.18:24 19:1, 1Co.1:12 3:4-6 3:22 4:6 16:12, Tit.3:13.† (AS)

apolluo.notes: ἀπ-όλλυμι and ἀπολλύω [in LXX for אָבַד, etc. (38 words in all)]. __1. Act., __1 to destroy utterly, destroy, kill: Mk 124, 922, al.; τ. ψνχήν, Mat.10:28, al.; __2 to lose utterly: Mat.10:42, al.; metaphorically, of…

apolluon.notes: Ἀπολλύων, -οντος, ὁ (present ptcp. of ἀπολλύω), Apollyon, i.e. the Destroyer: Rev.9:11 (cf. Ἀβάδδων). (Cremer, 453; DB, i, 125, 172.) † (AS)

apologeomai.notes: ἀπολογέομαι, -οῦμαι (ἀπό, λόγος), [in LXX: Jer.12:1 (רִיב) Jer.31:6, 2Ma.13:26 * ;] __1. to defend: with accusative, Rom.2:15. __2. to defend one's self: absol., Luk.21:14, Act.26:1; before ὅτι, Act.25:8; τί, to…

apologia.notes: ἀπολογία, -ας, ἡ (ἀπολογέομαι), [in LXX: Wis.6:10 * ;] a speech in defence: Act.25:16, 2Co.7:11, Php.1:7, 16, 2Ti.4:16; with dative of person(s), 1Co.9:3, 1Pe.3:15; before πρός, Act.22:1.† (AS)

apolouo.notes: ἀπο-λούω [in LXX: Job.9:30 (רָחַץ)* ;] to wash off, wash away; mid., metaphorically, with accusative of thing(s), to wash off oneself: ἀμαρτίας, Act.22:16; absol., ἀπελούσασθε, ye washed yourselves clean (cf. Cremer,…

apoluo.notes: ἀπο-λύω [in LXX for שׁוּב, etc., frequently in 1-4 Mac ;] __1. to set free, release: Luk.13:12, Jhn.19:10, al.; a debtor, Mat.18:27; metaphorically, of forgive­ness, Luk.6:37. __2. to let go, dismiss (Field, Notes, 9…

apolutrosis.notes: ἀπο-λύτρωσις, -εως, ἡ (ἀπολυτρόω, to release on payment of ransom, cf. λύτρον), [in LXX: Da (LXX) Dan.4:30 with * ;] release effected by pay­ment of ransom, redemption, deliverance; metaphorically, Heb.11:35; of…

apomasso.notes: απο-μάσσω (μάσσω, Att... -ττω, to touch, handle), [in LXX: Tob.7:17 * ;] to wipe off, wire clean: mid., Luk.10:11.† (AS)

aponipto.notes: ἀπο-νίπτω [in LXX for שָׁטַף, רָחַץ, מָחָה ;] to wash off: mid. (reflex.), τ. χεῖρας, Mat.27:24.† (AS)

apophero.notes: ἀπο-φέρω [in LXX for הָלַךְ, etc. ;] to carry off, bear, or lead away: with accusative, Mrk.15:1, 1Co.16:3, Rev.17:3 21:10. Pass., Luk.16:22, Act.19:12.† (AS)

apopheugo.notes: ἀπο-φεύγω [in LXX: Sir.22:22 * ;] to flee from, escape: with accusative, 2Pe.2:18 2:20; with genitive, 2Pe.1:4.† (AS)

apophortizomai.notes: ἀπο-φορτίζομαι (φορτίζω, to load), to discharge a cargo (Field, Notes, 134), to unlade: with accusative, τ. γόμον, Act.21:3.† (AS)

apophthengomai.notes: ἀπο-φθέγγομαι [in LXX for נָבָא, etc. ;] to speak forth, give utterance: Act.2:4, 14 26:25 (MM, s. see).† (AS)

apopipto.notes: ἀπο-πίπτω [in LXX for מָלַל, נָפַל, etc. ;] to fall off: Act.9:18.† (AS)

apoplanao.notes: ἀπο-πλανάω, -ῶ [in LXX for נָדַח hi., נָטָה hi., שׁוּב pi. ;] to cause to go astray; metaphorically, of leading into error: Mrk.13:22; pass., to be led astray: 1Ti.6:10.† (AS)

apopnigo.notes: ἀπο-πνίγω [in LXX: Nam.2:13 (חָנַק pi.), Tob.3:8 * ;] to choke: Mat.13:7, Luk.8:7; pass., of drowning (= καταποντίζομαι), Luk.8:33.† (AS)

apopsucho.notes: ἀπο-ψύχω [in LXX: 4Ma.15:18 * ;] __1. to breathe out life, expire (Thue.; LXX, l.with). __2. to leave off breathing, faint (Hom., Od., xxiv, 348): before ἀπό, Luk.21:26.† (AS)

aporeo.notes: ἀπορέω, -ῶ (ἄπορος, ἀ- neg., πόρος, a way, resource), [in LXX for צָרַר, מוּךְ, etc. ;] to be at a loss, be perplexed: absol., Mrk.6:20 (ἐποίει, R, mg.); mid., be in doubt: absol., 2Co.4:8; with accusative,…

aporia.notes: ἀπορία, -ας, ἡ (ἀπορέω), [in LXX for בֶּהָלָה, etc. ;] perplexity: Luk.21:25 (MM, see word; on the construction, see Field, Notes, 74 f.).† (AS)

aporphanizo.notes: ἀπ-ορφανίζω (ἀπό, ὀρφανός), to be bereaved (prop., of a parent, Lft., Notes, 36); metaphorically, 1Th.2:17 (where Field thinks it = χωρισθέντες, Notes, 199).† (AS)

aporripto.notes: ἀπο-ρίπτω (Rec. -ρρίπτω, c1.), [in LXX for שָׁלַךְ hi., etc. ;] to throw away, cast forth: reflexively, Act.27:43 (BV, cast themselves overboard; see MM, see word).† (AS)

aposkeuazo.notes: ἀπο-σκευάζω [in LXX: Lev.14:36 (פָּנָה pi.) * ;] to pack and carry off; mid., to pack and remove one's goods: Act.21:15, Rec. (see ἐπισ-).† ἐπι-σκευάζω, [in LXX for pm pi., etc. ;] to equip, make ready; mid., to make…

aposkiasma.notes: ἀπο-σκίασμα, -τος, τό (ἀποσκιάζω, to cast a shadow; v,s. σκία) a shadow: Jas.1:17 (MM, see word).† (AS)

apospao.notes: ἀπο-σπάω, -ῶ [in LXX for נָתַק: ni., hi., etc. ;] to draw off or away, tear away (MM, see word): Act.20:30; μάχαιραν, draw a sword, Mat.26:51; pass., to be parted or withdrawn (Field, Notes, 134; but see Thayer, see…

apostasia.notes: άποστασία, -ας, ἡ (ἀφίστημι), [in LXX for מַעַל, etc. ;] defection, apostasy, revolt; in late Gk. (MM, Exp., viii; Lft., Notes, 111; Cremer, 308) for cl. ἀπόστασις, frequently in sense of political revolt, in LXX…

apostasion.notes: ἀποστάσιον, -ου, τό (ἀφίστημι), [in LXX; Deu.24:1, 3, Jer.3:8, Isa.50:1 (כְּרִיתוּת) * ;] __1. in cl., only in phrase ἀποστασίου δίκη, an action against a freedman for forsaking his προστάτης (Dem.). __2. In LXX,…

apostegazo.notes: ἀπο-στεγάζω (στέγη), [in Sm.: Jer.29:11 * ;] to unroof: Mrk.2:4.† (AS)

apostello.notes: ἀπο-στέλλω, [in LXX very freq., almost always for שׁלח ;] prop., to send away, to dispatch on service; __1. to send with a commission, or on service; __(a) of persons: Christ, Mat.10:40; the apostles, 10:16;…

apostereo.notes: ἀπο-στερέω, -ῶ (στερέω, to rob), [in LXX: Exo.21:10 (גָּרַע), Deu.24:14 (עָשַׁק), Mal.3:5, Sir.4:1 29:6-7 31:21-22 * ;] to defraud, deprive of, despoil (in cl. chiefly of the misappropriation of trust funds, Field,…

apostole.notes: ἀπο-στολή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἀποστέλλω), [in LXX: Deu.22:7, 3Ki.4:34 9:16 Psa.78:49, Ecc.8:8, Sng.4:13 (for שָׁלַח and its cognates), 1Es.9:51 Est.9:54, Jer.32:36, Bar.2:25, 1Ma.2:18, 2Ma.3:2 *.] __1. In cl., a sending away…

apostolos.notes: ἀπόστολος, -ου, ὁ (ἀοστέλλω), [in LXX; 3Ki.14:6 A (שָׁלַח) * ;] __1. a fleet, an expedition (Dem.). __2. a messenger, one sent on a mission (Hdt., LXX, l.with, and π.; see M, Pr., 37 f.; MM, see word; M, Th., i, 2:7…

apostomatizo.notes: ἀποστοματίζω (στόμα), __1. In cl., to speak from memory, to dic­tate to a pupil (Plat.). __2. In late Gk., to catechize, question: Luk.11:53 (MM, see word).† (AS)

apostrepho.notes: ἀπο-στρέφω [in LXX chiefly for שׁוּב ;] trans., with accusative, __(a) to turn away, remove: Rom.11:26, 2Ti.4:4; metaphorically, to turn away from allegiance, pervert: Luk.23:14; __(b) to turn back, return: μάχαιραν,…

apostugeo.notes: ἀπο-στυγέω, -ῶ (στυγέω, to hate), to abhor: Rom.12:9.† (AS)

aposunagogos.notes: ἀποσυνάγωγος, -ον (συναγωγή), expelled from the congregation (Field, Notes, 96), excommunicated: Jhn.9:22 12:42 16:2 (Cremer, 64, 607).† (AS)

apotasso.notes: ἀπο-τάσσω [in LXX: Ecc.2:20 (יָאַשׁ pi.), I Est.6:27, Jer.20:2, 1-18; 1Ma.4:1-61 * ;] to set apart. Mid., in late Gk. (El., § 37, 1; Swete, Mk., 136 f.; MM, e. dative, __(a) to take leave of: Mrk.6:46, Luk.9:61,…

apoteleo.notes: ἀπο-τελέω, -ῶ [in LXX: I Est.5:73, 1Ma.15:39 * ;] to bring to an end, complete, accomplish (cf. MM, see word): Luk.13:32, Jas.1:15.† (AS)

apotheke.notes: ἀπο-θήκη, -ης, ἡ (ἀποτίθημι), [in LXX for אוֹצָר, טֶנֶא, etc. ;] a storehouse, granary: Mat.3:12 6:26 13:30, Luk.3:17 12:18, 24.† (AS)

apotheo.notes: ἀπ-ωθέω, -ῶ [in LXX for זָנַח, מָאַס, etc. ;] to thrust away. Mid., to thrust away from oneself, refuse, reject: with accusative of person(s) (MM, see word), Act.7:27, 39 13:46, Rom.11:1-2, 1Ti.1:19.† (AS)

apothesaurizo.notes: ἀπο-θησαυρίζω [in LXX: Sir.3:4 * ;] to treasure up, store away: 1Ti.6:19.† (AS)

apothlibo.notes: ἀπο-θλίβω [in LXX for לָחַץ, Num.22:25 * ;] to press hard: Luk.8:45.† (AS)

apothnesko.notes: ἀπο-θνῄσκω, [in LXX chiefly for מוּת ;] to die: of natural death, Mrk.5:35, al.; of violent death (pass. of ἀποκτείνω), esp. of Christ, Mat.26:35, Jhn.12:33, Heb.10:28, al.; of spiritual death, Jhn.6:50, Rom.8:13,…

apotinasso.notes: ἀπο-τινάσσω [in LXX: Jdg.16:20 A (נָעַר ni.), 1Ki.10:2 (נָטַשׁ), La 2:7 (נָאַר pi.) * ;] to shake of: Luk.9:5, Act.28:5.† (AS)

apotino.notes: ἀπο-τίνω (or -τίω) [in LXX chiefly for שׂוּם pi. ;] to pay off, repay: Phm 19 (MM, see word).† (AS)

apotithemi.notes: ἀπο-τίθημι [in LXX chiefly for נוּחַ hi. ;] to put off or aside; in NT always mid., __(a) to put of from oneself as a garment: τ. ἱμάτια, Act.7:58; metaphorically, in ethical sense, to put of, lay aside: Rom.13:12,…

apotolmao.notes: ἀπο-τολμάω, -ῶ to be quite bold, make a bold venture: Rom.10:20.† (AS)

apotomia.notes: ἀποτομία, -ας, ἡ (ἀποτέμνω, to cut of), [in Sm.: Jer.51:35, Nam.3:1 * ;] steepness, sharpness; metaphorically, severity (MM, see word): Rom.11:22.† (AS)

apotomos.notes: ἀποτόμως adv., [in LXX: Wis.5:22 * ;] abruptly, curtly, hence sharply, severely: 2Co.13:10, Tit.1:13 (MM, see word).† (AS)

apotrepo.notes: ἀπο-τρέπω [in LXX: Sir.20:29 48:18, 3Ma.1:23, 4Ma.1:33 4Mac 16:12 * ;] to turn away; mid., to turn oneself away from: with accusative, 2Ti.3:5.† (AS)

apousia.notes: ἀπουσία, -ας, ἡ (ἄπειμι); __1. absence (Æsch., Thuc.): Php.2:12, __2. deficiency, waste (MM, s.v).† (AS)

apphia.notes: Ἀπφία, -ας, ἡ Apphia: Phm 2 (MM, see word).† (AS)

appios.notes: Ἀππιος, -ου, ὁ Appius; Ἀππίου Φόρον, Appii Forum (Market of Appius), a town in Italy: Act.28:15.† (AS)

aprositos.notes: ἀ-πρόσ-ιτος, -ον (πρόσειμι, to go to), unapproachable: φῶς, 1Ti.6:16.† (AS)

aproskopos.notes: ἀπρόσκοπος, -ον (προσκόπτω), [in LXX: Sir.32:21, 3Ma.3:8 * ;] __1. act., not causing to stumble: metaphorically, of not leading others into sin, 1Co.10:32. __2. Pass., not stumbling, without offence, blameless:…

aprosopoleptos.notes: ἀπροσωπολήμπτως (Rec. -λήπτως, cl.), adv. (α- neg., προσωπολήμπτης), without respect of persons, impartially: 1Pe.1:17.† (AS)

apseudes.notes: ἀ-ψευδής, -ές (ψεῦδος), [in LXX: Wis.7:17 * ;] free from false-hood, truthful: Tit.1:2.† (AS)

apsinthos.notes: ἄψινθος, -ου, ἡ (also ἀψίνθιον, τό; ἀψινθία, ἡ), wormwood: as a proper name, Rev.8:11.† (AS)

aptaistos.notes: ἄ-πταιστος, -ον (πταίω), [in LXX: 3Ma.6:39 * ;] without stumbling, sure-footed: metaphorically (MM, see word), Ju 24.† (AS)

ara.notes: ἀρά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for אָלָה ]; __1. a prayer (MM, see word). __2. (as in Homer) a curse, malediction: Rom.3:14 (LXX),† (AS)

arabia.notes: Arabia , (Herdotus Historicus), poetry Ἀρραβία, (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus) (ML)

aram.notes: Ἀράμ indec. (Heb. רָם), Aram: Mat.1:3-4, Luk.3:33 (R, txt., WH, Ἀρνεί).† (AS)

archaios.notes: ἀρχαῖος, -αία, -αῖον (ἀρχή), [in LXX chiefly for קֶדֶם ;] original, ancient: Mat.5:21, 33, Luk.9:8, 19, Act.15:7, 21 21:16, 2Co.5:17, 2Pe.2:5, Rev.12:9 20:2.† SYN.: παλαιός, old, without the reference to beginning…

archangelos.notes: ἀρχ-άγγελος, -ου, ὁ (ἄρχι-, ἄγγελος), archangel, a chief angel: 1Th.4:16, Ju 9 (Cremer, 24; MM, see word).† (AS)

arche.notes: ἀρχή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for קֶדֶם, רֹאשׁ, רֹאשׁ, etc. ;] __1. beginning, origin; __(a) absol., of the beginning of all things: of God as the Eternal, the First Cause, Rev.21:6 (cf. 18); similarly, of Christ, Rev.22:13;…

archegos.notes: ἀρχηγός, -όν [in LXX for רֹאשׁ, נָשִׂיא etc. ;] beginning, originating: more freq., as subst.; __1. founder, author (Lat. auchor; so some­times in π., see MM, see word; Milligan, NTD, 75): Act.3:15 (R, mg.), Heb.2:10…

archelaos.notes: Ἀρχέλαος, -ου, ὁ Archelaus, son of Herod the Great, King of Judma, Samaria and Idumæa: Mat.2:22.† (AS)

archiereus.notes: ἀρχιερεύς, -έως, ὁ, [in LXX for כּ'' הָרֹאשׁ,כּ'' הַגָּדוֹל ,כּהֵן ;] __1. high-priest: Mrk.2:26 14:47, al.; of Christ: Heb.2:17 3:1, al. __2. In pl., chief priests, including ex-high-priests and members of…

archipoimen.notes: ἀρχι-ποίμην, -ενος, ὁ [in Sm.: 4Ki.3:4 * ;] found on an Egyptian mummy label (Deiss., LAE, 97 ff.; cf. MM, see word); used by modem Greeks of tribal chiefs; chief shepherd. of Christ, 1Pe.5:4.† (AS)

archisunagogos.notes: ἀρχισυνάγωγος, -ου, ὁ (συναγωγή), ruler of a synagogue, an administrative officer, supervising the worship (הַכְּנֵםֶת רֹאשׁ): Mrk.5:22, 35-36 5:38 Luk.8:49 13:14, Act.13:15 18:8, 17 (Inscr., see MM, see word; cf.…

architekton.notes: ἀρχι-τέκτων, -ονος, ὁ (τέκτων), [in LXX: Isa.3:3 (חָרָשׁ) Sir.38:27. 2Ma.2:29 * ;] a master-builder, architect: 1Co.3:10 (in π. of building contractors, MM, see word).† (AS)

architelones.notes: ἀρχι-τελώνης, -ου, ὁ a chief tax-collector, chief publican: Luk.19:2.† (AS)

architriklinos.notes: ἀρχι-τρίκλινος, -ου, ὁ (τρί-κλινος or -ον, a room with three couches) the superintendent of a banquet, whose duty it was to arrange the tables and food (DB, ii, 253): Jhn.2:8-9.† (AS)

archo.notes: ἄρχω [in LXX for חָלַל, מָשַׁל, etc. ;] __1. to begin. __2. to rule (see DCG, ii, 538b.): with genitive, Mrk.10:42, Rom.15:12. Mid., to begin: before ἀπό, Mat.16:21 20:8, Luk.14:18 23:5 24:27, 47, Jhn.8:9, Act.1:22…

archomai.notes: ἄρχω [in LXX for חָלַל, מָשַׁל, etc. ;] __1. to begin. __2. to rule (see DCG, ii, 538b.): with genitive, Mrk.10:42, Rom.15:12. Mid., to begin: before ἀπό, Mat.16:21 20:8, Luk.14:18 23:5 24:27, 47, Jhn.8:9, Act.1:22…

archon.notes: ἄρχων, -οντος, ὁ (present ptcp. of ἄρχω), [in LXX for נָשִׂיא., רֹאשׁ, שַׂר, etc. ;] a ruler, chief: Jesus, Rev.1:5; rulers of nations, Mat.20:25, Act.4:26 7:35; magistrates, Act.23:5, Rom.13:3; judges, Luk.12:58,…

areiospagos.notes: Ἄρειος Πάυος (T, Ἄριος Π.), -ου, ὁ Hill of Pres or Mars, Areopagus; also, the Court of Areopagus, the highest tribunal of Athens: Act.17:19, 22.† (AS)

aren.notes: ἀρήν, ἀρνός, ὁ (nom, not in use, exc. in early times: see MM, see word), [in LXX for מְרִיא, כֶּבֶשׂ, etc. ;] a lamb: Luk.10:3.† (AS)

areopagites.notes: Ἀρεοπαγίτης (T, -είτης), -ου, ὁ (see supr.), a judge of the Court of Areopagus: Act.17:34.† (AS)

areskeia.notes: ἀρεσκία (Rec. -εία), -ας, ἡ (ἀρέσκω), [in LXX (-εία): Pro.31:30 (חֵן) * ;] pleasing, desire to please: Col.1:19 In Gk. writers (Arist., Polyb.), most frequently in bad sense, but in π., Inscr., and in Philo, as above…

aresko.notes: ἀρέσκω [in LXX chiefly for טוֹב ;] __1. to please (Hom., Hdt., al.): with dative of person(s), Mat.14:6, Mrk.6:22, Rom.8:8 15:2, 1Th.2:15 4:1, 1Co.7:32-34 Gal.1:10, 2Ti.2:4; before ἐνώπισν (= Heb. בּעֵנֵי, Bl., § 37,…

arestos.notes: ἀρεστός, -ή, -όν (ἀρέσκω), [in LXX for יָשָׁר, etc. ;] pleasing, agreeable (Hdt., Xen., and later writers; see Cremer, 641 f; MM, see word): with dative of person(s), Jhn.8:29, Act.12:3; before ἐνώπιον, with genitive…

aretas.notes: Ἀρέτας (WH, Ἀρ.; Intr., 313), -α, ὁ, Aretas, an Arabian king: 2Co.11:32 (Deiss., BS, 183 f., thinks the proper spelling Ἀρέθας was changed, as Schürer suggests, "by desire to Hellenise a barbaric name by assimilation…

arete.notes: ἀρετή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX, in sing.: Hab.3:3, Zec.6:13 (הוֹד), in pl.: Isa.42:8, 12 43:21 63:7 (תְּהִלָּה), Wis.4:1 5:13 8:7 2:1-24; 4Mac.2.2 * ;] prop., whatever procures pre-eminent estimation for a person or thing, in…

argeo.notes: ἀργέω, -ῶ (ἀργός), [in LXX: Est.4:24, Ecc.12:3 (בָּטֵל), I Est.2:30, Sir.33:27), 2Ma.5:25 * ;] to be idle; τὸ κρίμα . . . ἀ., lingers (cf. MGr. ἀργά, late: MM, see word): 2Pe.2:3.† (AS)

argos.notes: ἀργός, -όν (in late Gk., incl. NT, -ή, -όν; ἀ- neg., ἔργον) [in LXX: 3Ki.6:7 (מַסָּע), Wis.14:5 15:15, Sir.37:11 38:28 * ;] inactive, idle: Mat.20:3, 6, 1Ti.5:13, Tit.1:12, 2Pe.1:8. Metaphorical, of things, inactive,…

argureos.notes: ἀργύρεος (see MM, see word), -οῦς, -ᾶ, -οῦν (ἄργυρος), [in LXX for כֶּסֶף ;] of silver: Act.19:24 (WH, br.), 2Ti.2:20, Rev.9:20.† (AS)

argurion.notes: ἀργύριον, -ου, τό (ἄργυρος), [in LXX for כֶּסֶף (Gen.13:2, al.), exc. La 4:1 (כֶּתֶם) ;] prop., a piece of silver (Lft., Notes, 191); in NT, __(a) silver: Act.3:6 7:16 19:19 20:33, 1Co.3:12, 1Pe.1:18; __(b) money:…

argurokopos.notes: ἀργυροκόπος, -ου, ὁ (ἄργυρος, κόπτω, to beat) [in LXX: Jdg.17:4, Jer.6:29 (צָרַף) * ;] a silversmith: Act.19:24 (Plut.; 77., see MM, see word).† (AS)

arguros.notes: ἄργυρος, -ου, ὁ (ἀργός, shining), [in LXX for כֶּסֶף ;] silver (on its relation to ἀργύριον, see MM, see word): Mat.10:9, Act.17:29, Jas.5:5, Rev.18:12.† (AS)

arimathaia.notes: Ἁριμαθεία (WH, Ἁρ.), -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Ἁριμαθάιμ, 1Ki.1:1, al. (רָמָה) ;] Arimathæa: Mat.27:57, Mrk.15:43, Luk.23:51, Jhn.19:38.† (AS)

aristao.notes: ἀριστάω, -ῶ (ἄριστον), [in LXX: Gen.43:25 (לֶחֶם אָכַל), 1Ki.14:24 (לֶחֶם), 3Ki.13:7 (סָעַד), Tob.2:1 * ;] __1. prop., to breakfast: Jhn.21:12, 15 __2. In late Gk., to take a meal, dine: Luk.11:37.† (AS)

aristarchos.notes: Ἀρίσταρχος, -ου, ὁ (ἄριστος, ἀρχός; i.e. best-ruling) Aristarchus: Act.19:29 20:4 27:2, Col.4:10, Phm 24 (AS)

aristeros.notes: ἀριστερός, -ά, -όν [in LXX for שְׂמֹאול ;] left, on the left: ὅπλα, 2Co.6:7; ἡ ἀ.. (sc. χείρ), Mat.6:3; ἐξ ἀριστερῶν, on the left (MM, see word ): Mrk.10:37, Luk.23:33.† (AS)

aristoboulos.notes: Ἀριστόβουλος, -ου, ὁ (ἄριστος, βουλή, i.e. best-counselling) Aristobulus, a Christian: Rom.16:10.† (AS)

ariston.notes: ἄριστον, -ου, τό [in LXX: 3Ki.3:1 (לֶחֶם), Tob.2:1, 4, al. ;] __1 1, prop., breakfast. __2. In late Gk. = cl. δεῖτνον, dinner: Mat.22:4, Luk.11:38 14:12.† (AS)

arithmeo.notes: ἀριθμέω, -ῶ [in LXX for סָפַר pi., פָּקַד pi., etc. ;] to number (esp. for payment, MM, see word): Mat.10:30, Luk.12:7, Rev.7:9.† (AS)

arithmos.notes: ἀριθμός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for מִסְפָּר ;] number, a number: Luk.22:3, Jhn.6:10, Act.4:4 5:36 6:7 11:21 16:5, Rom.9:27, Rev.5:11 7:4 9:16 13:17-18 15:2 20:8 (for exx. of mystical use in π., see MM, see word).† (AS)

arkeo.notes: ἀρκέω, -ῶ [in LXX for הוֹן, etc. ;] __1. to keep off; with dative, to assist. __2. to suffice: with dative of person(s), Jo 67, 2Co.12:9; impers., Mat.25:9, Jhn.14:8 (MM, see word). Pass., to be satisfied: with…

arketos.notes: ἀρκετός, -ή, -όν (ἀρκέω), [in Aq.: Deu.25:2 * ;] sufficient: Mat.6:34 (on the neut., see B1., § 31, 2); before ἵνα (M, Pr., 210), Mat.10:25; with inf., 1Pe.4:3 (for exx., see Deiss., BS, 257; MM, see word).† (AS)

arktos.notes: ἅρκος (Rec. (cl.) ἅρκιος), [in LXX for דֹּב ;] -ου, ὁ, ἡ, a bear: Rev.13:2. (This form is also found in late Inscr.; MM, see word)† (AS)

armageddon.notes: Ἁρμαγεδών (WH, Ἃρ Μαγεδών; Rec. Ἀρμαγεδδών, prop. = הַר מְגִדּוֹן), cf. LXX, Μαγεδών, 2Ch.35:22, Μαγεδώ, Jdg.1:27; Har-Magedon (AV, Armageddon): Rev.16:16 (see Swete, in l, but also Thayer, see word).† Ἅρ, indecl.,…

arneomai.notes: ἀρνέομαι -οῦμαι, depon., [in LXX: Gen.18:15 (כָּחַשׁ pi.), Wis.12:27 16:16 17:10, 4Ma.8:7 4Mac 10:15 * ;] __1. to deny, say no, opposite to είπεῖν: absol., Mat.26:70, Luk.8:45; before ἅτι, 1Jn.2:22; with inf.,…

arnion.notes: ἀρνίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of ἀρήν; see MM, see word), [in LXX: Psa.114:4, 6 (pl., צֹאן בֵּן), Jer.11:19 (כֶּבֶשׂ), Jer.50:45 (צָעִיר)* ;] a little lamb, a lamb: Jhn.21:15, Rev.2:7.† (AS)

aroma.notes: ἄρωμα, -τος, τό [in LXX for בֶּשֶׂם ;] spice: Mrk.16:1, Luk.23:56 24:1, Jhn.19:40.† (AS)

arotriao.notes: ἀροτριάω, -ῶ (ἄροτρον), [in LXX: Deu.22:10, al. (חָרַשׁ), Isa.7:25 (עֲדַר) ;] later form of ἀρόω, to plough (MM, see word): Luk.17:7, 1Co.9:10.† (AS)

arotron.notes: ἄροτρον, -ου, τό (ἀρόω, to plough), [in LXX chiefly for אֵת ;] a plough: Luk.9:62.† (AS)

arrabon.notes: ἀρραβών (T, ἀραβ-: 2Co, ll. with), -ώνος, ὁ, [in LXX: Gen.38:17-20 (עֲרָבוֹן)* ;] an earnest, part payment in advance for security, a first instalment: 2Co.1:22 5:5, Eph.1:14. (The word is found in cl. and was prob.…

arraphos.notes: ἄραφος, -ον (Rec. ἄρραφο), (ῥάπτω, to sew) without seam: Jhn.19:23.† (AS)

arrenarsen.notes: ἄρσην (ἄρρην, T, in Rom.1:27; Rec. in Rev.12:5, 13) -ενος, ὁ, ἡ, ἄρσεν, τό (old Attic for ἄρρην, see supr.; both forms are found in π.; MM, see word), [in LXX chiefly for זָכָר ;] male: Mat.19:4, Mrk.10:6, Luk.2:23,…

arretos.notes: ἄρρητος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ῥητός, ῥέω), [in Sm.: Lev.18:23 * ;] __1. unspoken (Hom., al.). __2. unspeakable (Hat., al.; frequently in Inscr.; MM, see word): 2Co.12:4.† (AS)

arrostos.notes: ἄρρωστος, -ον (ἀ- neg., ῥώννυμι), [in LXX: 3Ki.14:5Α, Mal.1:8 (חָלָה), Sir.7:35 * ;] feeble, sickly: Mat.14:14, Mrk.6:5, 13 16:18, 1Co.11:30.† (AS)

artemis.notes: Ἄρτεμις, -ιδος, ἡ Artemis, an Asiatic goddess, to be disting. from the Gk. goddess of the same name: Act.19:24, 27-28 (AS)

artemon.notes: ἀρτέμων, -ωνος (Rec. -ονος), ὁ (ἀρτάω, to fasten to), a fore-sail or top-sail: Act.27:40 (see DB, ext., 366b, 399a; MM, see word).† (AS)

arti.notes: ἄρτι adv., [in LXX: Dan LXX 9:22 10:11 (עַתָּה), al. ;] adv., of coincidence, denoting strictly present time, as contrasted with past or future, just, just now, this moment: Mat.3:15 9:18 26:53, Jhn.13:7, Gal.4:20,…

artos.notes: ἄρτος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for לֶחֶם ;] bread, a loaf: Mat.4:3, 4 Mrk.3:20, al.; ἄρτοι τ. προθέσεως, bread of the setting forth, i.e. the shewbread. Metaphorical, ὁ ἄ. τ. θεοῦ, τ. ζωῆς, ref. to Christ, Jhn.6:33,…

artuo.notes: ἀρτύω [in Sm.: Sng.8:2 * ;] __1. to arrange, make ready (Hom.). __2. Of food (as in comic writers), to season (MM, see word): Mrk.9:50, Luk.14:34, Col.4:6.† (AS)

asa.notes: Ἀσάφ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. אָסַף), [H609] Asaph, an obvious error for Ἀσά, found in the best texts, and adopted by LTTr. and WH, R, mg.: Mat.1:7-8.† (AS)

asaleutos.notes: ἀ-σάλευτος, -ον (σαλεύω), [in LXX: Exo.13:16, Deu.6:8 11:18 (טוֹפָפָה)* ;] unmoved, immovable: Act.27:41; metaphorically, Heb.12:28.† (AS)

asbestos.notes: ἄ-σβεστος, -ον (σβέννυμι), [in LXX for נָפַח לֹא, Job.20:26 א3 A (ἄκαυστον, א2 B)* ;] unquenched, unquenchable: πῦρ, Mat.3:12, Mrk.9:43, Luk.3:17.† (AS)

aschemon.notes: ἀσχήμων, -ον (ἀ- neg., σχῆμα) [in LXX: Gen.34:7 (נְבָלָה), Deu.24:1 (עֶרְוָה), Wis.2:20, Da TH Sus 1:63, 2Ma.9:2 * ;] __1. shapeless. __2. uncomely, unseemly: 1Co.12:23.† (AS)

aschemoneo.notes: ἀσχημονέω, -ῶ (ἀσχήμαων), [in LXX: Eze.16:7, 22 16:39 23:29 (עֶרְיָה); Deu.25:3 (קָלָה ni.)* ;] to act unbecomingly, behave dishonourably: 1Co.13:5; before ἐπί, ib. 1Co.7:36 (MM, see word).† (AS)

aschemosune.notes: ἀσχημοσύνη, -ης, ή (ἀσχήμων), [in LXX chiefly for עֶרְוָה ;] unseemliness: Rom.1:27 (MM, see word); euphemism for ἡ αἰσχύνη, as frequently in LXX, shame, nakedness: Rev.16:15.† (AS)

asebeia.notes: ἀσέβεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀσεβής), [in LXX for פֶּשַׁע, רֶשַׁע, etc. ;] ungodliness, impiety: Rom.1:18 11:26, 2Ti.2:16, Tit.2:12; ἔργα ἀσεβείας, ungodly deeds, Ju 15; ἐπιθυηίαι τ. ἀσεβειῶν, desires for ungodly things or…

asebeo.notes: ἀσεβέω, -ῶ (ἀσεβής), [in LXX for פָּשַׁע, רָשַׁע ;] to be ungodly, act profanely: 2Pe.2:6; with cogn. accusative (MM, see word), Ju 15.† (AS)

asebes.notes: ἀσεβής, -ές (σέβω, to reverence), [in LXX chiefly for רָשַׁע ;] un­godly, impious: Rom.4:5 5:6, 1Ti.1:9, 1Pe.4:18, 2Pe.2:5-6 3:7, Ju 4 15.† (AS)

aselgeia.notes: ἀσέλγεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀσελγής, licentious; see MM, see word), [in LXX: Wis.14:26, 3Ma.2:26 * ;] licentiousness, wantonness, excess: Mrk.7:22, Rom.13:13, 2Co.12:21, Gal.5:19 (Lft., in l), Eph.4:19, 1Pe.4:3, 2Pe.2:2 2:7…

asemos.notes: ἄσημος, -ον (σῆμα, a mark), [in LXX: Gen.30:42 (עָטַף), Job.42:11, 3Ma.1:3 * ;] without mark (in π. of an uncircumcised boy: Deiss., BS, 153; MM, see word). Metaphorical (MM, see word), unknown, obscure: litotes, οὐκ…

aser.notes: Ἀσήρ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. אָשֵׁר), Asher: Luk.2:36, Rev.7:6.† (AS)

asianos.notes: Ἀσιαός, -ή, -όν Asian, of Asia, Asiatic; as subst., ὁ (οἱ) Ἀ.: Act.20:4.† (AS)

asiarches.notes: Ἀσιάρχης, -ου, ὁ an Asiarch, one of ten officers elected by the various cities in the province of Asia whose duty it was to celebrate at their own charges the public games and festivals: Act.19:31 (Strab., Inscr.;…

asitia.notes: ἀσιτία, -ας, ἡ (ἄσιτος), fasting, abstinence from food: Act.27:21.† SYN.: νηστεία (MM, ut infr.) (AS)

askeo.notes: ἀσκέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ma.15:4 * ;] __1. to adorn (poët.). __2. to practise, exercise (Hdt., Xen.). __3. to endeavour (Xen., al.): with inf., Act.24:16.† (AS)

askos.notes: ἀσκός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX for חֵמֶת, נֹאד, נֶבֶל ;] a leather bottle, wine-skin: Mat.9:17, Mrk.2:22, Luk.5:37-38.† (AS)

asmenos.notes: ἀσμένως adv. (ἥδομαι, to be glad), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:12 2Mac 10:33, 3Ma.3:15 3Mac 5:21 * ;] gladly: Act.21:17.† (AS)

asophos.notes: ἄ-σοθος, -ον [in LXX: Pro.9:8 א2 Α * ;] unwise, foolish: Eph.5:15.† (AS)

asotia.notes: ἀσωτία, -ας, ή (ἀ- neg., σώζω), [in LXX: Pro.28:7 (זָלַל), 2Ma.6:4 * ;] prodigality, wastefulness, profligacy: Eph.5:18, Tit.1:6, 1Pe.4:4 (MM, see word).† SYN.: ἀσέλγεια, q.v (AS)

asotos.notes: ἀσώτως adv. (ἄσωτος, prodigal, wasteful), [in LXX for סָרַר, Pro.7:11 * ;] wastefully: Luk.15:13 (EV, in riotous living; but not necessarily dissolute; cf. MM, ut supr.; Milligan, NTD, 79).† (AS)

aspasmos.notes: ἀσπασμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἀσπάζομαι), a salutation (so always in RV), greeting: oral, Mat.23:7, Mrk.12:38, Luk.1:29, 41 1:44 11:43 20:46; written, 1Co.16:21, Col.4:18, 2Th.3:17.† (AS)

aspazomai.notes: ἀσπάζομαι depon., [in LXX: Eze.18:7, Jdg.18:15 (שָׁלוֹם שָׁאַל), Est.5:2, 1Ma.7:29, al. ;] to welcome, greet, salute: with accusative of person(s), Mat.5:47, Mrk.9:15, Act.21:7, al.; id. before ἐν φιλήματι,…

asphaleia.notes: ἀσφάλεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀσφαλής), [in LXX for בָּטַח, etc. ;] __1. firmness. __2. certainty: Luk.1:4. __3. security: Act.5:23, 1Th.5:3. (In π. it is used as a law-term, proof, security; see MM, see word; M, Th., l.with)† (AS)

asphales.notes: ἀσφαλής, -ές (ἀ- neg., σφάλλω, to trip up), [in LXX for אָשַׁר pu., etc. ;] certain, secure, safe: Act.21:34 22:30 25:26, Php.3:1, Heb.6:19 (MM, see word).† (AS)

asphalizo.notes: ἀσφαλίζω (ἀσφαλής), [in LXX: Neh.3:15 (חָזַק hi.), Isa.41:10 (תָּמַךְ), Wis.4:17 10:12 13:15 * ;] to make firm, secure: mid., Mat.27:65-66, Act.16:24; pass., Mat.27:64 (MM, see word).† (AS)

asphalos.notes: ἀσφαλῶς adv., [in LXX: Gen.34:25 (בֶּטַח), Tob.6:4, Wis.18:6, Bar.5:7, 1Ma.6:40, 3Ma.7:6 * ;] __(a) safely: Mrk.14:44, Act.16:23; __(b) assuredly: Act.2:36.† (AS)

aspilos.notes: ἄ-σπιλος, -ον (ἀ- neg., σπῖλος) [in Sm.: Job.15:15 (LXX, καθαρός)* ;] spotless, unstained: 1Pe.1:19; metaphorically, 1Ti.6:14, Jas.1:27, 2Pe.3:14 (for exx., see MM, see word).† SYN.: ἀμίαντος, ἄμωμος (AS)

aspis.notes: ἀσπίς, -ίδος, ἡ [in LXX for פֶּתֶן, etc. ;] an asp: Rom.3:13.† (AS)

aspondos.notes: ἄσπονδος, -ον (σπονδή, a libation); __1. without truce (Thuc.). __2. admitting of no truce, implacable (Dem., al.): 2Ti.3:3.† (AS)

assarion.notes: ἀσσάριον, -ον, τό (diminutive of Lat. as), an assarion, a farthing, one-tenth of a drachma: Mat.10:29, Luk.12:6 (MM, see word; DB, iii, 428; DCG, ii, 200).† (AS)

asson.notes: ἆσσον (Rec., after Vg., Ἄσσον), adv. (compar. of ἄγχι, near), nearer: Act.27:13 (RV, close in shore; see B1., § 11, 3; 44, 3; poets, Ion, and late prose).† (AS)

assos.notes: Ἄσσος, -ου (also Ἀσσός, -οῦ), ή Assos, a city on the E. coast of Asia Minor: Act.20:13-14 (see: ἆσσον).† (AS)

astateo.notes: ἀστατέω, -ῶ (ἄστατος, unstable), [in Aq.: Isa.58:7 (LXX, ἄστεγος)* ;] to be unsettled, be homeless, lead a vagabond life (Cremer, 738 MM, see word): 1Co.4:11 † (AS)

asteios.notes: ἀστεῖος, -ον (ἄστυ, a city), [in LXX: Exo.2:2 (טוֹב), Num.22:32 (οὐκ ἀ. יָרַט), Jdg.3:17 (בָּרִיא), Jdth.11:23, Dan LXX, Sus 1:7, 2Ma.6:23 * ;] __1. of the town. __2. (Like Lat. urbanus), __(a) courteous, __(b)…

aster.notes: ἀστήρ, -έρος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for כּוֹכָב ;] a star: Mat.2:2, 7-10 :, Mrk.13:25, 1Co.15:41, Rev.6:13 8:10-12 9:1 12:1, 4; metaphorically, ὁ ἀ. ὁ πρωϊνός Rev.2:28 22:16; ἀ. πλαῆται, Ju 13; ἀ. ἑπτά, symbolizing the…

astheneia.notes: ἀσθένεια, -ας, ἡ (ἀσθενής), [in LXX for כָּשַׁל, etc. ;] weakness, frailty, sickness: Luk.13:11-12, Jhn.11:4, Act.28:9, Rom.6:19 8:26, 2Co.11:30 13:4, Gal.4:13 (MM, see word), Heb.5:2 7:28 11:34; ἐν ἀ., Jhn.5:5,…

asthenema.notes: ἀσθένημα, -τος, τό (ἀσθενής), an infirmity (MM, see word): Rom.15:1.† (AS)

astheneo.notes: ἀσθενέω, -ῶ ( ἀσθενής) [in LXX chiefly for כָּשַׁל ;] to be weak, feeble: Act.20:35, Rom.8:3, 2Co.11:21 12:10 13:4 13:9; with dative, πίστει (Cremer, 527), Rom.4:19 14:1; same implied, Rom.14:2, 21, 1Co.8:11-12,…

asthenes.notes: ἀσθενής -ές (ἀ- neg., σθένος, strength), [in LXX for עָנִי, etc. ;] without strength, weak, feeble: 1Co.1:27 4:10 12:22, 2Co.10:10, Gal.4:9, 1Th.5:14, Heb.7:18, 1Pe.3:7. Rhetorically, τό ἀ. τ. θεοῦ, God's action of…

astocheo.notes: ἀστοχέω, -ῶ (στόχος, a mark), [in LXX: Sir.7:19 8:9 * ;] to miss the mark, fail: with genitive, 1Ti.1:6 (so in π., MM, see word); before περί, 1Ti.6:21, 2Ti.2:18.† (AS)

astorgos.notes: ἄστοργος, -ον (στοργή, family affection, love of kindred, see: ἀγάπη) without natural affection: Rom.1:31, 2Ti.3:3 (MM, see word).† (AS)

astrape.notes: ἀστραπή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for בָּרָק ;] lightning: Mat.24:27 28:3, Luk.10:18 17:24; pl., Rev.4:5 8:5 11:19 16:18; of a lamp, shining brightness, Luk.11:36.† (AS)

astrapto.notes: ἀστράπτω [in LXX for בָּרָק ;] to lighten, flash forth: Luk.17:24 24:4 (MM, see word).† (AS)

astron.notes: ἄστρον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for כּוֹכָב ;] __(a) mostly in pl. (as in cl.), the stars: Luk.21:25, Act.27:20, Heb.11:12; __(b) in sing. (Xen., al.), only of some noted star; the symbol or image of a star, Act.7:43…

asumphonos.notes: ἀ-σύμφωνος, -ον [in LXX: Wis.18:10, Da, LXX, Bel 1:15 * ;] dissonant, discordant; metaphorically, at variance: πρὸς ἀλλήλους, Act.28:25.† (AS)

asunetos.notes: ἀ-σύνετος, -ον [in LXX: Deu.32:21 (נָבָל). Job.13:2 (נָפַל), Psa.92:6 (כְּסִיל), Psa.76:5, Wis.2:1-24, Sir.6:1-37 * ;] without understanding or discernment: Mat.15:16, Mrk.7:18 (Swete, in l), Rom.1:21, 31 10:19 (for…

asunthetos.notes: ἀ-σύν-θετος, -ον (συντίθεμαι ν. M, Pr., 222; MM, see word), [in LXX: Jer.3:7-11 (בָּגַד)* ;] false to engagements, not keeping covenant, faithless (MM, see word): Rom.1:31.† (AS)

atakteo.notes: ἀτακτέω -ῶ (ἄτακτος), primarily, of soldiers marching, to be out of order, to quit the ranks; hence, metaphorically, to be remiss, fail in the performance of duty (in π., of truancy on the part of an apprentice):…

ataktos.notes: ἀ-τακτος, -ον (τάσσω), [in LXX: 3Ma.1:19 * ;] out of order, out of place (Lat. inordinatus), frequently of soldiers not keeping the ranks, or an army in disarray (cf. 3Ma.1:19); hence, metaphorically, irregular,…

ateknos.notes: ἄτεκνος, -ον (τέκνον), [in LXX: Gen.15:2, Lev.20:20-21 (עֲרִירִי), Isa.49:21, Jer.18:21 (שָׁכֹל), Sir.16:3 * ;] childless: Luk.20:28-29.† (AS)

atenizo.notes: ἀτενίζω (ἀτενής, strained, intent; τείνω), [in LXX: I Est.6:28, 3Ma.2:26 * ;] to look fixedly, gaze (MM, see word): with dative of person(s), gaze upon: Luk.4:20 22:56, Act.3:12 10:4 14:9 23:1; before εἰς, with…

athanasia.notes: ἀθανασία, -ας, ἡ (ἀ-θάνατος, undying; V. MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX: Wis.3:4 4:1 8:13,17 15:3, 4Ma.14:5 16:13 * ;] immortality: 1Co.15:53,54 1Ti.6:16 (cf. Cremer, 285 f.).† (AS)

athemitos.notes: ἀ-θέμιτος (late form of ἀθέμιστος, LS, MM, VGT, see word), -ον (θέμις, custom, right), [in LXX: 2Ma.6:5 7:1 10:34, 3Ma.5:20 * ;] __(a) of persons, lawless (III Mac, l.with); __(b) of things, lawless, unlawful:…

athenai.notes: Ἀθῆναι, -ῶν, αἱ (plural because consisting of several parts), Athens: Act.17:15,16 18:1, 1Th.3:1.† (AS)

athenaios.notes: Athenian, of or from Athens , (Iliad by Homer), etc (ML)

atheos.notes: ἄ-θεος, -ον, __1. in cl. __(a) slighting or denyiyig the gods (Plat.; cf. MM, VGT, see word); __(b) godless, ungodly (Pind.); __(with) abandoned by the gods (Soph.) __2. In the NT (cf. Lft. on Ign. ad Trail, §3), of…

athesmos.notes: ἄθεσμος, -ον (θεσμός, law, custom), [in LXX: 3Ma.5:12 6:26 * ;] lawless, esp. of those who violate the law of nature and conscience (cf. MM, VGT, see word): 2Pe.2:7 3:17.† SYN.: ἀθέμιτος, ἄνομος, κακός, πονηρός,…

atheteo.notes: ἀθετέω, -ῶ (τίθημι), [in LXX for seventeen different words, פּשׁע, מרד, בּגד, מעל, etc., often meaning, as 1Ki.13:3 (Heb., al.), to revolt; properly, to make ἄθετον , or do away with what has been laid down (see DCG,…

athetesis.notes: ἀθέτησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀθετέω), [in LXX, usually of unfaithful, rebellious action: 1Ki.24:12 (פֶּשַׁע), Jer.12:1 (בֶּגֶד), Da TH 9:7 (מַעַל), 2Ma.14:28 * ;] a disannulling, setting aside: Heb.7:18 9:26 (for similar usage…

athleo.notes: ἀθλέω, -ῶ (in cl. also ἀθλεύω, ἆθλος, a contest, in war or in sport), to contend in games, wrestle, combat: 2Ti.2:5.† (AS)

athlesis.notes: ἄθλησις, -εως, ἡ (ἀθλέω), a contest, combat, esp. of athletes; figuratively, a struggle: Heb.10:32 (for exx., see MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

athoos.notes: ἀθῷος (Rec. wrongly, -ῶος; LS, see word; Mayser, 131), -ον (θωή, a penalty), [in LXX chiefly for נקה ni., pi., נָקִי ;] __1. unpunished (MM, VGT, see word). __2. innocent: Mat.27:4 (WH, R, mg., δίκαιον) 27:24. (AS)

athumeo.notes: ἀθυμέω, -ῶ (ἄ-θυμος, without heart), [in LXX for חרה, etc. ;] to be disheartened: Col.3:21.† (AS)

atimazo.notes: ἀτιμάζω (ἄτιμος), [in LXX for בּוּז, קָלָה, etc. ;] to dishonour, insult: Mrk.12:4, TTr., mg., WH, Luk.20:11, Jhn.8:49, Rom.2:23, Jas.2:6; pass.: Act.5:41, Rom.1:24 (cf ἀτιμάω).† (AS)

atimia.notes: ἀτιμία, -ας, ἡ (ἄτιμος) [in LXX for כְּלִמָּה, קָלוֹן, etc. ;] dishonour, disgrace: 1Co.11:14, 2Co.6:8; εἰς ἀ., Rom.9:21, 2Ti.2:20; ἐν ἀ., 1Co.15:43; κατ᾽ ἀ., 2Co.11:21; πάθη ἀτιμίας, base passions, Rom.1:26.† (AS)

atimos.notes: ἄτιμος, -ον (τιμή), [in LXX: Isa.3:5 (קָלָה ni.), Isa.53:3 (בָּזָה), Job.30:4, 8 (שֵׁם בְּלִי), שֵׁם בְּלִי), Wis.5:1-23, Sir.1:1-30 * ;] without honour, dishonoured, despised: Mat.13:57, Mrk.6:4, 1Co.4:10; comp.,…

atmis.notes: ἀτμίς, -ίδος, ἡ [in LXX for עָנַן, תִּמֹּר, etc. ;] vapour: Jas.4:14; ἀ. καπνοῦ, Act.2:19 (LXX).† (AS)

atomos.notes: ἄ-τομος, -ον (τέμνω), [in Sm.: Isa.54:8 (MM, see word) * ;] indivisible; of time, ἄτομον, a moment: ἐν ἄ., 1Co.15:52.† (AS)

atopos.notes: ἄ-τοπος, -ον (τόπος), [in LXX: Job.4:8 11:11, Pro.30:20, al., for אָוֶן, etc. ;] __1. out of place, not befitting. __2. marvellous, strange (of symptoms, Hipp.): Act.28:6; hence, in late Greek, with ethical sense,…

attaleia.notes: Ἀτταλία (Rec. -άλεια), -υς, ἡ Attalia, a city of Pamphylia: Act.14:25.† (AS)

auchmeros.notes: αὐχμηρός, -ά, -όν (αὐχμός, drought); __1. dry __2. squalid, dismal: 2Pe.1:19 (MM, see word).† (AS)

augazo.notes: αὐγάζω (αὐγή), [in LXX: Lev.13:24-28, 38 14:56 (בֹּהֶרֶת) Lev.13:39 (כֵּהֶה)* ;] __1. Trans. (cl.), to irradiate. __2. Intrans. __(a) (poët.), to see clearly (so perh. 2 Co, l.with; MM, see word) __(b) as in LXX, to…

auge.notes: αὐγή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Isa.59:9 (נְגֹהָה), 2Ma.12:9 * ;] __1. brightness __2. Later (as in MGr.; MM, see word), daylight, dawn: Act.20:11 (Cremer, 118).† SYN.: φέγγος (see Thayer, s. φ.; DB, iii, 44a; Tr., Syn., §…

augoustos.notes: Αὔγουστος, -ου, ὁ (Lat.), Augustus, the Roman Emperor: Luk.2:1 (cf. Σεβαστός; and see MM, see word).† (AS)

aule.notes: αὐλή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for חָצֵר ;] __1. in Hom., an open courtyard before a house, hence, an enclosure in the open, a sheepfold: Jhn.10:1, 16 __2. the court, courtyard, round which a house is built: Mat.26:3,…

auleo.notes: αὐλέω, -ῶ (αὐλός), [in Al.: 3Ki.1:40 * ;] to play on a flute, to pipe: mid., Mat.11:17, Luk.7:32, 1Co.14:7.† (AS)

auletes.notes: αὐλητής, -οῦ, ὁ (αὐλέω), a flute-player: Mat.9:23, Rev.18:22 (MM, see word).† (AS)

aulizomai.notes: αὐλίζομαι (αὐλή), [in LXX chiefly for לוּן ;] __1. prop., to lodge in a courtyard. __2. to lodge in the open. __3. to pass the night, lodge (LXX; MM, see word): Mat.21:17, Luk.21:37.† (AS)

aulos.notes: αὐλός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἄω, to blow), [in LXX chiefly for חָלִיל ;] a pipe: 1Co.14:7.† (AS)

aurion.notes: αὔριον adv., [in LXX for מָחָר ;] to-morrow: Mat.6:30, Luk.12:28, Act.23:20 25:22, 1Co.15:32, Luk.13:32-33, Jas.4:13; ἡ αὔ. (sc. ἡμέρα, MM, see word), Mat.6:34, Act.4:3; ἐπὶ τὴν αὔ., Luk.10:35, Act.4:5; τὸ (WH om.)…

austeros.notes: αὐστηρός, -ά, -όν (αἄω, to dry up) [in LXX: 2Ma.14:30 * ;] prop., stringent, harsh to the taste. Metaphorical, in Inscr., of a rough country; of disposition and manners, strict, severe (as in π., of an inspector; MM,…

autarkeia.notes: αὐτάρκεια, -ας, ἡ (αὐτάρκης, which see), __(a) sufficiency (MM, see word): in subjective sense (see Milligan, NTD, 57), 2Co.9:8; __(b) contentment: 1Ti.6:6.† (AS)

autarkes.notes: αὐτάρκης, -ες (αὐτός, ἀρκέω), [in LXX: Pro.30:8 (חֹק), Sir.5:1 11:24 31:28 40:18, 4Ma.9:9 * ;] as in cl., in philosophical sense, self-­sufficient, independent; subjectively, contented: Php.4:11 (in non-lit. π., the…

authades.notes: αὐθάδης, -ες (αὐτος, ἥδομαι), [in LXX: Gen.49:3, 7 (עַז), Pro.21:24 (יָהִיר)* ;] self-pleasing, arrogant: Tit.1:7, 2Pe.2:10 (Cremer, 654).† SYN.: φίλαυτος (see Tr., Syn., § xciii) (AS)

authairetos.notes: αὐθαίρετος, -ον (αὐτός, αἱρέομαι), [in Sm.: Exo.35:5, 22 * ;] __1. self-chosen. __2. of one's own accord: 2Co.8:3 8:17.† (AS)

authenteo.notes: αὐθεντέω, -ῶ (αὐθέντης, i.e. αὐτο- ἕντης, one who acts on his own authority, in π., an autocrat; cf. Wis.12:6; cf. -ία, 3Ma.2:29; -ικος is frequently in vernacular, MM, see word) to govern, exercise authority over:…

autocheir.notes: αὐτό-χειρ, -ρος, ὁ, ἡ (αὐτός, χείρ), with one's own hand: Act.27:19.† (AS)

autokatakritos.notes: αὐτο-κατά-κριτος, -ον (αὐτός, κατακρίνω), self-condemned: Tit.3:11 (Eccl., Cremer, 377; MM, see word).† (AS)

automatos.notes: αὐτόματος -ον, and -η, -ον (etym. doubtful; see Boisacq, Prellwitz, see word), [in LXX, Lev.25:5, 11, 4Ki.19:29 (סָפִיחַ), Jos.6:5, Job.24:24, Wis.17:6 * ;] __1. of persons, acting of one's own will. __2. Of…

autou.notes: αὐτοῦ adv., prop. neut. genitive of αὐτός, [in LXX for פֹּה, בָּזֶה ;] there: Mat.26:36, Mrk.6:33, WH, mg., Luk.9:27, Act.15:34, WH, mg., R, mg., Act.18:19 21:4 .† (AS)

auxano.notes: αὐξάνω (and the earlier form αὔξω, Eph.2:21, Col.2:19; MM, see word), [in LXX chiefly for פָּרָה ;] __1. trans., to make to grow: 1Co.3:6-7, 2Co.9:10. Pass., to grow, increase, become greater: Mat.13:32, Mrk.4:8,…

auxesis.notes: αὔξησις, -εως, ἡ (αὔξω), [in LXX: 2Ma.5:16 * ;] increase, growth: Eph.4:16, Col.2:19.† (AS)

axine.notes: αξίνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for גַּרְזֶן, קַרְדֹּם ;] an axe: Mat.3:10, Luk.3:9.† (AS)

axioo.notes: ἀξιόω, -ῶ (ἄξιος), [in LXX chiefly for בְּעָא, בָּקַשׁ; frequently in Wis.1:1-16, 2 Mac ;] __(a) to deem worthy: with accusative and inf. (see MM, VGT, see word, and cf. κατ-αξιόω), Luk.7:7; id. and genitive of…

axios.notes: ἄξιος, -α, -ον (ἄγω, in sense, to weigh), [in LXX for בֵּן (Deu.25:2), מָלֵא, שָׁוָה; frequently in Wis, 2Mac ;] __1. Part One __(a) of weight, worth (often with genitive, cf. Pr 315, 811), before πρός: Rom.8:18 (see…

azotos.notes: Ἄζωτος, -ου, ἡ (Heb., אַשְׁדּוֹד), Azotus, a Philistine city: Act.8:40.† (AS)

azumos.notes: ἄζυμος, -ον (ζύμη), [in LXX for מָצָּה; τὰ ἄ. (sc. λάγανα, cakes) = הַמָצָּה ;] unleavened: ἡ ἑορτὴ τῶν ἄ. (הַג הַמָצָּה), the paschal feast (also called τὰ ἄ., Mrk.14:1), Luk.22:1; ἡμέρα τῶν ἄ., Mat.26:17,…

baal.notes: Βάαλ (Rec. βαάλ), ὁ, ἡ, indecl. (Heb. בַּעַל, lord), Baal: Rom.11:4 (LXX). The fem. art. here agrees with the usage of LXX, where, following a similar Hebrew practice (בֹּשֶׁת for בַּעַל), αἰσχύνη appears to have…

babulon.notes: Βαβυλών, -ῶνος, ἡ (בָּבֶל, Heb. form of Assyr. Bab-ili, Gate of God), Babylon: Mat.1:11, 12 17, Act.7:43" (LXX) ; symbolically, of Rome: Rev.14:8 16:19 17:5 18:2, 10, 21, and prob. also 1Pe.5:13.† (AS)

baion.notes: βαΐον, ου, τό (also βὰϊον, another form of βὰϊς, from the Egyptian), [in LXX: 1Ma.13:51 * ;] (frequently in Egyptian π., see MM, Exp., x) a palm-branch: Jhn.12:13 (DB, i, 314).† (AS)

balaam.notes: Βαλαάμ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. בִּלְעָם), as in LXX (FlJ has ὁ βάλαμος); Balaam (Num.22-24): 2Pe.2:15, Ju 11, Rev.2:14.† (AS)

balak.notes: Βαλάκ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. בָּלָק), Balak; (Num.22:2): Rev.2:14.† (AS)

ballantion.notes: βαλλάντιον (Rec. βαλά-), -ου, ὁ, [in LXX: Job.14:17 (צְרוֹר), Pro.1:14 (כִּיס), Tob.1:14 8:2, Sir.18:33 א 2 * ;] a purse: Luk.10:4 12:33 22:35, 36.† (AS)

ballo.notes: βάλλω, [in LXX for נפל, שׂום, ידד, etc. ;] prop., of a weapon or missile; then generally, of things and persons, lit. and metaphorically, to throw, cast, put, place: with accusative, before εἰς, Mat.4:18, and…

baptisma.notes: βάπτισμα, -τος, τό (βαπτίζω), prop., the result of the act, τὸ βαπτίζειν, as distinct from βαπτισμός, the act itself, immersion, baptism; __1. metaphorically, of affliction: Mrk.10:38, 39 Luk.12:50. __2. Of the…

baptismos.notes: βαπτισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (βαπτίζω), prop., the act of which βάπτισμα is the result; __1. a dipping, washing, lustration: Mrk.7:4; of Jewish ceremonial. Heb.9:10; in Heb.6:2, βαπτισμῶν διδαχήν (-ῆς), "the pl. and the…

baptistes.notes: βαπτιστής, -ου, ὁ (βαπτίζω), a baptizer: of John the Baptist, Mat.3:1 11:11, 12 14:2, 8 16:14 17:13, Mrk.6:25 8:28, Luk.7:20, 33 9:19.† (AS)

baptizo.notes: βαπτίζω (βάπτω), [in LXX: 4Ki.5:14 (טבל), Isa.21:4, Jdth.12:7, Sir.31 (34)30 * ;] to dip, immerse, sink; __1. generally (in Polyb., iii, 72, of soldiers wading breast-deep; in i, 51, of the sinking of ships);…

bapto.notes: βάπτω, [in LXX chiefly for טבל ;] __(a) to dip: Luk.16:24, Jhn.13:26 (ἐμβ-, L); __(b) to dip in dye, to dye: Rev.19:13 (Rec.; ῥεραντισμένον, WH; περιρεραμμένον, T; ῥεραμμένον, Swete, in l., which see). † (AS)

barabbas.notes: Βαραββᾶς, -ᾶ, ὁ (Aram. בַּר־אַבָּא, lit., son of a father, i.e. accusative to Jerome, filius magistri), Barabbas: Mat.27:16, 17 20, 21, 26, Mrk.15:7, 11 15, Luk.23:18, Jhn.18:40. (In Mat.27:16, some MSS. read Ἰησοῦν…

barachias.notes: Βαραχίας, -ου, ὁ (Heb. בֶּרֶכְיָה), Barachiah: Mat.23:35, see: Ζαχαρίας.† (AS)

barak.notes: Βαράκ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. בָּרָק), Barak (Jdg.4:6): Heb.11:32.† (AS)

barbaros.notes: βάρβαρος, -ον (prob. onomatop., descriptive of unintelligible sounds), [in LXX: Ps 113 (114):1 (לעז), Eze.21:31 (36) (בּער), 2Ma.2:21 4:25 10:4, 3Ma.3:24 * ;] barbarous, barbarian, strange to Greek language and…

bareo.notes: βαρέω, -ῶ (later form of βαρύνω, which see), [in LXX: Exo.7:14 (כָּבֵד), 2Ma.13:9 * ;] to depress, weigh down. In NT, in pass. only: Mat.26:43, Luk.9:32 21:34, 2Co.1:8 5:4, 1Ti.5:16.† (AS)

bariesous.notes: Βαρι-ησοῦς, -οῦ, ὁ (Aram. בַּר־יֵשׁוּעַ, son of Joshua), Bar-Jesus: Act.13:6 (see: Ἐλύμας).† (AS)

barionas.notes: Βαριωνᾶς, Βαριωνᾶ, -ᾶ, ὁ (Aram. בַּר־יוֹנָה, son of Jonah), Bar-Jonah, a surname of Peter: Mat.16:17.† (AS)

barnabas.notes: Βαρναβᾶς, -α, ὁ (Aram. בַּר, son, as prefix to another word interpreted in Act.4:36, τῆς παρακλήσεως, perh. נְבוּאָה, wh., however, should be rendered by προφητεία as in II Est.6:14, LXX. Deiss., BS, 309f., thinks Β.…

baros.notes: βάρος, -εος, τό, [in LXX: Jdg.18:21 (כּבד), Jdth.7:4, Sir.13:2, 2Ma.9:10, 3Ma.5:47 * ;] weight; __(a) a weight, burden, lit. and metaphorically: Mat.20:12, Act.15:28, 2Co.4:17, Rev.2:24; ἀλλήλων τὰ β., one another's…

barsabas.notes: Βαρσαββᾶς (Rec. -αβᾶς), -ᾶ (Aram., son of Sabba), Barsabbas: __1. the surname of one Joseph: Act.1:23. __2. The surname of one Judas: Act.15:22. † (AS)

bartholomaios.notes: Βαρθολομαῖος, -ου, ὁ (Aram. בַּר־תָּלְמַי, son of Tolmai), Bartholomew, the Apostle (see: Ναθαναήλ): Mat.10:3, Mrk.3:18, Luk.6:14, Act.1:13.† (AS)

bartimaios.notes: Βαρτίμαιος, -ου, ὁ (-μαῖος, T; perh. Aram. בַּר־טִמְאַי, see DB, iv, p. 762), Bartimæus: Mrk.10:46.† (AS)

barus.notes: βαρύς, -εῖα, ύ, [in LXX chiefly for כָּבֵד ;] heavy: Mat.23:4. Metaphorical, burdensome: ἐντολή, 1Jn.5:3; severe (perh. impressive): ἐπιστολή, 2Co.10:10; weighty: τὰ βαρύτερα τ. νόμου, Mat.23:23; αἰτιώματα, Act.25:7;…

barutimos.notes: βαρύτιμος, -ον (βαρύς, τιμή), of great value, very costly: Mat.26:7 (T, πολυτίμου) .† (AS)

basanismos.notes: βασανισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (βασανίζω), [in LXX: 4Ma.9:6 11:2 * ;] torture, torment: Rev.9:5 14:11 18:7, 10, 15.† (AS)

basanistes.notes: βασανιστής, -οῦ, ὁ (βασανίζω), prop., a torturer; used of a gaoler, Mat.18:34.† (AS)

basanizo.notes: βασανίζω (βάσανος), [in LXX: 1Ki.5:3, Sir.4:17, and frequently in Wi, II, 4Mac ;] __1. prop., to rub on the touchstone, put to the test. __2. to examine by torture, hence, generally, to torture, torment, distress:…

basanos.notes: βάσανος, -ου, ὁ (of Oriental origin), [in LXX chiefly for כְּלִמָּה ,אָשָׁם, and frequently in 4Mac ;] __1. prop., touchstone, a dark stone used in testing metals. __2. examination by torture. __3. torment, torture:…

basileia.notes: βασιλεία, -ας, ἡ (βασιλεύω), [in LXX chiefly for מַמְלָכָה ,מַלְכוּת ;] __1. prop. abstract, sovereignty, royal power, dominion: Luk.1:33 22:29, Jhn.18:36, Act.1:6, Heb.1:8, 1Co.15:24; λαβεῖν β., Luk.19:12, 15…

basileios.notes: βασίλειος, -ον (also -α, -ον; βασιλεύς), [in LXX; Exo.19:6, Deu.3:10 (מַמְלָכָה), Exo.23:22, Wis.18:15, 3Ma.3:28, 4Ma.3:8 * ;] royal: 1Pe.2:9 (LXX) (see Hort, in l.).† royal, regal, kingly;, metaphorically possessed…

basileuo.notes: βασιλεύω, (βασιλεύς), [in LXX for מלךְ, its parts and derivatives, exc. 4Ki.15:5 (ישׁב) ;] to be king, to reign, rule: 1Ti.6:15; with genitive (cl.), Mat.2:22; before ἐπί, with accusative (= Heb. מלךְ על; Bl., §36,…

basileus.notes: βασιλεύς, -έως, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for מֶלֶךְ ;] a king: Mat.1:6 2:1; used by courtesy of Herod the Tetrarch, Mat.14:9; of the Roman Emperor, as frequently in κοινή (Deiss., LAE, p. 367), 1Pe.2:13, 17; of the Christ,…

basilikos.notes: βασιλικός, -ή, -όν (βασιλεύς), [in LXX for מֶלֶךְ and its cognates ;] royal, belonging to a king: χώρα, Act 12:20; ἐσθής, Act.12:21; νόμος β., a supreme law, "a law which governs other laws and so has a specially…

basilissa.notes: βασίλισσα, -ης, ἡ (in Attic, βασιλεία, βασιλίς), [in LXX chiefly for מַלְכָּה ;] a queen: Mat.12:42, Luk.11:31, Act.8:27, Rev.18:7.† (AS)

basis.notes: βάσις, ־εως, ἡ (βαίνω), [in LXX chiefly for אֶדֶן ;] __1. a step (Æsch., al.). __2. Hence, a foot (Plat.; Wis.13:18): Act.3:7.† (AS)

baskaino.notes: βασκαίνω, [in LXX: Deu.28:54, 56 (רעע), Sir.14:6, 8*.] __1. to slander (Dem.). __2. to blight by the evil eye, to fascinate, bewitch: Gal.3:1.† (AS)

bastazo.notes: βαστάζω, [in LXX: Jdg.16:30 (נטה), Rut.2:16, 2Ki.23:5, 4Ki.18:14 and Job.21:3 (נשׂא), Sir.6:25, Da TH Bel 36 * ;] __1. to take up with the hands, to lift: λίθους, Jhn.10:31. __2. to bear, to carry, as a burden, and…

bathmos.notes: βαθμός, -οῦ, ὁ, Ion. form of βασμός (βαίνω, to step), [in LXX 1Ki.5:5 (מִפְתָּן), 4Ki.20:9, 10 11 (מַעֲלָה), Sir.6:36 * ;] a step (4Ki, l.with, of degrees of a dial); metaphorically, a degree, standing: 1Ti.3:13.† (AS)

bathos.notes: βάθος, -εος (-ους), τό, [in LXX for מְצוּלָה ,תַּחְתִּי, etc. ;] depth: Mat.13:5, Mrk.4:5, Rom.8:39, Eph.3:18; τὸ β., the deep sea: Luk.5:4; metaphorically, β. πλούτου . . . Θεοῦ, Rom.11:33; τὰ β. τ. Θεοῦ (the Divine…

bathuno.notes: βαθύνω (βαθύς), [in LXX for עמד: Psa.91 (92):5, Jer.29 (49):8 30(49)30 * ;] to deepen: Luk.6:48.† (AS)

bathus.notes: βαθύς (genitive -έως, vernac., Lk, l.with; Bl., §8, 5), -εῖα, -ύ, [in LXX chiefly for עמק ;] deep; Jhn.4:11; metaphorically, ὄρθρου βαθέως (see supr.), early dawn, Luk.24:1; ὕπνος, Act.20:9; τὰ β. τοῦ Σατανᾶ,…

batos.notes: βάτος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ, [in LXX (always masc, as in Attic): Exo.3:2-4, Deu.33:16 (סְנֶה), Job.31:40 (בָּאְשָׁה)* ;] a bramble-bush: Luk.6:44, Act.7:30, 35; ἐπὶ τοῦ (τῆς) β., in the place concerning the bush: Mrk.12:26,…

batrachos.notes: βάτραχος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX: Ex 8, Psa.77 (78):45 104 (105):30 (צְכַרִדֵּעַ), Wis.19:10 * ;] a frog: Rev.16:13.† (AS)

battologeo.notes: βατταλογέω, -ῶ (Rec. βαττολ-, D, βλαττ- = βατταρίζω, prob. onomatop.; see MM, see word; DCG, ii, 499b, 790a); to stammer, repeat idly: Mat.6:7 (Cremer, 765).† (AS)

bdelugma.notes: βδέλυγμα, -τος, τό (βδελύσσω), [in LXX chiefly for שֶׁקֶץ ,תּוֹעֵבָה ;] an abomination, a detestable thing: Luk.16:15, Rev.17:4, 5 21:27; τὸ β. τ. ἐρημώσεως (Dan LXX 12:11, cf . 1Ma.1:54; DB, i, 12f.; DCG, i, 6f.),…

bdeluktos.notes: βδελυκτός, ή, -όν (βδελύσσω), [in LXX: Pro.17:15 (תּוֹעֵבָה), Sir.41:5, 2Ma.1:27 * ;] abominable, detestable: Tit.1:16 (Cremer, 137).† (AS)

bdelussomai.notes: βδελύσσω, βδελύσσομαι (βδέω, to stink), [in LXX chiefly for שׁקץ ,תּעב ;] in cl., mid. only (Attic, -ττομαι); to make foul; pass., Rev.21:8; mid., to turn away in disgust from, to detest: Rom.2:22 (Cremer, 137).† (AS)

bebaioo.notes: βεβαιόω, -ῶ (βέβαιος), [in LXX: Ps 40 (41)12 (נצב hi.), 118 (119)28 (קוּם pi.), 3Ma.5:42 * ;] to confirm, establish, secure, of things (cl.): λόγον, Mk 16:[20]; ἐπαγγελίας, Rom.15:8; of persons (DCG, ii, 605):…

bebaios.notes: βέβαιος, -ον (also -α, -ον; βαίνω), [in LXX: Est.3:13, Wis.7:23, 3Ma.5:31 7:7, 4Ma.17:4 * ;] firm, secure: ἄγκυρα, Heb.6:19; metaphorically, sure (esp. "in the sense of legally guaranteed security," Deiss., BS, 109;…

bebaiosis.notes: βεβαίωσις, -εως, ἡ (βεβαιόω, which see), [in LXX: Lev.25:23 (צְמִיתֻת), Wis.6:19 * ;] confirmation: τ. εὐαγγελίου, Php.1:7; εἰς β., Heb.6:16, a phrase frequently in π. of guarantee in a business transaction (Deiss.,…

bebeloo.notes: βεβηλόω, -ῶ (βέβηλος), [in LXX chiefly for חלל ;] to profane: τ. σάββατον, Mat.12:5; τ. ἱερόν, Act.24:6 (Cremer, 141).† SYN.: κοινόω, which see (AS)

bebelos.notes: βέβηλος, -ον (βαίνω, whence βηλός, a threshold), [in LXX chiefly for חֹל ;] __1. permitted to be trodden, accessible (see DCG, ii, 422b); hence, __2. (opposite to ἱερός) unhallowed, profane: of things, 1Ti.4:7 6:20,…

beelzeboul.notes: Βεελζεβούλ (WH, App., 159, Rec., βεελζεβούλ), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. בַּעַל, lord, and the Talmudic זִבּוּל, from זֶבֶל, dung (Dalman, Gr., 137.n), or perh. זִבֻל, habitation, but, see DB, iv, 409f.; DCG, i, 181). The AV,…

belial.notes: Βελίαλ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. בְּלִיַּעַל, worthlessness, but see DB, i, 268), Belial, a name of Satan: 2Co.6:15, Rec. See next word.† Βελιάρ, ὁ, indecl. (another form of previous word, "due to harsh Syriac…

belos.notes: βέλος, -εος, τό (βάλλω), [in LXX chiefly for חֵץ ;] a missile, a dart: Eph.6:16.† (AS)

beltion.notes: βελτίων, -ον (genitive -ονος), comp. of ἀγαθός, [in LXX chiefly for טוֹב ;] neut. as adv., better, very well: 2Ti.1:18 (also Act.10:28 D). On elative use in these passages, see M, Pr., 78, 236.† (AS)

bema.notes: βῆμα, -τος, τό (βαίνω), [in LXX: Deu.2:5 (מִדְרָךְ), Neh.8:4 (מִגְדָּל), I Est.9:42, Sir.19:30 45:9, 2Ma.13:26 * ;] __1. a step, stride, pace: Act.7:5. __2. a raised place, a platform reached by steps, originally…

beniamin.notes: Βενιαμίν, (Rec. -μίν), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. בֶּנְיָמִין), Benjamin: Act.13:21, Rom.11:1, Php.3:5, Rev.7:8.† (AS)

bernike.notes: Βερνίκη (elsewhere Βερενίκη), Macedonian form of Φερενίκη, cf. Veronica, Victoria), -ης, ἡ, Bernice, Berenice, dau. of Herod Agrippa I: Act.25:13, 23 26:3o.† (AS)

beroia.notes: Βέροια, -ας, ἡ, Berœa, a city of Macedonia: Act.17:10, 13.† (AS)

berullos.notes: βήρυλλος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ, [in LXX: Tob.13:17 (-ύλλιον in Exo.28:20, שֹׁהַם * ;]) beryl, a jewel of sea-green colour: Rev.21:20.† (AS)

bethania.notes: Βηθανία, -ας (also -ιά, indecl., Luk.19:29 and in B*, Mrk.11:1), ἡ, (Heb. בֵּית עֲנִיָּה, house of affliction, accusative to Jerome, or perh., house of dates, cf. Bethphage), Bethany; __1. a village fifteen furlongs…

bethesda.notes: Βηθεσδά, ἡ, indecl. (deriv. uncertain, see Westc. on Jhn.5:2, and DB, i, 279), Bethesda, a pool in Jerusalem: Jhn.5:2, Rec. (Βηθζαθά, WH, Βηθσαϊδά, WH, mg.).† (AS)

bethleem.notes: Βηθλέεμ, ἡ, indecl. (Heb. בֵּית לֶחֶם, house of bread), Bethlehem, a town 6 m. S. of Jerusalem: Mat.2:1, 5 6, 8, 16, Luk.2:4, 15 Jhn.7:42.† (AS)

bethphage.notes: Βηθφαγή (L, -γῆ), ἡ, indecl. (Aram. בֵּית־פַּגֵּי, house of unripe figs; Dalman, Gr., 191), Bethphage: Mat.21:1, Mrk.11:1, Luk.19:29.† (AS)

bethsaida.notes: Βηθσαιδά (Rec. -σαϊδά), and -δάν (Mt, Mk, ll. with), ἡ, indecl. (Syr., house of fish), Bethsaida, a town on NE. shore of the Sea of Galilee: Luk.9:10. It is generally supposed that a second B. on the W. shore is…

bia.notes: βία, -ας, ἡ, [in LXX for פֶּרֶךְ, etc. ;] strength, force, violence: Act.5:26 21:35 24:7 27:41.† (AS)

biaios.notes: βίαιος, -α, -ον (βία), [in LXX for אָנַשׁ, עַז, צַר, etc. ;] violent: Act.2:2.† (AS)

biastes.notes: βιαστής, -οῦ, ὁ (βιάζω), late form of βιατάς; __1. strong, forceful. __2. violent (Philo): Mat.11:12 (see: βιάζω).† (AS)

biazo.notes: βιάζω (βία), and depon. -ομαι, [in LXX for פּרץ, פּצר, etc. ;] to force, constrain, rare in active (poet, and late prose), but found in cl. in pass., and so perh. βιάζεται, suffereth violence, Mat.11:12, EV (but see…

biblaridion.notes: βιβλαρίδιον, -ου, τό (diminutive of βιβλάριον, diminutive of βίβλος), a little book: Rev.10:2, 8 (WH, βιβλιόν, T2, 7 βιβλιδάριον) 9, 10. Not hitherto found elsewhere.† (AS)

biblion.notes: βιβλίον, -ου, τό (diminutive of βίβλος, which see), [in LXX also in the alternat. form βυβ-, chiefly for סֶפֶר and the most frequently of the cognate forms ;] __1. a paper, letter, written document: β. ἀποστασίου,…

biblos.notes: βίβλος, -ου, ἡ (variant form of βύβλος, the Egyptian papyrus, paper made from its fibrous coat), [in LXX for סֶפֶר, the form βύβ- being sometimes used ;] a book, a roll, used much less frequently than βιβλίον, and…

bibrosko.notes: βιβρώσκω, poët, and late prose, [in LXX for אכל ;] to eat: Jhn.6:13.† (AS)

bioo.notes: βιόω, -ῶ (βίος), [in LXX: Pro.7:2 9:6 (חיה), Job.29:18, Wis.4:4 א 1, 12:23, Sir.40:28, 4Ma.5:22 17:18 * ;] to spend life, to live: τ. χρόνον βιῶσαι (cl., more frequently 2 aor., -ναι), 1Pe.4:2 (cf. Jb, l.with).†…

bios.notes: βίος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for יָמִים ;] __1. period or course of life, life: Luk.8:14, 1Ti.2:2, 2Ti.2:4, 1Jn.2:16. __2. living, livelihood, means (in Pro.31:14 for לֶחֶם; see DCG, ii, 39a): Mrk.12:44, Luk.8:43…

biosis.notes: βίωσις, -εως, ἡ (βιόω), [in LXX: Si prol. 12 * ;] manner of life: Act.26:4.† (AS)

biotikos.notes: βιωτικός, -ή, -όν (βιός), pertaining to life, hence, worldly (Field, Notes, 171): Luk.21:34, 1Co.6:3, 4.† (AS)

bithunia.notes: Βιθυνία, -ַας, Bithynia, a province in Asia Minor: Act.16:7, 1Pe.1:1.† (AS)

blapto.notes: βλάπτω, [in LXX: Pro.25:20, Tob.12:2, Wis.10:8 18:2, 2Ma.12:22, 4Ma.9:7 * ;] to hurt, injure: with accusative, Mrk.16:[18], Luk.4:35.† (AS)

blasphemeo.notes: βλασφημέω, -ῶ (βλάσφημος), [in LXX: 4Ki.19:4 (יכח hi.) ib. 6, 22 (גּדף pi.), Isa.52:5 (נאץ hith.), Dan LXX 3:29 (96) (אמר שׁלח), Tob.1:18, Da TH Bel 9, 2Ma.2 * ;] __1. to speak lightly or profanely of sacred things…

blasphemia.notes: βλασφημία, -ας, ἡ (βλάσφημος), [in LXX: Eze.35:12 (נֶאָצָה), Da TH 3:29 (96) (שׁלה), Tob.1:18, 1Ma.2:6, 2Ma.8:4 10:35 15:24 * ;] __(a) railing slander: Mat.12:31 15:19, Mrk.3:28 7:22, Eph.4:31, Col.3:8, 1Ti.6:4, Ju…

blasphemos.notes: βλάσφημος, -ον (βλασ-, of uncertain deriv., see Thayer, Boisacq; + φήμη, speech), [in LXX: Isa.66:3 (מְבָרֵךְ אָוֶן), Wis.1:6, Sir.3:16, 2Ma.9:28 10:4, 36 * ;] __(a) evil-speaking, slanderous, blasphemous: Act.6:11,…

blastano.notes: βλαστάνω, [in LXX for צמח, etc. ;] __1. to sprout: Mat.13:26, Mrk.4:27, Heb.9:4. __2. In late Gk., causal, to make to grow, produce: with accusative, Jas.5:18.† (AS)

blastos.notes: Βλάστος, -ου, ὁ, Blastus, chamberlain of Agrippa: Act.12:20.† (AS)

blemma.notes: βλέμμα, -τος, τό (βλέπω), a look, a glance: βλέμματι καὶ ἀκοῇ, 2Pe.2:8, sight and hearing, a sense not found for β. in Gk. lit., but perh. recognized in the vernacular (ICC, in l.)† (AS)

blepo.notes: βλέπω, [in LXX chiefly for ראה, also for פּנה, etc. ;] __1. of bodily sight; __(a) to see, have sight (opposite to τυφλὸς): Mat.12:22, Jhn.9:7, Act.9:9, Rom.11:8, Rev.3:18, al.; __(b) to perceive, look (at), see:…

bleteon.notes: βλητέος, -α, -ον (gerundive of βάλλω), (that which) one must put: Luk.5:38.† (AS)

boanerges.notes: Βοανηργές, indecl. (on the derivation, see Dalman, Gr., 144; Words, 42; Swete, Mk., l.with; DCG, i, 216), Boanerges: Mrk.3:17.† (AS)

boao.notes: βοάω, -ῶ (βοή), [in LXX chiefly for זעק, צעק, קרא ;] __1. absol., to cry, call out: Mat.3:3, 27:46, Mrk.1:3 15:34, Luk.3:4 9:38 18:38, Jhn.1:23, Act.8:7 17:6 25:24, Gal.4:27. __2. C. dative, to call on for help (Heb.…

boe.notes: βοή, -ῆς, ἡ, [in LXX for זְעָקָה, etc. ;] a cry: Jas.5:4.† (AS)

boetheia.notes: βοήθεια, -ας, ἡ (see: βοηθέω), [in LXX for עֵזֶר, etc. ;] help: Heb.4:16; pl., helps, "frapping," a technical nautical term (MM, Exp., x; DB, ext., 367): Act.27:17.† (AS)

boetheo.notes: βοηθέω, -ῶ (βοή + θέω, to run), [in LXX chiefly for עזר ;] to come to aid, to help, succour: absol., Act.21:28; with dative, Mat.15:25, Mrk.9:22, 24 Act.16:9, 2Co.6:2" (LXX), Heb.2:18, Rev.12:16.† (AS)

boethos.notes: βοηθός, -ον (see: βοηθέω), [in LXX chiefly for עזר ;] __1. (poët. -όος), hasting to the war-cry (Hom.). __2. helping, auxiliary; as subst. (Hdt.), a helper: Heb.13:6 (LXX).† (AS)

bole.notes: βολή, -ῆς, ἡ (βάλλω), [in LXX: Gen.21:16 (טחה), 2Ma.5:3, 3Ma.5:26 * ;] a throw; λίθου β. (Thuc., v, 65, 2), a stone's throw: Luk.22:41.† (AS)

bolizo.notes: βολίζω (βολίς, in sense of sounding-lead), to heave the lead, take soundings: Act.27:28.† (AS)

bomos.notes: βωμός, -οῦ, ὁ (βαίνω), [in LXX (Hex.) for מִזְבֵּחַ, in Proph., chiefly for בָּמָה ;] __1. any raised place, a platform. __2. an altar: Act.17:23 (cf. θυσιαστήριον and see DB, i, 75).† (AS)

booz.notes: Βοός and Βοές (RV, Βοάζ, Rec. Βοόζ), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. בֹּעַז), Boaz (Rut.2:1): Mat.1:5, Luk.3:32.† (AS)

borboros.notes: βόρβορος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX: Je 45(38):6 (טִיט)* ;] mud, filth: 2Pe.2:22.† (AS)

borras.notes: βορρᾶς, -ᾶ (Attic contr. of Βορέας, the North wind personified), [in LXX for צָפוֹן ;] __1. Boreas, the North wind. __2. the north: Luk.13:29, Rev.21:13.† (AS)

bosko.notes: βόσκω, [in LXX for רעה ;] prop., of a herdsman, to feed: Mat.8:33, Mrk.5:14, Luk.8:34 15:15; metaphorically, of Christian pastoral care, Jhn.21:15, 17. Pass., of cattle, to feed, graze: Mat.8:30, Mrk.5:11, Luk.8:32.†…

bosorbeor.notes: Βοσόρ see Βεώρ - ὁ, indecl. (Heb. בְּעוֹר), Beor, father of Balaam: 2Pe.2:15 (Rec. Βοσόρ).† (AS)

botane.notes: βοτάνη, -ῆς, ἡ (βόσκω), [in LXX for עֵשֶׂב, דֶשֶׁא, חָצִיר ;] __1. grass, fodder. __2. green herb: Heb.6:7.† SYN.: λάχανον, a garden herb, a vegetable. (AS)

bothunos.notes: βόθυνος, -ου, ὁ (= βόθρος, more frequently in cl.), [in LXX chiefly for פַּחַת ;] a pit: Mat.12:11 15:14, Luk.6:39 (cf. DB, iii, 885; DCG, ii, 367).† (AS)

botrus.notes: βότρυς, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX for אֶשְׁכֹּל ;] a cluster of grapes: Rev.14:18 (cf. στραφυλή) .† (AS)

boule.notes: βουλή, -ῆς, ἡ (βούλομαι), [in LXX frequently for עֵצָה ;] counsel, purpose (in cl., esp. of the gods): Luk.23:51, Act.4:28 5:38 19:1 27:12, 42; pl., 1Co.4:5; of the Divine purpose, Heb.6:17; τ. βουλὴν τ. θελήματος…

boulema.notes: βούλημα, -τος, τό (βούλομαι), [in LXX: Pro.9:10 (דַּעַת), 2Ma.15:5, 4Ma.8:18 * ;] purpose, will: Act.27:43, Rom.9:19, 1Pe.4:3.† SYN.: θέλημα. (AS)

bouleuo.notes: βουλεύω (βουλή), [in LXX chiefly for יעץ ;] to take counsel, deliberate, resolve. In mid., __1. to take counsel with oneself, consider: before εἰ, Luk.14:31. __2. to determine with oneself, resolve: with inf.,…

bouleutes.notes: βουλευτής, -οῦ, ὁ (βουλεύω), [in LXX: Job.3:14 12:17 (יעץ)* ;] a councillor, a senator; of a member of the Sanhedrin: Mrk.15:34, Luk.23:50.† (AS)

boulomai.notes: βούλομαι, [in LXX for חפץ, אבה, יעץ, etc. ;] to will, wish, desire, purpose, be minded, implying more strongly than θέλω (which see), the deliberate exercise of volition (see Hort on Jas.1:18): with inf. (M, Pr.,…

bounos.notes: βουνός, -οῦ, ὁ (a Cyrenaїc word, Hdt., iv, 199), [in LXX chiefly for גִּבְעָה ;] a hill: Luk.3:5 (LXX) 23:30.† (AS)

bous.notes: βοῦς, βοός, ὁ, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for בָּקָר ;] an ox, a cow: Luk.13:15 14:5, 19 Jhn.2:14, 15 1Co.9:9" (LXX), 1Ti.5:18.† (AS)

brabeion.notes: βραβεῖον, -ου, τό (βραβεύς, an umpire), a prize in the games: 1Co.9:24; metaphorically, of the Christian's reward, Php.3:14.† (AS)

brabeuo.notes: βραβεύω (βραβεύς, an umpire), [in LXX: Wis.10:12 * ;] __(a) prop., to act as umpire; hence, __(b) generally, to arbitrate, decide (Isocr., Dem.; Lft., Col., l.with; MM, see word): Col.3:15; __(with) in some late…
