brachion.notes: βραχίων, -ὀνος, ὁ, [in LXX, β. Κυρίου, frequently for זְרוֹעַ יהוָֹה ;] the arm; as in OT, β. Κυρίου, metaphorically, for the Divine power: Luk.1:51, Jhn.12:38" (LXX), Act.13:17.† (AS)

brachus.notes: βραχύς, -εῖα, -ύ, [in LXX chiefly for מְעַט ;] short; __(a) of time; short, little: βραχύ, Act.5:34; μετὰ β., Luk.22:58; β. τι, a short time, Heb.2:7, 9 RV, mg.; __(b) of distance: Act.27:28; __(with) of quantity or…

braduno.notes: βραδύνω (βραδύς), [in LXX: Gen.43:10 (מההּ hith.), Deu.7:10, Isa.46:13 (אחר pi.), Si 32 (35):18 * ;] __1. trans., to retard (Soph., Is, l.with). __2. More frequently intrans., to be slow, to tarry: 1Ti.3:15,…

braduploeo.notes: βραδυπλοέω, -ῶ (βραδύς, πλοῦς), to sail slowly: Act.27:7.† (AS)

bradus.notes: βραδύς, -εῖα, -ύ, slow: εἰς τὸ λαλῆσαι, Jas.1:19; metaphorically, of the understanding: β. τ. καρδίᾳ, assoc. with ἀνόητος, Luk.24:25.† (AS)

bradutes.notes: 1. slowness , (Iliad by Homer), attic 2. of the mind, (Plato Philosophus) (ML)

brecho.notes: βρέχω, [in LXX chiefly for מטר ;] __1. to wet: Luk.7:38, 44. __2. In late Gk. writers and vernac. = ὕειν, to send rain, to rain (Kennedy, Sources, 39, 155): Mat.5:45; trop., β. πῦρ κ. θεῖον, Luk.17:29; impers.,…

brephos.notes: βρέφος, -ους, τό, [in LXX: Sir.19:11, 1Ma.1:61, 2Ma.6:10, 3Ma.5:49, 4Ma.4:25 * ;] __1. an unborn child: Luk.1:41, 44. __2. a newborn child, a babe: Luk.2:12, 16 18:15, Act.7:19, 1Pe.2:2; ἀπὸ βρέφους, from infancy,…

broche.notes: βροχή, -ῆς, ἡ (βρέχω), [in LXX: Psa.67 (68):9, 104 (105):32 (גֶּשֶׁם)* ;] __1. = βροχετός, a wetting (in π., of irrigation in Egypt; Deiss., LAE, 77). __2. As in MGr. (Kennedy, Sources, 153), = ὑετός, rain: Mat.7:25,…

broma.notes: βρῶμα, -τος, τό (cf. βιβρώσκω), [in LXX chiefly for אֹכֶל ;] food: Rom.14:15, 20 1Co.8:8, 13 10:3; pl., Mat.14:15, Mrk.7:19, Luk.3:11 9:13, 1Co.6:13, 1Ti.4:3, Heb.9:10 13:9; trop., of spiritual food, Jhn.4:34,…

brosimos.notes: βρώσιμος, -ον (βρῶσις), [in LXX: Lev.19:23, Neh.9:25, Eze.47:12 (מַאֲכָל)* ;] eatable: Luk.24:41.† (AS)

brosis.notes: βρῶσις, -εως, ἡ (cf. βιβρώσκω), [in LXX chiefly for parts and derivatives of אכל ;] __1. eating: β. καὶ πόσις, Rom.14:17, Col.2:16 (see Lft., ICC, in l.); with genitive obj., 1Co.8:4; metaphorically, corrosion, rust:…

brucho.notes: βρύχω (Attic, βρύκω), [in LXX: Job.16:10(9), Psa.34 (35):16 36 (37):12 111 (112):10, La 2:16 (חרק)* ;] __1. to bite or eat greedily. __2. to gnash, grind, with the teeth: Act.7:54.† (AS)

brugmos.notes: βρυγμός, -οῦ, ὁ (βρύχω), [in LXX: Pro.19:12 (נַהַם), Sir.51:3 * ;] a biting, a gnashing of teeth: Mat.8:12 13:42, 50 22:13 24:51 25:30, Luk.13:28.† (AS)

bruo.notes: βρύω, poët., late prose and vernac., to be full to bursting; __1. of the earth producing vegetation. __2. Of plants putting forth buds. __3. Of springs, to gush with water: Jas.3:11.† (AS)

burseus.notes: βυρσεύς, -έως, ὁ (βύρσα, a hide), late form for βυρσοδέψης, a tanner: Act.9:43 10:6, 32.† (AS)

bussinos.notes: βύσσινος, -η, -ον (βύσσος), [in LXX chiefly for שֵׁשׁ, בּוּץ, etc. ;] made of βύσσος, fine linen: Rev.18:12, 16 19:8, 14.† (AS)

bussos.notes: βύσσος, -οῦ, ἡ (cf. Heb. בּוּץ), [in LXX chiefly for שֵׁשׁ, בּוּץ ;] byssus, a fine species of flax, also the linen made from it: Luk.16:19.† (AS)

buthizo.notes: βυθίζω (βυθός), [in LXX: 2Ma.12:4 * ;] trans., to cause to sink, to sink: metaphorically, εἰς ὄλεθρον, 1Ti.6:9; pass., to sink, intrans.: Luk.5:7.† (AS)

buthos.notes: βυθός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX: Exo.15:5, Neh.9:11 (מְצוֹלָה), Psa.67 (68):22 68 (69):2, 15 106 (107):24 (מְצוּלָה)* ;] __1. the bottom. __2. the depth of the sea, the deep sea: 2Co.11:25.† (AS)

chairo.notes: χαίρω [in LXX for שָׂמַח (Gen.45:16, al.), גִּיל (Pro.2:14, al.), etc.; inf., as greeting (see infr.), Isa.48:22 57:21 שָׁלוֹם), 1Ma.10:18, 2Ma.1:1 and frequently in these books ;] __1. to rejoice, be glad:…

chalao.notes: χαλάω, -ῶ [in LXX: (Jer.38:6) (שָׁלַח pi.), etc. ;] __(a) to slacken, loosen; __(b) to let loose, let go; __(with) to lower, let down: with accusative of thing(s), Mrk.2:4, Luk.5:4-5, Act.9:25 27:17, 30; with…

chalaza.notes: χάλαζα, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for בָּרָד ;] hail: Rev.8:7 11:19 16:21.† (AS)

chaldaios.notes: Χαλδαῖος, -ου, ὁ a Chaldæan: γῆ Χαλδαίων, Act.7:4.† (AS)

chalepos.notes: χαλεπός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Isa.18:2 (יָרֵא), Wis.3:19, Sir.3:21, al. ;] hard; __(a) hard to do or deal with, difficult; __(b) hard to bear, painful, grievous: καιροί, 2Ti.3:1; __(with) of persons, hard to deal with,…

chalinagogeo.notes: χαλιναγωγέω, -ῶ (χαλινός, ἄγω), to lead with a bridle; metaphorically, to bridle, restrain: with accusative, γλῶσσαν, Jas.1:26; σῶμα, Jas.3:2.† (AS)

chalinos.notes: χαλινός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for מֶתֶג ;] a bridle: Jas.3:3, Rev.14:20.† (AS)

chalkedon.notes: χαλκηδών, -όνος, ὁ chalcedony, "supposed to denote a green silicate of copper found in the mines near Chalcedon " (Swete): Rev.21:19.† (AS)

chalkeos.notes: χάλκεος, -έα, -εον (-οῦς, -ῆ, -οῦν) (χαλκός), [in LXX chiefly for נְחֹשֶׁת ;] brazen (i.e. of copper): Rev.9:20.† (AS)

chalkeus.notes: χαλκεύς, -έως, ὁ (χαλκός), [in LXX for חָרָשׁ, etc. ;] a worker, in metal, esp. a copper-smith: 2Ti.4:14.† (AS)

chalkion.notes: χαλκίον, -ου, τό (χαλκός), [in LXX: Job.41:22(23) (סִיר), etc. ;] a brazen (copper) vessel: Mrk.7:4.† (AS)

chalkolibanos.notes: χαλκο-λίβανον, -ου, τό chalcolibanus, probably "a mixed metal of great brilliance" (Swete): Rev.1:15 2:18 (RV, burnished brass).† (AS)

chalkos.notes: χαλκός, -oῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for נְחֹשֶׁת ;] copper: 1Co.13:1, Rev.18:12; by meton., of copper coin, Mat.10:9, Mrk.6:8 12:41.† (AS)

chamai.notes: χαμαί adv., on or to the ground: Jhn.9:6 18:6.† (AS)

chanaan.notes: Χαναάν, ἡ indecl. (Heb. כְּנַעַן), Canaan: Act.7:11; γῆ Χ., Act.13:19.† (AS)

chara.notes: χαρά, -ᾶς, ἡ (χαίρω), [in LXX for שִׂמְחָה, שָׂשׂוֹן, etc. ;] joy, delight: Luk.1:14 15:7, 10, Jhn.15:11 16:22, 24 17:13, Act.8:8, 2Co.1:24 7:13 8:2, Gal.5:22, Col.1:11, Php.2:2, 1Jn.1:4, 2Jn.12; opposite to…

charagma.notes: χάραγμα, -τος, τό (χαράσσω, to engrave), __(a) a stamp, impress, mark: Rev.13:16-17 14:9, 11 16:2 19:20 20:4 (see Deiss., BS, 240 ff.); __(b) a thing graven: Act.17:29.† (AS)

charakter.notes: χαρακτήρ, -ῆρος, ὁ (χαράσσω, to engrave), [in LXX: Lev.13:28 (צָרֶבֶת), 2Ma.4:10, 4Ma.15:4 א R * ;] __1. a tool for graving. __2. a stamp or impress: as on a coin or seal; metaphorically, χ. τ. ὑποστάσεως, Heb.1:3.† (AS)

charax.notes: χάραξ, -ακος, ὁ (χαράσσω), [in LXX for סֹלְלָה, מָצוֹר, etc. ;] __1. a pointed stake __2. a palisade or rampart: Luk.19:43.† (AS)

charis.notes: χάρις, -ιτος, ἡ Abbott-Smith does not include the article here., accusative, χάριν (χάριτα in Act.24:27, Ju 4; see WH, App., 157), [in LXX chiefly for חֵן ;] __1. objectively, that which causes favourable regard,…

charisma.notes: χάρισμα, -τος, τό (χαρίζομαι) [in LXX: Sir.7:33 א (AB, χάρις) Sir.38:30 Bl (א AB2R, χρῖσμα) * ;] a gift of grace, a free gift, esp. of extra­ordinary operations of the Spirit in the Apostolic Church, but including…

charitoo.notes: χαριτόω, -ῶ (χάρις), [in LXX: Sir.18:17 (ἀνδρί κεχαριτωμένῳ; Vg., justificato; Syr., saintly) * ;] to endow with χάρις (which see), i.e. __1. (a) to make graceful; __(b) to make gracious (Sir, l.with). __2. In…

charizo.notes: χαρίζομαι (χάρις), [in LXX: Est.8:7 (נָתַן), Sng.1:4 א, Sir.12:3, 2 -4Ma.9:1-32 * ;] __1. to show favour or kindness: with dative of person(s), Gal.3:18. __2. to give freely, bestow: with accusative and dative,…

charran.notes: χαρράν indecl. (in FlJ., Ant., i, 16, 1, Κάρρα, -ας, ib. 6, 5, Χαρρά, -ᾶς; so LXX: Gen.29:4 E, Eze.27:28 B, elsewhere -άν­Heb. חָרָן), Haran, a town in N.W. Mesopotamia: Act.7:2, 4.† (AS)

chartes.notes: χάρτης, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Isa.8:1 A, Jer.36:2, 6 36:23 (מְגִלָּה) * ;] a sheet of paper, made of papyrus strips (see Kennedy, Sources, 42; Milligan, NTD, 10-12; DB, iv, 945 f.): II Jn 12 (Plat., Inscr.).† (AS)

chasma.notes: χάσμα, -τος, τό (χάσκω, to yawn), [in LXX: 2Ki.18:17 (פַּחַת)* ;] a chasm, wide space: Luk.16:26.† (AS)

cheilos.notes: χεῖλος, -ους, τό, genitive pl., -εων (see Bl., § 8, 4; Thackeray, Gr., I, 151), [in LXX chiefly for שָׂפָה ;] a lip (as in Heb., of the lip as the organ of speech): Mat.15:8, Mrk.7:6, Rom.3:13, 1Co.14:21, Heb.13:15,…

cheimarros.notes: χείμμαρος (shortened form of the more usual -οος, Attic contr., -ους), -ον (χεῖμα, ῥέω) [in LXX chiefly for נַחַל ;] winter-flowing; as subst., ὁ χ. (sc. ποταμός), a torrent: Jhn.18:1.† (AS)

cheimazo.notes: χειμάζω (χεῖμα, winter cold) [in LXX: Pro.26:10 (†) * ;] __1. to expose to winter cold, go into winter quarters. __2. to drive with storm; pass., to be driven with storm, tempest-tossed: Act.27:18.† (AS)

cheimon.notes: χειμών, -ῶνος, ὁ [in LXX for גֶּשֶׁם, סְתָו ;] __1. winter: Jhn.10:22, 2Ti.4:21; genitive, χειμῶνος, in winter, Mat.24:20, Mrk.13:18. __2. Prop., a winter storm, hence, generally, a storm, tempest: Mat.16:3 (T, WH,…

cheir.notes: χείρ, genitive, χειρος (accusative, χεῖραν, 1Pe.5:6 T), ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for יָד ;] the hand: Mat.3:12, Mrk.3:1, Luk.6:6, al. mult.; ἡ χ., acting subject, Luk.22:21; pl., Act.17:25 20:34, 1Jn.1:1; τ. ἔργα τῶν χ.,…

cheiragogeo.notes: χειραγωγέω, -ῶ (χειραγωγός), [in LXX: Jdg.16:26 A (יָד חָזַק), Tob.11:16 א * ;] to lead by the hand: Act.9:8 22:11.† (AS)

cheiragogos.notes: χειρ-αγωγός, -όν (χείρ, ἄγω) leading by the hand. As subst., ὁ χ., one to lead by the head, a guide: Act.13:11.† (AS)

cheirographos.notes: χειρόγραφος, -ον (χείρ, γράφω), [in LXX: Tob.5:3 9:2, 5 * ;] written with the hand. As subst., τὸ χ., a handwriting (Inscr., Polyb., al.): metaphorically, Col.2:14.† (AS)

cheiron.notes: χείρων, -ον, compar. of κακός [in LXX: 1Ki.17:43 B, Wis.15:18 17:6, 3Ma.5:20 * ;] worse: Mat.9:16 12:45 27:64, Mrk.2:21, Luk.11:26, Jhn.5:14, 1Ti.5:8, Heb.10:29, 2Pe.2:20; εἰς (ἐπὶ) τὸ χεῖρον, Mrk.5:26, 2Ti.3:13.† (AS)

cheiropoietos.notes: χειρο-ποίητος, -ον (χείρ, ποιέω), [in LXX (of idols) for אֱלִיל ;] made by hand (i.e. of human handiwork): of temples, Mrk.14:58, Act.7:48 17:24, Heb.9:11, 24; of circumcision, Eph.2:11.† (AS)

cheirotoneo.notes: χειρο-τονέω, -ῶ (χείρ, τείνω) __1. to vote by stretching out the hand in the Athenian ἐκκλησία (Luc., Plut.). __2. to appoint: __(a) by vote, 2Co.8:19 __(b) without vote, Act.14:23 (see Rackham, in l) (cf.…

cheroubim.notes: Χερουβείν (Rec. -βίμ), τά (Heb. כְּרוּב), Cherubim: Heb.9:6 (cf. Exo.25:17 (18), al.; see DB, i, 377 ff.).† (AS)

chiliarchos.notes: χιλίαρχος (in Hdt., al., -ης), -ου, ὁ (χίλιοι, ἄρχω) [in LXX chiefly for אַלּוּף שַׂר ;] a chiliarch, the commander of a thousand, esp. a Roman military tribune, the commander of a cohort: Jhn.18:12, Act.21:31-33, 37…

chilias.notes: χιλιάς, -άδος, ἡ (χίλιοι), [in LXX for אֶלֶף ;] the number one thousand, a thousand: Luk.14:31, Act.4:4, 1Co.10:8, Rev.5:11 7:4-8 11:13 14:1, 3 21:16.† (AS)

chion.notes: χιών, -όνος, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for שֶׁלֶג also (in Da) for תְּלַג ;] snow: Mat.28:3, Rev.1:14.† (AS)

chios.notes: χίος, -ου, ἡ Chios, an island in Ægean Sea: Act.20:15.† (AS)

chiton.notes: χιτών, ῶνος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for כְּתֹנֶת:] the garment worn next the skin (though two tunics were sometimes worn, see Swete, Mk., 117), a tunic: Mat.10:10, Mrk.6:9 14:63, Luk.3:11 9:3, Ju 23; dieting. from ἱμάτιον…

chlamus.notes: χλαμύς, -ύδος, ἡ [in LXX: 2Ma.12:35 * ;] a chlamys, or short cloak worn over the χιτών (which see): Mat.27:28, 31 (see Tr., Syn., § 1).† (AS)

chleuazo.notes: χλευάζω (χλεύη, a jest), [in LXX: Wis.11:14, 2Ma.7:7, 4Ma.5:22 * ;] to jest, mock, jeer: Act.17:32.† (AS)

chliaros.notes: χλιαρός, -ά, -όν (χλίω, to become warm), warm, tepid; metaphorically, of persons, luke-warm: Rev.3:16.† (AS)

chloros.notes: χλωρός, -ά, -όν (χλόη), [in LXX for יֶרֶק, etc. ;] __(a) pale green: χόρτος, Mrk.6:39, Rev.8:7; πᾶν χ., Rev.9:4; __(b) pale: ἵππος, Rev.6:8.† (AS)

choikos.notes: χοϊκός, -ή, -όν, (χοῦς) earthy, made of dust: 1Co.15:47-49 (see Field, Notes, 179 f.).† (AS)

choinix.notes: χοῖνιξ, -ικος, ἡ [in LXX: Eze.45:10-11 (בַּת) * ;] a chænix, a dry measure of rather less than a quart: Rev.6:6 (EV, measure).† (AS)

cholao.notes: χολάω, -ῶ (χολή), [in LXX: 3Ma.3:1 R * ;] __1. to be melancholy mad (Aristoph.). __2. = χολοῦμαι, to be angry: with dative of person(s), Jhn.7:23.† (AS)

chole.notes: χολή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for רֹאשׁ, לַעֲנָה, מְרֵרָה ;] gall: Mat.27:34 (here prob. = myrrh, cf. Mrk.15:23, see Swete, in l; DCG, i, 634); metaphorically, Act.8:23.† (AS)

choos.notes: χοῦς, -οός accusative, οῦν, ὁ, (contr. from χόος), [in LXX chiefly for עָפָר ;] __1. earth, soil. __2. In later writers (Pint., LXX), = κονιορτός, dust: Mrk.6:11, Rev.18:19.† (AS)

chora.notes: χώρα, -ας, ἡ [in LXX for אֶרֶץ, מְדִינָה, etc. ;] __1. most frequently in cl., a space, place. __2. land, i.e. __(a) a land, country, region: Mat.12:2, Mrk.5:10 6:55, Luk.2:8 15:13-15 19:12, Jhn.11:54, Act.13:49…

chorazin.notes: Χοραζείν (Rec. -ζίν), ἡ, Chorazin, a town of Galilee: Mat.11:21, Luk.10:13.† (AS)

choregeo.notes: χορηγέω, -ῶ (χορός, ἡγέομαι) [in LXX for כּוּל pilp. ;] __1. to lead a χορός (see LS, see word). __2. to defray the cost of a χορός. __3. In late writers, metaphorically, with accusative (see M, Pr., 65), to supply,…

choreo.notes: χωρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Gen.13:6 (נָשָׂא), 3Ki.7:26 7:38 (כּוּל hi.), 2Ch.4:5 (חָזַק hi.), Wis.7:23-24, al. ;] __I. Intrans., __1. to make room, give way, retire, pass: before εἰς, Mat.15:17; metaphorically (EV, come),…

chorion.notes: χωρίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of χώρα, χῶρος), [in LXX: 1Ch.27:27 (כֶּרֶם), 2Ma.11:5 2Mac 12:7 2Mac 12:21, 4Ma.15:20 * ;] __1. a place, region. __2. an estate, property, piece of land: Mat.26:36, Mrk.14:32, Jhn.4:5,…

chorizo.notes: χωρίζω [in LXX: 2Ch.12:8, al. (בָּדַל ni., etc.), Wis.1:3, 2Ma.5:21, al. ;] __1. to separate, divide, put asunder: with accusative of thing(s), opposite to συζεύγνυμι Mat.19:6, Mrk.10:9; with accusative of person(s),…

choros.notes: χορός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for מָחוֹל, מְחֹלָה ;] a dance: pl., Luk.15:25.† (AS)

chortasma.notes: χόρτασμα, -τος, τό (χορτάζω), [in LXX: Gen.24:25, 32 42:27 43:24, Jdg.19:19 (מִסְפּוֹא), Deu.11:16 (עֶשֶׂב), (Sir.33:24) Sir.38:26 * ;] fodder (Polyb., Plut., al.): pl., Act.7:11 (RV, sustenance).† (AS)

chortazo.notes: χορτάζω (χόρτος), [in LXX for שָׂבַע, Psa.17:14 al. ;] __(a) prop., of animals (see Lft. on Php.4:12), to feed, fatten: Rev.19:21; __(b) in late Gk. (Kennedy, Sources, 82, 156), of persons, to fill or satisfy with…

chortos.notes: χόρτος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for עֶשֶׂב, also for חָצִיר, etc. ;] __1. an enclosure, a feeding place (Hom.). __2. food, esp. for cattle, grass: Mat.13:26 14:19, Mrk.4:28 6:39, Luk.12:28, Jhn.6:10, 1Co.3:12,…

chrao.notes: χράομαι, χρῶμαι (χρή), [in LXX for עָשָׂה, etc. ;] to use, make use of: with dative (cf. M, Pr., 64, 158), Act.27:17, 1Co.9:12 9:15, 1Ti.1:8 5:23; μᾶλλον χρῆσαι (i.e. the opportunity; see M, Pr., 247), 1Co.7:21; as…

chre.notes: χρή impers. (χράω, see LS, see word), it is necessary: Jas.3:10 (cf. δεῖ.).† (AS)

chreia.notes: χρεία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: 2Ch.2:16 (15) (צֹרֶךְ), Est.7:20 (חַשְׁחוּת) frequently in Sir.1:1-30 -2 Mac ;] __1. need, necessity: Act.28:10, Tit.3:14; πρὸς οἰκοδομὴν τῆς χ. (RV, txt., for edifying as the need may be; but…

chrema.notes: χρῆμα, -τος, τό (χράομαι) [in LXX: Jos.22:8, 2Ch.1:11-12 (נֶכֶס), Job.27:17 (כֶּסֶף), etc.; frequently in Sir (5:1, 8, al.), 2 and 4 Mac ;] a thing that one uses or needs (and generally, a matter, event, business);…

chrematismos.notes: χρηματισμός, -οῦ), ὁ (χρηματίζω, which see), [in LXX: Pro.31:1 (מַשָּׂא), 2Ma.2:4 2Mac 11:17 * ;] a divine response, an oracle: Rom.11:4 (Xen., Plat., al.).† (AS)

chrematizo.notes: χρηματίζω (χρῆμα), [in LXX: Jer.26:2 29:23 30:2 36:2, 4 A (דָבַר pi.), Jer.25:30) (שָׁאַג), 3Ki.18:27, Job.40:8 * ;] __1. to transact business, hence, to consult, deliberate (Thuc., Dem., al.); hence in later…

chreopheiletes.notes: χρεοφειλέτης (Rec. χρεω-, WH, χρεοφιλ-; see WH, App., 152, 154), -ου, ὁ, (χρέος, a debt, + ὀφειλέτης) [in LXX: Job.31:37, Pro.29:13 * ;] a debtor: Luk.7:41 16:5.† (AS)

chresimos.notes: χρήσιμος, -η, -ον (χράομαι), [in LXX: Gen.37:26 בֶּצַע etc. ;] useful: 2Ti.2:14.† (AS)

chresis.notes: χρῆσις, -εως, ἡ (χράομαι), [in LXX: 1Ki.1:28 (שָׁאַל), Sir.18:8, al. ;] use: in a sexual sense, Rom.1:26; with genitive obj., θηλείας, Rom.1:27 (for exx., see Thayer, see word).† (AS)

chrestologia.notes: χρηστολογία, -ας, ἡ (χρηστός, λέγω), fair speaking: in bad sense, Rom.16:18 (in good sense also, eccl.).† (AS)

chrestos.notes: χρηστός, -ή, -όν (χράομαι), [in LXX chiefly for טוֹב (frequently of God: Psa.25:8, al.), also for יָקָר (Eze.27:22 28:13), יָשָׁר (Pro.2:21 א A) ;] serviceable, good; __(a) of things, good, pleasant: of food (as…

chrestotes.notes: χρηστότης, -ητος, ἡ (χρηστός), [in LXX for טוּב and cogn. forms ;] __1. goodness, excellence, uprightness: Rom.3:12 (LXX). __2. goodness of heart, kindness: Rom.2:4, 2Co.6:6, Gal.5:22, Col.3:12, Tit.3:4; before ἐπί,…

chrezo.notes: χρῄζω (χρή) [in LXX Jdg.11:7, 1Ki.17:18 A * ;] to need, have need of: with genitive of thing(s), Mat.6:32, Luk.11:8 12:30, 2Co.3:1; with genitive of person(s) before ἐν, Rom.16:2.† (AS)

chrio.notes: χρίω to anoint (Hom., al.); [in LXX chiefly for מָשַׁח of consecration to a sacred office: priest, Exo.28:41; prophet, 3Ki.19:16; king, 1Ki.10:1; of things, Exo.40:9, Lev.8:10, al.]. In NT, metaphorically, of God's…

chrisma.notes: χρίσμα (T, χρῖσμα, as in cl.; see Tdf., Pr., 102; Bl., § 4, 2), -τος, το (χρίω), later form of χρῖμα (Æsch., al.), [in LXX for מִשְׁחָה and cogn. forms (Exo.29:7, al.) ;] an anointing, unction (the result of the…

christianos.notes: Χριστιανός (D, Χρεισ-; on the form Χρησ-, see Bl., § 3, 6; 27, 4 on the ending, -ανος, see Bl., ll. with; Deiss., LAE, 382), -οῦ ὁ, a Christian, the name first given to the disciples by pagan gentiles at Antioch:…

christos.notes: χριστός (Χρ-), -ή, -όν (χρίω), [in LXX for מָשִׁיחַ and cogn. forms ;] __1. 1. as adj., __(a) of things, anointing, to be used as ointment (Æsch., Eur., al.; τ. ἔλαιον τὸ χ., Lev.21:10); __(b) of persons, anointed (ὁ…

chronizo.notes: χρονίζω (χρόνος), [in LXX chiefly for אָחַר pi. ;] to spend or take time, to tarry, linger, delay: Mat.24:48 25:5, Heb.10:37; before ἐν, with dative loc., Luk.1:21; with inf., Luk.12:45.† (AS)

chronos.notes: χρόνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for יוֹם, also for עֵת, etc. ;] time (a space of time, whether long or short; cf. Lft., Notes, 70): Mat.2:7, Mrk.9:21, Luk.1:57, Act.3:21 7:17, 23 13:18 17:30 27:9, Heb.11:32, 1Pe.1:17…

chronotribeo.notes: χρονοτριβέω, -ῶ (χρόνος, τρίβω), to spend time: Act.20:16.† (AS)

chros.notes: χρώς, genitive, χρωτός, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for בָּשָׂר ;] in cl. rare in prose, the surface of the body, skin: Act.19:12.† (AS)

chruseos.notes: Χρύσεος, -εα, -εον (-οῦς, -ῆ, -οῦν; on accusative sing. fem., -ᾶν, Rev.1:13 and genitive pl. uncontr., -εων, Rev.2:1, LTr., see M, Pr., 48 and cf. Thackeray, Gr., x, 172 f.) (χρυσός), [in LXX chiefly for זָהָב ;]…

chrusion.notes: χρυσίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of χρυσός, which see), [in LXX chiefly for זָהָב ;] a piece of gold, gold: 1Co.3:12, Heb.9:4, 1Pe.1:7, Rev.3:18 21:18, 21; of golden ornaments, 1Ti.2:9 (WH, txt., RV), 1Pe.3:3, Rev.17:4 18:16…

chrusodaktulios.notes: χρυσο-δακτύλιος, -ον with a gold ring: Jas.2:2.† (AS)

chrusolithos.notes: χρυσό-λιθος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Exo.28:20 36:20 (Exo.39:13), Eze.28:13 (תַּרְשִׁישׁ) * ;] a chrysolite (on its identification, see Swete, Ap., 288 f.; DB, iv, 620): Rev.21:20.† (AS)

chrusoo.notes: χρυσόω, -ῶ (χρυσός) [in LXX chiefly for צָפָה pi. ;] to gild, cover with gold: pass., with dative, χρυσίῳ (pleonast., cf. Exo.26:37), Rev.17:4 18:16.† (AS)

chrusoprasos.notes: χρυσό-πρασος, -ου, ὁ a chrysoprase (see Swete, Ap., 289): Rev.21:20.† (AS)

chrusos.notes: χρυσός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for זָהָב ;] gold: Mat.2:11, Rev.9:7; of golden ornaments, Mat.23:16-17, 1Ti.2:9 (Rec., WH, mg.), Jas.5:3, Rev.17:4 (Rec., WH, mg.) Rev.18:12, 16 (Rec., WH, mg.); of images, Act.17:29;…

chthes.notes: ἐχθές (Rec. χθές), adv., [in LXX for תְּמוֹל, etc. ;] yesterday: Jhn.4:52, Act.7:28, Heb.13:8 (on the form, see Rutherford, NPhr., 370 f.).† ἐχθές, adverb ={χθές}, yesterday, ( 5th-6th c.BC: Aristophanes Comicus…

daimon.notes: δαίμων, -ονος, ὁ, ἡ [in LXX for גַּד, Isa.65:11 (א; δαιμονίῳ, AB) * ;] in cl. and NT = δαιμόνιον; a demon: Mat.8:31.† (AS)

daimoniodes.notes: δαιμονιώδης, -ες (δαιμόνιον, εἶδος), [in Sm.: Ps 90(91):6 * ;] demon-like: Jas.3:15 (Cremer, 171).† (AS)

daimonion.notes: δαιμόνιον, -ου, τό (neut. of δαιμόνιος, -α, -ον, divine), [in LXX (so also in π; see MM, Exp., x) for שֵׁד, אֱלִיל, (frequently in To) ;] __1. as in cl.; __(a) the Divine power, Deity (Hdt., Plat., al.); __(b) an…

daimonizomai.notes: δαιμονίζομαι (in cl. also δαιμονάω; δαίμων), [in Aq.: Ps 90(91):6 * ;] to be under the power of a δαίμων (δαιμόνιον, which see), to be possessed: Mat.15:22; elsewhere always ptcp., -ιζόμενος, -ισθείς: Mat.4:24; 8:16,…

dakno.notes: δάκνω [in LXX for נשׁךְ, Gen.49:17, al., exc. Deu.8:15 (שׂרף) ;] to bite: metaphorically, Gal.5:15.† (AS)

dakrudakruon.notes: δάκρυον (poët. form δάκρυ), -ου, τό, [in LXX for דִּמְעָה ;] a tear: Mrk.9:24 (WH, txt., R, txt. omit), Act.20:19, 31, 2Co.2:4, 2Ti.1:4, Heb.5:7 12:17, Rev.7:17 21:4; metaphorically, dative pl., δάκρυσι (La 2:11,…

dakruo.notes: δακρύω [in LXX for בָּכָה, etc. ;] to weep, shed tears: Jhn.11:35.† SYN.: κλαίω, of audible weeping, to cry; ὀδύρομαι, of grief expressed verbally, to lament; θρηνέω, of formal lamentation, to sing a dirge; ἀλαλάζω,…

daktulios.notes: δακτύλιος, -ου, ὁ (δάκτυλος), [in LXX chiefly for טַבַּעַת ;] a ring: Luk.15:22.† (AS)

daktulos.notes: δάκτυλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for אֶצְבַּע ;] a finger: Mat.23:4, Mrk.7:33, Luk.11:46 16:24, Jhn.8:6, 8 20:25, 27; metaphorically, δ. θεοῦ (cf. Exo.8:19), Luk.11:20.† (AS)

dalmanoutha.notes: Δαλμανουθά, ἡ indecl., Dalmanutha, an unidentified place near the Sea of Galilee: Mrk.8:10 (cf. Μαγαδάν).† (AS)

dalmatia.notes: Δαλματία (L, Δελ-), -ας, ἡ Dalmatia, a part of Illyria on E. coast of the Adriatic: 2Ti.4:10.† (AS)

damalis.notes: δάμαλις, -εως, ἡ (fem. of δαμάλης, a bullock), [in LXX chiefly for פָּרָה, עֶגְלָה ;] a heifer, Heb.9:13.† (AS)

damaris.notes: Δάμαρις, -ιδος, ἡ (δάμαρ, poët., a wife), Damaris: Act.17:34.† (AS)

damaskos.notes: Δαμασκός, -οῦ, ἡ (Heb. דַּמֶּשֶׂק), Damascus: Act.9.2ff. 22:5ff.26.12,20, 2Co.11:32, Gal.1:17.† (AS)

damazo.notes: δαμάζω [in LXX: Dan.2:40 (חֲשַׁל) * ;] to tame, subdue: Mrk.5:4, Jas.3:7; metaphorically, τ. γλῶσσαν, Jas.3:8 (Field, Notes, 237f.).† (AS)

daneion.notes: δάνιον, -ου, τό (late form of δάνειον, Rec.; see: δανίζω), [in LXX: Deu.24:11 (נָשָׁה) 15:8, 10 (עָבַט hi.), 4Ma.2:8 * ;] a loan: Mat.18:27.† (AS)

daneistes.notes: δανιστής, -οῦ, ὁ (late form of -ειστής, Rec.; see: δανείζω), [in LXX: 4Ki.4:1, Psa.108(109):11 (נשׁה), Pro.29:13 (רָשׁ), Sir.29:28 * ;] a money-lender: Luk.7:41.† (AS)

daneizo.notes: δανίζω (late form of cl. -είζω, Rec., Bl., § 3; Thackeray, Gr., 85f.) [in LXX chiefly for לוה (Pro.19:17) ;] to lend money on interest: Luk.6:34, 35; mid., to borrow: Mat.5:42.† SYN.: κίχρημι (see: χράω), to lend in…

daniel.notes: Δανιήλ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. דָּנִיֵּאל, God is my Judge), Daniel, the prophet: Mat.24:15.† (AS)

dapanao.notes: δαπανάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Tob.1:7, 1Ma.14:32, al. ;] __1. to spend, expend: with accusative, Mrk.5:26; before ἐπί, with dative of person(s), Act.21:24; ὑπέρ, 2Co.12:15. __2. to consume, squander: Luk.15:14; ἐν τ. ἡδσναῖς…

dapane.notes: δαπάνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Est.6:4, 8 (נִפְקָא), Dan LXX Bel 1:21, al. ;] expense, cost: Luk.14:28.† (AS)

daueiddauiddabid.notes: Δαυείδ (Rec. Δαβίδ), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. דָּוִד), David, King of Israel: Mat.1:6; 12:3, and al.; σκηνὴ Δ., Act.15:16; κλεὶς Δ., Rev.3:7; θρόνος Δ., Luk.1:32; ῥίζα Δ., Rev.5:5; βασιλεία Δ., Mrk.11:10; υἱὸς Δ., the…

dechomai.notes: δέχομαι, depon. mid., [in LXX chiefly for לקח ;] to receive, accept; __1. with accusative of thing(s), of taking or accepting what is offered: γράμματα, Luk.16:6, 7; id. in different sense, Act.28:21; ποτήριον,…

deesis.notes: δέησις, -εως, ἡ (δέομαι), [in LXX for תְּחִנָּה, רִנָּה, תְּפִלָּה, etc. ;] __1. a wanting, need (so Ps 21(22):25). __2. an asking, entreaty, supplica­tion; in NT always addressed to God: Luk.1:13, 2Co.1:11,…

dei.notes: δεῖ impersonal (δέω), [in LXX chiefly for infin. with לְ ;] one must, it is necessary: with inf., Mat.26:54, Mrk.13:7, Act.5:29, al.; with accusative and inf., Mat.16:21, Mrk.8:31, Jhn.3:7, Act.25:10, al.; with…

deigma.notes: δεῖγμα, -τος, τό (δείκνυμι); __1. (cl.) a thing shown, a specimen. __2. = cl. παράδ- (cf. ὑπόδ-, 2Pe.2:6), an example (a warning): Ju 7.† (AS)

deigmatizo.notes: δειγματίζω (δεῖγμα), a rare word (ICC, ll. with), to make a show of, to expose: Mat.1:19 (Rec. παραδ-, which see), Col.2:15.† (AS)

deiknuo.notes: δείκνυμι, δεικνύω (see Bl., § 23, 1; Veitch, see word), [in LXX chiefly for רָאָה hi. ;] to show, with accusative of thing(s) (of person(s)), dative of person(s); __(a) to show, exhibit: Mat.4:8 8:4, Mrk.1:44 14:15,…

deilia.notes: δειλία, -ας, ἡ (δειλός), [in LXX for אֵימָה, מְחִתָּה, etc. ;] cowardice, timidity (never in good sense): 2Ti.1:7.† SYN.: φόβος, fear, in general, good or bad; εὐλάβεια (which see), apprehension generally, but…

deiliao.notes: δειλιάω, -ῶ (δειλία), [in LXX for חתת ni., פּחד, etc. ;] = the more frequently ἀποδ-, to be cowardly, timid, fearful: Jhn.14:27.† (AS)

deilos.notes: δειλός, -ή, όν (δέος), [in LXX for רַךְ, etc. ;] cowardly, fearful: Mat.8:26, Mrk.4:40, Rev.21:8.† (AS)

deina.notes: δεῖνα, ὁ, ἡ, τό (genitive -νος, dative -νι, accusative -να), [in Aq.: Rut.4:1, 1Ki.21:2 (3) (Sm. also), 4Ki.6:8 * ;] such an one, a certain one, whom one cannot or will not name: Mat.26:18.† (AS)

deinos.notes: δεινῶς , adv. (δέος), [in LXX: Job.10:16, Wis.17:3, al. ;] __1. terribly: Mat.8:6. __2. vehemently: Luk.11:53.† (AS)

deipneo.notes: δειπνέω, -ῶ (δεῖπνον, which see), [in LXX: Pro.23:1 (לחם), Tob.7:8 8:1, Dan LXX 11:27 * ;] to take the chief meal of the day, to dine, to sup: Luk.17:8 22:20 (WH, br., R, mg. omits), 1Co.11:25; metaphorically,…

deipnon.notes: δεῖπνον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for פַּת־בַּג (Da) ;] the chief meal of the day, dinner, supper: Mat.23:6, Mrk.12:39, Luk.14:17, 24 20:46, Jhn.13:2, 4 21:20, 1Co.11:21; δ. ποιεῖν, Mrk.6:21, Luk.14:12, 16, Jhn.12:2;…

deisidaimon.notes: δεισι-δαίμων, -ον (δείδω, to fear; δαίμων, deity) reverent to the deity, religious; compar. -μονεστέρους (AV, too superstitious, R, txt., somewhat superstitious, a sense in wh. the word is sometimes used; cf. Field,…

deisidaimonia.notes: δεισιδαιμονία, -ας, ἡ (δεισιδαίμων, which see); __1. fear of the gods; __(a) piety, religion; __(b) superstition. __2. Objectively, a religion: Act.25:19 (Cremer, 72, 682).† (AS)

deka.notes: δέκα, οἱ, αἱ, τά, ten: Mat.20:24, al.; θλίψις ἡμερῶν δ., i.e. of brief duration: Rev.2:10.† δεκα-οκτώ, T for δέκα ὀκτώ, eighteen: Luk.13:4.† (AS)

dekapente.notes: δεκα-πέντε [in LXX: Exo.27:15, 1Ma.10:40, al. ;] late form of πεντεκαίδεκα, fifteen: Jhn.11:18, Act.27:5, WH, mg., ib. 28, Gal.1:18.† (AS)

dekapolis.notes: Δεκά-πολις, -εως, ὴ Decapolis, a region east of the Jordan con­taining ten cities: Mat.4:25, Mrk.5:20 7:31.† (AS)

dekate.notes: δεκάτη, -ης, ἡ prop. fem. (sc. μέρος) of δέκατος, -η, -ον, [in LXX for מַעֲשֵׂר: Gen.14:20, Neh.12:44, and al. ;] a tenth part, a tithe: Heb.7:2, 4; 8, 9 (for a curious inversion of the Biblical use, see MM, see…

dekatessares.notes: δεκατέσσαρες, -ων, oἱ, αἱ, -α, τά [in LXX: Gen.31:41, Tob.8:19, al. ;] = cl. τεσσαρεσκαίδεκα, more frequently in later Gk. than the older form and in MGr. (for thirteen and upwards) universal, fourteen: Mat.1:17,…

dekatoo.notes: δεκατόω, -ῶ, = cl. -τεύω (δεκάτη), [in LXX: Neh.10:37 (38) (עשׂר pi.) * ;] to take tithe of: with accusative of person(s), Heb.7:6; pass. to pay tithe: Heb.7:9.† (AS)

dekatos.notes: δέκατος, -η, -ον (δέκα), [in LXX for מַעֲשֵׂר and cognate forms ;] tenth: Jhn.1:40, Act.19:9 (WH, txt., RV omit), Rev.11:13; 21:20.† (AS)

dektos.notes: δεκτός, -ή, -όν verbal adj. of δέχομαι, [in LXX chiefly for רָצוֹן: Pro.11:1, al. ;] acceptable: Luk.4:19 (LXX) 24, Act.10:35, 2Co.6:2" (LXX), Php.4:18 (cf. εὐπροσδ-).† (AS)

deleazo.notes: δελεάζω (δέλεαρ, a bait), to allure by a bait: metaphorically, to allure, entice: with accusative, 2Pe.2:14, 18; pass., Jas.1:14.† (AS)

deloo.notes: δηλόω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for ידע ;] to make plain, declare: with accusative, 1Co.3:13, Col.1:8, Heb.9:8 12:27; with dative of person(s), 2Pe.1:14; with dative of person(s), before περί (pass.), 1Co.1:11; before εἰς,…

delos.notes: δῆλος, -η, -ον [in LXX for אוּר, etc. ;] __1. visible. __2. clear to the mind, evident: Mat.26:73; δ. (sc. ἐστίν), before ὅτι, 1Co.15:27, Gal.3:11.† SYN.: φανερός, with ref. to outward appearance, manifest as…

demas.notes: Δημᾶς, ὁ (perh. contracted from Δημήτριος), Demas, a companion of St. Paul: Col.4:14, Phm 24, 2Ti.4:10.† (AS)

demegoreo.notes: δημηγορέω, -ῶ (δῆμος, ἀγορεύω, to speak in the assembly), [in LXX: Pro.24:66 (30:31), 4Ma.5:15 * ;] to deliver an oration: Act.12:21.† (AS)

demetrios.notes: Δημήτριος, -ου, ὁ Demetrius; __1. a silversmith, Act.19:24 38. __2. A Christian disciple, III Jn 12.† (AS)

demiourgos.notes: δημιουργός, -οῦ, ὑ (δῆμος, ἔργον), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:1 * ;] __1. one who works for the people. __2. Univ., an author, builder, maker; the maker of the world (Xen., Mem., I, 4, 9, al.), Heb.11:10.† SYN.: κτίστης,…

demos.notes: δῆμος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for מִשְׁפָּחָה ;] __1. a district, country. __2. the common people, the people generally; esp. the people assembled: Act.12:22 17:5 19:30 33.† SYN.: λαός, the people at large: δ., the…

demosios.notes: δημόσιος, -α, -ον (δῆμος), [in LXX: 2Ma.6:10, 3Ma.2:27 4:7 * ;] belonging to the people, public: Act.5:18; dative fem. used adverbially (cl.); __(a) at the public expense, by public consent; __(b) publicly: Act.16:37…

denarion.notes: δηνάριον, -ου, τό the Lat. denarius, a Roman coin, nearly equal to the δραχμή, which see: Mat.18:28 20:2, 9, 13 22:19, Mrk.6:37 12:15 14:5, Luk.7:41 10:35 20:24, Jhn.6:7 12:5, Rev.6:6; τὸ ἀνὰ δ., Mat.20:10.† (AS)

dendron.notes: δένδρον, -ου, τό [in LXX for עֵץ, etc. ;] a tree: Mat.3:10, al.; δ. ἀγαθόν, Mat.7:17, 18; δ. καλόν, Mat.12:33, Luk.6:43; δ. σαπρόν, Mat.7:17, 18 12:33, Luk.6:43; γίνεσθαι δ., Mat.13:32; γ. εἰς δ., Luk.13:19. (AS)

deo.notes: δέω (I), [in LXX chiefly for אסר ;] to tie, bind, fasten; __(1) with accusative of thing(s), before εἰς δεσμάς, Mat.13:30; of an ass, Mat.21:2, Mrk.11:2, 4 Luk.19:30. __(2) with accusative of person(s), of swathing a…

deomai.notes: δέομαι (mid. of δέω, II, which see, as depon.) [in LXX for חנן, etc. ;] to want for oneself; __1. to want, need: __(a) absol.; __(b) with genitive __2. to beg, request, beseech, pray; __(i) in general: absol.,…

derbe.notes: Δέρβη, -ης, ἡ, Derbe, a city of Lycaonia: Act.14:6, 20 16:1.† (AS)

dermatinos.notes: δερμάτινος, -η, -ον (δέρμα), [in LXX for עוֹר ;] of skin, leathern: Mat.3:4, Mrk.1:6 (cf. 4Ki.1:8).† (AS)

dero.notes: δέρω [in LXX: Lev.1:6, 2Ch.29:34; 35:11 (פָּשַׁט hi.)* ;] __1. to skin, flay. __2. (of. Eng. slang, hide) to beat, thrash: with accusative, Mat.21:35, Mrk.12:3, 5, Luk.20:10, 11 22:63, Jhn.18:23, Act.5:40 16:37…

desme.notes: δέσμη (Rec. δεσμή), -ης, ἡ (δέω), [in LXX for אֲגֻדָּה, Exo.12:22 * ;] a bundle: δήσατε αὐτὰ εἰς δέσμας (D, Orig., omit εἰς, and Blass thinks original reading, δέσμας δέσμας; see Deiss., LAE, 125.4), Mat.13:30.† (AS)

desmeuo.notes: δεσμεύω (δεσμός), [in LXX for אָסַר (Jdg.16:11, al.), אָלַם pi. (Gen.37:7, al.), etc. ;] __1. to put in chains: Luk.8:29, Act.22:4. __2. to bind, tie together: φορτία, Mat.23:4.† (AS)

desmios.notes: δέσμιος, -ον (also -α, -ον; δεσμός), [in LXX for אסר;] __1. binding. __2. bound, captive; ὁ δ., as subst., a prisoner: Mat.27:15, 16, Mrk.15:6, Act.16:25, 27 23:18 25:14, 27 28:16 (Rec.) 17, Heb.10:34 13:3; ὁ δ. τοῦ…

desmophulax.notes: δεσμο-φύλαξ, -κος, ὁ (δεσμός, φύλαξ), a prison-keeper, gaoler: Act.16:23, 27, 36 (cf. ἀρχιδ-, Gen.39:21).† (AS)

desmos.notes: δεσμός, -οῦ, ὁ (δέω), [in LXX chiefly for אסר ;] a band, bond: metaphorically, Luk.13:16; ὁ δεσμὸς τ. γλώσσης, Mrk.7:35 (for this expression in π., see Deiss., LAE, 306 ff.; of actual bonds, see Ruth., Gr., 9). Pl.,…

desmoterion.notes: δεσμωτήριον, -ου, τό [in LXX for בֵּית סֹהַר (Ge), אָסִיר ;] a prison: Mat.11:2, Act.5:21, 23 16:26.† (AS)

desmotes.notes: δεσμώτης, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for אסר, מַסְגֵּר ;] a prisoner: Act.27:1, 42.† (AS)

despotes.notes: δεσπότης, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for אָדוֹן, אֲדֹנָי; in Jdth.9:17, δ. τ. οὐρανῶν κ. τ. γῆς]; a master, lord, correlative of δοῦλος, οἰκέτης: 1Ti.6:1, 2, 2Ti.2:21, Tit.2:9, 1Pe.2:18; as title of God, voc., δέσποτα…

deuteraios.notes: δευτεραῖος, -α, -ον (δεύτερος), adj. with adverbial sense; on the second day: δευτεραῖοι ἤλθομεν, Act.28:13.† (AS)

deuteros.notes: δεῦτερος, -α, -ον, second in order, with or without idea of time: Mat.22:26, 39, al.; θάνατος, Rev.2:11 20:14 21:8; χάρις, 2Co.1:15. In neut. as adv., secondly, a second time; opposite to πρῶτον: Jhn.3:4 21:16,…

dexiolabos.notes: δεξιολάβος, -ου, ὁ (δεξιός, λαμβάνω), a kind of soldier, prob. a spearman (Vg., lancearius) or slinger: Act.23:23.† δεξιο-βόλος, -ου, ὁ (δεξιός, βάλλω), one who throws with the right hand: L for δεξιολάβος,…

dexios.notes: δεξιός, -ά, -όν [in LXX chiefly for יָמִין ;] the right: Mat.5:29, Jhn.18:10, al.; ὅπλα δ., weapons carried in the right hand, i.e. for offence, 2Co.6:7 ; ἡ δ. χείρ, Mat.5:30, Luk.6:6, al.; ἡ δ. (sc. χείρ), Mat.6:3,…

diabaino.notes: δια-βαίνω [in LXX chiefly for עבר ;] __1. to make a stride. __2. to step across, cross over; __(a) trans.: τ. θάλασσαι, Heb.11:29; __(b) intrans.: before εἰς, Act.16:9; πρός, Luk.16:26.† (AS)

diaballo.notes: δια-βάλλω [in LXX: Dan LXX 3:8, Da TH 3:8; 6:24 (25) (אֲכַל קְרַץ), 2Ma.3:11, 4Ma.4:1 * ;] __1. to throw across. __2. To slander, defame, accuse falsely or maliciously: Luk.16:1 (Cremer, 120).† (AS)

diabebaioo.notes: δια-βεβαιόομαι, -οῦμαι to affirm confidently: Tit.3:8; before περί, 1Ti.1:7 (Cremer, 140).† (AS)

diablepo.notes: δια-βλέπω __1. to took straight before one. __2. to see clearly: Mat.7:5, Mrk.8:25, Luk.6:42 (cf. διάβλεψις, Aq., Isa.61:1, for LXX ἀνάβ-).† (AS)

diabolos.notes: διάβολος, -ον (διαβάλλω, which see) [in LXX for שָׂטָן (as Job.1:6), exc. Est.7:4 8:1 (צַר, צָרַר) ;] slanderous, accusing falsely. As subst., ὁ, ἡ, δ.; __(a) generally (of. Es, ll. with), a slanderer, false accuser:…

diacheirizo.notes: δια-χειρίζω (χείρ), to have in hand, conduct, manage. Mid. __(a) = act.; __(b) in late writers (Polyb., FlJ, al.), to lay hands on, kill: with accusative, Act.5:30 26:21.† (AS)

diachorizo.notes: δια-χωρίζω [in LXX: Gen.1:4 ff (בָּדַל hi.), Gen.13:9 ff. (פָּרַד ni.), etc. ;] to separate entirely. Mid., to separate oneself, depart: before ἀπό Luk.9:33.† (AS)

diadechomai.notes: δια-δέχομαι [in LXX for מִשְׁנֶה (Deiss., BS, 115), פַּרְבָּר ;] to receive through another, receive in turn: Act.7:45 (RV, in their turn; see Field, Notes, 116).† (AS)

diadema.notes: διάδημα, -τος, τό (διαδέω, to bind round), [in LXX for כֶּתֶר (as Est.1:11), etc. ;] the band round the τιάρα of a Persian king; a diadem, the badge of royalty: Rev.12:3 13:1 19:12.† SYN.: στέφανος, the badge of…

diadidomi.notes: δια-διδωμι [in LXX for חלק pi., etc. ;] __1. to hand over, deliver: Rev.17:13, Rec. __2. to distribute: Luk.11:22 18:22, Jhn.6:11, Act.4:35† (AS)

diadochos.notes: διά-δοχος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ (διαδέχομαι), [in LXX: 1Ch.18:17 (לְיַד), 2Ch.26:11 (שַׂר) 28:7 (מִשְׁנֶה), Sir.46:1 48:8, 2Ma.4:29 14:26 * ;] a successor: Act.24:27 (for usage in LXX and π. in sense of court official, see…

diaginomai.notes: δια-γίνομαι (Ion. and late Gk. for διαγίγν-), [in LXX, 2Ma.11:26 * ;] __1. to go through, to pass, e.g. τ. νύκτα; absol., to live. __2. Of time, to intervene, elapse: ptcp., with ἡμερῶν τινῶν, Act.25:13; ἱκανοῦ…

diaginosko.notes: δια-γινώσκω (see previous word), [in LXX chiefly for ידע, and cf. 2Ma.9:15 ;] __1. to distinguish, ascertain exactly: Act.23:15 __2. As Athen. law-term, to determine: τὰ καθ᾽ ὑμᾶς, your case, Act.24:22 (Cremer,…

diagnosis.notes: διά-γνωσις, -εως, ἡ (διαγιγνώσκω), [in LXX: Wis.3:18 * ;] __1. a distinguishing, also as medical term. __2. As law-term (Lat. cognitio), determination, decision: Act.25:21 (Cremer, 674).† (AS)

diago.notes: δι-άγω [in LXX for עבר hi., etc. ;] __1. to carry over. __2. Of time, to pass: βίον, 1Ti.2:2; absol., to live, before ἐν, Tit.3:3.† (AS)

diagonguzo.notes: δια-γογγύζω [in LXX chiefly for לין, לון, as Exo.16:2, and cf. Si 34(31):24 ;] of a number, to murmur or mutter among themselves: Luk.15:2 19:7.† (AS)

diagregoreo.notes: δια-γρηγορέω, -ῶ __(a) prop., to remain awake (R, mg.); __(b) to be fully awake (R, txt.): Luk.9:32.† (AS)

diaireo.notes: δι-αιρέω, -ῶ [in LXX for בּתר (as Gen.15:10), חלק (as Jos.18:5), etc. ;] __1. to divide into parts, cut asunder. __2. to distribute: with accusative of thing(s), dative of person(s), Luk.15:12, 1Co.12:11.† (AS)

diairesis.notes: δι-αίρεσις, -εως, ἡ (διαιρέω), [in LXX chiefly for מַחֲלֹקֶת ;] __1. a distinction, diference. __2. a division, distribution: 1Co.12:4-6 (cf. διαιρέω; Cremer, 616).† (AS)

diakatharizo.notes: δια-καθαρίζω = θαίρω: Mat.3:12.† δια-καθαίρω, to cleanse thoroughly: Luk.3:17.† (AS)

diakoluo.notes: δια-κωλεύω [in LXX: Jdth.4:7 12:7 * ;] to hinder, prevent: with accusative, Mat.3:14.† (AS)

diakoneo.notes: διακονέω, ῶ (δίακονος); __1. generally, to minister, serre, wait upon, especially at table, to do one a service, cure for one's needs: absol., Mat.20:28, Mrk.10:45, Luk.10:40 22:26, 27, Jhn.12:2, 1Pe.4:11; ὅσα…

diakonia.notes: διακονία, -ας, ἡ (διάκονος), [in LXX for נַעַר, שָׁרַת pi.: Est.6:3, Est.6:5 A; 1Ma.11:58 * ;] the office and work of a διάκονος, service, ministry; __(a) of domestic duties (Field, Notes, 63): Luk.10:40 __(b) spec.…

diakonos.notes: διάκονος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ (derivation unknown), [in LXX for נַעַר, שָׁרַת pi.: Est.6:3, 5, א B Est.1:10 2:2, Pro.10:4, 4Ma.9:17 * ;] __1. in general, a servant, attendant, minister: Mat.20:26 22:13 23:11, Mrk.9:35 10:43,…

diakosioi.notes: διακόσιοι, -αι, -α two hundred: Mrk.6:37, and al. (AS)

diakouo.notes: δι-ακούω [in LXX: Deu.1:16 (שׁמע), Job.9:33 (יָד שִׁית hi.)* ;] to hear through, hear fully; technically, to hear judicially (as De, l.with; of. Deiss., BS, 230): Act.23:35.† (AS)

diakrino.notes: δια-κρίνω [in LXX for שׁפט, דִּין, etc. ;] __1. to separate, hence, to distinguish, discriminate, discern: μηδὲν δ., Act.11:12; οὐδὲν δ. μεταξύ, Act.15:9; σε, 1Co.4:7; τὸ σῶμα, 1Co.11:29. __2. to settle, decide,…

diakrisis.notes: διά-κρισις, -εως, ἡ (διακρίνω) [in LXX for מִפְלָשׂ, Job.37:16 * ;] the act of judgment, discernment: Rom.14:1, 1Co.12:10, Heb.5:14.† (AS)

dialaleo.notes: δια-λαλέω, -ῶ [in Sm.: Ps 50(51):16, and al. ;] __1. to talk with: πρός, Luk.6:11. __2. to talk over: pass., Luk.1:65.† (AS)

dialego.notes: δια-λέγομαι (mid. of διαλέγω, to pick out, distinguish, as depon.), [in LXX: Exo.6:27, Isa.63:1 (דּבר pi.), Jdg.8:1 (ריב), 1Es.8:46, Est.5:2, Sir.14:20, 2Ma.11:20 * ;] to converse with, discourse (see Cl. Rev., i,…

dialeipo.notes: δια-λείπω [in LXX for יחל (as 1Ki.10:8), etc. ;] to intermit, leave off for a time: Luk.7:45.† (AS)

dialektos.notes: διά-λεκτος, -ου, ἡ (διαλέγομαι), [in LXX: Dan LXX 1:4 (לָשׁוֹן), Est.9:26 * ;] __1. conversation, discourse, speech (Plat., Dem., al.). __2. As in Polyb. and later writers, the language or dialect of a particular…

diallasso.notes: δι-αλλάσσω [in LXX: 1Ki.29:4 (רָצָה hithp.), I Est.4:31, al. ;] __1. to change, exchange. __2. to change enmity for friendship, to reconcile: pass., with dative of person(s), Mat.5:24. "The word denotes mutual…

dialogismos.notes: δια-λογισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (διαλογίζομαι) [in LXX chiefly for מַחֲשָׁבָה ;] a thought, reasoning, inward questioning: Mat.15:19, Mrk.7:21, Luk.2:35 5:22 6:8 9:46, 47 24:38, Rom.1:21 14:1, 1Co.3:20" (LXX), Php.2:14; κριταὶ…

dialogizomai.notes: δια-λογίζομαι, depon. [in LXX chiefly for חשׁב, frequently in Pss. ;] __1. to balance accounts (Dem.). __2. to consider, reason (Isocr.): Luk.1:29 5:21; ἐν τ. καρδία, Mrk.2:6, 8, Luk.5:22; περί Luk.3:15; ἐν ἑαυτῷ…

dialuo.notes: δια-λύω [in LXX for אבה, חבל, etc. ;] to part asunder, dissolve; of an assembly, pass., to disperse: Act.5:36.† (AS)

diamachomai.notes: δια-μάχομαι [in LXX for לחם ni., Dan LXX 10:20; Sir.8:1, 3 38:28 51:19 * ;] __1. to struggle against. __2. In argument, to contend: Act.23:9.† (AS)

diamarturomai.notes: δια-μαρτύρομαι, depon., of Ionic origin, intensive of the simple μαρτύρομαι, which see, [in LXX chiefly for עוּד hi., usually with dative of person(s), Deu.4:26 8:19, 1Ki.8:9, al. ;] solemnly to protest: Luk.16:28,…

diameno.notes: δια-μένω [in LXX for עמד, etc.;] to remain, continue: Luk.1:22 22:28, Gal.2:5, Heb.1:11" (LXX), 2Pe.3:4.† (AS)

diamerismos.notes: δια-μερισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (διαμερίζω), [in LXX: Eze.48:29 (מַחֲלֹקֶת), Mic.7:12 * ;] a division: opposite to εἰρήνη, Luk.12:51.† (AS)

diamerizo.notes: δια-μερίζω [in LXX chiefly for חלק pi. ;] __1. to distribute: with dative of person(s), Act.2:45; before εἰς, Luk.22:17, pass., Act.2:3. Mid. to distribute among themselves: Mat.27:35, Mrk.15:24, Luk.23:34; with…

dianemo.notes: δια-νέμω [in LXX for חלק, Deu.29:25 * ;] to distribute, divide: pass., to be spread about, Act.4:17.† (AS)

dianeuo.notes: δια-νεύω [in LXX: Ps 34(35):19 (קרץ), Sir.27:22 * ;] to wink at, nod to, beckon to: Luk.1:22.† (AS)

diangello.notes: δι-αγγέλω [in LXX for ספר pi., etc. ;] to publish abroad, proclaim: with accusative, Luk.9:60, Act.21:26, Rom.9:17" (LXX) .† (AS)

dianoema.notes: δια-νόημα, -τος, τό (διανοέομαι, to think), [in LXX for מַחֲשָׁבָה, etc., Isa.55:9, al., frequently in Sir ;] a thought: Luk.11:17.† (AS)

dianoia.notes: διάνοια, -ας, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for לֵב, לֵבָב ;] the understanding, mind: Luk.1:51, Eph.4:18, Col.1:21, 1Pe.1:13, 2Pe.3:1, 1Jn.5:20; pl., Eph.2:3; in quotations from LXX, Mat.22:37, Mrk.12:30, Luk.10:27, Heb.8:10…

dianoigo.notes: δι-αν-οίγω [in LXX for פֶּטֶר (Ex, Nu), פָּקַח, פָּתַח, etc. ;] to open up completely, to open: Luk.2:23 (LXX); pass., Mrk.7:34, Act.7:56. Metaphorical, δ. τ. νοῦν, Luk.24:45, τ. καρδίαν, Act.16:14, pass., οἱ…

dianuktereuo.notes: δια-νυκτερεύω (cf. διημερεύω, to pass the day) [in LXX: Job.2:9 * ;] to pass the night: before ἐν τ. προσευχῆ, Luk.6:12.† (AS)

dianuo.notes: δι-ανύω [in LXX: 2Ma.12:17 * ;] __1. to accomplish fully, finish, complete: Act.21:7 (EV). __2. In late writers (Xen., al., Clem., I ad Cor., xxv, 3), to continue: Ac, l.with (Field, Notes, 134f.).† (AS)

diaperao.notes: δια-περάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Deu.30:13, Isa.23:2 (עבר), עבר), 1Ma.6:1-63 * ;] to pass over, cross over: Mat.9:1; before ἐπὶ τ. γῆν, Mat.14:34, Mrk.6:53; εἰς, Mrk.5:21, Act.21:2; πρὸς ἡμᾶς, Luk.16:28.† (AS)

diaphemizo.notes: δια-φημίζω in late writers only, to spread abroad: τ. λόγον (= דָּבָר, the matter), Mrk.1:45, Mat.28:15; with accusative of person(s), to spread abroad one's fame: Mat.9:31.† (AS)

diaphero.notes: δια-φέρω [in LXX for שְׁנָא (שְׁנָא (Dan.7:1-28 only); 1Ki.17:39, Est.3:13, al. ;] __1. trans., __(a) to carry through: before διά, Mrk.11:16; __(b) to carry about, spread abroad: pass., Act.13:49 27:27. __2.…

diapheugo.notes: δια-φεύγω [in LXX: Jos.8:22 (פָּלִיט), Jos.10:28 (שָׂרִיד), etc. ;] to flee through, escape: Act.27:42.† (AS)

diaphoros.notes: διά-φορος, -ον (διαφέρω), [in LXX for שְׁנָא (Dan.7:7, 19); also as in Polyb., iv, 188, al., Τὸ δ., τὰ δ., money: Sir.27:1 42:5, 2Ma.3:6 ;] __1. different: Rom.12:6, Heb.9:10. __2. excellent: compar., -ώτερος,…

diaphtheiro.notes: δια-φθείρω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁחַת hi. ;] __1. to destroy utterly: Luk.12:33, Rev.11:18; pass., to be destroyed, disabled: Rev.8:9, 2Co.4:16. __2. In moral sense, to corrupt, deprave: τ. γῆν, Rev.11:18; pass.,…

diaphthora.notes: δια-φθορὰ, -ᾶς, ἡ (διαφθείρω), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁחַת, and cogn. forms ;] __1. destruction. __2. corruption (physical or moral): of the grave, Act.2:27 (LXX), Act.2:31 13:34-37.† (AS)

diaphulasso.notes: δια-φυλάσσω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁמַר (as Psa.91:11) ;] to guard carefully (M, Pr., 116), defend: with accusative, Luk.4:10 (LXX).† (AS)

diapleo.notes: to sail across , Thuc.; εἰς Αἴγιναν (Aristophanes Comicus): metaphorically, δ. βίον to make life's voyage , (Plato Philosophus) (ML)

diaponeo.notes: δια-πονέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Ecc.10:9 (עָצַב ni.), 2Ma.2:28; in Aq.: Gen.6:6, 1Ki.20:3 * ;] to work out with labour. Pass., to be worn out, sore troubled: Mrk.14:4 (WH, mg.), Act.4:2 16:18.† (AS)

diaporeo.notes: δι-απορέω, -ῶ [in Sm.: Psa.77:5, Dan.2:1 * ;] to be quite at a loss, be in great perplexity: absol., Act.2:12; before διὰ τό, with inf., Luk.9:7; περί, Act.5:24; ἐν ἑαυτῷ, Act.10:17.† (AS)

diaporeuo.notes: δια-πορεύω [in LXX for עָבַר, הָלַךְ, etc. ;] to carry over. Pass., to pass across, journey through: absol., Luk.18:36, Rom.15:24; before κατὰ πόλεις κ. κώμας, Luk.13:22; διά, with genitive, Mrk.2:23, Luk.6:1 (cf.…

diapragmateuomai.notes: δια-πραγματεύομαι , "perfective compound" (see M, Pr., 118); __1. to examine thoroughly (Plat.). __2. In late writers (Dion. Hal.), to gain by trading: Luk.19:15.† (AS)

diaprio.notes: δια-πρίω [in LXX: 1Ch.20:3 (סוּר) * ;] to saw asunder. Pass., metaphorically (vernacular?), EV, cut to the heart: Act.5:33; before τ. καρδίαις αὐτῶν, Act.7:54.† (AS)

diarpazo.notes: δι-αρπάζω [in LXX for בָּזַז, גָּזַל, שָׁסַס, etc. ;] to plunder: Mat.12:29, Mrk.3:27.† (AS)

diarregnumi.notes: δια-ρήσσω (so WH, exc. Ac, l.with), δια-ρρήσσω (poetic and late form of διαρρήγνυμι), [in LXX chiefly for קָרָא ;] to break asunder, burst, rend: δεσμά, Luk.8:29; pass., δίκτυα, Luk.5:6; ἱμάτια, χιτῶνας, in grief or…

diasapheo.notes: δια-σαφέω, -ῶ (σαφής, clear), [in LXX: Deu.1:5 (בָּאַר pi.), Dan LXX 2:6 (חָוָה aphel.), I-III Ma 9 * ;] to make clear, explain fully: with accusative of thing(s), dative of person(s), Mat.13:26 18:31.† (AS)

diaseio.notes: δια-σείω [in LXX: Job.4:14 (פָּחַד hi.), 3Ma.7:21 * ;] to shake violently; metaphorically, to intimidate: Luk.3:14.† (AS)

diaskorpizo.notes: δια-σκορπίζω [in LXX for פּוּץ, זָרָה, etc. ;] to scatter abroad, disperse: of sheep, Mat.26:31 = Mrk.14:27" (LXX) ; of persons, Luk.1:51, Act.5:37, opposite to συνάγω, Jhn.11:52; of winnowing grain, Mat.25:24, 26;…

diasozo.notes: δια-σώζω [in LXX for מָלַט, יָשַׁע, etc. ;] to bring safely through a danger: Luk.7:3, Act.27:43; before πρός, Act.23:24. Pass., to come safe through: Act.28:1; ἐπὶ τ. γῆν, Act.27:44; ἐκ τ. θαλάσσης, Act.28:4; δι᾽…

diaspao.notes: δια-σπάω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for נָתַק, as Jdg.16:9 ;] to break or tear asunder: pass., ἁλύσεις, Mrk.5:4; Παῦλος, Act.23:10.† (AS)

diaspeiro.notes: δια-σπείρω [frequently in LXX for פּוּץ, etc. ;] to scatter abroad, disperse: Act.8:1, 4 11:19.† (AS)

diaspora.notes: δια-σπορά, -ᾶς, ἡ (διασπείρω), [in LXX of Israelites dispersed and exiled in foreign lands, as Deu.28:25 (זַעֲוָה) Deu.30:4 (נָדַח ni.), Isa.49:6 (נער); by meton., of the exiles themselves (as Psa.147:2, 2Ma.1:27) ;]…

diastello.notes: δια-στελλω [in LXX for בָּדַל hi. (Deu.10:8, al.), זְהַר hi. (Eze.3:18-19, al.), and 19 other words] __1. to divide, distinguish, define. __2. to command, charge expressly: pass., τὸ διαστελλόμενον, Heb.12:20. Mid.,…

diastema.notes: διάστημα, -τος, τό (διίστημι), [in LXX for רֶוַח, etc. ;] an interval, space: of time (Sir, prol. 24), Act.5:7.† (AS)

diastole.notes: δια-στολή, -ῆς, ἡ (διαστέλλω), [in LXX for פְּדוּת: Exo.8:23, etc. ;] __1. a separation. __2. a distinction, difference: Rom.3:22 10:12, 1Co.14:7.† (AS)

diastrepho.notes: δια-στρέφω [in LXX for הָפַךְ, עָקַשׁ, etc. ;] to distort, twist; metaphorically, to distort, pervert: Luk.23:2, Act.13:8, 10; διεστραμμένος, perverse: Mat.17:17, Luk.9:41, Act.20:30, Php.2:15.† (AS)

diatage.notes: δια-ταγή, -ῆς, ἡ (διατάσσω), [in LXX: Est.4:11 (פַּרְשֶׁגֶן)* ;] in late writers (Deiss., LAE, 86 ff.) for cl. διάταξις (WH., however, is found in LXX, Psa.119:91, al.); __(a) disposition (cf. διάταξις for צָבָא;…

diatagma.notes: διά-ταγμα, -τος, τό (διατάσσω), [in LXX: Est.7:11 (נִשְׁתְּוָן), Est.3:13, Wis.11:7 * ;] an edict, mandate: Heb.11:23.† (AS)

diatarasso.notes: δια-ταράσσω [in Sm.: 3Ki.20:43 * ;] to agitate greatly (Lat. perturbare): Luk.1:29,† (AS)

diatasso.notes: δια-τάσσω [in LXX for שׂוּם, שָׁמַר, etc. ;] to charge, give orders to, appoint, arrange, ordain: with dative, Mat.11:1, 1Co.9:14 16:1; before inf., Luk.8:55, Act.18:2. Mid., 1Co.7:17, Act.20:13; with accusative,…

diateleo.notes: δια-τελέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Deu.9:7 (הָיָה), Jer.20:7, 18 (כָּלָה), Est.8:13, 2Ma.5:27 * ;] prop. trans., to accomplish; used with ellipse of obj. as intrans. (Bl., § 81, 1), and joined to participles (in Ac, Lc., to…

diatereo.notes: δια-τηρέω, -ῶ [in LXX for שָׁמַר, נָצַר, etc.; before ἀπό (for מִן), as in Psa.12:7 (8) ;] to keep carefully: Luk.2:51; before ἐκ, Act.15:29.† (AS)

diatheke.notes: διαθήκη, -ης, ἡ (διατίθημι), [frequently in LXX, and nearly always for בְּרִית ;] __1. as usually in cl., a disposition, testament, will (Plat., al.): Gal.3:15 (R, mg., but see Lft., in l.), Heb.9:16, 17 (R, txt.;…

diatithemi.notes: δια-τίθημι [in LXX chiefly for כָּרַת, frequently δ. διαθήκην (כָּרַת) ;] to place separately, arrange, dispose. Mid, only in NT; __1. (a) in general, to dispose of; with dative of person(s), to assign to one,…

diatribo.notes: δια-τρίβω [in LXX Lev.14:8 (יָשַׁב), Jer.35:7 (גּוּר), Tob.11:8, 12, Jdth.10:2, 2Ma.14:23 * ;] to rub hard, rub away, consume; δ. χρόνον, ἡμέρας, to spend time: Act.14:3, 28 16:12 20:6 25:6, 14. Intransitively with…

diatrophe.notes: δια-τροφή, ῆς, ἡ (διατρέφω, to support, sustain), [in LXX: 1Ma.6:49 * ;] food, nourishment: 1Ti.6:8.† (AS)

diaugazo.notes: δι-αυγάζω [in Aq.: Job.25:5 * ;] to shine through, to dawn: 2Pe.1:19 (cf. ἕως οὗ διαπνεύσῃ ἡ ἡμέρα, Gal.2:17).† (AS)

diazonnumi.notes: δια-ζώννυμι, (also -ννύω) [in LXX for חֲגוֹר, Eze.23:15 A * ;] to gird round: ἑαυτόν, Jhn.13:4; pass., ib. 13:5; mid., to gird oneself with: with accusative, Jhn.21:7.† (AS)

dichazo.notes: διχάζω (δίχα, apart), [in Aq.: Lev.1:17, Deu.14:6 * ;] to cut apart, divide in two; metaphorically, to set at variance: Mat.10:35.† (AS)

dichostasia.notes: διχοστασία, -ας, ἡ (διχοστατέω, to stand apart), [in LXX: 1Ma.3:29 * ;] standing apart, dissension: Rom.16:17, Gal.5:20.† (AS)

dichotomeo.notes: διχοτομέω, -ῶ (διχοτόμος, δίχα, τέμνω), [in LXX: Exo.29:17 (נָתַח pi.) ;] to cut in two, cut asunder: perh. metaphorically of severe scourging (but see Meyer on Mt, l.with, and cf. 1Ki.15:33, 2Ki.12:31, Heb.11:37),…

didache.notes: διδαχή, -ῆς, ἡ (διδάσκω), [in LXX: Psa.60:1-12 title. (לָמַד pi.); Sm.: Deu.33:8 * ;] __1. objectively, teaching, doctrine, that which is taught: Mrk.1:27, Jhn.7:16, Act.17:19, Rom.6:7 16:17, 2Jn.10, Rev.2:24; with…

didaktikos.notes: διδακτικός, -ή, -όν (= cl. διδασκαλικός) apt at teaching: 1Ti.3:2, 2Ti.2:24.† (AS)

didaktos.notes: διδακτός, -ή, -όν (διδάσκω), [in LXX: Isa.54:13 (לִמּוּד), 1Ma.4:7 * ;] __1. that can be taught. __2. taught; with genitive, of source of teaching (in cl., poët. only); __(a) of persons: δ. θεοῦ, Jhn.6:45 (LXX);…

didaskalia.notes: διδασκαλία, -ας, ἡ (διδάσκω), [in LXX: Pro.2:17 (אַלּוּף), Isa.29:13 (לָמַד), Sir.24:33 39:8 * ;] teaching, instruction, in both active and objective senses, most frequently the latter: Rom.12:7 15:4, Eph.4:14,…

didaskalos.notes: διδάσκαλος, -ου, ὁ (διδάσκω), [in LXX: Est.6:1, 2Ma.1:10 * ;] given as rendering of Heb. רַבִּי ,רַבּוֺן (NT, Ῥαββεί, Ῥαββουνεί which see); a teacher: Jhn.1:39 20:16; of Jewish teachers, Luk.2:46, Jhn.3:10, cf.…

didasko.notes: διδάσκω [in LXX chiefly for למד pl., also for ידע hi., ירה hi., etc. ;] to teach (i.e. instruct) a person, teach a thing; __1. trans.: with accusative of person(s), Mat.5:2, Mrk.1:22, al.; before ὅτι, Mrk.8:31; περί,…

didomi.notes: δίδωμι, [in LXX chiefly for נָתַן (53 words in all) ;] to give—in various senses, accusative to context—bestow, grant, supply, deliver, commit, yield: absol., Act.20:35; with accusative of thing(s) and dative of…

didrachmos.notes: δίδραχμος, -ον (δίς, δραχμή), worth two drachmae; τὸ δ. (sc. νόμισμα, coin), [in LXX chiefly for שֶׁקֶל (Gen.23:15, a1.), also for כֶּסֶף: Gen.20:16, Deu.22:29 ;] a double drachma, nearly equal to the Jewish…

didumos.notes: Δίδυμος, -ου, ὁ (prop. name from δίδυμος, -η, -ον, double, sc. παῖς, twin) Didymus, surname of the apostle Thomas: Jhn.11:16 20:24 21:2.† (AS)

diegeiro.notes: δι-εγείρω [in LXX: Jdth.1:4, Est.1:1, 2Ma.7:21 2Mac 15:10, 3Ma.5:15 * ;] to arouse completely, arouse as from rest or sleep: Luk.8:24; pass., Mrk.4:39, Luk.8:24; of the sea, Jhn.6:18. Metaphorical, of the mind: ἐν…

diegeomai.notes: δι-ηγέομαι, -οῦμαι [in LXX chiefly for סָפַר pi. ;] to set out in detail, recount, describe: absol., Heb.11:32; with accusative of thing(s), Act.8:33; with dative of person(s), before πῶς, Mrk.5:16, Act.9:27 12:17; ἅ…

diegesis.notes: δι-ήγησις, -εως, ἡ (διηγέομαι), [in LXX: Jdg.7:15 מִסְפָּר Hab.2:6 חִידָה), frequently in (Sir.6:35, al.), 2Ma.2:32 2Mac 6:17 ;] a narrative: Luk.1:1 (cf. Milligan, NTD, 130).† (AS)

dienekes.notes: δι-ηνεκής, -ές (διήνεγκα, aorist of διαφέρω) [in Sm.: Psa.48:15, 89:30 ;] unbroken, continuous: adverbially, εἰς τὸ δ., continually (for exx., see Deiss., BS, 251), Heb.7:3 10:1, 12 10:14† (AS)

dierchomai.notes: δι-έρχομαι [in LXX for אֵבֶר, הָלַךְ, בּוֹא, etc. ;] __1. to go through, pass through; __(a) of things: Mat.19:24, Mrk.10:25, Luk.2:35, al.; __(b) of persons: Luk.19:4; with accusative loc., Luk.19:1, Heb.4:14,…

diermeneuo.notes: δι-ερμηνεύω [in LXX: 2Ma.1:36 * ;] intensive of ἐρμηνεύω, to interpret; __(a) to explain, expound: with accusative, Luk.24:27; absol., 1Co.12:30 14:5 14:13 14:27; __(b) to translate: Act.9:36.† (AS)

dierotao.notes: δι-ερωτάω, -ῶ to find by inquiry: with accusative, Act.10:17.† (AS)

dietes.notes: διετής, -ές (δίς, ἔτος), [in LXX: 2Ma.10:3 * ;] __1. lasting two years. __2. two years old: ἀπὸ δ. (sc. παιδός, or neuter; cf. 1Ch.27:23, ἀπὸ εἰκοσαετοῦς), Mat.2:16.† (AS)

dietia.notes: διετία, -ας, ἡ (διετής), [in LXX (Græc. Ven.): Gen.41:1 45:5 * ;] the space of two years: Act.24:27 28:30.† (AS)

diexodos.notes: δι-έ-οδος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for תּוֹצָאָה, as Num.34:4ff, and frequently in Jos;] in π. of the conclusion of a trial (MM, Exp., xi); a way out through, an outlet; pl., δ. τῶν ὁδῶν, RV, the partings of the…

diikneomai.notes: δι-ικνέομαι (Rec. διϊκ-), -οῦμαι, [in LXX for בָּרַח hi., Exo.26:28 * ;] to go through, penetrate: Heb.4:12.† (AS)

diischurizomai.notes: δι-ισχυρίζομαι (T, Rec. διϊσ-), depon.; __1. to lean upon. __2. to affirm confidently: with ptcp., Luk.22:59; with accusative and inf. (Bl., § 70, 3), Act.12:15.† (AS)

diistemi.notes: δι-ίσπημι (Rec. διΐσ), [in LXX: Eze.5:1 (חָלַק pi.), Pro.17:9 (פָּרַד hi.), etc. ;] to set apart, separate; of time (or space), to make an interval, intervene: διαστάσης ὥρας μιᾶς, Luk.22:59; βραχὺ διαστήσαντες,…

dikaiokrisia.notes: δικαιοκρισία, -ας, ἡ righteous judgment: Rom.2:5 (cf. τ. δικαίας κρίσεως, 2Th.1:5; τὴν δ. κ., Jhn.7:24. For use in π., see Deiss., LAE, 89 f.).† (AS)

dikaioma.notes: δικαίωμα, -τος, τ (δικαιόω), [in LXX most frequently for חֹק, in Ez, chiefly for מִשְׁפָּט, in Pss, τὰ δ., frequently for פִּקּוּד ;] a concrete expression of righteousness, the expression and result of the act of…

dikaioo.notes: δικαιόω, -ῶ (δίκαιος), [in LXX chiefly for צָדַק pi., hi., (1) as Eze.16:51, Jer.3:11 (cf. NT usage); (2) as Deu.25:1; Exo.23:7, Isa.50:8 ;] __1. in cl., __(a) with accusative of thing(s), to deem right; __(b) with…

dikaios.notes: δίκαιος, -α, -ον (δίκη), [in LXX chiefly for צַדִּיק (for rendering of צַדִּיק in sense of correct, see Deiss., BS, 115 f.); sometimes for נָקִי as Pro.1:11, al. ;] in early Gk. writers __(a) of persons, observant of…

dikaiosis.notes: δικαίωσις, -εως, ἡ (δικαιόω), [in LXX for מִשְׁפָּט, Lev.24:22 * ;] the act of pronouncing righteous, justification, acquittal: Rom.4:25 5:18 (Cremer, 199).† (AS)

dikaiosune.notes: δικαιοσύνη, -ης, ἡ (δίκαιος), [in LXX chiefly for צֶדֶק, and cognates, Gen.15:6, al., rarely for חֶסֶד, Gen.19:19 ;] the character of ὁ δίκαιος (which see) __1. in broad sense, righteousness, conformity to the Divine…

dikastes.notes: δικαστής, -οῦ, ὁ (δικάζω), [in LXX for שָׁפַט ;] a judge: Act.7:27, 35" (LXX) .† SYN.: κριτής, WH. "gives prominence to the mental process" (Thayer). δ. is the forensic term. In Attic law, the δικασταί were jurors,…

dike.notes: δίκη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for נָקָם, רִיב, etc. ;] __1. custom. __2. right. __3. a judicial hearing; hence its result, the execution of a sentence, punishment: δ. τίνειν, 2Th.1:9; δ. υπεχειν, Ju 7. __4. Personified (cf.…

diktuon.notes: δίκτυον, -ον, τό [in LXX chiefly for רֶשֶׁת, שְׂבָכָה ;] general term for a net: Mat.4:20-21, Mrk.1:18-19, Luk.5:2, 4-6, Jhn.21:6, 8 21:11 † SYN.: ἀμφίβληστρον (which see), σαγήνη. (AS)

dilogos.notes: διλογος, -ον (δίς, λέγω), __1. in sense of διλογεῖν, -ία (Xen.), given to repetition. __2. In NT, prob. (cf. δίγλωσσος, Pro.11:13, Sir.5:9) double-tongued: 1Ti.3:8.† (AS)

diodeuo.notes: δι-οδεύω, [in LXX chiefly for עבר ;] __1. to travel through: with accusative, Act.17:1 __2. to travel along (Bar.4:2, 1Ma.12:32-33): Luk.8:1.† (AS)

diogmos.notes: διωγμός, -oῦ, ὁ (διώκω), [in LXX: Pro.11:19 (רָדַף pi.), La 3:19 (מָרוּד), 2Ma.12:23 * ;] persecution: Mat.13:21, Mrk.4:17 10:30 Act.8:1 13:50, Rom.8:35, 2Co.12:10, 2Th.1:4, 2Ti.3:11.† (AS)

dioko.notes: διώκω [in LXX chiefly for רָדַף ;] __1. to put to flight, drive away: Mat.23:34. __2. to pursue; __(a) of persons; with accusative, without hostility, to follow after: Luk.17:23; with hostile purpose: Act.26:11,…

dionusios.notes: Διονύσιος, -ου, ὁ Dionysius, an Athenian: Act.17:34.† (AS)

diopetes.notes: διοπετής (written also διϊπ-), -ές (δῖος, πίπτω, see Page on Ac, l.with; DB, i, 605, n., ext., 112a; Field, Notes, 130 f.), fallen from heaven (R, mg.): τὸ δ. (sc. ἄγαλμα, statue, image), Act.19:35.† (AS)

diorthosis.notes: διόρθωσις, -εως, ἡ (see supr.), __1. a making straight. __2. a reforming, reformation (used in late writers of laws, etc.): Heb.9:10 (Cremer, 807).† διόρθωμα, -τος, τό (διορθόω, to make straight, set right), a…

diorusso.notes: δι-ορύσσω [in LXX: Job.24:16, Exo.12:5, 7 12:12, (חָתַר) * ;] to dig through: with accusative, of house-breaking (as in π.; MM, Exp., xi), Mat.24:43, Luk.12:39; absol., Mat.6:19-20.† (AS)

dioskouroi.notes: Διόσ-κουροι, -ων, οἱ (Ion. and κοινή form of Att..., Διόσκοροι; Διός, genitive of Ζεύς + κόρος, a son), the Dioscuri (Castor and Pollux), twin sons of Zeus and Leda (RV, The Twin Brothers): Act.28:11.† (AS)

diotrephes.notes: Διοτρέφης (Rec. -τρεψής), -ες (Διός, genitive of Ζεύς + τρέφω: cherished by Zeus), as proper name, Diotrephes: III Jn 9.† (AS)

diploo.notes: διπλόω, -ῶ (δυτλόος), to double: δ. τὰ διπλᾶ, Rev.18:6.† (AS)

diplous.notes: διπλόος (poët., διπλός, whence corp. -ότερον), -όη, -όον (-οῦ, -ῆ, -οῦν) [in LXX for כָּפַל, מִשְׁנֶה, and cognates ;] twofold, double: 1Ti.5:17, Rev.18:6; διπλότιρον, in twofold measure: Mat.23:15.† (AS)

dipsao.notes: διψάω,-ῶ (δίψα, thirst), [in LXX chiefly for צָמֵא ;] to thirst: absol., Mat.25:35, 37 25:42, 44, Jhn.4:13, 15 19:28, Rom.12:20" (LXX), 1Co.4:11; figuratively, Jhn.4:14 6:35 7:37, Rev.7:16 21:6 22:17; with accusative…

dipsos.notes: δίψος, -εος (-ους), τό (late form of δίψα), [in LXX chiefly for and cognates ;] thirst: 2Co.11:27.† (AS)

dipsuchos.notes: δίψυχος, -ον (δίς, ψυχή), of two minds, wavering: Jas.1:8 4:8 (Cremer, 588; DB, iv, 528).† (AS)

dis.notes: δίς adv., twice: Mrk.14:30, 72; δ. τ. σαββάτου, Luk.18:12; καὶ ἅπαξ κ. δ., Php.4:16, 1Th.2:18; δ. ἀποθανόντα, Ju 12 (see Mayor, ICC, in l); δ. μυριάδες, Rev.9:16.† (AS)

dischilioi.notes: two thousand , (Herdotus Historicus):—;singular with collective nouns, δισχιλίη ἵππος 2000 horse, (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)

distazo.notes: διστάζω (δίς), to doubt, hesitate: Mat.14:31 28:17.† SYN.: ἀπορέω, διαπορέω, διακρίνομαι, μετεωρίζομαι (see DCG, i, 491) (AS)

distomos.notes: δίστομος, -ον (δίς, στόμα), [in LXX: Jdg.3:16, Psa.149:6, Pro.5:4 (פֶּה), Sir.21:3 * ;] __1. of rivers and roads, double-mouthed, double branching. __2. Of swords (Eur.; LXX, ll with), two-edged: Heb.4:12, Rev.1:16…

diulizo.notes: δι-υλίζω (δια, ὑλίζω, to strain) [in LXX: Amo.6:6 * ;] __1. to strain thoroughly (Archytas; Am., l.with). __2. Later, to strain out: figuratively, Mat.23:24.† (AS)

doche.notes: δοχή, -ῆς, ἡ (δέχομαι), [in LXX: Gen.21:8, Est.1:3, al. (מִשְׁתֶּה), Dan LXX 5:1 (לֶחֶם) ;] a feast, banquet: Luk.5:29 14:13.† (AS)

dodeka.notes: δώδεκα, οἱ, αἱ, τά indecl. numeral, twelve: Mat.9:20 10:1, al.; οἱ δ., the apostles, Mat.10:5, Mrk.4:10, al.; in Act.19:7 24:11, for Rec. δεκαδύο. (AS)

dodekaphulos.notes: δωδεκά-φυλος, -ον (δώδεκα, φυλή), of twelve tribes: as subst. neut., τό δ. (cf. λαὸς ὁ δ., Sibyll. Orac.), the twelve tribes, Act.26:7.† (AS)

dogma.notes: δόγμα, -τος, τό (δοκέω), [in LXX chiefly for דָּת, טְעֵם ;] __1. an opinion. __2. A public decree, ordinance: of Roman rulers, Luk.2:1, Act.17:7; of the Jewish law, Eph.2:15, Col.2:14; of the Apostles, Act.16:4…

dogmatizo.notes: δογματίζω [in LXX: Est.3:9 (כָּתַב ni.), Dan LXX 2:13, 15 (דָּת), I Est.6:34, 2Ma.10:3 2Mac 15:36, 3Ma.4:11 * ;] to decree. Mid., to subject oneself to an ordinance: Col.2:20.† (AS)

dokeo.notes: δοκέω, -ῶ (δόκος, opinion, δέκομαι, Ion, form of δέχ-), [in LXX for טוֹב, נָדַב, etc. ;] __1. to be of opinion, suppose: Mat.24:44, Luk.12:40, Heb.10:29; with inf., Mat.3:9, Luk.8:18 24:37, Jhn.5:39 16:2, Act.12:9…

dokimazo.notes: δοκιμάζω (δόκιμος), [in LXX chiefly for בָּחַן ;] __1. primarily of metals (Pro.8:10 17:3, Sir.2:5, Wis.3:6), to test, try, prove (in the hope and expectation that the test will prove successful, see reff. s. Syn.):…

dokime.notes: δοκιμή, -ῆς, ἡ (δόκιμος), [in Sm.: Psa.68:31 * ;] __1. the process of trial, proving, test: 2Co.8:2 9:13 __2. The result of trial, approval, approvedness, proof: Rom.5:4, 2Co.2:9 13:3, Php.2:22 (Cremer, 212, 701).† (AS)

dokimion.notes: δοκίμιον, -ου, τό [in LXX: Pro.17:3 27:21 (מִצְרֵף) * ;] usually regarded as a variant form of δοκιμεῖον, a test, and so perh. Jas.1:3 (see Mayor, in l), but see next word (cf. Cremer, 212, 702).† (AS)

dokimos.notes: δοκίμιος, -α, -ον (δοκιμή), [in LXX: ἀργύριον δ. (עֲלִיל), Psa.12:6; and as seel. for δόκιμος (B), ἀργυρίου δ. (זָקַק pual.), 1Ch.29:4 (Bab); εἰ δ .ίστιν (יְקָר), Zec.11:13 א with a vid, Q *)* ;] = δόκιμος, tested,…

dokos.notes: δοκός, -οῦ, ἡ (δέχομαι) [in LXX for קוֹרָה, etc. ;] a beam of timber: Mat.7:3-5, Luk.6:41-42 (DCG, i, 176).† (AS)

dolioo.notes: δολιόω (δόλιος), [in LXX: Num.25:18, Psa.105:25 (נָכַל), Psa.5:6 (חָלַק hi.) * ;] to deceive: ἐδολιοῦσαν (-σαν, frequently in κοινή Gk. for imperfect 3rd of person(s) pl.), Rom.3:13 (LXX).† (AS)

dolios.notes: δόλιος, -α, -ον (δόλος), [in LXX—chiefly in Pss, Pr, Sir—for etc. ;] deceitful: 2Co.11:13.† (AS)

doloo.notes: δολόω, -ῶ (δόλος) [in LXX: Psa.15:3 (רָגַל), Psa.36:2 (חָלַק hi.) * ;] __1. to ensnare. __2. As of wine, to adulterate, corrupt: τ. λόγον τ. θεοῦ, 2Co.4:2.† SYN.: καπηλεύω which see (AS)

dolos.notes: δόλος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for מִרְמָה;]__1. in Hom., a bait. __2. a snare. __3. In the abstract, craft, deceit: Mat.26:4, Mrk.7:22 14:1, Jhn.1:47, Act.13:10, Rom.1:29, 2Co.12:16, 1Th.2:3, 1Pe.2:1 2:22 3:10"…

doma.notes: δόμα, -τος, τό (δίδωμι), [in LXX for מַתָּנָה, etc. ;] a gift: Mat.7:11, Luk.11:13, Eph.4:8" (LXX), Php.4:17.† SYN.: δόσις, δῶρον, δωρεά, δώρημα (AS)

dorea.notes: δωρεά, -ᾶς, ἡ (δίδωμι), [in LXX chiefly (-άν) for חִנָּם, as Gen.29:15 ;] a gift: Act.11:17, Rom.5:15, Heb.6:4; δ. τ. θεοῦ, Jhn.4:10, Act.8:20; τ. Χριστοῦ, Eph.4:7; τ. πνεύματος, Act.2:38 10:45; τ. δικαιοσύνης,…

dorean.notes: δωρεάν, see: δωρεά. δωρεά, -ᾶς, ἡ (δίδωμι), [in LXX chiefly (-άν) for חִנָּם, as Gen.29:15 ;] a gift: Act.11:17, Rom.5:15, Heb.6:4; δ. τ. θεοῦ, Jhn.4:10, Act.8:20; τ. Χριστοῦ, Eph.4:7; τ. πνεύματος, Act.2:38 10:45;…

dorema.notes: δώρημα, -τος, τό (δωρέω), [in LXX: Sir.31:18 (Sir.34:18)* ;] a gift, boon: Rom.5:16, Jas.1:17.† SYN.: see: δόμα (AS)

doreo.notes: δωρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Gen.30:20 (זָבַד), Est.8:1, Pro.4:2 (נָתַן), Lev.7:15 (קׇרְבָּן), 1Es.1:7 Est.8:14 Est.8:55, Sir.7:25 * ;] to present, bestow. As depon. (with same sense), -Ιομαι,.-οίμαι: Mrk.15:45, 2Pe.1:3-4.†…

dorkas.notes: Δορκάς, -άδος, ἡ (δορκάς, a gazelle), Dorcas, also called Ταβειθά, which see: Act.9:36, 39.† (AS)

doron.notes: δῶρον, -ου, τό (δίδωμι), [in LXX chiefly for קׇרְבָּן, also for מִנְחָה, etc. ;] a gift, present: Mat.2:11, Rev.11:10; of gifts and sacrifices to God, Mat.5:23-24 8:4 15:5 23:18-19, Mrk.7:11, Luk.21:1, 4, Heb.5:1…

dosis.notes: δόσις, -εως, ἡ (δίδωμι) [in LXX for חֹק (Gen.47:22), (Pro.21:14 25:14), frequently in Sir. ;] __1. properly, the act of giving: Php.4:15. __2. Objectively, a gift: Jas.1:17.† SYN.: see: δόμα (AS)

dotes.notes: δότης, -ου, ὁ (δίδωμι), [in LXX: Pro.22:8 * ;] = δοτήρ, a giver: 2Co.9:7 (LXX) (not elsewhere).† (AS)

doulagogeo.notes: δουλαγωγέω, -ῶ (δοῦλος, ἄγω), to make a slave, bring into bondage: 1Co.9:27 (Cremer, 703).† (AS)

doule.notes: δούλη, ἡ, see: δοῦλος. δοῦλος, -η, -ον, [in LXX, ὁ δ. nearly always for עֶבֶד; ὁ δ. chiefly for שִׁפְחָה ,אָמָה ;] __1. in bondage to, subject to: Rom.6:19. __2. As subst., ὁ, ἡ δ., a slave; __(a) fem., ἡ δ., a…

douleia.notes: δουλεία (T, -λία), -ας, ἡ (δουλεύω), [in LXX, as Exo.13:3, for עֶבֶד and cognates ;] slavery, bondage: Rom.8:15, 21 Gal.4:24 5:1, Heb.2:15.† (AS)

douleuo.notes: δουλεύω (δοῦλος), [in LXX for עָבַד, as Gen.14:4, exc. Da TH Dan.7:14, 27 (פְּלַח), Isa.56:6 (שָׁרַת pi.)* ;] to be a slave, be subject to, serve: absol., Rom.7:6, Gal.4:25, 1Ti.6:2; with dative of person(s),…

douloo.notes: δουλόω, -ῶ (δοῦλος), [in LXX for עָבַד ;] to enslave, bring into bondage: Act.7:6 (LXX), 2Pe.2:19; metaphorically, 1Co.9:19; pass., before ἐν, 1Co.7:15; τ. θεῷ, Rom.6:22; τ. δικαιοσύνῃ, Rom.6:18; οἴνῳ, Tit.2:3; ὑπὸ…

doulos.notes: δοῦλος, -η, -ον, [in LXX, ὁ δ. nearly always for עֶבֶד; ὁ δ. chiefly for שִׁפְחָה ,אָמָה ;] __1. in bondage to, subject to: Rom.6:19. __2. As subst., ὁ, ἡ δ., a slave; __(a) fem., ἡ δ., a female slave, bondmaid…

doxa.notes: δόξα, -ης, ἡ (δοκέω), [in LXX very frequently for כָּבוֹד, also for הוֹד ,פִּפְאֶרֶת, etc., 25 words in all ;] in cl., __1. expectation, judgment, opinion (4Ma.5:18). __2. opinion, estimation in which one is held,…

doxazo.notes: δοξάζω (δόξα), [in LXX chiefly for כָּבַד ni., pi., also for פָּאַר, etc. ;] __1. to think, suppose, hold an opinion (Æsch., Plat., al.). __2. To bestow δόξα (which see) on, to magnify, extol, praise (Thuc., iii, 45;…

drachme.notes: δραχμή, -ῆς, ἡ (δράσσομαι), [in LXX: in Hex. for in בֶּקַע, שֶׁקֶל Ezra for אֲדַרְכֹּן, דַּרְכְּמוֹן ;] a drachma, nearly equal to the Roman denarius (see: δηνόριον): Luk.15:8-9 (DCG, ii, 200).† (AS)

drakon.notes: δράκων, -ο τος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for תַּנִּין ;] a dragon, a mythical monster: figuratively, of Satan, Rev.12:3-17 13:2, 4 13:11 16:13 20:2.† (AS)

drassomai.notes: δράσσομαι [in LXX for נָשַׁק pi., Psa.2:12; elsewhere קָמַץ, as Lev.2:2 ;] to grasp with the hand, to lay hold of: metaphorically, with accusative (M, Pr., 65), 1Co.3:19 (LXX).† (AS)

drepanon.notes: δρέπανον, -ου, τό (later form of Attic δρεπάνη, δρέπω, to pluck), [in LXX for מַזְמֵדָה, חֶרְמֵשׁ, etc. ;] a sickle, pruning-hook; Mrk.4:29, Rev.14:14-19.† (AS)

dromos.notes: δρόμος, -ου, ὁ (δραμεῖν, see: τρέχω) [in LXX chiefly for מְרוּצָה ;] a course: figuratively, of life or ministry, Act.13:25 20:24, 2Ti.4:7.† (AS)

drousilla.notes: Δρούσιλλα (Rec. Δρουσίλλα), ης, ἡ Drusilla, wife of Felix: Act.24:24.† (AS)

dunamai.notes: δύναμαι, depon., [in LXX chiefly for יָכֹל ;] to be able, have power, whether by personal ability, permission, or opportunity: with inf. (M, Pr., 205; WM, §44, 3) pres., Mat.6:24, Mrk.2:7, Jhn.3:2, 1Co.10:21, al.;…

dunamis.notes: δύναμις, -εως, ἡ (δύναμαι), [in LXX for עֹז ,גּבוּרָה ,צָבָה ,(חֵיל) חַיִל, etc.; 35 words in all ;] power, might, strength; relatively, ability, power to perform: Mat.25:15, Act.3:12, Heb.11:11; κατὰ δ., 2Co.8:3;…

dunamoo.notes: δυναμόω, -ῶ (δύναμις), [in LXX for עָזַז, Psa.52:7 68:28; גָּבַר pi., hi., Ecc.10:10, Da TH 9:27 * ;] to make strong, strengthen: Eph.6:10 (WH, mg.; ἐνδυν-, WH, txt., RV), Col.1:11, Heb.11:34.† (AS)

dunastes.notes: δυνάστης, -ου, ὁ (δύναμαι), [in LXX for גִּבּוֹר, עָרִיץ, בַּיִת, etc. ;] a prince, ruler, potentate: Luk.1:52; of God (Sir.46:5, 16, 2Ma.15:3), 1Ti.6:15; of a high official (cf. δυνάσται Φαραώ, Gen.50:4), Act.8:27…

dunateo.notes: δυνατέω, -ῶ (δυνατός), to be able, be powerful, mighty: with inf., Rom.14:4, 2Co.9:8; absol., opposite to ἀσθενῶ 2Co.13:3.† (AS)

dunatos.notes: δυνατός, -ή, -όν (δύναμαι), [in LXX for גִּבּוֹר, חַיִל, etc. ;] __1. strong, mighty, powerful: absol., Luk.1:49, 1 Col.1:26; οἱ δ., the chief men, Act.25:5 ; of spiritual strength, Rom.15:1, 2Co.12:10 13:9; before…

dunodumi.notes: δύνω Ion, and trag. form of δύω, [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא ;] to enter, sink into; of the sun (sc. όντον), set: Mrk.1:32, Luk.4:40 (cf. ἐκ-, ἀπ-εκ-, ἐν-, ἐπ-εν-, παρ-εισ-, ἐπι-δύνω).† δύω, see: δύνω δύνω Ion, and…

duo.notes: δύο, numeral, indecl. exc. in dative, δυσί, δυσίν (Attic δυοῖν), two: Mat.19:6, Mrk.10:8, Jhn.2:6, al.; with pl. noun, Mat.9:27 10:10, al.; οἱ, τῶν, τοὺς δ., Mat.19:5 20:24, Mrk.10:8, Eph.2:15, al.; δ. ἐξ, Luk.24:13;…

dusbastaktos.notes: δυσ-βάστακτος, -ον (βαστάζω), [in LXX for נֵטֶל, Pro.27:3 * ;] hard to be borne: Mat.23:4 (om. WH, txt., R, mg.), Luk.11:46.† (AS)

dusenteria.notes: δυσεντερία, -ας, ἡ, Rec. for δυσεντέριον (which see), Act.28:8.† (AS)

duskolos.notes: δύσκολος, -ον (κόλον, food), [in LXX for אֵיד, Jer.29:9 (Jer.49:8) (of. δυσκολία, Job.34:30; εὔκολος, 2Ki.15:3) * ;] __1. properly, of persons, hard to satisfy with food, hence, generally, hard to please (Eur.,…

dusme.notes: δυσμή, -ῆς, ἡ (= δύσις, δύνω), [in LXX for בּוֹא, עֲרָבָה, etc. ;] mostly in pl., opposite to ἀνατολαί; __1. a setting, as of the sun (accusative to Thayer, see word, so perhaps in Luk.12:54). __2. the quarter of…

dusphemia.notes: δυσφημία, -ας, ἡ (δύσφημος, slanderous), [in LXX: 1Ma.7:38, 3Ma.2:20 * ;] evil-speaking, defamation: opposite to εὐφημία, 2Co.6:8.† (AS)

ean.notes: ἐάν, contr. fr. εἰ ἄν, conditional particle, representing something as "under certain circumstances actual or liable to happen," but not so definitely expected as in the case of εἰ with ind. (Bl., §65, 4; cf.…

eao.notes: ἐάω, -ῶ, [in LXX for רפה hi., etc. ;] __1. to let, permit: with accusative, with inf., with accusative and inf. (M, Pr., 205): Mat.24:43, Luk.4:41 22:51, Act.14.16; 16.7, 19:30 23.32 27:32 28:4 , 1Co.10:13. __2. to…

eber.notes: Ἔβερ (Rec. Ἐβέρ), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. עֵבֶר, Gen.10:24), Eber (OT, Heber): Luk.3:35.† (AS)

echeo.notes: ἠχέω, -ῶ (ἦχος), [in LXX for הָמָה, etc. ;] to sound: 1Co.13:1 (cf. ἐξ-, κατ-ηχέω).† (AS)

echidna.notes: ἔχιδνα, -ης, ἡ [in OT (Aq.), Isa.59:5 * ;] a viper: Act.28:3; metaphorically, γεννήματα ἐχιδνῶν, Mat.3:7 12:34 23:33, Luk.3:7.† (AS)

echos.notes: ἦχος, -ου, ὁ (also in late and MGr., -εος, τό, Luk.21:25), [in LXX for הָמוֹן, etc. ;] __1. a noise, sound: Act.2:2; ἦ. θαλάσσης, Luk.21:25; σάλπιγγος, Heb.12:19 __2. a report: before πεφί, Luk.4:37.† (AS)

echthra.notes: ἔχθρα, -ας, ἡ (ἐχθρός), [in LXX for אֵיבָה, שִׂנְאָה, etc. ;] enmity: Luk.23:12, Rom.8:7, Gal.5:20, Eph.2:15-16, Jas.4:4.† ἀηδία, -ας, ἡ (ἀ- neg., ἦδος, pleasure), [in LXX: Pro.23:29 (שִׂיחַ)* ;] __1. of things or…

echthros.notes: ἐχθρός, -ά, -όν (ἔχθος, hatred), [in LXX chiefly for אֵיבָה, also for עָר, etc. ;] __1. hated, hateful (Hom.): opposite to ἀγαπητός, Rom.11:28. __2. Actively, hating, hostile: Rom.5:10, 1Co.15:25, 2Th.3:15; with…

edaphizo.notes: ἐδαφίζω (ἔδαφος) [in LXX chiefly for רטשׁ pu. ;] __1. to beat level like a threshing floor (Theophr.). __2. to dash to the ground (Field, Notes, 74): Luk.19:44 (cf. Psa.136(137):9, Hos.14:1).† (AS)

edaphos.notes: ἔδαφος, -εος (-ους), τό, [in LXX for עָפָר, קַרְקע, etc. ;] bottom, pavement, ground: Act.22:7.† (AS)

ede.notes: ἤδη adv., in NT, always of time; now, already: Mat.3:10, Mrk.4:37, Luk.7:6, Jhn.4:36, al.; νῦν . . . ἤ., now already, 1Jn.4:3; ἤ. ποτέ, now at length, with fut., Rom.1:10 (cf. ἄρτι) (AS)

egchrio.notes: ἐγχρίω, [in LXX: Jer.4:30 (קרע). Tob.2:10 6:8 11:7 * ;] to rub in, anoint: mid., with dupl. accusative, Rev.3:18.† (AS)

egeiro.notes: ἐγείρω, [in LXX for קוּם, etc. ;] trans. (imperat. ἔγειρε used intransitively, Mat.9:5, Mrk.2:11, al.); __1. to awaken, arouse from sleep: Mrk.4:38, Act.12:7; metaphorically, of spiritual awakening, Rom.13:11…

egersis.notes: ἔγερσις, -εως, ἡ (ἐγείρω, -ομαι)), [in LXX: Jdg.7:19, Psa.138 (139)2 (קוּם), I Est.5:62 * ;] __1. a rousing (Plat.). __2. a rising (Ps, l.with): from death, Mat.27:53.† (AS)

egkainia.notes: ἐγκαίνια, see: ἐνκ- ἐνκαίνια (Rec. ἐγκ-), -ων, τά (ἐν, καινός), [in LXX for HSjrj, II Est.6:16, 17, Neh.12:27, Da TH 3:2 (and cf. ἐγκαινισμός, Num.7:10, al., -ισις, Num.7:88) *;] dedication (anniversary of the…

egkainizo.notes: ἐγκαινίζω, see: ἐνκ-. ἐν-καινίζω (Rec. ἐγκ-, see: ἐν), [in LXX: (to renew) 1Ki.11:14, 2Ch.15:8, Ps 50(51):10 (Br-m pi.); (to dedicate) Deu.20:5, 2Ch.7:5 (-^jn); Isa.16:11 41:1 45:16 (aliter in Heb.), Si 33(36):6,…

egkaleo.notes: ἐγκαλέω, -ῶ, [in LXX for אמר, etc.; with dative, Zec.1:4 (קָרָא אֶל), Wis.12:12, Sir.46:19 ;] __1. to call in, demand. __2. to bring a charge against, accuse: with dative of person(s) (as in cl.), Act.19:38 23:28;…

egkataleipo.notes: ἐγκαταλείπω, [in LXX chiefly for עזב ;] __1. to leave behind: ἡμῖν σπέρμα, Rom.9:29 (LXX). __2. to abandon, desert, forsake: with accusative of person(s), Mat.27:46 (LXX), Mrk.15:34 (ib.), Act.2:27 (LXX) (WH, ἐνκ-),…

egkathetos.notes: ἐνκάθετος (Rec. ἐγκ-), -αν (ἐγκαθίημι), [in LXX: Job.31:9 (אָרַב), Job.19:12 * ;] suborned to lie in wait, lying in wait: as subst., Luk.20:20.† (AS)

egkatoikeo.notes: ἐγκατοικέω, see: ἐνκ-. ἐν-κατοικέω, -ῶ (Rec. ἐγκ-, see: ἐν), to dwell among: before ἐν, 2Pe.2:8.† (AS)

egkentrizo.notes: ἐγκεντρίζω, see: ἐνκ-. ἐν-κεντρίζω (Rec. ἐγρ-, see: ἐν; κεντρίζω, to graft), [in LXX: Wis.16:11 *;] to ingraft, graft in. figuratively, with accusative of person(s), Rom.11:17,19,23,24.† (AS)

egklema.notes: ἔγκλημα -τος, τό (ἐγκαλέω), an accusation, charge: Act.23:29 25:16 (Cremer, 743).† (AS)

egkomboomai.notes: ἐγκομβόομαι, -οῦμαι (κόμβος, a knot, whence ἐγκόμβωμα, a garment tied on over others, used especially of a frock or apron worn by slaves), to put on oneself, as a garment, gird on: ἀλλήλοις τ. ταπεινοφροσύνην (as for…

egkopto.notes: ἐγκόπτω, see: ἐνκ-. ἐν-κόπτω (Rec. ἐγκ-, see: ἐν; and in I Pe, l.with, ἐκκ-); 1. to cut into (as in breaking up a road), hence, 2. to hinder: with accusative Act.24:4, 1Th.2:18, . inf., Gal.5:7; before τοῦ, with…

egkrateia.notes: ἐγκράτεια, -ας, ἡ (-ἐγκρατής), [in LXX: Sir.18:15, 30 4Ma.5:34 * ;] __1. prop., mastery, control. __2. (sc. ἑαθτοῦ) self-control: Act.24:25, Gal.5:23, 2Pe.1:6 (see DB, iv, 558b 695a; Page on Ac, l.with).† (AS)

egkrates.notes: ἐγκρατής, -ές (κράτος), [in LXX: Wis.8:20, Sir.6:27 15:1 26:15 27:30, al. ;] __1. strong, powerful. __2. C. genitive of thing(s), master of, hence, __3. (sc. ἑαυτοῦ), self -controlled, exercising self-control:…

egkrateuomai.notes: ἐγκρατεύομαι, depon., [in LXX for אפק, Gen.43:31, 1Ki.13:12, Est.5:10 א 3 * ;] to exercise self-control: 1Co.7:9; with accusative, πάντα (see Bl., 91), 1Co.9:25.† (AS)

egkrino.notes: ἐγ-κρίνω, see: ἐνκ-. ἐν-κρίνω (Rec. ἐγκ-, see: ἐν), to reckon among : ἑαυτούς, 2Co.10:12.† (AS)

egkrupto.notes: ἐγ-κρύπτω, [in LXX for טמן, etc. ;] to conceal in: with accusative, before εἰς, Mat.13:33.† (AS)

egkuos.notes: ἔγκυος, see: ἐνκ-. ἔνκυoς (Rec. ἐγκ-, see: ἐν), -ον (κύω, to conceive), [in LXX: Sir.42:10 * ;] pregnant, big with child : Luk.2:5.† (AS)

ehis.notes: εἷς ιά, ἕν, genitive ἑνός, μιᾶς, ἑνός, cardinal numeral, one; __1. one, as opposite to many: Mat.25:15, Rom.5:12, 1Co.10:8, al.; as subst., Rom.5:15, Eph.2:14; id. with genitive partit., Mat.5:19, al.; before ἐκ…

ehurisko.notes: εὑρίσκω, [in LXX chiefly for מצא, also for נשׂג hi., etc. ;] to find, with or without previous search: absol., opposite to ζητέω, Mat.7:7, 8 Luk.11:9, 10; with accusative, Mat.2:8, Mrk.1:37, Act.13:22, 2Ti.1:17, al.;…

ei.notes: εἰ, conjunctive particle, used in conditions and in indirect questions. __I. Conditional, if; __1. with indic, expressing a general assumption; __(a) pres.: before indic, pres., Mat.11:14, Rom.8:25, al.; before…

eido.notes: οἶδα, (from same root as εἶδον, which see), [in LXX chiefly for ידע ;] pf. with present meaning (plpf. as impf.; on irregular tense-forms, see App.), to have seen or perceived, hence, to know, have knowledge of: with…

eidoleion.notes: εἰδώλον (Rec. -εῖον), -ου, τό (εἴδωλον), [in LXX: Dan LXX 1:2 (בֵּית אוֹצַר אֱלֹהִים), Bel 9, I Est.2:10, 1Ma.1:47 10:83 * ;] an idol's temple: 1Co.8:10 (cf. Ἀσταρτεῖον, 1Ki.31:10; see ICC, in I Co, l.with).† (AS)

eidololatreia.notes: εἰδωλο-λατρία (-εία, Rec.), -ας, ἡ (εἴδωλον, λατρεία), idolatry: 1Co.10:14, Gal.5:20, Col.3:5; pl. (Bl, §32, 6), 1Pe.4:3 (Cremer, 390).† (AS)

eidololatres.notes: εἰδωλολάτρης, -ου, ὁ (εἴδωλου + λάτρις, a hireling), an idolator: 1Co.5:10, 11 6:9 10:7 Eph.5:5 Rev.21:8 22:15 (Cremer, 709).† (AS)

eidolon.notes: εἴδωλον, -ου, τό (εἶδος), [in LXX for אֱלוֹהָּ, גְּלוּלִים, etc. ;] __1. in cl. __(a) a phantom, image, likeness; __(b) an image in the mind, an idea, fancy. __2. In LXX and NT, __(a) an image of a god, an idol (cf.…

eidolothutos.notes: εἰδωλόθυτος, -ον, (εἴδωλον, θύω), [in LXX: 4Ma.5:2 * ;] sacrificed to idols: τὸ, τὰ εἰ, Act.15:29 21:25, 1Co.8:1, 4 7, 10 10:19, Rev.2:14, 2o.† (AS)

eidos.notes: εἶδος, -ους, τό, [in LXX for מַרְאֶה,, etc. ;] __1. that which is seen, appearance, external form: Luk.3:22 9:29, Jhn.5:37, 2Co.5:7 (ICC in l.). __2. form, sort, kind: 1Th.5:22.† (AS)

eike.notes: εἰκῇ (-ῆ, Rec., as in cl.), adv., [in LXX: Pro.28:25 (εἰκῇ)* ;] __1. without cause or reason: Mat.5:22 (E, mg.), Col.2:18 (ICC). __2. vainly, fruitlessly, to no purpose: Rom.13:4, 1Co.15:2, Gal.3:4 4:11, Col.2:18.† (AS)

eiko.notes: εἴκω, [in LXX: 2Ki.12:7 (ענה), Wis.18:25, 4Ma.1:6 * ;] to yield: Gal.2:5 (cf. ὑπ-είκω).† (AS)

eikon.notes: εἰκών, -όνος (cf. ἔοικα), [in LXX chiefly for צֶלֶם ;] an image, likeness: Mat.22:20, Mrk.12:16, Luk.20:24, Rom.1:23, 1Co.15:49, Rev.13:14, 15 14:9, 11 15:2 16:2 19:20 20:4; opposite to σκιά, Heb.10:1; of man, εἰ.…

eikosi.notes: εἴκοσι (never -ιν in WH, cf. Bl., §5, 3, and note), indecl., οἱ, αἱ, τά, twenty: Luk.14:31, Jhn.6:19, Act.1:15 27:28, 1Co.10:8, Rev.4:4, 10 5:8 11:16 19:4.† (AS)

eilikrineia.notes: εἰλικρινία (Rec., cl., -κρινεία), -ας, ἡ (εἰλικρινής), [in LXX: Wis.7:25 A * ;] sincerity, purity: 1Co.5:8, 2Co.1:12 2:17.† (AS)

eilikrines.notes: εἰλικρινής, -ές, [in LXX: Wi 72^ kB * ;] unalloyed, pure (Lat. sincerus; see DCG, ii, 635a); __(a) of unmixed substances; __(b) of abstract ideas; __(with) of ethical purity: Php.1:10, 2Pe.3:1.† SYN.: ἁγνός (which…

eiper.notes: εἴ-περ, see: εἰ. εἰ, conjunctive particle, used in conditions and in indirect questions. __I. Conditional, if; __1. with indic, expressing a general assumption; __(a) pres.: before indic, pres., Mat.11:14, Rom.8:25,…

eipon.notes: εἶπον, 2 aorist of obsolete present ἔπω (cf. Veitch), used as aorist of λέγω, which see (AS)

eirene.notes: εἰρήνη, -ης, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for שָׁלוֹם ;] peace; __1. of public peace, freedom from war: Luk.14:32, Act.12:20 24:2; of the church, Ac 9:31. __2. Of peace between persons, concord, agreement : Mat.10:34,…

eireneuo.notes: εἰρηνεύω (εἰρήνη), [in LXX chiefly for שׁלם, שׁקט ;] __1. to bring to peace, reconcile (so 1Ma.6:60). __2. to keep peace, be at peace: Mrk.9:50, Rom.12:18, 2Co.13:11, 1Th.5:13 (cf. Sir.28:9; Cremer, 246).† (AS)
