eirenikos.notes: είρηνικός, -ή, -όν (εἰρήνη), [in LXX for שָׁלוֹם and cognates ;] peaceful: Heb.12:11, Jas.3:17.† (AS)

eirenopoieo.notes: εἰρηνο-ποιέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Pro.10:10 * ;] to make peace: Col.1:20.† (AS)

eisago.notes: εἰσ-άγω [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא, hi. ;] to bring in: with accusative, Luk.2:27, Jhn.18:16, Act.7:45; before εἰς, Luk.22:54, Act.9:8 21:28-29, 37 22:24, Heb.1:6; ὧδε, Luk.14:21.† (AS)

eisakouo.notes: είσ-ακούω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁמַע, also for עָנָה, etc. ;] to listen to, in two senses; __(a) to obey: 1Co.14:21 (cf. Deu.1:43, Sir.3:6) __(b) to listen, assent to; pass., to be heard: of persons praying, Mat.6:7,…

eisdechomai.notes: εἰσ-δέχομαι [in LXX for קָבַץ ;] to admit, receive: 2Co.6:17 (LXX) (Cremer, 687).† (AS)

eiseimi.notes: εἴσ-ειμι [in LXX for בּוֹא ;] to go in, enter: before εἰς, Act.3:3 21:26, Heb.9:6; πρὸς Ἰάκωβον, Act.21:18.† (AS)

eiserchomai.notes: εἰσ-έρχομαι, [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא ;] to go in or into, enter: Mat.9:25, Luk.7:45, al.; before εἰς, Mat.10:12, Mrk.2:1, al.; before διά (πύλης, θύρας, etc.), Mat.7:13, Jhn.10:1, al.; ὑπὸ τ. στέγην, Mat.8:8; with…

eiskaleo.notes: εἰσ-καλέω, -ῶ, to call in: mid., with accusative of person(s), Act.10:23.† (AS)

eisodos.notes: εἴσ-οδος, -ου, ὁ (ὁδός) [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא ;] __1. a means of entering, place of entrance: Heb.10:19, 2Pe.1:11 (cf. Westc., He., l.with; MM, Exp., xii; but see infr.). __2. a going in, entrance: Act.13:24;…

eispedao.notes: εἰσ-πηδάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Amo.5:19 (בּוֹא), Da TH Sus 1:26* ;] to spring in, rush in: Act.14:14 (Rec.), Act.16:29 (for exx. from π., see MM, Exp., xii).† (AS)

eisphero.notes: εἰσ-φέρω [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא hi. ;] to bring in, into: with accusative of person(s), Luk.5:18-19; before εἰς, Mat.6:13, Luk.11:4; ἐπί, Luk.12:11; with accusative of thing(s), before εἰς, 1Ti.6:7; pass.,…

eisporeuo.notes: εἰσ-πορεύομαι [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא ;] to go into, enter: Luk.8:16 11:33 19:30; before εἰς, Mrk.1:21 6:56 11:2, Luk.22:10, Act.3:2; πρός, with accusative of person(s), Act.28:30; ὅπου, Mrk.5:40; κατά τ. οἴκους,…

eistrecho.notes: εἰσ-τρέχω [in LXX: 2Ma.5:26 * ;] to run in: Act.12:14.† (AS)

eita.notes: εἶτα adv., denoting sequence; __1. of time; then, next: Mrk.8:25, Luk.8:12, Jhn.13:5 19:27 20:27, 1Ti.3:10, Jas.1:15; before genitive abs., Mrk.4:17; in enumerations, 1Co.15:5 15:7 15:24, 1Ti.2:13. __2. In argument;…

eite.notes: εἴτε, see: εἰ. εἰ, conjunctive particle, used in conditions and in indirect questions. __I. Conditional, if; __1. with indic, expressing a general assumption; __(a) pres.: before indic, pres., Mat.11:14, Rom.8:25,…

ekballo.notes: ἐκ-βάλλω [in LXX chiefly for גָּרַשׁ pi., also for שָׁלַךְ hi., יָצָא hi., יָרַשׁ hi., etc. ;] __1. to drive, cast or send out, to expel: with accusative of thing(s), mid. (σῖτον), before εἰς, Act.27:38; pass.,…

ekbasis.notes: ἔκ-βασις, -εως, ἡ (ἐκβαίνω), [in LXX: Wis.2:17 8:8 11:14 * ;] __1. a way out (Hom., Xen.): 1Co.10:13. __2. the issue (Menand.) Heb.13:7.† (AS)

ekbole.notes: ἐκ-βολή, -ῆς (ἐκβάλλω), [in LXX: Exo.11:1 (גָּרַשׁ), Jos.1:5 (ἐκβολὴν ποιεῖσθαι, טול hi.), Eze.47:8 (יָצָא)* ;] __1. a throwing out. __2. a jettison, a throwing overboard of cargo: Act.27:18 (cf. Jos, l.with; and see…

ekcheo.notes: ἐκ-χέω also Hellenistic, ἐκχύνω (in Th.: 4Ki.14:14 *), and ἐκχύννω (which see) [in LXX chiefly for שָׁפַךְ ;] to pour out: φιάλην, Rev.16:1-4, 8 16:10, 12 16:17; κέρματα, Jhn.2:15; αἷμα, Mat.23:35 (cf. MM, Exp.,…

ekchoreo.notes: εκ-χύννω Hellenistic form of ἐκχέω, which see (E1., § 17): Mat.23:35 26:28, Mrk.14:24, Luk.5:37 11:50 22:20, Act.1:18 10:45 22:20, Rom.5:5, Ju 11.† ἐκ-χωρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Num.16:45 (רוּם ni.), Jdg.7:3 (צָפַר),…

ekdapanao.notes: εκ-δαπανάω, -ῶ strengthened form of δαπανάω, to spend wholly; pass., with reflexive force, to spend oneself wholly: before ὑπέρ, 2Co.12:15.† (AS)

ekdechomai.notes: ἐκ-δέχομαι [in LXX for עֶרֶב, קָבַץ, etc. ;] __1. to take or receive from (Hom., Hdt., a1.). __2. (Rare in cl.), to expect, await: with accusative of thing(s), Jhn.5:3, Heb.11:10, Jas.5:7; with accusative of…

ekdelos.notes: ἔκ-δηλος, -ον (δῆλος), [in LXX: 3Ma.3:19 3Mac 6:5 * ;] strengthened form of δῆλος, which see, quite clear, evident: 2Ti.3:9.† (AS)

ekdemeo.notes: ἐκ-δημέω, -ῶ (ἔκδημος, from home; cf. -ία, 3Ma.4:11)*; to be from home, absent: 2Co.5:6; before ἀπό, 2Co.5:8; before ἐκ, 2Co.5:9 (cf. ἀπο-, ἐνδημέω).† (AS)

ekdidomi.notes: ἐκ-δίδωμι [in LXX for נָתַן, etc. ;] __1. to surrender, give up, give out. __2. to let out for hire (Hdt.); mid. (as frequently in π.; see MM, Exp., xii); to let out to one's advantage, Mat.21:33, 41, Mrk.12:1,…

ekdiegeomai.notes: ἐκ-δι-ηγέομαι, -οῦμαι depon., [in LXX chiefly for סָפַר, pi. ;] to tell in detail, relate, declare: Act.13:41 (LXX) Act.15:3.† (AS)

ekdikeo.notes: ἐκδικέω, -ῶ (ἔκδικος), [in LXX for פָּקַד, נָקַם, שָׁפַט, etc. ;] __1. to vindicate: with accusative of person(s), Luk.18:3, 5. __2. to avenge: with accusative of person(s), ἑαυτούς, Rom.12:19; with accusative of…

ekdikesis.notes: ἐκ-δίκησις, -εως, ἡ (ἐκδικέω), [in LXX chiefly for נָקָם ;] vengeance, vindication: Luk.21:22, Rom.12:19 (see MM, Exp., xii), Heb.10:30 (LXX), 2Co.7:11; of the injured person, with genitive, Luk.18:7-8; with dative,…

ekdikos.notes: ἔκδικος, -ον (δίκη), [in LXX: Wis.12:12, Sir.30:6, 4Ma.15:29 * ;] __1. without law, unjust. __2. exacting penalty from; as subst., an avenger: Rom.13:4; before περί, 1Th.4:6 (in π., a legal representative; Milligan,…

ekdioko.notes: ἐκ-διώκω [in LXX for בָּרַח hi., צָמַת, הָדַף, etc. ;] to chase away, drive out: 1Th.2:15 (cf. Deu.6:19, Jol.2:20).† (AS)

ekdoche.notes: ἐκ-δοχή ῆς, ἡ (ἐκδέχομαι); __1. in cl., __(a) a receiving from, succession; __(b) an interpretation. __2. In NT, = προσδοκία, expectation: Heb.10:27 (cf. Field, Notes, 231; Cremer, 688).† (AS)

ekdotos.notes: ἔκ-δοτος, -ον (ἐκδίδωμι), [in LXX: Da TH Bel 1:22 * ;] given up, delivered over: Act.2:23 (for construction, see Field, Notes, 111 f.).† (AS)

ekduo.notes: ἐκ-δύω [in LXX chiefly for פָּשַׁט ;] to take of, strip of, strip: with accusative of person(s) (sc. clothing), Mat.27:28; with accusative of person(s) and of thing(s), Mat.27:31, Mrk.15:20, Luk.10:30; mid., to put…

ekei.notes: ἐκεῖ adv., [in LXX chiefly for שָׁם ;] __1. properly, of place, there: Mat.2:13 5:24, al.; οἱ ἐ., Mat.26:71; οὗ . . . ἐ., Mat.6:21 18:20 24:28, Mrk.6:10, Luk.12:34; pleonastic, ὅπου . . . ἐ. (= שָׁם אֲשֶׁר, Deu.4:5,…

ekeise.notes: ἐκεῖσε adv., [in LXX: Job.39:29 שָׁם * ;] thither: Act.21:3; constr. pregn. (MM, Exp., xii; Field, Notes, 134), τοὺς ἐ. ὄντας, Act.22:5.† (AS)

ekeithen.notes: ἐκεῖθεν adv., [in LXX chiefly for שָׁם ;] __1. of place, thence: Mat.4:21, Mrk.6:1, al. __2. Of time, thereafter (see: κἀκεῖθεν). (AS)

ekgonos.notes: ἔκ-γονος, -ον (ἐκγίγνομαι, to be born of), [in LXX for פְּרִי (neut.), בֵּן, etc. ;] __1. with genitive, born of. __2. As subst., ὁ, ἡ ἔ., a child, son or daughter; in pl., descendants: τέκνα ἢ ἔ., children or…

ekkaio.notes: ἐκ-καίω [in LXX chiefly for בָּעַר ;] __1. to burn up. __2. to kindle. Pass., to burn: metaphorically, of the passions (cf. Sir.16:6, Job.3:17), Rom.1:27.† (AS)

ekkakeo.notes: ἐκκακέω, -ῶ, see: ἐνκακέω.ἐν-κακέω, -ῶ (LTr., ἐγκ-; Rec. ἐκκ-; cf. WH, Notes, 157 f.; κακός, cowardly), [in Sm.: Gen.27:46, Num.21:5, Pro.3:11, Isa.7:16 *;] to lose heart : Luk.18:1, 2Co.4:1,16, Gal.6:9, Eph.3:13,…

ekkathairo.notes: ἐκ-καθαίρω [in LXX for בָּעַר, etc. ;] __1. to cleanse thoroughly, cleanse out: with accusative, ἑαυτόν, 2Ti.2:21; of the impurity removed, ζυμήν, 1Co.5:7.† (AS)

ekkenteo.notes: ἐκ-κεντέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for דָּקַר ;] __1. to prick out, put out (Arist.). __2. to pierce (Polyb., LXX): with accusative of person(s), Jhn.19:37 (LXX), Rev.1:7.† (AS)

ekklao.notes: ἐκ-κλάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Lev.1:17 (שָׁסַע pi.)* ;] to break off: pass., Rom.11:17-20.† (AS)

ekkleio.notes: ἐκ-κλείω [in LXX: ἐ. κρίσιν, for נָטָה hi., Exo.23:2 (also as seel., Job.34:20, Psa.67:1-7 (Psa.68:30) * ;] to shut out: Gal.4:17. Pass., Rom.3:27.† (AS)

ekklesia.notes: ἐκκλησία, -ας, ἡ (ἐκ-καλέω), [in LXX chiefly for קָהָל, otherwise for one of its cogn. forms ;] __1. prop., an assembly of citizens regularly convened (in Thuc., ii, 22, opposite to σύλλογος, a concourse): Act.19:32,…

ekklino.notes: ἐκ-κλίνω [in LXX for נָטָה, סוּר, etc. ;] intrans., to turn aside, turn away: metaphorically, from the right path, absol., Rom.3:12 (LXX); from evil, absol., 1Pe.3:11; before ἀπό, with genitive of person(s),…

ekkolumbao.notes: to swim out of , with genitive, (Euripides) (ML)

ekkomizo.notes: ἐκ-κομίζω to carry out: as freq., a corpse for burial, Luk.7:12.† (AS)

ekkopto.notes: ἐκ-κόπτω, [in LXX for כָּרַת, etc. ;] to cut out, cut off, cut down: of a hand, foot, Mat.5:30 18:8; a tree, Mat.3:10 7:19 Luk.3:9 13:7, 9; figuratively, of a branch, Rom.11:22; before ἐκ, Rom.11:24; metaphorically,…

ekkremannumi.notes: ἐκ-κρέμαννυμι, [in LXX for קָשַׁר, Gen.44:30 * ;] to hang from or upon; mid., ἐκκρέμαμαι: figuratively, ἐξεκρέματο αὐτοῦ άκούων (Rec.; WH read ἐξεκρέμετο, which implies a Pres. ἐκκρέμομαι, otherwise unknown; cf.…

eklaleo.notes: ἐκ-λαλέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Jdth.11:9 * ;] to speak out, divulge: Act.23:22.† (AS)

eklampo.notes: ἐκ-λάμπω [in LXX for אוֹר hi., etc. ;] to shine forth: Mat.13:43.† (AS)

eklanthano.notes: ἐκ-λανθάνω [in Sm.: Psa.13:2 * ;] to escape notice utterly; mid., to forget utterly: Heb.12:5.† (AS)

eklego.notes: ἐκ-λέγω [in LXX chiefly for בָּחַר ;] to pick out, choose. In NT always mid. (exc. Luk.9:35, ἐκλελεγμένος, WH, ἀγαπητός, R, mg.), to pick out for oneself, choose (cf. M, Pr., 157 f.): with accusative of thing(s),…

ekleipo.notes: ἐκ-λείπω [in LXX for כָּלָה, כָּרַת ni., תָּמַם, etc., 47 different words in all ;] __1. trans., to leave out, pass over. __2. Intrans., to leave off, cease, fail: μαμωνᾶς, Luk.16:9; πίστις, Luk.22:32; ἔτη, Heb.1:12…

eklektos.notes: ἐκ-λεκτός, -ή, -όν (ἐκλέγω), [in LXX for בָּחַר (so prob. in Isa.28:16, Pro.17:3, for MT בָּחַן), בָּרִיא, etc. ;] __1. choice, select (cl., rarely; Thuc., Plat., al.), hence, eminent: Rom.16:13 (cf. Eze.27:24). __2.…

ekloge.notes: ἐκλογή, ῆς ἡ (ἐκλέγω), [in Aq.: Isa.22:7; Sm., Th.: Isa.37:24 * ;] a choice, selection; in NT, always of the Divine choice (EV, election): σκεῦος ἐκλογῆς, genitive qual., a chosen vessel; κατ ἐ., Rom.9:11 11:5, 28;…

ekluo.notes: ἐκ-λύω [in LXX for רָפָה, etc. ;] __1. to loose, release. __2. to unloose, as a bow-string, to relax, enfeeble; pass., to be faint, grow weary: Mat.15:32, Mrk.8:3; of mental weariness, Gal.6:9, Heb.12:3, 5" (LXX) .† (AS)

ekmasso.notes: ἐκ-μάσσω [in LXX: Sir.12:11, Ep. Jer.13:1-27 24:1-10 * ;] to wipe off: with accusative, Luk.7:38, 44, Jhn.11:2 12:3 13:5.† (AS)

ekmukterizo.notes: ἐκ-μυκτηρίζω [in LXX: Psa.2:4 22:7 35:16 (לָעַג), I Est.1:51 A* ;] to hold up the nose in derision at, scoff at: with accusative, Luk.16:14 23:35.† (AS)

eknepho.notes: ἐκ-νήφω [in LXX: Gen.9:24, Hab.2:7 (יָקַץ), Jol.1:5, Hab.2:19 (יָקַץ hi.), 1Ki.25:37 (יָצָא), Sir.31:2 * ;] to become sober after drunkenness: metaphorically, of sobriety of mind, 1Co.15:34.† (AS)

ekneuo.notes: ἐκ-νεύω [in LXX: Jdg.4:18 (סוּר) Jdg.18:26, 4Ki.2:24 23:16 (פָּנָה), Mic.6:14 (סוּג hi.), 3Ma.3:22 * ;] __1. to bend the head aside (Xen.). __2. __(a) to shun, avoid (Diod.); __(b) to withdraw: Jhn.5:13† (AS)

ekpalai.notes: ἔκ-παλαι adv. (of a class of compound adverbs common in late Gk.; see Mayor on 2 Pe, l.with), for a long time, from of old: 2Pe.2:3 3:5.† (AS)

ekpeirazo.notes: ἐκ-πειράζω [in LXX: Deu.6:16 8:2, 16 Psa.78:18 (נָסָה pi.) * ;] = cl. ἐκπειράομαι, to put to the proof or test, make trial of, tempt: with accusative, of God, Mat.4:7 (LXX), Luk.4:12 (ib.), 1Co.10:9; of Christ,…

ekpempo.notes: ἐκ-πέμπω [in LXX for שָׁלַח ;] to send forth: Act.13:4 17:10.† (AS)

ekpetazo.notes: ἐκ-πετάννυμι [in LXX chiefly for פָּרַשׂ, as Isa.65:2 (hithp.) ;] to spread out (as a sail), stretch forth: Rom.10:21(LXX).† (AS)

ekphero.notes: ἐκ-φέρω [in LXX chiefly for יָצָא hi. ;] __1. to carry out, bring out: with accusative of thing(s), Luk.15:22, 1Ti.6:7; with accusative of person(s), Mrk.8:23, Act.5:15; of the dead for burial (cf. κομίζω), Act.5:6,…

ekpheugo.notes: ἐκ-φεύγω [in LXX for נוּס, etc. ;] to flee away, escape: absol., Act.16:27, 1Th.5:3, Heb.2:3; before ἐκ, Act.19:10; with accusative of person(s), Heb.12:25; with accusative of thing(s), Luk.21:36, Rom.2:3; τ. χεῖρας…

ekphobeo.notes: ἐκ-φοβέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for חָרֵד hi. ;] to frighten away, terrify: with accusative of person(s), 2Co.10:9.† (AS)

ekphobos.notes: ἔκφοβος, -ον [in LXX: ἔ. εἶναι for יָגֹר, Deu.9:19; also 1Ma.13:2 * ;] affrighted, terrified: Mrk.9:16, Heb.12:21.† (AS)

ekphuo.notes: ἐκ-φύω [in OT (Sm.) Psa.104:14; (Al.) Isa.61:11 * ;] to cause to grow out, put forth (leaves): Mat.24:32, Mrk.13:28.† (AS)

ekpipto.notes: ἐκ-πίπτω [in LXX: Isa.40:8 28:1, 4 (הֶבֶל), Job.14:2 (מָלַל, see RV. mg), Job.15:30 (סוּר), Job.15:33 (שָׁלַךְ hi.), etc. ;] to fall out of, fall from, fall off: before ἐκ τ. χειρῶν, Act.12:7; absol., Act.27:32; of…

ekpleo.notes: ἐκ-πλέω, -ῶ to sail away: Act.20:6; before εἰς, Act.15:39 18:18.† (AS)

ekpleroo.notes: ἐκ-πληρόω [in LXX: 2Ma.8:10, 3Ma.1:2 3Mac 1:22 * ;] __1. to fill full, make up a number. __2. to fulfil (MM, Exp., xii; Cremer, 839), Act.13:32.† (AS)

ekplerosis.notes: ἐκ-πλήρωσις, -εως, ἡ) [in LXX: 2Ma.6:14 * ;] a completion, fulfilment: Act.21:26.† (AS)

ekplesso.notes: ἐκ-πλήσσω (Attic -ττω, Act.13:12), [in LXX: (pass.) Ecc.7:16, (שָׁמֵם hithp.), Wis.13:4, 2Ma.7:12, 4Ma.8:4 4Mac 17:16 * ;] __1. prop., to strike out, drive away. __2. to strike with panic or shock, to amaze,…

ekpneo.notes: ἐκ-πνέω, -ῶ, to breathe out; sc. βίον, ψυχήν (expressed in cl., Æsch., al.; cf. LS, see word), to breathe one's last, expire: Mrk.15:37, 39 Luk.23:46. For force of aorist, see Swete, Mrk.15:37.† (AS)

ekporeuo.notes: ἐκ-πορεύω [in LXX chiefly for יָצָא ;] to make to go out; pass. and mid., to go forth: Luk.3:7, Act.25:4; of demons leaving one possessed, Mat.17:21 (WH om.), Act.19:12; of excrement, Mk 719; before ἀπό, Mat.20:29,…

ekporneuo.notes: ἐκ-πορνεύω [in LXX chiefly for זָנָה, frequently of spiritual unfaithfulness ;] strengthened form of πoρνεύω, implying excessive indulgence; mid, to give oneself up to fornication: Ju 7.† (AS)

ekpsucho.notes: ἐκ-ψύχω [in LXX: Jdg.4:21 A (עוּף), Eze.21:7 (כָּהָה pi.)* ;] to expire, breathe one's last: Act.5:5, 10 12:23 (cf. ἐκπνέω; Cremer, 906).† (AS)

ekptuo.notes: ἐκ-πτύω __1. to spit out. __2. to spit at in disgust, to abominate, loathe (= cl. ἀποπτ-, καταπτ-): Gal.4:14.† (AS)

ekrizoo.notes: ἐκ-ριζόω, -ῶ [in LXX: Jdg.5:14 (שֶׁרֶשׁ), Jer.1:10 (נָתַשׁ), Zep.2:4 (גָּרַשׁ A, עָקַר BS), Dan.7:8 (עָקַר), Dan LXX 4:11, 23, Wis.4:4, Sir.3:9 49:7, 1Ma.5:51, 2Ma.12:7 * ;] to root out, pluck up by the roots: with…

ekstasis.notes: ἔκ-στασις, -εως, ἡ (ἐξίστημι) [in LXX: Gen.27:33, 1Ki.14:15, Eze.26:16, al. (חֲרָדָה); 2Ch.14:14 17:10 20:29 (פַּחַד), al. ;] __1. a displacement (Arist.). __2. An abnormal condition of the mind, in which the subject…

ekstrepho.notes: ἐκ-στρέφω [in LXX: Deu.32:20, Amo.6:13 Eze.16:34 A (הָפַךְ), Za 11:16 (פָּרַק pi.), Eze.13:20 (צוּד pi.)* ;] __1. to turn out of (Hom.). __2. to turn inside out; metaphorically, to change entirely, pervert…

ektarasso.notes: ἐκ-ταράσσω [in LXX: Psa.18:4 (עֵת pi.), Psa.88:16 (צוּם), צוּם), Wis.17:3-4 18:17 * ;] to throw into great trouble, agitate: Act.16:20.† (AS)

ekteino.notes: ἐκ-τείνω [in LXX for שָׁלַח, נָטָה, etc. ;] to stretch out or forth: τ. χεῖρα (as often in LXX), Mat.8:3 12:13 14:31 26:51, Mrk.1:41 3:5, Luk.5:13 6:10 Jhn.21:18, Act.26:1; before ἐπί, with accusative of person(s),…

ekteleo.notes: ἐκ-τελέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Deu.32:45 (כָּלָה), 2Ch.4:5, (Da TH Dan.3:40), 2Ma.15:9 * ;] to bring to an end, finish, complete: Luk.14:29-30.† (AS)

ektenes.notes: ἐκτενής, -ές (ἐκτείνω), [in LXX: 3Ma.3:10 3Mac 5:29 * ;] stretched, strained. Metaphorical, earnest, zealous: 1Pe.4:8 .† (AS)

ektenesteron.notes: deeply, earnestly intensely, fervently, earnestly, deeply Lk. 22:44; Acts 12:5; 1Pet. 1:22*

ektenos.notes: ἐκτενῶς adv., [in LXX: Jon.3.8 (חׇזְקָה), Jol.1:14, Jdth.4:12, 3Ma.5:9 * ;] fervently, earnestly: Act.12:5, 1Pe.1:22; compar., Luk.22:44 (WH br., R, mg., omits).† (AS)

ekthambeo.notes: ἐκ-θαμβέω, -ῶ (ἔκθαμβος), [in LXX: Sir.30:9 * ;] __1. to be amazed. __2. to amaze, terrify (Sir.30:9). Pass., to be amazed, terrified: Mrk.9:15 14:33 16:5-6.† (AS)

ekthambos.notes: ἔκ-θαμβος, -ον (θάμβος), [in LXX: Wis.10:19, Da TH Dan.7:7 (dreadful, terrible: אֵמְתָּנִי) * ;] amazed (cf. Polyb., xx, 10, 9): Act.3:11.† (AS)

ekthetos.notes: ἔκ-θετος, -ον (ἐκτίθημι), [in Al.: Eze.42:3 * ;] cast out: ποιεῖν ἔ. = ἐκτιθέναι, Act.7:19.† (AS)

ektinasso.notes: ἐκ-τινάσσω [in LXX for נָעַר ni., pi., etc. ;] to shake off: κονιορτόν, Mat.10:14; χοῦν, Mrk.6:11. Mid.: κονιορτόν, Act.13:51; ἱμάτια, Act.18:6 (cf. MM, Exp., iii).† (AS)

ektithemi.notes: ἐκ-τίθημι [in LXX for נָתַן ni., etc. ;] to set out, expose: Act.7:21, Metaphorical, to set forth, expound: Act.11:4 28:23; with accusative of thing(s), Act.18:26.† (AS)

ektrepho.notes: ἐκ-τρέφω [in LXX for גָּדַל, etc. ;] __1. prop., of children, to nurture, bring up: Eph.6:4. __2. to nourish: Eph.5:29.† (AS)

ektrepo.notes: ἐκ-τρέπω [in LXX for הָפַךְ, Amo.5:8 * ;] to turn out of the course, turn aside, with accusative Pass., with middle sense, intrans., to turn aside: Heb.12:13 (R, txt., for be put out of joint, R, mg., see Thayer, see…

ektroma.notes: ἔκ-τρωμα, -τος, τό (ἐκτιτρώσκω, to miscarry), [in LXX: Job.3:16, Ecc.6:3 (; also in Aq., Psa.58:9, Num.12:12 (נָפַל) * ;] an abortion, an untimely birth (see Field, Notes, 179): 1Co.15:8.† (AS)

ekzeteo.notes: ἐκ-ζητέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for דָּרַשׁ, also for נָצַר, בָּקַשׁ, etc. ;] __1. to seek out or after, search for: with accusative of person(s) (1Ma.9:26); figuratively, τ. κύριον, θεόν (cf. Psa.14:2, Amo.5:4, al.),…

elachistos.notes: ἐλάχιστος, -η, -ον (see: ἐλάσσων), smallest, least: as proper superlat., 1Co.15:9; elsewhere, as usually in late Gk., intensive (B1., § 11, 3); Mat.2:6 (LXX) Mat.25:40, 45 Luk.12:26 16:10 19:17, 1Co.4:3 6:2, Jas.3:4;…

elachistoteros.notes: ἐλαχιστότερος (for corresp. superl., see LS; see also Bl., § 44, 3); less than the least: Eph.3:8.† (AS)

elaia.notes: ἐλαία (Attic, ἐλάα), -ας, ἡ [in LXX for זַיִת ;] __1. an olive tree: Rom.11:17, 24, Rev.11:4; τ. ὄρος τῶν ἐ. (זַיִת הַר, Zec.14:4), the Mount of Olives: Mat.21:1 24:3 26:30, Mrk.11:1 13:3 14:26, Luk.19:37 22:39; τὸ…

elaion.notes: ἔλαιον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for שֶׁמֶן ;] olive-oil: Luk.16:6, Rev.6:6 18:13; for lamps, Mat.25:3-4, 8; for healing, Mrk.6:13, Luk.10:34, Jhn.5:14; for anointing at feasts, Luk.7:46, Heb.1:9" (LXX) .† SYN.:…

elamites.notes: Ἐλαμείτης (Rec. -αμίτης), -ου, ὁ (Heb. אֵילָם) [in LXX (cl.) Ἐλυμαίος, Ἔλαμος: Jdth.1:6; Ἀιλαμείτης (vv.ll. Ἐλ-, -αμίτης; Bl., § 3, 7): Isa.11:11 21:2 22:6) ;] an Elamite: Act.2:9.† (AS)

elaphros.notes: ἐλαφρός, -ά, -όν [in LXX chiefly for קַל, קָלָל ;] light in weight easy to bear: Mat.11:30; θλίψρις (EV, our light affliction), 2Co.4:17.† (AS)

elasson.notes: ἐλάσσων (-ττων, Heb_7:7, WH, 1Ti_5:9), -ον (formed, with superl. ἐλάχιστος, from the epic ἐλαχύς, little, and serving as compar. of μικρός), [in LXX for מָעַט, etc. ;] less, in age, rank or quality: Jhn.2:19,…

elattoneo.notes: ἐλαττονέω, -ῶ (ἔλαττον), [in LXX (with -όω) chiefly for חָסֵר ;] to be less (RV, had no lack): 2Co.8:15 (LXX) (a rare word; cf. MM, Exp., xii).† (AS)

elattoo.notes: ἐλαττόω, -ῶ (ἐλάιττων), [in LXX (where also -σσῶ) chiefly for חָסֵר, and very frequently in Sir ;] to make less: Heb.2:7 (LXX); pass., Jhn.3:30, Heb.2:9.† (AS)

elauno.notes: ἐλαύνω [in LXX: Isa.41:7 (הָלַם) Isa.33:21 (שַׁיִט), etc. ;] to drive: of the wind, Jas.3:4, 2Pe.2:17; of sailors rowing or sailing a boat, Mrk.6:48, Jhn.6:19; of demons, Luk.8:29 (cf. ἀπ-, συν-ελαύνω).† (AS)

eleazar.notes: Ἐλεάζαρ(Heb. אֶלְעָזָר), ὁ, indecl., Eleazar: Mat.1:15.† (AS)

eleeinos.notes: ἐλεεινός, -ή, -όν (ἔλεος in Re, l.with, WH have the Attic poëtic form, ἐλεινός), pitiable, miserable: Rev.3:17; comparat., 1Co.15:19.† (AS)

eleemon.notes: ἐλεήμων, -ον [in LXX chiefly for חַנּוּן ;] merciful: Mat.5:7, Heb.2:17.† (AS)

eleemosune.notes: ἐλεημοσύνη, -ης, ἡ (ἐλεέω), [in LXX chiefly for חֶסֶד, צְדָקָה ;] __1. mercy, pity. __2. almsgiving, alms (like the German Almosen, a corruption of the Greek word ἐ): Mat.6:4; ποιεῖν ἐ., Mat.6:2-3, Act.9:36 10:2…

eleeo.notes: ἐλεέω (in Rom.9:16, Ju 22, -άω, which see), -ῶ (ἔλεος), [in LXX (Hex, Pss, Pr) chiefly for חָנַן, also frequently in Proph. for רָחַם, etc. ;] to have pity or mercy on, to show mercy: absol., Rom.9:16 12:8; with…

elegcho.notes: ἐλέγχω [in LXX chiefly for יָכַח hi. ;] __1. in Hom., to treat with contempt. __2. to convict: with accusative, Mat.18:15 (RV, show him his fault), Tit.1:9; before περί, Jhn.8:46 16:8, Ju 15; pass., Jas.2:9. __3. to…

elegchos.notes: ἔλεγχος, -ου, ὁ (ἐλέγχω) [in LXX: frequently in Pr, Jb (יָכַח) יָכַח) Wis.5:1-23, Sir.3:1-31, etc. ;] a proof, test: Heb.11:1.† (AS)

eleos.notes: ἔλεος, -ους, τό (cl. -ου, ὁ, and so Rec., Mat.9:13 12:7 23:23, Tit.3:5, Heb.4:16; on the Hellenistic form τό ἔ., see WH, App., 158; M, Pr., 60; Mayser, 277; Kühner, i, 515), [in LXX chiefly for חֶסֶד ;] mercy, pity,…

elephantinos.notes: ἐλεφάντινος, -η, -ον (ἐλέφας, ivory), [in LXX for שֵׁן ;] of ivory: Rev.18:12.† (AS)

eleusis.notes: a coming:—;the Advent of our LORD, (New Testament) (ML)

eleutheria.notes: ἐλευθερία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Lev.19:20 (חֻפְשָׁה), 1Es.4:49 Est.4:53, Sir.7:21, Sir.33:25, 1Ma.14:27, 3Ma.3:28 * ;] liberty: with reference to the religious life, 1Co.10:29, 2Co.3:17, Gal.2:4 5:1, 1Pe.2:16, 2Pe.2:19; ὁ…

eleutheroo.notes: ἐλευθερόω, -ῶ [in LXX: Pro.25:10. 2Ma.1:27 2Mac 2:22 * ;] to make free from sin, Jhn.8:32, 36; before ἀπό, Rom.6:18, 22 8:2, 21; τ. ἐλευθερία (dative commodi), Gal.5:1 (on the "punctiliar" force of this verb, see M,…

eleutheros.notes: ἐλεύθερος, -α, -ον [in LXX chiefly for חׇפְשִׁי ;] free; __(a) in civil sense, not a slave: Jhn.8:33, 1Co.7:21-22 12:13, Gal.3:28, Eph.6:8, Col.3:11, Rev.6:15 13:16 19:18; fem., Gal.4:22-23, 30; __(b) as regards…

eli.notes: ἡλεί (Rec. ἠλί L, ἠλί; Heb. אֵל), Eli, my God: Mat.27:46 (cf. ἐλωΐ).† (AS)

eliakeim.notes: Ἐλιακείμ(Heb. אֶלְיָקִים), Eliakim, an ancestor of Jesus: Mat.1:13, Luk.3:30.† (AS)

eliezer.notes: Ἐλιέζερ(Heb. אֱלִיעֶזֶר), ὁ, indecl., Eliezer, an ancestor of Jesus: Luk.3:29.† (AS)

elioud.notes: Ἐλιούδ, ὁ indecl., Eliud, an ancestor of Jesus: Mat.1:14-15.† (AS)

elisabet.notes: Ἐλεισάβετ (T, Rec. Ἐλισ-; see WH, App., 155), ἡ indecl. (Heb. אֱלִישֶׁבַע) Elizabeth: Luk.1:5 ff.† (AS)

elissaios.notes: Ἐλισαῖος (Rec. Ἐλισσαῖος; Τ, Ἑλισ-), -ου, ὁ (Heb. אֱלִישָׁע), Elisha, the prophet: Luk.4:27.† (AS)

ellogeo.notes: ἐλλογάω, -ῶ (a κοινή word, elsewhere usually -έω; cf. Bl., § 22, 2), to charge to one's account, impute: Phm 18 (on parallels, cf. Deiss., LAE, 79 f., 335 f.; Milligan, NTD, 73; MM, Exp., xii); of sin, Rom.5:13…

elmodam.notes: Ἐλμαδάμ (L, Ἑλ-; Rec. -μωδάμ), ό, indecl., Elmadam, an ancestor of Jesus: Luk.3:28. (AS)

eloi.notes: ἐλωί (-ΐ Rec.; ἑλωΐ LT; Aram. אֱלָהּ), Eloi: Mat.27:46, Mrk.15:34" (LXX) .† (AS)

elpis.notes: ἐλπίς (ἑλ-, Rom.8:20, WH, see Bl., § 4, 3; M, Pr., 44), -ίδος, ἡ [in LXX for בָּטַח and its derivatives, תִּקְוָה (frequently in Jb), etc. ;] expectation (in cl., rarely of evil, mostly of good, and so always in NT),…

elpizo.notes: ἐλπίζω [in LXX chiefly for בָּטַח, also for חָסָה, יָחַל pi., hi., etc. ;] to look for, expect, hope (for): with accusative of thing(s), Rom.8:24-25, 1Co.13:7, Heb.11:1; with dative of thing(s) (τ. τύχῃ, Thuc., iii,…

elumas.notes: Ἐλύμας, -α, ὁ (Aram. or Arab., cf. DB, i, 246 b) Elymas: Act.13:8.† (AS)

embaino.notes: ἐμ-βαίνω [in LXX for עָלָה, etc. ;] to step into: Jhn.5:4 (WH, RV omit); εἰς πλοῖον, to embark: Mat.8:23 9:1 13:2 14:22 15:39, Mrk.4:1 5:18 6:45 8:10, 13, Luk.5:3 8:22, 37, Jhn.6:17, 24 21:3, Act.21:6.† (AS)

emballo.notes: ἐμ-βάλλω [in LXX for שׂוּם, etc. ;] to cast into: before εἰς, Luk.12:5 (cf. MM, Exp., vii, 93).† (AS)

embapto.notes: ἐμ-βάπτω to dip in: τ. χεῖρα ἐν τ. τρυβλίῳ, Mat.26:23; mid., before εἰς, Mrk.14:20.† (AS)

embateuo.notes: ἐμβατεύω [in LXX: with accusative, Jos.19:49 (נָחַל, Jos.19:51 (חָלַק pi.); before εἰς, 1Ma.12:25 1Mac 13:20 1Mac 14:31 1Mac 15:40; metaphorically, 2Ma.2:30 * ;] (ἐμβάτης ἐμβαίνω) __1. to step in or on (Soph.), hence…

embibazo.notes: ἐμ-βιβάζω [in LXX: 4Ki.9:23 (רָכַב hi.), Pro.4:11 (דָּרַךְ hi.) * ;] __1. to set in, put in. __2. to put on board ship, embark: with accusative of person(s), before εἰς, Act.27:6.† (AS)

emblepo.notes: ἐμ-βλέπω [in LXX for רָאָה, 3Ki.8:8, al.), פָּנָה (Job.6:28 A, al.); metaphorically, Isa.51:1 (נָבַט hi.), Sir.2:10, etc. ;] to look at: with accusative of thing(s), Mrk.8:25; with dative of person(s) (part., before…

embrimaomai.notes: ἐμ-βριμάομαι (T, -έομαι; Bl., § 22, 1), -ῶμαι (βρίμη, strength, bulk, whence βριμάομαι, to snort with anger), depon., with aor. mid. and pass., [in LXX (Hatch, Essays, 25): Dan LXX 11:30 (also Aq., Psa.7:12; Sm.,…

emeo.notes: ἐμέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Isa.19:14 (קֵא)* ;] to vomit: figuratively, Rev.3:16.† (AS)

emmainomai.notes: ἐμ-μαίνομαι depon., to rage against: with dative, Act.26:11.† (AS)

emmanouel.notes: Ἐμμανουήλ, ὁ (Heb. עִמָּנוּאֵל, Isa.7:14), Immanuel: Mat.1:23 (LXX).† (AS)

emmaous.notes: Ἐμμαούς, ἡ Emmaus, a place 60 furlongs from Jerusalem: Luk.24:13.† (AS)

emmeno.notes: ἐμμένω [in LXX chiefly for קוּם ;] __1. to abide in: Act.28:30. __2. to abide by, be true to: before ἐν; τ. πίστει, Act.14:22; τ. διαθήκῃ, Heb.8:9 (LXX); with dative, τ. γεγραμμένοις (dative ptcp. as in legal…

emmor.notes: Ἑμμώρ (T, Ἐμμώρ, Rec. -όρ indecl. (Heb. חֲמוֹר), Emmor (Gen.33:19): Act.7:16.† (AS)

emos.notes: ἐμός, -ή, -όν poss. prop. of first of person(s), representing the em­phasized genitive ἐμοῦ, mine, subjectively and objectively, i.e. belonging to, proceeding from or related to me: Mat.18:20, Mrk.8:38, Jhn.3:29…

empaigmos.notes: ἐμ-παιγμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἐμπαίζω, which see), [in LXX: Eze.22:4 (קַלָּסָה), Ps 38:7 B א1 קָלָה ni.), Wis.12:2, Sir.27:28, 2Ma.7:7, 3Ma.5:22 * ;] a mocking: Heb.11:36.† (AS)

empaiktes.notes: ἐμ-παίκτης, -ου, ὁ (ἐμπαίζω, which see), [in LXX: Isa.3:4 (תַּעֲלוּל) * ;] a mocker: 2Pe.3:3, Ju 18.† (AS)

empaizo.notes: ἐμ-παίζω [in LXX for עָלַל hithp., שָׂחַק, etc. ;] = Attic προσ-, καταπαίζω, to mock at, mock (Hdt.): with dative, Mat.27:29, 31, Mrk.15:20, Luk.14:29 22:63 23:36; pass., Mat.2:16, Luk.18:32; absol., Mat.20:19 27:41,…

emperipateo.notes: ἐν-περι-πατέω, -ῶ (Rec. ἐμπ-, see: ἐν) [in LXX: Lev.26:12 Job.1:7, al. (הָלַךְ hithp.), Wis.19:21 ;] to walk about in or among: before ἐν., dative of person(s), 2Co.6:16 (LXX),† (AS)

emphanes.notes: ἐμφανής, -ές (ἐμφαίνω, to show in, exhibit), [in LXX: Mic.4:1, Isa.2:2 (כּוּן ni.), Wis.6:22 7:21 14:17; ἐ. γίνεσθαι, Exo.2:14 (יָדַע ni.), Isa.65:1 (דָּרַשׁ ni.)* ;] manifest: Act.10:40; metaphorically, Rom.10:20…

emphanizo.notes: ἐμφανίζω [in LXX for יָדַע hi., etc. ;] __1. to manifest, exhibit: ἐαυτόν, with dative of person(s), Jhn.14:21-22 (DCG, ii, 112b). Pass. and mid., to show oneself, appear: Mat.27:53, Heb.9:24 (cf. MM, Exp., xii).…

emphobos.notes: ἔμ-φοβος [in LXX: Sir.19:24, 1Ma.13:2 * ;] __1. terrible. __2. in fear (of Godly fear, Sir.19:24), terrified: Luk.24:5, 37, Act.10:4 24:25, Rev.11:13.† (AS)

emphusao.notes: ἐμ-φυσάω, -ῶ (φυσάω, to blow), [in LXX for נָפַח, etc. ;] to breathe into (cf. Gen.2:7, Wis.15:11, a1.), breathe upon: Jhn.20:22.† (AS)

emphutos.notes: ἔμ-φυτος, -ον (ἐμφύω, to implant), [in LXX: ἔ. ἡ κακία αὐτῶν, Wis.12:10 * ;] __1. innate (Wis.12:10). __2. rooted, implanted: Jas.1:21 (see Mayor, in l).† (AS)

empimplemi.notes: ἐμ-πίπλημι (on ἐμπίμπ-, see LS, see word, Bl., § 6, 8), and ἐμπιπλάω (Act.14:17), [in LXX chiefly for מָלֵא, שָׂבַע ;] to fill full, fill up, satisfy: with accusative of person(s) and genitive of thing(s), Luk.1:53,…

empipto.notes: ἐμ-πίπτω [in LXX chiefly for נָפַל ;] to fall into: before εἰς, Mat.12:11, Luk.6:39 10:36; metaphorically, εἰς κρίμα, 1Ti.3:6; ὀνειδισμόν 1Ti.3:7; πειρασμόν, 1Ti.6:9; εἰς χεῖρας θεοῦ (cf. 2Ki.24:14, 1Ch.21:13,…

empleko.notes: ἐμ-πλέκω [in LXX: Pro.28:18 (נָפַל), 2Ma.15:17 * ;] to weave in, entwine; pass., metaphorically, to be involved, entangled in: 2Ti.2:4, 2Pe.2:20.† (AS)

emploke.notes: ἐμ-πλοκή, ῆς, ἡ (ἐμπλίκω), a braiding: τριχῶν, 1Pe.3:3.† (AS)

empneo.notes: ἐν-πνέω, -ῶ (Rec. ἐμπ-, see: ἐν) [in LXX: Deu.20:16, Jos.10:28 ff. Jos.11:11, 14 (ptcp. neut., for נֶפֶשׁ, נְשָׁמָה), Wis.15:11 * ;] __1. to breathe on. __2. to breathe; __(a) absol.; __(b) with genitive part.:…

emporeuomai.notes: ἐμ-πορεύομαι depon. (ἔμπορος), [in LXX chiefly for סָחַר ;] __1. to travel, esp. for business. __2. to traffc, trade: Jas.4:13. __3. C. accusative of thing(s) __(a) to traffic in; __(b) to import: (Hos.12:1, for…

emporia.notes: ἐμπορία, -ας, ἡ (ἔμπορος), [in LXX for סָחַר, רָכַל etc. ;] commerce, business, trade: Mat.22:5.† (AS)

emporos.notes: ἔμ-πορος, -ου, ὁ (πόρος, a journey), [in LXX chiefly for סָחַר, רָכַל ;] __1. a passenger on shipboard, one on a journey. __2. a merchant: Mat.13:45, Rev.18:3, 11 18:15, 23.† (AS)

empretho.notes: ἐμ-πίπρημι, ἐμπρήθω (for the form, see: ἐμπίπλημι, and cf. Veitch, see word πίμπρημι), [in LXX chiefly for שָׂרַף ;] to set on fire: πόλιν, Mat.22:7; pass., of the body, to become inflamed: Act.28:6 (T; πίμπρημι WH,…

emptuo.notes: ἐμ-πτύω [in LXX: before εἰς, Num.12:14 A, Deu.25:9 (יָרָק)* ;] = cl., καταπτύω (Ruth., NPhr., 66), to spit upon: with dative, Mrk.10:34 14:65 15:19; before εἰς, Mat.26:67 27:30. Pass., Luk.18:32.† (AS)

enagkalizomai.notes: ἐν-αγκαλίζομαι (ἀγκάλη), [in LXX for חִבֻּק pi., Pro.6:10 24:33 * ;] to take into one's arms: Mrk.9:36 10:16.† (AS)

enalios.notes: ἐν-άλιος, -ον (also -α, -ον; ἅλς, the sea), of the sea: τὰ ἐ., marine creatures, Jas.3:7.† (AS)

enantios.notes: ἐν-αντίος, -α, -ον (ἀντίος, set against), [in LXX: ἐξ ἐναντίας, for נֶגֶד, etc.; ἐναντίον, for פָּנִים etc. ;] over against, opposite, contrary: ἄνεμος, Mat.14:24, Mrk.6:48, Act.27:4; ἐξ ἐναντίας (ellipse obscure,…

enarchomai.notes: ἐν-άρχομαι [in LXX chiefly for חָלַל hi. ;] to begin, make a beginning: Gal.3:3, Php.1:6.† (AS)

endechomai.notes: έν-δέχομαι [in LXX: Psa.119:122 (עָרַב), 2Ma.11:18 * ;] __1. to admit, approve. __2. to be possible; impers., ἑνδέχεται, it is possible: with accusative and inf., Luk.13:33 (Cremer, 687).† (AS)

endees.notes: ἐνδεής, -ές (ἐνδέω, to lack) [in LXX for חָסֵר, אֶבְיוֹן, etc. ;] in want, needy: Act.4:34,† (AS)

endeigma.notes: ἔν-δειγμα, -τος (ἐνδείκνυμι), a plain token, proof: 2Th.1:5 (cf. ἔνδειξις, which refers rather to the "act of proving"; ἔ., with the passive formation, to the thing proved, see Lft., Notes, 100; M, Th., l.with).†…

endeiknumi.notes: ἐν-δείκνυμι [in LXX for לָכַד, גָּמַל, רָאָה hi. ;] to mark, point out. Mid„ __1. to show forth, prove: with accusative of thing(s), Rom.2:15 9:22, Eph.2:7, Tit.2:10 3:2, Heb.6:11; before ἐν, with dative of…

endeixis.notes: ἔν-δειξις, -εως, ἡ (ἐνδείκνυμι), a pointing out, showing forth, proof (see: ἔνδειγμα): Rom.3:25-26, 2Co.8:24, Php.1:28.† (AS)

endemeo.notes: ἐνδημέω, -ῶ (ἔνδημος, living in a place), to live in a place, be at home: ἐν τ. σώματι, 2Co.5:6 5:9; πρὸς τ. Kύριον, 2Co.5:8.† (AS)

endidusko.notes: ἐνδιδύσκω [in LXX: 2Ki.1:24 13:18, Pro.31:21 (לָבַשׁ), Jdth.9:1 10:3, Sir.50:11 * ;] to put on: with dupl. accusative, Mrk.15:17 (ἐνδύουσι, Rec.). Mid., to put on oneself, be clothed in: with accusative of thing(s),…

endomesis.notes: ἐν-δώμησις (Rec. -δόμησις), -εως, ἡ (δωμάω, to build), a build­ing in: ἡ ἐ. τ. τείχους αὐτῆς ἴασπις, its wall had jasper built into it, Rev.21:18 (see MM, Exp., xiii; Swete, Ap., l.with).† (AS)

endoxazomai.notes: ἐν-δοξάζω [in LXX for כָּבַד ni., Exo.14:4, 17-18, Eze.28:22, etc.; for עָרַץ, Psa.89:7; Sir.38:6, al. ;] to glorify: pass., 2Th.1:10-12.† (AS)

endoxos.notes: ἔνδοξος, -ον (δόξα [in LXX for כָּבַד, etc. ;] __1. held in honour, of high repute: 1Co.4:10. __2. glorious, splendid: of deeds, τά ἔ., Luk.13:17; of clothing, Luk.7:25. Metaphorical, ἐκκλησία, Eph.5:27 (cf.…

enduma.notes: ἔνδυμα, -τος, τό (ἐνω), [in LXX chiefly for לְבוּשׁ ;] raiment, clothing, a garment: Mat.3:4 6:25, 28 7:15 22:11-12 28:3, Luk.12:23.† (AS)

endunamoo.notes: ἐν-δυναμόω, -ῶ [in LXX: Jdg.6:34, 1Ch.12:18 A (לָבַשׁ), Psa.52:7 (עָזַז)* ;] to make strong, strengthen: with accusative, of person(s), Php.4:13, 1Ti.1:12, 2Ti.4:17. Pass., Act.9:22; C. dative, Rom.4:20; before ἐν,…

enduno.notes: ἐν-δύνω, see: ἐνδύω. ἐν-δύω (ἐνδύνω, 2Ti.3:6), [in LXX chiefly for לָבַשׁ ;] __1. with accusative of person(s), Mat.27:28 (WH, mg., R, mg.); with dupl. accusative, Mat.27:31, Mrk.15:20, Luk.15:22; mid., to put on…

enduo.notes: ἐν-δύω (ἐνδύνω, 2Ti.3:6), [in LXX chiefly for לָבַשׁ ;] __1. with accusative of person(s), Mat.27:28 (WH, mg., R, mg.); with dupl. accusative, Mat.27:31, Mrk.15:20, Luk.15:22; mid., to put on oneself, be clothed…

enecho.notes: ἐν-έχω [in LXX: Gen.49:23 (סָתַם), Eze.14:4, 7, 3Ma.6:10 * ;] __1. to hold in; pass., to be held, entangled: with dative of thing(s); jig., ζυγῷ δουλείας, Gal.5:1 (cf. MM, Exp., xii); θλίψρεσιν, 2Th.1:4 (cf.…

enedra.notes: ἐν-έδρα, -ας, ἡ (ἕδρα, a seat), [in LXX: Jos.8:7-9, Psa.10:8 (אָרַב)* ;] a lying in wait, an ambush: Act.23:16 (Rec. ἔνεδρον, a form frequently in LXX), Act.25:8.† (AS)

enedreuo.notes: ἐνεδρεύω (ἐνέδρα), [in LXX chiefly for אָרַב ;] to lie in wait for: with accusative of person(s), Luk.11:54, Act.23:21.† (AS)

eneileo.notes: ἐν-ειλέω, -ῶ [in LXX 1Ki.21:9 לוּט * ;] to roll in, wind in: with accusative of person(s) and dative of thing(s), Mrk.15:46.† (AS)

eneimi.notes: ἔν-ειμι __1. to be in, within (Job.27:3, al.): ptcp. pl., τὰ ἐνόντα, Luk.11:41 (R, txt., cf. MM, Exp., xii). __2. to be possible: Luk.11:41 (R, mg.).† (AS)

enenekonta.notes: ἐννενήκοντα, see: ἐνεν-. ἐνενήκοντα (Rec. ἐννεν-), οἱ, αἱ, τά indecl., ninety: Mat.18:12-13, Luk.15:4, 7.† (AS)

energeia.notes: ἐνέργεια, -ας, ἡ (ἐνεργής), [in LXX: Wis.7:17, 26 13:4 18:22, 2Ma.3:29, 3Ma.4:21 3Mac 5:12 3Mac 5:28 * ;] operative power (as distinct from δύναμις, potential power), working: of God, Eph.1:19 3:7 4:16, Php.3:21,…

energeo.notes: ἐνεργέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Num.8:24 B (עֲבֹדָה צָבָא), Isa.41:4, Pro.21:6 (פָּעַל), Pro.31:12 (גָּמַל), I Est.2:20, Wis.15:11 16:17 * ;] (for full lexical treatment, see AR, Eph., 243 ff.); __1. intrans., to be at work or…

energesenarges.notes: ἐνεργής, -ές (late form of ἐνεργός, on WH. cf. AR, Eph., 241), at work, active, effective: 1Co.16:9, Phm 6, Heb.4:12 (Cremer, 261).† (AS)

eneulogeomai.notes: ἐν-ευλογέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for בָּרַךְ ;] to bless: pass., before ἐν, Act.3:25 (LXX), Gal.3:8 (LXX) (Cremer, 770).† (AS)

engizo.notes: ἐγγίζω (ἐγγύς). [in LXX for נגשׁ, קרב, etc. ;] __1. trans., to bring near: Gen.48:10, Isa.5:8. __2. Intrans., to come near: absol., Mat.26:46, Mrk.14:42, Luk.18:40 19:41 21:20, 28 24:15, Act.21:33 23:15; with adv.,…

engrapho.notes: 1. to mark in or on, to paint on , (Herdotus Historicus) 2. to inscribe, write in or on , (Herdotus Historicus):—;Pass., ἐγγεγραμμένος τι having something written on it, (Sophocles Tragicus); so Virgil flores…

enguos.notes: ἔγγυος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ, [in LXX: Sir.29:15, 16 2Ma.10:28 * ;] a surety: Heb.7:22 (exx. from π., see MM, Exp., xi; cf. Cremer, 222).† (AS)

enguteros.notes: ἐγγύτερος, see: ἐγγύς. ἐγγύς, adv., [in LXX chiefly for קָרוֹב ;] near; __1. of place: Jhn.19:20, 42; as prep. with genitive (M, Pr., 99), Luk.19:11, Jhn.3:23 6:19, 23 11:18, 54, Act.1:12; with dative, Act.9:38 27:8;…

eni.notes: ἔνι Ionic form of ἐν (ἐνί), with strengthened accent; [in LXX: Sir.37:2, 4Ma.4:22 * ;] = ἔνεστι, is in, has place, can be: 1Co.6:5, Gal.3:28 (tris) Col.3:11, Jas.1:17 (cf. Lft., Ga.; Hort and Mayor, Ja., ll. with).† (AS)

eniautos.notes: ἐνιαυτός, -oῦ, ὁ [in LXX for שָׁנֶה ;] __1. prop., a cycle of time. __2. = ἔτος, a year: Jhn.11:49, 51 18:13, Act.11:26 18:11, Jas.5:17, Rev.9:15; pl., of sabbatical years, Gal.4:10; ποιεῖν ἐ., to spend a year,…

enischuo.notes: ἐν-ισχύω [in LXX for חָזַק, etc. ;] to strengthen: in spiritual sense, Luk.22:43; pass., Act.9:19 (Rec. ἐνισχύσεν, became strong, as in LXX, Gen.12:10 48:2, al.).† (AS)

enistemi.notes: ἐν-ίστημι [in LXX: 4Ki.13:6 A (עֶמְדָּה), 3Ki.12:24, 1Es.5:47 Est.9:6, Est.3:13, 1; 4Ma.9.1-32 * ;] to place in; in pf., plpf., 2 aor. and in mid., intrans.; __(a) to be at hand, impend, threaten: 2Ti.3:1; __(b) to…

ennatos.notes: ἐνατος, (Rec. ἔνν-), -η, -ον ninth: Rev.21:20; of the ninth hour (3 o'clock, p.m.), Mat.20:5 27:45-46, Mrk.15:33-34, Luk.23:44, Act.3:1 10:3, 30.† (AS)

ennea.notes: ἐννέα, οἱ, αἱ, τά, indecl., nine: Luk.17:17; ἐνενήκοντα ἐ., Mat.18:12-13, Luk.15:4, 7.† (AS)

enneos.notes: ἐνεός (Rec. ἐνν-), -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX: Isa.56:10 (אִלֵּם), Ep. Jer.41:1-18; ἐ. ποιεῖν, Pro.17:28 * ;] dumb, speechless: Act.9:7.† (AS)

enneuo.notes: ἐν-νεύω [in LXX: Pro.6:13 10:10 (קָרַץ), Sir.27:22 A * ;] to nod to, make a sign to: with dative of person(s), Luk.1:62.† (AS)

ennoia.notes: ἔννοια, -ας, ἡ (νοῦς), [in LXX: Pro.1:4 2:11 3:21 4:1 5:2 8:12 16:22 18:15 19:7 23:4, 19 24:7 (מְזִמָּה, etc.), Wis.2:14, Da TH Sus 1:28* ;] __1. thinking, consideration. __2. a thought, purpose, design: Heb.4:12,…

ennomos.notes: ἔν-νομος, -ον [in LXX: Sir, Pro.1:1-33 ogue 12* ;] __1. lawful, legal (MM, Exp., xiii): Act.19:39. __2. Of persons, __(a) law-abiding; __(b) under law: ἔ. Χριστοῦ, in relation to Christ, 1Co.9:21 (Cremer. 435).† (AS)

ennuchos.notes: ἔννυχος, -ον (νύξ), [in LXX: 3Ma.5:5 * ;] (in cl. poët.; prose in late Gk. only) nightly. Neut., adverbially, ἔννυχα (Rec. -χον), by night: Mrk.1:35.† (AS)

enoch.notes: Ἐνώχ (Heb. חֲנוֹךְ), ὁ, Enoch (Gen.5:18): Luk.3:37, Heb.11:5, Ju 14.† (AS)

enochleo.notes: ἐν-οχλέω, -ῶ (ὄχλος), [in LXX for חָלָה ;] to trouble: with accusative, Heb.12:15. Pass., before ἀπό, Luk.6:18.† (AS)

enochos.notes: ἔνοχος, -ον (= ἐνεχόμενος), (in LXX for רָשַׁע hi., etc. ;] __1. held in, bound by: with genitive (cl. with dative), δουλείας, Heb.2:15. __2. In law-phrases __(a) liable to a charge or action (cl. with dative, of…

enoikeo.notes: ἐν-οικέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly (29/36) for יָשַׁב ;] to dwell in; metaphorically, before ἐν, with dative of person(s): ὁ θεός, 2Co.6:16; τ. πνεῦμα, Rom.8:11, 2Ti.1:14; ὁ λόγος, Col.3:16; πίστις, 2Ti.1:5; ἁμαρτία,…

enotizomai.notes: ἐνωτίζομαι (οὖς), depon. mid., [in LXX chiefly for אָזַן hi. ;] to give ear to, hearken to: with accusative, Act.2:14.† Also used for יָחַל שָׁמַע (AS)

entalma.notes: ἔνταλμα, -τος, τό (ἐντέλλω), [in LXX: Job.23:11 (אָשֻׁר) Job.23:12, Isa.29:13 (מִצְוָה), Isa.55:11 (aliter in Heb.)* ;] a precept: pl., Mat.15:9 (LXX), Mrk.7:7, Col.2:22.† (AS)

entaphiasmos.notes: ἐνταφιασμός, -οῦ (ἐνταφιάζω), preparation for burial: Mrk.14:8, Jhn.12:7.† (AS)

entaphiazo.notes: ἐνταφιάζω [in LXX: Gen.50:2 (חָנַט; cf. ἐνταφιαστής, Gen.50:2 for רָפָא; see Deiss., BS, 120 f.; MM, Exp., xiii)* ;] a κοινή word (Deiss., LAE, 723), to prepare for burial: Mat.26:12, Jhn.19:40.† (AS)

entello.notes: ἐν-τέλλω [in LXX, as in NT (and mostly in Hdt.), always mid., chiefly for צָוָה pi. ;] -ομαι, to command, enjoin, instruct: before περί Heb.11:22; with inf., Mat.19:7; with dative of person(s), Act.1:2; οὕτως,…

enteuthenenthen.notes: ἐντεῦθεν adv. (ἔνθεν), [in LXX chiefly for זֶה ;] __1. of place, hence: Luk.4:9 13:31, Jhn.2:16 7:3 14:31 18:36; ἐ. καὶ ἐ. (for cl. ἔνθεν κ. ἔνθεν), on this side and on that, on each side, Jhn.19:18; similarly, ἐ.…

enteuxis.notes: ἐν-τευξις, -εως, ἡ (ἐντυγχάνω, which see), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:3 * ;] __1. a lighting upon, meeting with. __2. conversation. __3. a petition (in this sense common in π.; cf. Deiss., BS, 121 f., 146; MM, Exp., iii):…

enthumeomai.notes: ἐνθυμέομαι, -οῦμαι (θυμός), [in LXX for דָּמָה pi., etc. ;] to reflect on, ponder: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.1:20 9:4.† (AS)

enthumesis.notes: ἐνθύμησις, -εως, ἡ (ἐνθυμέομαι), [in Sm.: Job.21:27, Eze.11:21 * ;] consideration, pondering (EV, device): Act.17:29; pl., thoughts, feel­ings: Mat.9:4 12:25, Heb.4:12.† SYN.: ἔννοια, the action of the reason; while…

entimos.notes: ἔντιμος, -ον (τιμή), [in LXX for חוֹר, etc. ;] honoured, prized, precious: of persons, Luk.7:2, Php.2:29; compar., Luk.14:8; of things, metaphorically, λίθος, 1Pe.2:4 2:6" (LXX) .† (AS)

entole.notes: ἐντολή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἐντέλλω, which see), [in LXX chiefly for מִצְוָה; in pl. frequently in Pss for פִּקּוּד ;] __1. generally, a charge, injunction, order, command: Luk.15:29, Jhn.10:18 11:57 12:49-50 14:31, Act.17:15,…

entopios.notes: ἐντόπιος, -ον (τόπος), of a place, resident: Act.21:12.† (AS)

entrepho.notes: ἐν-τρέφω to train up, nurture; pass., metaphorically, τοῖς λόγοις τ. πίστεως, 1Ti.4:6.† (AS)

entrepo.notes: ἐν-τρέπω [in LXX for כָּנַע ni., כָּלַם ni., etc. ;] to turn about; metaphorically, put to shame: with accusative, 1Co.4:14; pass., 2Th.3:14, Tit.2:8; mid., to reverence: with accusative of person(s) (cl. with…

entromos.notes: ἔν-τρομος, -ον [in LXX: Da TH Dan.10:11 (רָעַד hi.); ἔ. γίγνεσθαι, Psa.18:7 77:18 (רַעַשׁ), Wis.17:10, 1Ma.13:2 * ;] trembling with fear (Pint.): Act.7:32 16:29, Heb.12:21 (ἔκτρ-, WH, mg.).† (AS)

entrope.notes: ἐν-τροπή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Job.20:3, Psa.35:26 44:15 69:7, 19 71:13 109:29 (כְּלִמָּה)* ;] __1. with genitive of person(s), respect, reverence (Soph., Polyb., al.). __2. Absol., shame (Hipp.): 1Co.6:5 15:34.† (AS)

entruphao.notes: ἐν-τρυφάω, ῶ [in LXX: Isa.55:2 57:4 (עָנַג hith.), Hab.1:10 (קָלַס hith.), 4Ma.8:8, etc. ;] to revel in: ἐν-τ. ἀπάταις, 2Pe.2:13 (see Mayor, in l).† (AS)

entugchano.notes: ἐν-τυγχάνω [in LXX: Da TH Dan.6:12-13 (קֶרֶב) Wis.8:20 16:28, 2Ma.2:25 2Mac 4:36 2Mac 6:12 2Mac 15:39, 3Ma.6:37; before κατά, 1Ma.8:32 1Mac 10:61-64 1Mac 11:25 * ;] __1. to fall in with. __2. to meet with in order to…

entulisso.notes: ἐν-τυλίσσω to wrap up (LS), roll or coil about (DCG, ii, 227, 507a): with accusative and dative, Mat.27:59 (iv, Tr. [WH], cf. similar sentence in π.; MM, Exp., xiii), Luk.23:53; pass., Jhn.20:7.† (AS)

entupoo.notes: ἐν-τυπόω, -ῶ (τύπος), [in LXX for פִּתּוּחַ, Exo.39:30) A * ;] to imprint, engrave: pass. ptcp., with dative, 2Co.3:7.† (AS)

enubrizo.notes: ἐν-υβρίζω [in OT (Al.), Lev.24:11 * ;] to insult, mock at: Heb.10:29.† (AS)

enupniazo.notes: ἐνυπνιάζω (ἐνύπνιον), [in LXX, as in NT, -ομαι, depon., chiefly for חָלַם ;] to dream: ἐνυπνίοις ἐ., Act.2:17 (LXX); present ptcp., Ju 8.† (AS)

enupnion.notes: ἐνύπνιον, -ου, τό (ὕπνος), [in LXX chiefly for חֲלוֹם ;] a dream: pl., Act.2:17.† (AS)

epago.notes: ἐπ-άγω [in LXX for בּוֹא hi., etc. (29 words in all) ;] to bring upon: with dative and accusative, 2Pe.2:5; ἑαυτοῖς ἐ. (for cl. mid., see Mayor, in l), 2Pe.2:1; ἐ. τὸ αἷμα (cf. Ge 209), Act.5:28.† (AS)

epagonizomai.notes: ἐπ-αγωνίζομαι depon.; __1. to contend with (Plut.). __2. to contend for (C.I., 2335, 19): with dative of thing(s), Ju 3.† (AS)

epaineo.notes: ἐπ-αινέω, -ῶ [in LXX for הָלַל pi., שָׁבַח pi. ;] to praise: with accusative, Rom.15:11, 1Co.11:22; before ὅτι, Luk.16:8, 1Co.11:2; absol., before ὅτι, 1Co.11:17.† (AS)

epainetos.notes: Ἐπαίνετος (Rec. -τός), -ου, ὁ Epænetus, a Christian of Borne: Rom.16:5.† (AS)

epainos.notes: ἔπ-αινος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for תְּהִלָּה, etc. ;] praise: Rom.2:29 Rom.13:3, 1Co.4:5, 2Co.8:18, Eph.1:6, 12 1:14, Php.1:11 Php.4:8, 1Pe.1:7 2:14.† (AS)

epairo.notes: ἐπ-αίρω [in LXX for נָשָׂא, רוּם, etc. ;] to lift up, raise: τ. ἀρτέμονα, Act.27:40; χεῖρας, Luk.24:50, 1Ti.2:8; κεφαλάς, Luk.21:28; ὀφθαλμούς, Mat.17:8, Luk.6:20 16:23 18:13, Jhn.4:35 6:5 17:1; φωνήν, Luk.11:27,…

epaischunomai.notes: ἐπ-αισχύνομαι [in LXX: Job.34:19 (נָשָׂא), Psa.119:6 (בּוּשׁ), Isa.1:29 A (חָפֵר) * ;] to be ashamed (of): absol., 2Ti.1:12; with accusative of person(s), Mrk.8:38, Luk.9:26; with accusative of thing(s), Rom.1:16,…

epaiteo.notes: ἐπ-αιτέω, -ῶ, [in LXX: Psa_109:10 (H7592) Sir_40:28 * ;] __1. to ask besides. __2. to beg (as a mendicant; cf. MM, Exp., xiv): Luk.16:3 18:35 (Cremer, 74).† (AS)

epakoloutheo.notes: ἐπ-ακολουθέω, -ῶ [in LXX (chiefly metaphorically) for אָחַר, הָלַךְ, etc. ;] to follow after; in NT metaphorically; absol.: Mrk.16:20 (illustrated by use in verifying accounts; see MM, Exp., xiv; Milligan; NTD, 78);…

epakouo.notes: ἐπ-ακούω [in LXX for שָׁמַע, עָנָה, etc. ;] __1. to listen to. __2. to hearken to, hear with favour (one's prayer): with genitive of person(s), 2Co.6:2 (LXX).† (AS)

epakroaomai.notes: ἐπ-ακροάομαι, -ῶμαι to listen attentively: Act.16:25 (cf. Page, in l).† (AS)

epanagkes.notes: ἐπανάγκης, -ες (ἀνάγκη), only in neut.; __1. ἐπάναγκες (sc. ἐστί), it is compulsory, necessary. __2. As adv., of necessity: Act.15:28.† (AS)

epanago.notes: ἐπ-αν-άγω [in LXX: Zec.4:12 (רוּק hi.), Sir.17:26 26:28, 2Ma.9:21 2Mac 12:4 * ;] to bring up or back (sc. ναῦς); to put out to sea (DB, iii, 63b): Luk.5:3-4; intrans., to return: Mat.21:18.† (AS)

epanamimnesko.notes: ἐπ-ανα-μιμνήσκω to remind again: with accusative of person(s), Rom.15:15.† (AS)

epanapauo.notes: ἐπ-ανα-παύω [in LXX, mid., for נוּחַ, שָׁעַן ni ;] to refresh, cause to rest (upon); mid., to rest upon: metaphorically, with dative, Rom.2:17; before ἐπί, with accusative, Luk.10:6 (Cremer, 827).† (AS)

epanerchomai.notes: ἐπ-αν-έρχομαι [in LXX for שׁוּב, etc. ;] to return: Luk.10:35 19:15.† (AS)

epangelia.notes: ἐπ-αγγελία, -ας, ἡ (ἐπαγγέλλω), [in LXX: Psa.56:8 (סֵפֶר), etc. ;] __1. a summons (as Attic law-term, Dem., al.). __2. a promise (Dem., Arist., al.): Act.23:21; esp. in NT of the divine promises, Act.7:17, Rom.4:14,…

epangello.notes: ἐπ-αγγέλλω [in LXX: Est.4:8 (אָמַר), Pro.13:12, Wis.2:13, al. ;] __1. to announce, proclaim. __2. (a) to promise; __(b) to profess. Mid., also frequently in both these senses; __(with) to promise: with dab. of…

epanistemi.notes: ἐπ-αν-ίστημι [in LXX chiefly for קוּם ;] to raise up against; mid., to rise up against: before ἐπί, with accusative of person(s), Mat.10:21, Mrk.13:12.† (AS)

epanorthosis.notes: ἐπ-αν-όρθωσις, -εως, ἡ (ἐπανορθόω, to correct, restore; of. 2Ma.2:22) [in LXX: I Est.8:52, 1Ma.14:34 * ;] correction: of life, 2Ti.3:16 (cf. MM, Exp., xiv).† (AS)

epaphrizo.notes: ἐπ-αφρίζω to foam up: metaphorically, τ. αἰσχύνας, Ju 13.† (AS)

eparchia.notes: ἐπαρχεία (Rec. -χία, see Bl., § 3, 5), -ας, ἡ (ἔπαρχος, a prefect), [in LXX: Est.4:11 (מְדִינָה), Jdth.3:6 A* ;] the jurisdiction of a prefect, a province: Act.23:3, 34 25:1.† *†ἐπάρχεισς, -ον, of a prefect: ἡ ἐ.…

eparkeo.notes: ἐπ-αρκέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 1Ma.8:26 1Mac 11:35 * ;] __1. to be strong enough for. __2. to ward off. __3. to aid, relieve: with dative of person(s), 1Ti.5:10 5:16 (mid., WH, mg.).† (AS)

epathroizomai.notes: ἐπ-αθροίζω to assemble besides (Plut.): pass., Luk.11:29.† (AS)

epaulis.notes: ἔπ-αυλις, -εως, ἡ (αὐλή), [in LXX for חָצֵר, טִירָה, (l.with), etc. ;] a dwelling, habitation: Act.1:20 (LXX).† (AS)

epaurion.notes: ἐπ-αύριον, adv. [in LXX for מָחָר, Gen.30:33, elsewhere, Exo.9:6, al., for מׇחֳרָת ;] on the morrow: in NT, ἡ ἐ. (sc. ἡμέρα), Mat.27:62, Mrk.11:12, Jhn.1:29, Act.10:9, al (AS)

epautophorosautophoros.notes: αὐτόφωρος, -ον (αὐτός, φώρ, a thief) [in Sm.: Job.34:11 * ;] prop. with ref. to theft, then generally, in the very act: as freq., neut. dative after ἐπί, Jhn.8:4 (Rec., ἐπαυτοφώρῳ).† (AS)

epecho.notes: ἐπ-έχω [in LXX for חָדַל, etc.; also Sir.8:1 34:2, 2Ma.5:25 2Mac 9:25, al. ;] __1. to hold upon. __2. Like παρέχω (as in Hom., al.), to hold out, offer: λόγον ζωῆς, Php.2:16. __3. to hold or direct towards, Sc.νοῦν;…

epegeiro.notes: ἐπ-εγείρω [in LXX for עוּר hi., קוּם hi., etc. ;] to rouse up, excite: C. accusative of thing(s), διωγμόν, Act.13:50; ψυχάς, Act.14:2.† (AS)

epeidon.notes: ἐπ-εῖδον 2 aor. without Pres. in use; [in LXX chiefly for רָאָה ;] to regard with attention, look upon (in cl., of the gods) __1. with a view to bless: with inf., Luk.1:25 (cf. DB, 136b). __2. To punish: before ἐπί,…

epeisagoge.notes: ἐπ-εισ-αγωγή, -ῆς, ἡ a bringing in besides or in addition (Hipp., FlJ, al.): Heb.7:19 (cf. MM, Exp., xiv).† (AS)

epeita.notes: ἐπειτα, adv. of sequence [in LXX: Num.19:10 A, Isa.16:2, 4Ma.6:3 * ;] thereupon, thereafter, then: Luk.16:7, Gal.1:21, Jas.4:14; before μετὰ τοῦτο, Jhn.11:7; μετὰ ἔτη τρία, Gal.1:18; διὰ δεκατεσσάρων ἐτῶν, Gal.2:1;…

epekteinomai.notes: ἐπ-εκ-τείνω to extend: mid., to stretch forward: with dative, Php.3:13.† (AS)

ependuo.notes: ἐπ-εν-δύω = -δύνω (Hdt.), to put on over; pass. (Plut., al.), to have on over, be clothed upon: 2Co.5:2-4.† (AS)

ependutes.notes: ἐπενδύτης, -ου, ὁ (ἐπενδύω), [in LXX for מְעִיל Lev.8:7 A (Aq. ἐπένδυμα), 3Ki.18:4 A, 4Ki.13:18 * ;] an outer tunic (RV, coat): Jhn.21:7 .† (AS)

eperchomai.notes: ἐπ-έρχομαι, [in LXX for בּוֹא, עָבַר, etc. ;] __1. to come to, arrive, come on: before ἀπό, with genitive loc., Act.14:19 (ἐπῆλθαν; cf. M, Pr., 65; Deiss., BS, 191); of time, Eph.2:7. __2. to come upon (as in Hom.):…

epereazo.notes: ἐπηρεάζω (ἐπήρεια, spiteful abuse), to revile: with accusative of person(s), Luk.6:28; with accusative of thing(s) (but see ICC, in l), 1Pe.3:16.† (AS)

eperotao.notes: ἐπ-ερωτάω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for שָׁאַל, also for דָּרַשׁ, etc. ;] __1. to in­quire of, consult, question: with accusative of person(s), Mrk.12:34, Luk.2:46, al.; with dupl. accusative, Mrk.7:17 11:29, Luk.20:40,…

eperotema.notes: ἐπ-ερώτημα, -τος, τό [in LXX: Da TH Dan.4:14 (שְׁאֵלָא), Sir.33:3 * ;] __1. a question, au inquiry (Hdt., Thuc.). __2. a demand: 1Pe.3:21 (see ICC, in l).† (AS)

ephallomai.notes: ἐφάλλομαι [in LXX for צָלַח, 1Ki.10:6 11:6 16:13 * ;] to leap upon: before ἐπί with accusative of person(s) Act.19:16.† (AS)

ephapax.notes: ἐφ-άπαξ, adv. __1. once for all (Eupol.): Rom.6:10 Heb.7:27 9:12 10:10. __2. at once: 1Co.15:6.† (AS)

ephemeria.notes: ἐφ-ημερία, -ας, ἡ (ἐφήμερος), [in LXX chiefly for מִשְׁמֶרֶת, מַחֲלֹקֶת ;] __1. a course of daily services (Neh.13:30, 1Ch.25:8, al.). __2. a class or course of priests detailed for service in the temple (1Ch.23:6,…

ephemeros.notes: ἐφ-ήμερος, -ον (ἐπί, ἡμέρα), __1. lasting for a day. __2. daily, for the day: Jas.2:15.† (AS)

ephesos.notes: Ἔφεσος, -ου, ἡ Ephesus, a city in Asia Minor Act.18:19, 21 18:24, 27 19:1, 17 19:26 (on the genitive, see M, Pr., 73) Act.20:16-17, 1Co.15:32 16:8, Eph.1:1, 1Ti.1:3, 2Ti.1:18 4:12, Rev.1:11 2:1.† (AS)

epheuretes.notes: ἐφ-ευρετής, -οῦ, ὁ (ἐφευρίσκω, to find out) an inventor, contriver: κακῶν (cf. κακίας εὑρετής, 2Ma.7:31), Rom.1:30.† (AS)

ephikneomai.notes: ἐφ-ικνέομαι, -οῦμαι [in LXX: Sir.43:27, 30 R (ἀφικ- ABא)* ;] to come to, to reach: before ἄχρι, 2Co.10:13; εἰς, 2Co.10:14.† (AS)

ephistemi.notes: ἐφ-ίστημι [in LXX for נָצַב ni., שִׁית, נָתַן, etc. ;] __1. causal in pres., impf., fut., 1 aor., to set upon or by, set up, etc. (Æsch., Hdt., Arist., al). __2. Intrans. in mid, and in pf. and 2 aor. act.; __(a) to…

ephphatha.notes: ἐφφαθά (Aram. אֶתְפְּתח, see Abbott, Essays, 142 ff.; DCG, i, 522) ephphatha, be opened: Mrk.7:34.† (AS)

ephraim.notes: Ἐφραίμ, (-ΐμ, Tr.) Ephraim, a town near Jerusalem: Jhn.11:54.† (AS)

epibaino.notes: ἐπι-βαίνω [in LXX chiefly for רָכַב ;] __1. to get up on, mount: before ἐπί, with accusative, Mat.21:5 (LXX); to embark in (a boat), go aboard: with dative, Act.27:2; before εἰς, Act.21:6 (Rec.); absol., Act.21:2.…

epiballo.notes: ἐπι-βάλλω [in LXX for שָׁלַח, שִׁית, etc. ;] __1. trans., to cast, lay or put upon: with accusative and dative, Mrk.11:7, 1Co.7:35; with accusative, before ἐπί with accusative, Rev.18:10, WH, mg.; τ. Χεῖρα (-ας) ἐπί…

epibareo.notes: ἐπι-βαρέω, -ῶ to put a burden on, be burdensome: figuratively, absol., 2Co.2:6; with accusative of person(s), 1Th.2:9, 2Th.3:8 (cf. M, Th., I, 29).† (AS)

epibibazo.notes: ἐπι-βιβάζω [in LXX chiefly for רָכַב hi. ;] to place upon: with accusative of person(s), Luk.10:34 19:35, Act.23:24.† (AS)

epiblema.notes: ἐπί-βλημα, -τος, τό [in LXX: Isa.3:22 * ;] __1. that which is thrown over, a cover. __2. a tapestry, hanging (Is, l.with). __3. that which is put on; __(a) embroidery; __(b) a patch: Mat.9:16, Mrk.2:21, Luk.5:36.† (AS)

epiblepo.notes: ἐπι-βλέπω [in LXX for נָבַט hi., פָּנָה, רָאָה, etc. ;] to look upon. In NT, as in LXX (1Ki.1:11 9:1, Psa.25:16; Tob.3:3, al.), to look on with favour: before ἐπί with accusative of person(s), Luk.1:48 9:38,…

epiboule.notes: ἐπι-βουλή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Est.2:22, I Est.5:73, 2Ma.5:7, al. ;] a plan against, a plot: Act.9:24 20:3, 19 23:30.† (AS)

epicheireo.notes: ἐπιχειρέω, -ῶ (χεῖρ), [in LXX for חָשַׁב, Exo.9:25; גָּמַל, 2Ch.20:11, al. ;] __1. to pat one's hand to. __2. to take in hand, attempt: with inf., Luk.1:1, Act.9:29 19:13.† (AS)

epicheo.notes: ἐπι-χέω [in LXX chiefly for יָצַק. Gen.28:18, al. ;] to pour upon: Luk.10:34.† (AS)

epichoregeo.notes: ἐπι-χορηγέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Sir.25:22, 2Ma.4:9 A * ;] to supply, provide: with accusative of thing(s), 2Pe.1:5; id. with dative of person(s), 2Co.9:10, Gal.3:5; pass., Col.2:19, 2Pe.1:11 (cf. χορηγέω, and see MM, Exp.,…

epichoregia.notes: ἐπι-χορηγία, -ας, ἡ (ἐπιχορηγέω), a supply: Eph.4:16, Php.1:19.† (AS)

epichrio.notes: ἐπι-χρίω [in Sm.: Eze.13:10 22:28 * ;] to spread on, anoint: with accusative, Jhn.9:11; id. before hi-I, with accusative, Jhn.9:6 (cf. MM, Exp., xiv).† (AS)

epidechomai.notes: ἐπι-δέχομαι [in LXX: Jdth.13:13 B2, 1Ma.10:1 1Mac 14:23, Sir.51:26, al. ;] __1. in cl., of things, to allow of, admit of (Dem., Arist., al.). In late writers, __2. to accept besides (Polyb.), to accept (in π. of the…

epideiknumi.notes: ἐπι-δείκνυμι [in LXX: Pro.12:17 (פּוּחַ hi.), Isa.37:26 (בּוֹא hi.), Esp. Jer.59, 2Ma.15:32, al ;] __1. to show, exhibit, display: with accusative and dative, Mat.16:1 22:19 24:1, Luk.17:14. Mid., to display for…

epidemeo.notes: ἐπι-δημέω, -ῶ (δῆμος); __1. to be at home (Thuc., Plat., al.), __2. to stay in a place, sojourn (Plat., Xen., al.; and see MM, Exp., xiv): Act.2:10 17:21; before ἐν, Act.18:27 (WH, mg.).† (AS)

epidiatassomai.notes: ἐπι-δια-τάσσομαι to add provisions to a document: Gal.3:15 (cf. ἐπιδιαθήκη, a second will, F1J, BJ, ii, 2, 3; the word is used of wills in π., cf. Deiss., LAE, 87).† (AS)

epididomi.notes: ἐπι-δίδωμι [in LXX for נָתַן, etc. ;] __1. to give over, to hand: with accusative of thing(s) and dative of person(s), Mat.7:9, 19, Luk.11:11-12 24:30, 42, Act.15:30; pass. with dative of person(s), Luk.4:17. __2. to…

epidiorthoo.notes: ἐπι-δι-ορθόω to set in order further: Tit.1:5 (Inscr.; Cremer, 8O8).† (AS)

epiduo.notes: ἐπι-δύω [in LXX: Deu.24:15, Jos.8:29, Jer.15:9 (בּוֹא)* ;] to go down, set (of the sun): Eph.4:26.† (AS)

epieikeia.notes: ἐπιεικία (Rec. -είκεια), -ας, ἡ (ἐπιεικής), [in LXX: Wis.2:19 12:18 Bar.2:27, from LXX Bar.4:24, TH (3:42), 2Ma.2:22 2Mac 10:4, 3Ma.3:15 3Mac 7:6 * ;] fairness, moderation, gentleness ("sweet reasonableness," Matthew…

epieikes.notes: ἐπιεικής, -ές (εἰκός, likely), [in LXX: Psa.86:5 (סַלָּח), Est.8:13 * ;] __1. seemly, fitting (Hom.). __2. equitable, fair, moderate: 1Ti.3:3, Tit.3:2, 1Pe.2:18, Jas.3:17; τὸ ἐ. (Thuc., i, 76), Php.4:5 (cf. Mayor,…

epigambreuo.notes: ἐπι-γαμβρεύω (γαμβρός, a connection by marriage) [in LXX: Gen.34:9, 1Ki.18:21 ff., 2Ch.18:1, Est.9:14 (חָתַן hithp.), Gen.38:8 R (יָבַם pi.), 1Ma.10:54-56 * ;] __1. to enter into affinity with: with dative (LXX, H.…

epigeios.notes: ἐπί-γειος, -ον (ἐπί, γῆ), of the earth, earthly: τὰ ἐ., Jhn.3:12 Php.2:10 3:19 (anarth.); σώματα, 1Co.15:40; οἰκία, 2Co.5:1; σοφία, Jas.3:15 (Cremer, 153).† (AS)

epiginomai.notes: ἐπι-γίνομαι (see: γίνομαι) [in LXX: Ep. Jer.47:1-7, 3Ma.2:6 * ;] to arrive, arise, come on: Act.28:13.† (AS)

epiginosko.notes: ἐπι-γινώσκω [in LXX chiefly for נָכַר: hi., also for יָדַע, etc. ;] "directive" of γινώσκω (AR, Eph., 249), as in cl.; __1. to observe, perceive, discern, recognize; __(a) absol.: Act.25:10, 1Co.13:12; before ὅτι,…

epignosis.notes: ἐπί-γνωσις, -εως, ἡ (ἐπιγινώσκω, which see), [in LXX: 3Ki.7:14 (B. γν-), Pro.2:5, Hos.4:1, 6 6:6 (דַּעַת), Jdth.9:14, 2Ma.9:11 * ;] acquaintance, discernment, recognition (Plut., al.): Php.1:9, Col.3:10; with…

epigraphe.notes: ἐπι-γραφή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἐπιγράφω), an inscription: Mat.22:20, Mrk.12:16 15:26, Luk.20:24 23:33.† (AS)

epigrapho.notes: ἐπι-γράφω [in LXX for כָּתַב, Num.17:2; fig., Jer.31:33, al. ;] to write upon, inscribe: Mrk.15:26, Act.17:23, Rev.21:12; figuratively, Heb.8:10 (LXX) Heb.10:16.† (AS)

epikaleo.notes: ἐπι-καλέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for קָרָא ;] __1. to call, name, surname: with accusative (cl.), Mat.10:25; pass., Act.1:23 4:36 10:5, 18 10:22 11:13 12:12, 25, Heb.11:16; τ. ὄνομα, before ἐπί (denoting possession, as…

epikalumma.notes: ἐπι-κάλυμμα, -τος, τό (ἐπικαλύπτω), [in LXX: Exo.26:14 39:34, (מְכַסֶּה), 4Ki.17:19 (מָסָךְ), Job.19:29 (aliter in Heb.)* ;] a cover, veil: metaphorically, τ. κακίας, 1Pe.2:16.† (AS)

eulabes.notes: εὐλαβής, -ές (εὖ, λαβεῖν), [in LXX: Mic.7:2 AB2 (חָסִיד); εὐ. ποιεῖν, Lev.15:31 (נָזַר hi.), Sir.11:7 א2* ;] __1. cautious, circumspect. __2. devout, religious, reverent: Act.2:5 8:2 22:12; δίκαιος καὶ εὐ.,…

eulogeo.notes: εὐλογέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for בָּרַךְ pi. ;] __1. to speak well of, praise (cl.; LXX Deu.8:10, al.): τ. θεόν, Luk.1:64 2:28 24:51, 53 (αἰνοῦντες, T, WH, mg.), Jas.3:9; absol., to give praise, Mat.14:19 26:26 (see…

eulogetos.notes: εὐλογητός, -όν (εὐλογέω), [in LXX chiefly for בָּרַךְ ;] blessed; __(a) of men (Gen.12:2 A, Deu.7:14, Jdg.17:2 B, Rut.2:20, 1Ki.15:13); __(b) of God (Lft., Notes, 310 f.), as chiefly in LXX (Gen.9:26, Exo.17:10,…

eulogia.notes: εὐλογία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for בְּרָכָה ;] __1. fair speaking, flattering speech: χρηστολογίας καὶ εὐ., Rom.16:18. __2. praise: of God (as in late Inscr.; LS, see word) and Christ, Rev.5:12-13 7:12 __3. In LXX…

eumetadotos.notes: εὐ-μετά-δοτος, -ον (εὖ, μεταδιδωμι), ready to impart: assoc. with κοινωνικός (for the distinction between the two, see Field, Notes, 213; CGT, in l), 1Ti.6:18.† (AS)

eunoeo.notes: εὐ-νοέω, -ῶ (εὔνοος, friendly), [in LXX: Est.8:13 Bא1, Dan LXX 2:43, 3Ma.7:11 * ;] to be favourable, kindly disposed: with dative, Mat.5:25.† (AS)

eunoia.notes: εὔνοια, -ας, ἡ (εὔνοος), [in LXX: Est.2:23, al. ;] goodwill: Eph.6:7 (of slaves; cf. MM, Exp., xiv).† (AS)

eunouchizo.notes: εὐνουχίζω (εὐνοῦχος), to make a eunuch of, castrate: pass., Mat.19:12; metaphorically, εὐ. έαυτόν, ib.† (AS)

eunouchos.notes: εὐνοῦχος, -ου, ὁ (i.e. ὁ τὴν εὐνὴν(bed) ἔχων), [in LXX for סָרִיס (perhaps not of necessity an actual eunuch; DB, see word), Gen.39:1, al., Wis.3:14, Sir.20:4 30:20 ;] an emasculated man, a eunuch: Mat.19:12; one…

euodia.notes: Εὐοδία (Rec. -ωδία), -ας, ἡ Euodia (not as AV, Euodias), a Christian woman: Php.4:2.† (AS)

euodoo.notes: εὐ-οδόω, -ῶ (ὁδός), [in LXX chiefly for צָלַח hi. ;] to help on one's way (Soph., al.). Pass., to have a prosperous journey; metaphorically (Hdt., al.), to prosper, be prospered, be successful: 3Jn.2, Rom.1:10,…

euonumos.notes: εὐώνυμος, -ον (εὖ, ὄνομα), [in LXX chiefly for שְׂמֹאול ;] __1. of good name or omen. __2. Euphemistic for ἀριστερός, left: Act.21:3, Rev.10:2; ἐξ εὐωνύμων, on the left: Mat.20:21, 23 25:33, 41 27:38, Mrk.10:40…

eupeithes.notes: εὐ-πειθής, ές, (εὖ, πείθομαι), [in LXX: 4Ma.12:6 AR* ;] ready to obey, compliant: Jas.3:17.† (AS)

euperistatos.notes: εὐ-περί-στατος, -ον (εὖ, περιΐστημι), of sins, readily besetting: Heb.12:1 (on form and sense of the word, see Westc., in l).† (AS)

euphemia.notes: εὐφημία, -ας, ἡ (εὔφημος), [in Sm.: Psa.42:5 47:2 100:2 126:2 * ;] good report, praise: opposite to δυσφημία, 2Co.6:8.† (AS)

euphemos.notes: εὔφημος, -ον (εὖ, φήμη), [in Sm.: Psa.63:6 ;] primarily, utter­ing words or sounds of good omen, hence, __1. avoiding ill-omened words, religiously silent. __2. fair-sounding, auspicious (R, mg., gracious): Php.4:8.†…

euphraino.notes: εὐφραίνω [in LXX chiefly for שָׂמַח, qal, pi. ;] to cheer, gladden: with accusative of person(s), opposite to λυπεῖν, 2Co.2:2. Pass., to be happy, rejoice, make merry: Luk.15:32, Act.2:26" (LXX) Rom.15:10 (LXX),…

euphrates.notes: Εὐφράτης, -ου, ὁ the river Euphrates: Rev.9:14 16:12.† (AS)

euphrosune.notes: εὐφροσύνη, -ης, ἡ (εὔφρων, cheerful) [in LXX chiefly for שִׂמְחָה ;] rejoicing, gladness: Act.2:28 (LXX) Act.14:17.† (AS)

eupoiia.notes: εὐ-ποιία (Rec. -ιΐα), -ας, ἡ __1. beneficence, doing good: Heb.13:16. __2. a benefit (FlJ, Ant., ii, ll, 2, al.).† (AS)

euporeo.notes: εὐ-πορέω, -ῶ (εὔτορος, well provided for) [in LXX: Lev.25:26, 49 נָשַׂג hi.), Lev.25:23 (seel.), Wis.10:10 * ;] to prosper, be well off: Act.11:29.† (AS)

euporia.notes: εὐ-πορία, -ας, ἡ (εὔπορος), [in LXX for חַיִל, 4Ki.25:10 A (frequently in Aq.)* ;] __1. facility. __2. plenty, wealth: Act.19:25.† (AS)

euprepeia.notes: εὐ-πρέπεια, -ας, ἡ (εὐπρεπής, comely), [in LXX for הָדָר, etc. ;] goodly appearance, comeliness: Jas.1:11.† (AS)

euprosdektos.notes: εὐ-πρόσ-δεκτος, -ον (εὖ, προσδέχομαι) more usual than δέκτος, which see, acceptable: Rom.15:16, 31, 2Co.6:2 8:12, 1Pe.2:5.† (AS)

euprosedroseuparedros.notes: εὐ-πρόσ-εδρος, -ον Rec. for εὐπάρεδρος, which see: 1Co.7:35.† (AS)

euprosopeo.notes: εὐ-προσωπέω, -ῶ (εὐπρόσωπος, fair of face), [in Al.: Psa.141:6, seel. for -ίζω* ;] to look well, make a fair show: metaphorically (as in 7.; see Deiss., LAE, 96), Gal.6:12.† (AS)

eupsucheo.notes: εὐψυχέω, -ῶ (εὔψυχος, courageous), to be of good courage: Php.2:19.† (AS)

eurokludon.notes: Εὐρ-ακύλων (Rec. εὐροκλύδων, which see), -ωνος (Εὖρος, the East wind, and Lat. Aquilo; Vg., Euroaquilo), the Euraquilo, a N.E. wind (i.e. between Eurus and Aquilo): Act.27:14.† εὐρο-κλύδων (G, εὐροκλ-), -ωνος, ὁ…

euruchoros.notes: εὐρύ-χωρος, -ον (εὐρύς, broad + χώρα), [in LXX for רָחַב ni., and cognate forms (Isa.30:23, al.), exc. 2Ch.18:9 (גֹּרֶן ;) spacious, broad: Mat.7:13.† (AS)

euschemon.notes: εὐσχήμωι, -ον (εὖ, σχῆμα), [in LXX: Pro.11:25 * ;] __1. elegant, graceful, comely (Eur., Plat., al.): τὰ εὐ. ἡμῶν (opposite to τὰ ἀσχ- ἡμ-), 1Co.12:24; in moral sense, seemly, becoming, 1Co.7:35. __2. Also in late…

euschemonos.notes: εὐσχημόνως adv. (εὐσχήμων), decorously, becomingly: 1Co.14:40; περιπατεῖν, Rom.13:13, 1Th.4:12.† (AS)

eusebeia.notes: εὐ-σέβεια, -ας, ἡ (εὐσεβής, which see), [in LXX: Pro.1:7, Isa.33:6 (יִרְאָה), Pro.13:11 (aliter in Heb.), Isa.11:2 (יְהוָֹה יִרְאָה), I Est.1:23, Wis.10:12, Sir.49:3, and very frequently in 4Mac ;] __1. piety,…

eusebeo.notes: εὐ-σεβέω, -ῶ (εὐσεβής, which see), [in LXX: Dan LXX Sus 1:64, 4Ma.9:6 אR, 4Ma.11:5 4Mac 11:8 4Mac 11:23 4Mac 18:2 * ;] to reverence, show piety towards; with accusative (else­where more frequently before εἰς., περί,…

eusebes.notes: εὐσεβής, -ές (εὖ, σέβομαι), [in LXX: Pro.12:12, Isa.24:16 26:7 (צַדִּיק), Mic.7:2 (חָסִיד), Isa.32:8 (נָדִיב), and frequently in Sirach and 4Maccabees ;] pious, godly, devout: Act.10:2, 7, 2Pe.2:9.† SYN.: θεοσεβής,…

eusebos.notes: εὐσεβῶς adv., [in LXX: 4Ma.7:21 * ;] piously, religiously: 2Ti.3:12, Tit.2:12.† (AS)

eusemos.notes: εὔσημος, -ον (εὖ + οῆμα, a sign) [in LXX for כֶּסֶא, Psa.81:3 (-ως, from LXX 219)* ;] __1. conspicuous (cf. Ps, l.with). __2. clear to the understanding, distinct: 1Co.14:9.† (AS)

eusplagchnos.notes: εὔσπλαγχνος, -ον (εὖ, σπλάγχνον, which see), [in Man.1.7 (Camb. Manual LXX, iii, 825) ;] __1. in Hippocr., as medical term (LS, see word). __2. Metaphorical (cf. εὐσιλαγχνία, Eurip., Rhes., 192), in NT,…

eutheos.notes: εὐθέως adv. (εὐθύς), [in LXX: Job.5:3 (פִּתְאוֹם), Wis.5:12, 1Ma.11:12, al. ;] straightway, at once, directly: Gal.1:16, Jas.1:24, 3Jn.14 (cf. Dalman, Words, 28 f.), Rev.4:2, and frequently in Mt, Lk, Jo, Ac (in Mk,…

euthetos.notes: εὔ-θετος, -ον (τίθημι), [in LXX: Psa.32:6 (מָצָא), From Th Sus 1:15* ;] ready for use, fit: of things, with dative, Heb.6:7; before εἰς, Luk.14:35; of persons, with dative, Luk.9:62 (for rabbinic parallels, see…

euthudromeo.notes: εὐθυδρομέω, -ῶ of ships, to run a straight course (Philo): Act.16:11 21:1.† (AS)

euthumeo.notes: εὐθυμέω, -ῶ (ὔθυμος), [in Sm.: Psa.32:11, Pro.15:15 * ;] __1. trans., to make cheerful (Æsch.). __2. Intrans. (Eur., Plut.; so mid. in Xen., Plat.), to be of good cheer: Act.27:22, 25, Jas.5:13.† (AS)

euthumos.notes: εὔ-θυμος, -ον [in LXX: 2Ma.11:26 * ;] __1. kind (Hom.). __2. of good cheer (Æsch., al.): Act.27:36.† (AS)

euthuno.notes: εὐθύνω (εὐθύς), [in LXX: Num.22:23, Jos.24:23 (נָטָה hi.), Jdg.14:7, 1Ki.18:20 18:26 (יָשַׁר) Pro.20:24, Sir.2:2, 6, al. ;] __1. to direct: Jas.3:4. __2. to make straight: Jhn.1:23 (LXX, ἑτοιμάσατε).† (AS)

euthus.notes: εὐθύς, -εῖα, -ύ [in LXX chiefly for יָשַׁר ;] __1. straight, direct: τρίβοι, Mat.3:3, Mrk.1:3, Luk.3:4" (LXX) ; εἰς εὐθειας, (sc. ὁδούς), Luk.3:5; εὐ. ὐδός, figuratively, Act.13:10, 2Pe.2:15; as (Proper name) of a…

euthutes.notes: εὐθύτης, -ητος, ἡ (εὐθύς), [in LXX chiefly for יֹשֶׁר, מִישׁוֹר and cognate forms ;] uprightness: Heb.1:8 (LXX).† (AS)

eutonos.notes: εὐτόνως adv. (εὖ, τείνω), [in LXX forשׁוֹפָר Jos.6:8 * ;] vigorously, vehemently: Luk.23:10, Act.18:28.† (AS)

eutrapelia.notes: εὐτραπελία, -ας, ἡ (εὖ, τρέπω), __1. versatility, wit, facetiousness (Hippocr., Plat., al.). __2. = βωμολογία, coarse jesting, ribaldry (Abbott, Essays, 93): Eph.5:4.† SYN.: μωρολογία, see Tr., Syn., § xxxiv (AS)

eutuchos.notes: Εὔτυχος, -ου, ὁ (εὖ, τυχή), Butychus, a young man: Act.20:9.† (AS)

exago.notes: ἐξ-άγω [in LXX chiefly for יָצָא hi. ;] to lead out: with accusative, Mrk.15:20, Jhn.10:3, Act.5:19 7:36 16:37-39; before ἔξω, Luk.24:50; ἐκ, Act.7:40 12:17 13:17, Heb.8:9; εἰς, Act.21:38.† (AS)

exagorazo.notes: ἐξ-αγοράζω [in LXX: καιρόν ὑμεῖς ἐξαγοράζετε (זְבַן), Dan LXX TH Dan.2:8 * ;] __1. to redeem, ransom (esp. of slaves): metaphorically, Gal.3:13 4:5. __2. to buy up; mid., to buy up for oneself: τ. καιρόν, Eph.5:16,…

exaiphnes.notes: ἐξ-αίφνης (WH, ἐξέφνης, exc. Act.22:6; see App., p. 151, and cf. M, Pr., 35), adv. (ἄφνω), [in LXX chiefly for פִּתְאוֹם ;] suddenly Mrk.13:36, Luk.2:13 9:39, Act.9:3 22:6.† (AS)

exaireo.notes: ἐξ-αιρέω -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for נָצַל hi. ;] to take out: with accusative, ὀφθαλμόν, Mat.5:29 18:9; mid. __(a) to take out for oneself, choose: Act.26:17 (Thayer, see word; Page, Ac., l.with, but see infr.); __(b) to…

exairo.notes: ἐξ-αίρω [in LXX for נָסַע, יָרַשׁ hi., כָּרַת ni., מוּת, בָּעַר pi., etc. ;] to lift up, lift off the earth, remove: 1Co.5:13 (LXX)† (AS)

exaiteo.notes: ἐξ-αιτέω, -ῶ to ask from; mid., to ask for oneself, demand: aor., ἐξῃτήσατο, with accusative (obtained you by asking, R, mg.), Luk.22:31 (see Field, Notes, 76; Cremer, 73).† (AS)

exakoloutheo.notes: ἐξ-ακολουθέω, -ῶ [in LXX Amo.2:4, Jer.2:2 (אַחַר הָלַךְ), Isa.56:11 (פָּנָה), Job.31:9 (פָּתָה ni.), Sir.5:2, Dan LXX TH Dan.3:1-30 :(41) * ;] to follow, follow up (in various senses): metaphorically, 2Pe.1:16 2:2…

exaleipho.notes: ἐξ-αλείφω [in LXX for טוּחַ, Lev.14:42, al.; metaphorically, מָחָה, שָׁחַת, etc. ;] __1. to plaster, wash over (LXX). __2. to wipe off, wipe out: δάκρυον, Rev.7:17 21:4; metaphorically, χειρόγραφον, Col.2:14; τ.…

exallomai.notes: ἐξ-άλλομαι [in LXX for קָלַל (Hab.1:8), etc. ;] to leap up: Act.3:8.† (AS)

exanastasis.notes: ἐξ-ανάστασις, -εως, ἡ (ἐξανίστημι), a rising again: ἐκ τ. νεκρῶν, Php.3:11 (Cremer, 308).† (AS)

exanatello.notes: ἐξ-ανα-τέλλω [in LXX: Gen.2:9, Psa.104:14 Psa.132:17 Psa.147:8 (צָמַח hi.); Psa.112:4 (זָרַח) * ;] __1. trans., to cause to spring up (LXX). __2. Intrans. (as ἀνατέλλω, Gen.3:18), to spring up: Mat.13:5, Mrk.4:5.† (AS)

exangello.notes: ἐξ-αγγέλλω [in LXX chiefly for סָפַר pi. ;] to tell out, proclaim: 1Pe.2:9 [Mrk.16:1-20, "shorter conclusion"] (Cremer, 29).† (AS)

exanistemi.notes: ἐξ-ανίσημι [in LXX chiefly for קוּם ;] __1. trans., to raise up: σπέρμα (cf. Gen.38:8), Mrk.12:19, Luk.20:28. __2. In 2 aor. act., intrans., to rise: Act.15:5.† (AS)

exapatao.notes: ἐξ -απατάω, -ῶ strengthened form of ἀπατάω, [in LXX: Exo.8:29 (הָתַל hi.), Da TH Sus 5:6* ;] to deceive: with accusative, Rom.7:11 Rom.16:18, 1Co.3:18, 2Co.11:3, 2Th.2:3; pass., 1Ti.2:14.† (AS)

exapina.notes: ἐξάπινα = ἐξαπίνης, ἐξαίφνης (which see), [in LXX for פִּתְאוֹם, etc. ;] suddenly: Mrk.9:8.† (AS)

exaporeo.notes: ἐξ-απορέω, -ῶ [in LXX, pass., for פּוּן, Psa.88:15 * ;] so in NT, depon. pass., to be utterly at a loss, be in despair: absol. (as Ps, l.with), 2Co.4:8; τοῦ ζῆν, 2Co.1:8.† (AS)

exapostello.notes: ἐξ-απο-στέλλω [in LXX freq., chiefly for שָׁלַח pi. ;] __1. to send forth: with accusative of person(s), Act.7:12 12:11, Gal.4:4; τ. ἐπαγγελίαν, Luk.24:49; τ. πνεῦμα, Gal.4:6; [τ. κήρυγμα, Mrk.16:1-20, "shorter…

exartizo.notes: ἐξ-αρτίζω (ἄρτιος), [in LXX. Exo.28:7 (חָבַר pu.) * ;] __1. to com­plete, finish: τ. ἡμέρας, Act.21:5. __2. to furnish, supply: pass., 2Ti.3:17 (for exx., see MM, Exp., xiii; Cremer, 651).† (AS)

exastrapto.notes: ἐξ-αστράπτω [in LXX: Num.3:3 (בָּרָק), Eze.1:4 (לָקַח hithp.) 17 Dan LXX 106 (קָלַל)* ;] to flash like lightning, gleam, be radiant: ἱματισμός, Luk.9:29.† (AS)

exautes.notes: ἐξ-αυτῆς (a κοινή word, = ἐξ αὐτῆς τ. ὥρας) at once, forthwith: Mrk.6:25, Act.10:33 Act.11:11 Act.21:32 Act.23:30, Php.2:23.† (AS)

execheo.notes: ἐξ-ηχέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Jol.3:14 (הָמוֹן), Sir.40:13, 3Ma.3:2 * ;] to sound forth (as a trumpet, or thunder; see M, Th., l.with): pass., 1Th.1:8.† (AS)

exegeiro.notes: ἐξ-εγείρω [in LXX for עוּר ni., hi., etc. ;] to raise up: Rom.9:17 (cf. ICC, in l); from the dead, 1Co.6:14.† (AS)

exegeomai.notes: ἐξ-ηγέομαι, -οῦμαι [in LXX chiefly for סָפַר pi. ;] to lead, show the way; metaphorically, to unfold, narrate, declare: with accusative of thing(s), Luk.24:35 Act.21:19; with dative of person(s), Act.10:8; θεόν…

exeimi.notes: ἔξ-ειμι (εἶμι), to go forth: Act.13:42 Act.17:15 Act.20:7; before ἐπί, with accusative, Act.27:43.† (AS)

exelko.notes: ἐξ-έλκω [in LXX: Gen.37:28 (מָשַׁךְ), etc. ;] to draw out or away: metaphorically, ὑπὸ τ. ἐπιθυμίας, Jas.1:14 (see Mayor, in l).† (AS)

exerama.notes: ἐξέραμα, -τος, τό (ἐξεράω, to evacuate, disgorge) a vomit: 2Pe.2:22 (LXX).† (AS)

exerchomai.notes: ἐξέρχομαι, [in LXX chiefly and very frequently for יצא, also for עלה ,בּוֹא, etc. ;] depon., to go, or come out of: Mat.10:11, Mrk.1:35, Jhn.13:30, al.; with inf., Mat.11:8, Mrk.3:21, Luk.7:25, 26 Act.20:8; id.…

exereunao.notes: ἐξ-εραυνάω (Rec. ἐξερευνάω), -ῶ, [in LXX for חָקַר, נָצַר, etc. ;] to search out, search carefully: before περί, 1Pe.1:10.† (AS)

exestiexon.notes: ἔξ-εστι (εἰμί), impers. verb., it is permitted, lawful: Mrk.2:24, Act.8:37 (R, mg.), 1Co.10:23; with inf., Mat.12:2, 10 12 14:4 15:26 19:3 22:17 27:6, Mrk.3:4 12:14, Luk.6:2, 9 14:3, Jhn.5:10; before accusative,…

exetazo.notes: ἐξ-ετάζω (ἐτάζω (rare), to examine), [in LXX: Deu.13:14-15 Deu.19:18, 1Ch.28:9 A (דָּרַשׁ), דָּרַשׁ), Psa.11:5-6 (בָּחַן), Wis.6:3, Sir.3:21, al. ;] to examine closely, inquire carefully (of ): before περί (with…

exischuo.notes: ἐξ-ισχύω [in LXX: Sir.7:6 * ;] to have strength enough, to be quite able: with inf., Eph.3:18.† (AS)

existemi.notes: ἐξ-ίστημι (also in Hellenistic -ιστάνω, Act 8:9), [in LXX for H2730, etc. (29 words in all) ;], __1. causal in Pres., impf., fut., 1 aor., to put out of its place; metaphorically, ἐ. τινά φρενῶν (Eur.), to drive one…

exoche.notes: ἐξοχή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἐξέχω, to stand out), [in LXX for שֵׁן, Job.39:28 * ;] __1. a projection (ἐ. πέτρας, Job.39:28). __2. Metaphorical, eminence, excellence: οἱ κατ᾽ ἐ., the chief men, Act.25:23.† (AS)

exodos.notes: ἔξ-οδος, -ου, ὁ (ὁδός), [in LXX chiefly for מוֹצָא also חוּץ, etc. ;] a going out, departure: Heb.11:22; of death, Luk.9:31, 2Pe.1:15.† (AS)

exolothreuo.notes: έξ-ολεθρεύω (so best MSS. and WH; also read -οθρεύω), [in LXX frequently (rare in Gk. writers) for כָּרַת ni., hi., etc. ;] to destroy utterly: before ἐκ τ. λαοῦ, Act.3:23 (LXX).† (AS)

exomologeomai.notes: ἐξ-ομολογέω, -ῶ and depon. mid., -έομαι, -οῦμαι [as always in LXX chiefly for יָדָה hi. ;] __1. act. = cl. ὁμολογέω, to profess or agree to do (Field, Notes, 75): Luk.22:6. __2. Mid., to acknowledge, confess (MM,…

exorkistes.notes: ἐξ-ορκιστής, -οῦ, ὁ (ἐξορκίζω), __1. one who administers an oath. __2. an exorcist: Act.19:13.† (AS)

exorkizo.notes: ἐξ-ορκίζω (later form of ἐξορκόω ), [in LXX: Jdg.17:2 (אָלָה), Gen.24:3, 3Ki.22:16 (שָׁבַע hi.)* ;] __1. to administer an oath to (Dem., Polyb., al.). __2. to adjure: with accusative of person(s), before κατά, with…

exorusso.notes: ἐξ-ορύσσω [in LXX: Pro.29:22; ὀφθαλμόν (-ούς), Jdg.16:21, 1Ki.11:2 (נָקַר)* ;] to dig out, dig up: στέγην, Mrk.2:4; metaphorically, ὀφοαλμούς (cf. LXX, ll with; Herod., viii, 116), Gal.4:15.† (AS)

exoteros.notes: ἐξώτερος, -α, -ον (compar., from ἔξω; opposite to ἐσώτερος) [in LXX chiefly for חִיצוֹן ;] outer: σκότος, Mat.8:12 Mat.22:13 Mat.25:30.† (AS)

exotheo.notes: ἐξ-ωθέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for נָדָה hi. ;] __1. to thrust out: Act.7:45. __2. to drive out of the sea, drive on shore: Act.27:39 (WH, txt., ἐκσῶσαι).† *ἐκ-σώζω, to preserve from danger, bring safe: Act.27:39…

exoudenoo.notes: ἐξ-ουδενέω (Rec. -όω; T, -θανόω), -ῶ, [in LXX (with vv. ll. -όω, -θενέω, -θενόω) for בּוּז, בָּזָה, מָאַס, בּוּס, etc. ;] to despise, set at nought: Mrk.9:12 (cf. ἐξουθενέω).† (AS)

exousia.notes: ἐξουσία, -ας, ἡ (ἔξεστι), [in LXX: 4Ki.20:13, Psa.113 (114):2 135 (136)8, 9, Isa.39:2, Jer.28 (51):28 (מֶמְשָׁלָה), frequently in Da for Aram. שָׁלְטָן, etc., Wis.10:14, Sir.9:13, al. ;] __1. prop., liberty or power…

exousiazo.notes: ἐξουσιάζω (ἐξουσία) [in LXX (frequently in Ec) chiefly for שָׁלַט ;] __1. to exercise authority (Arist.). __2. Trans., to exercise authority over: with genitive of person(s), Luk.22:25; with genitive of thing(s),…

exoutheneo.notes: ἐξ-ουθενέω, -ῶ (οὐθείς, which see), [in LXX (see: ἐξουθενέω): 1Ki.2:30, al., and as seel. for -δενέω, -όω, -θενόω* ;] to set at nought, despise utterly, treat with contempt: with accusative of person(s), Luk.18:9…

exupnizo.notes: ἐξ-υπνίζω [in LXX: Jdg.16:14, 20, 3Ki.3:13 (יָקַץ), Job.14:12 (עוּר ni.)* ;] to awaken out of sleep (= ἀφυπνίζω): with accusative of person(s), Jhn.11:11.† (AS)

exupnos.notes: ἔξ-υπνος, -ον (ὕπνος), [in LXX: I Est.3:3 * ;] roused out of sleep: Act.16:27.† (AS)

ezekias.notes: Ἑζεκίας (Rec. Ἐζ-), -ου, ὁ (Heb. חִזְקִיָּה, strength of Jehovah), Hezekiah, King of Judah: Mat.1:9, 10.† (AS)

gabbatha.notes: Γαββαθα (Rec. -θᾶ), ἡ, indecl., Gabbatha, the Greek transliteration of an uncertain Aramaic word (DB, see word; Dalman, Words, 7), used as the equivalent of λιθόστρωτον, stone pavement: Jhn.19:13.† (AS)

gabriel.notes: Γαβριήλ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. גַּבְרִיאֵל, hero of God), the archangel Gabriel: Luk.1:19, 26.† (AS)

gad.notes: Γάδ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. גָּד), Gad (Gen.30:11 49:13, al.): Rev.7:5.† (AS)

gadarenos.notes: Γαδαρηνός, -ή, -όν (Γαδαρά, Gadara, the capital of Peræa), Gadarene, of Gadara: Mat.8:28 (Rec. Γεργεσηνῶν), Mrk.5:1, Rec. (Edd., Γερασηνῶν), Luk.8:26, 37 Rec. (Edd., Γερασηνῶν).† (AS)

gaios.notes: Γαῖος, -ου, ὁ (Γάϊος, Rec.), Gaius, the name of a Christian; __1. of Macedonia: Act.19:29. __2. Of Derbe: Act.20:4. __3. Of Corinth: Rom.16:23, 1Co.1:14. __4. The one to whom in Ep. Jo is addressed: III Jo 1.† (AS)

gala.notes: γάλα, -ακτος, τό [in LXX for חלב ;] milk: 1Co.9:7. Metaphorical, of elementary Christian teaching: 1Co.3:2, Heb.5:12, 13; τὸ λογικὸν ἄδολον γ., the rational (spiritual) genuine milk (see Hort, in l.), 1Pe.2:2 (in…

galates.notes: Γαλάτης, -ου, ὁ (originally syn. with cl. Κέλτης; cf. 1Ma.8:2, and see next word), a Galatian: Gal.3:1.† (AS)

galatia.notes: Γαλατία, -ας, ἡ, Galatia; __1. a gentilic region in Asia Minor, settled by Gauls (iii/B.C). __2. A Roman Province which included this region (DB, ii, 85ff.): 1Co.16:1, Gal.1:2, 2Ti.4:10 (T, Tr., mg., Γαλλίαν),…

galatikos.notes: Γαλατικός, -ή, -όν (see previous word), Galatian, belonging to Galatia: Act.16:6 18:23.† (AS)

galene.notes: γαλήνη, -ης, ἡ, a calm: Mat.8:26, Mrk.4:39, Luk.8:24.† (AS)

galilaia.notes: Γαλιλαία, -ας, ἡ (Heb. הַגָּלִיל, the circle, district), Galilee, the name of the northern region of Palestine in NT times: Γ. τῶν ἐθνῶν, Mat.4:15 (LXX); θάλασσα τῆς Γ., Mat.15:29 (cf. Jhn.6:1). (AS)

galilaios.notes: Γαλιλαῖος, -αία, -αῖον, Galilæan: Mat.26:69, Mrk.14:70, Luk.13:1, 2 22:59, 23:6, Jhn.4:45, Act.1:11 2:7 5:37.† (AS)

gallion.notes: Γαλλίων, -ωνος, ὁ, Gallio, proconsul of Achaia: Act.18:12, 14 17.† (AS)

gamaliel.notes: Γαμαλιήλ, ὁ (Heb. גַּמְלִיאֵל), Gamaliel the elder, a Pharisee and Doctor of the Law: Act.5:34 22:3.† (AS)

gameo.notes: γαμέω, -ῶ, [in LXX: Est.10:3, 2Ma.14:25, 4Ma.16:9 * ;] to marry; __1. of the man, to marry, take to wife (ducere): absol., Mat.19:10 22:25, 30 24:38, Mrk.12:25, Luk.17:27, 20:34, 35, 1Co.7:28, 33; with accusative:…

gamiskogamizo.notes: γαμίζω (γάμος), to give in marriage, a daughter: 1Co.7:38. Pass., Mat.22:30 24:38, Mrk.12:25, Luk.17:27 20:35 (WH, mg., γαμίσκονται; cf. γαμίζω).† (AS)

gamos.notes: γάμος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX for מִשְׁתֶּה ;] __1. a wedding, esp. a wedding-feast: Mat.22:8, 10 11, 12, Jhn.2:1-3, Rev.19:7, 9; pl. (Field, Notes, 16), Mat.22:2-4, 9 25:10, Luk.12:36 14:8. __2. marriage: Heb.13:4 (Cremer,…

gangraina.notes: γάγγραινα, -ης, ἡ, a gangrene, an eating sore, which leads to mortification: 2Ti.2:17.† (AS)

gaster.notes: γαστήρͅ, -τρός, ἡ, [in LXX for בֶּטֶן, ἐν γ. ἔχειν for הרה, ἐν γ. λαμβάνειν for חרה ;] __1. the belly: metaphorically, a glutton, Tit.1:12. __2. the womb: ἐν γ. ἔχειν, to be with child, Mat.1:18, 23" (LXX) 24:19,…

gaza.notes: γάζα, -ης, ἡ (a Persian word), [in LXX for גִּנְזִין, II Est.5:17 6:1 7:20, 21, Est.4:7; גִּזְבָּר, II Est.7:21; Isa.39:2 * ;] treasure: Act.8:27.† (AS)

gazophulakion.notes: γαζο-φυλάκιον, -ου, τό (γάζα, φυλακή), [in LXX chiefly for לִשְׁכָּה, נִשְׁכָּה (4Ki.23:11, al.), once for גְּנָזִים (Est.3:9); το͂ γ., τᾶ γ., of a temple treasury, Neh.10:37, 2Ma.3:6, al. ;] treasury: also,…

ge.notes: γε, enclitic postpositive particle, rarer in κοινή than in cl., giving special prominence to the word to which it is attached, distinguishing it as the least or the most important (Thayer, see word), indeed, at…

gedeon.notes: Γεδεών, ὁ, indecl. in LXX and NT, in FlJ, Ant., see 6, 3 and 4 -ῶνος (Heb. גִּדְעוֹן), Gideon (Jdg.6-8): Heb.11:32.† (AS)

geenna.notes: γέεννα (γέενα, Mrk.9:45, Rec.), -ης, ἡ (perh. through Aram. גֵּיהִנָּם, from Heb. גֵּי הִנֹּם, Neh.11:30; גֵּי בֶּן־הִנֹּם, Jhn.18:16; גֵּי בּנֵי־הִנֹּם, 4Ki.23:10; valley of (the son, sons of) lamentation); [in LXX…

geiton.notes: γείτων, -ονος, ὁ, ἡ (γῆ), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁכֵן ;] a neighbour: Luk.14:12 15:6, 9 Jhn.9:8.† (AS)

gelao.notes: γελάω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for צחק, שׂחק ;] to laugh: Luk.6:25; fut., γελάσω) (M, Pr., 154), ib. 21 (cf. κατα-γελάω).† (AS)

gelos.notes: γέλως, -ωτος, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for שְׂחוֹק ;] laughter: Jas.4:9.† (AS)

gemizo.notes: γεμίζω (γέμω), [in LXX: Gen.45:17 (טען), 3Ma.5:47, 4Ma.3:14 * ;] to fill; __1. properly, of a ship (Thuc, al.): Mrk.4:37 (pass.). __2. In late writers, generally (MGr., see Kennedy, Sources, 155), with accusative,…

gemo.notes: γέμω (used only in present and impf.), [in LXX for מלא, נשׂא ;] to be full; __1. properly, of a ship (Xen.). __2. Generally (Plat., al.), __(a) with genitive of thing(s): Mat.23:27, Luk.11:39, Rom.3:14" (LXX),…

genea.notes: γενεά, -ᾶς, ἡ (γίγνομαι), [in LXX chiefly for דּוֹר, דֹּר (Cremer, 148) ;] __1. race, stock, family (in NT, γέννημα, which see). __2. generation; __(a) of the contemporary members of a family: pl., Mat.1:17 (cf.…

genealogeo.notes: γενεαλογέω, -ῶ (γενεά, λέγω), [in LXX: 1Ch.5:1 (יחשׁ)* ;] to trace ancestry, reckon genealogy; pass., before ἐκ: Heb.7:6.† (AS)

genealogia.notes: γενεαλογία, -ας, ἡ (see previous word); __1. the making of a pedigree. __2. a genealogy: pl., 1Ti.1:4, Tit.3:9 (see CGT, in l., and ib. Intr., xlix ff.; DB, ii, p. 141).† (AS)

genesiagenethlios.notes: γενέσια, -ων, τά, neut. pl. of adj. -ιος, -ον, relating to birth (γένεσις); __1. in Attic Gk., a commemoration of the dead. __2. In late Gk., a birthday feast (= cl. γενέθλια; so in π., see MM, Exp., x; cf. also…
