genesis.notes: γένεσις, -εως, ἡ (γίγνομαι), [in LXX chiefly for תּוֹלֵדֹת ;] __1. origin, lineage: βίβλος γενέσεως (as in Gen.2:4a 5:1), Mat.1:1. __2. birth: Mat.1:18, Luk.1:14 (Rec. γέννησις); πρόσωπον τῆς γ., face of his birth…

genete.notes: γενετή, -ῆς, ἡ = γενεή, Ion. for γενεά, [in LXX: Lev.25:47 (עֵקֶר מִשְׁפָּחָו), Est.4:17 * ;] birth: ἐκ γ., Jhn.9:1.† (AS)

gennao.notes: γεννάω, -ῶ (γέννα, poët. for γένος), [in LXX chiefly for ילד ;] __1. of the father, to beget: with accusative, Mat.1:1-16, Act.7:8, 29; before ἐκ, Mat.1:3, 5, 6. __2. Of the mother, to bring forth, bear: Luk.1:13, 57…

gennema.notes: γέννημα, -τος, τό (γεννάω), [in LXX chiefly for תְּבוּאָה ;] offspring of men or animals: ἐχιδνῶν, Mat.3:7 12:34 23:33, Luk.3:7 (and elsewhere in Rec. for γένημα, which see).† (AS)

gennesaret.notes: Γεννησαρέτ (Rec. Γενησ-, Mk, l.with; Targ. גְּנֵיסַר), [in LXX: Γεννησάρ (as in some MSS. Mt, Mk), 1Ma.11:67 * ;] Gennesaret, a fertile plain on W. shore of the Sea of Galilee; ἡ γῆ, Mat.14:34, Mrk.6:53; ἡ λίμνη (יָם…

gennetos.notes: γεννητός, -ή -όν (γεννάω), [in LXX: Job.11:2, 12 14:1 15:14 25:4 (ילד)* ;] begotten, born: pl., γ. γυναικῶν (cf. יְלוּד אִשָּׁה, Job.14:1), periphrasis for mankind, Mat.11:11, Luk.7:28 (Cremer, 147). (AS)

genos.notes: γένος, -ους, τό (γίγνομαι), [in LXX for עַם, מִין, זֶרַע, etc. ;] __1. family: Act.4:6 7:13 13:26. __2. offspring: Act.17:28, 29 Rev.22:16. __3. race, nation; Mrk.7:28, Act.4:36 7:19 18:2, 24, 2Co.11:26, Php.3:5,…

georgeo.notes: γεωργέω, -ῶ (γεωργός), [in LXX: 1Ch.27:26 (עָשָׂה מְלֶאכֶת הַשָּׂדֶה), I Est.4:6, 1Ma.14:8 * ;] to till the ground: pass., Heb.6:7. (AS)

georgion.notes: γεώργιον, -ου, τό (γεωργός), [in LXX, frequently in Pr., for שָׂדֶה, etc. ;] __1. a field (Pro.24:5, 30, Strabo). __2. cultivation, husbandry, tillage (Pro.6:7 9:12, Jer.28:23, Sir.27:6): 1Co.3:9.† (AS)

georgos.notes: γεωργός, -οῦ, ὁ (γῆ, ἔργω = ἔρδω, to do), [in LXX for אִכָּר, etc. ;] __1. a husbandman: 2Ti.2:6, Jas.5:7. __2. a vine-dresser (cf. ἀμπελουργός, Luk.13:7): Mat.21:33-35, 38, 40, 41, Mrk.12:1, 2 7, 9, Luk.20:9, 10 14,…

geras.notes: γῆρας, Attic, genitive (-αος) -ως, dative ᾳ; Ion. and κοινή, -εος (-ους), dative -ει, τό, [in LXX for שֵׂיבָה, etc. ;] old age: ἐν γήρει, Luk.1:36.† (AS)

gerasenos.notes: Γεργασηνός, -ή, -όν, Gerasene, of Gerasa, not the G. of Decapolis, 30 m. S.E. of the Lake, but a Gerasa or Gergesa (perh. the mod. Kersa) on E. shore; as subst., pl. οἱ Γ., Mrk.5:1, Luk.8:26, 37 (Γεργεσηνῶν, RV, mg.…

gerasko.notes: γηράσκω (also γηράω; γῆρας), [in LXX chiefly for זקן ;] to grow old: Jhn.21:18, Heb.8:13.† SYN.: παλαιοῦμαι. (AS)

geron.notes: γέρων, -οντος, ὁ, [in LXX for זָקֵן ;] an old man: Jhn.3:4.† (AS)

gerousia.notes: γερουσία, -ας, ἡ (γέρων), [in LXX (Hex. only in OT) for זָקֵן ;] a council of elders, senate; in NT, of the Sanhedrin (Cl. Rev., i, 43f.; DB, ext., 99): Act.5:21.† (AS)

gethsemane.notes: Γεθσημασεί (Rec. -νῆ, LTr. -νεῖ) indecl. (Heb. גֵּת שְׁמָנִי, oil-press), Gethsemane: called in Jhn.18:1 a κῆπος, but named only in Mat.26:36, Mrk.14:32.† (AS)

geuo.notes: γεύω, γεύομαι, [in LXX chiefly for טעם ;] to make to taste. Mid., to taste eat: absol., Act.10:10 20:11, Col.2:21; with genitive, Mat.27:34, Luk.14:24, Act.23:14; with accusative (not cl., but see Westc, Heb.,…

ginosko.notes: γινώσκω (= γιγν-: see previous word. So also vulgar Attic, in Inscr., see Thumb, MGV, 207), [in LXX chiefly for ידע ;] to be taking in knowledge, come to know, recognize, perceive, understand; in past tenses to know,…

gleukos.notes: γλεῦκος (-εος), -ους, τό, [in LXX for יַיִן, Job.32:19 * ;] must, sweet new wine (Arist.): Act.2:13.† (AS)

glossa.notes: γλῶσσα, -ης, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for לָשׁוֹן ;] __1. the tongue, as the organ of speech: Mrk.7:33, 35 Luk.1:64 16:24, Act.2:26" (LXX), Rom.3:13 (LXX) 14:11 (LXX), 1Co.14:9, Php.2:11, Jas.1:26 3:5, 6 8, 1Pe.3:10"…

glossokomon.notes: γλωσσόκομον, -ου, τό, vernac. form of cl. γλωσσοκομεῖον (γλῶσσα, κομέω), [in LXX for אָרוֹן, 2Ki.6:11, 2Ch.24:8, 10 11 * ;] __1. = cl. -εῖον (see supr.), a case for holding the reeds or tongues of musical…

glukus.notes: γλυκύς, -εῖα, -ύ, [in LXX chiefly for מָתוֹק ;] Jas.3:11, 12; opposite to πικρόν, ἁλυκόν, Rev.10:9, 10.† (AS)

gnapheus.notes: γναφεύς, -έως, ὁ, Ion. and κοινή form of Att. κναφεύς (κνάπτω, to card wool), [in LXX for כָּבַס, 4Ki.18:17, Isa.7:3 36:2 * ;] a fuller, cloth-dresser: Mrk.9:3.† (AS)

gnesios.notes: γνήσιος, -α, -ον (γίγνομαι), [in LXX: Sir.7:18, 3Ma.3:19 * ;] __1. prop., lawfully begotten, born in wedlock (in π., γυνὴ γ., a lawful wife; MM, see word). __2. true, genuine, sincere: Php.4:3, 1Ti.1:2, Tit.1:4; as…

gnome.notes: γνώμη, -ης, ἡ (γιγνώσκω), [in LXX: Da TH 2:15 (דָּת), Ps 82(83):3 (סוֹד), elsewhere, chiefly II Es, for טְעֵם ;] __1. a means of knowing, a token (Theogn.). __2. In Attic writers, the mind, its operations and results…

gnophos.notes: γνόφος (later form of δν-), [in LXX for אָפֵל, עֲרָפֶל, etc. ;] darkness, gloom (including "an element of tempest," Tr., Syn., §with): Heb.12:18.† SYN.: ἀχλύς, ζόφος, σκότος (Tr., l.with; DB, i, 457a). (AS)

gnorizo.notes: γνωρίζω (γιγνώσκω), [in LXX chiefly for ידע hi. ;] __1. as most commonly in cl., to come to know, discover, know: Php.1:22 (but cf. R, mg.). __2. to make known: with accusative, Rom.9:22, 23; with accusative of…

gnosis.notes: γνῶσις, -εως, ἡ (γιγνώσκω), [in LXX chiefly for דַּעַת ;] __1. a seeking to know, inquiry, investigation. __2. knowledge, in NT, specially of the kn. of spiritual truth: absol., Luk.11:52, Rom.2:20 15:14, 1Co.1:5…

gnostes.notes: γνώστης, -ου, ὁ (γιγνώσκω), [in LXX chiefly for יִדְּעֹנִי ;] one who knows, an expert: Act.26:3.† (AS)

gnostosgnorimos.notes: γνωστός, -ή, -όν, later form of γνωστός (γιγνώσκω), [in LXX for various parts of ידע ;] known: Act.9:42; with dative, Jhn.18:15, 16 (Rec.), Act.1:19 2:14 4:10 13:38 15:18 19:17 28:22, 28; γ. σημεῖον, a notable (EV)…

goes.notes: γόης, -ητος, ὁ (γοάω, to wail), __1. a wailer. __2. a wizard. __3. an impostor (cf. γοητεία, trickery, 2Ma.12:24): 2Ti.3:13.† (AS)

gog.notes: Γώγ, ὁ (Heb. גּוֹג), indecl., Gog, assoc. with Magog (q.v): Rev.20:8.† (AS)

golgotha.notes: Γολγοθά (Rec. -θᾶ; WH, mg., Γολγόθ, Jo, l.with, Aram. גָּלְגָּלְתָּא = Heb. גֻּלְגֹּלֶת, LXX, κρανίον, Jdg.9:53, 2Ki.9:35); indecl. (exc. Mrk.15:22, -άν), Golgotha, the place of the crucifixion: Mat.27:33, Mrk.15:22,…

gomorra.notes: Γόμορρα, -ας, ἡ, and -ων, τά (Heb. עֲמֹרָה), Gomorrah, one of the cities of the plain (Gen.19): Mat.10:15, Rom.9:29" (LXX), 2Pe.2:6, Ju 7.† (AS)

gomos.notes: γόμος, -ου, ὁ (γέμω), [in LXX: Exo.23:5, 4Ki.5:17 (מַשָּׂא) * ;] a ship's freight, cargo: Act.21:3, Rev.18:11, 12.† (AS)

goneus.notes: γονεύς, -έως, ὁ (γίγνομαι), [in LXX for אָב, אֵם ;] a begetter, a father; mostly in pl., οἱ γ., parents: Luk.2:41, 43 8:56 21:16, Jhn.9:2, 3 20, 22, 23 Rom.1:30, 2Co.12:14, Eph.6:1, Col.3:20, 2Ti.3:2; accusative pl.…

gongusmos.notes: γογγυσμός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX: Isa.58:9 (אָוֶן), Exo.16:7-9, Num.17:5, 10 (תְּלוּנָה), Wis.1:10, 11 Sir.46:7 * ;] a murmuring, muttering: Jhn.7:12, Act.6:1; ἄνευ γ., 1Pe.4:9; pl., χωρὶς γ., Php.2:14.† (AS)

gongustes.notes: γογγυστής, -οῦ, ὁ (γογγύζω); [in Sm.: Pro.26:22, Isa.29:24; Th.: Pro.26:20 * ;] a murmurer: Ju 16.† (AS)

gonguzo.notes: γογγύζω, [in LXX chiefly for לוּן ;] to mutter, murmur: 1Co.10:10; before κατά, Mat.20:11; πρός, Luk.5:30; περί, Jhn.6:41, 61; μετ᾽ ἀλλήλων, Jhn.6:43; with accusative, before περί, Jhn.7:32 (cf. δια-γογγύζω).† (AS)

gonia.notes: γωνία, -ας, ἡ (γόνυ), [in LXX chiefly for פִּנָּה ;] an angle, a corner: Mat.6:5, Act.26:26; τ. τέσσαρας γ. τ. γῆς, Rev.7:1, 20:8; κεφαλὴ γνωνίας (רֹאשׁ פִּנָּה, Ps 117(118):22, LXX), Mat.21:42, Mrk.12:10, Luk.20:17,…

gonu.notes: γόνυ, -ατος, τό, [in LXX chiefly for בֶּרֶךְ ;] the knee: Heb.12:12; of a suppliant, προσπίπτειν τοῖς γ., Luk.5:8; so also τιθέναι τὰ γ., to kneel: Luk.22:41, Act.7:60 9:40 20:36 21:5; in mockery, Mrk.15:19; κάμπτειν…

gonupeteo.notes: γονυπετέω, -ῶ) (γόνυ, πίπτω); __1. to fall on the knees: before ἔμπροσθεν, Mat.27:29. __2. to fall down before one: with accusative, Mat.17:14, Mrk.1:40, R, txt., 10:17.† (AS)

gramma.notes: γράμμα, -τος, τό (γράφω), [in LXX for סֵפֶר, etc. ;] __1. that which is traced or drawn, a picture. __2. that which is written; __(1) a character, letter: Gal.6:11; __(2) a writing, a written document; __(a) a bill…

grammateus.notes: γραμματεύς, -έως (accusative pl., -εῖς, see Bl., §8, 2), ὁ (γράμμα), [in LXX always for שׁטר in Hex., elsewhere chiefly for ספר ;] __1. a secretary; γ. τ. πόλεως, a state-clerk: Act.19:35. __2. In π., of a military…

graodes.notes: γραώδης, -ες (γραῦς, an old woman, εἶδος), anile, old-womanish: 1Ti.4:7.† (AS)

graphe.notes: γραφή, -ῆς, ἡ (γράφω), [in LXX chiefly for כְּתָב ;] __1. a drawing, painting. __2. __(a) writing; __(b) that which is written, a writing: πᾶσα γ., 2Ti.3:16; γ. ἅγιαι, Rom.1:2; προφητικαί, Rom.16:26; al αἱ γ. τ.…

grapho.notes: γράφω, [in LXX chiefly for כּתב ]; __1. to scrape, graze (Hom.), and later (Hdt.) to sketch, draw. __2. to write; __(a) of forming or tracing letters on writing material: Jhn.8:[6], Gal.6:11, 2Th.3:17; __(b) to…

graptos.notes: γραπτός, -ή, -όν (γράφω), [in LXX for מִכְתָּב ;] __1. painted. __2. written: Rom.2:15.† (AS)

gregoreo.notes: γρηγορέω, -ῶ, = Attic ἐργήγορα, pf. of ἐγείρω, which see, [in LXX (later bks. only) chiefly for שׁקד ;] __1. to be awake; metaphorically, of being alive, 1Th.5:10. __2. to watch: Mat.24:43 26:38, 40 Mrk.13:34 14:34,…

gumnazo.notes: γυμνάζω (γυμνός), [in LXX: 2Ma.10:15 * ;] __1. properly, to exercise naked. __2. Generally, to exercise, train the body or mind: 1Ti.4:7, Heb.5:14 12:11, 2Pe.2:14.† (AS)

gumneteuogumniteuo.notes: γυμνιτεύω (Rec. γυμνητ-, V. Tdf ., Pr., 81), to be naked or scantily clad: 1Co.4:11 (cf. ἐν ψύχει κ. γυμνότητι), 2Co.11:27; γυμνοί, Jas.2:15. In κοινή writers, γ. also means to go light-armed.† (AS)

gumnos.notes: γυμνός, -ή, -όν, [in LXX chiefly for עָרוֹם ;] naked, without clothing, and sometimes (as frequently in cl.) scantily or poorly clad (Isa.20:2ff., Tob.1:16, 2Ma.11:12): Mat.25:36, 38 43, 44, Mrk.14:52, Jhn.21:7,…

gumnotes.notes: γυμνότης, -ητος, ἡ (γυμνός), [in LXX for עֵירֹם, Deu.28:48 * ;] nakedness: Rom.8:35, 2Co.11:27, Rev.3:18.† (AS)

gunaikarion.notes: γυναικάριον, -ου, τό (diminutive of γυνή), a little woman; contemptuously, a silly woman (EV): 2Ti.3:6.† (AS)

gunaikeios.notes: γυναικεῖος, -α, -ον (γυνή), [in LXX chiefly for אִשָּׁה ;] female: 1Pe.3:7.† (AS)

gune.notes: γυνή, -αικός, ἡ, [in LXX for אִשָּׁה ;] __1. a woman, married or unmarried: Mat.11:11 14:21, al.; ὕπανδρος γ., Rom.7:2; γ. χήρα, Luk.4:26; in vocat., γύναι implies neither reproof nor severity, but is used frequently…

hades.notes: ᾅδης, -ου, ὁ [in LXX ehiefly for שְׁאוֹל, also for דּוּמָה, מָוֶת, etc. ;] __1. in Hom., Hades (Pluto), the god of the underworld. __2. the abode of Hades, the underivorld; in NT, the abode of departed spirits,…

hadrotes.notes: ἁδρότης, -τος, ἡ (ἁδρός, thick, well-grown), __1. thickness, vigour. __2. abundance, bounty: 2Co.8:20. † (AS)

hagiasmos.notes: ἁγιασμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ἁγιάζω), [in LXX: Eze.45:4 (מִקְדָּשׁ), Sir.7:31, etc. ;] as an active verbal noun in -μός, it signifies properly the process τὸ ἁγιάζειν, rather than the resultant state, ἁγιωσύνη, hence, __1.…

hagiazo.notes: ἁγιάζω, Hellenistic form of ἁγίζω (ἅγιος), to make holy, consecrate, sanctify; [in LXX chiefly for קדשׁ pi., hi. ;] __1. to dedicate, separate, set apart for God; of things: Mat.23:17,19, 2Ti.2:21; of persons:…

hagios.notes: ἅγιος, -α, ον (τό ἅγός, religious awe; ἅζω, to venerate), [in LXX chiefly for קֹ֫דֶשׁ ;] primarily, dedicated to the gods, sacred (Hdt.; rare in Att., never in Hom., Hes. and Trag., who use ἁγνός), hence, holy,…

hagiosune.notes: ἁγιωσύνη (cf. ἀγαθωσύνη), -ης, ἡ (ἅγιος), [in LXX: Psa.29 (30):4 96(97):12 (קֹדֶשׁ), 95(96):6 (עֹז), 144(145):5 (הוֹד), 2Ma.3:12 * ;] holiness, the state in man resulting from ἁγιασμός, which see: Rom.1:4, 2Co.7:1,…

hagiotes.notes: ἁγιότης, -ητος, ἡ (ἅγιος), [in LXX: 2Ma.15:2 * ;] sanctity, holiness, regarded, properly, as an abstract quality (see next word, and cf. Lft. Notes, 49; MM, VGT, see word): 2Co.1:12, Heb.12:10.† (AS)

hagneia.notes: ἁγνεία (WH, ἁγνία), [in LXX for טָהֳרָה, נָזִיר, נֶזֶר, etc. ;] purity: 1Ti.4:12, 5:2 (Cremer, 58. For exx. of ceremonial use in π., see MM, Exp., iv).† (AS)

hagnismos.notes: ἁγνισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (> ἁγνίζω), [in LXX: Num.6:5 (נֵזֶר) 8:7 19:17 (חַטָּאָה), etc. ;] purification: in ceremonial sense, Act.21:26 (LXX).† (AS)

hagnizo.notes: ἁγνίζω (> ἁγνός), [in LXX always ceremonially, chiefly for קָדַשׁ ;] to purify, cleanse from defilement; __(a) ceremonially: Jhn.11:55, Act.21:24, 26 24:18; __(b) morally: Jas.4:8, 1Pe.1:22, 1Jn.3:3. † SYN.:…

hagnos.notes: ἁγνός, -ή, -όν (ἅγος, see: ἅγιος), [in LXX chiefly for טָהוֹר (Pss, and Pr. only), also 2Ma.13:8, 4Ma.18:7, al. ;] __1. free from ceremonial defilement, in a condition prepared for worship (for exx. of pagan usage,…

halas.notes: ἅλας (T, ἅλα), -ατος, τό, late form of cl. ἅλς, -ος, ὁ (MM, VGT, see word), [in LXX chiefly for מְלַח ;] salt, lit, and figuratively: Mat.5:13, Mrk.9:50 Luk.14:34; like cl. ἅλες, wit, of wisdom and grace in speech:…

halieuo.notes: ἁλιεύω (ἁλιεύς), [in LXX: Jer.16:16 (דִּיג)* ;] to fish: Jhn.21:3 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

halieus.notes: ἁλεεύς (Rec. ἁλιεύς, the older form; WH, App., 151, -έως, ὁ (ἅλς, the sea) [in LXX for דָּג, דַּוָּג ;] a fisherman: Mat.4:18, 19, Mrk.1:16, 17, Luk.5:2 (AS)

halizo.notes: ἁλίζω (ἅλς), [in LXX for מָלַח ;] to salt, season with salt: Mat.5:13, Mrk.9:49.† (AS)

hallomai.notes: ἅλλομαι [in LXX for צָלַח, דָּלַג pi., etc. ;] to leap: Act.3:8 14:10; of water, to spring up, Jhn.4:14 (MM, VGT, see word).† (AS)

halon.notes: ἅλων, -ωνος (for Attic ἅλως, -ω, see MM, VGT, see word), ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for גֹּרֶן ;] a threshing floor: Mat.3:12, Luk.3:17 (here prob. by meton. = the grain on the threshing floor).† (AS)

halosis.notes: ἅλωσις, -εως, ἡ (ἁλίσκομαι), [in LXX: Jer.50:46 (תָּפַשׂ ni.)* ;] a taking, capture: 2Pe.2:12.† (AS)

halukos.notes: ἁλυκός, -ή, -όν (ἅλς), [in LXX for מְלַח, שִׂדִּים ;] salt: Jas.3:12.† (AS)

halusis.notes: ἅλυσις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX: Wis.17:17 * ;] a chain, bond: Mrk.5:3-4, Luk.8:29, Act.12:6-7 21:33 28:20, Eph.6:20, 2Ti.1:16, Rev.20:1.† (AS)

hama.notes: ἅμα adv., at once (Lat. simul): Act.24:26 27:40, Rom.3:12 (one and all = יַחַד, Psa.14:3), Col.4:3, 1Ti.5:13, Phm 22; before σύν, 1Th.4:17 5:10; as prep. with dative, together with: Mat.13:29 (see MM, VGT, see word);…

hamartano.notes: ἁμαρτάνω (present formed from aor. ἁμαρτεῖν), [in LXX for חָטָא, also for אָשַׁם, רָשַׁע, etc. ;] __1. to miss the mark (Hom., Æsch., al.), hence metaphorically (Hom., al.), to err, do wrong __2. In LXX and NT, to…

hamartema.notes: ἁμάρτημα, -τος, τό (ἁμαρτεῖν, see supr.), [in LXX for חַטָּאָה, עָוֺן etc. ;] an act of disobedience to divine law (Lft., Notes, 273), a sinful deed, a sin: Mrk.3:28-29, Rom.3:25, 1Co.6:18, 2Pe.1:9, WH, mg.; αἰώνιωv…

hamartia.notes: ἁμαρτία, -ας, ἡ (ἁμαρτάνω, which see), [in LXX chiefly for חַטָּאת and cogn. forms, also for פֶּשַׁע ,עָוֹן, etc. ;] prop. a missing the mark; in cl. (see reff. to CR in MM, VGT, see word); __(a) guilt, sin (Plat.,…

hamartolos.notes: ἁμαρτωλός, -όν (ἁμαρτάνω), [in LXX chiefly for רָשָׁע ;] sinful, a sinner: of all men, 1Ti.1:15; of those especially wicked, 1Ti.1:9, 1Pe.4:18; p1., Mat.9:10-13 11:19 26:45, al. (see MM, VGT, see word; Cremer, 102,…

hapalos.notes: ἀπαλός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for רַךְ ;] tender: Mat.24:32, Mrk.13:28.† (AS)

hapas.notes: ἅπας, -ασα, -αν (strengthened form of πᾶς, see: ἅ-), all, the whole, altogether: bef. subst. with art., as Luk.3:21; or after, as Mrk.16:15]; absol., in masc., as Luk.5:26; in neut., as Act.2:44; ἅ. οὗτοι, Act.2:7…

hapax.notes: ἅπαξ adv., [in LXX for אֶחָד, פַּעַם ;] __(a) once: 2Co.11:25, Heb_9:26-27; ἔτι ἅ., Heb.12:26-27; ἅ. τ. ἐνιαυτοῦ, Heb.9:7; καί ἅ. κ. δίς, twice: Php.4:16, 1Th.2:18; __(b) once for all: Heb.6:4 9:28 10:2, 1Pe.3:18, Ju…

haphe.notes: ἁφή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἅπτω, to fasten, fit), [in LXX for נֶגַע, frequently in Le ;] a joint (MM, see word): Eph.4:16, Col.2:19 (Lft., in l).† (AS)

haplos.notes: ἁπλῶς adv. (ἁπλοῦς), [in LXX: Pro.10:9 (תֹּם), Wis.16:27, 2Ma.6:6 * ;] simply, sincerely, graciously: Jas.1:5. ("Later writers comprehend under the one word the whole magnanimous and honour­able type of character in…

haplotes.notes: ἀπλότης, -ητος, ή (ἁπλοῦς), [in LXX: 4Ki.15:11 (תֹּם), 1Ch.29:17 (לֵבָב יֹשֶׁר, ἁ. τῆς καρδίας, cf. Col.3:22, where see Lft.), Wis.1:1, al. ;] simplicity, sincerity: Rom.12:8, 2Co.11:3, Eph.6:5, Col.3:22; as…

haplous.notes: ἁπλοῦς, -ῆ, -οῦν (contr. fr. -όος; ἁ.- cop., πλόος), [in LXX: Pro.11:25 * ;] simple, single: in a moral sense (DCG, ii, 628 f.), ἀφθαλμός, Mat.6:22, Luk.11:34. (In π. of a marriage dowry, see MM, see word).† ἁπλόος,…

hapto.notes: to touch, hold, handle; (act.) to start a fire; "to touch a woman" means "to get married" primarily to bring in contact, fit, fasten; to light, kindle, Mk. 4:21; Lk. 8:16; to touch, Mt. 8:3; to meddle, venture to…

harma.notes: ἅρμα, -τος, τό (ἀραρίσκω, to join), [in LXX רָכַב ;] a chariot: Act.8:28-29, 38, Rev.9:9.† (AS)

harmos.notes: ἁρμός, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Sir.27:2, 4Ma.10:5 * ;] a joining, joint: Heb.4:12.† (AS)

harmozo.notes: ἁρμόζω (ἁρμός), [in LXX for אמן, etc. ;] __1. to fit, join. __2. of marriage, to betroth. Mid., __(a) to join to oneself, marry, take to wife; (b) to give in marriage: 2Co.11:2 (for this there is no direct parallel.…

harpage.notes: ἁρπαγή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἁρπάζω), [in LXX (as also ἅρπαγμα) for גָּזֵל, etc. ;] pillage, plundering, robbery: Mat.23:25, Luk.11:39, Heb.10:34.† (AS)

harpagmos.notes: ἁρπαγμός, οῦ, ὁ (ἁρπάζω); __1. prop., accusative, to the rule of its formation (Bl., § 27, 2), actively, the act of seizing, robbery (Plut., Deu Puer. Educ., p. 12A), Php.2:6, AV (Waterland, Works, II, 108; Cremer,…

harpax.notes: ἅρπαξ, -αγος, ὁ, ἡ (ἁρπάζω), [in LXX: Gen.49:27 (טָרַף) * ;] rapacious: Mat.7:15, Luk.18:11; as subst., a swindler, an extortioner (MM, see word), 1Co.5:10-11 6:10.† (AS)

harpazo.notes: ἁρπάζω [in LXX chiefly for גָּזַל, טָרַף ;] to seize, catch up, snatch away, carry off by force: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.12:29 13:19, Jhn.10:12, 28-29; τ. βασιλείαν τ. θεοῦ, Mat.11:12; with accusative of…

hebdomekonta.notes: ἑβδομήκοντα, οἱ, αἱ, τά, indecl. (ἑπτά), seventy: Luk.10:1, 17 Act.7:14 23:23 27:37.† (AS)

hebdomekontakis.notes: ἑβδομηκοντάκις, adv., [in LXX for שִׁבְעִים, Gen.4:24 * ;] seventy times: ἑ. ἑπτά, seventy times seven, Mat.18:22 (R, txt., ICC, in l.), or seventy-seven times (R, mg.; cf. M, Pr., 98; WM, 314; Meyer, in l.).† (AS)

hebdomos.notes: ἕβδομος, -η, -ον (ἑπτὰ), [in LXX chiefly for שְׁבִיעִי ;] seventh: Jhn.4:52, Heb.4:4" (LXX), Ju 14, Rev.8:1 10:7 11:15 16:17 21:20.† (AS)

hebraios.notes: Ἑβραῖος (WH, Ἐβ-), -α, -ον (Aram, עִבְרַי), as subst., ὁ Ἑ., [in LXX for עֵבֶר, עִבְרִי ;] a Hebrew. __1. In OT, of Israelites in contrast with those of another race (Gen.14:13, Exo.1:15, Deu.15:12, al.). __2. In NT…

hebrais.notes: Ἑβραΐς (WH, Ἐβ-), -ίδος (Aram. עִבְרַי), peculiar form of Ἑβραϊκός, [in LXX, ἡ Ἑ. φωνή, 4Ma.12:7 16:15 * ;] in NT, ἡ Ἑ. διάλεκτος, Hebrew, i.e. the Aramaic vernacular of Palestine: Act.21:40 22:2 26:14.† (AS)

hebraisti.notes: Ἑβραϊστί (WH, Ἐβ-), adv., [in LXX: Si prol. 13 * ;] in Hebrew: Rev.9:11; elsewhere, in Aramaic (see supr.): Jhn.5:2 19:13, 17, 20 20:16, Rev.16:16.† (AS)

hedeos.notes: ἡδέως adv. (ἡδύς, sweet), [in LXX: Pro.3:24 (עָרַב), etc. ;] gladly, with pleasure: Mrk.6:20 12:37, 2Co.11:19; superlat., ἥδιστα, very gladly (B1., § 11, 3), 2Co.12:9 12:15.† (AS)

hedista.notes: ἥδιστα, see: ἡδέως. ἡδέως adv. (ἡδύς, sweet), [in LXX: Pro.3:24 (עָרַב), etc. ;] gladly, with pleasure: Mrk.6:20 12:37, 2Co.11:19; superlat., ἥδιστα, very gladly (B1., § 11, 3), 2Co.12:9 12:15.† (AS)

hedone.notes: ἡδονή, -ῆς, ἡ (ἥδομαι, to be glad) [in LXX: Num.11:8 (טַעַם), Pro.17:1, Wis.7:2 16:20, and frequently in 4Mac ;] pleasure: 2Pe.2:13; pl., Luk.8:14, Tit.3:3, Jas.4:1, † (AS)

hedraioma.notes: ἑδραίωμα, -τος, τό (ἑδραῖος), a support, bulwark, stay (Vg. fermamentum): 1Ti.3:15 (eccl.).† (AS)

hedraios.notes: ἑδραῖος, -ον (ἕδρα, a seat), [in Sm.: Ps 32 (33):14, al. ;] __1. sitting, seated. __2. steadfast, firm; metaphorically, of moral fixity: 1Co.7:37 15:58, Col.1:23.† (AS)

heduosmos.notes: ἡδύ-οσμος, -ον (ἡδύς, ὀσμή) sweet-smelling; as subst., τὸ ἡ., mint: Mat.23:23, Luk.11:42.† (AS)

hegemon.notes: ἡγεμών, -όνος, ὁ (ἡγέομαι) [in LXX for אַלּוּף, שַׂר, etc. ;] __1. a leader, guide. __2. a commander. __3. a governor of a province (pro­consul, proprietor, legate, or procurator; but cf. ἀνθύπατος): Mat.10:18,…

hegemoneuo.notes: ἡγεμονεύω (ἡγεμών), __1. to lead the way. __2. to lead in war, command (of. Ramsay, Was Christ born at Bethlehem?; DCG, ii, 463 f.). __3. to be governor of a province: with genitive loc., Luk.2:2 3:1.† (AS)

hegemonia.notes: ἡγεμονία, -ας, ἡ (ἡγεμών), [in LXX: Gen.36:30 (אַלּוּף), Num.1:52 2:17, (דֶּגֶל), Sir.7:4 10:1, 4Ma.6:33 4Mac 13:4 * ;] rule, sovereignty: Luk.3:1.† (AS)

hegeomai.notes: ἡγέομαι, -οῦμαι depon. mid., [in LXX (chiefly Pres. ptcp.) for נָגִיד, רֹאשׁ, שַׂר, etc. ;] __1. to lead; __(a) to guide, go before; __(b) to rule, be leader: present ptcp., ἡγούενος, a ruler, leader (MM, Exp., xiv),…

hekastos.notes: 1. every, every one, each, each one , Lat. quisque, (Homer), etc.; the singular is often joined with a pl. Verb, ἔβαν οἴκονδε ἕκαστος they went home every one of them , (Iliad by Homer); ἕκαστος ἐπίστασθε (Xenophon…

hekaton.notes: ἑκατόν, οἱ, αἱ, τά indecl., a hundred: Mat.13:8, 23 Luk.15:4, al.; κατὰ ἑ, Mrk.6:40; εἰς, ἐν ἑ., Mrk.4:8, 20 (AS)

hekatontaetes.notes: ἑκατονταετής (Rec. έτης), -ές (ἑκατόν, ἔτης) [in LXX for שָׁנֶה מֵאָה בֵּן, Gen.17:17 * ;] a hundred years old: Rom.4:19.† (AS)

hekatontaplasion.notes: a hundred times as much or many , (Xenophon Historicus) (ML)

hekatontarchos.notes: ἑκατοντάρχης (-άρχος, Mat.8:5, 8 27:54, Luk.7:2, Act.22:25 28:16; cf. M, Pr., 48); -ου (ἑκατόν, ἄρχω,), [in LXX for מֵאָה שַׂר ;] a centurion: Mat.8:13, Luk.7:6 23:47, Act.10:1, 22 21:32 22:26 23:17, 23 24:23 27:1, 6…

heko.notes: ἥκω [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא ;] pf. with present meaning (hence imperfect = plpf.), to have come, be present: Mat.24:50, Mrk.8:3 (late pf., ἧκα, see Swete, in l; WH, App., 169), Luk.12:46 15:27, Jhn.8:42, Heb.10:7,…

hekon.notes: ἑκών, -οῦσα, -όν [in LXX: Exo.21:13, Job.36:19 * ;] willing, of one's own free will: Rom.8:20, 1Co.9:17 (Cremer, 246).† (AS)

hekousios.notes: ἐκούσιος, -ον (έκών), [in LXX chiefly for נְדָבָה, as Num.15:3 (κατ᾽ ἑ) ;] usually of actions, voluntary: κατὰ ἑ., of free will (Lft., in l), Phm 14.† (AS)

hektos.notes: ἐκτός, adv. [in LXX Jdg.8:26 20:15, 3Ki.10:13, al. (בַּד, Jdg.5:28 (בַּד), בַּד), Sng.4:1-3 (בַּד), al, ;] __1. as adv., outside, beyond: τὸ ἐ., with poss. genitive, the outside, Mat.23:26; in late Gk. (see Deiss.,…

heli.notes: Ἡλεί (Rec. Ἡλί; RV, Ἡλί), ὁ, indecl., Heli, Joseph's father: Luk.3:23.† (AS)

helikia.notes: ἡλικία, -ας, ἡ (ἧλιξ, of the same age, mature), [in LXX: Eze.13:18 (קוֹמָה), Job.29:18, Wis.4:9, Sir.26:17, frequently in 2-4 Mac ;] always of age or maturity; "a stage of growth whether measured by age or stature";…

helisso.notes: ἑλίσσω [in LXX: Isa.34:4 (גָּלַל ni.), Psa.102:26 (חָלַף hi.), etc. ;] to roll, roll up: Heb.1:12 (LXX), Rev.6:14.† (AS)

helkoo.notes: ἑλκόω, -ῶ __1. to wound. __2. to ulcerate; pass., to suffer from sores: pf. ptcp., εἱλκωμίνος (Rec. ἡλκ-), EV, full of sores, Luk.16:20.† (AS)

helkos.notes: ἑλκος, -εος (-ους), τό [in LXX: Exo.9:9-11, Lev.13:18-27, 4Ki.20:7, Job.2:7 (שְׁחִין) ;] __1. a wound (Hom.). __2. a sore, an ulcer (Thuc., al.): Luk.16:21, Rev.16:2, 11.† (AS)

helkuohelko.notes: ἕλκω (Hellenistic form ἑλκύω in Jhn.11:1-57. with, Act.16:19), [in LXX for מָשַׁךְ, etc. ;] to draw: with accusative of thing(s), Jhn.18:10 21:6; with accusative of person(s), before ἔξω, Act.21:30; εἰς, Act.16:19,…

hellas.notes: Ἑλλάς, -άδος, ἡ [in LXX: Isa.66:19, Eze.27:13 (יָוָן), 1Ma.1:1 1Mac 8:9 * ;] with varying usage as to geographical limits; in NT = 'Αχαία (cf. Act.18:12), Greece: Act.20:2.† (AS)

hellen.notes: Ἕλλην, -ηνος, ὁ [in LXX: Jol.3:6, Zec.9:13 (יָוָן), etc.; 1Ma.1:19, al. * ;] a Greek; opposite to βάρβαρος, Rom.1:14; usually in ΝΤ of Greek Gentiles, opposite to Ἰουδαῖοι: Jhn.7:35, Act.11:29 14:1 16:1, 3 18:4…

hellenikos.notes: Ἑλληνικκός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Jer.46:6 50:16 (יָנָה: aliter in Heb.), 2Ma.4:10 2Mac 4:15 2Mac 6:9 2Mac 11:24 2Mac 13:2, 4Ma.8:8 * ;] Greek: τ. Ἑλληνικῇ (sc. γλωσσῇ), Rev.9:11.† (AS)

hellenis.notes: Ἑλληνίς [in LXX: 2Ma.6:8 A * ;] a Greek (i.e. Gentile) woman: Mrk.7:26, Act.17:12.† (AS)

hellenistes.notes: Ἑλληνιστής, -οῦ, ὁ (Ἑλληνίζω, to Hellenize, affect Greek cus­toms), a Hellenist (RV, Grecian Jew): Act.6:1 9:29 11:20.† (AS)

hellenisti.notes: Ἑλληνιστί adv., in Greek: Jhn.19:20; Ἑ. (sc. λαλεῖν) γινώσκεις, Act.21:37 (cf. Field, Notes, 135).† (AS)

helos.notes: ἧλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Isa.41:7 מַסְמֵר etc. ;] a nail: Jhn.20:25.† (AS)

hemera.notes: ἡμέρα, -ας, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly (very freq.) for יוֹם ;] day; __1. as distinct from night: genitive ἡμέρας, by day (WM, §30, 11), Rev.21:25; ἡ. κ. νυκτός (ν. κ. ̔ἡ.), Act.9:24, 1Th.2:9, 2Th.3:8, Rev.4:8 (BL, §36, 13);…

hemeteros.notes: ἡμέτερος, -α, -ον (ἡμεῖς), poss. prop. of first of person(s), our: Act.2:11 24:6 26:5, Rom.15:4, 2Ti.4:15, 1Jn.1:3 Jn 2:2; τὸ ἡ., Luk.16:12, WH, txt., R, mg. (τὸ ὑμ., Rec., WH, mg., R, txt.); oἱ ἡ., our (people),…

hemiorion.notes: ἡμίωρον (Rec. -ιον), -ου, τό (ἥμι, half + ὥρα), half an hour: Rev.8:1.† (AS)

hemisus.notes: ἥμισυς, -εια, -υ genitive, -ους (late, as in π., for cl., -εος Bl., § 8, 4; Mayser, 294), n. p1., ἡμίσια (Rec., cl., -εια), [in LXX for חֵצִי, מַחֲצִית ;] half; __1. as adj., agreeing in gender and number with the…

hemithanes.notes: ἡμιθανής, -ές (ἥμι, half + θνήσκω) [in LXX: 4Ma.4:11 * ;] half-dead: Luk.10:30.† (AS)

hendeka.notes: ἕν-δεκα, οἱ, αἱ, τά indecl., eleven: of the eleven apostles, οἱ, ἕ., Mat.28:16, Mrk.16:14, Luk.24:9, 33, Act.1:26 2:14.† (AS)

heortazo.notes: ἑορτάζω (ἑορτή), [in LXX for חָגַג ;] to keep festival: 1Co.5:8.† (AS)

heorte.notes: ἑορτή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for חַג (chiefly), מוֹעֵד ;] a feast, festival: Luk.2:42, Jhn.5:1, Jhn.6:4 Jhn.7:37, Col.2:10; ἡ ἑ. τοῦ πάσχα, Luk.2:41, Jhn.13:1; τ. ἀζύμων, Luk.22:1; ἡ σκηνοπηγία, Jhn.7:2 (Deiss., LAE, 116);…

hepta.notes: ἐπτά, οἱ, αἱ, τά indecl., seven: Mat.12:45 18:22 (cf. ἑβδομηκοντάκις), Mrk.8:5, al.; οἱ ἐ, Act.21:8 (AS)

heptakis.notes: ἐπτάκις adv., seven times: Mat.18:21-22, Luk.17:4.† (AS)

hermas.notes: Ἑρμᾶς, -ᾶ, accusative, -ᾶν (Doric form of Ἑρμῆς), Hermas, a Christian: Rom.16:14.† (AS)

hermeneia.notes: ἑρμηνεία, see: ἑρμηνία. ἑρμηνία (T, cl., -εία), -ας, ἡ, (ἑρμηνεύω), [in LXX (-εία), Sir, prol. 14 Sir.47:17, from LXX Sir.5:1 * ;] interpretation: 1Co.12:10 14:26.† (AS)

hermeneuo.notes: ἑρμηνεύω [in LXX: Est.4:7 (תִּרְגַּם), Est.10:3 Job.42:17β* ;] __1. to explain. __2. to interpret: Luk.24:27 (WH, mg.), Jhn.1:39, 43 9:7, Heb.7:2.† (AS)

hermes.notes: Ἑρμῆς, -οῦ accusative, Ἑρμῆν, ὁ, Hermes; __(a) the Greek god (Lat. Mercurius): Act.14:12; __(b) a Christian: Rom.16:14.† (see) (AS)

herodes.notes: Ἡρῴδης (Rec. -ώδης), -ου, ὁ Herod; __1. Herod the Great: Mat.2:1-23, Act.23:35. __2. Herod Antipas (ὁ τετραάρχης, Mat.14:1): Mat.14:1, 3 14:6, Mrk.6:1-56, Mrk.8:15, Luk.1:5 3:1, 19 8:3 9:7, 9 13:31, 23, Act.4:27…

herodianoi.notes: Ἡρῳδιανοί (Rec. Ἡρω-), -ῶν, οἱ, Herodians, partisans of Herod (cf. τὸυς τὰ Ἡρῴδου φρονοῦνιτας, FlJ, Ant., xiv, 15, 10): Mat.22:16, Mrk.3:6 12:13.† (AS)

herodias.notes: Ἡρῳδιάς (Rec. Ἡρω-), -άδος, ἡ Herodias, granddaughter of Herod the Great: Mat.14:3, 6, Mrk.6:17, 19 6:22, Luk.3:19.† (AS)

herpeton.notes: ἑρπετόν, -οῦ, τό (ἕρπω, to crawl), [in LXX chiefly for רֶמֶשׂ, שֶׁרֶץ ;] a creeping thing, reptile: Act.10:12 11:6, Rom.1:23, Jas.3:7.† (AS)

hespera.notes: ἑσπέρα, -ας, ἡ (prop, fem, of ἕσπερος), [in LXX chiefly for עָרַב ;] __(a) (sc. ὥρα), evening: Luk.24:29, Act.4:3 20:15 28:23; __(b) (so. χώρα), the west.† (AS)

hessaomai.notes: ἡττάω, -ῶ (ἡσσ-, 2Co.12:13, see: ἥσσων) [in LXX for חָתַת, etc. ;] pass., __1. to be inferior: before ὑπέρ, 2Co.12:13. __2. to be overcome: absol., 2Pe.2:20; with dative, 2Pe.2:19.† (AS)

hesuchazo.notes: ἡσυχάζω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁקַט ;] to be still; __(a) to rest from labour: Luk.23:56; __(b) to live quietly: 1Th.4:11; __(with) to be silent: Luk.14:3, Act.11:18 21:14 (cf. Job.32:7, Neh.5:8).† SYN.: σιγάω (which…

hesuchia.notes: ἡσυχία, -ας, ἡ (ἡσύχιος, which see), [in LXX for אִישׁוֹן, שָׁלֵו, etc. ;] __1. quietness: 2Th.3:12. __2. stillness, silence: Act.22:2, 1Ti.2:11-12 † (AS)

hesuchios.notes: ἡσύχιος, -α, -ον (= the more frequently ἥσυχος; cf. Wis.18:14, Sir.25:20), [in LXX: Isa.66:2 (רוּחַ נָכֶה)* ;] quiet, tranquil: 1Ti.2:2, 1Pe.3:4.† SYN.: ἤρεμος, of tranquillity arising from without; ἡ., from within…

hetairos.notes: ἑταῖρος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for רֵעַ and cognate forms, also Sir.11:6 37:2 ff., al. ;] a companion, comrade: Mat.11:16 (WH, ἑτέροις); voc., as term of address, my friend: Mat.20:13 22:12 26:50.† (AS)

heterodidaskaleo.notes: ἑτεροδιδασκαλέω, -ῶ to teach other or different doctrine: 1Ti.1:3 6:3 (cf. CGT, in l; Milligan, NTD, 102).† (AS)

heteroglossos.notes: ἑτερό-γλωσσος (Att.. -ττος), -ον, [in Aq.: Psa.114:1, Isa.33:19 * ;] of alien speech, of another tongue (see Cremer, 681): 1Co.14:21 (aliter in LXX).† (AS)

heteros.notes: ἕτερος, -α, -ον, [in LXX chiefly for אַחֵר ;] distributive pron., prop. dual (BL, §13, 5; 51, 6), denoting the second of a pair, but in late Gk. encroaching on ἄλλος (M, Pr., 79f.); __1. of number, other; with art.,…

heterozugeo.notes: ἑτερο-ζυγέω, -ῶ [in LXX cf. ἑτερόζυγος, Lev.19:19 (יְקַמְעָם)* ;] to be unequally yoked: metaphorically, with dative of person(s), 2Co.6:14.† (AS)

hetoimasia.notes: ἑτοιμασία, -ας, ἡ (ἑτοιμάζω, which see), [in LXX for כּוּן hi., כֵּן, מָכוֹן, and cognate forms, Est.2:68 3:3, Psa.10:17 65:9 89:14, Nam.2:4, Zec.5:11, Eze.43:11, Da TH Dan.11:7, 20-21, Wis.13:12 * ;] __1. =…

hetoimazo.notes: ἑτοιμάζω (ἕτοιμος), [in LXX chiefly for כּוּן hi. (Hatch, Essays, 51 a.) ;] to prepare, make ready; __(a) absol., of hospitable preparation: Mrk.14:15, Luk.9:52 12:47 22:9, 12; with inf., Mat.26:17; ἵνα, Mrk.14:12;…

hetoimos.notes: ἕτοιμος, -ον also (in cl. after Thuc.) -η (2Co.9:5, 1Pe.1:5), -ον, [in LXX chiefly for כּוּן, מָכוֹן (cf. Hatch, Essays, 51 ff.) ;] prepared, ready; __(a) of things: Mat.22:4, 8, Mrk.14:15, 2Co.9:5 10:16, 1Pe.1:5;…

hettema.notes: ἥττημα, -τος, τό [in LXX for מַס, Isa.31:8 * ;] defect, loss, defeat: Rom.11:12 (cf. Isa, l.with), 1Co.6:7 (cf. Field, Notes, 160 f., 171 f.; Lft., Notes, 212).† (AS)

hettonhesson.notes: ἥσσων (Rec. ἥττ-, the Attic literary form), ἧσσων, inferior, less; neut., τὸ ἧ., adverbially, less: 2Co.12:15; εἰς τὸ ἧ., for the worse (opposite to κρεῖττον): 1Co.11:17.† (AS)

hex.notes: ἕξ, οἱ, αἱ, τά indecl., six: Mat.17:1, Luk.13:14, al. (AS)

hexekonta.notes: ἑξήκοντα, οἱ, αἱ, τά indecl., sixty: Mat.13:8, 23, Mrk.4:8, 20, Luk.24:13, 1Ti.5:9, Rev.11:3 Rev.12:6 Rev.13:18.† (AS)

hexes.notes: ἑξῆς adv. (ἔχω), in order, successively, next: τῇ ἑ. ἡμέρᾳ, Luk.9:37; ἐν τῷ ἑ. (sc. χρόνῳ), soon after, Luk.7:11; τῇ ἑ. (sc. ἡμέρα), Act.21:1 Act.25:17 Act.27:18.† (AS)

hexis.notes: ἐξις, -εως, ἡ (ἔχω), [in LXX, cf. Sir, prol.9 ;] habit, use, experience: Heb.5:14.† (AS)

hidros.notes: ἱδρώς (Tr. -ῶς), -ῶτος, ὁ [in LXX: Gen.3:19 (זֵעָה), 2Ma.2:29, 4Ma.7:8 * ;] sweat: Luk.22:44 (WH, omit).† (AS)

hierateia.notes: ἱερατεία (WH, -ία), -ας, ἡ (ἱερατεύω), [in LXX for כָּהַן pi., כְּהֻנָּה (Exo.29:9, al.), exc. Hos.3:4 (אֵפוֹד)* ;] priesthood, office of priest: Luk.1:9, Heb.7:5.† (AS)

hierateuma.notes: ἱεράτευμα, -τος, τό (ἱερατεύω), [in LXX: βασίλειον ἱ., Exo.19:1-25 6:1-30 (כֹּהֵן מַמְלָכָה) Exo.23:22, 2Ma.2:17 * ;] a priesthood, body of priests: ἱ ἅγιον, 1Pe.2:5; βασ. ἱ, 1Pe.2:9 (LXX, Ex, l.with).† (AS)

hierateuo.notes: ἱερατεύω (cl. = ἱεράομαι) [in LXX chiefly for כָּהַן pi. ;] to be a priest, officiate as a priest: Luk.1:8 (the word is frequently in Inscr.; see LS, see word; Deiss., BS, 215; LAE, 70; Cremer, 734).† (AS)

hieremias.notes: Ἰερεμίας (T, Rec. Ἱερ-; see WH, § 408), -ου, ὁ Heb. Jeremiah the prophet: Mat.2:17 16:14 27:9 (a ref. to Zec.11:13).† (AS)

hiereus.notes: ἱερεύς, -έως, ὁ (ἱερός), [in LXX for כָּהֵן ;] a priest: ἱ. τ. Διός, Act.14:13; of Jewish priests, Mat.8:4 12:4-5, Mrk.1:44 2:26, Luk.1:5 5:14, Jhn.1:19, Heb.8:4, al.; of Christ, Heb.5:6 (LXX) Heb.10:21; of…

hiericho.notes: Ἰερειχώ (T, Ἱερ-; Rec. Ἱεριχώ; cf. Bl., § 3, 4, 4; WH, App., 155), ἡ indecl. (Heb. יְרִיחוֹ), Jericho: Mat.20:29, Mrk.10:46, Luk.10:30 18:35 19:1, Heb.11:30.† (AS)

hieroprepes.notes: ἱεροπρεπής, -ές (ἱερός, πρέπει) [in LXX: 4Ma.9:25 4Mac 11:20 * ;] suited to a sacred character, reverend (RV, reverent): Tit.2:3 (cf. Tr., Syn., § xcii).† (AS)

hieros.notes: ἱερός, -ά, όν [in LXX: Eze.28:18 (מִקְדָּשׁ); τό ἱ, 1Ch.29:4 (בַּיִת), Eze.45:19 (עֲזָרָה); very frequently in I Est.1:1-58 -4 Mac ;] __1. in Hom., marvellous, mighty, divine. __2. (Also in Hom, and later cl.)…

hierosoluma.notes: Ἱεροσόλυμα (WH, Ἰερ-, see Intr., § 408), -ων, τά (on πᾶσα Ἱ., Mat.2:3, see WM, 794; M, Pr., 48, 244; Thayer, see word), and Ἱερουσαλήμ (WH, Ἰερ-), ἡ., indecl., as always in LXX exc. some parts of Apocr.. (Heb.…

hierosolumites.notes: Ἱεροσολυμεῖτης (WH, Ἰερ-; Rec. -μίτης), -ου, ὁ, [in LXX: Sir.50:27 (28) 2Ma.4:22 2Mac 4:39 (V* -μήτης) 2Ma 18:5* ;] an inhabitant of Jerusalem: Mrk.1:5, Jhn.7:25.† (AS)

hierosuleo.notes: ἱερο-συλέω, -ῶ (ἱερόσυλος, which see), [in LXX: 2Ma.9:2 * ;] to rob a temple (commit sacrilege, R, mg.): Rom.2:22.† (AS)

hierosulos.notes: ἱερόσυλος, -ον (ἱερόν, συλάω), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:42 (cf. -λημα, 2Ma.4:39; -λία, 2Ma.13:6)* ;] robbing temples: Act.19:37.† (AS)

hierosune.notes: ἱερωσύνη, -ης, ἡ (ἱερός), [in LXX: 1Ch.29:22 (כָּהַן), I Est.5:38, Sir.45:24, 1Ma.2:54 1Mac 3:49 1Mac 7:9 1Mac 7:21, 4Ma.5:35 4Mac 7:6 * ;] priesthood: Heb.7:11-12, 24.† (AS)

hierourgeo.notes: ἱερουργέω, -ῶ (ἱερουργός, a sacrificing priest) [in LXX: 4Ma.7:3 R (of. -γία, 4Mac 3:30 א R)* ;] to perform sacred rites; with accusative, to minister in priestly service (minister in sacrifice, R, mg.), τὸ…

hierousalem.notes: Ἱερουσαλήμ, see: Ἱεροσόλυμα - (WH, Ἰερ-, see Intr., § 408), -ων, τά (on πᾶσα Ἱ., Mat.2:3, see WM, 794; M, Pr., 48, 244; Thayer, see word), and Ἱερουσαλήμ (WH, Ἰερ-), ἡ., indecl., as always in LXX exc. some parts of…

hikanoo.notes: ἱκανόω, -ῶ (ἱκανός), [in LXX chiefly for רַב ;] to make sufficient, render fit: with dupl. accusative, 2Co.3:6; with accusative of person(s) before εἰς, Col.1:12.† (AS)

hikanos.notes: ἱκανός, -ή, -όν (ἵκω, ἱκάνω, to reach, attain), [in LXX for דַּי, etc. ;] __1. of persons, suffcient, competent, fit: with inf., Mat.3:11, Mrk.1:7, Luk.3:16, 1Co.15:9, 2Co.3:6, 2Ti.2:2; before πρός, 2Co.2:16; before…

hiketeria.notes: ἱκετήριος, -α, -ον (ἱκέτης, a suppliant), [in LXX: Job.40:22, 2Ma.9:18 * ;] of a suppliant; as subst., ἡ ἱ. (Sc. ῥάβδος), __1. in cl., an olive-branch, carried by a suppliant. __2. In late Gk. = ἱκεσία, supplication:…

hilaros.notes: ἱλαρός, -ά, -όν (ἵλαος = ἵλεως, which see), [in LXX: Pro.19:12 (רָצוֹן) Pro.22:8, Est.5:1, Job.33:26, Sir.13:26 26:4, 3Ma.6:35 * ;] cheerful, joyous: 2Co.9:7 (Pro.22:8" (LXX) )† (AS)

hilarotes.notes: ἱλαρότης, -ητος, ἡ (ἱλαρός), [in LXX: Pro.18:22 (רָצוֹן)* ;] cheerfulness: Rom.12:8.† (AS)

hilaskomai.notes: ἱλάσκομαι (ἵλαος = ἵλεως) [in LXX (cf. Westc., Epp. Jo., 85 f.) for סָלַח, 4Ki.5:18 24:4, Psa.25:11, La 3:42, Da TH Dan.9:19; כָּפַר pi., Psa.65:3 78:38 79:9; נָחַם ni., Exo.32:14, Est.4:17 * ;] __1. in cl., with…

hilasmos.notes: ἱλασμός, -οῦ (ἱλάσκομαι), [in LXX: Lev.25:9, Num.5:8 (כִּפֻּר), Psa.130:4, Da TH Dan.9:9 (סְלִיחָה), Amo.8:14 (אַשְׁמָה), Eze.44:27 (חַטָּאָה), 1Ch.28:20, Sir.18:20 A, 2Ma.3:33 * ;] __1. an appeasing (Plut.). __2. a…

hilasterios.notes: ἱλαστήριος, -α, -ον (ἱλάσκομαι), [in LXX: 4Ma.17:22; neut., Exo.25:16 (17) ff. Exo.31:7 35:12 37:6 ff., Lev.16:2, 13 ff. Num.7:89 (כַּפֹּרֶת), Eze.43:14, 17 43:20 (עֲזָרָה) Amo.9:1 (on the original here, see Deiss.,…

hileos.notes: ἵλεως, -ων (Att.. for ἵλαος), [in LXX for חָלִיל, 1Ki.14:45, 2Ki.20:20, al.; ἵ. εἶναι, סָלַח, 3Ki.8:30 ff. Jer.31:34, al. ;] propitious, merciful: τ. ἀδικίαες, Heb.8:12 (Je, l.with; cf.1 Ki, l.with); ἵ. σοι (sc. ἔστω…

himas.notes: ἱμάς, -άντος, ὁ [in LXX: Isa.5:27 (שְׂרוֹךְ), Sir.33:26, 4Ma.9:11, al ;] a thong, strap: for binding prisoners (Ma, Sir, ll. with), Act.22:25; for fastening sandals, Mk, Luk.3:16, Jhn.1:27.† (AS)

himation.notes: ἱμάτιον, -ου, τό (dim, of εἷμα, a garment), [in LXX chiefly for בֶּגֶד also for שִׂמְלָה, שַׂלְמָה, etc. ;] a garment, but in usage always (exc. in p1., see infr.) of an outer garment, a mantle, cloak (thrown over…

himatismos.notes: ἱματισμός, -ού (ιματίζω), [in LXX: Gen.24:53, 2Ch.18:29, Eze.16:18, al. (בֶּגֶד); Exo.3:22 12:35, Rut.3:3, al. (שִׂמְלָה), etc. ;] clothing, apparel (usually of sumptuous attire; see Tr., Syn., § 1): Luk.7:25 9:29,…

himatizo.notes: ἱματίζω (ἱμάτιον), to clothe: Mrk.5:15, Luk.8:35 (elsewhere only in π.; cf. MM, Exp., xv; Deiss., LAE, 78 f.).† (AS)

himeirohimeiromai.notes: ἱμείρω [in LXX: Job.3:21 B3R (ABא, ὁμ-) ;] 1Th.2:8 (Rec.), see: ὁμείρομαί. (AS)

hippeus.notes: ἱππεύς, -έως (on accusative pl., -εῖς, ν. BI., § 8, 2; Thack., Gr., 148), ὁ (ἵππος), [in LXX chiefly for פָּרָשׁ ;] a horseman: Act.23:23, 32.† (AS)

hippikos.notes: ἱππικός, -ή, -όν (ἵππος), [in LXX: 1Ma.15:33, 3Ma.1:1 * ;] of a horse or of horsemen, equestrian; as subst., τὸ ἱ (sc. στράτευμα), cavalry: Heb.9:16.† (AS)

hippos.notes: ἵππος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for סוּס, sometimes for פָּרָשׁ, רֶכֶב ;] a horse: Jas.3:3, Rev.6:2 ff. Rev.9:7 ff. Rev.14:20 18:13 19:11 ff,† (AS)

histemi.notes: ἵστημι, and in late writers, also ἱστάνω (Veitch, see word; Bl., §23, 2; M, Pr., 55), [in LXX chiefly for עמד, קוּם, also for נצב ni., hi., יצב hith., etc.]. __I. Trans, in pres., impf., fut. and 1 aor. act. and in…

historeo.notes: ἱστορέω, -ῶ (ἵστωρ, one learned or skilled in) [in LXX: 1Es.1:33 Est.1:42 * ;] __1. with accusative of thing(s), to inquire into, learn by inquiry. __2. C. accusative of person(s), to inquire of or about. __3. to…

hode.notes: ὧδε, adv., [in LXX for חֲלֹם ,פֹּה ,הִנֶּה, etc. ;] __1. prop., of manner, so (Hom., al.). __2. In poets (rarely) and late writers, of place, __(a) hither (BL, §25, 2): Mat.8:29, Mrk.11:3, Luk.9:41, Jhn.6:25, al.;…

hodegeo.notes: ὁδηγέω, -ῶ, (ὁδηγός) [in LXX chiefly for נוּחַ, also for דֶּרֶךְ hi., הָלַךְ hi., etc. ;] to lead on one's way, to guide: with accusative of person(s), Mat.15:14, Luk.6:39; id. before ἐπί, Rev.7:17. Metaphorical, to…

hodegos.notes: ὁδηγός, -οῦ, ὁ (ὁδός, ἡγέομαι) [in LXX : Est.8:1, Wis.7:15 18:3, 1Ma.4:2, 2Ma.5:15 * ;] a leader on the way, a guide: Act.1:16. Fig., ὁ. τυφλῶν, Rom.2:19; pl., Mat.15:14 23:16, 24.† (AS)

hodeuo.notes: ὁδεύω (ὁδός), [in LXX : 3Ki.6:12 A (metaphorically, הָלַךְ), Tob.6:5, Wis.5:7 (figuratively)* ;] to travel, journey: Luk.10:33 (cf.δι-, συν-οδεύω).† (AS)

hodoiporeo.notes: ὁδοιπορέω, -ῶ (ὁδοιπόρος, a traveller, Gen.37:25, al.), to travel, journey: Act.10:9.† (AS)

hodoiporia.notes: ὁδοιπορία, -ας, ἡ (see supr.), [in LXX : Wis.13:18 18:3 19:5, 1Ma.6:41 * ;] a journey. Jhn.4:6, 2Co.11:26.† (AS)

hodos.notes: ὁδός, -οῦ, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly (very freq.) for דֶּרֶךְ ;] __1. a way, path, road: Mat.2:12, Mrk.10:46, Luk.3:5 al.; κατὰ τὴν ὁ., Luk.10:4, al.; παρὰ τὴν ὁ., Mat.13:4, al.; with genitive of person(s), (subj.), τὴν ὁ.…

holokautoma.notes: ὁλοκαύτωμα, -τος, τό (ὅλος, καίω), [in LXX chiefly for עֹלָה ;] a whole burnt offering: Mrk.12:33, Heb.10:6, 8" (LXX) (of. Kennedy, Sources, 113 f.).† SYN.: see: θυσία (AS)

holokleria.notes: ὀλοκληρία, -ας, ἡ (ὁλόκληρος), [in LXX: Isa.1:6 (מְתֹם)* ;] completeness, soundness: Act.3:16.† (AS)

holokleros.notes: ὁλό-κληρος, -ον (ὅλος, κλῆρος, i.e. with all that has fallen by lot), [in LXX: Lev.23:15, Eze.15:5 (תָּמִים), Deu.27:6, Jos.8:31 (9:2) (שָׁלֵם), Zec.11:16 (נָצַב ni.), Wis.15:3, 1Ma.4:47, 4Ma.15:17 * ;] complete,…

holos.notes: ὅλος, -η, -ον, [in LXX chiefly for כֹּל ;] of persons and things, whole, entire, complete; __1. of indefinite ideas, with subst. anarth.: Luk.5:5, Act.11:26 28:30, Tit.1:11; ὅλον ἄνθρωπον (an entire man; see Field,…

holoteles.notes: ὁλοτελής, -ές (ὅλος, τίλος), complete, perfect.. 1Th.5:23.† SYN.: ὁλόκληρος (which see), τέλειος. "As regards meaning, ὁλόκληρος can hardly be distinguished from ὁλοτελής though, in accordance with its derivation, it…

homileo.notes: ὁμιλέω, -ῶ (ὅμιλος), [in LXX: Pro.5:19 (רָוָה pi.), al. ;] to be in company with, consort with; hence, to converse with: Act.20:11; with dative, Act.24:26; before πρός, Luk.24:14-15.† (AS)

homilia.notes: όμιλία, -ας, ή (όμιλος), [in LXX: Exo.21:10 (עוֹנָה), al. ;] company, association: 1Co.15:33.† (AS)

homoioma.notes: ὁμοίωμα, -τος, τό (ὁμοιόω), [in LXX for דְּמוּת, תַּבְנִית, תְּמוּנָה, etc. ;] that which is made like something; __(a) concrete, an image, like­ness (Psa.106:20, Eze.1:5, 1Ma.3:48, al.) : Rev.9:7; __(b) abstract,…

homoioo.notes: ὁμοιόω, -ῶ (ὅμοιος), [in LXX chiefly for דָּמָה ;] __1. to make like, with genitive and dative; pass., to be made or become like: Mat.6:8 13:24 18:23 22:2 25:1, Act.14:11, Heb.2:17; before ὡς (cf. Eze.32:2, Heb.),…

homoiopathes.notes: ὁμοιοπαθής, -ές (ὅμοιος, πάσχω), [in LXX: Wis.7:3, 4Ma.12:13 * ;] of like feelings or affections: with dative, Act.14:15, Jas.5:17.† (AS)

homoios.notes: ὅμοιος, -οία, -οιον [in LXX for כְּמוֹ, etc. ;] like, resembling, such as, the same as: with dative, of form or appearance, Jhn.9:9, Rev.1:13 (WH, mg., R, but see infr.) Rev.1:15 2:18 4:6-7 9:7, 10 (B1., § 37, 62)…

homoiosis.notes: ὁμοίωσις, -εως, ἡ (ὁμοιόω) [in LXX chiefly for דְּמוּת, Psa.58:4, al. ;] __1. a making like, becoming like (Plat.). __2. likeness: Jas.3:9 (LXX).† SYN.: see: ὁμοίωμα, and cf. Tr., Syn., § xv (AS)

homoiotes.notes: ὁμοιότης, -ητος, ἡ (ὅμοιος), [in LXX: Gen.1:11-12 (מִין), Wis.14:19, 4Ma.15:4 * ;] likeness: καθ᾽ ὁμοιότητα, in like manner, Heb.4:15; id. with genitive (MM, xviii), after the likeness (of), Heb.7:15.† (AS)

homologeo.notes: ὁμολογέω, -ῶ (ὁμόλογος, of one mind: Dan LXX Su 1:60 *), [in LXX: Job.40:9 (14) (יָדָה hi.), Jer.44:25 (נָדַר), al. ;] __1. to speak the same language (Hdt.). __2. to agree with (Hdt., Plat., al.). __3. to agree,…

homologia.notes: ὁμολογία, -ας, ἡ (όμολογέω), [in LXX: Deu.12:6, 17. Amo.4:5, Eze.46:12 (נְדָבָה), Lev.22:18, Jer.44:25 (נֶדֶר), I Est.9:8 * ;] __1. in cl., an agreement assent, compact (in π., of a contract; Deiss., BS, 249). __2.…

homologoumenos.notes: ὁμολογουμένως adv. (ὁμ oλογέω), [in LXX: 4Ma.6:31 4Mac 7:16 4Mac 16:1 * ;] __1. as agreed, conformably with. __2. confessedly, by common consent: 1Ti.3:16.† (AS)

homophron.notes: ὁμόφρων, -ον (ὁμός, φρήν), = ὁμόνοος, agreeing, of one mind: 1Pe.3:8.† (AS)

homotechnos.notes: ὁμότεχνος, -ον (ὁμός, τέχνη), practising the same craft, of the same trade: Act.18:3.† (AS)

homothumadon.notes: ὁμοθυμαδόν (ὁμός, θυμός), [in LXX for יַחַד (frequently in Jb), Wis.10:20 18:5, 12, al. ;] with one mind, with one accord: Act.1:14 2:46 4:24 5:12 7:57 8:6 12:20 15:25 18:12 19:29, Rom.15:6 (Hatch, Essays, 63 f.,…

homou.notes: ὁμοῦ adv. (ὁμός), together; __(a) prop., of place: Jhn.21:2, Act.2:1; __(b) without idea of place: Jhn.4:36 20:4.† (AS)

hoplizo.notes: ὁπλίζω (ὅπλον), [in Sm.: Jer.52:25 * ;] to make ready, equip; of soldiers, to arm. Mid., to arm oneself; figuratively, ἔννοιαν: 1Pe.4:1 (cf. θράσος ὁ., Soph., Elec., 995), (cf. καθ-ὁπλίζω).† (AS)

hoplon.notes: ὅπλον, -ου, τό [in LXX for מָגֵן, צִנָּה, etc. ;] __1. a tool, implement, instrument: ὅπλα ἀδικίας (Opp. to ὅ. δικαιοσύνης), Rom.6:13. __2. Freq., in pl., arms, weapons: Jhn.18:3, 2Co.10:4; metaphorically, τ. φωτός,…

hora.notes: ὥρα, -ας, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for עֵת and in Da for שָׁעָה ;] __1. any time or period fixed by nature, esp. a season (Hom., Hdt., Plat., al.). __2. A part of the day, and esp. a twelfth part of day or night, an hour:…

horaios.notes: ὡραῖος, -α, -ον (ὥρα), [in LXX for נָאָה, טוֹב, יָפָה, etc. ;] seasonable, timely, esp. of ripe fruits; hence, blooming, beautiful (both of things and persons): Mat.23:27, Act.3:2, 10, Rom.10:15 (LXX, ὥρα).† (AS)

horama.notes: ὅραμα, -τος, τό (ὁράω), [in LXX for חָזוֹן, מַרְאֶה, etc. ;] that which is seen; __(a) a sight, spectacle: Mat.17:9, Act.7:31; __(b) an appearance, vision: Act.9:10, 12 (Rec.) Act.10:3, 17 10:19 11:5 12:9 16:9-10…

horao.notes: ὁράω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for ראה, also for חזה, etc. ;] in "durative" sense (hence aor. act., εἶδον, pass., ὤφθην, fut., ὄψομαι, from different roots; see M, Pr., 110f.), to see (in colloq. even the present is rare,…

horasis.notes: ὅρασις, -εως, ἡ (ὁράω) [in LXX chiefly for מַרְאֶה, חָזוֹן and cognate forms ;] __1. in Arist. and later writers, the act of seeing, the sense of sight, and by meton., pl., the eyes. __2. appearance (Num.24:4,…

horion.notes: ὅριον, -ου, τό (ὅρος, a boundary), [in LXX chiefly for גְּבוּל ;] a boundary, bound; chiefly in pl., and so always in NT: Mat.2:16 4:13 8:34 15:22, 39 19:1, Mrk.5:17 7:24, 31 10:1, Act.13:50.† (AS)

horizo.notes: ὁρίζω (ὅρος, a boundary), [in LXX for אָסַר, גְּבוּל, etc. ;] __1. to separate, mark off by boundaries (so Num.34:6, Jhn.13:27). __2. to determine, appoint, designate: of time, with accusative, Act.17:26, Heb.4:7;…

horkizo.notes: ὁρκίζω (ὅρκος), [in LXX: Gen.24:37, al. (שָׁבַע hi.) ;] __1. to make one swear (Xen., Polyb., a1.). __2. to adjure: with dupl. accusative, Mrk.5:7, Act.19:13 (cf. ἐν-, ἐξ-ορκίζω).† (AS)

horkomosia.notes: ὁρκωμοσία, -ας, ἡ (ὅρκος, ὄμνυμι; by metapl. for τὰ ὁρκωμόσια, asseverations on oath; see MM, xviii), [in LXX: Eze.17:18-19 (עָלָה) 1Es 9:93* ;] affirmation on oath, an oath: Heb.7:20-21, 28.† (AS)

horkos.notes: ὅρκος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for שֶׁוַע, שִׁבְעָה and cogn. forms ;] an oath: Mat.14:7 26:72, Luk.1:73, Act.2:30, Heb.6:16-17, Jas.5:12; p1., Mat.5:33 (LXX) Mat.14:9, Mrk.6:26.† (AS)

hormao.notes: ὁρμάω, -ῶ (ὁρμή), [in LXX for שׁוּב, etc. ;] __1. causal, to set in motion, urge on. __2. Intrans., to hasten on, rush: before εἰς, Mat.8:32, Mrk.5:13, Luk.8:33, Act.19:29; ἐπί, with accusative, Act.7:57.† (AS)

horme.notes: ὁρμή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Pro.3:25 (שׁוֹא), etc. ;] __1. a violent movement, impulse: Jas.3:4; __2. a hostile movement, onset, assault. Act.14:5.† (AS)

hormema.notes: ὅρμημα, -τος, τό (ὁρμάω) [in LXX: Hos.5:10, Amo.1:11 (עֶבְרָה), etc. ;] a rush: Rev.18:21.† (AS)

horothesia.notes: ὁροθεσία, -ας, ἡ (ὅρος, a boundary, + τίθημι) a setting of boundaries; in pl., bounds; Act.17:26.† (AS)

hosanna.notes: ὡσαννά (T, ὠσ-) (Heb נָא and הוֹשֵׁעַ; see Dalman, Words, 220 IL; Gr., 249), hosanna: Mat.21:9, Mrk.11:9-10, Jhn.12:13; τ. υἱῷ Δανείδ, Mat.21:9, 15.† (AS)

hosautos.notes: ὡσαύτως adv., strengthened for ὡς (in Hom, at the beginning of a clause, in the form ὣς δ᾽ αὔτως, later in one word), in like manner, just so, likewise: Mat.20:5 21:30, 36 25:17, Mrk.14:31, Luk.13:5 20:31, Rom.8:26,…

hosee.notes: Ὠσηέ (Tr., Ὁσ-), ὁ (Heb. הוֹשֵׁעַ), Hosea: Rom.9:25.† (AS)

hosios.notes: ὅσιος, -ον (so sometimes in cl., but most frequently -α, -ον), [in LXX chiefly for חָסִיד, also for טָהוֹר, יָשָׁר, תָּמִים ;] religiously right, right­eous, pious, Holy: of men, Tit.1:8. Heb.7:26; by meton., ὁ.…

hosiotes.notes: ὁσιότης, -ητος, ἡ (ὅσιος), [in LXX: Deu.9:5 (יֹשֶׁר), 1Ki.14:41, 3Ki.9:4 (תֹּם, תָּמִים), Pro.14:32, Wis.2:22 5:19 9:3 14:30 * ;] piety, holiness: assoc. with δικαιοσύνη, Luk.1:75, Eph.4:24.† SYN.: see: ἅγιος (AS)

hosos.notes: ὅσος, -η, -ον, correlat. of τοσοῦτος, how much, how many, how great, how far, how long, as much as, etc. (= Lat. quantus); __(a) of number and quantity: m. pl., Mat.14:36, Mrk.3:10, Rom.2:12, al.; n. pl., Mat.17:12,…

huakinthinos.notes: ὑακίνθινος, -η, -ον (ὑάκινθος), [in LXX for תַּחַשׁ, תְּכֵלֶת ;] of hyacinth, hyacinthine (see: ὑάκινθος), "doubtless meant to describe the blue smoke of a sulphurous flame" (Swete): Rev.9:17 (Hom., Eur., al.).† (AS)

huakinthos.notes: ὑακίνθος, -ου, ό [in LXX chiefly for תְּכֵלֶת ;] hyacinth; __(a) in cl., a flower, prob. the dark blue iris; __(b) in late writers, a precious stone of the same colour, perhaps the sapphire: Rev.21:20 (Phil., FlJ,…

hualos.notes: ὕαλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Job.28:17 (זְכוּכִית) * ;] __1. (Hdt.) a clear transparent stone. __2. (from Plat. on) glass: Rev.21:18, 21.† (AS)

hubris.notes: ὕβρις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for גָּאוֹן and cogn. forms ;] __1. wantonness, insolence. __2. = ὕβρισμα, an act of wanton violence, an outrage, injury: 2Co.12:10; metaphorically, of a loss by sea (find.), Act.27:10,…

hubristes.notes: ὑβριστής, -οῦ, ὁ (ὑβρίζω), [in LXX chiefly for גֵּאֶה ;] a violent, insolent man: Rom.1:20, 1Ti.1:13 (EV, injurious).† SYN.: ἀλαζών, ὑπερήφανος, see Tr., Syn., § xxix (AS)

hubrizo.notes: ὑβρίζω (ὕβρις), [in LXX for גֵּאֶה, etc. ;] __1. intrans., to wax wanton, run riot. __2. Trans., to outrage, insult, treat insolently: with accusative of person(s), Mat.22:6, Luk.11:45 18:32, Act.14:5, 1Th.2:2.† (AS)

hudorhudatos.notes: ὕδωρ genitive, ὕδατος, τό, [in LXX chiefly for מַיִם ;] water: Mat.3:16, Mrk.1:10, Luk.7:44, Jhn.4:7, Jas.3:12, Rev.8:10, al.; pl., Mat.14:28-29, Jhn.3:23, Rev.1:15, al.; βαπτίζειν (ἐν) ὕδατι, Mat.3:11, Mrk.1:8,…

hudria.notes: ὑδρία, -ας, ἠ (ὕδωρ), [in LXX for בַּד ;] __1. prop., a water-pot or jar: Jhn.2:6, 7 John 4:28. __2. More frequently in Attic = ἄγγυς, a pot, urn or jar of any kind, as for holding wine, coins, etc. (see Rutherford,…

hudropikos.notes: ὑδρωπικός, -ή, -όν (ὕδρωψ, dropsy), dropsical, suffering from dropsy: Luk.14:2.† (AS)

hudropoteo.notes: ὑδροποτίω, -ῶ (ὕδωρ, πίνω), [in LXX: Dan LXX 1:12 שָׁתָה מַיִם) * ;] to drink water, opposite to οἴνῳ χρῆσθαι: 1Ti.5:23 (Hdt., i, 71; Plat., Rep., 561 with, al.).† (AS)

huetos.notes: ὑετός, -οῦ, ὁ (ὕω, to rain), [in LXX chiefly for ;] rain: Act.14:17 28:2, Heb.6:7, Jas.5:18, Rev.11:6; ὄψιμος (which see), sc. ὑ., Jas.5:7.† (AS)

hugiaino.notes: ὑγιαίνω (ὑγιής), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁלוֹם, and frequently in To ;] to be sound, healthy, in good health: Luk.5:31 7:10 15:27, 3Jo.2. In Past. Epp. (as also in cl.) metaphorically, ὑ. ἐν τ. πίστει, Tit.1:13 ; τ.…

hugies.notes: ὑγιής, -ές, accusative, ὑγιῆ (Attic usually -ιᾶ), [in LXX for חַי, etc. ;] sound, whole, healthy: Mat.12:13 15:31, Jhn.5:[4], 6, 9, 11, 14, 15 7:23, Act.4:10; before ἀπό, Mrk.5:34; of words, opinions, etc. (as in…

hugros.notes: ὑγρός, -ά, -όν, [in LXX: Jdg.16:7,8 (לַח), Job.8:16 (רָשׂב), Sir.39:13 wet, moist, opposite to ξηρός: of wood, sappy, green: Luk.23:31.† (AS)

hule.notes: ὕλη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Job.19:29 (†) Job.38:40 (סֻכָּה), Psa.69:2 B.l א (יָוֵן), Isa.10:17 (שָׁמִיר), Wis.11:17 15:13, Sir.28:10, 2Ma.2:24, 4Ma.1:29 * ;] __1. wood, forest, woodland (Thus., Xen., al.). __2. wood,…

humeteros.notes: ὑμέτερος, -α, -ον poss. pron. of second of person(s) pl. (= emphasized genitive, ὑμῶν), your, yours: Jhn.7:6 8:17 15:20, Act.27:34, Rom.11:31, 1Co.16:17, 2Co.8:8, Gal.6:13; as pred., Luk.6:20; τὸ ὑ., as subst.,…

humneo.notes: ὑμνέω, -ῶ (ὕμνος) [in LXX for הָלַל pi., שִׁיר, יָדָה hi., etc. ;] __1. trans., with accusative of person(s) (in cl. also with accusative of thing(s)), to sing to, laud, sing to the praise of: Act.16:25, Heb.2:12.…

humnos.notes: ὕμνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for תְּהִלָּה, נְגִינָה, שִׁיר, etc. ;] a hymn; __(a) in cl. a festal song in praise of gods or heroes; __(b) in LXX and NT a song of praise addressed to God: Eph.5:19, Col.3:16.† SYN.: ψαλμός,…

hupago.notes: ὑπ-άγω [in LXX: Exo.14:21 (הָלַךְ hi.), elsewhere only as seel., Tob.8:21, al. ;] a word of the vulgar language, in Pres. and imperfect only (Bl., § 53, 1). __I. Trans. __1. to lead or bring under, subdue (Hom.,…

hupakoe.notes: ὑπακοή, -ῆς, ἡ (ὑπακούω), [in LXX: 2Ki.22:36 (עֲנָוָה); in Aq.: 2Ki.23:23 * ;] obedience (opposite to παρακοή); __1. in general, absol., εἰς ὑ., Rom.6:16; with genitive subjc., 2Co.7:15 10:6, Phm 21; with genitive…

hupakouo.notes: ὑπακούω [in LXX: chiefly for שָׁמַע ;] to listen, attend, hence, __(a) to answer a knock at a door (Plat., Xen., al.): Act.12:13; __(b) to attend to, submit to, obey (Hdt., Thuc., al.): absol., Php.2:12; with inf.,…

hupandros.notes: ὕπανδρος, -ον [in LXX: Num.5:20, 29 (אִישׁ תַּחַת), Pro.6:24, 29, Sir.9:9 41:21 * ;] under or subject to a man, married: γυνή, Rom.7:2 (Polyb., Diod., al.).† (AS)

hupantao.notes: ὑπ-αντάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Dan LXX 10:14 (קָרָה), Sir.9:3, al. ;] to go to meet, meet: with dative of person(s) (see M, Pr., 64), Mat.8:28 28:9, Mrk.5:2, Luk.8:27 17:12 (ἀπ-, WH, txt.), Jhn.4:51 11:20, 30 12:18,…

hupantesis.notes: ὑπ-άντησις, -εως, ἡ (ὑπαντάω), [in LXX: Jdg.11:34, εἰς ὑ. (קִרְאָה), etc. ;] a going to meet: εἰς ὑ. (see M, Pro.14:1-35 n), Mat.8:34 25:1, Jhn.12:13.† (AS)

huparcho.notes: ὑπάρχω [in LXX for הָיָה, יֵשׁ, לוּן, etc.; τ. ὑπάρχοντα for מִקְנֶה, רְכוּשׁ, etc. ;] __1. to begin, make a beginning (Hom., Hdt., al.). __2. to be in existence, be ready, be at hand (Hdt., Thuc., al.): Act.19:40…

huparxis.notes: ὕπαρξις, -εως, ἡ (ὑπάρχω), [in LXX for רְכוּשׁ, הוֹן, etc. ;] __1. subsistence, existence (Arist., al.). __2. In late writers, = τὰ ὑπάρχοντα, substance, property: Heb.10:34; pl., Act.2:45.† (AS)

hupecho.notes: ὑπ-έχω [in LXX: Psa.89:50 (נָשָׂא), La 5:7 (סָבַל), Wis.12:21, 2Ma.4:48 * ;] to hold or put under. Metaphorical, to undergo, suffer: δίκην, Ju 7 (Soph., Eur., al.).† (AS)

hupeiko.notes: ὑπ-είκω [in LXX: 4Ma.6:35 * ;] __1. to retire, withdraw. __2. to yield, submit: metaphorically, Heb.13:17.† (AS)

hupekoos.notes: ὑπήκοος, -ον (ὑπακσύω), [in LXX: Jhn.17:13 (לָמַם) Pro.21:28 (שָׁמַע), etc. ;] giving ear, obedient, subject: Php.2:8; with dative of person(s), Act.7:39; εἰς πάντα, 2Co.2:9.† (AS)

hupenantios.notes: ὑπ-εναντίος, -α, -ον [in LXX for אָיַב, צַר, etc. ;] set over against, opposite. Metaphorical (Plat., Arist., al.), opposed to, contrary to: with dative of person(s), Col.2:14; absol., as subst., ὁ ὑ., Heb.10:27 (cf.…

huperairo.notes: ὑπερ-αίρω [in LXX: 2Ch.32:23 (נָשָׂא ni.), etc. ;] to lift or raise over. Mid., to uplift oneself: 2Co.12:7; before ἐπί, with accusative, of person(s), 2Th.2:4 (cf. 2Ma.5:23).† (AS)

huperakmos.notes: ὑπέρακμος, -ον past the bloom of youth: 1Co.7:36 (Eustath.).† (AS)

huperauxano.notes: ὑπερ-αυξάνω to increase beyond measure: 2Th.1:3 (see Lft., Notes, 98).† (AS)

huperbaino.notes: ὑπερ-βαίνω [in LXX for עָבַר, etc. ;] __1. trans., to step over, trans­gress (R, overreach: 1Th.4:6). __2. Intrans., to transgress: metaphorically, 1Th.4:6 (R, txt.; see M, Th., in l).† (AS)

huperballo.notes: ὑπερ-βάλλω [in LXX: Job.15:11 A (†), Sir.5:7 25:11, al. ;] __1. trans., to throw over or beyond. __2. Intrans., to run beyond. In both senses, metaphorically, to exceed, surpass, transcend: 2Co.3:10 9:14, Eph.1:19…

huperballontos.notes: ὑπερβαλλόντως [in LXX: Job.15:11 א B (†) * ;] above measure: 2Co.11:23.† (AS)

huperbole.notes: ὑπερ-βολή, -ῆς, ἡ (ὑπερβάλλω), [in LXX: καθ᾽ ὑ., 4Ma.3:18 * ;] a throwing beyond. Metaphorical, excess, superiority, excellence: 2Co.4:7 12:7; καθ᾽ ὑπερβολήν, beyond measure, exceedingly, Rom.7:13, 1Co.12:31,…

huperecho.notes: ὑπερ-έχω [in LXX: Gen.25:23 (אָמַץ), Exo.26:13 (עֲדַף), Sir.36:7, al. ;] __1. trans., to hold over or above. __2. Intrans. (when a noun follows, the case is governed by the prep.; see Bl., § 34, 1; 36, 8), to rise…

hupereido.notes: ὑπερ-εῖδον, aor. [in LXX for עָלַם hi., מַעַל, etc. ;] to overlook: with accusative of thing(s), Act.17:30 (AS)

huperekchuno.notes: ὑπερ-εκ-χύννω (Rec. -ύνω), late form of -χέω to pour out over. Pass., to overflow, run over: Luk.6:38 (not elsewhere).† (AS)

huperekteino.notes: ὑπερ-εκ-τείνω to stretch out overmuch: metaphorically, ἑαυτούς, 2Co.10:14.† (AS)

huperentugchano.notes: ὑπερ-εν-τυγχάνω to intercede or make petition for (see Deiss., BS, 121 f.): before ὑπέρ, Rom.8:26.† (AS)

huperephania.notes: ὑπερηφανία, -ας, ἡ (ὑπερήφανος, which see), [in LXX chiefly for גָּאוֹן and cogn. forms ;] haughtiness, arrogance, disdain: Mrk.7:22 (Plat., Xen.).† (AS)

huperephanos.notes: ὑπερήφανος, -ον (ὑπέρ, φαίνομαι, with η pleonast., see Kühner3, I, 189) [in LXX for זֵד, גֵּאֶה, לוּץ, etc. ;] showing oneself above others; __(a) in good sense (Plat., al.), pre-eminent, splendid; __(b) more…

hupereteo.notes: ὑπηρετέω, -ῶ (ὑπηρέτης) [in LXX: Wis.16:21, 24-25 19:6, Sir.39:4 * ;] prop., to serve as rower on a ship (Diod., al.). In cl. always metaphorically, to minister to, serve: with dative of person(s), Act.13:36 20:34…

huperetes.notes: ὑπηρέτης, -ου, ὁ (ὑπό + ἐρέτης, a rower) [in LXX: Pro.14:35 (עֶבֶד), Wis.6:4, al. ;] prop., an under rower; hence, generally, a servant, attendant, minister: of a magistrate's attendant, Mat.5:25; of officers of the…

hupernikao.notes: ὑπερ-νικάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Da TH Dan.6:3; in Sm.: Psa.43:1 * ;] to be more than conqueror: Rom.8:37 (eccl.).† (AS)

huperoche.notes: ὑπεροχή, -ῆς, ἡ (ὑπερέχω) [in LXX: Jer.52:22 (קוֹמָה), 2Ma.3:11, al. ;] a projection, eminence, as the peak of a mountain. Metaphorical (Arist.), excellence, pre-eminence: λόγου ἢ σοφίας, 1Co.2:1; οἱ ἐν ὑ (for a…

huperogkos.notes: ὑπέρ-ογκος, -ον [in LXX: Deu.30:11 (פָּלָא), etc. ;] of excessive weight or size; metaphorically, excessive, immoderate, in late writers, of arrogant speech (see Mayor on Ju, l.with): n. pl., 2Pe.2:18, Ju 16.† (AS)

huperoon.notes: ὑπερῷον, -ου, τό (neut. of ὑπερῷος, above, ὑπέρ) [in LXX for עֲלִיָּה and cogn. forms ;] __1. in cl., the upper story or upper rooms where the women resided (Hom., a1.). __2. In LXX and NT, an upper chamber,…

huperperisseuo.notes: ὑπερ-περισσεύω to abound more exceedingly: Rom.5:21. Mid., in same sense (RV, overflow): with dative of thing(s), 2Co.7:4.† (AS)

huperphroneo.notes: ὑπερ-φρονέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 4Ma.13:1 4Mac 14:11 4Mac 16:2 * ;] __1. to be overproud, high-minded (Æsch.): μὴ ὑ. παρ᾽ ὃ δεῖ φρονεῖν (on the paranom., see Vau., in l), Rom.12:3. __2. to overlook, think slightly of (Thuc.,…

huperpleonazo.notes: ὑπερ-πλεονάζω to abound exceedingly: 1Ti.1:14 (Ps Sol 5:19; Herm., Mand., v, 2, 5).† (AS)

huperupsoo.notes: ὑπερ-υψόω, -ῶ [in LXX: Psa.37:35 (עָרִיץ) Psa.97:9 (עָלָה ni.), Da TH Dan.4:34 11:12 (רוּם), ib. LXX TH Dan.3:52 ff.* ;] __1. to exalt beyond measure, exalt to the highest place: Php.2:9. __2. to extol (Da, ll.…

huphantos.notes: ὑφαντός, -ή, -όν (ὑφαίνω), [in LXX chiefly for חָשַׁב ;] woven: Jhn.19:23.† ὑφαίνω, [in LXX for אָרַג, etc. ;] to weave: Luk.12:27, T, WH, mg.† (AS)

hupnos.notes: ὕπνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for שֵׁנָה, חֲלוֹם, etc. ;] sleep: Mat.1:24, Luk.9:32, Jhn.11:13, Act.20:9; metaphorically, Rom.13:11.† (AS)

hupoballo.notes: ὑπο-βάλλω [in LXX: ??Dan.3:9 A, I Est.2:18 * ;] to throw or put under. Metaphorical, __(a) to subject, submit; __(b) to suggest, whisper, prompt; __(with) to suborn (see Field, Notes, 113), instigate: with accusative…

hupochoreo.notes: ὑπο-χωρέω [in LXX: Jdg.20:37 B (-), Sir.13:9, 2Ma.12:12 * ;] to go back, retire: before ἐν, Luk.5:16; before εἰς, Luk.9:10.† (AS)

hupodechomai.notes: ὑπο-δέχομαι [in LXX: Tob.7:8-9 Jdth.13:13 A, 1Ma.16:15, 4Ma.13:17 * ;] to receive under one's roof, receive as a guest, entertain hospitably: with accusative of person(s), Luk.19:6, Act.17:7, Jas.2:25; εἰς τ. οἶκον,…

hupodeigma.notes: ὑπό-δειγμα, -τος, τό (ὑποδείκνυμι), [in LXX: Eze.42:15, Sir.44:16, 2Ma.6:28 2Mac 6:31, 4Ma.17:23 * ;] used by later writers (Xen. onwards) for παράδειγμα (see Rutherford, NPhr., 62), __(a) a figure, copy: Heb.8:5…

hupodeiknumi.notes: ὑπο-δείκνυμι [in LXX for נָגַד hi., etc. ;] __1. to show secretly. __2. to show by tracing out; hence, generally, to teach, make known: with dative of person(s), Luk.6:47 12:5, Act.9:16; id. with inf., Mat.3:7,…

hupodema.notes: ὑπόδημα, -τος, τό (ὑποδέω), [in LXX for נָעַל ;] a sole bound under the foot, a sandal: Mat.3:11 10:10, Mrk.1:7, Luk.3:16 10:4 15:22 22:35, Jhn.1:27; τ. ποδῶν, Act.7:33 (LXX) Act.13:25.† SYN.: σανδάλιον, q.v (AS)

hupodeo.notes: ὑπο-δέω [in LXX: 2Ch.28:15, Eze.16:10 (נָעַל) * ;] to blind under, esp. of foot gear. Most frequently in mid, and pass. with accusative; __(a) of the foot, ὑποδησάμενοι τ. πόδας, your feet shod: Eph.6:15; __(b) of…

hupodikos.notes: ὑπόδικος, -ον brought to trial, answerable to: with dative of person(s), τ. θεῷ, Rom.3:19 (see MM, xxv).† (AS)

hupogrammos.notes: ὑπογραμμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ὑπογράφω, (a) to write under; (b) to trace letters for copying), [in LXX: 2Ma.2:28 * ;] __1. a writing-copy, hence, __2. an example: 1Pe.2:11 (Philo).† (AS)

hupokrino.notes: ὑπο-κρίνομαι [in LXX: (Job.40:2) אּ1 (AB אּ2 ἀπο-, עָנָה), Sir.1:29 35:15 (32:15) Sir.36:2 (33:2), 2Ma.5:25 2Mac 6:21 2Mac 6:24, 4Ma.6:15 4Mac 6:17 * ;] __1. = Att.. ἀποκρίνομαι (which see), to answer, reply (Hom.,…

hupokrisis.notes: ὑπό-κρισις, -εως, ἡ (ὑποκρίνομαι, which see) [in LXX: 2Ma.6:25 * ;] __1. a reply, answer (Hdt.). __2. play-acting (Arist., Polyb., al.). Metaphorical, pretence, hypocrisy: Mat.23:28, Mrk.12:15, Luk.12:1, Gal.2:13,…

hupokrites.notes: ὑπο-κριτής, -οῦ, ὁ (ὑποκρίνομαι, which see) [in LXX Job.34:30 36:13 (חָנֵף) * ;] __1. one who answers, an interpreter (Plat.). __2. a stage player, actor (Plut., Xen., al.). Metaphorical (in LXX and NT), a pretender,…

hupolambano.notes: ὑπο-λαμβάνω [in LXX: Job.2:4 4:1 and frequently (עָנָה), Psa.48:9 (דָּמָה pi.), Tob.6:17, Wis.17:2, 3Ma.3:8 3Mac 3:11, al. ;] __1. to take or bear up (by supporting from beneath): with accusative of person(s),…

hupoleipo.notes: ὑπο-λείπω [in LXX chiefly (pass.) for יָתַר ni., שָׁאַר ni. ;] to leave remaining (Hom., Thuc., al.): pass., of survivors, Rom.11:3.† (AS)

hupolenion.notes: ὑπολήνιον, -ου, τό (ὑπό, ληνός), [in LXX: Jol.3:13 (4:13), Hag.2:17, (16), Zec.14:10, Isa.16:10 (יֶקֶב) * ;] a vessel or trough beneath a winepress to receive the juice (RV, a pit for the winepress): Mrk.12:1 (see…

hupolimpano.notes: ὑπο-λιμτάνω collat. form of ὑπολείπω, to leave behind: 1Pe.2:21.† (AS)

hupomeno.notes: ὑπο-μένω [in LXX chiefly for קָוָה, also for יָחַל hi., etc. ;] __1. in-trans., to stay behind: before ἐν, Luk.2:43; ἐκεῖ, Act.17:14. __2. Trans., __(a) with accusative, to await, wait for: Rom.8:24 (Hom., Hdt.,…

hupomimnesko.notes: ὑπο-μιμνήσκω [in LXX: 3Ki.4:3 B ( hi.), Wis.12:1 18:22, 4Ma.18:14 * ;] to cause one to remember, put one in mind or remind one of: with accusative of thing(s), 2Ti.2:14, 3Jn.10; with dupl. accusative (Thus., al.),…

hupomnesis.notes: ὑπόμινησις, -εως, ἡ (ὑπομιμνήσκω) [in LXX: Psa.71:6 א (תְּהִלָּה), Wis.16:11, 2Ma.6:17 * ;] a reminding, reminder: ἐν ὑ., 2Pe.1:13 3:1; with genitive, 2Ti.1:5.† (AS)

hupomone.notes: ὑπο-μονή, -ῆς, ἡ (ὑπομένω) [in LXX for מִקְוֶה and cogn. forms; frequently in 4Mac ;] __1. a remaining behind (Arist.). __2. patient enduring, endurance: Luk.8:15 21:19, Rom.5:3-4 15:4-5, 2Co.6:4 12:12, Col.1:11,…

huponoeo.notes: ὑπο νοέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Da TH Dan.7:25 (סְבַר), Tob.8:16, Jdth.14:14, Sir.23:21 * ;] to suspect, conjecture: Act.25:18; with accusative and inf., Act.13:25 27:27.† (AS)

huponoia.notes: ὑπόνοια, -ας, ἡ (ὑπονοέω), [in LXX: Dan LXX 4:16, 32 5:6 (רַעְיוֹן), Sir 3:34* ;] a suspicion: 1Ti.6:4.† (AS)

hupophero.notes: ὑπο-φέρω [in LXX for נָשָׂא, etc. ;] to bear by being under. Metaphorical, to endure: with accusative of thing(s), 1Co.10:13, 2Ti.3:11, 1Pe.2:19.† (AS)

hupopiazo.notes: ὑπωπιάζω (ὑπώπιον, (a) the part of the face below the eyes; (b) a blow on the face), to strike under the eye, give a black eye: metaphorically, __(a) of persistent annoyance (RV, wear out), Luk.18:6; __(b) of severe…

hupopleo.notes: ὑπο-πλέω to sail under, i.e. under the lee of: with accusative, Act.27:4, 7.† (AS)

hupopneo.notes: ὑπο-πνέω __1. to blow underneath (Arist.). __2. to blow gently: Act.27:13.† (AS)

hupopodion.notes: ὑποπόδιον, -ου, τό (ὑπό, πούς), [in LXX: Psa.99:5 110:1, Isa.66:1, La 2:1 (הֲדֹם) * ;] a footstool (= cl. θρᾶνος): Jas.2:3; metaphorically, Mat.5:35, Mrk.12:36 (ὑποκάτω, WH, R, mg.), Luk.20:43, Act.2:35 7:49,…

hupostasis.notes: ὑπό-στασις, -εως, ἡ (ὑφίστημι, to set under, stand under, support) [in LXX for מַצָּב (1Ki.13:23 14:4 B), תִּקְוָה (Rut.1:12, Eze.19:5), etc., also in Wis.16:21 ;] __1. a support, base or foundation (in various…

hupostello.notes: ὑπο-στέλλω [in LXX: Deu.1:17 (גּוּר), Job.13:8 (נָשָׂא פָּנִים), Hab.2:4 (עָפַל pu.), Hag.1:10 (כָּלָא), Exo.23:21, Wis.6:7, 3Ma.5:28 * ;] __1. to draw in, let down (ἱστίον, οὐράν, etc.). __2. to draw back, withdraw:…

hupostole.notes: ὑπο-στολή, -ῆς, ἡ (ὑποστέλλω), __1. a letting down, lowering (Plut.). __2. a shrinking back (Hesych.): οὐκ ἐσμὲν ὑποστολῆς (on the genitive, see Bl., § 35, 2), Heb.10:39.† (AS)

hupostrepho.notes: ὑπο-στρέφω [in LXX chiefly for שׁוּב ], __1. trans., to turn back or about (Hem.). __2. Intrans., to turn back, return: Luk.2:20, 43 8:37, 40 9:10 10:17 17:15 19:12 23:48, 56, Act.8:28; with inf., Luk.17:18; before…

hupostronnumi.notes: ὑπο-στρωννύω [in LXX: Isa.58:5 (יָצָא hi.), etc. ;] late form of ὑπο-στορέννυμι, to spread or strew under: with accusative of thing(s), Luk.19:36.† (AS)

hupotage.notes: ὑπο-ταγή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Wis.18:16 A * ;] subjection: 2Co.9:13, Gal.2:5. 1Ti.2:11 3:4.† (AS)

hupotasso.notes: ὑπο-τάσσω [in LXX for דָבַר hi., דָּמַם, שׂוּם, שִׁית, etc. ;] __1. as a military term, to place or rank under (Polyb.). __2. to subject, put in subjection: 1Co.15:27, Php.3:21, Heb.2:5, 8; pass., Rom.8:20,…

hupotithemi.notes: ὑπο-τίθημι [in LXX for שׂוּם, etc. ;] to place under, lay down. Metaphorical, τ. τράχηλον ὑποθεῖναι, to risk one's life (see Deiss., LAE, 119 f.): Rom.16:4; mid., to suggest: 1Ti.4:6.† (AS)

hupotrecho.notes: ὑπο-τρέχω to run in under; of navigators, to run in the lee of: with accusative (see M, Pr., 65), Act.27:16.† (AS)

hupotuposis.notes: ὑπο-τύπωσις, -εως, ἡ (ὑποτυπόω, to delineate), an outline, sketch. Metaphorical, a pattern, example: 1Ti.1:16, 2Ti.1:13.† SYN.: ὁμοίωμα, τύπος, ὑπόδειγμα (see DB, iii, 696b) (AS)

hupozonnumi.notes: ὑπο-ζώννυμι [in LXX: 2Ma.3:19 * ;] to undergird (Hdt., al.; ὑπὸ τ. μάστους, 2Mac, l.with); of a ship, to undergird or frap: Act.27:17 (see DB, ext. 367a).† (AS)

hupozugion.notes: ὑπο-ζύγιος, -α, -ον [in LXX for חֲמוֹר ;] under the yoke; as subst., τ . (Hdt., al.), a beast of burden; colloq., an ass (see Deiss., BS, 160 f.), and so always in LXX and NT: Mat.21:5 (LXX), 2Pe.2:16.† (AS)

hupselophroneo.notes: ὑψηλο- φρονέω, -ῶ = μευαλοφρονεῖν (Xen., Plat., al.), to be high-minded: 1Ti.6:17 (WH, mg., ὑψηλὰ, φρονεῖν).† (AS)

hupselos.notes: ὑψηλός, -ή, -όν [in LXX chiefly for בָּמָה, also for בָּמָה, רוּם, etc. ;] high, lofty: ὄρος, Mat.4:8 17:1, Mrk.9:2, Rev.21:10; τεῖχος, Rev.21:12; μετὰ βραχίονος., figuratively, Act.13:17 (cf. Exo.6:6, al); pl.,…

hupsistos.notes: ὕψιστος, -η, -ον superlat., without positive in use, in cl. chiefly poët. (Æsch., Soph., al.), [in LXX chiefly for עֶלְיוֹן, also for מָרוֹם, etc. ;] highest, most high: of place, τὰ ὕ. (of the heavens), Mat.21:9,…

hupsoma.notes: ὕψωμα, -τος, τό [in LXX: Job.24:24 (†), Jdth.10:8 13:4 15:9 * ;] __(a) height: Rom.8:39; __(b) that which is lifted up, a barrier: 2Co.10:5.† (AS)

hupsoo.notes: ὑψόω, -ῶ (ὕψος), [in LXX chiefly for רוּם, also for גָּבַהּ, נָשָׂא, etc. ;] to lift or raise up: with accusative, Jhn.3:14 8:28 12:32, 34; ἕως τ. οὐρανοῦ, figuratively, pass., Mat.11:23, Luk.10:15. Metaphorical, to…

hupsos.notes: ὕψος, -ους, τό [in LXX for קוֹמָה, מָרוֹם, etc. ;] height: Eph.3:18 Jas.1:9, Rev.21:16; of heaven (EV, on high), ἐξ ὕ., Luk.1:78 24:49; εἰς ὕ., Eph.4:8 (LXX)† (AS)

hus.notes: ὗς, ὑός, ὁ, ἡ [in LXX for חֲזִיר ;] swine: fem. (sow), 2Pe.2:22,† (AS)

hussopos.notes: ὕσσωπος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX for אֵזוֹב ;] hyssop, of which a bunch was used in ritual sprinklings: Heb.9:19; of a branch or rod (?) of hyssop, Jhn.19:29 (but see Field, Notes, 106 ff., for suggestion to substitute ὑσσῷ…

husterema.notes: ὑστέρημα, -τος, τό (ὑστερέω), [in LXX: Jdg.18:10 19:19-20, Psa.34:9 (מַחְסוֹר), Ecc.1:15 (חֶסְרוֹן), Est.6:9 (ὑ. εἶναι, חֲשַׁח) * ;] __(a) that which is lacking, deficiency, shortcoming: with genitive puss. (prop.…

hustereo.notes: ὑστερέω, -ῶ (ὕστερος), [in LXX for חָסֵר, חָדַל etc. ;] to come late, be behind (opposite to προτερέω, φθάνω; with genitive of thing(s), for; with genitive of person(s), later than). Metaphorical, __1. of persons,…

husteresis.notes: ὑστέρησις, -εως, ἡ (ὑστερέω), [in Aq.: Job.30:3 * ;] need, want: opposite to τὸ περισσεῦον, Mrk.12:44; καθ᾽ ὑ., Php.4:11 (eccl.).† (AS)

husteron.notes: (comp.) later, second; (neu.) finally, last of all posterior, in place or time; subsequent, later, last, finally, last of all Mt. 21:31; 1Tim. 4:1

husteros.notes: ὕστερος, -α, -ον [in LXX for אַחֲרוֹן and cogn. forms ;] latter, later: ἐν ὑ. καιροῖς, 1Ti.4:1 (on the reading ὁ ὕ., WH, for ὁ πρῶτος, see WH, App., in l). Neut., τὸ ὕ., used adverbially instead of ὑστέρως,…

iaeiros.notes: Ἰάειρος, -ου, ὁ (Heb. יָאִיר, Num.32:41), Jairus: Mrk.5:22, Luk.8:41.† (AS)

iakob.notes: Ἰακώβ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. יַעֲקֹב), Jacob; __1. The patriarch: Mat.1:2 8:11, Jhn.4:5-6, Act.7:8, al; as in Heb. (cf. Num.23:7, Isa.41:8, Sir.23:12, al.), of his descendants, Rom.11:26 (LXX). __2. The father-in-law of…

iakobos.notes: Ἰάκωβος, -ου, ὁ (Heb., see previous word) James; __1. Son of Zebedee: Mat.4:21, Mrk.1:19, 29, Act.1:13 12:2, al. __2. Son of Alphæus: Mat.10:3, Mrk.2:14, al.; commonly identified with Ἰ. ὁ μικρός, James the little,…

iama.notes: ἴαμα, -τος, τό (ἰάομαι), [in LXX for מַרְפֵּא (2Ch.36:16, Ecc.10:4, Jer.33:6, etc. ;] __1. most frequently in cl., a means of healing, remedy (Thuc., al.; Wis.11:4 10:9). __2. = ἴασις (which see), a healing (Plat.;…

iaomai.notes: ἰάομαι, -ῶμαι [in LXX for רָפָא (Gen.20:17, al.), exc. Isa.30:26 61:1 (חָבַשׁ) ;] to heal: with accusative of person(s), Luk.5:17 6:19 9:2, 11 9:42 14:4 22:51, Jhn.4:47, Act.9:34 10:38 28:8; pass., Mat.8:8, 13 15:28,…

iared.notes: Ἰάρετ (Rec. -ρέδ, L, Ἰάρεθ), ὁ (Heb. יֶרֶד, LXX Ἰάρεδ, FlJ, Ἰάρεδος), Jared (Gen.5:15): Luk.3:37.† (AS)

iasis.notes: ἴασις, -εως, ἡ (ἰάομαι), [in LXX chiefly for מַרְפֵּא ;] a healing, cure: Act.4:22, 30; pl., Luk.13:32.† SYN.: see: θεραπεία, 2; ἴαμα, 2 (AS)

iason.notes: Ἰάσων, -ονος, ὁ Jason: Act.17:5-7, 9; perhaps the same, Rom.16:21.† (AS)

iaspis.notes: ἴασπις, -ιδος, ἡ [in LXX: Exo.28:18 36:38 (Exo.39:11) (יַהֲלֹם), Isa.54:12 (כַּדְכֹּד), Eze.28:13 (יָשְׁפֵה, see BDB, Lex., see word)* ;] jasper, apparently not the modern stone of that name, but a translucent stone…

iatros.notes: ἰατρός, -οὔ, ὁ (ἰάομαι), [in LXX for רָפָא ;] a physician: Mat.9:12, Mrk.2:17 5:26, Luk.4:23 5:31 8:43 (om. WH, R, mg.), Col.4:14 (on the status of physicians, see MM, Exp., xv.).† (AS)

ichnos.notes: ἴχνος, -εος (-ους), τό [in LXX for כַּף, etc. ;] a track, footstep: metaphorically (as frequently in cl.), στοιχεῖν τοῖς ἴ., Rom.4:12; περιπατεῖν τοῖς ἴ., 2Co.12:18; ἐπακολουθεῖν τοῖς ἴ., 1Pe.2:21 (cf. MM, Exp.,…

ichthudion.notes: ἰχθύδιον, -ου, τό (dimin. of ἰχθύς), a little fish: Mat.15:34, Mrk.8:7.† (AS)

ichthus.notes: ἰχθύς, -ύoς, ὁ [in LXX for דָּג, דָּגָה ;] a fish: Mat.7:10, Mrk.6:38, al. (AS)

idea.notes: ἰδέα, -ας, ἡ, see: εἰδέα εἰδέα (Rec. ἰδ-, as in cl.; see Tdf., Pr., 81), -ας, ἡ (εἶδον), [in LXX (seel. ἰδ-): Gen.5:3 (דְּמוּת), Da TH 1:13, 15 (מַרְאֶה), Ep. Jer.63, 2Ma.3:16 * ;] appearance, look: Mat.28:3.† (AS)

idios.notes: ἴδιος, -α, -ον (in Attic usually -ος, -ον), [in LXX chiefly for of person(s) suff., also for לָהֶם, etc.; (τὰ ἴ.) בֵּיתוֹ ;] __1. one's own; __(a) of that which is private and personal (in cl. opposite to κοινός,…

idiotes.notes: ἰδιώτης, -ου, ὁ (ἴδιος), [in LXX: Pro.6:8 (no Heb.)* ;] __1. a pri­vate person, as opposite to the State or an official (βασιλεῖς κ. ἰδιῶται, Pr, l.with; and cf. MM, Exp., xv). __2. one without professional…

idoumaia.notes: Ἰδουμαία, -ας, ἡ (Heb. אֱדֹם), [in LXX: 2Ki.8:14, a1.; elsewhere, as Gen.25:30, Ἐδώμ ;] Idumæa: Mrk.3:8.† (AS)

iechonias.notes: Ἰεχονίας, -ον, ό (Heb. יְכׇנְיָה, Jehoiakin), Jechoniah: Mat.1:11-12.† (AS)

iesous.notes: Ἰησοῦς, -οῦ, dative, voc. -οῦ, accusative, -οῦν (Heb. יֵשׁוּעַ ,יְהֹשֻׁעַ ,יְהוֹשׁוּעַ), __1. JESUS: Mat.1:21, al.; ὁ Ἰ, ib. 3:13, al.; Ἰ. Χριστός, ib. 1:1, Mrk.1:1, al.; Χρ. Ἰ., Rom.2:26, al.; κύριος Ἰ., Act.28:31,…

iessai.notes: Ἰεσσαί (FlJ, -σσαῖος), ὁ (Heb. יִשַׁי Rut.4:17, al.) Jesse: Mat.1:5-6, Luk.3:32, Act.13:22" (LXX), Rom.15:12 (LXX),† (AS)

iezabel.notes: Ἰεζάβελ (L, Ἰεζ-; Tr. -βέλ; Rec. -βήλ) ἡ, indecl. (Heb. אִיזֶבֶל; LXX as txt.; FlJ, Ἰεζαβέλη), Jezebel (3Ki.16:31, al.): symbolically, Rev.2:20 (see Swete, in l).† (AS)

ikmas.notes: ἰκμάς, -άδος, ἡ [in LXX. Jer.17:8 (יוּבַל), Job.26:14 (שֶׁמֶץ)* ;] moisture: Luk.8:6.† (AS)

ikonion.notes: Ἰκόνιον, -ου, τό Iconium, a city of the province of Galatia: Act.13:51 14:1, 19 14:21 16:2, 2Ti.3:11.† (AS)

illurikon.notes: Ἰλλυρικόν, -οῦ, τό Illyricum, a region bordering on the Adriatic Sea: Rom.15:19.† (AS)

ioannaioanan.notes: Ἰωάνα (TR, -ννα; see WH, App., 159), ης, ἡ (Aram יוֺחַן), Joanna: Luk.8:3 24:10.† (AS)

ioannas.notes: Ἰωανάν (Rec. Ἰωαννᾶς, -ᾶ), ὁ indecl. (Heb. יוֹחָנָן), Joanan (cf. Ἰωάνης): Luk.3:37.† (AS)

ioannes.notes: Ἰωάνης (Rec. ννης, which see; cf. Dalman, Gr., 142; Tdf., Pr., 79; WH, App., 159; Bl., §3, 10; 10, 2), -ου, dative, -ῃ (but in Mat.11:4. Luk.7:18, 22 Rev.1:1, ει), accusative, -ην, ὁ (Heb. יוֹחָנָן, LXX: Ἰωανάν,…

ioatham.notes: Ἰωάθαμ (WH, -θάμ), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. יוֹתָם), Jotham, King of Judah: Mat.1:9.† (AS)

ioel.notes: Ἰωήλ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. יוֹאֵל,), Joel: Act.2:16.† (AS)

ionas.notes: Ἰωνᾶς, -ᾶ, ὁ (Heb. יוֹנָה), Jonah, the prophet: Mat.12:39-41 16:4, Luk.11:29-30, 32.† (AS)

ioppe.notes: Ἰόππη (FlJ, id., and also Ἰόπη), -ης, ἡ, (Heb. יָפוֹ), Joppa: Act.9:1-43 10:1-48 11:1-30.† (AS)

ioram.notes: Ἰωράμ, ὁ, indecl. (Heb. יוֹרָם), Joram, Jehoram: Mat.1:8.† (AS)

iordanes.notes: Ἰορδάνης, -ου, ὁ (Heb. יַרְדֵּן), the Jordan: Mat.3:5, Mrk.1:5, al (AS)

ios.notes: ἰός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX: Eze.24:6, 11-12 (חֶלְאָה), Psa.140:3 (חֵמָה), al. ;] __1. an arrow. __2. rust (Ez, l.with): Jas.5:3 (cf. MM, Exp., xv). __3. poison: figuratively, Rom.3:13 (Ps. l.with), Jas.3:8.† (AS)

iosaphat.notes: Ἰωσαφάτ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. יְהוֹשָׁפָט), Jehoshaphat: Mat.1:8.† (AS)

ioseph.notes: Ἰωσήφ indecl. (FlJ, Ἰώσηπος, -ου), ὁ (Heb. יוֹסֵף), Joseph; __1. the Son of Jacob: Jhn.4:5, Act.7:9, 13-14 7:18, Heb.11:21-22, Rev.7:8. __2. In the genealogy of our Lord, __(a) the son of Matthias: Luk.3:24; __(b)…

ioses.notes: Ἰωσῆς, -ῆ (Rec. -ή Luk.3:29; AV, Jose; see: Ἰησοῦς, 3), and -ῆτος (Mk, ll with), ὁ, Joses; __1. brother of our Lord: Mrk.6:3, Mat.13:55 (Rec., see: Ἰωσήφ). __2. Son of Mary: Mat.27:56 (-σήφ), WH, txt), Mrk.15:40, 47…

iosias.notes: Ἰωσείας (-σίας, Rec.), -ου, ὁ (Heb. יֹאשִׁיָּה), Josiah: Mat.1:10-11 † (AS)

iota.notes: ἰῶτα, τό , iota (Heb. י, i.e. the smallest letter): Mat.5:18.† (AS)

iouda.notes: Ἰούδα, (a) Ἰουδαῖος, ὁ, a Jew: Jhn.4:9, Act.18:24, Rom.2:28; pl., Rev.2:9 3:9; οἱ Ἰ., Mat.2:2, Mrk.7:3, Jhn.2:6, al.; Ἰ. τε καὶ Ἕλληνες, Act.14:1, al.; κ. προσήλυτοι, Act.2:10; ἔθνη τε κ. Ἰ., Act.14:5; οἱ κατὰ τὰ…

ioudaia.notes: § Judea, Judean, "land of the Judahites" Judea, the southern party of the country, below Samaria, Mt. 2:1, 5, 22; 3:1; metonymy the inhabitants of Judea

ioudaikos.notes: Ἰουδαϊκός, ή, -όν [in LXX: 2Ma.8:11 V, 2Ma.13:21 * ;] Jewish: Tit.1:14.† (AS)

ioudaios.notes: Ἰουδαῖος, -αία, -αῖον (Ἰούδας), Jewish: ἀνήρ, Act.10:28 22:3; ἄνθρωπος, Act.21:39; ψευδοπροφήτης, 13:6; ἀσχιερεύς, 19:14; γυνή, 16:1 24:24; γῆ, Jhn.3:22; χώρα, Mrk.1:5. Substantively, __(a) Ἰουδαῖος, ὁ, a Jew:…

ioudaismos.notes: Ἰουδαϊσμός, -οῦ, ὁ (Ἰουδαΐζω), [in LXX: 2Ma.2:21 2Mac 8:1 2Mac 14:38, 4Ma.4:26 * ;] Judaism, the observance of Jewish rites: Gal.1:13-14.† (AS)

ioudaizo.notes: Ἰουδαΐζω (Ἰουδαῖος), [in LXX: Est.8:17 (יָהַד hith.)* ;] to can-form to Jewish practice, to Judaize: Gal.2:14.† (AS)

ioudas.notes: Ἰούδας, -α dative -ᾳ, accusative -αν (so in LXX, and also rarely, Ἰουδά, indecl.; cf. Thack., Gr., 163) (Heb. יְהוּדָה) __1. Judah, son of Jacob: Mat.1:2-3, Luk.3:33; φυλή Ἰ., Rev.5:5 7:5; by meton., of the tribe,…

iounias.notes: Ἰουνίας, -α, ὁ (or Ἰουνία, -ας, ἡ, Junia; AV, H, mg.) Junias: Rom.16:7.† (AS)

ioustos.notes: Ἰοῦστος, -ου, ὁ Justus, the surname of, __1. Joseph Barsabbas: Act.1:23. __2. Titus, of Corinth: Act.18:7. __3. Jesus, a Christian of Rome: Col.4:11.† (AS)

iris.notes: ἶρις, -ιδος, ἡ [in LXX: Exo.30:24 (קִדָּה)*, (קֶשֶׁת, Gen.9:13, Eze.1:28, is rendered by τόξον) ;] __1. Iris, the messenger of the gods. __2. a rain­bow or halo: Rev.4:3 10:1. __3. The plant Iris (cf. Ex., l.with).† (AS)

isaak.notes: Ἰσαάκ (in MSS. sometimes -σάκ; cf. Deiss., BS, 189; Thack., Gr., 100), ὁ indecl. (in FlJ, Ἴσακος, -ου), (Heb. יִצְחָק), Isaac (Gen.17:19, al.): Mat.1:2, Rom.9:10, al (AS)

isachar.notes: Ἰσσαχάρ (Rec. Ἰσαχ-, Ἰσασχ- (Elz.), T, -άχαρ), ὁ indecl. (FlJ, Ἰσάχαρις, Ἰσάσχ-) (Heb. יִשָּׂשכָר, Gen.30:18), Issachar: Rev.7:7.† (AS)

isangelos.notes: ἰσάγγελος -ον (ἴσος, ἄγγελος), like or equal to angels: Luk.20:36.† (AS)

ischuo.notes: ἰσχύω (ἰσχύς), [in LXX for חָזַק, גִּבּוֹר, עָצַם, etc. ;] __1. to be strong in body: Mat.9:12, Mrk.2:17. __2. to be powerful, have power, prevail: Act.19:20, Rev.12:8; before κατά, with genitive of person(s),…

ischuros.notes: ἰσχυρός, -ά, -όν (ἰσχύω), [in LXX for אֵל, גִּבּוֹר, עָצוּם, חָזַק, etc. ;] strong, mighty, powerful; __(a) of persons, as to body or spirit: Mat.12:29, Mrk.3:27, Luk.11:21-22, 1Co.4:10 (opposite to ἀσθενής),…

ischus.notes: ἰσχύς, -ύος, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for כֹּחַ, also for חַיִל, עַז, גְּבוּרָה, etc. ;] strength, might, power, force, ability: Rev.5:12 7:12; ἰ. κ. δύναμις, 2Pe.2:11; τ. κράτος τῆς ἰ. (Isa.40:26), Eph.1:19 6:10; ἡ δόξα…

iskariotesiskarioth.notes: ἰσκαριώθ, ὁ indecl. (Mrk.3:19 14:10, Luk.6:16, elsewhere -ιώτης; V. infr.) and Ἰσκαριώτης, -ου (Heb. prob. קִרְיָא אִישׁ; see Swete, Mk., 319), Iscariot: surname of Judas, ll. with supr., also Mat.10:4 26:14,…

isopsuchos.notes: ἰσόψυχος -ον (ἴσος, ψυχή), [in LXX: Psa.55:13 (עֵרֶךְ)* ;] __1. of equal spirit (Æsch., Agam., 1470). __2. like-minded: Php.2:20.† (AS)

isos.notes: ἴσος (epic ἶσος, so sometimes Rec.), -η, -ον, [in LXX for כְּ֜, Jb 5:14; Job.10:10, al.; אֶחָד, Eze.40:5 ff.; 2Ma.9:15, 4Ma.13:20-21, al. ;] equal, the same in size, number, quality, etc.: δωρεά, Act.11:17;…

isotes.notes: ἰσότης, -ητος, ἡ (ἴσος), [in LXX: Job.36:30, Zec.4:7 * ;] __1. equality: 2Co.8:13-14. __2. equity, fairness: τὸ δίκαιον κ. τὴν ἰ., Col.4:1 (for dis­tinction bet. τὸ δ. and ἡ ἰ, see ICC, in l).† (AS)

isotimos.notes: ἰσότιμος, -ον (ἴσος, τιμή), equally privileged, held in equal honour: πίστις, 2Pe.1:1 (R, mg., equally precious, but v Field, Notes, 240; Mayor, in l).† (AS)

israel.notes: Ἰσραήλ (FlJ, Ἰσράηλος, -ου), ὁ indecl. (Heb. יִשְׂרָאֵל, Gen.32:28), Israel: ὁ οἶκος Ἰ., Act.7:42 (LXX), al.; λαός, Act.4:10; υὁοί, Act.5:21, al.; αἱ φυλαὶ τοῦ Ἰ., Mat.19:28, al. By meton., for the Israelites,…

israelites.notes: Ἰσραηλείτης (Rec. -λίτης), ου, ὁ [in LXX: Num.25:8 (יִשְׂרָאֵל), 3Ki.21:1 (יִזְרְעֵאלִי; Luc. Ἰεζραηλίτης), etc. ;] an Israelite, the name expressive of theocratic privilege (see: Ἑβραῖος): Rom.9:4 11:1, 2Co.11:22;…

italia.notes: Ἰταλία, -ας, ἡ Italy: ἡ Ἰ. (see B1., § 46, 11), Act.18:2 27:1, 6, Heb.13:14.† (AS)

itouraia.notes: Ἰτουραῖος, -αία, -αῖον [in LXX: 1Ch.5:19 A (יְטוּר) * ;] Ituræan (in cl. always ὁι Ἰ., the Ituræans) χώρα (ἡ Ἰτουραίων ὀρεινή, τὰ τῶν Ἰ. μέρη, Strabo, XVI, ii, 16, 20), Luk.3:1 (cf. Exp. (1894), ix, 51 ff., 143 ff.,…

kaiaphas.notes: Καιάφας (T, Καϊ-), -α ὁ, Caiaphas (on the name, see Exp. Times, x, 185): Mat.26:3, 57, Luk.3:2, Jhn.11:4 18:13-14, 24 18:28 Act.4:6.† Καΐφας, for Καιάφας, which see: Luk.3:2 (L) (AS)

kain.notes: Καίν (Τ, Κάϊν), ὁ, indecl. (in FlJ, Κάϊς, -ιος), (Heb. קַיִן; Gen.4:1), Cain: Heb.11:4, 1Jn.3:12, Ju 11.† (AS)
