kainankainam.notes: Καινάμ (T, Καϊ-; Rec. Καϊνάν), ό, indecl. (Heb. קֵינָן), Cainam, Cainan; __1. son of Enos (Gen.5:9): Luk.3:37. __2. Son of Arphaxad (Gen.10:24 (LXX): Luk.3:36.† (AS)

kainos.notes: καινός, -ή, -όν [in LXX (Eze.11:19, al.) for חָדָשׁ, exc. Isa.65:15 (אַחֵר) ;] of that which is unused or unaccustomed, new in respect to form or quality, fresh, unused, novel: opposite to παλαιός, ἀρχαῖος, Mat.9:17…

kainotes.notes: καινότης, -ητος, ἡ (καινός), [in LXX: 3Ki.8:53, Eze.47:12 * ;] __1. novelty (Thuc., Isocr., LS, see word). __2. freshness, newness: ἐν κ. ζωῆς (see Lft., Notes, 296), Rom.6:4; ἐν κ. πνεύματος, Rom.7:6.† (AS)

kaio.notes: καίω [in LXX for בָּעַר, יָקַד, שָׂרַף, etc. ;] __1. to kindle, light: with accusative, λύχνον, Mat.5:15; pass., to be lighted, to burn: ptcp., Luk.12:35, Rev.4:5 8:10 19:20; πυρί Heb.12:18, Rev.8:8 21:8;…

kairos.notes: καιρός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for עֵת, also for מוֹעֵד, etc. ;] __1. due measure, fitness, proportion (Eur., Xen., al.). __2. Of Time (cl. also) in the sense of a fixed and definite period, time, season (Kennedy,…

kaisar.notes: Καῖσαρ, -αρος, ὁ Cæsar: Mat.22:17, Luk.2:1, Jhn.19:12, Act.25:8 ff., al (AS)

kaisareia.notes: Καισαρία (Rec. -άρεια), -ας, ἡ Cæsarea; __1. Cæsarea Philippi, at the foot of Lebanon: Mat.16:13, Mrk.8:27. __2. Cæsarea of Palestine, on the sea-coast: Act.8:4 9:30 10:1, 24 11:11 12:19 18:22 21:8, 16 23:23, 33…

kakei.notes: κἀκεῖ (by crasis for καὶ ἐκεῖ, and so sometimes written, see infr.), and there, there also: Mat.5:23 (κ. ἐ., Tr., mg.) Mat.10:11 28:10 (κ. ἐ., T), Mrk.1:35 (κ. ἐ., L) Mrk.1:38 (κ. ἐ., WH) Mrk.14:15 (κ. ἐ., WH),…

kakeithen.notes: κἀκεῖθεν (by crasis for καὶ ἐκεῖθεν, and so sometimes written, see: ἐκεῖθεν), of place, and from thence, and thence: Mrk.9:30, Luk.11:53, Act.7:4 14:26 16:12 20:15 21:1 27:4 28:15.† (AS)

kakia.notes: κακία, -ας, ἡ (κακός), [in LXX chiefly for רָאָה ;] __1. badness in quality (opposite to ἀρετή, excellence). __2. wickedness, depravity, malignity: Act.8:22, Rom.1:29, 1Co.5:8 14:20, Eph.4:31, Col.3:8, Tit.3:3,…

kakoetheia.notes: κακοήθεια (WH, -θία), -ας, ἡ (κακός, ἦθος), [in LXX: Est.8:13, 3Ma.3:22 3Mac 7:3 A, 4Ma.1:4 4Mac 3:4 * ;] malignity, malevolence: Rom.1:29 (Cremer, 329).† (AS)

kakologeo.notes: κακολογέω, -ῶ (κακολόγος, slanderous), [in LXX: Exo.21:16 22:28, 1Ki.3:13, Pro.20:20, Eze.22:7 (קָלַל pi., hi.), 2Ma.4:1 * ;] to speak ill of, revile, abuse: with accusative of person(s), Mat.15:4, Mrk.7:10" (LXX)…

kakoo.notes: κακόω, -ῶ (κακός), [in LXX chiefly for רָעַע hi., עָנָה pi. ;] __1. to ill-treat, afflict, distress: with accusative of person(s), Act.7:6, 19 12:1 18:10, 1Pe.3:13. __2. (not in cl.), to embitter (Psa.106:32):…

kakopatheia.notes: κακοπάθεια (WH, -θία), -ας, ἡ (κακοπαθής, suffering), [in LXX: Mal.1:13 (תְּלָאָה), תְּלָאָה), 2Ma.2:26-27, 4Ma.9:8 * ;] distress, affliction: Jas.5:10.† (AS)

kakopatheo.notes: κακοπαθέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Jon.4.10 (עָמָל)* ;] to suffer evil, endure Affliction: 2Ti.2:9 4:5, Jas.5:13.† (AS)

kakopoieo.notes: κακο-ποιέω, ῶ [in LXX chiefly for רָעַע hi. ;] to do harm, to do evil: Mrk.3:4, Luk.6:9, 1Pe.3:17, 3Jn.11 (Cremer, 329).† (AS)

kakopoios.notes: κακοποιός, -όν (κακόν, ποιέω), [in LXX: Pro.12:4 (בּוּשׁ hi.) Pro.24:19 (רָעַע hi.)* ;] doing evil; as susbt., an evil-doer: 1Pe.2:12 2:14 4:15.† (AS)

kakos.notes: κακός, -ή, -όν [in LXX chiefly for רַע ;] __1. in general, opposite to ἀγαθός, καλός, in various senses, bad, mean, base, worthless (cl.). __2. In ethical sense, base, evil, wicked: of persons, Mat.21:41 24:48,…

kakosis.notes: κάκωσις, -εως, ἡ (κακόω) [Ín LXX (for עָנִי, יָדַע, etc.): Exo.3:7, Jer.2:28, Wis.3:2, al. ;] ill-treatment: Act.7:34 (LXX),† (AS)

kakoucheo.notes: κακουχέω, -ῶ (κακόν, ἔχω), [in LXX: 3Ki.2:26 11:39 (עָנָה pi., hith.)* ;] to ill-treat, hurt, torment: pass. ptcp., Heb.11:37 13:3.† (AS)

kakourgos.notes: κακοῦργος, -ον (contr. from epic κακόεργος), [in LXX. Pro.21:15 (אָוֶן, פָּעַל), Est.8:13, Sir.11:33 33:26 (Sir.33:26)* ;] a malefactor, criminal: Luk.23:32-33, 39, 2Ti.2:9.† (AS)

kalame.notes: καλάμη, -ης, ἡ (cf. κάλαμος), [in LXX chiefly for קַשׁ, Exo.5:12, Isa.5:24, al. ;] a stalk of corn, stubble: 1Co.3:12.† (AS)

kalamos.notes: κάλαμος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for קָנֶה, Isa.42:3, Eze.40:3 ff., al. ;] a reed: Mat.11:7 12:20" (LXX) Luk.7:24; hence, accusative, to its various uses, __(a) a reed-pipe, flute; __(b) a reed-staff, staff (cf.…

kaleo.notes: καλέω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for קרא ;] __1. to call, summon: with accusative of person(s), Mat.20:8 25:14, Mrk.3:31, Luk.19:13, Act.4:18; before ἐκ, Mat.2:15 (LXX); metaphorically, 1Pe.2:9. __2. to call to one's…

kallielaios.notes: καλλιέλαιος, ου, ἡ the garden olive (opposite to ἀγριέλ-): Rom.11:24.† (AS)

kallion.notes: καλλίων, -ον compar. of καλός, see: καλῶς καλῶς adv. (καλός), [in LXX for parts and derivatives of יָטַב ;] finely, rightly, well: Luk.6:48, 1Co.14:17, Gal.4:17 5:7, Jas.2:3; λέγειν, λαλεῖν, etc., Mat.15:7, Mrk.7:6…

kalodidaskalos.notes: καλο-διδάσκαλος, -ου, ὁ a teacher of that which is good: Tit.2:3.† (AS)

kalopoieo.notes: καλο-ποιέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Lev.5:4 F (καλῶς π., B) * ;] to do well, act honourably: 2Th.3:13 (cf. Gal.6:9 τὸ καλὸν π.).† (AS)

kalos.notes: καλός, -ή, -όν, [in LXX chiefly for טוֹבָה ,טוֹב, also for יָפֶה, etc. ;] __1. primarily, of outward form ("related to . . . ἀγαθός as the appearance to the essence," Cremer, 339), fair, beautiful: λίθοι (BV,…

kalumma.notes: κάλυμμα, -τος, τό (καλύπτω), [in LXX: Num.3:25 4:8 ff. (מְכַסֶּה), Exo_34:33 ff. (מַסְוֶה), etc. ;] a covering, veil: 2Co.3:13-16 † (AS)

kalupto.notes: καλύπτω [in LXX almost always for כָּסָה pi. ;] in cl., rare in prose, to cover: with accusative of person(s), Luk.23:30; with accusative of thing(s) and dative, Luk.8:16; pass., Mat.8:24; metaphorically, to veil,…

kamelos.notes: κάμηλος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ [in LXX for גָּמַל ;] camel: Mat.3:4, Mrk.1:6; in proverbs, Mat.19:24 23:24, Mrk.10:25, Luk.18:25 (on the seel. κάμιλος, see WH, Notes, 151).† κάμιλος, see: κάμηλος, and cf. Thayer; LS, s.v (AS)

kaminos.notes: κάμινος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX: Gen.19:28, Exo.19:18, Isa.48:10, al. (כִּבְשָׁן, כּוּר), Dan LXX TH Dan.3:6 ff. (אַתּוּן) ;] a furnace: Mat.13:42, 50, Rev.1:15 9:2.† (AS)

kammuo.notes: καμμύω (syncopated form of καταμύω, used by Ep. and κοινή writers; Rutherford, NPhr., 426 f.) [in LXX: Isa.6:10 (שָׁעַע hi.) Isa.29:10 33:15 (עָצַם), La 3:44 AR* ;] to shut the eyes: τ. ὀφθαλμούς, Mat.13:15,…

kamno.notes: κάμνω [in LXX: Job.10:1 (קוּט ni.) Job.17:2, Wis.4:16 15:9, 4Ma.3:8 4Mac 7:13 * ;] __1. to work; hence, from the effect of continued work, __2. to be weary: Heb.12:3 __3. to be sick: Jas.5:15.† (AS)

kampto.notes: κάμπτω [in LXX chiefly for כָּרַע, 4Ki.1:13, 2Ch.29:29, Isa.45:24 (23), al. ;] to bend, bow: with accusative of thing(s), γόνυ, τὰ γ.; with dative of person(s), τῇ Βάαλ, Rom.11:4 (LXX); πρὸς τ. πατέρα, Eph.3:14; by…

kan.notes: κἄν by crasis for καὶ ἄν (= ἐάν; WH, App., 145b; Thayer, see word), with subjunc., __1. and if: Mrk.16:18 Luk.12:38, Jhn.8:55, 1Co.13:2-3, Jas.5:15; with ellips., Luk.13:9. __2. Concessive, even if: Mat.21:21 26:35,…

kana.notes: Κανά (Rec. -νᾶ), ἡ, indecl., Cana, of Galilee: Jhn.2:1, 11 4:46 21:2.† (AS)

kananiteskananaios.notes: Καναναῖος, -ου, ὁ (late Heb. קָנָא), a Cananæan or Zealot (cf. ζηλωτής): Mat.10:4, Mrk.3:18 (Rec. -νίτης).† (AS)

kanon.notes: κανών, -όνος, ὁ (cf. κάννα, and Heb. קָנֶה, a reed), [in LXX: Mic.7:4, Jdth.13:6, 4Ma.7:21 (and in Aq., Psa.19:5, Job.38:5)* ;] __1. a rod or bar (Jth, l.with). __2. a measuring rule; hence, metaph, __3. a rule or…

kapeleuo.notes: καπηλεύω (κάπηλος, a huckster, cf. Isa.1:22 (LXX), Sir.26:29) to make a trade of (RV, mg., make merchandise of), or perhaps (cf. Isa, l.with) to corrupt (RV, tit.): 2Co.2:17.† (AS)

kapernaoum.notes: καφαρναούμ (Καπερ-, Rec., see WH, App. 160), ἡ (Heb. נַחוּם כָּפָר Nahum's village) Capernaum: Mat.4:13, Mrk.1:21, Jhn.2:12, al (AS)

kapnos.notes: καπνός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX for עָשָׁן ;] smoke: Rev.8:4 9:2-3, 17-18 14:11 15:8 18:9, 18 19:3; ἀτμὶς καπνοῦ, Act.2:19 (LXX).† (AS)

kappadokia.notes: Καππαδοκία, -ας, ἡ Cappadocia, a province of Asia Minor: Act.2:9, 1Pe.1:1.† (AS)

kardia.notes: καρδία, -ας, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for לֵבָב ,לֵב ;] the heart, __1. the bodily organ which is regarded as the seat of life ( 2Ki.18:14, 4Ki.9:24, al.). __2. In a psychological sense, the seat of man's collective…

kardiognostes.notes: καρδιο-γνώστης, -ου, ὁ (καρδία, γνώστης), knower of hearts: Act.1:24 15:8.† (AS)

karphos.notes: κάρφος, -εος (-ους) τό [in LXX: Gen.8:11 (טָרַף) * ;] a small dry stalk, a twig; metaphorically, of a minor fault: Mat.7:3-5, Luk.6:41-42.† (AS)

karpophoreo.notes: καρπο-φορέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Hab.3:17 (פָּרַח), Wis.10:7 * ;] to bear fruit: χόρτον, Mrk.4:28 (cf. Wis, l.with). Metaphorical, of conduct: Mat.13:23, Mrk.4:20, Luk.8:15, Rom.7:4-5, Col.1:10; mid., Col.1:6.† (AS)

karpophoros.notes: καρπο-φόρος, -ον (καρπύς, φέρω), [in LXX: Jer.2:21 (זֶרַע), Psa.107:34 148:9 (פְּרִי) * ;] fruitful: Act.14:17.† (AS)

karpos.notes: καρπός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for פְּרִי ;] fruit: of trees, Mat.12:33 21:19, Mrk.11:14, Luk.6:44 13:6-7; of vines, Mat.21:34, Mrk.12:2, Luk.20:10, 1Co.9:7; of fields, Mrk.4:29, Luk.12:17, 2Ti.2:6, Jas.5:7;…

kartereo.notes: καρτερέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Job.2:9 (חָזַק hi.), Isa.42:14 (פָּאָה), Sir.2:2 12:15, 2 12:1-18, 4Macc 7:1-23 * ;] to be steadfast, patient: Heb.11:27 (cf. προσ-καρτερέω).† (AS)

katabaino.notes: κατα-βαίνω [in LXX chiefly for יָרַד ;] to go or come down, descend. __1. Of persons: Mat.3:16 24:17, Luk.2:51 6:17 10:31 17:31 19:5-6, Jhn.4:47, 49 4:51 5:7, Act.7:34 8:15 10:20 20:10 23:10 24:1, 22 Eph.4:10. before…

kataballo.notes: κατα-βάλλω [in LXX chiefly for נָפַל hi. ;] __1. to cast down, prostrate: metaphorically, pass., 2Co.4:9. __2. to put down, lay down: metaphorically, mid., with accusative, θεμέλιον, Heb.6:1.† (AS)

katabareo.notes: κατα-βαρέω, -ῶ to weigh down: metaphorically, with accusative of person(s), 2Co.12:16.† (AS)

katabasis.notes: κατα-βαρύνω [in LXX: 2Ki.13:25 14:26 (כָּבַד), Jol.2:8, Sir.8:15 * ;] = καταβαρέω: pass., Pres. ptcp., Mrk.14:40.† (AS)

katabole.notes: κατα-βολή, -ῆς, ἡ (καταβάλλω), [in LXX: 2Ma.2:29 * ;] __1. a laying down: εἰς κ. σπέρματος, Heb.11:11 (EV, to conceive seed). __2. a foundation (of a house, ii Mac, l.with): metaphorically, ἀπὸ κ. κόσμου, Mat.13:35…

katabrabeuo.notes: κατα-βραβεύω (βραβεύς, an umpire, cf βραβεῖον) to give judg­ment against, condemn (see Field, Notes, 196; Abbott, Essays, 104 f.): Col.2:18.† (AS)

katacheo.notes: κατα-χέω [in LXX for יָצַק, נָטָה, עָטָה hi. ;] to pour down upon: with genitive (cl.), Mrk.14:3; before ἐπί, with genitive, Mat.26:7.† (AS)

katachraomai.notes: κατα-χράομαι, -ῶμαι [in LXX: Ep. Je.28, 3Ma.4:5 3Mac 5:22 * ;] to make full use of, use to the uttermost, use up: 1Co.7:31; with dative, 1Co.9:18 (for other senses, see LS, see word)..† (AS)

katachthonios.notes: κατα-χθόνιος, -ον (χθών, the earth), subterranean, under the earth (in cl., of the infernal gods): of the departed in Hades, opposite to ἐπουράνιος, ἐπίγειος, Php.2:10.† (AS)

katadeo.notes: κατα-δέω, -ῶ [in LXX for חָבַשׁ, etc. ;] to bind up: τ. τραύματα (cf. Sir.27:21), Luk.10:34.† (AS)

katadikazo.notes: κατα-δικάζω [in LXX: Psa.94:21 (רָשַׁע hi.), La 3:36 (עָוַת pi.), Wis.2:20, al. ;] to pass sentence upon, condemn; in cl., with genitive of person(s) (accusative of thing(s)); in late writers (so LXX), with…

katadioko.notes: κατα-διώκω [in LXX chiefly for רָדַף ;] "perfective" compound (M, Pr., 116), to pursue closely, follow up __(a) with hostile intent (Thuc., i, 49, Gen.31:36 al.); __(b) with kindly intention (Psa.23:6, 1Ki.30:22, cf.…

katadouloo.notes: κατα-δουλόω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for עָבַד ;] to enslave: 2Co.11:20, Gal.2:4.† (AS)

katadunasteuo.notes: κατα-δυναστεύω [in LXX for יָנָה, עָשַׁק, etc. ;] to exercise power over, oppress: with accusative of person(s) (Xen.; LXX, Mic.2:2, Wis.2:10, al.); with genitive of person(s), Jas.2:6 (ὑμᾶς, T); pass., Act.10:38…

katagelao.notes: κατα-γελάω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for שָׂחַק, Job.5:22, Pro.29:9, al. ;] to deride, laugh scornfully at: with genitive of person(s), Mat.9:24, Mrk.5:40, Luk.8:53.† (AS)

kataginosko.notes: κατα-γινώσκω (see: γινώσκω), [in LXX: Deu.25:1 (רָשַׁע hi.), Pro.28:11 (חָקַר), Sir.14:2 19:5 * ;] to blame, condemn: with genitive of person(s), 1Jn.3:20-21; pass., κατεγνωσμένος ἦν (RV, he stood condemned, see…

katagnumi.notes: κατ-άγνυμι [in LXX for נָחַת pi., etc. ;] to break: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.12:20 (LXX), Jhn.19:31-33.† (AS)

katago.notes: κατ-άγω [in LXX chiefly for יָרַד hi. ;] to bring down: with accusative of person(s), Act.22:30, Rom.10:6; id. before εἰς, Act.9:30 23:15, 20 23:28; as nautical term, to bring to land: τ. πλοῖον ἐπὶ τ. γῆν, Luk.5:11;…

katagonizomai.notes: κατ-αγωνίζομαι depon., "perfective" compound (M, Pr., 116); __1. to struggle against. __2. to conquer, overcome: Heb.11:33.† (AS)

kataischuno.notes: κατ-αισχύνω [in LXX chiefly for בּוּשׁ ;] to disgrace, dishonour, put to shame: with accusative of thing(s), 1Co.11:4-5; with accusative of person(s), 1Co.1:27 11:22; pass., to be ashamed: Luk.13:17, 2Co.7:14 9:4,…

katakaio.notes: κατα-καίω [in LXX chiefly for שָׂרַף; in Exo.3:2 (אָכַל pu.), distinguished from καίω ;] to burn up, burn completely: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.13:30 Act.19:19; pass., 1Co.3:15, Heb.13:11, 2Pe.3:10, Rev.8:7;…

katakalupto.notes: κατα-καλύπτω [in LXX chiefly for כָּסָה pi. ;] to cover up; mid., to cover or veil oneself: 1Co.11:6; τ. κεφαλήν, 1Co.11:7.† (AS)

katakauchaomai.notes: κατα-καυχάομαι, -ῶμαι [in LXX: Zec.10:12 (הָלַךְ hith.), Jer.50:11 (עָלַז), Jer.50:38 (הָלַל)* ;] __1. to boast against, exult over: with genitive, Rom.11:18, Jas.2:13; before κατά, with genitive, Jas.3:14 (T, om.…

katakeimai.notes: κατά-κειμαι [in LXX: Pro.6:9 23:34 (שָׁכַב), Jdth.13:15, Wis.17:7 * ;] __1. to lie down. __2. to lie sick: Mrk.1:30 2:4, Jhn.5:6, Act.28:8; before ἐπί with genitive, Act.9:33; id. with accusative, Luk.5:25; ἐν,…

kataklao.notes: κατα-κλάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Eze.19:12 (נָתַשׁ hoph.)* ;] to break up, break in pieces: Mrk.6:41, Luk.9:16.† (AS)

katakleio.notes: κατα-κλείω [in LXX: Jer.32:3 (כָּלָא), Wis.17:2, 16, 2Ma.13:21, 3Ma.3:25 * ;] to shut up:. with accusative of person(s), before ἐν φυλακῇ (-αῖς), Luk.3:20, Act.26:10.† (AS)

kataklerodoteo.notes: κατα-κληρονομέω, -ῶ [in LXX for נָחַל, יָרַשׁ, etc. ;] __1. to distribute by lot or as an inheritance (Num.34:18, Jos.14:1, al.): Act.13:19. __2. to receive by inheritance (Deu.1:38, al.).† κατα-κληροδοτέω, ῶ, [in…

kataklino.notes: κατα-κλίνω [in LXX: Exo.21:18 (נָפַל), Num.24:9, Jdg.5:27, 1Ki.16:11 (כָּרַע), Jdth.12:15, 3Ma.1:3 * ;] to lay down, make to lie down, esp. for meals: with accusative of person(s), Luk.9:14-15; mid., to recline at…

kataklusmos.notes: κατα-κλυσμός, -οῦ, ὁ (κατακλύζω), [in LXX chiefly for מַבּוּל, Gen.6:17, al.; also for שֶׁטֶף, Psa.32:6, al. ;] a flood, deluge: Mat.24:38-39 Luk.17:27, 2Pe.2:5.† (AS)

katakluzo.notes: κατα-κλύζω [in LXX for שָׁטַף, Psa.78:20, al. ;] to inundate, deluge: pass., 2Pe.3:6.† (AS)

katakoloutheo.notes: κατ-ακολουθέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Jer.17:16 (רָאָה), 1Ma.6:23, al. ;] to follow after: Luk.23:55; with dative of person(s), Act.16:17.† (AS)

katakopto.notes: κατα-κόπτω [in LXX for נָכָה hi., כָּתַת pi., etc. ;] to cut up, cut in pieces (cf. Isa.27:9, Jer.21:7, 2Ch.34:7): ἑαυτὸν λίθοις, Mrk.5:5.† (AS)

katakremnizo.notes: κατα-κρημνίζω (κρημνός), [in LXX: 2Ch.25:12 (שָׁלַךְ hi.), 2Ma.12:15 2Mac 14:43, 4Ma.4:25*.] to throw over a precipice, cast down head­long: Luk.4:29.† (AS)

katakrima.notes: κατά-κριμα, -τος, τό (κατακρίνω), penalty (RV, condemnation; but cf. Deiss., BS, 264 f.; MM, Exp., xv): Rom.5:16, 18 8:1.† (AS)

katakrino.notes: κατα-κρίνω [in LXX: Est.2:1 (גָּזַר ni.), Wis.4:16, Dan LXX TH Sus 1:53, 4:34, TH Sus 1:41,48,53* ;] to give judgment against, condemn: Rom.8:34; with accusative of person(s), Mrk.14:64, Jhn.8:10-11; disting. fr.…

katakrisis.notes: κατά-κρισις, -εως, ἡ (κατακρίνω), condemnation: 2Co.3:9 7:3 (cf. Deiss., LAE, 91 f.).† (AS)

katakurieuo.notes: κατα-κυριεύω [in LXX: Gen.1:28 (כָּבַשׁ), Psa.110:2 Sir.17:4, al. ;] to gain or exercise dominion (over); __(a) absol. (Arist.); __(b) with genitive (Arist., al.): Mat.20:25, Mrk.10:42, Act.19:16, 1Pe.5:3.† SYN.:…

katalaleo.notes: κατα-λαλέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for דָבַר ni., pi. ;] to speak evil of, rail at: in cl., with accusative; in LXX, __(a) with genitive, __(b) before κατά, with genitive; in NT, with genitive (M, Pr., 65): Jas.4:11,…

katalalia.notes: κατα-λαλιά, -ᾶς, ἡ (κατάλαλος), [in LXX: Wis.1:11 * ;] evil-speaking, railing: p1., 2Co.12:20, 1Pe.2:1.† (AS)

katalalos.notes: κατά-λαλος, -ου, ὁ a railer, defamer: Rom.1:30.† SYN.: (ψιθυριστής), a whisperer (see Tr., Syn., § cvii, 15; Lft., Notes, 256) (AS)

katalambano.notes: κατα-λαμβάνω [in LXX for נָשַׂג hi., לָכַד, etc. ;] __1. to lap hold of, seize, appropriate: Mrk.9:18; with accusative of thing(s), 1Co.9:24, Php.3:12-13 __2. to overtake: as carrel, of διώκω (Field, Notes, 158 f.),…

katalego.notes: κατα-λέγω [in LXX: Deu.19:16 (עָנָה), 2Ma.7:30 R * ;] __1. to lay down; mid. (in Hom.), to lie down. __2. to narrate (LXX, ll. with). __3. to choose out, hence, to enrol (as of soldiers, Hdt., Thuc., a1., see LS, see…

kataleipo.notes: κατα-λείπω [in LXX chiefly far שָׁאַר, ni., hi., also for עָזַב, יָתַר, etc. ;] __1 1, to leave behind, leave: with accusative of person(s) (of thing(s)), Mat.4:13, Mrk.12:19, 21, Luk.20:31, Act.24:27 25:14,…

katalithazo.notes: κατα-λιθάζω (= cl. -θόω; cf. -θοβολέω, Exo.17:4, Num_14:10) to cast stones at, to stone: Luk.20:6.† (AS)

katallage.notes: κατ-αλλαγή, -ῆς, ἡ (καταλλάσσω), [in LXX: Isa.9:5(4), 2Ma.5:20 * ;] __1. exchange. __2. reconciliation: Rom.5:11; κ. κόσμου, Rom.11:15; διακονία τῆς κ., 2Co.5:18; λόγος τῆς κ., 2Co.5:19.† (AS)

katallasso.notes: κατ-αλλάσσω (Att.. -ττω; cf. ἀλλάσσω) [in LXX: Jer.48:39 (חָתַת), 2Ma.1:5 2Mac 7:33 2Mac 8:29 * ;] prop., to change, exchange (esp. of money); hence, of persons, to change from emnity to friendship, to reconcile (for…

kataloipos.notes: κατά-λοιπος, -ον [in LXX for שָׁאַר, שְׁאֵרִית, יָתַר, etc. ;] left remaining: ἱο κ. τ. ἀνθρώπων, Act.15:17 (LXX).† (AS)

kataluma.notes: κατά-λυμα, -τος, τό (καταλύω), [in LXX: Exo.4:24 (מָלוֹן), 1Ki.9:22 (לִשְׁכָּה), etc. ;] __1. = cl. καταγώγιον (cf. πανδοχεῖον), an inn, lodgingplace: Luk.2:7 (so Ex, l.with, and MGr.). __2. a guest-room…

kataluo.notes: κατα-λύω [in LXX for לוּן, שָׁבַת, etc. ;] __1. to destroy, cast down: Mat.24:2, Mrk.13:2, Luk.21:6; τ. ναόν, Mat.26:61 27:40, Mrk.14:58 15:29, Act.6:14; οἰκίαν, 2Co.5:1; opposite to οἰκοδομεῖν, Gal.2:18.…

katamanthano.notes: κατα-μανθάνω [in LXX: Gen.34:1 (רָאָה), Job.35:4 (5) (שׁוּר), Sir.9:5, 8, al. ;] to learn thoroughly, observe well, consider carefully (on distinction bet. κ. and μανθάνω, cf. M, Pr., 117): Mat.6:28.† (AS)

katamartureo.notes: κατα-μαρτυρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Job.15:6, Pro.25:18 (עָנָה), al. ;] to bear witness againt: with accusative of thing(s) and genitive of person(s), Mat.26:62 27:13, Mrk.14:60.† (AS)

katameno.notes: κατα-μένω [in LXX for יָשַׁב (Num.20:1, al.), etc. ;] to remain permanently, abide: Act.1:13, 1Co.16:6, WH (παραμ-, T, EV).† (AS)

katanalisko.notes: κατ-αν-αλίσκω [in LXX chiefly forאָכַל ;] __1. to use up, spend. __2. to consume: of fire, ptcp., Heb.12:29 (LXX).† (AS)

katanarkaomai.notes: κατα-ναρκάω, -ῶ (ναρκάω, to grow numb, in LXX, in causal sense, for יָקַע, Gen.32:25 (26); without Heb. equiv., Gen.32:32 (33) Job.33:19, Dan LXX 11:6*); __1. to cause to grow numb. __2. Intrans., to grow numb; hence…

katanathematizo.notes: κατα-θεματίζω to curse vehemently: Mat.26:74 (cf. ἀναθεμ-).† (AS)

kataneuo.notes: κατα-νεύω __1. to nod assent, __2. to make a sign by nodding the head: with dative of person(s), Luk.5:7.† (AS)

katangeleus.notes: καταγγελεύς, -έως, ὁ a proclaimer, herald (in Inscr., κ. ἀγώνων, Deiss., LAE, 97): with genitive obj., Act.17:18.† (AS)

katangello.notes: κατ-αγγέλλω [in LXX: Pro.17:5 A, 2Ma.8:36 2Mac 9:17 * ;] __1. to pro­claim, declare: with accusative of thing(s), Act.3:24 16:21 17:23; τ. ἀνάστασιν, Act.4:2; τ. λόγον τοῦ Θ., τ. Κ., Act.13:5 15:36; ὁδὸν σωτηρίας,…

katanoeo.notes: κατα-νοέω, -ῶ [in LXX for נָבַט hi., רָאָה, etc. ;] to take note of, perceive, consider carefully: Act.7:31-32; with accusative of thing(s), Mat.7:3, Luk.6:41 12:24, 27 20:23, Act.11:6 27:39, Rom.4:19; with…

katantao.notes: κατ-αντάω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ki.3:29 (חוּל), 2Ma.4:21 2Mac 4:24 2Mac 4:44 2Mac 6:14 * ;] to come to, arrive at: before εἰς, with accusative loc., Act.16:1 18:19, 24 21:7 25:13 (see M, Pr., 132) Act.27:12 28:13; before…

katanusso.notes: κατα-νύσσω [in LXX: Gen.34:7 (עָצַב hith.), Lev.10:3, Psa.4:4, Psa 29:12, al. (דָּמַם), Dan LXX TH Sus 1:10 (רָדַם ni.), Sir.12:12 14:1, al. ;] __1. to strike or prick violently. __2. to stun. __3. Of strong emotion,…

katanuxis.notes: κατά-νυξις, -εως, ἡ (κατανύσσω, which see), [in LXX: Psa.60:3 (תַּרְעֵלָה), Isa.29:10 (תַּרְדֵּמָה)* ;] __1. a pricking. __2. (Perhaps through resembl. of κατανύσσω to -νυστάζω), torpor of mind, stupefaction:…

katapateo.notes: κατα-πατέω, -ῶ [in LXX for דָּרַךְ, רָמַס, שָׁאַף, etc. ;] to tread down, trample under foot: Mat.7:6, Luk.12:1; pass., Mat.5:13, Luk.8:5. Metaphorical, τ. υἱὸν τ. θεοῦ, Heb.10:29.† (AS)

katapauo.notes: κατα-παύω [in LXX for נוּחַ, שָׁבַת, etc. ;] __1. trans., __(a) to cause to cease, restrain: Act.14:18; __(b) to cause to rest: Heb.4:8. __2. Intrans., to rest: before ἀτό, Heb.4:4 (LXX), Heb.4:10.† (AS)

katapausis.notes: κατά-παυσις, -εως, ἡ (καταπαύω), [in LXX chiefly for מְנוּחָה, Psa.95:11, Isa.66:1, al.; ἡμέρα τῆς κ., 2Ma.15:1] __1. in cl., a putting to rest, causing to cease. __2. In LXX and NT, rest, repose: Act.7:49 (LXX),…

katapetasma.notes: κατα-πέτασμα, -τος, τό (= cl. παραπέτασμα) [in LXX chiefly for פֹּרֶכֶת (the veil of the Holy of Holies), Exo.26:31, Lev.21:23, al.; also for מָסָךְ (the outer veil), Exo.35:12, Num_3:26, al. (elsewhere κάλυμμα) ;]…

kataphero.notes: κατα-φέρω [in LXX for יָרַד hi., etc. ;] to bring down: αἰτιώματα, Act.25:7; ψῆφον, to cast a ballot, Act.26:10; pass., to be borne down: ὕπνῳ, ἀπὸ τ. ὕ., Act.20:9.† (AS)

katapheugo.notes: κατα-φεύγω [in LXX for נוּס, etc. ;] to flee for refuge: before εἰς, Act.14:6; metaphorically, with inf., Heb.6:18.† (AS)

kataphileo.notes: κατα-φιλέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for נָשַׁק ;] to kiss fervently, kiss affectionately: Mat.26:49, Mrk.14:45, Luk.7:38, 45 15:20, Act.20:37.† (AS)

kataphroneo.notes: κατα-φρονέω, -ῶ [in LXX for בָּגַד, בּוּז, etc. ;] to think little of, despise: with genitive, Mat.6:24 18:10, Luk.16:13, Rom.2:4, 1Co.11:22, 1Ti.4:12 6:2, Heb.12:2, 2Pe.2:10.† (AS)

kataphronetes.notes: κατα-φρονητής, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX for בָּגַד, בֹּגְדוֹת, Hab.1:5 2:5, Zep.3:4 * ;] a despiser: Act.13:41.† (AS)

kataphtheiro.notes: κατα-φθείρω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁחַת hi., Gen.6:12, al. ;] __1 1 to destroy entirely. __2. In moral sense (as LXX), to deprave, corrupt: pass. pf. ptcp., before τ. νο ν, 2Ti.3:8.† (AS)

katapino.notes: κατα-πίνω [in LXX chiefly for בָּלַע ;] __1. to drink down, swallow: Mat.23:24, Rev.12:16. __2. to devour: 1Pe.5:8. Metaphorical, to swallow up, consume: pass., 1Co.15:54 (LXX), 2Co.2:7 5:4, Heb.11:29.† (AS)

katapipto.notes: κατα-πίπτω [in LXX: Psa.145:14 (נָפַל), etc. ;] to fall down: Act.28:6; before εἰς, Act.26:14; ἐπί with accusative, Luk.8:6.† (AS)

katapleo.notes: κατα-πλέω, -ῶ to sail down, sail to land, put in: εἰς τ. χώραν, Luk.8:26.† (AS)

kataponeo.notes: κατα-πονέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ma.8:2 (A), 3Ma.2:2 3Mac 2:13 * ;] to wear down, oppress, treat hardly: pass., Act.7:24, 2Pe.2:7.† (AS)

katapontizo.notes: κατα-πονίζω [in LXX for טָבַע pu., Exo.15:4 A, בָּלַע pi., שָׁטַף, Psa.55:9 69:2, al. ;] to throw into the sea, sink or drown therein: Mat.14:30; pass., Mat.18:6.† (AS)

katapsucho.notes: κατα-ψύχω [in LXX: Gen.18:4 (שָׁעַן ni.)* ;] to cool: with accusative, Luk.16:24.† (AS)

katara.notes: κατ-άρα, -ας, -ἡ [in LXX chiefly for קְלָלָה ;] a curse: Gal.3:10, 13 Heb.6:8, 2Pe.2:14; opposite to εὐλογία, Jas.3:10; concrete, of Christ, Gal.3:13 (see Lft. in l).† (AS)

kataraomai.notes: κατ-αράομαι, -ώμαι depon. (κατάρα), [in LXX chiefly for קָלַל pi. ;] to curse: with accusative, Mrk.11:21; opposite to εὐλογέω, Luk.6:26, Rom.12:14, Jas.3:9; pass. pf. ptcp., accursed, under a curse (see M, Pr.,…

katargeo.notes: κατ-αργέω, -ῶ (κατά, causative, ἀργός = ἀ-εργός), [in LXX: 2Es.4.21; 2Es.4.23; 2Es.5.5; 2Es.6.8 (בְּטֵל)* ;] to make idle or inactive (χέρας, Eur., Phœn., 753): of soil occupied by an unfruitful tree, Luk.13:7.…

katarithmeo.notes: κατ-αριθμέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ch.31:19 (יָחַשׂ hith.), al. ;] to number or count among: before ἐν, Act.1:1.† (AS)

katartisis.notes: κατ-άρτισις, -εως, ἡ (καταρτίζω), a strengthening, making fit: in ethical sense, 2Co.13:9.† (AS)

katartismos.notes: καταρτισμός, -οῦ, ὁ [in Sm.: Isa.38:12 * ;] = κατάρτισις (but see ICC, Eph. l.with): Eph.4:12.† (AS)

katartizo.notes: κατ-αρτίζω [in LXX chiefly in Ps (Psa.8:2 al.; כּוּן, etc.) and Ezra (כְּלַל) ;] to render ἄρτιος, i.e. fit, complete; __(a) to mend, repair: Mat.4:21, Mrk.1:19; __(b) to furnish completely, complete, equip, prepare:…

kataschesis.notes: κατά-σχεσις, -εως, ἡ (κατέχω), [in LXX nearly always for אֲחֻזָּה ] __1. a holding back. __2. LXX and NT, a holding fast, possession: Act.7:5, 45.† (AS)

kataseio.notes: κατα-σείω [in LXX: Da TH Bel 1:14 AR, 1Ma.6:38 * ;] __1. to shake down. __2. Of the hand, to shake or wave as a signal: τ. χεῖρα, Act.19:33; τ. χειρί, Act.13:16; id. with dative of person(s), Act.21:40; before inf.,…

kataskapto.notes: κατα-σκάπτω [in LXX for נָתַץ, הָרַס, etc. ;] to dig down: Rom.11:3 (LXX).† (AS)

kataskenoo.notes: κατα-σκηνόω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for סָכַךְ ;] to pitch one's tent, lodge, dwell: before ἐν, Mat.13:32, Luk.13:19; ὑπό, with accusative, Mrk.4:32; ἐπ᾽ ἐλπιδι, Act.2:26 (LXX).† (AS)

kataskenosis.notes: κατα-σκήνωσις, εως, ἡ [in LXX: 1Ch.28:2 (בָּנָה), Eze.37:27 (מִשְׁכָּן) Tob.1:4, Wis.9:8, 2Ma.14:35 (R)* ;] __1. prop., an encamping, taking up one's quarters (Polyb., al., see LS; and cf. LXX, ll. with). __2. a…

kataskeuazo.notes: κατα-σκευάζω [in LXX: Isa.40:19, 28, (בָּרָא), etc. ;] to prepare, make ready: τ. ὁδόν, Mat.11:10, Mrk.1:2, Luk.7:27; οἶκον, Heb.3:3-4; σκηνήν, Heb.9:2, 6; κιβωτόν, Heb.11:7, 1Pe.3:20; pass. pf. ptcp., λαον κ.,…

kataskiazo.notes: κατα-σκιάζω to overshadow: with accusative, Heb.9:5.† (AS)

kataskopeo.notes: κατα-σκοπέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 4Ki.10:3, 1Ch.19:3 (רָגַל pi.), 1Ma.5:38 (A)* ;] to view closely, inspect, spy out: with accusative, Gal.2:4.† (AS)

kataskopos.notes: κατά-σκοπος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for רָגַל pi. ;] a spy: Heb.11:31.† (AS)

katasophizomai.notes: κατα-σοφίζομαι [in LXX: Exo.1:10 (חָכָם hith.), Jdth.5:11 10:19 * ;] to deal craftily with, outwit: Act.7:19 (LXX) † (AS)

katasphazo.notes: κατα-σφάζω [in LXX: Zec.11:5 (הָרַג), Eze.16:40 (בָּתַק pi.), 2Ma.8:1-36 ;] to kill off, slay: with accusative, Luk.19:27.† (AS)

katasphragizo.notes: κατα-σφραγίζω [in LXX: Job.9:7 37:7 (חָתַם), Wis.2:5 * ;] to seal up, secure with a seal: Rev.5:1.† (AS)

katastello.notes: κατα-στέλλω [in LXX: 2Ma.4:31, 3Ma.6:1 * ;] __1. to let down, lower. __2. to keep down, restrain: with accusative, Act.19:35-36 † (AS)

katastema.notes: κατά-στημα, -τος, τό (καθίστημι), in LXX: 3Ma.5:45 (-εμα, A) * ;] __1. condition, of health, etc. __2. demeanour: Tit.2:3 (for exx. see Field, Notes, 220).† (AS)

katastole.notes: κατα-στολή, -ῆς, ἡ (καταστέλλω), [in LXX: Isa.61:3 (מַעֲטֶה)* ;] __1. a letting down, checking. __2. steadiness, quietness in demeanour. __3. LXX and NT (cf. Plut., ii, 65 D; -ίζω = vestire), a garment, dress,…

katastreniao.notes: κατα-στρηνιάω, -ῶ to wax wanton against: with genitive, 1Ti.5:11 (AS)

katastrepho.notes: κατα-στρέφω [in LXX for הָפַךְ, etc., Gen.19:21, Jer.20:16, al. ;] __1. to turn down, turn over; as, the soil. __2. to overturn, overthrow: Mat.21:12, Mrk.11:15; pass. pf. ptcp., Act.15:16 (LXX).† (AS)

katastronnumi.notes: κατα-στρώννυμι [in LXX: Num.14:16 (שָׁחַט), Job.12:23 (שָׁטַח), שָׁטַח), Jdt.4.1-15; Jdt.2.1-28 Mac.4 ;] __1. to strew or spread over. __2. to lay low, overthrow: 1Co.10:5.† (AS)

katastrophe.notes: κατα-στροφή, -ῆς, ἡ (καταστρέφω), [in LXX: Job.21:17, Pro.1:27 (אֵיד), al. ;] __1. overthrow: 2Pe.2:6 (WH, om.). __2. Metaphorical, subversion, upsetting (cf. καθαίρεσις, 2Co.13:10): 2Ti.2:14.† (AS)

katasuro.notes: κατα-σύρω [in LXX: Jer.49:10 (חָשַׂף), Dan LXX, Dan.11:10, 26 (שָׁטַף) * ;] __1. to pull down. __2. to drag away: with accusative of person(s), Luk.12:58.† (AS)

katatithemi.notes: κατα-τίθημι [in LXX: 1Ch.21:27 שׁוּב hi.), Psa.41:8 (יָצַק), 1Ma.10:23, al. ;] to lay down, deposit, lay by: Mrk.15:46, T; mid., to lay up for oneself: χάριν (Hdt., al., see LS, see word), with dative of person(s),…

katatome.notes: κατα-τομή, -ῆς, ἡ [in Sm.: Jer.31:37 (κατατέμνω is used in LXX of forbidden mutilations: Lev.21:5, al.) ;] __1. incision. __2. excision, concision, mutilation: in sarcasm, by paronomasia, in contrast to (true)…

katatrecho.notes: κατα-τρέχω [in LXX: Lev.26:37, Jdg.1:6 (רָדַף), al. ;] to run down: before ἐπί, with accusative of person(s), Act.21:32.† (AS)

kataxioo.notes: κατ-αξιόω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ma.13:12, 3Ma.3:21 3Mac 4:11, 4Ma.18:3 * ;] to deem worthy: with accusative of person(s) and genitive of thing(s), 2Th.1:5; pass. before inf., Luk.20:35, Act.5:41.† (AS)

katecheo.notes: κατ-ηχέω, -ῶ __1. to resound. __2. to teach by word of mouth, instruct, inform (see 131., Phil. Gosp., 20, 31): Gal.6:6; with accusative of person(s), 1Co.14:19; pass. with accusative of thing(s), Luk.1:4 (ICC, in…

katecho.notes: κατ-έχω [in LXX for אָחַז, חָזַק hi., etc. ;] __1. perfective of ἔχω (M, Pr., 116; M, Th., 155), __(a) to possess, hold fast: Luk.8:15, Jhn.5:4, Rom.1:18 (Lft., Notes, 251), 1Co.7:30 11:2, 2Co.6:10, 1Th.5:21,…

kategoreo.notes: κατηγορέω, -ῶ (κατά, ἀγορεύω), [in LXX: Dan LXX 6:5, (9), 1Ma.7:6 1Mac 7:25, 2Ma.4:47 2Mac 10:13 2Mac 10:21, 4Ma.9:14 * ;] to make accusation, accuse, __(a) in general: absol., Rom.2:15; with genitive of person(s)…

kategoria.notes: κατηγορία, -ας, ἡ (κατήγορος), an accusation, charge: with genitive of person(s), Jhn.18:29; before κατά, with id., 1Ti.5:19; with genitive of thing(s), Tit.1:6.† (AS)

kategoros.notes: κατήγορος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Pro.18:17 (רִיב), 2Ma.4:5 * ;] an accuser: Act.23:30, 35 24:8 (WH, R, txt. om.) Act.25:16, 18.† (AS)

katepheia.notes: κατήφεια, -ας, ἡ (κατηφής, downcast, Wis.17:4 *), dejection: Jas.4:9.† (AS)

katephistemi.notes: κατ-εφ-ίστημι to rise up against: Act.18:12.† (AS)

katerchomai.notes: κατ-έρχομαι [in LXX: Tob.1:22, א Tob.2:1, Est.3:13, Wis.11:22, 2Ma.11:29 * ;] to come down, go down: before εἰς, Luk.4:31, Act.8:5 13:4 15:30 19:1, Τ; ἀπό, Luk.9:37, Act.15:1 18:5 21:10; ἀπό and εἰς, Act.11:27 12:10;…

katergazomai.notes: κατ-εργάζομαι (emphatic form of ἐργάζομαι), [in LXX for פָּעַל, etc. (9 exx., each for a different Heb. word) ;] to effect by labour, achieve, work out, bring about: with accusative, Rom.4:15 5:3 7:8, 15-20,…

katesthio.notes: κατ-εσθίω ("perfective" comp. of ἐσθίω, which see, and cf. M, Pr., 111) [in LXX chiefly for אָכַל ;] to eat up, devour: with accusative, of seed, Mat.13:4, Mrk.4:4, Luk.8:5; τέκνον, Rev.12:4; βιβλαρίδιον,…

kateuthuno.notes: κατ-ευθύνω [in LXX for כּוּן ni., hi., etc. ;] to make or keep straight, direct, guide: metaphorically, τ. ὁδόν, 1Th.3:11; τ. πόδας εἰς ὁδὸν εἰρήνης, Luk.1:79; τ. καρδίας εἰς τ. ἀγάπην, 2Th.3:5.† (AS)

katexousiazo.notes: κατ-εξουσιάζω to exercise authority over: with genitive of person(s), Mat.20:25, Mrk.10:42.† SYN: (κατακυριεύω) (see Swete, Mk., l.with) (AS)

kathaireo.notes: καθ-αιρέω, -ῶ [in LXX for נָתַץ, פָּרַץ, יָרַד, etc. ;] __1. to take down: with accusative of person(s) (the technical term for removal after crucifixion, Field, Notes, 44), Mrk.15:36, 46, Luk.23:53, Act.13:29. __2.…

kathairesis.notes: καθ-αίρεσις, -εως, ἡ (καθαιρέω), [in LXX: Exo.23:24 (הָרַס), 1Ma.3:43 * ;] a pulling down, destruction: figuratively, κ. ὀχυρωμάτων, 2Co.10:4; opposite to οἰκοδομή, 2Co.10:8 13:10.† (AS)

kathairo.notes: καθαίρω (καθαρός), [in LXX: Isa.28:27 (דּוּשׁ ho.), 2Ki.4:6, Jer.51:39 א* ;] to cleanse: of pruning, Jhn.15:2 (cf. καθαρίζω).† (AS)

kathapto.notes: καθ-άπτω [in Sm.: Sng.1:6 * ;] __1. to fasten on, put upon, with accusative __2. Act. for mid. (cf. Bl., § 53, 3), to lay hold of, attack: with genitive, χειρός, Act.28:3.† (AS)

katharismos.notes: καθαρισμός, -oῦ, τό (καθαρίζω). [in LXX for טָהֵר, טׇהֳרָה, etc. ;] cleansing, purification: Jhn.3:25; with genitive subjc., τ. Ἰουδαίων, Jhn.2:6; with genitive obj., of women after childbirth, Luk.2:22; of lepers,…

katharizo.notes: καθαρίζω (Hellenistic-FlJ, Inscr.-for Attic καθαίρω, which see, on the vulgar -ερ-, Mat.8:3, Mrk.1:42, see Bl., § 6, 1; Thackeray, Gr., 74) [in LXX chiefly for טָהֵר ;] to cleanse, make clean. __1. In physical sense:…

katharos.notes: καθαρός, -ά, -όν [in LXX chiefly for טָהוֹר ;] pure, clean. __1. Physically: Mat.23:26 27:59, Jhn.13:10-11 (fig.), Jhn.15:3 (figuratively, as of a vine cleansed by pruning), Heb.10:22, Rev.15:6 19:8, 14 21:18, 21.…

katharotes.notes: καθαρότης, -ητος, ἡ (καθαρός), [in LXX: Exo.24:10 (טֹהַר), Wis.7:24 * ;] purity, cleanness: with genitive, Heb.9:13.† (AS)

kathedra.notes: καθ-έδρα, -ας, ἡ (κατά, + ἔδρα, a seat) [in LXX for מוֹשָׁב and cogn. forms ;] a chair, seat: Mat.21:12, Mrk.11:16; of teachers, Mat.23:2.† (AS)

kathegetes.notes: καθηγητής, -οῦ, ὁ (καθηγέομαι, to go before, guide), __1. prop., a guide. __2. a master, teacher (MGr. professor): Mat.23:16.† (AS)

katheko.notes: καθ-ήκω [in LXX: impers., Lev.5:16, Eze.21:27 (32), al. (מִשְׁפָּט); τὰ κ., Exo.5:13, al. (דָבַר), etc. ;] __1. to come down, come to. __2. to be fit, proper: impers., with accusative and inf., Act.22:22 (on the…

kathemai.notes: κάθ-ημαι [in LXX chiefly for יָשַׁב ;] in cl. present and imperfect only (prop. pf. and plpf. of καθέζομαι; cf. Bl., § 24), in LXX and NT fut. also, to sit, be seated: Mat.11:16 22:44" (LXX), Mrk.2:6, Jhn.6:3,…

kathemerinos.notes: καθημερινός, -ή, -όν (καθ ̓ ἡμέραν), [in LXX: Jdth.12:15 * ;] daily (MGr. καθημερνός): Act.6:1.† (AS)

katheudo.notes: καθεύδω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁכַב, Gen.28:13, Psa.88:5, al.; also for יָשֵׁן, Sng.5:2, Dan LXX TH Dan.12:2, al.; for יָשַׁב, 1Ki.19:9 ;] to sleep: Mat.8:24 9:24 13:25 25:5 26:46, 43 26:45, Mrk.4:27, 38 5:39 14:37,…

kathexes.notes: καθ-εξῆς (= cl. ἑξῆς, ἐφεξῆς) adv., __1. successively, in order: Luk.1:3, Act.11:4 18:23; τῶν κ.. (those that succeeded him), Act.3:24. __2. after­wards: ἐν τῷ κ. (sc. χρόνῳ), Luk.8:1.† (AS)

kathezomai.notes: καθ-έζομαι [in LXX: Lev.12:5, al. (יָשַׁב), Job.39:28 (שָׁכַן) ;] to sit down, sit: Jhn.20:12; before ἐν, Mat.26:55, Luk.2:46, Jhn.11:20, Act.6:15; before ἐπί, with genitive, Act.20:9; id. with dative, Jhn.4:6 (cf.…

kathiemi.notes: καθ-ίημι [in LXX: Exo.17:11 (נוּחַ hi.), and seel., 1Ch.21:27, Zec.11:13, Jer.32:14 * ;] to send or let down: before εἰς, Luk.5:19; διά, with genitive, ib., Act.9:25; ptcp., before ἐπί, with genitive, Act.10:11; ἐκ,…

kathistemi.notes: καθ-ίστημι (and καθιστάνω, Act.17:15), [in LXX for פָּקַד, קוּם, שׂוּם, etc., 24 words in all* ;] __1. __(a) to set down; __(b) to bring down to a place (Hom., Xen., al.; 2Ch.28:15, 1Ki.5:3): Act.17:15. __2. to set…

kathizo.notes: καθ-ίζω [in LXX chiefly for יָשַׁב ;] __1. causal, to make to sit down, set, appoint: Act.2:30, 1Co.6:4, Eph.1:20. __2. Intrans., to sit down, be seated, sit: Mat.5:1, Mrk.9:35, Luk.7:15, Jhn.8:2, al.; with inf.…

katholou.notes: καθόλου (= καθ᾽ ὅλον and so in cl. bef. Arist.) adv., [in LXX: Exo.22:11(10) Amo.3:3-4; Eze.13:3, 22 17:14 (τὸ κ, μή = בִּלְתִּי), Dan LXX TH Dan.3:50* ;] on the whole, in general: μή κ., not at all, Act.4:18.† (AS)

kathoplizo.notes: καθ-οπλίζω [in LXX: Jer.46:9 (תָּפַשׂ), Mac.9* ;] to arm fully: pass., Luk.11:21.† (AS)

kathorao.notes: καθ-οράω, -ῶ [in LXX: Num.24:2 (רָאָה), Job.10:4 39:26, 3Ma.3:11 * ;] __1. to look down. __2. to discern clearly: Rom.1:20.† (AS)

katioomai.notes: κατ-ιόω, -ῶ (intensive of ἰόω; ἰός) [in LXX: Sir.12:11 * ;] to rust over; pass., become rusted over: Jas.5:3.† (AS)

katischuo.notes: κατ-ισχύω [in LXX chiefly for חָזַק ;] to overpower, prevail against, prevail: absol., Luk.23:23; with inf., Luk.21:36; with genitive, Mat.16:18.† (AS)

katoikeo.notes: κατ-οικέω, -ῶ [in LXX very frequently and nearly always for יָשַׁב ;] __1. trans., to inhabit, dwell in: with accusative, Luk.13:4, Act.1:10 2:9, 14 4:16 9:32, 35 19:10, 17. Rev.17:2; of God, Mat.23:21. __2.…

katoikesis.notes: κατ-οίκησις, -εως, ἡ (κατοικέω), [in LXX for יָשַׁב, Gen.10:30, al. ;] dwelling: Mrk.5:3.† (AS)

katoiketerion.notes: κατ-οικητήριον, -ου, τό (κατοικέω), [in LXX for מוֹשָׁב, מָעוֹן, etc. ;] a habitation, dwelling-place: Eph.2:22, Rev.18:2.† (AS)

katoikia.notes: κατ-οικία, -ας, ἡ (κατοικέω), [in LXX chiefly for מוֹשָׁב, Exo.35:3, al. ;] __1. a dwelling: Act.17:26. __2. a settlement (Polyb.).† (AS)

katoptrizo.notes: κατοπτρίζω (κάτοπτρον, a mirror), to show as in a mirror. Mid., to see oneself mirrored (see MM, Exp., xv); with accusative of thing(s) (R, txt., but see mg.), to reflect as a mirror: 2Co.3:18 (cf. Abbott, Essays,…

katoteros.notes: κατώτερος, -έρα, -ερον (κάτω), [in LXX for תַּחְתּוֹן, תַּחְתִּי ;] lower; τὰ. κ. τῆς γῆς (see AR, Eph., l.with; for rendering this lower earth, see ICC, Eph, l.with), the lower parts of the earth (cf. Psa.139:15;…

kauchaomai.notes: καυχάομαι, -ῶμαι [in LXX for הָלַל hith., etc. ;] to boast or glory (in LXX, of joyous exultation, and so in the NT quotations, infr.; cf. DB, ii, 790b; Hort on Jas.1:9): absol., 1Co.1:31 (LXX) 1Co.4:7 13:3,…

kauchema.notes: καύχημα, -τος, τό (καυχάομαι), [in LXX chiefly for תְּהִלָּה, תִּפְאָרָה ;] __1. a boast (Pind.): 2Co.5:12 9:3 (Thayer, see word; e. contra, 2, Ellic., Lft., on Gal.6:4; Lft., Notes, 204, 277). __2. ground or matter…

kauchesis.notes: καύχησις, -εως, ἡ (καυχάομαι), [in LXX for תִּפְאָרָה (1Ch.29:13, Eze.16:12, al) ;] a boasting, glorying: Rom.3:27, 2Co.11:10 11:17, Jas.4:16; before ὑπέρ, 2Co.7:4 8:24; ἐτί, with genitive, 2Co.7:14; ἔχω τὴν κ. ἐν…

kauma.notes: καῦμα, -τος, τό (καίω), [in LXX for חֹם, חֹרֶב, etc. ;] heat: Rev.7:16 16:9.† (AS)

kaumatizo.notes: καυματίζω (καῦμα), to burn or scorch up: with accusative before ἐν πυρί, Rev.16:8. Pass., Mat.13:14, Mrk.4:6; before καῦμα μέγα, Rev.16:9.† (AS)

kausis.notes: καῦσις, -εως, ἡ (καίω), [in LXX for בָּעַר pi., etc. ;] burning: Heb.6:8.† (AS)

kauson.notes: καύσων, -ωνος, ὁ (καίω), [in LXX: Gen.31:40 א (חֹרֶב), Isa.49:10 (שָׁרָב), Job.27:21, Hos.12:1, (2), Hos.13:15, Jon.4.8, Jer.18:17, Eze.17:10 19:12 (קָדִים); Jer.51:1, From Theodotion Dan.3:67) Jdth.8:3, Sir.18:16…

kausoo.notes: καυσόω, -ῶ (καῦσος, burning heat, fever), only in pass., __1. to burn with fever (Galen., al.). __2. In NT, to burn with great heat: 2Pe.3:10 3:12 (V. Mayor, in l).† (AS)

kauteriazo.notes: καυστηριάζω (Rec. καυτηρ-, and so usually in Gk. writers, see Soph., Lex., see word) to mark by branding, brand (AV, scar, but see CGT, in l): metaphorically, pass. pf. ptcp., 1Ti.4:2 (cf. καυτήριον, 4Ma.15:22 *).† (AS)

kedros.notes: κέδρος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for אֶרֶז, Num.24:6, al.; χειμάρρους τῶν Κέδρων, 2Ki.15:23, 3Ki.15:13 (קִדְרוֹן) ;] a cedar: χείμαρρος τῶν Κ. (as in 2Sam, 1Ki, ll. with), Jhn.18:1 (Rec. Tr., WH, R, mg.; τοῦ Κέδρου, T,…

kegchreai.notes: Κενχρεαί (Rec. Κεχγ-; ν. WH, App., 150), -ῶν, αἱ, Cenchreæ, a port of Corinth: Act.18:18, Rom.16:1.† (AS)

keimai.notes: κεῖμαι [in LXX: Jer.24:1 (יָעַד hoph,), al. ;] used as passive of τίθημι (LS, see word), to be laid, to lie; __(a) of persons: an infant, Luk.2:12, 16; a dead body, Mat.28:6, Luk.23:53, Jhn.20:12; __(b) of things, to…

keiria.notes: κειρία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Pro.7:16 (מַרְבַד)* ;] a vernacular word, __1 1, a bed-cord (Aristoph., AV., 816; Pr, l.with). __2. In pl., swathings (cf. Field, Notes, 96 f.): Jhn.11:44.† (AS)

keiro.notes: κείρω [in LXX chiefly for גָּזַז, Isa.53:7, al. ;] to cut short the hair, shear: a sheep, Act.8:32 (Isa, l.with). Mid., to have one's hair cut off, be shorn: absol., 1Co.11:6; τ-. κεφαλήν, Act.18:18.† (AS)

keleuo.notes: κελεύω [in LXX: I Est.9:53, Tob.8:18, al., and frequently in 1-4Mac ;] to urge on, bid by word of mouth, order, command (mostly of one in authority): with accusative and inf. aor., Mat.14:19, 28 18:25 27:64,…

keleusma.notes: κέλευσμα, -τος, τό (κελεύω), [in LXX: Pro.30:27 * ;] a call, summons, shout of command: 1Th.4:16 (see M, Th., in l).† (AS)

kenodoxia.notes: κενοδοξία, -ας, ἡ (κενόδοξος), [in LXX: Wis.14:14, 2Ma.2:15 2Mac 8:10 * ;] __1. foolish fancy, vain opinion, error (LXX, ll. with). __2. vain­glory, groundless conceit: Php.2:3.† (AS)

kenoo.notes: κενόω, -ῶ (κενός, which see), [in LXX: Jer.14:2 15:9 (עָמָל pu.)* ;] to empty. Metaphorical, to empty, make empty, vain or of no effect: καύχημα, 1Co.9:15; ἑαυτόν, of Christ, Php.2:7 (v Lft.; ICC, in 1, and esp.…

kenophonia.notes: κενοφωνία, -ας, ἡ (κενός, φωνέω), [cf. κενολογέω (צָפַף), Isa.8:19 * ;] empty talk, babbling: pl., 1Ti.6:20, 2Ti.2:16.† (AS)

kenos.notes: κενός, -ή, -όν [in LXX chiefly for רִיק and cognate forms ;] empty (Gen.37:24, Jdg.7:16). Metaphorical, __(a) empty, vain: λόγοι, Eph.5:6; ἀπάτη, Col.2:8; κήρυγμα, πίστις, 1Co.15:14; __(b) vain, fruitless: ἡ χάρις,…

kensos.notes: κῆνσος, -ου, ὁ (Lat. census), a poll-tax: Mat.17:25 22:17, Mrk.12:14 (WH, mg., ἐπικεφάλαιον); τ. νόμισμα τοῦ κ., Mat.22:19 (see MM, Exp., xv).† (AS)

kentron.notes: κέντρον, -ου, τό (εκντέω, to prick), [in LXX: Pro.26:3 (מֶתֶג), Hos.13:14 (קֹטֶב), Hos.5:12, Sir.38:25, 4Ma.14:19 * ;] __1. a sting (4Mac, l.with): Rev.9:10. Metaphorical (as Hos.13:14): τ. θανάτου, 1Co.15:55 (LXX),…

kenturion.notes: κεντυρίων, -ωνος, ὁ (Lat. centurio); a centurion: Mrk.15:39, 44-45 (Mt, Lk, use ἑκιτόνταρχος (-ης), q.v).† (AS)

kephalaioo.notes: κεφαλιόω (Rec. -αιόω, [so in LXX: Sir.32:8 *]), -ῶ (κεφάλιον, dimin. of κεφαλή, in late writers) __1. (-αιόω) to sum up (Thuc., al.). __2. (In NT, only) to wound on the head: Mrk.12:4.† (AS)

kephalaios.notes: κεφάλαιος, -α, -ον (κεφαλή), [in LXX (-αιον, τό) for רֹאשׁ, Num_4:2, al. ;] of the head; metaphorically, principal, chief; mostly as subst., κεφάλαιον, τό; __1. the chief point: Heb.8:1. __2. the sum total, amount:…

kephale.notes: κεφαλή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX nearly always for רֹאשׁ ;] the head: Mat.5:35, Mrk.6:24, Rev.1:14 9:7, al. mult.; figuratively, ἄνθρακες ἐπὶ τὴν κ., Rom.12:20 (LXX); αἷμι, Act.18:6; metaphorically, of a husband, with…

kephalis.notes: κεφαλίς, -ίδος, ἡ (dimin. of κεφαλή), [in LXX for מְגִלָּה, וָו, etc. ;] __1. a little head. __2. an extremity, capital (of a column). __3. (As in Exo.2:9, Psa.40:8, Est.6:2) a roll: κ. βιβλίου, Heb.10:7 (LXX).† (AS)

kepos.notes: κῆπος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for גַּן, גַּנָּה, גִּנָּה, Deu.11:10, Sng.4:12, al. ;] a garden: Luk.13:19, Jhn.18:1, 26 19:41.† (AS)

kepouros.notes: κηπ-ουρός, -οῦ, ὁ (κῆπος + οὖρος, a watcher) a gardener: Jhn.20:15.† (AS)

keraia.notes: κεραία (WH, κερέα, see App., 151), -ας, ἠ (κέρας), a little horn: of the point or extremity which distinguishes some Heb. letters from others (e.g. ד from ר; DCG, ii, 733), Mat.5:18, Luk.16:17.† (AS)

kerameus.notes: κεραμεύς, -εως, ὁ (κεράννυμι), [in LXX for יָצַר, Psa.2:9, Jer.18:2, al. ;] a potter: Rom.9:21; ὁ ἀγρὸς τοῦ κ., Mat.27:7, (LXX).† (AS)

keramikos.notes: κεραμικός, -ή, -όν (κέραμος), [in LXX: Dan LXX 2:41 (פֶּחָר)* ;] __1. (cl.) of a potter (e.g. γῆ). __2. In LXX and NT, = cl., κεραμεοῦς (Plut., κεράμειος, Polyb., -μαῖος, etc), earthen: Rev.2:27.† (AS)

keramion.notes: κεράμιον, -ου, τό (κέραμος), [in LXX: Jer.35:5 (גְּבִיעַ), etc. ;] an earthen vessel, a jar or jug: ὕδατος, Mrk.14:13, Luk.22:10.† (AS)

keramos.notes: κεραμος, -ου, ὁ (κεράννυμι), [in LXX: 2Ki.17:28 (יָצַר כְּלִי)* ;] __1. potter's clay. __2. an earthen vessel. __3. a tile: Luk.5:19.† (AS)

kerannumi.notes: κεράννυμι [in LXX for מָסַךְ, Isa.5:22, al. ;] to mix, mingle, chiefly of the diluting of wine: Rev.18:6; by oxymoron, κεκεράσμενος ἄκρατος, Rev.14:10.† SYN.: μίγνυμι. κ. implies "a mixing of two things, so that they…

keras.notes: κέρας, -ατος (pl. not irreg. as in Attic), τό, [in LXX chiefly for קָרַן ;] a horn: Rev.5:6 12:3 13:1, 11 17:3, 7 17:12, 16; of the projections at the corners of the altar (Exo.29:12, al.): Rev.9:13. Metaphorical, as…

keration.notes: κεράτιον, -ου, τό (dimin. of κέρας), __1. a little horn (Arist.). __2. In pl. (the fruit of the κερατέα), carob-pods: Luk.15:16.† (AS)

kerdaino.notes: κερδαίνω [in Sm.: Job.22:3 * ;] to gain: with accusative, Mat.25:16-17, 20 25:22; τ. κόσμον, Mat.16:26, Mrk.8:36, Luk.9:25; absol., to make profit, get gain: Jas.4:13. Metaphorical, with accusative of thing(s), to…

kerdos.notes: κέρδος, -εος (-ους), τό [in Aq.: Eze.27:24; Sm.: Psa.30:10, al. ;] gain: Php.1:21 3:7, Tit.1:11.† (AS)

kerma.notes: κέρμα, -τος, τό (κείρω), __1. a slice, hence, __2. a small coin: pl., Jhn.2:15.† (AS)

kermatistes.notes: κερματιατής, -οῦ, ὁ (κερματίζω, to cut small, coin into small money), a money changer: Jhn.2:14.† (AS)

kerugma.notes: κήρυγμα, -τος, τό (κηρύσσω), [in LXX: 2Ch.30:5 (קוֹל), Jon.3.2 (קְרִיאָה), Pro.9:3, I Est.9:3 * ;] in cl., that which is cried by a herald, a proclamation. In NT (see Lft., Notes, 161), of God's heralds,…

kerusso.notes: κηρύσσω [in LXX chiefly for קָרָא ;] to be a herald, to proclaim: Mrk.1:45 7:36, Luk.4:18-19" (LXX) (but see Field, Notes, 174) Luk.8:39, Rom.2:21, 1Co.9:27, Gal.5:11, al.; in NT, chiefly of the proclamation of the…

kerux.notes: κῆρυξ (κήρ-, T), -υκος, ὁ [in LXX: Dan LXX TH Dan.3:4 (כָּרוֹז), Gen.41:43, Sir.20:15, 4Ma.6:4 * ;] a herald: 1Ti.2:7, 2Ti.1:11, 2Pe.2:5.† (AS)

ketos.notes: κῆτος, -εος (-ους), τό [in LXX: Jon.2.1; Jon.2.10 (דָּג) Gen.1:21 (תַּנִּין), etc. ;] a huge fish, sea-monster: Mat.12:40 (LXX).† (AS)

kibotos.notes: κιβωτός, -οῦ, ἡ [in LXX: Gen.6:1-22 7:1-24 8:1-22 9:1-29 (תֵּבָה), elsewhere, very freq., as Exo.25:9 (10), for אָרוֹן ;] a wooden box or chest: of Noah's ark, Mat.24:38, Luk.17:27, Heb.11:7, 1Pe.3:20; of the ark of…

kilikiakilikia.notes: Κιλικία, -ας, ἡ Cilicia, a province of Asia Minor: Act.6:9 15:23, 41 21:39 22:3 23:34 27:5, Gal.1:21.† (AS)

kinamomon.notes: κιννάμωμον(Rec. κινάμ-), -υο, τό (from the Phænician, see LS, see word, and cf. Heb. קִנָּמוֹן) cinnamon: Rev.18:13.† (AS)

kinduneuo.notes: κινδυνεύω (κίνδυνος), [in LXX for יָקֹשׁ ni., etc. ;] __1. to be daring, to venture. __2. to be in danger: Luk.8:23, Act.19:27, 40, 1Co.15:30.† (AS)

kindunos.notes: κίνδυνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Psa.116:3 (מֵצַר), Sir.3:26, al. ;] danger, peril: Rom.8:35; pl., 2Co.11:26.† (AS)

kineo.notes: κινέω, -ῶ [in LXX for נוּעַ hi., רָמַשׂ, etc. ;] __1. to set in motion, move: Mat.23:4; τ. κεφαλήν (Psa.22:8, al.), Mat.27:39, Mrk.15:29; pass. (cf. Gen.7:21), Act.17:28. __2. to remove: Rev.2:5 6:14. __3. to excite,…

kis.notes: Κείς (Rec. Κίς), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. קִישׁ), Kish: Act.13:21.† (AS)

kithara.notes: κιθάρα, -ας, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for כִּנּוֹר, frequently in Psa.33:2, al.); and in (Dan.3:5, al.) for קִיתָרֹס, itself a transliteration of κίθαρις, the Homeric form of κ. (cf. CB, Dn., lviii) ;] a lyre, harp:…

kitharizo.notes: κιθαρίζω [in LXX: Isa.23:16 (נָגַן pi.)* ;] to play upon the lyre or harp: 1Co.14:7, Rev.14:2.† (AS)

kitharodos.notes: κιθαρ-ωδός, -οῦ, ὁ (κιθάρα, ἀοιδός, a singer) one who plays and sings to the lyre, a harper: Rev.14:2 18:22.† (AS)

klados.notes: κλάδος, -ου, ὁ (κλάω), [in LXX for דָּלִיָּה, כַּף, etc. ;] a young tender shoot broken off for grafting; then, a branch: Mat.13:32 21:8 24:32, Mrk.4:32 13:28, Luk.13:19; metaphorically, of descendants (cf.…

klaio.notes: κλαίω [in LXX chiefly for בָּכָה ;] of any loud expression of pain or sorrow, esp. for the dead, to weep, lament; __(a) intrans.: Mrk.5:38-39 14:72 (M, Pr., 131) Mrk.16:10, Luk.7:13, 32 7:38 8:52, Jhn.11:31, 33 16:20…

klao.notes: κλάω [in LXX: Jer.16:7 (פַּרְנַךְ), al. ;] to break, break in pieces: of bread, Mat.14:19 15:36 26:26, Mrk.8:6 14:22, Luk.22:19 24:30, Act.2:46 20:7, 11 27:35, 1Co.10:16 11:24; before εἰς, with accusative of…

klasis.notes: κλάσις, -εως, ἡ (κλάω), a breaking: Luk.24:35, Act.2:42.† (AS)

klasma.notes: κλάσμα, -τος, τό (κλάω), [in LXX for פַּת, פֶּלַח ;] a broken piece, fragment: Mat.14:20 15:37, Mrk.6:43 8:8, 19-20, Luk.9:17, Jhn.6:12-13 † (AS)

klaudekauda.notes: Καῦδα (TR, mg., Κλαῦδα; Rec. Κλαύδή), ἡ, Cauda, Clauda, an island near Crete: Act.27:16.† (AS)

klaudios.notes: Κλαύδιος, -ου, ὁ Claudius; __1. the Emperor: Act.11:28 18:2. __2. A military tribune, C. Lysias: Act.23:26.† (AS)

klauthmos.notes: κλαυθμός, -οῦ, ὁ (κλαίω), [in LXX chiefly for בְּכִי ;] crying, weeping, Act.20:37; κ. καὶ ὀδυρμός, Mat.2:18 (LXX); ὁ κ. καὶ ὁ βρυγμός, Mat.8:12 13:42, 50 22:13 24:51 25:30, Luk.13:28.† (AS)

kleio.notes: κλείω [in LXX chiefly for סָגַר ;] to shut: θύραν, Mat.6:6; τ. ἄβυσσον, Rev.20:3; pass., θύρα, Mat.25:10, Luk.11:7 (pl.), Jhn.20:19, 26, Act.21:30; δεσμωτήριον, Act.5:23; πυλῶνες, Rev.21:25. Metaphorical, τ. οὐρανόν,…

kleis.notes: κλείς, -δός Att.. accusative, κλεῖν, later -εῖδα, pl., κλεῖδες, -ας, contr., -εῖς, ἡ, [in LXX for מַפְתֵּחַ, Isa.22:22, al. ;] a key. Fig., τ. βασιλείας τ. οὐρανῶν, Mat.16:19; τ. γνώσεως, Luk.11:52; of David (cf.…

klema.notes: κλῆμα, -τος, τό (κλάω), [in LXX chiefly for זְמוֹרָה, דָּלִיָּה, Eze.15:2 17:6-7, al. ;] a vine-twig, vine-branch: Jhn.15:2, 4-6.† (AS)

klemma.notes: κλέμμα, -τος, τό (κλέπτω), [in LXX: Gen.31:39, Exo.22:3-4 (2-3) (גָּנַב, גְּנֵבָה)* ;] __1. a thing stolen (Arist., LXX) __2. = κλοπή, theft (Plato, al.): pl., Rev.9:21.† (AS)

kleos.notes: κλέος, -ους, τό [in LXX: Job.28:22 (שֵׁמַע) Job.30:8 * ;] __1. a rumour, report. __2. good report, fame, glory: 1Pe.2:20.† (AS)

kleptes.notes: κλέπτης, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for גַּנָּב ;] a thief: Mat.6:19-20 24:43, Luk.12:33, 39, Jhn.10:1, 10 12:6, 1Co.6:10, 1Pe.4:15. Fig., ὡς κ. ἐν νυκτί, 1Th.5:2 5:4 (κλέπτας, WH, txt., R, mg.; see Lft., Notes, 73; but cf. also…

klepto.notes: κλέπτω [in LXX for גָּנַב ;] to steal: absol., Mat.6:19-20 19:18, Mrk.10:19, Luk.18:20, Jhn.10:10, Rom.2:21 13:9, Eph.4:28; with accusative, Mat.27:64 28:13.† (AS)

kleronomeo.notes: κληρονομέω, -ῶ (κληρονόμος) [in LXX chiefly for יָרַשׁ, also for נָחַל, etc. ;] __1. to receive by lot. __2. to inherit (in cl., usually with genitive of thing(s)): absol., Gal.4:30 (LXX); in general, to possess…

kleronomia.notes: κληρονομία, -ας, ἡ (κληρονόμος) [in LXX chiefly for נַחֲלָה, also for יְרֻשָּׁה, etc. ;] prop., an inherited property, an inheritance: Mat.21:38, Mrk.12:7, Luk.12:13 20:14; in general, a possession, inheritance:…

kleronomos.notes: κληρο-νόμος, -ου, ὁ (κλῆρος, νέμομαι, to possess) [in LXX: Jdg.18:7, 2Ki.14:7, Mic.1:15, Jer.8:10 (יָרַשׁ), Sir.23:22 * ;] __1. (as in cl.) an heir: Mat.21:38, Mrk.12:7, Luk.20:14, Gal.4:1; of those who as sons of…

kleroo.notes: κληρόω, -ῶ [in LXX: 1Ki.14:41 (לָכַד ni.), Est.4:11 A (קָרָא ni.), Isa.17:11 * ;] __1. to cast lots. __2. to choose by lot. __3. to assign by lot, assign a portion: pass., ἐν ᾧ καὶ ἐκληρώθημεν, Eph.1:11 (on the…

kleros.notes: κλῆρος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for נַחֲלָה גּוֹרָל ;] a lot; __(a) that which is cast or drawn: Mat.27:35, Mrk.15:24, Luk.23:34, Jhn.19:24" (LXX), Act.1:26; __(b) that which is obtained by casting: Act.1:17 8:21; of…

klesis.notes: κλῆσις, -εως, ἡ (καλέω) [in LXX: Jer.31:6 (קָרָא), Jdth.12:10 A, 3Ma.5:14 * ;] a calling, call; in NT, always of the Divine call to salvation: Rom.11:29, 1Co.1:26 7:20, Eph.1:18 4:1, 4, Php.3:14, 2Th.1:11, 2Ti.1:9,…

kletos.notes: κλητός, -ή, -όν (καλέω), [in LXX for קָרָא, מִקְרָא, Exo.12:16, al. ;] called, invited (as to a banquet, Æsch., 1Sa 1:41, 3Ma.5:14); in NT, always of the Divine call; __(a) to some office: κ. ἀπόστολος, Rom.1:1,…

klibanos.notes: κλίβανος, -ον, ό (Att.., κρίβ-, but κλ- in Ion., Hdt., ii, 92), [in LXX for תַּנּוּר, Gen.15:17, Hos.7:4, 6 al. ;] __1. in cl., a clibanus, cribanus, an earthen vessel for baking bread. __2. In LXX and NT, a furnace…

klima.notes: κλίμα, -τος, τό (κλίνω) [in LXX: Jdg.20:2 A (פִּנָּה)* ;] __1. an inclination, slope, esp. the slope from the equator to the pole (Arist). __2. a region (Polyb., al.): pl., Rom.15:23, 2Co.11:10, Gal.1:21.† (AS)

kline.notes: κλίνη, -ης, ἡ (κλίνω), [in LXX chiefly for מִטָּה ;] a bed, couch: Mat.9:2, 6, Mrk.4:21 7:4, 30, Luk.5:18 8:16 17:34, Rev.2:22 (cf. MM, Exp., xv).† (AS)

klinidion.notes: κλινίδιον, -ου, τό dimin. of κλίνη, a conch: Luk.5:19, 24 (cf. κλενάριον).† (AS)

klino.notes: κλίνω [in LXX chiefly for נָטָה ;] __1. to make to bend, to bow: τ. κεφαλήν (of one dying), Jhn.19:30; τ. πρόσωτον (of terrified persons), Luk.24:5. __2. to make to lean, to rest: τ. κεφαλήν (in sleep), Mat.8:20,…

klisia.notes: κλισία, -ας, ή (κλίνω), [in LXX: 3Ma.6:31 A* ;] __1. a place for reclining; hence, __(a) a hut; __(b) an easy chair; __(with) a couch. __2. a company reclining: pl., Luk.9:14 (cf. FlJ, Ant., xii, 2, 12) (AS)

klopas.notes: Κλωπᾶς, -ᾶ, ὁ (Aram.; on the original, see Lft., Gal., 267 f.), Clopas: Jhn.19:25.† (AS)

klope.notes: κλοπή, -ῆν, ἡ (κλέπτω), [in LXX for גָּנַב ;] theft: pl., Mat.15:19, Mrk.7:22.† (AS)

kludon.notes: κλύδων, -ωνος, ὁ (κλύζω, of the sea, to wash over), [in LXX: Jon.1.4; Jon.1.12 (סַעַר), al. ;] a billow, surge: Luk.8:24, Jas.1:6.† SYN.: κῦμα, a wave (AS)

kludonizo.notes: κλυδωνίζομαι (κλύδων), [in LXX: Isa.57:20 (גָּרַשׁ)* ;] to be tossed by waves; metaphorically, to be tossed like waves (cf. FlJ, Ant., ix, 11, 3): Eph.4:14.† (AS)

knetho.notes: κνήθω (late form of κνάω), __1. to scratch. __2. to tickle; pass., to itch: 2Ti.4:3.† (AS)

knidos.notes: Κνίδος, -ου, ἡ Cnidus, a city (and peninsula) on S.W. coast of Asia Minor: Act.27:7.† (AS)

kodrantes.notes: κοδράντης, -ου, ὁ (Lat. quadrans, the fourth part of an as), a quadrans (AV, farthing): Mat.5:26, Mrk.12:42.† (AS)

koilia.notes: κοιλία, -ας, ἡ (κοῖλος, hollow), [in LXX chiefly for בֶּטֶן, מֵעֶה, קֶרֶב, Deu.7:13, 2Ki.7:12, Gen.41:21, al.; also (Job.3:11 10:18 31:15 38:8) for רֶחֶם ;] __1. the belly (stomach or intestines or both): Mat.12:40…

koimao.notes: κοιμάω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for שָׁכַב ;] to lull to sleep, put to sleep. Mid and pass., to fall asleep (M, Pr., 162; M, Th., 1, 4, 13): Mat.28:13, Luk.22:45, Jhn.11:12, Act.12:6. Metaph, of death: Mat.27:52,…

koimesis.notes: κοίμησις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX: Sir.46:19 48:13 * ;] a reclining, resting: Jhn.11:13.† (AS)

koinoneo.notes: κοινωνέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ch.20:35, Job.34:8, Ecc.9:4 (חָבַר), Pro.1:11, Wis.6:23, Sir.13:1, al. ;] to have a share of, go shares in (something) with (some one), take part in: with genitive of thing(s) (as usually in…

koinonia.notes: κοινωνία, -ας, ἡ (κοινωνός), [in LXX: Lev.6:2 (יָד תְּשׂוּמֶת), Wis.8:18, 3Ma.4:6 * ;] __1. fellowship, communion: Act.2:42, 2Co.6:14, Php.1:5; τ. υἱοῦ, 1Co.1:9; τ. αἵματος, σώματος Χρ., 1Co.10:10 : παθημάτων αὐτσῦ,…

koinonikos.notes: κοινωνικός, -ή, -όν (κοινωνία), __1. sociable. __2. ready to com­municate or impart (R, mg., ready to sympathize, see Field, Notes, 213 f.): 1Ti.6:18.† (AS)

koinonos.notes: κοινωνός, -ή, -όν (κοινός), [in LXX: Pro.28:24, Isa.1:23 (חָבֵר), Mal.2:14 (חֲבֶרֶת), Est.8:13, al. ;] __1. as adj., = κοινός. __2. As subst., ὁ, ἡ κ., __(a) a partner, associate, companion: 2Co.8:23, Phm 17; with…

koinoo.notes: κοινόω, -ῶ [in LXX: 4Ma.7:6 א R* ;] __1. in cl., to make common. __2. In LXX, l.with, and NT (as κοινός, 2), to make ceremonially unclean, to profane (= cl., βεληλόω): Mat.15:11, 18 15:20, Mrk.7:15, 18 7:20, 23,…

koinos.notes: κοινός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Pro.1:14 (אֶחָד) Pro.21:9 25:24 (חֶבֶר), Wis.7:3, al. ;] __1. common (general; = Lat. communis): Act.2:44 4:32; κ. πίστις, Tit.1:4; σωτηρία, Ju 3 __2. common (ordinary; = vulgaris); hence in…

koite.notes: κοίτη, -ης, ἡ (in Hom., κοῖτος) [in LXX chiefly for מִשְׁכָּב, also for שְׁכָבָה, etc. ;] a bed: Luk.11:7; esp. the marriage-bed: κ. ἀμίαντος, Heb.13:4; κ. ἔχειν, of a woman conceiving, Rom.9:10 (cf. peculiar phrases…

koiton.notes: κοιτών, -ῶνος, ὁ (κοίτη = Att.., δωμότιον, see Kennedy, Sources, 40), [in LXX chiefly for חֶדֶר, 2Ki.4:7, Jol.2:16; I Est.3:3, al. ;] a bed-chamber; ὁ ἐπὶ τοῦ κ., a chamberlain: Act.12:20.† (AS)

kokkinos.notes: κόκκινος, -η, -ον (κόκκος, as used of the "berry" of the ilex coccifera, see DB, iv, 416), [in LXX for תּוֹלָע, תּוֹלָע, שָׁנִי, Exo.25:4, Gen.38:23, Isa.1:18, al. ;] scarlet: Mat.27:28, Heb.9:19, Rev.17:3; neut.,…

kokkos.notes: κόκκος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: La 4:5 B א R (תּוֹלָע), Sir.45:11 * ;] a grain: Mat.13:31 17:20, Mrk.4:31, Luk.13:19 17:6, Jhn.12:24, 1Co.15:37.† (AS)

kolakeia.notes: κολακία (Rec. -εία), -ας, ἡ (κολακεύω, to flatter), flattery: 1Th.2:5 (see Lft., Notes, 23).† (AS)

kolaphizo.notes: κολαφίζω (κόλαφος = Att.., κόνδυλοι, the knuckles, the closed fist) to strike with the fist, to buffet: Mat.26:67, Mrk.14:65, 2Co.12:7; pass., 1Co.4:11, 1Pe.2:20.† (AS)

kolasis.notes: κόλασις, -εως, ἡ (κολάζω), [in LXX: Eze.14:3-7 18:30 44:12, (מִכְשׁוֹל) Eze.43:11, λαμβάνειν τὴν κ. (כָּלַם ni.); Wis.11:13 16:2, 24 19:4, Jer.18:20, 2-4 Mac.5 * ;] correction, penalty, punishment: Mat.25:46,…

kolazo.notes: κολάζω (κόλος, docked), [in LXX: Dan.6:12 (13) (no Heb.), I Est.8:24, Wis.3:4 (and freq.), 1Ma.7:7, al. ;] __1. to curtail, dock, prune. __2. to check, restrain. __3. to chastise, correct, punish: pass., 2Pe.2:9;…

kollao.notes: κολλάω, -ῶ (κόλλα, glue), [in LXX chiefly for דָּבַק ;] __1. to glue or cement together. __2. Generally, to unite, to join firmly. Pass., to cleave to, join (oneself to): with dative of person(s), Mat.19:5(LXX),…

kollourion.notes: κολλούριον (TTr., κολλύ-, the more usual form), -ου, τό (dimin. of κολλύρα = κόλλιξ, a coarse bread roll), [in LXX: 3Ki.12:24 hil B* ;] __1. a small bread roll (LXX, ll. with). __2. (Usually in pl., LS, see word) an…

kollubistes.notes: κολλυβιστής, -οῦ, ὁ (κόλλυβος, a small coin, a rate of exchange), a money-changer: Mat.21:12, Mrk.11:15, Jhn.2:15 (of. κερματιοτής).† (AS)

koloboo.notes: κολοβόω, -ῶ (κολοβός, docked), [in LXX: 2Ki.4:12 (קָצַץ pi.) * ;] to cut off, amputate (LXX), hence, to curtail, shorten: Mat.24:22, Mrk.13:20.† (AS)

kolon.notes: κῶλον, -ου, τό a limb, member of a body, [but in LXX (Lev.26:30, Num.14:29, 32-33, 1Ki.17:46, Isa.66:24*) for פֶּגֶר ;] hence, carcase: Heb.3:17 (LXX).† (AS)

kolonia.notes: κολωνία (Rec. -ώνια), -ας, ἡ (Lat. colonic), a colony: Act.16:12 (see Lft., Phi., 50 f.).† (AS)

kolossai.notes: Κολοσσαί (so in cl.; -ασσαί, Rec., LTr.), -ῶν, αἰ Colossæ, a city on the Lycus in Phrygia: Col.1:2.† (AS)

kolpos.notes: κόλπος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for חֵיק ;] __1. prop., the bosom: Jhn.13:23; fig., of close association, ἐν (εἰς τὸν) τοῖς κ. Ἀβραάμ, Luk.16:22-23 (cf. 4Ma.13:16); ὁ ὢν εἰς τὸν κ. τ. πατρός, Jhn.1:18 __2. The bosom…

kolumbao.notes: κολυμβάω, -ῶ [in AI.: Isa.25:11 * ;] to dive, plunge into the sea: Act.27:43 (cf. ἐκ-κολνμβάω).† (AS)

kolumbethra.notes: κολυμβήθρα, -ας, ἡ (κολνμβάω), [in LXX for בְּרֵכָה, 4Ki.18:27, al. ;] a swimming pool, pool: Jhn.5:2, 4 5:7 9:7.† (AS)

koluo.notes: κωλύω (κόλος), [in LXX for כָּלָא, מָנַע, etc. ;] to hinder, re­strain, forbid, withhold: with accusative and inf., Mat.19:14, Luk.23:2, Act.8:36 16:6 24:23, 1Th.2:16, Heb.7:23; inf. om., Mrk.9:38-39 10:14,…

kome.notes: κόμη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Num.6:5, Eze.44:20 (פֶּרַע), al. ;] the hair: 1Co.11:15.† (AS)

komizo.notes: κομίζω [in LXX for נָשָׂא, etc. ;] __1. to take care of. __2. to carry off safe. __3. to carry off as booty. __4. to bear or carry: Luk.7:37. Mid., to bear for oneself, hence, __(a) to receive: Heb.10:36 11:13, 39,…

komopolis.notes: κωμόπολις, -εως, ἡ a country town: Mrk.1:38 (see Swete, in l).† (AS)

komos.notes: κῶμος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Wis.14:23, 2Ma.6:4 * ;] a revel, carousal: Rom.13:13, Gal.5:21, 1Pe.4:3.† SYN.: see: κραιπάλη (AS)

koniao.notes: κονιάω, -ῶ (κονία, dust, lime), [in LXX: Deu.27:2, 4 (שִׂיד), Pro.21:9 * ;] to plaster or whiten over: of tombs, Mat.23:27; figuratively, of a hypocrite, Act.23:3.† (AS)

koniortos.notes: κονιορτός, -οῦ, ὁ (κόνις or κονία, dust, ὄρνυμι, to stir up), [in LXX for אָבָק, etc. ;] in cl., dust stirred up (Exo.9:9, Isa.5:24); in NT, simply dust: Mat.10:14, Luk.9:5 10:11, Act.13:51 22:23.† (AS)

konops.notes: a gnat, mosquito , Lat. culex, (Herdotus Historicus), etc. (ML)

kopazo.notes: κοπάζω (κόπος), [in LXX: Gen.8:1, Est.2:1 7:10 (שָׁכַךְ), Rut.1:18, al. (חָדַל), חָדַל), Jon.1.11-12 (שָׁתַק), al. ;] to grow weary; of the wind (Hdt., Jh., l.with), to abate, cease raging: Mat.14:32, Mrk.4:39 6:51.†…

kope.notes: κοπή, -ῆς, ἡ (κόπτω), [in LXX: Gen.14:17, Jos.10:20 (נָכָה hi., מַכָּה), Deu.28:25 (נָגַף), Jdth.15:7 * ;] __1. in cl., a stroke, a pounding (as in a mortar). __2. In LXX, a smiting in battle: Heb.7:1 (LXX).† (AS)

kopetos.notes: κοπετός, -οῦ, ὁ (κόπτω, mid.), [in LXX chiefly for מִסְפֵּד ;] = cl. κομμός, a beating of the head and breast, lamentation: before ἐπί with dative of person(s), Act.8:2.† (AS)

kophinos.notes: κόφινος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Jdg.6:19 (סַל), Psa.81:6 (דּוּד)* ;] a basket, probably of wicker-work, such as were carried by Jews for food: Mat.14:20 16:9, Mrk.6:43 8:19 9:17 13:8, Jhn.6:13 (cf. σφυρίς).† (AS)

kophos.notes: κωφός, -ή, -όν (κόπτω), [in LXX chiefly (Exo.4:11, al.) for אִלֵּם; for חָרָשׁ, Hab.2:18 ;] blunt, dull. Metaphorical, of the senses, esp. __(a) of speech, dumb: Mat.9:32-33 12:22 15:30-31, Luk.1:22 11:14; __(b) of…

kopiao.notes: κοπιάω, -ῶ (κόπος), [in LXX chiefly for יָגַע ;] __1. (as in cl.) to grow weary: Mat.11:28, Jhn.4:6, Rev.2:3 (cf. Isa.40:31). __2. Hence, in LXX and NT, to work with effort, to toil: absol., Mat.6:23, Luk.5:5 12:27,…

kopos.notes: κόπος, -ου, ὁ (κόπτω) [in LXX chiefly for עָמָל, also for אָוֶן, etc. ;] __1. a striking, beating (in Jer.45:3 = κοπετός) __2. laborious toil, trouble: Jhn.4:38, 1Co.3:8 15:58, 1Th.3:5; κ. τ. ἀγάπης, 1Th.1:3; ἔργα…

kopria.notes: κοπρία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for אַשְׁפֹּת ;] a dung hill (1Ki.2:8, Psa.113:7 al.): Luk.14:35.† κόπριον, -ου, τό, [in LXX (pl.): Jer.25:33 (דֹּמֶן), Sir.22:2, 1Ma.2:62 * ;] = κόπρος, dung: pl., Luk.13:8 (WH, mg.,…

kopto.notes: κόπτω [in LXX for כָּרַת, סָפַד, נָכָה hi., etc. ;] __1. to strike, smite. __2. to cut off: with accusative of thing(s), before ἀπό, Mat.21:8; ἐκ, Mrk.11:8. Mid., to beat one's breast with grief, to mourn, bewail:…

korasion.notes: κοράσιον, -ου, τό (dimin. of κόρη), [in LXX chiefly for נַעֲרָה, Rut.2:8, al.; in Joel 4:3, Zec.8:5 for יַלְדָּה ;] a colloquial word which survives in MGr. (Kennedy, Sourcs, e154), girl, maiden: Mat.9:24-25 14:11,…

korax.notes: κόραξ, -ακος, ὁ [in LXX for עֹרֵב ;] a raven: Luk.12:24.† (AS)

korban.notes: κορβάν (Rec., T, -βᾶν), indecl. (Heb. קׇרְבָּן), an offering, a gift offered to God: Mrk.7:11 (cf. κορβανᾶς).† (AS)

kore.notes: Κορέ indecl. (in FlJ, -έου), ὁ (Heb. קֶרַח) Korah: Ju 11.† (AS)

korennumi.notes: κορέννυμι (κόρος, surfeit), [in Symmachus.: Psa.22:27, Al, Psa.103:5 * ;] to satisfy: pass., τροφῆς, Act.27:38; metaphorically, of spiritual things, 1Co.4:8.† (AS)

korinthios.notes: κορίνθιος, -α, -ον Corinthian; as subst., ὁ Κ., a Corinthian: Act.18:8 27:1-44 (WH, txt., R, omit), 2Co.6:11; 1Co, 2Co, tit.† (AS)

koros.notes: κόρος, -ου, ὁ (Heb. כֹּר), [in LXX: Lev.27:16, Num.11:32, Eze.45:13 (חֹמֶר); more freq., 2Ch.27:5, al.(כֹּר) ;] a cor, a Hebrew measure (AV, RV, measure): Luk.16:7.† (AS)

kos.notes: Κῶς genitive Κῶ, ἡ, Cos, an island in the Ægean Sea: accusative Κῶ, Act.21:1 (Κῶν, Rec.).† (AS)

kosmeo.notes: κοσμέω, -ῶ (κόσμος), [in LXX for עָדָה, תָּקַן, etc. ;] __1. to order, arrange, prepare (in Hom. esp. of marshalling armies): Mat.25:7 (cf. Sir.50:9, al.). __2. to adorn, furnish: οἶκον, pass., Mat.12:44, Luk.11:25;…

kosmikos.notes: κοσμικός, -ή, -όν (κόσμος) __1. pertaining to the world or universe. __2. of this world, earthly. Heb.9:1. __3. In ethical sense, worldly: ἐπιθυμίαι, Tit.2:12.† (AS)

kosmios.notes: κόσμιος, -ον (in cl. -α, -ον), (κόσμος), [in LXX: Ecc.12:9 (תָּקַן)* ;] orderly, decent, modest: 1Ti.2:9 (WH, mg., -ίως, which see) 1Ti.3:2.† (AS)

kosmokrator.notes: κοσμοκράτωρ, -ορος, ὁ (κόσμος, κρατέω), __1. in Orphic hymns, al. (see AR, Eph., l.with), a ruler of the whole world (and so in Rabbinic writings: קוֺזמוֺקְרָטוֺר). __2. a ruler of this world (in contrast to παντο…

koumi.notes: κούμ (Tr., txt., κοῦμ, Rec. κοῦμι) (Heb. imperat. masc., used as an interjection, קוּם), koum (arise): Mrk.5:41.† (AS)

kouphizo.notes: κουφίζω (κοῦφος, light), [in LXX: Exo.18:22, Jon.1.5, al. (קָלַל hi.), Est.5:1, al. ;] __1. intrans., to be light (poetic chiefly). __2. to lighten, make light: with accusative, Act.27:38.† (AS)

koustodia.notes: κουστωδία, -ας, ἡ (Lat. custodia), a guard: Mat.27:65-66 28:11.† (AS)

krabbatos.notes: κράβαττος (Rec. κράββατος), ου, ὁ (a Macedonian word, = Lat. grabatus, cl. σκίμπους), [in Aquila.: Amo.3:12 * ;] a camp bed, pallet: Mrk.2:4, 9 2:11-12 6:55, Jhn.5:8-11, Act.5:15 9:33 (see Swete, Mk., 24, and cf.…

kraipale.notes: κραιπάλη (κρεπάλη, WH), -ης, ἡ (cf. κραιπαλάω for שִׁכּוֹר, Isa.24:20 29:9) drunken nausea (EV, surfeiting): Luk.21:34.† SYN.: κῶμος, revelling; μέθη, drunkeness; οἰνοφλυγία, a debauch; πότος, a drinking bout (see…

kranion.notes: κρανίον, -ου, τό (κάρα, the head), [in LXX: Jdg.9:53, 4Ki.9:35 (גֻּלְגֹּלֶת)* ;] a skull: Mat.27:33, Mrk.15:22, Luk.23:33, Jhn.19:17.† (AS)

kraspedon.notes: κράσπεδον, -ου, τό [in LXX: Num.15:38-39 (צִיצִת) Deu.22:12, Zec.8:23 (כָּנָף)* ;] __1. in cl., an edge, border. __2. In LXX (see supr.) and NT, a tassel or corner: Mat.9:20 14:36 23:5, Mrk.6:56, Luk.8:44.† (AS)

krataioo.notes: κραταιόω, -ῶ (late form of κρατύνω; κράτος) [in LXX chiefly for חָזַק ;] to strengthen; pass., to wax strong: Luk.2:40, 1Co.16:13; πνεύματι, Luk.1:80; δυνάμει κ. διὰ τ. πνεύματος, Eph.3:16.† (AS)

krataios.notes: κραταιός, -ά, -όν (κράτος), (in cl. poët, form of κρατερός), [in LXX chiefly for חָזַק and cognates ;] strong, mighty: 1Pe.5:6.† (AS)

krateo.notes: κρατέω, -ῶ (κράτος), [in LXX chiefly for חָזַק hi., also for אָחַז, etc. ;] __1. to be strong, mighty, hence, to rule, be master, prevail (so chiefly in cl.; in LXX: Est.1:1, I Est.4:38, Wis.14:19, al.). __2. to get…

kratistos.notes: κράτιστος, -η, -ον superl, of κρατύς (Hom.), __1. strongest, mightiest (Hom.). __2. noblest, best (cf. κρείσσων), most excellent (find., Soph., al.): voc., κρατίστε, as title of honour and respect (DCG, ii, 727a),…

kratos.notes: κράτος, -εος (-ους), τό [in LXX chiefly for עֹז, Job.12:16, Psa.90:11 al. ;] __1. strength, esp. as in Hom., of bodily strength. __2. power, might: Heb.2:14; τὸ κ. τῆς ἰσχύος αὐτοῦ, Eph.1:19 6:10 (Isa.40:26, Da TH…

kraugazo.notes: κραυγάζω (κραυγή) [in LXX: Est.3:13 (רוּעַ hi.)* ;] = κράζω __1. of animals, to bay, to croak, etc. __2. Of men, to cry out, shout: Mat.12:19, Jhn.12:13 19:15 Act.22:23; before λέγων, Jhn.18:40 19:6, 12; καὶ λέγων,…

krauge.notes: κραυγή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for צְוָחָה, תְּרוּעָה, שַׁוְעָה, etc. ;] crying, outcry, clamour: Mat.25:6, Luk.1:42, Act.23:9, Eph.4:31, Heb.5:7, Rev.21:4.† (AS)

krazo.notes: κράζω [in LXX for זָעַק, צָעַק, קָרָא, etc. ;] in cl. chiefly poët., __1. prop. onomatop., of the raven, to croak, hence generally, of inartic. cries, to scream, cry out (Æsch., al.): Mrk.5:5 9:26 15:39 (Rec., R,…

kreas.notes: κρέας, -έως, pl., κρέα [in LXX for בָּשָׂר (freq.) ;] flesh, meat: Rom.14:21, 1Co.8:13.† (AS)

kreisson.notes: κρείσσων (Epic and old Att..) and κρείττων (later Att..), prop. comparat. of κρατύς, strong, but in sense often (as in cl., see LS, see word) as comparat. of ἀγαθός, [in LXX chiefly for טוֹב ;] better; __(a) as to…

kreitton.notes: κρείσσων (Epic and old Att..) and κρείττων (later Att..), prop. comparat. of κρατύς, strong, but in sense often (as in cl., see LS, see word) as comparat. of ἀγαθός, [in LXX chiefly for טוֹב ;] better; __(a) as to…

kremao.notes: κρεμάννυμι (also κρεμαννύω, κρεμάω; the Pres. is not found in NT), [in LXX (where also κρεμάζω, Job.26:7) chiefly for תָּלָה ;] trans., to hang, suspend: with accusative of person(s), before ἐπὶ ξύλου, Act.5:30…

kremnos.notes: κρημνός, -οῦ, ὁ (κρεμάννυμι), [in LXX: 2Ch.25:12 (סֶלַע)* ;] a steep bank: Mat.8:32, Mrk.5:13, Luk.8:33.† (AS)

kres.notes: Κρής, ὁ, pl., Κρῆτες a Cretan: Act.2:11, Tit.1:12.† (AS)

krima.notes: κρίμα (B1., § 27, 2), -τος, τό (κρίνω), [in LXX chiefly for מִשְׁפָּט ;] the issue of a judicial process, hence, __1. judgment, the decision passed on the faults of others: Mat.7:2; in forensic sense, with genitive…

krino.notes: κρίνω, [in LXX chiefly for שׁפט, also for ריב ,דּין, etc. ;] __1. to separate, select, choose (cl.; in LXX: 2Ma.13:15). __2. to approve, esteem: Rom.14:5. __3. to be of opinion, judge, think: Luk.7:43, 1Co.11:13;…

krinon.notes: κρίνον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for שׁוּשַׁן ;] a lily: Mat.6:28, Luk.12:27.† (AS)

krisis.notes: κρίσις, -εως, ἡ (κρίνω), [in LXX chiefly for מִשְׁפָּט, also for רִיב, etc. ;] __1. a seperating, selection (Arist., al.) __2. a decision, judgment (cl.): Jhn.8:16, 1Ti.5:24, 2Pe.2:11, Ju 9; κ. κρίνειν, Jhn.7:24; in…

kriterion.notes: κριτήριον, -ου, τό (κριτής), [in LXX: 3Ki.7:7 (מִשְׁפָּט), Dan LXX TH Dan.7:10, 26, (דִּין), Su 1:49, Exo.21:6, Jdg.5:10 * ;] __1. a means of judging, test, criterion. __2. a tribunal: 1Co.6:2 6:4, Jas.2:6.† (AS)

krites.notes: κριτής, -οῦ, ὁ (κρίνω), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁפַט ;] a judge: Mat.5:25, Luk.12:14, 58 18:2; τ. ἀδικίας, Luk.18:6; with gem of thing(s) (obj.), Act.18:15, Jas.4:11; (qual.), διαλογισμῶν πονηρῶν, Jas.2:4; of a Roman…

krithe.notes: κριθή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for שְׂעֹרָה ;] barley: pl. (as usually in cl.), Rev.6:6.† (AS)

krithinos.notes: κρίθινος, -η, -ον (κριθή), [in LXX for שְׂעֹרָה ;] of barley: Jhn.6:9, 13 (AS)

kritikos.notes: κριτικός, -ή, -όν (κρίνω), critical, able to discern or judge: with genitive obj., Heb.4:12.† (AS)

krouo.notes: Κρούω [in LXX: before ἐπί Jdg.19:22, Sng.5:2 (דָּפַק) ;] with, accusative, τ. αὐλαίαν, Jdth.14:14 * ;] to strike, knock: at a door (Κόπτειν, in Att..), Mat.7:7-8, Luk.11:9-10 12:36, Act.12:16, Rev.3:20; with…

kruphe.notes: κρυΦῇ (prop. -ῆ, Rec.; later spelling is due to assimilation to dative; cf. εἰκῆ), adv., [in LXX chiefly for סָתַר ;] secretly, in secret: Eph.5:12.† (AS)

krupte.notes: κρύπτη (WH, R; κρυπτή, LT, Tr.; -όν, Rec.), -ης, ἡ, a crypt, cellar: Luk.11:33.† (AS)

krupto.notes: Κρύπτω [in LXX for חָבָא, טָמַן, סָתַר, צָפַן, etc. ;] to hide, conceal: with accusative, Mat.13:44 25:18; before ἐν, Mat.25:25 (pass., Mat.13:44, Col.3:3); pass., Mat.5:14, Heb.11:23, 1Ti.5:25, Rev.2:17; ἐκρύβη (on…

kruptos.notes: κρυπτός, -ή, -όν (κρύπτω) [in LXX for אָטַם, מִסְתָּר, etc. ;] hidden, secret: Mat.10:26, Mrk.4:22, Luk.8:17 12:2; ὁ κ. τῆς καρδίας ἄνθρωπος, 1Pe.3:4; neut., ἐν τῷ κ., Mat.6:4, 6; ἐν κ., Jhn.7:4, 10 18:20; ὁ ἐν κ.…

krustallizo.notes: κρυσταλλίζω (κρύσταλλος), to shine like crystal, be crystal-clear: Rev.21:11 (ἅπ, λεγ.).† (AS)

krustallos.notes: κρύσταλλος, -ου, ὁ (κρύος, frost), [in LXX: Job.38:29, Psa.147:17, Eze.1:22 (קֶרַח), etc. ;] crystal: Rev.4:6 22:1.† (AS)

ktaomai.notes: κτάομαι, -ῶμαι [in LXX chiefly for קָנָה ;] in pres., impf., fut. and aor., to procure for oneself, get, gain, acquire (the pf. and plpf., to have acquired, hence to possess, do not occur in NT): with accusative of…

ktema.notes: κτῆμα, -τος, τό (κτάομαι), [in LXX for כֶּרֶם, etc. ;] a possession, property: Mat.19:22, Mrk.10:22, Act.2:45 5:1.† (AS)

ktenos.notes: κτῆνος, -ους, τό (κτάομαι, hence primarily a possession), [in LXX chiefly for בְּהֵמָה Gen.1:25, al., also for מִקְנֶה, צֹנֵא, etc. ;] a beast, (in late Gk. esp.) a beast of burden: Luk.10:34 pl. (as chiefly in cl.),…

ktetor.notes: κτήτωρ, -ορος, ὁ (κτάομαι) [in Sm.: Jol.1:11 * ;] a possessor: Act.4:34.† (AS)

ktisis.notes: κτίσις, -εως, ἡ (κτίζω), [in LXX: Psa.104:24 (קִנְיָן), Pro.1:13 A (הוֹן), Tob.8:5, Wis.2:6, Sir.16:17, 3Ma.2:2, al. ;] __1. a founding, settling, foundation (cl.). __2. In LXX and NT, __(a) the act of creating,…

ktisma.notes: κτίσμα, -τος, τό (κτίζω), [in LXX: Wis.9:2 13:5 14:11, Sir.36:20 (17), Sir.38:34, 3Ma.5:11 * ;] __1. (cl.) a colony. __2. a created thing, creature: 1Ti.4:4, Jas.1:18, Rev.5:13 8:9.† (AS)

ktistes.notes: κτίστης, -oυ, ὁ (κτίζω), [in LXX: 2Ki.22:32 (Heb., al.), Jdth.9:12, Sir.24:8, 2 24:1-34; 4Ma.5:1-38 * ;] __1. (cl.) a founder. __2. a creator: of God, 1Pe.4:19.† (AS)

ktizo.notes: κτίζω [in LXX chiefly for בָּרָא, Psa.51:10, al.; also for קָנָה, Gen.14:19, Pro.8:22; יָצַר, Isa.22:11 46:11; Wis.2:23, Sir.1:4, 9 (and freq.), I Est.4:53, a1. ;] __1. in cl., to people or found a region or city…

kubeia.notes: κυβία (Mt. -εία, and so Rec.), -ας, ἡ (κύβος, a cube, a die), dice-playing; metaphorically, trickery, sleight: Eph.4:14.† (AS)

kubernesis.notes: κυβέρνησις, -εως, ἡ (κυβερνάω, Lat. gubernare, to guide), [in LXX: Pro.1:5 11:14 24:6 (תַּחְבֻּלָה) * ;] __1. steering, pilotage (Plat.). __2. Metaphorical, government: pl., 1Co.12:28.† (AS)

kubernetes.notes: κυβερνήτης, -ου, ὁ (see: κυβέρνησις), [in LXX for חֹבֵל, Eze.27:8, 27-28, Pro.23:24, 4Ma.7:1 * ;] __1. a steersman, pilot: Act.27:11, Rev.18:17. __2. Metaphorical, a guide, governor (Eur., Plat.).† (AS)

kuklo.notes: κύκλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for סָבִיב ;] a ring, circle. Dat., κύκλῳ, as adverb., round about, around: Mrk.3:34 6:6, 36, Luk.9:12, Rom.15:19; with genitive, Rev.4:6 5:11 7:11† (AS)

kukloo.notes: κυκλόω, -ῶ (κύκλος), [in LXX chiefly for סָבַב ;] __1. to move in a circle, revolve. __2. to surround, encircle: with accusative of person(s), Jhn.10:24 (WH, txt., cf. -εύω), Act.14:20; pass., Luk.21:20, Heb.11:30…

kulio.notes: κυλίω late form of κυλίνδω, [in LXX: Jos.10:18, 1Ki.14:33, al. (גָּלַל), 4Ki.9:33 (שָׁמַט), al. ;] to roll, roll along. Pass., to be rolled; of persons (in Hom, as sign of grief), to roll or wallow: Mrk.9:20.† (AS)

kulisma.notes: κύλισμα, -τος, τό (κυλίω), [in Sm.: Eze.10:13 * ;] __1. a roll. __2. = κυλισμός (which see), a rolling, wallowing (or, as κυλίστρα, Xen., Eq., see 3, a rolling place): 2Pe.2:22 (Rec.).† (AS)

kullos.notes: κυλλός, -ή, -όν __1. crooked, crippled: Mat.15:30-31 (WH, txt. om.); __2. maimed: Mat.18:8, Mrk.9:43.† (AS)

kuma.notes: κῦμα, -τος, τό (κύω, to be pregnant, to swell) [in LXX chiefly for גַּל ;] a wave: pl., Mat.8:24 14:24, Mrk.4:37; κ. θαλάσσης, figuratively, Ju 13.† SYN.: κλύδων, q.v (AS)

kumbalon.notes: κύμβαλον, -ου, τό (κύμβη, a cup), [in LXX chiefly for מְצִלָּה ;] a cymbal: 1Co.13:1.† (AS)

kuminon.notes: κύμινον, -ου, τό [in LXX: Isa.28:25, 27 (כַּמֹּן)* ;] cumin: Mat.23:23.† (AS)

kunarion.notes: κυνάριον, -ου, τό (in Att.. also κυνίδιον, diminutive of κύων) a little dog: Mat.15:26-27 Mrk.7:27-28 † (AS)

kuon.notes: κύων, κυνός, ὁ, ἡ (in NT masc. only), [in LXX forכֶּלֶב ;] a dog: Luk.16:21, 2Pe.2:22; metaphorically, as a word of reproach, Mat.7:6, Php.3:2, Rev.22:15.† (AS)

kupto.notes: κύπτω [in LXX chiefly for קָדַד ;] to bow the head, stoop down: Mrk.1:7; before κάτω, Jhn.8:6, 8 (cf. ἀνα-, παρα-, συν-κύπτω).† κατα-κύπτω, [in LXX: 4Ki.9:32 (שָׁקַף hi.)* ;] to bend down, stoop: Jhn.8:8 (κάτω κύψας,…

kurenaios.notes: Κυρηναῖος, -α, -ον (Κυρήνη), of Cyrene, a Cyrenæan: Mat.27:32, Mrk.15:21, Luk.23:2, Act.6:9 11:20 13:1.† (AS)

kurene.notes: Κυρήνη, -ης, ἡ Cyrene, a city in Libya: Act.2:10.† (AS)

kurenios.notes: Κυρήνιος (prop. -ίνιος, see Bl., 13; -ῖνος L, -εῖνος, Tr., WH, mg.), -ου, ὁ, Quirinus, prop. Quirinius: Luk.2:2.† (AS)

kuria.notes: κυρία (Κυρία, T, WH, mg.), -ας, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for גְּבֶרֶת ;] a lady: 2Jn.1 Jn 5 (on the interpretation, see ICC, 167 ff.).† (AS)

kuriakos.notes: κυριακός, -ή, -όν (κύριος), [in LXX: κ. φωνη (Συριακή φ., R), 2Ma.15:36 A * ;] __1. as frequently in Inscr. (LS, see word; Deiss., BS, 217 f.), of the lord or master, imperial. __2. of the Lord (i.e. Christ):…

kurieuo.notes: κυριεύω (κύριος), [in LXX chiefly for מָשַׁל ;] to be lord or master of, to rule (over): C. genitive obj., Luk.22:25, Rom.14:9, 2Co.1:24; absol., 1Ti.6:15; metaphorically, ὁ θάνατος, Rom.6:9; ἡ ἁμαρτία, Rom.6:14; ὁ…

kurios.notes: κύριος, -α, -ον (also -ος, -ον), [in LXX (subst.) chiefly for יהוה, also for בַּעַל ,אָדוֹן, etc. ;] having power (κῦρος) or authority; as subst., ὁ κ., lord, master; __1. in general: with genitive of thing(s),…

kuriotes.notes: κυριότης, -ητος, ἡ (κύριος), lordship, dominion: Eph.1:21, 2Pe.2:10, Ju 8; pl., Col.1:16 (cf. Lft., Col; Mayor, Ju., in ll.; DB, i, 616 f.).† (AS)

kuroo.notes: κυρόω, -ῶ (κῦρος, 1. authority. 2. validity), [in LXX for קוּם ;] to confirm, ratify, make valid: 2Co.2:8; pass., Gal.3:15 (cf. προ-κυρόω).† (AS)

lachanon.notes: λάχανον, -ου, τό (λαχαίνω, to dig), [in LXX: Gen.9:3; 3Ki.21:2, Psa.37:2, Pro.15:17 (יֶרֶק, יָרָק) * ;] a garden herb, vegetable: Luk.11:42; usually in pl., Mat.13:32, Mrk.4:32, Rom.14:2.† SYN.: βοτάνη, q.v (AS)

lagchano.notes: λαγχάνω [in LXX: 1Ki.14:47 (לָכַד, see Th., Gr., 38), Wis.8:19, 3Mac 61 * ;] __1. to obtain by lot, to obtain (in cl., with genitive): with inf. art. (B1., § 36, 3; 71, 3), Luk.1:9; with accusative of thing(s),…

lailaps.notes: λαῖλαψ, -απος, ἡ [in LXX: Job.21:18 27:20 א (סוּפָה) Job.38:1, Jer.25:32 (סַעַר), Wis.5:14, 23, Sir.48:9, 12 * ;] a hurricane, whirlwind: Mrk.4:37, Luk.8:23, 2Pe.2:17.† SYN.: θύελλα, which see, and cf. ἄνεμος (AS)

laktizo.notes: λακτίζω (λάξ, with the foot), to kick: Act.26:14.† (AS)

laleo.notes: λαλέω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for דּבר pi., also for אמר, etc. ;] __1. to utter: of inanimate things, Rev.4:1 10:4; metaphorically, Heb.11:4 12:24. __2. to talk, speak, say: absol., Mat.9:33 12:46, Mrk.5:35, Luk.8:49;…

lalia.notes: λαλία, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for דָּבָר, דִּבְרָה ;] __1. loquacity = Very talkative. __2. talk, speech, conversation: Mat.26:73 (cf. Ca 43), Jhn.4:42 8:43.† (AS)

lama.notes: λαμά (Heb. מָה, seel. λεμά = Aram. לְמָא) why: Mat.27:46 (WH, mg., λεμά TTr., WH, txt., λεμά L), Mrk.15:34 (λεμά LT, λαμμᾶ, Rec.).† (AS)

lambano.notes: λαμβάνω, [in LXX chiefly for לקח, also for אחז ,לכד ,נשׂא, etc. ;] __1. to take, lay hold of: absol., Mat.26:26, Mrk.14:22; with accusative of thing(s), Mat.5:40 26:52, al. mult.; with accusative of person(s),…

lamech.notes: Λάμεχ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. לֶמֶךְ), Lamech (Gen.5:25): Luk.3:36.† (AS)

lampas.notes: λαμπάς, -άδος, ἡ (λάμπω), [in LXX for לַפִּיד ;] a torch (frequently fed, like a lamp, with oil): Mat.25:1 ff., Jhn.18:3, Act.20:8, Rev.4:5 8:10.† (AS)

lampo.notes: λάμπω [in LXX for נָגַהּ, etc. ;] to shine: Mat.5:15-16 17:2, Luk.17:24, Act.12:7, 2Co.4:6 (cf. ἐκ-, περι-λάμπω).† (AS)

lampros.notes: λαμπρός, -ά, -όν (λάμπω), [in LXX: Tob.13:11, Wis.6:12 17:20, Sir.29:22 33:13 (Sir.30:25) Sir.31:23, Ep. Je 60 * ;] bright, brilliant: ποταμός, Rev.22:1 (ΕΥ, clear); ἀστήρ, Rev.22:16; of clothing, brilliant,…

lamprotes.notes: λαμπρότης, -ητος, ἡ [in LXX: Psa.110:3 (הָדָר), al. ;] brightness, brilliancy: τ. ἡλίου, Act.26:13.† (AS)

lanthano.notes: λανθάνω [in LXX for עָלַם ni., etc. ;] to escape notice, be hidden (from): Mrk.7:24, Luk.8:47; with accusative of person(s), Act.26:26, 2Pe.3:5 3:8; as in common cl. idiom, before ptcp., ἔλαθον ξενίσαντες,…

laodikeia.notes: Λαοδικία (Rec. -εία), -ας, ἡ Laodicea, a city on the Lycus in Phrygia: Col.2:1 4:13, 15-16, Rev.1:11 3:14.† (AS)

laos.notes: λαός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX very frequently for עַם, Gen.14:16, al.; occasionally for לְאֹם ( Gen.25:23, al.), etc. ;] a word rarely found in Att. prose; __1. the people at large (Hom., al.), esp. of people assembled:…

larugx.notes: λάρυγξ, -γγος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for חֵךְ, Job.6:30, al; Psa.5:9 (גָּרוֹן) ;] the larynx, throat: metaphorically, of speech (cf. Sir.6:5), Rom.3:13" (LXX) .† (AS)

lasaia.notes: Λασέα (Rec. -αία), -ας, ἡ Lasea, a city of Crete, otherwise un­known: Act.27:8.† (AS)
