lascho.notes: λάσκω __1. (in cl., poët.) to clang, crash, crack; in late prose, to crack or burst noisily: Act.1:18 (ἐλάκησεν, perh., however, from λακέω, which see; Bl., in l). __2. (in cl., prose) to scream, shout.† λακέω, Dor.…

lathra.notes: λάθρᾳ (Att..; in Hom., -ρῃ, Rec. -ρα), adv., [in LXX chiefly for ‎סֵתֶר ;] secretly: Mat.1:19 2:7, Mrk.5:33 (WH, mg.), Jhn.11:28, Act.16:37.† (AS)

latomeo.notes: λατομέω, -ῶ (λατόμος, a stonecutter, 4Ki.12:12, al.; λᾶς, τέμνω; cf. λαξευτός) [in LXX: 1Ch.22:2, al. (חָצַב); Exo.21:33, Num_21:18 (כָּרָה) ;] to hew, hew out stones: Mat.27:60, Mrk.15:46.† (AS)

latreia.notes: λατρεία, -ας, ἡ (λατρεύω, which see), [in LXX (always of divine service): Exo.12:25-26 13:5, Jos.22:27, 1Ch.28:13 (עֲבֹדָה), 1Ma.1:43 1Mac 2:19 1Mac 2:22, 3Ma.4:14 * ;] __1. hired service, service. __2. (in cl. also)…

latreuo.notes: λατρεύω (λάτρις, a hired servant), [in LXX (always, as λατρεία, of the service of God or of heathen divinities) chiefly for עָבַד, Exo.3:12, al.; in Dan LXX TH (Dan.3:12, al.) for פְּלַח ;] __1. to work for hire.…

laxeutos.notes: λαξευτός, -ή, όν (λαξεύω; λᾶς, a stone, ξέω, to scrape), [in LXX: Deu.4:49 (פִּסְגָּה); in Aq.: Num.21:20; Th.: Jdg.7:11 * ;] hewn (in stone); Luk.23:53 (elsewhere κοινή writers use λατομητός, 4Ki.12:12, al.; cf.…

lazaros.notes: Λάζαρος, -ου, ὁ colloquial abbreviation of Ἐλεαζάρ (άζαρος), which see), Lazarus; __1. of Bethany: Jhn.11:1 ff. Jhn.12:1-2, 9-10 12:17. __2. The beggar in the parable: Luk.16:20, 23-25.† (AS)

legeon.notes: λεγιών (Rec. -εών), -ῶνος, ἡ, (Lat. legio), a legion: Mat.26:53, Mrk.5:9, 15, Luk.8:30.† (AS)

leimma.notes: λεῖμμα (WH, λίμμα, see their App., 154), -τος, τό (λείπω), [in LXX: 4Ki.19:4 A (שְׁאֵרִית)* ;] a remnant: Rom.11:5.† (AS)

leios.notes: λεῖος, -εία, -εῖον [in LXX: Gen.27:11, 1Ki.17:40 R (חָלַק, חַלֻּק); ὁδὸς λ., Isa.40:4 Α (בִּקְעָה); Pro.2:20 12:13 26:23 * ;] smooth: opposite to τραχύς, Luk.3:5 (LXX).† (AS)

leipo.notes: λείπω [in LXX: Job.4:11, Pro.19:4 (פָּרַד), al. ;] __1. trans., to leave, leave behind; pass., to be left behind, to lack before prep. (as more usual in cl.), ἐν, Jas.1:4; with genitive of thing(s), Jas.1:5 2:15.…

leitourgeo.notes: λειτουργέω, -ῶ (λειτουργός), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁרַת pi., also for עָבַד, צָבָא, etc. ;] __1. in cl., at Athens, to supply public offices at one's own cost, render public service to the State, hence, generally,…

leitourgia.notes: λειτουργία, -ας, ἡ (λειτουργέω), [in LXX chiefly for עֲבֹדָה, Num.4:24, 1Ch.9:13, al. ;] __1. in cl. (chiefly of Athens), the discharge of a public office at one's own expense (see LS, see word), hence, __2. a…

leitourgikos.notes: λειτουργικός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for עֲבֹדָה, שָׁרַת; ἔργα, Num_7:5; στολαί Exo.31:10 39:32; σκευή, Num.4:12, 26, 2Ch.24:14 * ;] of or for service, ministering: πνεύματα, Heb.1:14.† (AS)

leitourgos.notes: λειτουργός, -οῦ, ὁ (λιός, ἔργον), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁרַת, Jos.1:1 A, 3Ki.10:5, Psa.103:21, Sir.7:30, al. ;] __1. in cl., one who discharges a public office at his own expense, then, generally, __2. a public…

lenos.notes: ληνός, -οῦ, ἡ (in some MSS., LXX and NT, ὁ), [in LXX chiefly for יֶקֶב Num.18:27, al.; also for רַהַט (Gen.30:38, 41), גַּת (Neh.13:15, al.), etc. ;] a trough or vat; esp. for the treading of grapes: Mat.21:33,…

lention.notes: λέντιον, -ου, τό (Lat. linteum), a linen cloth, towel: Jhn.13:4-5.† (AS)

leon.notes: λέων, -οντος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for אֲרִי, אַרְיֵה, also for כְּפִיר, etc. ;] a lion: Heb.11:33, 1Pe.5:8, Rev.4:7 9:8, 17 10:3 13:2; metaphorically, 2Ti.4:17, Rev.5:5.† (AS)

lepis.notes: λεπίς, -ίδος, ἡ (λέπω, to peel) [in LXX chiefly for קַשְׂקֶשֶׂת ;] a scale: Act.9:18† (AS)

lepra.notes: λέπρα, -ας, ἡ (λεπρός), [in LXX for צָרַעַת ;] leprosy: Mat.8:3, Mrk.1:42, Luk.5:12-13† (AS)

lepros.notes: λεπρός, -ά, -όν (λεπίς), [in LXX for צָרַע ;] __1. (in cl.) scaly, rough. __2. leprous; chiefly as subst., ὁ λ., a leper: Mat.8:2 10:8 11:5, Mrk.1:40, Luk.4:27 7:22 17:12; of Simon, formerly a leper, Mat.26:6,…

lepsis.notes: λῆμψις (Rec. λῆψις, so in cl.), -εως, ἡ (λαμβάνω), [in LXX (λῆψ-): Pro.15:29; λ. δώρων, Pro.15:27 (מַתָּנָה); λ. καὶ δόσις, Sir.41:19 42:7 * ;] receiving: δόσις καὶ λ., Php.4:15.† (AS)

leptos.notes: λεπτός, -ή, -όν (λέπω, to peel), __1. peeled. __2. fine, thin, small, light; hence, in late Gk., as subst. τὸ λ., a small coin (one-eighth of an as, AV, mite): Mrk.12:42, Luk.12:59 21:2.† (AS)

leros.notes: λῆρος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: 4Ma.5:10 * ;] silly talk, nonsense: Luk.24:11.† (AS)

lestes.notes: λῃστής, -οῦ, ὁ (Ep. ληίς = λεία, booty), [in LXX for גְּדוּד, etc. ;] a robber, brigand: Mat.21:13 (LXX) Mat.26:55 27:38, 44, Mrk.11:17 14:48 15:27, Luk.10:30, 36 19:46 22:52, Jhn.10:1, 8 18:40, 2Co.11:26.† SYN.:…

lethe.notes: λήθη, -ης, ἡ (λήθω = λανθάνω) [in LXX: Lev.5:15 (מַעַל), Wis.16:11, Sir.14:7, al. ;] forgetfulness: λ. λαβεῖν (on the phrase, see Mayor, in l): 2Pe.1:9.† (AS)

leui.notes: Λευεί (indecl.) and Λευείς (Rec. -υΐ, -υΐς), genitive Λευεί, accusative -είν, ὁ (Heb. H3878) Levi; __1. the son of Jacob: Heb.7:5, 9, Rev.7:7. __2. Son of Melchi: Luk.3:24. __3. Son of Simeon: Luk.3:29. __4. Son of…

leuis.notes: Λευεί (indecl.) and Λευείς (Rec. -υΐ, -υΐς), genitive Λευεί, accusative -είν, ὁ (Heb. H3878) Levi; __1. the son of Jacob: Heb.7:5, 9, Rev.7:7. __2. Son of Melchi: Luk.3:24. __3. Son of Simeon: Luk.3:29. __4. Son of…

leuites.notes: Λευείτης (Rec. Λευΐτης), -ου, ὁ [in LXX for לֵוִיִּי ;] a Levite: Luk.10:32, Jhn.1:19, Act.4:36.† (AS)

leuitikos.notes: Λευειτικός (Rec. Λευϊτ-), -ή, -όν [in LXX: Le, tit.* ;] Levitical: Heb.7:11.† (AS)

leukaino.notes: λευκαίνω (λευκός), [in LXX: Psa.51:7, Isa.1:18 (לָבַן hi.), etc. ;] to whiten, make white: with accusative of thing(s), Mrk.9:3, Rev.7:14.† (AS)

leukos.notes: λευκο-βύσσινος, -ον (cf. λευκολινής, a robe of white flax, C.I., 155, 17), white linen: Rev.19:14 (WH, mg.).† λευκός, ή, -όν [in LXX chiefly for לָבָן ;] __1. bright, brilliant: of clothing, Mat.17:2, Mrk.9:3 16:5,…

lian.notes: λίαν, adv. [in LXX chiefly for מְאֹד Gen.1:31 4:5, Jer.24:3, al.; Tob.9:4, 2Ma.11:1, al. ;] very, exceedingly: Mat.2:16 4:8 8:28 27:14 Mrk.1:35 6:51 (Rec. λ. ἐκ περισσοῦ) Mrk.9:3 16:2, Luk.23:8, 2Ti.4:15, II Jn 4,…

libanos.notes: λίβανος, -ου, ὁ (rarely ἡ), (from the Semitic; cf. the Heb. equiv.), [in LXX for לְבוֹנָה Exo.30:34, Isa.60:6; Sir.24:15, al. ;] __1. in cl., the frankincense-tree. __2. (Occas. in cl.) frankincense (so Lev.2:1; cf.…

libanotos.notes: λιβανωτός, -οῦ, ὁ (λίβανος), [in LXX: 1Ch.9:29, 3Ma.5:2 * ;] __1. in cl. (and LXX), frankincense, the gum of the libanus. __2. = Late Gk., λιβανωτρίς, a censer: Rev.8:3, 5 (the same form appears in Inscr.; MM, xvi).†…

libertinos.notes: Λιβερῖτνος, -ου, ὁ (Lat. libertinus), a freedman: ἡ συναγωγὴ ἡ λεγομένη Λιβερτίνων Act.6:9 (Bl. thinks the original reading was Λιβυστίνων, Phil. Gosp., 69 f.).† (AS)

libue.notes: Libya, the north part of Africa west of Egypt, (Odyssey by Homer); in later writers also for the whole Continent (ML)

likmao.notes: λικμάω, -ῶ (λικμός = λίκνον, a winnowing-fan), [in LXX chiefly for זָרָה ni., pi., Rut.3:2 3Ki.14:15, Isa.17:13, Jer.31:10, Dan.2:44, al. ;] __1. in cl., to winnow (so Ru, l.with). __2. In LXX (ll with, exc. Ru), to…

limen.notes: λιμήν, -ένος, ὁ [in LXX: Psa.107:30 (מָחוֹז), Psa.107:35, 1Es.5:55 Est.5:1-2 Est.5:1-73 4Macc 7:1-23 * ;] a harbour, haven: Act.27:8, 12 (cf. Καλοὶ Λιμένες).† (AS)

limne.notes: λίμνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Psa.107:35 114:8 (אֲגַם), Sng.7:5 (בְּרֵכָה) 1Ma.11:35, 2Ma.12:16 * ;] a lake: of the Sea of Galilee (Mt, Mk, see: θάλασσα), Luk.5:2 8:22-23, 33; λ. Γεννησαρέτ (which see), Luk.5:1; λ. τ.…

limos.notes: λιμός, -οῦ, ὁ (so in Att..; in Dor. ἡ, and so sometimes in LXX, see Th., Gr., 146; in NT: Luk.15:14, Act.11:28; cf. M, Pr., 60), [in LXX chiefly for רָעָב ;] hunger, famine: Luk.4:25 15:14, 17, Act.7:11" (LXX)…

linon.notes: λίνον (Tr. λῖνον), -ου, τό [in LXX: Exo.9:31 (פִּשְׁתָּה), al. ;] __1. flax: Mat.12:20 (LXX). __2. linen: Rev.15:6 (R, mg., see: λίθος).† λίθος, -ου, ὁ (and, in Att.., of precious stones, ἡ.) [in LXX for אֶבֶן,…

linos.notes: 1. Linos , a mythical minstrel, son of Apollo and Urania (Calliope), teacher of Orpheus, (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus), etc. 2. as appellative, the song or lay of Linos , sung by a boy to the cithara while the…

liparos.notes: λιπαρός, -ά, -όν (λίπος, fat), [in LXX: Jdg.3:29, Neh.9:35, Is 30:23 (שָׁמֵן)* ;] oily, fatty; metaphorically, of living, rich, dainty: τὰ λ. καὶ τ. λαμπρά, Rev.18:14.† (AS)

lips.notes: λίψ, λιβός, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for נֶגֶב, also for תֵּימָן, מַעֲרָב, etc., Gen.13:14, Num.2:10, 2Ch.32:30, al. ;] the SW. wind: βλέποντα κατὰ λ., Act.27:12 (see Page, in l; Deiss., ES, 141).† (AS)

lithazo.notes: λιθάζω (λίθος), [in LXX (before ἐν λίθοις): 2Ki.16:6 16:13 (סָקַל pi.)* ;] __1. to throw stones (Arist., Polyb., al.). __2. = λιθοβολέω (LXX, NT), to pelt with stones, to stone: with accusative of person(s), Jhn.8:6…

lithinos.notes: λίθινος, -η, -ον (λίθος) [in LXX for אֶבֶן ;] of stone: Jhn.2:6, 2Co.3:3, Rev.9:20.† (AS)

lithoboleo.notes: λιθο-βολέω, -ῶ (λίθος, βάλλω), [in LXX for סָקַל, רָגַם, Exo.19:13, Lev.20:2, al. ;] to pelt with stones, to kill by stoning, to stone (cf. λιθάζω): with accusative of person(s), Mat.21:35 23:37, Luk.13:34…

lithos.notes: λίθος, -ου, ὁ (and, in Att.., of precious stones, ἡ.) [in LXX for אֶבֶן, Gen.11:3, al.; λ. τίμιος, for פָּז, Psa.19:10 21:3, Pro.8:19, al. ;] a stone: Mat.4:6, al.; pl., Mat.3:9, al.; at the entrance of a tomb,…

lithostrotos.notes: λιθό-στρωτος, -ον (στρώννυμι), [in LXX: 2Ch.7:3, Est.1:6, Sng.3:10 (רִצְפָּה, רָצַף)* ;] paved with stones, esp. of tessallated work (Ca, i.e.); as subst., τὸ λ., a tessallated pavement: Jhn.19:13 (cf. Γαββαθᾶ).† (AS)

litra.notes: λίτρα, -ας, ἡ (cf. Lat. libra), __1. a Sicilian coin = Rom. libra or as. __2. In weight, a pound: Jhn.12:3 19:39.† (AS)

logche.notes: λόγχη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for רֹמַח, etc. ;] __1. a spear-head. __2. a lance, spear: Mat.27:49 ([WH], R, mg.), Jhn.19:34.† (AS)

logia.notes: λογία (prop., -εία, see BS, 142 ff.), -ας, ἡ (λογεύω, to collect, a word found in π., see Deiss., BS, l.with; LAE, 70, 103; MM, xvi), a collection: 1Co.16:1-2.† (AS)

logikos.notes: λογικός, -ή, -όν (λόγος, reason) reasonable, rational: λατρεία, Rom.12:1; τὸ λ. (i.e. spiritual) γάλα (see Hort, in l; MM, xvi), 1Pe.2:2 (in support of AV, milk of the word, see ICC, in l).† (AS)

logion.notes: λόγιον, -ου, τό (dimin. of λόγος, see ICC, Ro., 70), [in LXX chiefly for אִמְרָה, אֵמֶר, Psa.18:30 19:14, al.; also for דָּבָר, Isa.28:13, al.; (cf. λογεῖον (-ιον) for חֹשֶׁן the oracular breastplate of the H.P.,…

logios.notes: λόγιος, -ον (λόγος), __1. in cl., learned (Ac, l.with, R, txt.). __2. In late Gk., eloquent: Act.18:24 (see Page, in l; Field, Notes, 129).† (AS)

logismos.notes: λογισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (λογίζομαι), [in LXX: Psa.33:10-11, Pro.19:21, Isa.66:18, al. (מַחֲשָׁבָה) ;] a reasoning, thought: Rom.2:15, 2Co.10:5.† (AS)

logizomai.notes: λογίζομαι (λόγος), [in LXX chiefly for חָשַׁב ;] __1. prop., of numerical calculation, to count, reckon: with accusative before μετά, Mrk.15:23 (LXX) (Rec., R, mg.), Luk.22:37. __2. Metaphorical, without reference to…

logomacheo.notes: λογομαχέω, -ῶ (λόγος, μάχομαι), to strive with words: 2Ti.2:14.† (AS)

logomachia.notes: λογομαχία, -ας, ἡ (λογομαχέω), a strife of words: pl., 1Ti.6:4.† (AS)

logos.notes: λόγος, -ου, ὁ (λέγω) [in LXX chiefly for דָּבָר, also for מִלָּה ,אֵמֶר, etc. ;] __I. Of that by which the inward thought is expressed, Lat. oratio, sermo, vox, verbum. __1. a word, not in the grammatical sense of a…

loidoreo.notes: λοιδορέω, -ῶ (λοίδορος) [in LXX chiefly for רִיב ;] to abuse, revile: with accusative of person(s), Jhn.9:28, Act.23:4; pass., 1Co.4:12, 1Pe.2:23 (cf. ἀντι-λοιδορέω).† (AS)

loidoria.notes: λοιδορία, -ας, ἡ (λοιδορέω), [in LXX chiefly for רִיב, מְרִיבָה ;] abuse, railing: 1Ti.5:14, 1Pe.3:9.† (AS)

loidoros.notes: λοίδορος, -ον [in LXX: Pro.25:24 26:21 27:15 (מִדְיָן) Sir.23:8 * ;] railing, abusive; as subst., ὁ λ., a railer: 1Co.5:11 6:10.† (AS)

loimos.notes: λοιμός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX for לוּץ, עָרִיץ, בְּלִיַּעַל, etc. ;] pestilence: pl., Luk.21:11; metaphorically (as in cl.; LXX: Psa.1:1, Pro.21:24, 1Ma.15:21, al.), of persons, a pest: Act.24:5 (also as adj., ἄνδρες…

loipon.notes: λοιπός, -ή, -όν (λείπω), [in LXX chiefly for יָתַר, also for שְׁאָר, etc. ;] the remaining, the rest, __1. pl., οἱ λ.: with subst., Mat.25:11, Act.2:37, Rom.1:13, al.; absol., Mat.22:6, Mrk.16:13, Luk.24:10, al.; οἱ…

loipos.notes: λοιπός, -ή, -όν (λείπω), [in LXX chiefly for יָתַר, also for שְׁאָר, etc. ;] the remaining, the rest, __1. pl., οἱ λ.: with subst., Mat.25:11, Act.2:37, Rom.1:13, al.; absol., Mat.22:6, Mrk.16:13, Luk.24:10, al.; οἱ…

loipou.notes: λοιπός, -ή, -όν (λείπω), [in LXX chiefly for יָתַר, also for שְׁאָר, etc. ;] the remaining, the rest, __1. pl., οἱ λ.: with subst., Mat.25:11, Act.2:37, Rom.1:13, al.; absol., Mat.22:6, Mrk.16:13, Luk.24:10, al.; οἱ…

lot.notes: Λώτ, ὁ (Heb. לוֹט), indecl., Lot (Gen.11:27, al.): Luk.17:28-29, 32, 2Pe.2:7.† (AS)

loukas.notes: Λουκᾶς, -ᾶ, ὁ (prob. an abbreviation of Λουκανός; see Lft., Col., 240; ICC, Lk., xviii; Bl., § 29; accusative to Ramsay, Exp., Dec., 1912, pp. 502 ff., a by-form of Λούκιος, from Lat. Lucius), Luke: Lk, tit.,…

loukios.notes: λούκιος, -ου, ὁ (Lat. Lucius), Lucius: Act.13:1, Rom.16:21.† (AS)

louo.notes: λούω [in LXX chiefly for רָחַץ (frequently of ceremonial washing; cf. Deiss., BS, 226 f.) ;] to bathe, wash the body: with accusative of person(s), Act.9:37 (of a dead body); id. before ἀπό (Deiss., BS, l.with),…

loutron.notes: λουτρόν, -οῦ, τό (λούω), [in LXX: Sng.4:2 6:5, (6) (רַחְצָה), Sir.34:25 * ;] a washing, bath: τ. ὕδατος, Eph.5:26; τ. παλιγγενεσίας, Tit.3:5 (see AR, Eph., l.with).† (AS)

luchnia.notes: λυχνία, -ας, ἡ (vulgar form of λυχνίον = λυχνοῦχος; see Kennedy, Sources, 40), [in LXX for מְנוֹרָה (Exo.25:31 40:4, al.) ;] a lampstand: Mat.5:15, Mrk.4:21, Luk.8:16 11:33; of that in the Tabernacle, Heb.9:2;…

luchnos.notes: λύχνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for נִיר (Exo.25:37), al.) ;] a lamp (portable, and usually set on a stand, λυχνία): Mat.5:15, Luk.11:36; ἔρχεται ὁ λ., Mrk.4:21; λ. ἅπτειν Luk.8:16 11:33 15:8; φῶς λύχνου, Rev.18:23; id.…

ludda.notes: λύδδα, -ας (Act.9:38, -ης Rec.), ἡ and λύδδα, -ων, τά (Act.9:32, 35; -αν, Rec.), (Heb. לֹד), Lydda (modern Ludd): Ac, ll. with† (AS)

ludia.notes: Λυδία, -ας, ἡ Lydia, a woman of Thyatira: Act.16:14, 40.† (AS)

lukaonia.notes: Λυκαονία, -ας, ἡ Lycaonia, a region in Asia Minor: Act.14:6.† (AS)

lukia.notes: Λυκία, -ας, ἡ Lydia, a region of Asia Minor; Act.27:5.† (AS)

lukos.notes: λύκος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for זְאֵב ;] a wolf: Mat.10:16, Luk.10:3, Jhn.10:12; figuratively (as Eze.22:27, Zep.3:3, Jer.5:6, al.), Mat.7:1, Act.20:29.† (AS)

lumainomai.notes: λυμαίνομαι (λύμη, outrage), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁחַת pi., hi., also for כִּרְסֵם, etc. ;] __1. to outrage, maltreat: with accusative, Act.8:3. __2. to corrupt, defile (Eze.16:25, Pro.23:8, 4Ma.18:8, al.).† (AS)

luo.notes: λύω [in LXX for פָּתַח, נָתַר hi., etc. ;] __1. to loose, unbind, release: of things, Mrk.1:7, Luk.3:16, al.; of beasts, Mat.21:2, Luk.13:15, al.; of persons, Jhn.11:44, Act.22:30; of Satan, Rev.20:3, 7;…

lupe.notes: λύπη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for עֶצֶב and cogn. forms, etc. ;] pain of body or mind, grief, sorrow: Jhn.16:6, 2Co.2:7; opposite to χαρά, Jhn.16:20, Heb.12:11; ἀπὸ τῆς λ., Luk.22:45; ἐκ λ., 2Co.9:7; ἡ κατὰ θεὸν λ., opposite…

lupeo.notes: λυπέω, -ῶ (λύπη), [in LXX for חָרָה, etc. ;] to distress, grieve, cause pain or grief: with accusative of person(s), 2Co.2:2 2:5 7:8; pass., Mat.14:9 17:23 18:31 19:22 26:22, Mrk.10:22 14:19, Jhn.16:20 21:17,…

lusias.notes: Λυσίας, -ου, ὁ Lysias (Claudius L., Act.23:26), Act.24:7, 22.† (AS)

lusis.notes: λύσις, -εως, ἡ (λύω), [in LXX: Ecc.8:1 (Ecc.8:1) (פֵּשֶׁר), Dan LXX 12:8, Wis.8:8 * ;] a loosing: of divorce, 1Co.7:27.† (AS)

lusitelei.notes: λυσιτελέω, -ῶ (λυσιτελής, useful, prop., τὰ τέλη λύων), [in LXX: Tob.3:6, Sir.20:10, 14 29:11 * ;] __1. prop., to indemnify, pay expenses. __2. to be useful, to profit; usually impers., λυσιτελεῖ, it profits: with,…

lustra.notes: λύστρα, -ας, ἡ and (in Act.14:8 16:2, 2Ti, l.with) -ων, τά (cf. Λύδδα), Lystra, a city of Lycaonia: Act.14:6, 8 14:21 16:1-2, 2Ti.3:11.† (AS)

lutron.notes: λύτρον, -ον, τό (λύω), [in LXX (Pent. .15 Pr .2, Isa .1) for פִּדְיוֹם and cogn. forms, גְּאֻלָּה, כֹּפֶר, מְחִיר ;] a ransom (as for a life, Exo.21:30; for slaves, Lev.19:20; for captives, Isa.45:13): ἀντὶ πολλῶν,…

lutroo.notes: λυτρόω, -ῶ (λύτρον, which see), [in LXX chiefly for פָּדָה, גָּאַל ;] to release on receipt of ransom; mid., to release by paying ransom, to redeem: in spiritual sense, Tit.2:14; pass, 1Pe.1:18; in general sense, to…

lutrosis.notes: λύτρωσις, -εως, ἡ (λυτρόω), [in LXX: Lev.25:29, 48, Isa.63:4 גְּאֻלָּה), Num.18:16, Psa.49:8 111:9 130:7 (פָּדָה, פִּדְיוֹם, פְּדוּת), Jdg.1:15 * ;] a ransoming, redemption (αἰχμαλώτων, Plut., Arat., 11): of the…

lutrotes.notes: λυτρωτής, -οῦ, ὁ (λυτρόω), [in LXX: of God, Psa.19:14 78:35 (גָּאַל)* ;] a redeemer, deliverer: Act.7:35 (not found elsewhere).† (AS)

machaira.notes: μάχαιρα, -ης (Att.. -ας; see WH, App., 1568; ΒΙ., § 7, 1), ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for חֶרֶב; also for מַאֲכֶלֶת, etc. ;] __1. (in Hom., al.) a large knife or dirk, for sacrificial purposes (Gen.22:6, 10, Jdg.19:20 A).…

mache.notes: μάχη, -ης, ἡ (μάχομαι), [in LXX chiefly for רִיב ;] __1. a fight. __2. a strife, contention, quarrel: 2Co.7:5, 2Ti.2:23, Jas.4:1; pl., Tit.3:9.† (AS)

machomai.notes: μάχομαι [in LXX chiefly for רִיב, also for נָצָה ni., etc. ;] __1. to fight: Act.7:26. __2. to quarrel, dispute: 2Ti.2:24, Jas.4:2; πρὸς ἀλλήλους, Jhn.6:52 (cf. δια-μάχομαι).† (AS)

madian.notes: Μαδιάμ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. מִדְיָן), Midian; __1. son of Abraham (Gen.25:2). __2. An Arabian tribe (Gen.36:35, Psa.83:9, al). __3. γῆ Μ. (Heb. מִדְיָן אֶרֶץ), the land of Midian (Exo.2:15, al.): Act.7:29.† (AS)

magdalamagadan.notes: Μαγαδάν Magadan, an unidentified place on the coast of the Sea of Galilee: Mat.15:39 (Rec. Μαγδαλά; cf. Mrk.8:10, where for Δαλμανουθά, D* has Μελεγαδά, D1 Μαγαιδά, some cursives Μαγαδά, and Euseb. Onomast. Μεγαιδάν;…

magdalene.notes: Μαγδαληνός, -ά, όν Magdalene, of Magdala: Μαρία (which see) ἡ MM., Mat.27:56, 61 28:1, Mrk.15:40, 47 16:1, 9, Luk.8:2 24:10, Jhn.19:25 20:1, 18† (AS)

mageia.notes: μαγία (Rec. -εία), -ας, ἡ (μάγος), __1. the lore of the Magians (Plat.). __2. magic: pl., magic arts, sorceries: Act.8:11.† (AS)

mageuo.notes: μαγεύω (Eur., Plut., al.), __1. to be a Magus, or skilled in Magian lore. __2. to practise magic: Act.8:9.† (AS)

magog.notes: Μαγώγ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. מָגוֹג, Gen.10:2, Exo.38:2, al.), Magog, associated with Gog: Rev.20:8 (see Swete, in l).† (AS)

magos.notes: μάγος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for אַשָּׁף, Dan LXX TH Dan.2:2, 10, al. (cf. chief magian, Jer.39:3, 13) ;] __1. one of the Μάγοι, a Median tribe (Hat). __2. a Magian, one of a sacred caste, originally Median, who seem to…

mainan.notes: Μεννά (L, Μεννᾶς, -ᾶ; Rec. Μαϊνάν) ὁ, Menna: Luk.3:31.† (AS)

mainomai.notes: μαίνομαι [in LXX: Jer.25:16 (הָלַל hith.) Jer.29:26 (שָׁגַע pu.), Wis.14:28, a). ;] __1. to rage, be furious. __2. to rave, be mad: Jhn.10:29, Act.12:15 26:24-25, 1Co.14:23 (cf. ἐμ-μαίνομαι).† (AS)

makarios.notes: μακάριος, -α, -ον (collat. form of poët. μάκαρ, in Hom., Hes., chiefly of the gods and the departed), [in LXX for אֹשֶׁר ;] blessed, happy (DCG, i, 177, 213): θεός (δυνάστης), 1Ti.1:11 6:15; ἐλπίς, Tit.2:13; esp. in…

makarismos.notes: μακαρισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (μακαρίζω) a declaration of blessedness, felicitation: Rom.4:6, 9, Gal.4:15 (Plat., Arist.).† (AS)

makarizo.notes: μακαρζω (μακάριος), [in LXX for אָשַׁר pi., pu. ;] to bless, pronounce blessed or happy: with accusative of person(s), Luk.1:48, Jas.5:11.† (AS)

makedonia.notes: Μακεδονία, -ας, ἡ Macedonia: Act.16:9-10, 1Co.16:5, 2Co.1:16, Php.4:15, al.; M. καὶ Ἀχαία, Act.19:21, Rom.15:26, 1Th.1:7-8 (AS)

makellon.notes: μάκελλον, -ου, τό (Lat. macellum), a meat-market: 1Co.10:25 (see Deiss., LAE, 274; MM, xvi).† (AS)

makran.notes: μακράν (prop. fem. accusative of μακρός, Sc. ὁδόν), adv. , [in LXX for רָחוֹק hi., רָחַק, etc. ;] a long way, far: Luk.15:20, Act.22:21; before ἀπό, Mat.8:30, Luk.7:6, Jhn.21:8, Act.17:27; id. metaphorically,…

makrochronios.notes: μακρο-χρόνιος, -ον (μακρός, χρόνος), [in LXX. μ. γίγνεσθαι, εἶναι (יוֹם אָרַךְ), Exo.20:12, Deu.4:40 5:16 17:20 * ;] of long duration, longlived: Eph.6:3 (LXX).† (AS)

makros.notes: μακρός, -ά, -όν [in LXX for אָרֹךְ, רָחוֹק and cognate forms, etc. ;] of space and time, long: μακρὰ προσεύχεσθαι, Mrk.12:40, Luk.20:47. of distance, far, far distant: χώρα, Luk.15:13 19:12.† (AS)

makrothen.notes: μακρόθεν (μακρός), adv. (chiefly late), [in LXX for רָחוֹק, רָחַק, etc. ;] from afar, afar: Luk.18:13 22:54; ἀπὸ μ. (Psa.138:6, Est.3:13, al.), Mat.26:58 27:55, Mrk.5:6 8:3 11:13 14:54 15:40, Luk.16:23 23:49,…

makrothumeo.notes: μακροθυμέω, -ῶ (μακρό-θυμος, long-tempered; see: -ία), [in LXX: Ecc.8:12 א2, Pro.19:11 (אָרַךְ, אַף אָרַךְ), Job.7:16, Sir.2:4, al. ;] __1. actively = καρτερέω, to persevere (Plut., 2, 593 F). __2. Passively, to be…

makrothumia.notes: μακροθυμία, -ας, ἡ (μακρό-θυμος), [in LXX: Pro.25:15 (אַף אֹרֶךְ), Jer.15:15 (אַף אָרֵךְ) Isa.57:15, Sir.5:11, 1Ma.8:4 * ;], patience, longsuffering: of men, esp. in experiencing troubles and difficulties, Col.1:11,…

makrothumos.notes: μακροθύμως adv., with forbearance, patiently: Act.26:3.† (AS)

malakia.notes: μαλακία -ας, ἡ (μαλακός), [in LXX chiefly for חֳלִי Deu.7:15 28:61, Isa.38:9 53:3, al. ;] __1. prop., softness, effeminacy (Hdt., Thuc., al). __2. In NT, as in LXX, = ἀσθένεια, weakness, sickness: νόσος καὶ μ.,…

malakos.notes: μαλακός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Pro.25:15 (רַךְ) Pro.26:22 (לָהַם)* ;] soft; __1. prop., to the touch (opposite to σκληρός): of clothing, pl., Mat.11:8, Luk.7:25. __2. Of persons and their mode of living; __(a) mild,…

malchos.notes: Μάλχος, -ου, ὁ (Hellenistic form of Heb. מֶלֶךְ), Malchus: Jhn.18:10.† (AS)

maleleel.notes: Μαλελεήλ (T, Μελ-), ὁ (Heb. מַהֲלַלְאֵל), Mahalaleel: Luk.3:37.† (AS)

malista.notes: μάλιστα, see: μάλα. μάλα adv., [in LXX for אֲבָל, 3Ki.1:43, Dan LXX 10:21, al.; compar. for מ, Num.13:32 (31), al.; μᾶλλον ἠ (מ), Gen.19:9, al.; superlat., 2Ma.8:7, 4Ma.4:22 12:9 15:4 ;] __I. Pos., very, very much,…

mallon.notes: μάλα adv., [in LXX for אֲבָל, 3Ki.1:43, Dan LXX 10:21, al.; compar. for מ, Num.13:32 (31), al.; μᾶλλον ἠ (מ), Gen.19:9, al.; superlat., 2Ma.8:7, 4Ma.4:22 12:9 15:4 ;] __I. Pos., very, very much, exceedingly (cl.; LXX…

mamme.notes: μάμμη, -ης, ἡ (onomatop.), [in LXX: 4Ma.16:9 * ;] __1. in cl., a child's name for mother. __2. In late Gk. (= cl., τήθη), a grandmother: (LXX, l.with), 2Ti.1:5.† (AS)

mammonas.notes: μαμωνᾶς (Rec. μαμμ-), -ᾶ (Bl., § 7, 4), ό (Aram. מָמוֺנָא), mammon, riches: Mat.6:24, Luk.16:9, 11 16:13.† (AS)

manasses.notes: Μανασσῆς, -ῆ, ὁ (Heb. מְנַשֶּׁה), Manasseh; __1. (a) the first-born son of Joseph; __(b) the tribe which bore his name: Rev.7:6. __2. King of Judah: Mat.1:10.† (AS)

mania.notes: μανία, -ας, ἡ (μαίνομαι) [in LXX: Hos.9:7-8 מַשְׂטֵמָה), Wis.5:4, al. ;] frenzy, madness: Act.26:24.† (AS)

manna.notes: μάννα, τό , (in FlJ, also ἡ), indecl. [in LXX: τὸ μάν, Exo.16:31 ff., elsewhere τ. μάννα, Num.11:6 ff., al. (מָן, Aram. מַנָּא) ;] manna: Jhn.6:31, 49, Heb.9:4; symb., Rev.2:17.† (AS)

manteuomai.notes: μαντεύομαι (μαντίς, a seer, diviner), [in LXX for קָסַם, Deu.18:10, al. ;] to divine, practise divination: Act.16:16.† SYN.: προφητεύω, which see, in distinction from which μ. is used in LXX and NT only of false…

manthano.notes: μανθάνω [in LXX chiefly for לָמַד ;] __1. to learn, esp. by inquiry: absol., 1Co.14:31, 1Ti.2:11, 2Ti.3:7; with accusative of thing(s), Jhn.7:15 (sc. αὐτά), Rom.16:17, 1Co.14:35, Php.4:9, 2Ti.3:14, Rev.14:3; before…

maraino.notes: μαραίνω [in LXX: Job.15:30 (יָבֵשׁ pi.), Job.24:24, Wis.2:8 19:21 * ;] in cl., __1. prop., to quench fire; pass., of fire, to die away, go out. __2. In various relations, to quench, waste, wear out (cf. Wis.19:21);…

maranatha.notes: μαρὰν ἀθά (Rec. μαραναθά; Aram.: on the original form see Dalman, Gr., § 41, 1; 74, 3; Words, 328), Maranatha, i.e. the Lord cometh (but see Dalman, ll. with; Field, Notes, 180; ICC, in l): 1Co.16:22.† (AS)

margarites.notes: μαργαρίτης, -ου, ὁ a pearl: Mat.13:45-46, 1Ti.2:9, Rev.17:4 18:12, 16 21:21 (-ῖται, WH); proverbially, Mat.7:6.† (AS)

maria.notes: Μαρία, -ας (Hellenized form), and Μαριάμ, indecl., ἡ (Aram. מַרְיָם; Heb. מִרְיָם) Mary. In NT; __1. the mother of Jesus: Mat.1:16 ff. Mat.2:11 13:55, Mrk.6:3, Luk.1:27 ff. Luk.2:5, 16 2:19, 34, Act.1:14 __2. M.…

markos.notes: Μάρκος, -ου, ὁ, Mark: Mk., title., Act.12:12, 25 15:37, 39, Col.4:10, 2Ti.4:11, Phm 24, 1Pe.5:13 (see Swete, Mk., Intr., xiii ff.; DB, iii, 245 ff).† (AS)

marmaros.notes: μάρμαρος, -ου, ὁ (μαρμαίρω, to glisten), [in LXX: Ep. Jer.7:2 * ;] __1. any crystalline stone (Hom., Eur., al.). __2. In later writers, marble: Rev.18:12.† (AS)

martha.notes: Μάρθα, -ας (Bl., § 7, 2), ἡ (Aram. מָרְתָּא), Martha: Luk.10:38, 40-41, Jhn.11:1, 5 11:19 ff. Luk.12:2.† (AS)

martureo.notes: μαρτυρέω, -ῶ (μάρτυς), [in LXX chiefly for עֵד (עֵד (Gen.31:47-48, al.), also for עוּד hi. (Gen.43:3, La 2:13), עָנָה (Num.35:30) ;] __(a) prop., to be a witness, bear witness, testify: absol. (find., al.),…

marturia.notes: μαρτυρία, -ας, ἡ (μαρτυρέω), [in LXX: Gen.31:47 R (גַּלְעֵד), Exo.20:16, Deu.5:20 (17), Pro.25:18, Psa.19:7 (עֵד, עֵדוּת), Pro.12:19, Sir.31:23-24, 4Ma.6:32 * ;] witness, testimony, evidence: Mrk.14:56, Luk.22:71,…

marturion.notes: μαρτύριον, -ου, τό (μάρτυρ) [in LXX chiefly for מוֹעֵד, also for עֵדָה, עֵדוּת and cogn. forms ;] a testimony, witness, proof (Hdt., Thuc., al.): with genitive subj., 2Co.1:12, 2Th.1:10; with genitive obj., Act.4:33,…

marturomai.notes: μαρτύρομαι (μάρτυρ), [in LXX: Jdth.7:28, 1Ma.2:56 א)* ;] to summon as witness (M, Th., 25 f.; Hort., 1Pe., 53 f.; Lft., Notes, 29; Ga 203), hence, __(a) to protest, affirm solemnly: before ὅτι, Act.20:26, Gal.5:3;…

martus.notes: μάρτυς (Æolic μάρτυρ), -υρος, ὁ (also ἡ), [in LXX for עֵד ;] a witness: Act.10:41, 1Ti.6:12, 2Ti.2:2, Heb.12:1 (Westc., in l); in forensic sense, Mat.18:16 26:65, Mrk.14:63, Act.6:13 7:58, 2Co.13:1, 1Ti.5:19,…

massaomai.notes: μασάομαι (Rec. μασσ), -ῶμαι (Aristoph. and late writers, but not in Trag. or in good Att.. prose), [in LXX: Job.30:4 (לֶחֶם), Sir.19:9 A * ;] to bite, chew: Rev.16:10.† (AS)

mastigoo.notes: μαστιγόω, -ῶ (μάστιξ) [in LXX chiefly for נָכָה hi. ;] to scourge: with accusative, Mat.10:17 20:19 23:34, Mrk.10:34, Luk.18:33, Jhn.19:1; metaphorically, Heb.12:6 (cf. Pro.3:12, Jer.5:3, Jdth.8:27).† (AS)

mastix.notes: μάστιξ, -ιγος, ἡ [in LXX for שׁוֹט, etc. ;] a whip, scourge: Act.22:24, Heb.11:36; metaphorically, of disease or suffering as a divine chastisement (cf. Psa.89:33, Pro.3:12, 2Ma.9:11), Mrk.3:10 5:29, 34, Luk.7:21 (AS)

mastizo.notes: μαστίζω (Ep. and late prose = Att.. μαστιγόω) [in LXX: Num.22:25 (נָכָה hi.), Wis.5:11, 2Ma.2:21 * ;] to whip, scourge: with accusative, Act.22:25.† (AS)

mastosmazos.notes: μαστός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for שַׁד ;] the breast: pl., Luk.11:27 23:29, Rev.1:13 (T, μασθοῖς; WH, App., 149; L, μαζοῖς).† (AS)

mataiologia.notes: ματαιολογία, -ας, ἡ (ματαιολόγος), idle or foolish talk: 1Ti.1:6.† (AS)

mataioo.notes: ματαιόω, -ῶ (μάταιος), [in LXX: 4Ki.17:15, Jer.2:5, al. הָבַל, etc. ;] to make vain, foolish: Rom.1:21.† (AS)

mataios.notes: μάταιος, -ον (as in Att.., but -αία, -αιον, 1Co.15:17, 1Pe.1:18; μάτην), [in LXX for שָׁוְא, הֶבֶל, כָּזָב, etc. ;] vain, useless: ἀνωφελὴς κ. μ., Tit.3:9; πίστις, 1Co.15:17; θρησκεία, Jas.1:26; διαλογισμοί, 1Co.3:20…

mataiotes.notes: ματαιότης, -ητος, ἡ (μάταιος), [in LXX for הֶבֶל, Psa.31:6 39:5 62:9, al., and nearly 40 times in Ec; for שָׁוְא, Psa.25:4, al.; רִיק, etc. ;] vanity, emptiness, frailty, folly: Rom.8:20, 2Pe.2:18; τ. νοός, Eph.4:17…

maten.notes: μάτην (prop. accusative of μάτη, a fault, folly), adv., [in LXX for הָבַל, שָׁוְא, etc. ;] in vain, to no purpose: Mat.15:9, Mrk.7:7" (LXX) .† (AS)

mathetes.notes: μαθητής, -οῦ, ὁ (μανθάνω), [in LXX only as seel. (A) in Jer.13:21 20:11 26(46):9 * ;] a disciple: opposite to διδάσκαλος, Mat.10:24, Luk.6:40; Ἰωάννου, Mat.9:14, Luk.7:18, Jhn.3:25; τ. Φαρισσίων, Mat.22:16, Mrk.2:18,…

matheteuo.notes: μαθητεύω (μαθητής), __1. intrans. (as prop. vb. in -εύω, and so Plut., mor. 837 with and elsew.), to be a disciple: with dative, Mat.27:57 (Rec., WH, mg.). __2. Trans., to make a disciple: with accusative, Mat.28:19,…

mathetria.notes: μαθήτρια, -ας, ἡ (= μαθητρίς, fem, of μαθητής, which see) a female disciple: Act.9:36.† (AS)

mattatha.notes: Ματταθά, ὁ indecl. (Heb. מַתַּתָּה), Mattatha: Luk.3:31.† (AS)

matthaios.notes: Μαθθαῖος (Rec. Ματθ-, see WH, App., 159; Bl., § 3, 11; on the Semitic form, see Dalman, Words, 51; Gr., 142), -ου, ὁ, Matthew: Mt title., Mat.9:9 10:3, Mrk.3:18, Luk.6:15, Act.1:13 (cf. Λευεί).† (AS)

matthan.notes: Μαθθάν (Rec. Ματθ-, see: Μαθθαῖος), ὁ indecl. (Heb. מַתָּן), Matthan: Mat.1:15.† (AS)

matthatmaththat.notes: Μαθθάτ (T, -άθ; Rec. Ματθ-, see: Μαθθαῖος), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. מַתָּת), Matthat: Luk.3:29 (cf. Ματθάτ).† Matthat: Luk.3:24 (T, Μαθθάθ),† Ματθάτ (see: Μαθθάτ), (AS)

matthiasmaththias.notes: Μαθθίας (Rec. Ματθ-, see: Μαθθαῖος), -α, ὁ (Heb. מַתָּת), Matthias: Act.1:23, 26.† (AS)

medeis.notes: μηδείς, -δεμία, -δέν (and -θέν, Act.27:33, a Hellenistic form; see Bl., 6, 7; Thackeray, Gr., 58) related to οὐδείς as μή to οὐ, no, none, no one; neut., nothing: Mat.16:20, Mrk.5:43 6:8, Luk.3:14, Act.8:24,…

medepo.notes: μηδέπω (μηδέ, πώ), adv., not yet: Heb.11:7.† (AS)

megalaucheo.notes: μεγαλ-αυχέω, -ῶ (= μεγάλα αὐχέω) [in LXX: Eze.16:50 (גָּבַהּ), al. ;] to boast great things: Jas.3:5 (Rec. for μεγάλα αὐχεῖ, WH).† (AS)

megaleios.notes: μεγαλεῖος, -εία, -εῖον (μέγας) [in LXX: Psa.71:19 (גָּדוֹל), al., frequently in Sir ;] magnificent, splendid (Xen., Plut., al.) : Act.2:11.† (AS)

megaleiotes.notes: μεγαλειότης, -ητος, ἡ (μεγαλεῖος) [in LXX: Jer.33:9 (תִּפְאָרָה), Dan LXX 7:27, 1Es.1:5 Est.4:40 * ;] splendour, magnificence: Luk.9:43, Act.19:27, 2Pe.1:16 (frequently in π. as a ceremonial title, MM, xvi).† (AS)

megaloprepes.notes: μεγαλοπρεπής, ές (= μεγάλῳ πρέπων), [in LXX: Deu.33:26 (גַּאֲוָה) 2Ma.8:15 2Mac 15:13, 3Ma.2:9 * ;] befitting a great man, magnificent, majestic: 2Pe.1:17 (cf. MM, xvi).† (AS)

megalos.notes: μεγάλως adv., [in LXX for גָּדוֹל, etc. ;] greatly: Php.4:10.† (AS)

megalosune.notes: μεγαλωσύνη, -ης, ἡ (μέγας) [in LXX chiefly for גְּדוּלָה and cogn. forms, 2Ki.7:23, Psa.145:3, al. ;] greatness, majesty: Heb.1:3 8:1, Ju 25 (elsewhere Eccl. only).† (AS)

megaluno.notes: μεγαλύνω (μέγας) [in LXX chiefly for גָּדַל pi., hi. ;] __1. to make great: Mat.23:5, Luk.1:58. __2. to declare great, extol, magnify: Luk.1:46, Act.5:13 10:46 19:17, 2Co.10:15; pass., before ἐν, Php.1:20.† (AS)

megas.notes: μέγας, μεγάλη, μέγα, [in LXX chiefly for גָּדוֹל, also for מַרְבֶּה ,רַב (incl. μείζων), רֹב (μέγιστος) ;] great; __1. of external form, bodily size, measure, extent: λίθος, Mat.2:60; δράκων, Rev.12:3; ἰχθῦς,…

megethos.notes: μέγεθος, -ους, τό (μέγας) [in LXX chiefly for קוֹמָה ;] greatness: Eph.1:19 (cf. MM, xvi, see word μέγας).† (AS)

megistan.notes: μεγιστάν, -ᾶνος, ὁ (μέγιστος) [in LXX chiefly for שַׂר, Jer.24:8, al.; רַבְרְבָן, Dan LXX TH Dan.5:23, al.; frequently in Sir (sing., Sir.4:7) ;] usually pl., οἱ μ., the chief men, nobles (Manetho, FlJ, al.):…

meketi.notes: μηκέτι (μή, ἔτι) adv., no more, no longer: with 2 aor. subjc., Mrk.9:25; οὐ μ., Mat.21:19; with praes. subjc., Rom.14:13; with praes. imperat., Luk.8:49, Jhn.5:14 8:11 Eph.4:28, 1Ti.5:23; with optative, Mrk.11:14;…

mekos.notes: μῆκος, -εος (-ους), τό [in LXX chiefly for אֹרֶךְ ;] length: Eph.3:18, Rev.21:16.† (AS)

mekuno.notes: μηκύνω (μῆκος), [in LXX: Isa.44:14 (גָּדַל pi.), Eze.12:25, 28 (מָשַׁךְ ni.) * ;] to lengthen, extend: of causing plants to grow, Isa, l.with; pass. (mid., Swete, in l), to grow: Mrk.4:27.† (AS)

melan.notes: μέλαν, τό, see: μέλας. μέλας, -αινα, -αν genitive, -ανος, -αίνης, -ανος, [in LXX: Sng.1:5, Zec.6:2 (שָׁחֹר), etc. ;] black: Rev.6:5, 12; opposite to λευκός, Mat.5:36; neut., τὸ μ., ink : 2Co.3:3, II Jn 12, 3Jn.13.† (AS)

melas.notes: μέλας, -αινα, -αν genitive, -ανος, -αίνης, -ανος, [in LXX: Sng.1:5, Zec.6:2 (שָׁחֹר), etc. ;] black: Rev.6:5, 12; opposite to λευκός, Mat.5:36; neut., τὸ μ., ink : 2Co.3:3, II Jn 12, 3Jn.13.† (AS)

melchi.notes: Μελχεί (Rec. -χί), indecl., ὁ (Heb. מֶלֶךְ), Melchi: Luk.3:24, 28.† (AS)

melchisedek.notes: Μελχισεδέκ indecl., ὁ (Heb. מַלְכִּי־צֶדֶק), Melchizedek: Heb.5:6, 10 6:20 7:1, 10-11 7:15, 17.† (AS)

meleas.notes: Μελεά (Rec. -ᾶς, genitive, -ᾶ) indecl., ὁ (Heb. מְלֵאָה), Melea: Luk.3:31.† (AS)

meletao.notes: μελετάω, -ῶ (μελέτη, care), [in LXX chiefly for הָגָה m ;] __1. with genitive, to care for (Hes.). __2. C. accusative to attend to, practise: 1Ti.4:15 (RV, be diligent in; cf. Souter in Exp., VIII, vi, 429, but see…

meli.notes: μέλι, -τος, τό [in LXX frequently (Gen.43:11, al.) for דְּבַשׁ; for נֹפֶת, Pro.5:3 ;] honey: Rev.10:9-10; ἄγριον (which see), Mat.3:4, Mrk.1:9.† (AS)

melite.notes: Μελίτη (Rec., R, txt.), Μελιτήνη (WH, R, mg., see WH, App., 160), Melita, Melitene (mod. Malta) : Act.28:1.† (AS)

mello.notes: μέλλω, [in LXX: Job.3:8 (עָתִיד) 19:25 (אַחֲרוֹן); elsewhere for fut., and frequently in Wi, II, 4Mac ;] to be about to be or do; __1. with inf. (Bl., §62, 4; 68, 2; M, Pr., 114); __(a) of intending or being about to…

melo.notes: μέλω [in LXX: Job.22:3 (חֵפֶץ), Tob.10:5, Wis.12:13, 1Ma.14:42-43 * ;] __1. intrans., to be an object of care, be a care; commonly in third of person(s): with dative of person(s), Act.18:17; very frequently impers.,…

melos.notes: μέλος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for נֵתַח ;] a member, limb of the body: 1Co.12:14 12:19 12:26 Eph.4:16 (WH, mg.), Jas.3:5; pl. (as always in cl.), τὰ μ. : Mat.5:29-30, Rom.6:13, 19 7:5, 23 12:4, 1Co.12:12 ff,…

melote.notes: μηλωτή, -ῆς, ἡ (μῆλον, a sheep or goat) [in LXX for אַדֶּרֶת, 3Ki.19:13 19:19, 4Ki.2:8 2:13-14 * ;] a sheepskin: Heb.11:37.† (AS)

mempsimoiros.notes: μεμψίμοιρος, -ον (μέμφομαι, + μοῖρα, fate, lot), complaining of one's fate, querulous: Ju 16 † (AS)

men.notes: μέν, conjunctive particle (originally a form of μήν), usually related to a following δέ or other adversative conjunction, and distinguishing the word or clause with which it stands from that which follows. It is…

meno.notes: μένω, [in LXX for קוּם ,עמד, etc. ;] to stay, abide, remain. __1. Intrans.; __(i) of place: before ἐν, Luk.8:27, al.; παρά, with dative of person(s), Jhn.1:40, al; σύν, Luk.1:56; καθ᾽ ἑαυτόν, Act.28:16; with adv.,…

menuo.notes: μηνύω [in LXX: 2Ma.3:7 2Mac 6:11 2Mac 14:37, 3Ma.3:28, 4Ma.4:3 * ;] to disclose, declare, make known: Luk.20:37, 1Co.10:28; in forensic sense, to inform, report: Jhn.11:57; pass., with dative of person(s),…

merimna.notes: μέριμνα, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Psa.55:22 (יְהַב), Job.11:18, Sir.30:24, al ;] (in cl. chiefly poët.) care, anxiety: 1Pe.5:7; pl., Luk.8:14 21:34; with genitive obj., Mat.13:22, Mrk.4:19, 2Co.11:28.† (AS)

merimnao.notes: μεριμνάω, -ῶ (μέριμνα), [in LXX: Psa.38:18 (דָּאַג), etc. ;] __1. to be anxious: absol., Mat.6:27, 31, Luk.12:25; μηδὲν μ., Php.4:6; with dative of thing(s), Mat.6:25, Luk.12:22; before περί, Mat.6:28, Luk.10:41…

meris.notes: μερίς, -ίδος, ἡ [in LXX chiefly חֵלֶק, חֶלְקָה ;] __1. (as in cl.) a part, portion: Luk.10:42, Act.8:21, 2Co.6:15, Col.1:12. __2. In later Gk (see MM, xvi), as geographical term, a division, district: Act.16:12.† (AS)

merismos.notes: μερισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (μερίζω), [in LXX for מַחְלְקָה, מַחֲלֹקֶת, Jos.11:23, Est.6:18 * ;] __1. a dividing, division: ψυχῆς κ. πνεύματος (i.e. between them or of the things themselves, see Westc., in l), Heb.4:12. __2. a…

merizo.notes: μερίζω (μέρος), [in LXX chiefly for חָלַק ;] to divide; __(a) to divide into parts : metaphorically, pass., 1Co.7:34 (WH, R, ing.), 1Co.7:34 (Rec., R, txt.) ( reading and punctuation, see ICC, in l); μεμέρισται ὁ…

meros.notes: μέρος, -ους, τό (μείρομαι), [in LXX chiefly for קָצֶה ;] __1. a part, share, portion: Jhn.13:8, Act.19:27 (Page, in l), Rev.20:6 22:19; hence (cl.), lot, destiny, Mat.24:51, Luk.12:46, Rev.21:8. __2. a part as…

mesembria.notes: μεσημβρία, -ας, ἡ (μέσος, ἡμέρα), [in LXX chiefly for צֹהַר, Gen.3:16, al; also for נֶגֶב, Dan LXX 8:4, 9, al. ;] __1. noon: Act.22:6. __2. the South: Act.8:26.† (AS)

mesites.notes: μεσίτης, -ου, ὁ (μέσος), [in LXX: Job.9:33 (בֵּין)* ;] an arbitrator, mediator: Gal.3:19; with dupl. genitive of person(s), θεοῦ κ. ἀνθρώπων, 1Ti.2:5; with genitive of thing(s), διαθήκης, Heb.8:6 9:15 12:24; ὁ δε μ.…

mesonuktios.notes: μεσο-νύκτιος (on seel, μεσα-, see Bl., § 6, 2), -ον (μέσος, νύξ), [in LXX chiefly for לַיִל חֵצִי ;] of or at midnight; as subst., neut., τὸ μ., midnight (Arist. and late writers) : genitive, Luk.11:5; μέχρι μ.,…

mesoo.notes: μεσόω, -ῶ (μέσός), [in LXX: μεσούσης τ. νυκός, Exo.12:29 (חֵצִי), etc. ;] to be in the middle, esp. of time: τ. ἑορτῆς μεσούσης, in the middle of the feast, Jhn.7:14.† (AS)

mesos.notes: μέσος, -η, -ον [in LXX chiefly for תָּוֶךְ ;] middle, in the middle or midst; __1. prop., as an adj.: Luk.23:45, Jhn.19:18, Act.1:18; with genitive pl., Luk.22:55, Jhn.1:26; genitive temp. (Bl., § 36, 13), μέσης…

mesotoichos.notes: μεσότοιχον, -ου, τό (μέσος, τοῖχος) a partition wall: Eph.2:14 (not elsewhere, but see LS, see word μεσότοιχος).† (AS)

mesouranema.notes: μεσουράνημα, -τος, τό (μεσουρανέω, to be in mid-heaven, of the sun at the meridian), the zenith, mid-heaven: Rev.8:13 14:6 19:17.† (AS)

messias.notes: Μεσσίας, -ου, ὁ (Aram. מְשִׁיחָא = Heb. מָשִׁיחַ = Χριστός, which see), Messiah: Jhn.1:42 4:22.† (AS)

mestoo.notes: μεστόω, -ῶ (μεστός), [in LXX: 3Ma.5:1 3Mac 5:10 * ;] to fill: pass., with genitive of thing(s), Act.2:13.† (AS)

mestos.notes: μεστός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Nam.1:10, Eze.37:1 (מָלֵא), Est.5:2, Pro.6:34 * ;] full: with genitive of thing(s), Jhn.19:29 21:11, Jas.3:8; metaphorically, of thoughts and feelings, Mat.23:28, Rom.1:29 15:14, 2Pe.2:14,…

metabaino.notes: μετα-βαίνω [in LXX: Wis.7:27 19:19, 2Ma.6:1 2Mac 6:9 2Mac 6:24 * ;] to pass over from one place to another : Mat.17:20, Luk.10:7; with reference to the point of departure only, to withdraw, depart: Mat.8:34 11:1 12:9…

metaballo.notes: μετα-βάλλω [in LXX chiefly for הָפַךְ ;] to turn about, change. Pass. and mid., to turn oneself about; metaphorically, to change one's mind: Act.28:6.† (AS)

metadidomi.notes: μετα-δίδωμι [in LXX: Pro.11:29 (שָׁבַר hi.), Wis.7:13, al. ;] to give a share of, impart: with dative of person(s) and accusative of thing(s) (in cl. more freq., with genitive part., but accusative of that which is…

metago.notes: μετ-άγω [in LXX: 3Ki.8:47-48, 2Ch.6:37 (שָׁבָה) 2Ch.36:3 (סוּר hi.), 1Es.1:45 Est.2:10 Est.5:69, Est.8:17, Sir prol.16 Sir.10:8, 2Ma.1:33 * ;] __1. in Xen., Plut., and later writers, to transfer, transport (so LXX).…

metairo.notes: μετ-αίρω [in LXX: 4Ki.16:17 25:11, Psa.80:8, Pro.22:28 (סוּר hi., גָּלָה hi., etc.)* ;] __1. trans., to remove (LXX, ll. with). __2. (not cl.) to depart: Mat.13:53 19:1 (cf. Aq.: Gen.12:9).† (AS)

metakaleo.notes: μετα-καλέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Hos.11:1-2 (קָרָא), I Est.1:50 * ;] to call from one place to another. Mid., to send for: with accusative, Act.7:14 10:32 20:17 24:25.† (AS)

metakineo.notes: μετα-κινέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Deu.19:14 (סוּג hi.), Isa.54:10 (מוֹט), etc. ;] trans., to move away, remove (ὅρια, Deu, l.with). Mid., to remove oneself, remove, shift: metaphorically, ἀπὸ τ. ἐλπίδος, Col.1:23.† (AS)

metalambano.notes: μετα-λαμξάνω [in LXX: Est.5:1, Wis.18:9; 4Ma.12:1-19 * ;] to have or get a share of, partake of: with genitive of thing(s), 2Ti.2:6, Heb.6:7 12:10; τροφῆς, Act.2:46 27:33-34; with accusative of thing(s) (of the…

metalepsis.notes: μετά-λημψις (Rec. -ληψις), -εως, ἡ (μεταλαμβάνω) participation, taking, receiving: of food, 1Ti.4:3.† (AS)

metallasso.notes: μετ-αλλάσσω [in LXX: Est.2:7 (מוּת), Est.2:20, 1Es.1:31 Est.1:2 Mac.9 * ;] __1. to exchange: τ. ἀλήθειαν . . . ἐν τ. ψεύδει, the truth for a lie (see Bl., § 36, 8), Rom_1:25. __2. to change: with accusative before…

metamorphoo.notes: μετα-μορφόω, -ῶ [in Sm.: Psa.34:1 * ;] to transform, trans­figure: pass., of Christ's transfiguration, Mat.17:2, Mrk.9:2 (cf. Luk.9:29); of Christians, Rom.12:2, 2Co.3:18.† SYN.: μετασχηματίζω, to change in fashion…

metanoia.notes: μετάνοια, -οίας, ἡ (μετανοέω), [in LXX: Pro.14:15, Wis.11:23 12:10, 19, Sir.44:16 * ;] after-thought, change of mind, repentance: Heb.12:17; of repentance from sin, Mat.3:8, 11 Luk.3:8 15:7 24:47, Act.26:20,…

metapempo.notes: μετα-πέμπω [in LXX (mid.) : Gen.27:45 (לָקַח), Num.23:7 (נָחָה: hi.), 2Ma.15:31, 3Ma.5:18 R, 4Ma.12:3 4Mac 12:6 * ;] to send after or for: pass., Act.10:29 a. Chiefly in mid., to send for, summon Act.10:5, 22 10:29 b…

metaschematizo.notes: μετα-σχηματίζω [in LXX: 4Ma.9:22 * ;] to change in fashion or appearance: with accusative of thing(s), τ. σῶμα, Php.3:21; mid., before εἰς, 2Co.11:13-14; before ὡς, 2Co.11:15; of a rhetorical device, to transfer by a…

metastrepho.notes: μετα στρέφω [in LXX chiefly for הָפַךְ ;] to turn about, turn, change: pass., Act.2:20 (LXX), Jas.4:9 (WH, txt., μετατρέπω, which see); in evil sense, to pervert, corrupt (cf. primary sense reverse) : Gal.1:7.†…

metathesis.notes: μετά-θεσις, -εως, ἡ (μετατίθημι) [in LXX: 2Ma.11:24 * ;] __1. change of position, removal : Heb.11:5. __2. change, as of that which has been established : Heb.7:12 12:27.† (AS)

metatithemi.notes: μετα-τίθημι [in LXX: Gen.5:24 (לָקַח), Deu.27:17, al. (סוּג hi.), Sir.44:16, 2Ma.7:24, al ;] __1. to transfer to another place: with accusative, pass., Heb.11:5 (LXX); before εἰς, Act.7:16 __2. to change: with…

metecho.notes: μετ-έχω [in LXX: Pro.5:17 (עֵת), Pro.1:18, 1Es.5:40 Est.8:70, Sir.51:28, al. ;] to partake of, share in: ἐπ᾽ ἐλπίδι τοῦ μετέχειν, 1Co.9:10; with genitive of thing(s), 1Co.9:12 10:21 10:30, Heb.2:14; in sacramental…

meteorizo.notes: μετ-εωρίζω (μετέωρος, (a) in mid air; (b) buoyed up; (with) in suspense; Thuc.; in π. opposite to ἀμέριμνος, see Zorell, see word) [in LXX: Ob 4 (גָּבַהּ hi.), Mic.4:1 (נָשָׂא ni.), Psa.131:1, Eze.10:16-17, 19…

metepeita.notes: μετ-έπειτα, adv. [in LXX: Jdth.9:5, Est.3:13, 3Ma.3:24 * ;] after­wards: Heb.12:17.† (AS)

meter.notes: μήτηρ genitive, μητρός, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for אֵם ;] mother: Mat.1:18 2:11, al.; figuratively, of one who takes the place of a mother, ἰδοὺ, ἡ μ. μου, Mat.12:49 (cf. Mat.12:50, Mrk.3:35, Jhn.19:27, Rom.16:13,…

methe.notes: μέθη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for שָׁכַר and cognate forms ;] drunkenness: Luk.21:34; pl., Rom.13:13, Gal.5:21 † SYN.: see: κῶμος (AS)

methistemi.notes: μεθ-ίστημι and (late form, 1Co.13:2) μεθιστάνω, [in LXX for סוּר hi., etc. ;] trans. in Pres., impf., fut. and aor. 1, to change, remove: with accusative of thing(s), ὄρη, 1Co.13:2 (cf. Isa.54:10); with accusative of…
