methuo.notes: μεθύω (μέθυ, wine, cf. μέθη), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁכַר, רָוָה ;] to be drunken: Mat.24:49, Jhn.2:10, Act.2:15, 1Co.11:21, 1Th.5:7; metaphorically, Rev.17:2, 6.† (AS)

methusko.notes: μεθύσκω [in LXX: Psa.23:5 (רָוָה), Pro.4:17 (שָׁתָה), etc. ;] causal of μεθύω, to make drunk, intoxicate; pass., to get drunk: Luk.12:45, Eph.5:18, 1Th.5:7.† (AS)

methusos.notes: μέθυσος, -α, -ον (also -ος, -ον; prop., only of women, but in late writers also = μεθυστικός, of men), [in LXX: Pro.23:21 (סָבָא) Pro.26:9 (שִׁכּוֹר), Sir.19:1 26:8, 4Ma.2:7 * ;] drunken: 1Co.5:11 6:10.† (AS)

metoche.notes: μετοχή, -ῆς, ἡ (μετέχω), [in LXX: Psa.122:3 א R (חָבַר pu.) * ;] sharing, fellowship: 2Co.6:14.† (AS)

metochos.notes: μέτοχος, -ον (μετέχω), [in LXX chiefly for חָבֵר ;] __1. sharing in, partaking of: with genitive of thing(s), Heb.3:1 6:4 12:8; τ. Χριστοῦ, Heb.3:14. __2. As subst., ὁ μ., a partner, associate: Luk.5:7, Heb.1:9"…

metoikesia.notes: μετοικεσία, -ας, ἡ (= cl. μετολκία, -κησις; μετοικέω, to change one's abode), [in LXX chiefly for גּוֹלָה and cogn. forms, Eze.12:11, Ob 20, al. ;] change of abode, migration: of the Babylonian exile, μ. Βαβυλῶνος,…

metoikizo.notes: μετ-οικίζω (μέτοικος, an emigrant), [in LXX chiefly for גָּלָה hi. ;] to remove to a new abode, cause to migrate: Act.7:4, 43" (LXX) .† (AS)

metopon.notes: μέτωπον, -ου, τό (μετά + ὤψ, an eye), [in LXX for מֵצַח ;] the forehead: Rev.7:3 9:4 13:16 14:1, 9 17:5 20:4 22:4.† (AS)

metra.notes: μήτρα, -ας, ἡ (μήτμρ), [in LXX chiefly for רֶחֶם ;] the womb: Luk.2:23 (LXX), Rom.4:19.† (AS)

metraloas.notes: μητρολῴας (Rec. -αλῴας, in cl., -αλοίας, see B1., § 3, 3; 6, 2), -ου, ὁ (μήτρρ + ἀλοιάω, to smite) __(a) a matricide: 1Ti.1:9 (AV, R, txt., but see infr.); __(b) a smiter of his mother: 1Ti.1:9 (R, mg., cf.…

metreo.notes: μετρέω, -ῶ (μέτρον), [in LXX: Exo.16:18, Num.35:5, Rut.3:15, Isa.40:12, (מָדַד), Da Th Dan.5:26 (מָדַד), Wis.4:8 * ;] __1. to measure, of space, number, value, etc.: with accusative of thing(s), Rev.11:2 21:15, 17;…

metriopatheo.notes: μετριοπαθέω, -ῶ (μετριοπαθής, moderating one's passions), to hold one's passions or emotions in restraint; hence, to bear gently with, feel gently towards: Heb.5:2.† (AS)

metrios.notes: μετρίως adv. (μέτριος, moderate), [in LXX: 2Ma.15:1-39 * ;] moderately: litotes, οὐ μ., exceedingly, Act.20:12.† (AS)

metron.notes: μέτρον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for מִדָּה, also for אֵיפָה, etc. ;] __1. that which is used for measuring, a measure; __(a) a vessel: fig., Mat.23:32, Luk.6:38; ἐκ μ., by measure, Jhn.3:34; __(b) a rod or rule:…

miaino.notes: μιαίνω [in LXX chiefly for טָמֵא ;] __1. to dye or stain. __2. to stain, defile, soil; __(a) in physical sense; __(b) in moral sense : Tit.1:15, Heb.12:15, Ju 8; __(with) in ritual sense (cf. Lev.22:5, al.):…

miasma.notes: μίασμα, -τος, τό (μιαίνω), chiefly in trag. and late writers; [in LXX: Lev.7:8, 18, (פִּגּוּל), Jer.32:34 (שִׁקּוּץ), Eze.33:31 (בֶּצַע), Jdth.9:2, 4 13:16, 1Ma.13:50 * ;] a stain, defilement: pl., 2Pe.2:20.† (AS)

miasmos.notes: μιασμός, -οῦ, ὁ (μιαίνω), [in LXX: Wis.14:26, 1Ma.4:43 * ;] __1. prop., the act of defiling. __2. = μίασμα (which see) : 2Pe.2:10 (cf. Plut., Mor., 393c).† (AS)

michael.notes: Μιχαήλ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. מִיכָאֵל, who like God?), Michael, the Archangel (cf. Dan.12:1) : Ju 9, Rev.12:7.† (AS)

migma.notes: μίγμα (LT, cl., μῖγμα; on the orthogr., see B1., § 3, 5), -τος, τό (μίγνυμι), [in LXX: Sir.38:8 * ;] a mixture: Jhn.19:39 (ἕλιγμα, WH, R, mg.).† (AS)

mignumi.notes: μίγνυμι (on the spelling μείγ-, ν. B)., § 3, 5) [in LXX for עָרַב hith., etc. ;] to mix, mingle: with accusative and dative, Rev.15:2; with accusative before ἐν, Rev.8:7; before μετά, Mat.27:34, Luk.13:1.† SYN.:…

mikros.notes: μικρός, -ά, -όν [in LXX chiefly for קָטָן, מָעַט ;] small, little; __1. of persons; __(a) lit., of stature: Mrk.15:40 (MM, iii, xvi; on the view that age is meant, see Deiss., BS, 144), Luk.19:3; οἱ μ., the little…

miletos.notes: Μίλητος, -ου, ἡ, Miletus a maritime city of Caria : Act.20:15, 17, 2Ti.4:20.† (AS)

milion.notes: a Roman mile, milliarium , = 1000 paces,= 8 stades, = 1680 yards, i. e. 80 yards less than our mile, (Polybius Historicus), etc. (ML)

mimeomai.notes: μιμέομαι, -οῦμαι (μῖμος, a mimic, an actor), [in LXX: Psa.31:6, Wis.4:2 15:9, 4Ma.9:23 4Mac 9:13 : * ;] to imitate: 2Th.3:7 3:9 Heb.13:7, 3Jn.11.† (AS)

mimetes.notes: μιμητής, -οῦ, ὁ (μιμέομαι), in NT always (like the verb) in good sense, an imitator: 1Co.4:16 11:1, Eph.5:1, 1Th.1:6 2:14, Heb.6:12.† (AS)

mimnesko.notes: μιμνήσκω (Bl., -ῄ-, § 3, 3), [in LXX chiefly for זָכַר ;] to remind: mid, and pass.; __(a) reflexive, to remind oneself of, hence, to remember: with genitive of thing(s), Mat.26:75, Luk.1:54, 72 24:8, Act.11:16,…

miseo.notes: μισέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for שָׂנֵא ;] to hate: with accusative of person(s) Mat.5:43 24:10, Luk.1:71 6:22, 27 19:14, Jhn.7:7 15:18-19, 23-26 17:14, Tit.3:3, 1Jn.2:9 Jn 2:11 Jn 3:13 Jn 3:15 Jn 4:20 Rev.17:16; pass.,…

misthapodosia.notes: μισθαποδοσία, -ας, ἡ (μισθός, ἀποδίδωμι; cl. μισθοδοσία), pay­ment of wages, recompense; meton., __(a) of reward: Heb.10:35 11:26; __(b) of punishment : Heb.2:2.† (AS)

misthapodotes.notes: μισθ-απο-δότης, -ου, ὁ (see supr.), one who pays wages; meton., a rewarder: Heb.11:6.† (AS)

misthios.notes: μίσθιος, -α, -ον (also -ος, -ον), [in LXX: Lev.19:13 A Lev.25:50, Job.7:1 (שָׂכִיר), Tob.5:11, Sir.7:10 31:22 37:11 * ;] hired; as subst., ὁ μ., a hired servant: Luk.15:17, 19 15:21 (Anth., Plut.).† (AS)

misthoma.notes: μίσθωμα, -τος, τό (μισθόω), [in LXX: Deu.23:18 (19), Mic.1:7, Eze.16:31, 34 16:41 (אֶתְנַן), Eze.16:33, (נֵדֶה, נָדָן), Eze.16:32, Pro.19:13 * ;] __1. price, hire (cl, and LXX). __2. In sense not found elsewhere, a…

misthoo.notes: μισθόω, -ῶ (μισθός) [in LXX (mid.) chiefly for שָׂכָר ;] to let out for hire. Mid., to hire: with accusative, Mat.20:1, 7.† (AS)

misthos.notes: μισθός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for שָׂכָר ;] __1. prop., wages, hire: Mat.20:8, Luk.10:7, Rom.4:4, 1Ti.5:18, Jas.5:4, Ju 11; μ. ἀδικίας, Act.1:18, 2Pe.2:13 (but see Mayor and ICC, in l), 2Pe.2:15. __2. Generally,…

misthotos.notes: μισθωτός, -ή, όν (μισθόω), [in LXX for שָׂכִיר, Exo.12:45, al. ;] hired; as subst., ὁ μ., a hired servant, hireling: Mrk.1:20, Jhn.10:12-13.† (AS)

mitulene.notes: Μιτυλήνη, -ης, ἡ (late form - Strab., Plut. - of cl. Μυτιλ-), Mytilene, Mitylene, chief city of Lesbos: Act.20:14.† (AS)

mna.notes: μνᾶ, -ᾶς, ἡ (a Semitic word; cf. Heb. מָנֶה, Aram. מְנֵא, a weight and a sum of money = 100 shekels, cf. 3Ki.10:17), a mina (Let.), mna, in Attic a weight and sum of money = 100 δραχμαί (which see) : Luk.19:13, 16…

mneia.notes: μνεία, -ας, ἡ (μιμνήσκω), [in LXX for זָכַר, its parts and deriva­tives ;] remembrance, mention (= μνήμη) : Php.1:3; μ. ποιεῖσθαι, with genitive of person(s), Rom.1:9, Eph.1:16, 1Th.1:2, Phm 4 (cf. Psa.111:4); μ.…

mnema.notes: μνῆμα, -τος, τό (μνάομαι), [in LXX for קֶבֶר, קְבוּרָה ;] __1. a memorial. __2. a sepulchral monument, a sepulchre, tomb: Mrk.5:3, 5 15:46 16:2 (WH, μνημεῖον), Luk.8:27 23:53 24:1, Act.2:29 7:16, Rev.11:9.† SYN.:…

mneme.notes: μνήμη, -ης, ἡ (μνάομαι), [in LXX for זֵכֶר, זִכְרוֹן ;] memory, remembrance, mention: μ. ποιεῖσθαι, with genitive, to remember, 2Pe.1:15 (but in cl., π., μ. π. more frequently = to make mention, and so perh. here,…

mnemeion.notes: μνημεῖον, -ου, τό [in LXX for קֶבֶר, קְבוּרָה ;] __1. a memorial, record (cl., cf. Wis.10:7). __2. (a) (cl.) a monument: Luk.11:47; __(b) a sepulchre, tomb (Gen.23:6, 9, Isa.22:16, al.): Mat.23:29, Mrk.5:2,…

mnemoneuo.notes: μνημονεύω (μνήμων, mindful) [in LXX for זָכַר ;] __1. to call to mind, remember : absol., Mrk.8:18; with genitive of person(s), Luk.17:32, Col.4:18, 1Th.1:3, Heb.11:15 (but see infr.) Heb.13:7; τ. πτωχῶν, Gal.2:20;…

mnemosunon.notes: μνημόσυνον, -ου, τό (μνήμων, mindful), [in LXX frequently for זֵכֶר, זִכְרוֹן and cogn. forms ;] a memorial: Mat.26:13, Mrk.14:9, Act.10:4 (where cf. Lev.2:9, 16 5:12, Num_5:28, Sir.45:16, al.).† (AS)

mnesteuo.notes: μνηστεύω [in LXX for אָרַשׂ pi., pu. ;] __1. to woo and win, espouse. __2. to promise in marriage, betroth; pass., of the woman, to be be­trothed: with dative of person(s), Mat.1:18, Luk.1:27 2:5.† (AS)

mochthos.notes: μόχθος, -ου, ὁ (= Hom. μόγος), in cl. chiefly poët., [in LXX for תְּלָאָה, עָמָל, etc. ;] toil, labour, hardship, distress: 2Co.11:27, 1Th.2:9, 2Th.3:8.† SYN.: κόπος (which see), πόνος (AS)

modios.notes: μοιχαλίς, -ίδος, ἡ (= Att.. μοιχάς, fem. of μοιχός), [in LXX: Pro.18:22 30:20), Eze.16:38 23:45, Hos.3:1, Mal.3:5 (נַאֲפוּף, נָאַף) * ;] an adulteress: Rom.7:3; meton., for μοιχεία, 2Pe.2:14. Metaphorical, of…

mogilalos.notes: μογι-λάλος, -ον (μόγις, λάλος), [in LXX: Isa.35:6 (אִלֵּם) * ;] speaking with difficulty: Mrk.7:32 (Tr., txt., μογγιλάλος, thick-voiced, see Swete, in l).† (AS)

mogis.notes: μόγις adv. (μόγος, toil), [in LXX: Wis.9:16 א A (μόγις, B), 3Ma.7:6 * ;] with toil or difficulty, hardly: Luk.9:39 (μόγις, WH).† (AS)

moichalis.notes: 1. an adulteress , (New Testament):as adjective adulterous , (New Testament) 2. as Substantive = μοιχεία, (New Testament) (ML)

moichao.notes: μοιχάω, -ῶ (= cl. μοιχεύω), [in LXX (mid., absol. and with accusative, with party of either sex as subj.) : Jer.3:8 5:7 7:9 9:2 23:14 29:23, Eze.16:32 23:37, 43 (נָאַף) * ;] to commit adultery with: with accusative,…

moicheia.notes: μοιχεία, -ας, ἡ (μοιχύω), [rn LXX: Hos.2:2 (נַאֲפוּף), Hos.4:2 (נָאַף), Jer.13:27 (נִאֻף), Wis.14:26 * ;] adultery: Jhn.8:3; pl. (see WM, 220; Bl., § 32, 6), Mat.15:19, Mrk.7:21,† (AS)

moicheuo.notes: μοιχεύω (μοιχός), [in LXX: Exo.20:13, Lev.20:10, al. (נָאַף) ;] to commit adultery: absol., Mat.5:27 19:18, Mrk.10:19, Luk.16:18 18:20, Rom.2:22 13:9, Jas.2:11; with accusative fem., Mat.5:28. Pass., of the woman,…

moichos.notes: μοιχός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX for נָאַף ;] an adulterer: Luk.18:11, 1Co.6:9, Heb.13:4.† (AS)

molis.notes: μόλις adv. (μόλος, toil) post-Hom. alternative for μόγις, [in LXX: Pro.11:31, Wis.9:16, al. ;] with difficulty, hardly, scarcely: Luk.9:39 (T, μόγις), Act.14:8 27:7-8, 16, Rom.5:7, 1Pe.4:18" (LXX) .† (AS)

moloch.notes: Μολόχ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. מֶלֶךְ, prop. מֶלֶךְ King, but vocalized to read בֺּשֶׁת, shame, of. Βάαλ, and see DB, iii, 415 f.), Moloch, the god of the Ammonites : Act.7:43 (LXX).† (AS)

molops.notes: μώλωψ, -ωπος, ὁ [in LXX for חַבּוּרָה, Exo.21:25, al. ;] a bruise, wound from a stripe: 1Pe.2:24 (LXX) (Arist., Plut., al.).† (AS)

moluno.notes: μολύνω [in LXX: Gen.37:31 (טָבַל), Isa.59:3 (גָּאַל ni.), Zec.14:2 (שָׁכַב ni.), Sir.21:28, al. ;] to stain, soil, defile; in NT always symb. and figuratively: 1Co.8:7, Rev.3:4 14:4.† SYN.: μιαίνω, q.v (AS)

molusmos.notes: μολυσμός, -οῦ, ὁ (μολύνω), [in LXX: Jer.23:15 (חֲנֻפָה), I Est.8:83, 2Ma.5:27 * ;] defilement: with genitive obj., 2Co.7:1 (Plut., FlJ).† (AS)

momaomai.notes: μωμάομαι, -ῶμαι (μῶμος), poët. and late prose, [in LXX: Pro.9:7 (מאוּם), Wis.10:14, Sir.31:18 * ;] to find fault with, blame: 2Co.8:20; pass., 2Co.6:3.† (AS)

momos.notes: μῶμος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX, of physical blemishes : Lev.21:17 ff., Deu.15:21, Sng.4:7, al. (מאוּם); of mental defect, Sir.20:24, al. ;] __1. in cl. poets and late prose, blame, disgrace. __2. In LXX, perb. because of…

momphemempsis.notes: μομφή, ῆς, ἡ (μέμφομαι), poët. form of μέμψις, blame, com­plaint: Col.3:13.† (AS)

mone.notes: μονή, -ῆς, ἡ (μένω), [in LXX: 1Ma.7:38 * ;] __1. in cl., __(a) a staying, abiding; __(b) continuance (LXX, 1 with) __2. In late Gk., __(a) a statson (Paus.); __(b) an abode: Jhn.14:2, 23; __(with) a monastery (cf.…

monogenes.notes: μονογενής, -ές (μόνος, γένος), [in LXX: Jdg.11:34, Psa.22:20 25:16 35:17 (יָחִיד), Tob.3:15 6:10, 14 8:17, Wis.7:22, Bar.4:16 * ;] only, only begotten (DCG, ii, 281), of sons and daughters : Luk.7:12 8:42 9:38,…

monon.notes: μόνον, see: μόνος. μόνος, -η, -ον, [in LXX chiefly for לְבַד ;] __1. adj., alone, solitary, forsaken: with verb., Mat.14:23, Mrk.6:47, Luk.9:36, al.; with pron., Mat.18:15, Mrk.9:2, al.; with subst., Mrk.9:8,…

monoo.notes: μονόω, -ῶ (μόνος), [in Aq.: Gen.49:6 ;] to leave alone, forsake: of a childless widow, pf. ptcp. pass., 1Ti.5:5.† (AS)

monophthalmos.notes: μον-όφθαλμος, -ον (μόνος), Ionic and κοινή, one-eyed, having one eye: Mat.18:9, Mrk.9:47.† (AS)

monos.notes: μόνος, -η, -ον, [in LXX chiefly for לְבַד ;] __1. adj., alone, solitary, forsaken: with verb., Mat.14:23, Mrk.6:47, Luk.9:36, al.; with pron., Mat.18:15, Mrk.9:2, al.; with subst., Mrk.9:8, Luk.4:8, al.; pleonast.,…

moraino.notes: μωραίνω (μωρός), [in LXX: Isa.19:11, Jer.10:14 51:17 (בָּעַר ni.), 2Ki.24:10, Isa.44:25 R (שָׂכַל ni., pi.)* ;] __1. cl., to be foolish, play the fool. __2. LXX and NT, causal, to make foolish: 1Co.1:20; pass., to…

moros.notes: μωρός, -ά, -όν [in LXX for נָבָל, etc.; frequently in Sir. ;] __1. prop., of the nerves, dull, sluggish (Hipp., Arist.). __2. Of the mind, dull, stupid, foolish: Mat.5:22 (see Field, Notes, 3 ff.) Mat.7:26 23:17, 19…

morphe.notes: μορφή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Jdg.8:18 A (תֹּאַר), Job.4:16 (תְּמוּנָה), Isa.44:13 (תַּבְנִית), Dan LXX 3:19 (צֶלֶם), Da TH Dan.4:33 5:6, 9-10 7:28 (זִיו), Tob.1:13, Wis.18:1, 4Ma.15:4 * ;] form, shape, appearance (Hom.,…

morphoo.notes: μορφόω, -ῶ (μορφή), [in Aq.: Isa.44:13 * ;] to form: figuratively, Gal.4:19 (cf. μετα-, συμ-μορφόω) (AS)

morphosis.notes: μόρφωσις, -εως, ἡ (μορφόω), __1. a forming, shaping (Theophr.). __2. form, outline, semblance: Rom.2:20; opposite to δύναμις, 2Ti.3:5.† SYN.: μορφή (which see), σχῆμα (AS)

moschopoieo.notes: μοσχο-ποιέω, -ῶ to make a calf (as an image) : Act.7:41 (LXX, ἐποίησε μόσχον).† (AS)

moschos.notes: μόσχος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for פַּר, also for שׁוֹר, עֵגֶל. etc. ;] __1. a young shoot or twig. __2. ὁ, ἡ, μ., offspring; __(a) of men; __(b) of animals; most frequently (as always in LXX), a calf, bullock,…

mousesmoses.notes: Μωυσῆς (Μωϋσῆς, T; Μωσῆς, Rec.), -έως, dative - (as LXX: Exo.5:20, al.), and -εῖ, accusative -ῆν (ac LXX) and -έα (Luk.16:29 only) (Heb. מֹשֶׁה), Moses: Mat.8:4 17:3-4, al.; νόμος Μωυσέως, Luk.2:22 24:44, Jhn.7:23,…

mousikos.notes: μουσικός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Gen.31:27, Eze.26:13 (שִׁיר), Dan LXX TH Dan.3:5 ff. (זְמָר), Sir.22:6, al. ;] skilled in the arts, esp. in music; as subst., ὁ μ., a minstrel, musician: Rev.18:22.† (AS)

muelos.notes: μυελός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX: Gen.45:18 (חֶלֶב), Job.21:24 (מֹחַ) Job.33:24 * ;] marrow: Heb.4:12.† (AS)

mueo.notes: μυέω, -ῶ (μύω, to shut the mouth), [in LXX: 3Ma.2:30 * ;] to initiate into the mysteries (so chiefly in cl.; LXX, l.with); hence, to instruct: pass., Php.4:12 (RV, I have learned the secret).† (AS)

mukaomai.notes: μυκάσμαι, -ῶμαι in cl. chiefly poët., [in Sm.: Job.6:5 * ;] prop., of oxen (onomatop.), to low, bellow; of a lion, to roar: Rev.10:3.† (AS)

mukterizo.notes: μυκτηρίζω (μυκτήρ, the nose), [in LXX: 4Ki.19:21, Job.22:19, Psa.80:6, al. (לָעַג), Pro.1:30 (נָאַץ) Pro.15:20 (בָּזָה), 1Ma.7:34, al. ;] to turn up the nose or sneer at, mock: pass., Gal.6:7 (cf. ἐκ-μυκτηρίζω).† (AS)

mulos.notes: μύλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for רֵחֶה, Num.11:8, Deu.24:6, al. ;] __1. = μύλη, a mill (Strab., Plut., LXX) : Mat.24:41, Rev.18:22. __2. a mill-stone (Anth.) : Rev.18:21 (T); μ. ὀνικός, Mat.18:6, Mrk.9:42 (see Swete, in…

muopazo.notes: μυ-ωπάζω (μύωψ, closing the eyes, short-sighted; μύω, ὤψ) to be short-sighted: 2Pe.1:9 (R, mg., closing his eyes; see ICC, in l).† (AS)

mura.notes: Μύρα, Μύρρα (LT, Tr., WH), -ων, τά, Myra, a city of Lycia : Act.27:5.† (AS)

murias.notes: μυριάς, -άδος, ἡ (μυρίος), [in LXX chiefly for רְבָבָה ;] ten thousand, a myriad: pl., Act.19:19, Rev.5:11 9:16; hyperb., of vast numbers, Luk.12:1, Act.21:20, Heb.12:12, Ju 14.† (AS)

murioi.notes: μυρίος, -α, -ον __1. numberless, countless, infinite: 1Co.4:15 14:19. __2. As a definite numeral, in pl., μύριοι, -αι, -α, ten thousand: Mat.18:24.† (AS)

murizo.notes: μυρίζω (μύρον), Ionic and poët. (comic), to anoint: Mrk.14:8.† SYN.: see: ἀλείφω, and cf. μύρον (AS)

muron.notes: μύρον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for שֶׁמֶן, Pro.27:9, Psa.133:2, al. ;] ointment: Mat.26:7, 12, Mrk.14:3-5, Luk.7:37-38, 46 23:56, Jhn.11:2 12:3, 5, Rev.18:13.† SYN.: ἔλαιον, q.v (AS)

musia.notes: Μυσία, -ας, ἡ Mysia, a province of Asia Minor: Act.16:7-8.† (AS)

musterion.notes: μυστήρισν, -ου, τό (μυέω), [in LXX: Dan LXX TH Dan.2:18 ff. (רָז), Tob.12:7, 11, Jdth.2:2, Wis.2:22 6:22 14:15, 23, Sir.3:18 22:22 27:16-17, 21, 2Ma.13:21 * ;] __1. that which is known to the μύστης (initiated), a…

muthos.notes: μῦθος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Wis.17:4 A, Sir.20:19 * ;] __1. speech, con­versation. __2. (a) a story, narrative (Hom.); __(b) later, opposite to λόγος (a true narrative) = Lat. fabula, a myth, fable, fiction: 1Ti.1:4 4:7,…

naasson.notes: Ναασσών, ὁ indecl. (Heb. נַחְשׁוֹן), Naasson: Mat.1:4, Luk.3:32.† (AS)

nachor.notes: Ναχώρ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. נָחוֹר), Nahor: Luk.3:34.† (AS)

nain.notes: Ναίν (Rec. Ναΐν), ἡ, indecl. (Heb. נָאָה), Nain, a village of Galilee: Luk.7:11.† (AS)

naos.notes: ναός, -οῦ, ὁ (Att.. νεώς; ναίω, to inhabit), [in LXX (νεώς, 2Ma.6:2, al.) chiefly for הֵיכָל ;] __1. a temple (Hom., Find., al.). __2. The inmost part of a temple, the shrine (Hat., Xen., al.); in NT, __(a)…

naoum.notes: Ναούμ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. נַחוּם;), Nahum: Luk.3:25.† (AS)

nardos.notes: νάρδος, -ου, ἡ (Heb. נֵרְדְּ, both from Sanscrit narda, see Boisacq, see word), [in LXX: Sng.1:12 4:13-14 (נֵרְדְּ)* ;] nard; __(a) an Indian plant, the Nardostachys nardus jatamansi, used for the preparation of a…

narkissos.notes: the narcissus , (Homeric Hymns), etc. From ναρκάω, because of its narcotic properties. (ML)

nathan.notes: Ναθάμ (Rec. Ναθάν), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. נָתַן), Nathan: Luk.3:31.† (AS)

nathanael.notes: Ναθαναήλ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. נְתַנְאֵל), Nathaniel, prob. to be identified with Bartholomew (which see): Jhn.1:45-49 21:2.† (AS)

nauageo.notes: ναυαγέω, -ῶ (ναῦς, + ἄγνυμι, to break) to suffer shipwreck: 2Co.11:25; metaphorically, before περὶ τ. πίστιν, 1Ti.1:19.† (AS)

naukleros.notes: ναύ-κληρος, -ου, ὁ (ναῦς, κλῆρος), a shipowner, shipmaster: Act.27:11.† (AS)

naus.notes: ναῦς, νεώς accusative ναῦν, ἡ, [in LXX for עָנִי, אֲנִיָּה ;] a ship: Act.27:41 (elsewhere in NT always τ. πλοῖον; see M, Pro.25:1-28 f.; Bl., Gosp., 186 f.).† (AS)

nautes.notes: ναύτης, -ου, ὁ (ναῦς), [in Aq.: Eze.27:9; Sm.: Eze.27:29 * ;] a seaman, sailor: Act.27:27, 30, Rev.18:17.† (AS)

nazarenos.notes: Ναζαρηνός, -οῦ, ὁ (on the Semitic form, see Dalman, Gr., 141 n.) a Nazarene: Mrk.1:24 10:47 14:67 16:6, Luk.4:34 24:19.† (AS)

nazarethnazara.notes: Ναζαρά (Mat.4:13­ L, -άθ­Luk.4:16) Ναζαρέθ (Mat.21:11, Act.10:38), Ναζαρέτ (so always Rec.; WH, in foll, instances, where -έθ, T), ἡ, indecl. (Semitic form uncertain), Nazareth: Mat.2:23, Mrk.1:9, Luk.1:26 2:4, 39…

nazoraios.notes: Ναζωραῖος, -ου, ὁ (= -ρηνός, which see), a Nazarene: Mat.2:23 (LXX) Mat.26:71, Luk.18:37, Jhn.18:5, 7 19:19, Act.2:22 3:6 4:10 6:14 22:8 24:5 26:9.† (AS)

neanias.notes: νεανίας, -ου, ὁ (νεάν = νέος) [in LXX for נַעַר, בָּחוּר ;] a young man: Act.7:58 20:9 23:17-18.† (AS)

neaniskos.notes: νεανίσκος, -ου, ὁ (dimin. of νεανίας) [in LXX chiefly for נַעַר, also for בָּחוּר, etc. ;] a young man, youth: Mat.19:20, 22, Mrk.14:51 16:5, Luk.7:14, Act.2:17" (LXX) Act.23:18, 22, 1Jn.2:13-14; of an attendant (cf.…

neemannaiman.notes: Ναιμάν (Rec. Νεεμάν), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. נַעֲמָן), Naaman: Luk.4:27.† (AS)

nekroo.notes: νεκρόω, ῶ (νεκρός), to make dead, put to death; pass., to be dead: hyperbolically, of impotent age, Heb.11:12; σῶμα, Rom.4:19. Trop., of carnal impulses, τὰ μέλη, Col.3:5.† (AS)

nekros.notes: νεκρός, -α, -ον, [in LXX chiefly for מֵת ;] dead, __I. as adj., __1. prop.: Act.5:10 20:9, Jas.2:26, Rev.1:18, al.; ὡσεὶ ν., Mat.28:4, Mrk.9:26, Rev.1:17; of that which is subject to death, Rom.8:10. __2.…

nekrosis.notes: νέκρωσις, -εως, ἡ (νεκρόω), __1. a putting to death. __2. a state of death, death: Rom.4:19, 2Co.4:10 (see Deiss., LAE, 94).† (AS)

neokoros.notes: νεω-κόρος, -ου a temple-keeper; as honorary title given to a city (see DB, i, 722 b) : Act.19:35.† (AS)

neophutos.notes: νεό-φυτος, -ον (νέος, φύω) [in LXX: Job.14:9, Psa.144:12, Isa.5:7 (נֶטַע), Psa.128:3 (שְׁתִל)* ;] newly planted (LXX). Metaphorical, as subst., ὁ ν., a new convert, neophyte, novice: 1Ti.3:6† (AS)

neos.notes: νέος, -α, -ον [in LXX for נַעַר (Gen.37:2, Exo_33:11, al.), חָדָשׁ (Lev.23:16, Num.28:26, al.), etc.; compar. -ώτερος for קָטָן, צָעִיר, etc. ;] __1. young, youthful: Tit.2:4. __2. new (prop., in respect of time;…

neossos.notes: νοσσός (νεοσσός, Rec., as in cl. Att.. -ττός; Phryn. rejects the dissyl. form), -οῦ, ὁ (νόος), [in LXX chiefly for בֵּן ;] a young bird: Luk.2:24 (LXX).† (AS)

neoterikos.notes: νεωτερικός, -ή, -όν (νεώτερος), [in LXX: 3Ma.4:8 * ;] = νεανικός, youthful, esp. of qualities: ἐπιθυμίαι, 2Ti.2:22 (Polyb.).† (AS)

neotes.notes: νεότης, -ητος, ἡ (νέος), [in LXX chiefly for נָעוּר ;] youth: Mrk.10:20, Luk.18:21, Act.26:4, 1Ti.4:12.† (AS)

nephaleos.notes: νηφάλιος (-λεος, Rec., in 1Ti, ll. with), -ον (in cl., -α, -ον), (νήφω), __1. in cl., of drink, not mixed with wine. __2. In later writers (Plut., al.), of persons, sober, temperate: 1Ti.3:2 3:11, Tit.2:2.† (AS)

nephele.notes: νεφέλη, -ης, ἡ (νέφος), [in LXX chiefly for עָנָן, also for עָב, etc. ;] a cloud (single and specific as opposite to νεφος, a great indefinite mass of vapour): Mat.17:5 24:30 26:64, Mrk.9:7 13:26 14:62, Luk.9:34-35…

nepho.notes: νήφω to be sober, abstain from wine; metaphorically, of moral alertness, to be sober, calm, circumspect: 1Th.5:6 5:8, 2Ti.4:5 (see Ellic., in l), 1Pe.1:13 4:7 5:8 (cf. ἐκ-, ἐκ-νήφω, and see MM, xvii).† SYN.:…

nephos.notes: νέφος, -ους, τό [in LXX for עָב, שַׁחַק, עָנַן ;] a mass of clouds, a cloud (cf. νεφέλη); metaphorically as in Hom., Hdt., al.), of a dense throng: Heb.12:1.† (AS)

nephros.notes: νεφρός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX for כִּלְיָה, Exo.29:13, al.; metaphorically, Psa.7:9 16:7 26:2, Wis.1:6, al. ;] a kidney; pl., the kidneys, reins; metaphorically, of the will and affections: ν. καί καρδίαι (thoughts),…

nephthaleim.notes: Νεφθαλείμ (-λίμ, WH in Re, l.with), ὁ indecl. (Heb. נַפְתָּלִי) Naphtali: Mat.4:13, 15" (LXX) Rev.7:6.† (AS)

nepiazo.notes: νηπιάζω (νήπιος), (Hippocr., = νηπιαχεύω, Hom.) to be a babe: 1Co.14:20.† (AS)

nepios.notes: νήπιος, -α, -ον [in LXX chiefly for עוֹלֵל, also for פְּתִי, etc. ;] infant; of children and minors: Mat.21:16 (LXX), 1Co.13:11, Gal.4:1 (see Lft., in l). Metaphorical, childish, unskilled, simple (Psa.19:8, Pro.1:32…

nereus.notes: Nereus , a sea-god, son of Πόντος (the sea) , father of the Nereids, (Hesiod) (ML)

neri.notes: Νηρεί (Rec. -ρί), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. נֵרִיָּה), Neri: Luk.3:27.† (AS)

nesion.notes: νησίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of νῆσος), = νησίς (Hdt., Thuc., al.) a small island: Act.27:16.† (AS)

nesos.notes: νῆσος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for אִי ;] an island: Act.13:6 27:26 28:1, 7 28:9, 11 Rev.1:9 6:14 16:20.† (AS)

nesteia.notes: νηστεία, -ας, ἡ (νηστεύω), [in LXX for צוֹם ;] fasting, a fast; __(a) of voluntary abstinence from food: Mat.17:21 (WH, R, txt., om.), Mrk.9:29 (WH, txt., R, txt., om.), Luk.2:37, Act.14:23; of the Day of Atonement,…

nesteuo.notes: νηστεύω (νῆστις), [in LXX for צוּם ;] to fast (Arist., Aristoph., al.): Mat.4:2 6:16-18 9:14-15 Mrk.2:18-20, Luk.5:33-35 18:12, Act.13:2-3.† (AS)

nestis.notes: νῆστις, -ιος, ὁ, ἡ (νη-, neg. prefix, + ἐσθίω), in cl., chiefly poët., [in LXX: Dan LXX 6:18 (19) (טְוָת)* ;] not eating, fasting: Mat.15:32, Mrk.8:3.† (AS)

netho.notes: νήθω [in LXX for טָוָה, שָׁזַר hoph., Exo.26:31 35:25, al. ;] to spin: Mat.6:28, Luk.12:27.† (AS)

neuo.notes: νεύω [in LXX: Pro.4:5 21:1 * ;] to nod or beckon, as a sign : with dative of person(s) and inf., Jhn.13:24, Act.24:10 (cf. δια-, ἐκ-, ἐν-, ἐπι-, κατα-νεύω).† (AS)

nikao.notes: νικἀω, -ῶ (νίκη), [in LXX: Psa.51:4 (זָכָה), Pro.6:25 frequently (חָמַד); in 4Mac ;] to conquer, prevail: absol., of Christ, Rev.3:21 6:2; C. inf., Rev.5:5; of Christians, Rev.2:7, 11 2:17, 26 3:5, 12 3:21 21:7;…

nike.notes: νίκη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: 1Ch.29:11 (נֶצַח), frequently in 1-4 Mac ;] victory: 1Jn.5:4.† (AS)

nikolaites.notes: Νικολαΐτης, -αυ, ὁ a Nicolaitan: pl., Rev.2:6, 15.† (AS)

nikopolis.notes: Νικόπολις, -εως, ἡ Nicopolis, prob. the city of that name in Epirus (CGT, in l): Tit.3:12† (AS)

nikos.notes: νῖκος, -ους, τό late form of νίκη, [in LXX: La 3:18 (נֶצַח), I Est.3:9, 2Ma.10:38, 4Ma.17:12; εἰς ν. (instead of εἰς τέλος, Job.14:20), 2Ki.2:26, Job.36:7, Amo.1:11 8:7, Jer.3:5, La 5:20 (נֶצַח, as נ֝ in Syr., =…

nineuites.notes: Νινευείτης (Rec. -ενίτης, L, -ΐτης), -ου, ὁ a Ninevite: Mat.12:41, Luk.11:30, 32.† (AS)

nipto.notes: νίπτω late form of νίζω, [in LXX chiefly for רָחַץ ;] to wash, usually of a part of the body: with accusative of person(s), Jhn.13:8; τ. πόδας, Jhn.13:5-6, 8 13:12, 14, 1Ti.5:10; mid., reflexive, to wash oneself:…

noe.notes: Νῶε (as LXX, FlJ, who also gives Νώεος, Νῶχος), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. נֹחַ) Noah: Mat.24:37-38, Luk.3:36 17:26-27 Heb.11:7, 1Pe.3:20, 2Pe.2:5.† (AS)

noema.notes: νόημα, -τος, τό (νοέω) [in LXX: Sir.21:11, Bar.2:8, 3Ma.5:30 * ;] a thought, purpose, design: 2Co.2:11 10:5 11:3, Php.4:7. Meton., of the mind, 2Co.3:14 4:4.† (AS)

noeo.notes: νοιεω νοέω, -ῶ (νοῦς), [in LXX chiefly for בִּין, also for שָׂכַל hi., etc ;] __1. to perceive with the mind, understand (for the phrase νοῶν κ. φρονῶν, in wills, see MM, xvii): absol., Mat.16:9, Mrk.8:17; with…

nome.notes: νομή, -ῆς, ἡ (νέμω, to pasture), [in LXX chiefly for מִרְעֶה, also for נָוֶה, etc. ;] __1. a pasture, pasturage: figuratively, Jhn.10:9 __2. a grazing, feeding; metaphorically, of a spreading sore, 2Ti.2:17…

nomikos.notes: νομικός, -ή, -όν (νόμος), [in LXX: 4Ma.5:4 * ;] __1. relating to law: μάχαι, Tit.3:9. __2. learned in the law; as subst., ὁ ν. (EV, lawyer): Mat.22:35, Luk.10:25, Tit.3:13; pl., Luk.7:30 11:45-46, 52 14:3 (cf. MM,…

nomimos.notes: νομίμως adv. (νόμιμος, conformable to law), [in LXX: 4Ma.6:18 * ;] rightly, lawfully: 1Ti.1:8, 2Ti.2:5.† (AS)

nomisma.notes: νόμισμα, -τος, τό (νομίζω), [in LXX: Est.8:36 (דָּת), Neh.7:71 R (דַּרְכְּמוֹן), 1Ma.15:6 * ;] __1. that which is established by usage, a custom. __2. The current coin of a state: Mat.22:19.† (AS)

nomizo.notes: νομίζω (νόμος), [in LXX: Wis.13:2 17:3, Sir.29:4, 1-28; 2Ma.4:1-50, 4Ma.8:1-29 * ;] __1. to practise, hold by custom: Act.16:13 (Rec., but see infr.). __2. to deem, consider, suppose: Mat.5:17 10:34 20:10, Luk.2:44…

nomodidaskalos.notes: νομο-διδάσκαλος, -ου, ὁ a teacher of the law: Luk.5:17, Act.5:34, 1Ti.1:7 (NT and eccl. only; cf. νομοδείκτης, -διδάκτης, Plut.).† SYN.: γραμματεύς, q.v (AS)

nomos.notes: νόμος, -ου, ὁ (νέμω, to deal out, distribute), [in LXX chiefly for תּוֹרָה, also for חֻקָּה, etc. ;] that which is assigned, hence, usage, custom, then law; in NT (only in Mt, Jo, Ja, and the Lucan and Pauline bks.);…

nomothesia.notes: νομοθεσία, -ας, ἡ (νόμος, τίθημι), [in LXX: 2Ma.6:23, 4Ma.5:35 4Mac 17:16 * ;] legislation, lawgiving: Rom.9:4.† (AS)

nomotheteo.notes: νομοθετέω, -ῶ [in LXX for יָרָה hi. ;] __1. intrans., to make laws; pass., to be furnished with laws: Heb.7:11. __2. Trans., to ordain by law, enact: pass., Heb.8:6.† (AS)

nomothetes.notes: νομο-θέτης, -ου, ὁ (νόμος, τίθημι), [in LXX: Psa.9:20 * ;] a law­giver: Jas.4:12.† (AS)

noseo.notes: νοσέω, -ῶ (νόσος), [in LXX (metaphorically): Wis.17:8 * ;] to be sick: metaphorically, of mental ailment, before περί, 1Ti.6:4 (cf. Plat., Mor., 546 d).† (AS)

nosos.notes: νόσος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for חֲלִי, etc. ;] disease, sickness: Mat.4:23-24 8:17 (Aq.) Mat.9:35 10:1, Mrk.1:34, Luk.4:40 6:17 7:21 9:1, Act.19:12.† SYN.: see: ἀσθένεια (AS)

nosphizo.notes: νοσφίζω (νόσφι, apart, aside), [in LXX: Jos.7:1 (לָקַח), 2Ma.4:32 * ;] __1. in Hom., as depon., to turn away (from), abandon. __2. After Hom., in act., to set apart, remove. Mid., to set apart for oneself, peculate,…

nossia.notes: νοσσιά, -ᾶς, ἡ (νοσσός), late form of cl., νεοσσιά, [in LXX chiefly for קֵן ;] __1. a nest of birds. __2. a brood of young birds: Luk.13:34.† (AS)

nossion.notes: νοσσίον, -ου, τό dimin. of νοσσός, which see, [in LXX: Psa.84:3 (אֶפְרֹחַ) * ;] a young bird: Mat.23:37.† (AS)

nothos.notes: νόθος, -η, -ον [in LXX: Wis.4:3 * ;] a bastard, base born, i.e. born of a slave or concubine: Heb.12:8.† (AS)

nothros.notes: νωθρός, -ά, -όν [in LXX: Pro.22:29 (חָשֹׁךְ), Sir.4:29 11:12 * ;] sluggish, slothful: Heb.5:11 6:12 (for similar usage in π., see MM, xvii). SYN.: ἀργός (which see), βραδύς (AS)

noton.notes: νῶτος, -ου, ὁ in Att.. most frequently τὸ νῶτον, and in pl. always τὰ νῶτα, [in LXX, ὁ ν., pl., οἱ νῶτοι and τὰ νῶτα, chiefly for כָּתֵף, also for עֹרֶף, מֹתֶן ;] /the back: Rom.11:10 (LXX).† (AS)

notos.notes: νότος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for נֶגֶב, also for דָּרוֹם, תֵּימָן and קָדִים ;] __1. prop., the south wind: Luk.12:55, Act.27:13 28:13 __2. South: Luk.13:29, Rev.21:13 __3. the South, as a region (cf. נֶגֶב):…

noumenia.notes: νεομηνία (Att.. contr., νουμ-, Rec.), -ας, ἡ (νέος, μήν), [in LXX chiefly for חֹדֶשׁ ;] new moon: of the Jewish festival, Col.2:16.† (AS)

nounechos.notes: νουνεχῶς adv. (νοῦς, ἔχω) sensibly, discreetly: Mrk.12:34.† (AS)

nous.notes: νοῦς (contr. from νόος), ὁ, genitive, dative, νοός, νοΐ (late forms, = cl., νοῦ, νῷ; Bl., § 9, 3), accusative, νοῦν, [in LXX chiefly for לֵב, לֵבָב ;] __1. prop., of the ruling faculty, mind, understanding, reason…

nouthesia.notes: νουθεσία, -ας ἡ (νουθετέω), = cl. νουθέτησις [in LXX: Wis.6:6 * ;] admonition: 1Co.10:11, Eph.6:4, Tit.3:10 (Aristoph., Diod., al.).† (AS)

noutheteo.notes: νουθετέω, -ῶ (νοῦς, τίθημι, hence, put in mind), [in LXX: 1Ki.3:13 (כָּהָה pi.), Job.8:1-22 (יָסַר pi., בִּין), Wis.11:10 12:2, 26 * ;] to admonish, exhort: with accusative of person(s), Act.20:31, Rom.15:14,…

nuchthemeron.notes: νυχθήμερος, -α, -ον (νύξ, ἡμέρα, see B1., § 28, 4), lasting a night and a day; as subst., (τὸ) ν., a night and a day: 2Co.11:25 (pl., Or. Sib., 8, 203).† (AS)

numphas.notes: Νυμφᾶς, -ᾶ, ὁ (WH, R, mg., Νύμφα, which see), Nymphas: Col.4:15, R, txt. (cf. ICC, Lft., in l).† Νύμφα (Rec., R, txt., Νυμφᾶς, which see), -ης, ἡ, Nympha (see M, Pr., 48): Col.4:15, WH, R, mg.† (AS)

numphe.notes: νύμφη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for כַּלָּה ;] __1. cl., a bride, young wife, young woman: Mat.25:1, WH, mg., Jhn.3:29, Rev.18:23 21:2, 9 22:17. __2. As frequently in LXX (Gen.38:11, al., for כַּלָּה; __(a) bride;…

numphios.notes: νυμφίος, -ου, ὁ (νύμφη), [in LXX for חָתָן ;] a bridegroom: Mat.9:15 25:1, 5-6 25:10, Mrk.2:19-20, Luk.5:34-35, Jhn.2:9 3:29, Rev.18:23.† (AS)

numphon.notes: νυμφών, -ῶνος, ὁ (νύμφη), [in LXX: Tob.6:13,16* ;] the bride-chamber (Heb. חֻפָּה, LXX, παστός, Psa.19:5, Jol.2:16): Mat.22:10 (WE; γάμος, RV); οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ ν. (cf. cl. νυμφαγωγός, νυμφευτής), the bridegroom's friends…

nun.notes: νῦν, adv., [in LXX chiefly for עַתָּה ;] __1. prop., of time, now, i.e. at the present time: as opposite to past, Jhn.4:18, Act.7:52, Rom.13:11, 2Co.7:9, Col.1:24, al.; opposite to fut., Jhn.12:27, Rom.11:31, al.;…

nuni.notes: νῦνί an Attic strengthened form of νῦν (in cl. always of time, and most often strictly of the pres.), [in LXX : Job.5:1-27, Psa.2:1-12, 1-12, 4Ma.4:1-26, Ep. Je* ;] now; __1. of time: with praes., Act.24:13,…

nusso.notes: νύσσω (Att.. -ττω), [in LXX: Sir.22:19, 3Ma.5:14 * ;] to pierce: τ. πλευρὰν λόγχῃ, Jhn.19:34, [Mat.27:49], WH.† (AS)

nustazo.notes: νυστάζω (cf. νεύω), [in LXX for נוּם, etc ;] to nod in sleep, fall asleep: Mat.25:5. Metaphorical, of negligence or delay, ἡ ἀπώλεια αὐτῶν οὐ νυστάζει (cf. Psa.121:4, Isa.5:27) : 2Pe.2:3.† (AS)

nux.notes: νύξ genitive νυκτός, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly for לַיִל ;] night: Mat.12:40, Mrk.6:48, Jhn.13:30, al.; genitive temp. (of the time within which something happens; M, Pr., 73; B1., § 36, 13), νυκτός, by night, Mat.2:14,…

obed.notes: Ἰωβήδ (WH, -βήλ, Lk, l.with; Rec., RV, Ὠβήδ), ὁ, indecl., Jo bed (Obed): Mat.1:5, Luk.3:32.† (AS)

ochleo.notes: ὀχλέω, -ῶ (ὄχλος), [in LXX: Tob.6:7, 3Ma.5:41 * ;] to move, disturb; hence, generally, to trouble, vex: pass., Act.5:6 (act. absol., = pass., to be in a tumult, 3Mac, l.with; cf. ἐν-, παρ-εν-οχλέω, and see MM,…

ochlopoieo.notes: ὀχλο-ποιέω, -ῶ to gather a crowd, make a riot: Act.17:5 (not elsewhere).† (AS)

ochlos.notes: ὄχλος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX for הָמוֹן (chiefly in Da TH), קָהָל ,חַיִל, etc. ;] __1. a moving crowd or multitude of persons, a throng: Mat.9:23, Mrk.2:4, Luk.5:1, Jhn.5:13, al.; pl., Mat.5:1, Mrk.10:1, Luk.3:7, and…

ochuroma.notes: ὀχύρωμα, -τος, τό (ὀχυρόω, to fortify, make firm), [in LXX for מִבְצָר, etc. ;] a stronghold, fortress (Psa.89:40, Nam.3:12, 14 al.); in LXX and NT, also metaphorically of that in which confidence is placed…

ode.notes: ᾠδή, -ῆς, ή [in LXX chiefly for שִׁיר ;] a song, ode, whether sad or joyful; in LXX and NT always in praise of God or Christ: Rev.5:9 14:3 15:3; ᾠ πνευματικαί Eph.5:19, Col.3:16.† (AS)

odin.notes: ὠδίν (late form of ὠδίς), -ῖνος, ἡ [in LXX for חֵבֶל חֶבֶל (and wrongly for חֶבֶל חֶבֶל, cf. Ac, l.with), חִיל, etc. ;] a birth pang, travail pain: 1Th.5:3; metaphorically, of extreme suffering, Mat.24:8, Mrk.13:8;…

odino.notes: ὠδίνω [in LXX chiefly for חוּל, also for חָבַל pi., etc. ;] to have birthpangs, to travail: Gal.4:27 (LXX), Rev.12:2; metaphorically, Gal.4:19 (cf. συν-ωδίνω).† (AS)

odous.notes: ὀδούς, -όντος, ὁ [in LXX for שֵׁן ;] a tooth: Mat.5:38, Mrk.9:18, Act.7:54; pl., Rev.9:8, ὁ βρυγμὸς (which see) τ. ὀδόντων, Mat.8:12 13:42, 50 22:13 24:51 25:30, Luk.13:28.† (AS)

odunao.notes: ὀδυνάω, -ῶ (ὀδύνη), [in LXX: Zec.9:5 (חוּל), Zec.12:10 (מָרַר hi.), La 1:13 (דָּוֶה), Wis.14:24, al. ;] to cause pain or suffering; pass. and mid., to suffer pain, be tormented or greatly distressed: Luk.2:48…

odune.notes: ὀδύνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for יָגוֹן, מַר, etc. (26 words in all) ;] pain, distress, of body or mind : Rom.9:2, 1Ti.6:10.† (AS)

odurmos.notes: ὀδυρμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ὀδύρομαι, to lament), [in LXX : Jer.31:15 (תַּמְרוּר), 2Ma.11:6 * ;] lamentation, mourning: Mat.2:18 (LXX), 2Co.7:7.† (AS)

ogdoekonta.notes: eighty , Lat. octoginta, (Thucydides), etc.:—;ionic and doric ὀγδώκοντα, (Iliad by Homer) (ML)

ogdoos.notes: ὄγδοος, -η, -ον the eighth. Luk.1:59, Act.7:3" (LXX), Rev.17:11 21:20; one of eight, with seven others (usually, in this sense, with αὐτός added, but cf. Plat., Legg., iii, 695 with; Plut., Pelop., 13; 2Ma.5:27):…

ogkos.notes: ὄγκος, -ου, ὁ bulk, mass; metaphorically, an encumbrance: Heb.12:1.† SYN.: βάρος, a weight; φορτίον, a burden, that which is borne (AS)

ohios.notes: οἷος, -α, -ον relat. pron., qualitative (related to ὅς as qualis to qui), what sort or manner of, such as, in NT usually without its correl. τοιοῦτος : Mat.24:21, Mrk.9:3, 2Co.12:20, 1Th.1:5, 2Ti.3:11, Rev.16:18;…

ohu.notes: oὗ (prop. genitive of ὅς), adv. of place, where, whither; __(a) in answer to the question "where?" (= ubi): Mat.2:9 18:20, Luk.4:16-17 23:53, Act.1:13 2:2 7:29 12:12 16:13 20:6, 8 25:10 28:14, Rom.9:26" (LXX),…

ohutoohutos.notes: οὕτως, rarely (Bl., §5, 4; WH, App., 146f.) οὕτω, adv. (οὗτος), [in LXX chiefly for כֵּן ;] in this way, so, thus; __1. referring to what precedes: Mat.5:16 6:30, Mrk.10:43 14:59, Luk.1:25 2:48 15:7, Jhn.3:8,…

oikeios.notes: οἰκεῖος, -α, -ον (οἶκος), [in LXX for דּוֹד, שְׁאֵר, etc.; in Isa.58:7, οἰ. τοῦ σπέρματος for בָּשָׂר ;] in or of the house (opposite to ξένος, ἀλλότριος); __(a) of things; τὰ οἰ., household affairs or goods; __(b)…

oikema.notes: οἴκημα, -τος, τό (οἰκέω), [in LXX: Eze.16:24 (גַב), Tob.2:4, Wis.13:15 * ;] a dwelling. As a euphemism for other definite terms (e.g. brothel. Hdt., ii, 121; cf. Ez, l.with), a prison (Thus., iv, 47 f.); Act.12:7.† (AS)

oikeo.notes: οἰκέω, -ῶ (οἶκος), [in LXX chiefly for יָשַׁב ;] __(a) trans., to inhabit: with accusative, 1Ti.6:16; __(b) intrans., to dwell: before μετά (of married life), 1Co.7:12-13; metaphorically, before ἐν: ἀγαθόν, Rom.7:18;…

oiketerion.notes: οἰκητήριον, -ου, τό (οἰκητήρ = οἰκήτωρ, an inhabitant), [in LXX: 2Ma.11:2, 3Ma.2:15 * ;] a habitation: Ju 6; trop., 2Co.5:2.† (AS)

oiketes.notes: οἰκέτης, -ου, ὁ (οἰκέω), [in LXX for עֶבֶד ;] a house-servant: Luk.16:13, Rom.14:4; pl., Act.10:7, 1Pe.2:18 (In Plat., Hdt., Sir.4:30 6:11, the pl. includes all the inmates of the house, the familia, οἰκετεία.)†…

oikia.notes: οἰκία, -ας, ἡ (οἶκος), [in LXX chiefly (very freq.) for בַּיִת ;] a house, dwelling: Mat.2:11 7:24-27, Mrk.1:29, al.; ἐν οἰκίᾳ (= cl. κατ ̓ οἰκίαν), at home, Luk.8:27; εἰς οἰ., II Jn 10; οἰ, with genitive of…

oikiakos.notes: οἰκιακός (in Plut., al., -ειακός), ή, όν (οἰκία), = οἰκεῖος belonging to the household, one's own: Mat.10:36; opposite to οἰκοδεσπότης, Mat.10:25.† (AS)

oikodespotes.notes: οἰκο-δεσπότης, -ου, ὁ (οἶκος, δεσπότης), the master of a house, a householder: Mat.10:25 13:27 20:11 24:43, Mrk.14:14, Luk.12:39 13:25 14:21; ἄνθρωπος οἰ., Mat.13:52 20:1 21:33; pleonast., oἰ. τ. οἰκίας, Luk.22:11…

oikodome.notes: οἰκοδομή, -ῆς, ἡ (οἶκος, + δέμω, to build), [in LXX: 1Ch.29:1 A (בִּירָה), Eze.17:17 40:2 (בָּנָה, מִבְנֶה), Sir.22:16 40:19, al. ;] = cl. οἰκοδομία (which see), -δόμησις (Thuc., Plat.), __1. the act of building; in…

oikodomeo.notes: οἰκοδομέω, -ῶ (οἰκοδόμος), [in LXX chiefly for בָּנָה ;] to build a house, to build: absol., Luk.11:48 14:30 17:28; οἱ οἰκοδομοῦντες, the builders (as Psa.118:22, בָּנָה), Mat.21:42, Mrk.12:10, Luk.20:17, 1Pe.2:7"…

oikonomeo.notes: οἰκονομέω, -ῶ (οἰκονόμος), [in LXX: Psa.112:5 (כּוּל pilp.), 2Ma.3:14 * ;] to manage as house-steward, be a steward: absol., Luk.16:2; hence, generally (see: οἰκονομία), to manage, regulate, arrange (cl., 2Mac, Lc.,…

oikonomia.notes: οἰκονομία, -ας, ἡ (οἰκονομέω), [in LXX: Isa.22:19, 21 (מַצָּב, מֶמְשָׁלָה)* ;] __1. prop. (Plat., Arist.), the office of οἰκονόμος, stewardship: Luk.16:2-4. __2. In later writers (Plut., al.; see AR on Eph.1:10; MM,…

oikonomos.notes: οἰκονόμος, -ου, ὁ (οἶκος + νέμω, to manage) [in LXX chiefly for עַל ;] __1. prop., the manager of a household or estate, a house- or land-steward (usually a slave or freedman): Luk.12:42 16:1, 3 16:8, 1Co.4:2,…

oikos.notes: οἶκος, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for בַּיִת, also for אֹהֶל ,הֵכָל, etc. ;] __1. prop., a house, dwelling: Act.2:2 19:16; with genitive poss., Mat.9:6, 7 Mrk.2:11, Luk.1:23, al.; with genitive attrib., ἐμπορίου,…

oikoumene.notes: οἰκουμένη, -ης, ἡ (fern. Pres. pass. ptcp. of οἰκέω; sc. γῆ) [in LXX chiefly for תֵּבֵל, אֶרֶץ ;] the inhabited earth; __(a) in cl., the countries occupied by Greeks, as disting. from barbarian lands (Hdt., Dem.,…

oikouros.notes: οἰκ-ουρός, -ον (οἶκος + οὖρος, a keeper) __1. watching or keeping the house; as subst., ἡ οἰ., a housekeeper (Soph., Eur.; see LS, see word). __2. keeping at home: Tit.2:5, Rec. (see Field, Notes, 220 ff.; CGT, in l,…

oikteiro.notes: οἰκτείρω (οἶκτος, pity), [in LXX for רָחַם pi., חָנַן, etc. ;] to pity, have compassion on. with accusative of person(s), Rom.9:15 (LXX).† SYN.: ἐλεέω, q.v (AS)

oiktirmon.notes: οἰκτίρμων, -ον (οἰκτείρω), in cl. poët, for ἐλεήμων (which see), [in LXX chiefly for רַחוּם ;] merciful: Luk.6:36, Jas.5:1.† (AS)

oiktirmos.notes: οἰκτιρμός, -οῦ, ὁ (οἰκτείρω), [in LXX, usually in pl., chiefly for רַחַם ;] compassion, pity. σπλάγχνα οἰκτιρμοῦ, a heart of compassion, Col.3:12; in pl. (as LXX for Heb. רַחַם), οἰ. τ. θεοῦ, Rom.12:1, Heb.10:28; ό…

oinophlugia.notes: οἰνοφλυγία, -ας, ἡ (φλύω, to bubble up, overflow), cf. -γέω, Deu.21:20 drunkenness, debauchery: 1Pe.4:3.† SYN.: see: κραιπάλη (AS)

oinopotes.notes: οἰνο-πότης, -ου, ὁ (οίνος + πότης, a drinker), poët. and late prose, [in LXX: Pro.23:20 (יַיִן סָבָא)* ;] a wine-drinker, wine-bibber: Mat.11:19, Luk.7:34.† (AS)

oinos.notes: οἶνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for יַיִן also for תִּירוֹשׁ, etc. ;] wine; Mat.9:17, Luk.1:15, Jhn.2:3, al.; οἴνῳ προσέχειν, 1Ti.3:8; δουλοῦσθαι, Tit.2:3. Metaphorical, Rev.14:8, 10 16:19 17:2 18:3 19:15. By meton.…

oiomai.notes: οἴομαι, οἶμαι [in LXX for הִנֵּה, Gen.37:7, al. ;] to suppose, expect, imagine; with accusative and inf., Jhn.21:25; with inf., Php.1:17; before ὅτι, Jas.1:7.† οἶμαι, see: οἴομαι SYN.. see: ἡγέομαι (AS)

okneo.notes: ὀκνέω, -ῶ (ὄκνος, shrinking, hesitation), [in LXX: Num.22:16 (מָנַע ni.), Jdg.18:9 (אָצַל ni.), Tob.12:6, 13, Jdth.12:13, Sir.7:35, 4Ma.14:4 * ;] to shrink from doing, hesitate to do (Hom., Thuc., al.); hence, to…

okneros.notes: ὀκνηρός, -ά, -όν (ὀκνέω), [in LXX forPro.6:6, 9 al. ;] shrinking, hesitating, timid: with dative (Bl., § 38, 2), Rom.12:11; hence, slothful: Mat.25:26. Of things, that which causes shrinking, irksome: Php.3:1.† (AS)

oktaemeros.notes: ὀκταήμερος, -ον (ὀκτώ, ἡμέρα), of the eighth day, eight days old: with dative ref., περιτομῇ., Php.3:5 (words of this class denote duration, cf. τεταρταῖος).† (AS)

okto.notes: ὀκτώ, οἱ, αἱ, τά indecl., eight: Luk.2:21, Jhn.5:5, al (AS)

olethros.notes: ὄλεθρος, -ον (ὄλλυμι, to destroy), [in LXX for שֹׁד. שָׁדַד, etc. ;] ruin, destruction, death: 1Th.5:3, 1Ti.6:9; αἰώνιος, 2Th.1:9 (L, txt., ὀλέθριος, which see); εἰς ὄ. τῆς σαρκός, for physical discipline, to destroy…

oligopistos.notes: ὀλιγό-πιστος, -ον of little faith or trust: Mat.6:30 8:26 14:31 16:8, Luk.12:28.† (AS)

oligopsuchos.notes: ὀλιγόφυχος, -ον [in LXX: Isa.35:4 (מָהַר ni.), etc. ;] faint-hearted: 1Th.5:14.† (AS)

oligoreo.notes: ὀλιγωρέω, -ῶ (ὀλίγος + ὤρα, care), [in LXX: Pro.3:11 (מָאַס)* ;] to esteem lightly, think little of: with genitive, Heb.12:5 (LXX),† (AS)

oligos.notes: ὀλίγος, -η, -ον (on οὐχ ὁλ., see infr.), [in LXX chiefly for מָעַט ;] of number, quantity, size, few, little, small, slight: Mat.9:37 15:34, Mrk.6:5 8:7, Luk.10:2 12:48 (sc. πληγάς, opposite to πολλάς), 1Ti.5:23,…

ololuzo.notes: ὀλολύζω (onomatop.), [in LXX chiefly for יָלַל hi. ;] (in Hom., of women crying to the gods in prayer or thanksgiving), to cry aloud: Jas.5:1.† (AS)

olothreuo.notes: ὀλοθρεύω (ὄλεθρος), late (Alex.) form of ὄλεθρεύω ω (cf. MGr., ξολοθρεύω) [in LXX for כָּרַת, שָׁחַת hi., etc. ;] to destroy: Heb.11:28 (cf. ἐξ-ολοθρεύω).† ὀλεθρεύω, see: ὀλοθρεύω ὀλοθρεύω (ὄλεθρος), late (Alex.)…

olothreutes.notes: ὀλοθρευτής (Rec. όλ-), -οῦ, ὁ (ὀλοθρεύω), a destroyer: 1Co.10:10 (not elsewhere).† (AS)

olumpas.notes: Ὀλυμπᾶς (perh. contr. fr. Ὀλνμπιόδωρος, Bl., § 29), -ᾶ, Olympas: Rom.16:15.† (AS)

olunthos.notes: ὄλυνθος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Sng.2:13 (פַּג)* ;] an unripe fig, which grows in winter and usually falls off in the spring : Rev.6:13.† (AS)

ombros.notes: ὄμβρος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Deu.32:2 (שָׂעִיר), Wis.16:16, al. ;] a storm of rain, a shower: Luk.12:54.† (AS)

omega.notes: Ω, ω, ὦ μέγα (cf. ὂ μικρόν), omega, ô, the twenty-fourth and last letter. As a numeral, ω᾽ = 800, ω, = 800,000. As a symbol of the last (= τὸ τέλος), τὸ Ὦ (Rec. Ω, L, ὦ, T, ω), the Omega: Rev.1:8 21:6 22:13 (cf.…

omma.notes: ὄμμα, -τος, τό in cl. chiefly poët.; [in LXX for עַיִן (עַיִן (Pro.5:1-23, Wis.2:1-24, 4Ma.3:1-21)* ;] an eye: p1., Mat.20:34 Mrk.8:23.† (AS)

omnumi.notes: ὀμνύω (so Hdt. and some Att.. writers) and ὄμνυμι (so generally in Att.. prose and always in Trag.; Mrk.14:71, -ύναι), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁבַע ni. ;] to swear, affirm by oath: Mat.26:74, Mrk.14:71, Heb.7:21;…

omos.notes: ὦμος, οὐ, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for שְׁכֶם, כָּתֵף ;] the shoulder: Mat.23:4, Luk.15:5.† (AS)

onar.notes: ὄναρ, τό indecl., used only in nom, and accusative sing. (the other cases are supplied by ὄνειρος), a dream: κατ᾽ ὄ. (in later writers only), in a dream, Mat.1:20 2:12-13, 19 2:22 27:19.† (AS)

onarion.notes: ὀνάριον, -ου, τό (dimin. of ὄνος), a young ass: Jhn.12:14.† (AS)

oneidismos.notes: ὀνειδισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ὀνειδίζω) [in LXX chiefly for חֶרְפָּה ;] a reproach: Rom.15:3, Heb.10:33; εἰς ὀ. ἐμπεαεῖν, 1Ti.3:7; ὁ ὀ. τοῦ Χριστοῦ Heb.11:26 13:13.† (AS)

oneidizo.notes: ὀνειδίζω (ὄνειδος), [in LXX chiefly for חָרַף pi. ;] to reproach, upbraid: absol., Jas.1:5; with accusative of person(s) (in cl. more frequently with dative of person(s); Bl., § 34, 2; WM, 278), Mat.5:11, Mrk.15:32,…

oneidos.notes: ὄνειδος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for חֶרְפָּה, also for כְּלִמָּה, etc. ;] __1. reproach, censure, blame. __2. matter of reproach, disgrace: Luk.1:25.† (AS)

oneomai.notes: ὠνέομαι, -οῦραι to buy: ὠνήσατο (= cl. ἐπρίατο; see Rutherford, NPhr., 210 ff.; Veitch, see word), with genitive pret., Act.7:16.† (AS)

onesimos.notes: Ὀνήσιμος, -ου, ὁ (i.e. profitable, ὄνησις, profit), Onesimus: Col.4:9, Phm 10 (a common name among slaves; see MM, iii, xviii).† (AS)

onikos.notes: ὀνικός, -ή, -όν (ὄνος), of or for an ass. μύλος ὀ., Mat.18:6, Mrk.9:42 (elsewhere only in π.; see MM, xviii).† (AS)

oninemi.notes: ὀνίνημι [in LXX: Tob.3:8, Sir.30:2 * ;] to profit, benefit, help; mid., to have profit, derive benefit: optative (M, Pr., 195), with genitive, Phm 20.† (AS)

onoma.notes: ὄνομα, -τος, τό, [in LXX chiefly for שֵׁם ;] __1. in general, the name by which a person or thing is called: Mat.10:2, Mrk.3:16, Luk.1:63, Jhn.18:10, al.; ἄνθρωπος (etc.), ᾧ (οὗ) ὄ. (τ. ὄ), sc. ἦν or ἐστίν (BL, §30,…

onomazo.notes: ὀνομάζω (ὄνομα), [in LXX for זָכַר, נָקַב, קָרָא ;] __1. to name, mention, or address by name: Act.19:13; pass., Rom.15:20, Eph.1:21 5:3; of the use of the Divine name in praise and worship, 2Ti.2:19 (LXX, Num.16:26;…

onos.notes: ὄνος, -ου, ὁ, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for חֲמוֹר, also for אָתוֹן, etc. ;] an ass: Mat.21:2, 5" (LXX), Luk.14:5, Jhn.12:15" (LXX) ; ὁ, Luk.13:15; ἡ, Mat.21:7 .† (AS)

ontos.notes: ὄντως adv. (ὤν, ptcp. of εἰμί, sum) [in LXX: Num.22:37 (אׇמְנָם), Jer.3:23 (אָכֵן), Jer.10:19 (אַךְ), 3Ki.12:24, Wis.17:14 * ;] really, actually, truly: Mrk.11:32, Luk.23:47 24:34, Jhn.8:36, 1Co.14:25, Gal.3:21…

oon.notes: ᾠόν (Rec. ὠόν), -οῦ, τό [in LXX for בֵּיצָה ;] an egg: Luk.11:12.† (AS)

ope.notes: ὀπή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for חוֹר, חֲגָו, etc. ;] an opening, a hole: Jas.3:11 (cf. Exo.33:22), Heb.11:33 (cf. Ob 3).† (AS)

opheile.notes: ὀφειλή, -ῆς, ἡ (ὀφείλω), a debt: Mat.18:32; metaphorically, one's due: Rom.13:7, 1Co.7:3 (found also in π.; see Deiss., BS, 221; MM, xviii).† (AS)

opheilema.notes: ὀφείλημα, -τος, τό (ὀφείλω), [in LXX: Deu.24:10 (מַשָּׁאָה), I Est.3:20, 1Ma.15:8 * ;] that which is owed, a debt: Rom.4:4; metaphorically (as Aram. חוֺבָא חוֺב), of sin as a debt, Mat.6:12.† (AS)

opheiletes.notes: ὀφειλέτης, -ου, ὁ (ὀφείλω), a debtor: with genitive (of the amount), Mat.18:24. Metaphorical, of obligation or duty in general, with reference to favours received or injury done, etc.: Mat.6:12, Rom.1:14 8:12 15:27,…

opheilo.notes: ὀφείλω [in LXX: Deu.15:2, Isa.24:2 (נָשָׁה), Eze.18:7 (חוֹב), Wis.12:15, 20, al. ;] to owe, be a debtor: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.18:28, Luk.7:41 16:7, Phm 18; id. with dative of person(s), Mat.18:28,…

opheleia.notes: ὠφέλεια (WH, -λία), -ας, ἡ [in LXX for יָעַל hi., בֶּצַע, etc. ;] __1. assistance, help (Thuc., Plat., al.). __2. profit, advantage, benefit (Hdt., Plat., al.): Rom.3:1, Ju 16.† (AS)

opheleo.notes: ὠφελέω, -ῶ (ὄφελος), [in LXX chiefly for יָעַל hi. ;] to help, benefit, do good, profit: absol., Rom.2:25; οὐδέν (do no good; see Field, Notes, 21), Mat.27:24, Jhn.6:63 12:19; with accusative of person(s), Heb.4:2;…

ophelimos.notes: ὠφέλιμος, -ον (ὠφελέω), useful, serviceable, profitable: with dative commod., Tit.3:8; before πρός, with accusative, 1Ti.4:8, 2Ti.3:16.† (AS)

ophelos.notes: ὄφελος, -ονς, τό (ὀφέλλω, to increase) [in LXX: Job.15:3 (יָעַל hi.)* ;] advantage, help: 1Co.15:32, Jas.2:14, 16.† (AS)

ophis.notes: ὄφις, -εως, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for נָחָשׁ ;] a serpent, snake: Mat.7:10 Mrk.16:18, Luk.10:19 11:11, Jhn.3:14, 1Co.10:9, Rev.9:19; as typical of wisdom and cunning, Mat.10:16 23:23, 2Co.11:3 (cf. Gen.31:1-55); of Satan…

ophrus.notes: ὀφρύς -ύος, ἡ [in LXX: Lev.14:9 (עַיִן גַב) * ;] an eyebrow, the brow of a hill: Luk.4:29.† (AS)

ophthalmodouleia.notes: ὀφθαλμο-δουλία (Rec. -εία), -ας, ἡ (ὀφθαλμός, δοῦλος), eye-service: Eph.6:6, Col.3:22 (not found elsewhere).† (AS)

ophthalmos.notes: ὀφθαλμός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for עַיִן ;] the eye (as in cl., chiefly pl.): Mat.5:38, Mrk.9:47, Luk.6:41, Jhn.9:6, al.; τοὺς ὀ. ἐξορύθσσειν (figuratively), Gal.4:15; ἐπᾶραι, Luk.6:20, Jhn.6:5; ἀνοῖξα, Act.9:40;…

opiso.notes: ὀπίσω adv. of place and time, [in LXX chiefly for אַחַר and cogn. forms ;] __1. prop., as in cl., adv., __(a) of time (not in NT); __(b) of place, back, behind, after: Mat.24:18, Luk.7:38; τὰ ὀ., Php.3:13; εἰς τὰ ὀ.,…

opora.notes: ὀπώρα, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Jer.48:32 40:10, 12 (קַיִץ) * ;] __1. late summer, early autumn (the time between the risings of Sirius and Arcturus, i.e. late July, all August and early September). __2. By meton. (as being…

opsarion.notes: ὀψάριον, -ου, τό (dimin. of ὄψον, (1) cooked meat; (2) a relish or dainty, esp. fish, cf. MGr. τὸ ψάρι, fish; in comic poets and late prose writers only) [in LXX: Tob.2:2 א (Β, ὄψον)* ;] fish: Jhn.6:9, 11 21:9-10, 13…

opse.notes: ὀψέ, adv. of time [in LXX: Gen.24:11, Exo.30:8 (עֶרֶב, עֶרֶב בֵּין), Jer.2:23; τὸ ὀ., Isa.5:11 (נֶשֶׁף)* ;] __1. long after, late. __2. late in the day, at evening (opposite to πρωί); in late writers used almost as…

opsia.notes: ὄψιος, -α, -ον (ὀψέ), late: ἡ ὥρα, Mrk.11:11 (Rec., WH, mg.). In late writers, ἡ ὀψία (sc. ὥρα), as subst., evening [in LXX: Jdth.13:1*]: Mat.8:16 14:15, 23 16:2 20:8 26:20 27:57, Mrk.1:32 4:35 6:47 14:17 15:42,…

opsimos.notes: ὄψιμος, -ον (ὀψέ), [in LXX: Deu.11:14, Pro.16:15, Hos.6:4 (3), Jol.2:23, Zec.10:1, Jer.5:24 (מַלְקוֹשׁ), Exo.9:32 (אָפִיל)* ;] poët. and late for ὄψιος, late: ὑετὸς ὄ., the latter rain (see DB, see word "rain"), with…

opsis.notes: ὄψις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for מַרְאֶה ;] __1. the act of seeing, the sense of sight. __2. face, countenance: Jhn.11:44, Rev.1:16. __3. appear­ance: κατ᾽ ὄ. κρίνειν (see MM, xviii), Jhn.7:24.† (AS)

opsonion.notes: ὄψώνιον, -ου, τό (ὀψον- see: ὀψάριον— and ὠνέομαι), [in LXX: I Est.4:56, 1Ma.3:28 1Mac 14:32 * ;] __1. provisions, provision-money, soldiers' pay: Luk.3:14, 1Co.9:7. __2. Generally, wages, hire: 2Co.11:8; ὄ. τῆς…

optanomai.notes: ὀπτάνω [in LXX: 3Ki.8:8 (רָאָה ni.), Tob.12:19 * ;] late present as from ὤφθην (= ὁράω); mid., ὀπτάνομαι, to allow oneself to be seen, to appear. with dative, Act.1:3. (For exx. from π., see Deiss., LAE, 79, 2526;…

optasia.notes: ὀπτασία, -ας, ἡ (ὀπτάζομαι, Num.14:14* = ὀπτάνομαι), later form of ὄψις, [in LXX: Mal.3:2 (רָאָה ni.), Da TH Dan.9:23 10:1, 7-8 10:16 (מַרְאֶה, מַרְאָה LXX, ὅραμα, -σις), Est.4:7, Sir.43:2, 16 * ;] __1. an appearing,…

optos.notes: ὀπτός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Exo.12:8-9 (צָלִי) * ;] roasted, boiled Luk.24:42.† (AS)

orcheomai.notes: ὀρχέομαι, -οῦμαι [in LXX for רָקַד, etc. ;] to dance; Mat.11:17 14:6, Mrk.6:22, Luk.7:32.† (AS)

orego.notes: ὀρέγω [in Sm.: Job.8:20, Eze.16:49 * ;] to reach, stretch out; pass. and mid., to stretch oneself out, reach forth; metaphorically, to reach after, grasp at, aspire to: with genitive of thing(s), 1Ti.3:1, Heb.11:16;…

oreinos.notes: ὀρεινός (WH, ὀριν-; see MM, xviii,) -ή, -όν (ὄρος), mountainous, hilly; ἡ ὀ. (sc. χώρα), the hill-country (LXX for הַר): Luk.1:39, 65 † (AS)

orexis.notes: ὄρεξις, -εως, ἡ (ὀρέγομαι), [in LXX Wis.14:2 15:5 16:2-3, Sir.18:30 23:6, 4Ma.1:33 4Mac 1:35 * ;] the most general ward for all kinds of desire, longing, appetite: of lust, Rom.1:27 SYN.: see: πάθος (AS)

orge.notes: ὀργή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for אַף, also for חָרוֹן, חֵמָה, קָצַף, etc. ;] __1 1, impulse, propensity, disposition. __2. anger, wrath; __(a) of men: Mrk.3:5, Eph.4:31, Col.3:8, 1Ti.2:8, Jas.1:19-20; __(b) of God;…

orgilos.notes: ὀργίλος, -η, -ον (ὀργή), [in LXX: Pro.22:24 29:22 (חֵמָה), etc. ;] inclined to anger, passionate: Tit.1:7.† (AS)

orgizo.notes: ὀργίζω (ὀργή), [in LXX chiefly for חָרָה, also for קָצַף, etc. ;] to make angry, provoke to anger; in cl. most frequently in pass., and so always in NT, to be provoked to anger, be angry: absol., Mat.18:34 22:7,…

orguia.notes: ὀργυιά, -ᾶς, ἡ (ὀρέγω), the length of the outstretched arms, a fathom: Act.27:28.† (AS)

orneon.notes: ὄρνεον, -ου, τό = ὄρνις, [in LXX chiefly for צִפּוֹר ;] a bird: Rev.18:2 19:17, 21 (Hom., al.).† (AS)

ornis.notes: ὄρνις, -ιθος, ὁ, ἡ [in LXX: ὄ. ἐκλεκταί, I2Ki.3:1 4:23 (3Ki.5:3) (בַּרְבֻּר)* ;] a bird; specif., a cock, a hen: Mat.23:3, Luk.13:34 (WH).† (AS)

oros.notes: ὄρος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly, and very freq., for הַר ;] a mountain: Mat.4:8 17:1, Mrk.9:2, Jhn.4:20, 2Pe.1:18, al.; opposite to βουνός, Luk.3:5 (LXX); ὄ. τ. ἐλαιῶν, Mat.21:1 24:3, al. (see: ἐ.); ὄ. Σιών (Σ., prob.…

orphanos.notes: ὀρφανός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for יָתוֹם ;] __1. prop., orphan, fatherless: Mrk.12:40, WH, mg, Jas.1:27. __2. In a general sense (as also in cl.; see LS, see word; and cf. MM, xviii), bereft, friendless, desolate:…

orthopodeo.notes: ὀρθοποδέω, -ῶ (ὀρθό-πους, going straight), = cl. εὐθυπορέω, to walk straight: metaphorically, Gal.2:14 (not elsewhere).† (AS)

orthos.notes: ὀρθός, -ή, -όν [in LXX chiefly for יָשָׁר ;] straight; __(a) in height, straight, upright: Act.14:10; __(b) in line, straight, direct: figuratively, Heb.12:13 (LXX).† (AS)

orthotomeo.notes: ὀρθοτομέω, -ῶ (- ὀρθός, τέμνω), [in LXX: Pro.3:6 11:6 (יָשַׁר pi.)* ;] to cut straight, as a road (τ. ὁδούς, figuratively, Pr, ll with). Metaphorical, τ. λόγον τ. ἀληθείας, 2Ti.2:15 (V. Ellic. and CGT, in l; not…

orthrinos.notes: ὀρθρινός, -ή, -όν (ὄρθρος), [in LXX: Hos.6:4 13:3 (שָׁכַם hi.), Hag.2:14, Wis.11:22 * ;] late form of ὄρθριος (which see), early: Luk.24:22.† (AS)

orthrizo.notes: ὀρθρίζω (ὄρθρος), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁכַם hi., Gen.19:2, al.; also for שָׁחַר pi., Psa.63:1, al., etc. ;] = cl. poët., ὀρθρεύω, to rise early: before πρός, with accusative of person(s) (as Ps, l.with, al.),…

orthros.notes: ὄρθρος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for שַׁחַר, בֹּקֶר, etc. ;] daybreak, dawn: ὄρθρου βαθέως (M, Pr., 73), at early dawn, Luk.24:1; ὄρθρου, Jhn.8:2; ὑπὸ τὸν ὄ., Act.5:21 (cf. MM, xviii).† (AS)

oruomai.notes: ὠρύομαι depon., [in LXX for שָׁאַג ;] of animals (also of men, Hdt., al.), to roar, howl: λέων, 1Pe.5:8.† (AS)

orusso.notes: ὀρύσσω [in LXX chiefly for חָפַר also for כָּרָה, etc. ;] to dig: with accusative, ληνόν, Mat.21:33; ὁπολήνιον, Mrk.12:1; γῆν, Mat.25:18 (cf. δι-, ἐξ-ορύσσω).† (AS)

osme.notes: ὀσμή, -ῆς, ἡ (ὄζω), [in LXX chiefly for רֵיחַ ;] a smell, odour: Jhn.12:3; metaphorically (EV, savour), 2Co.2:14 2:16; of the effect of sacrifice (cf. נִיחוֹחַ רֵיחַ, Gen.8:21, al., see: εὐωδία), ὀ. εὐωδίας, Eph.5:2,…

osphresis.notes: ὄσφρησις, -εως, ἡ (ὀσφραίνομαι, to smell), the sense of smell, smell­ing: 1Co.12:17.† (AS)

osphus.notes: ὀσφύς, -ύος, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for מֹתֶן;, also for חָלַץ, etc. ;] the loin: Mat.3:4, Mrk.1:6; metaphorically, περιζώννυσθαι (ἀναζ-) τ. ὀσφύας, Luk.12:35, Eph.6:14, 1Pe.1:13; regarded, as by the Hebrews, as the seat…

osteon.notes: ὀστέον (Att.. contr. ὀστοῦν, -οῦ, and so Jo, l.with), -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for עֶצֶם ;] a bone: contr., ὀστοῦν (see supr.), Jhn.19:36 (LXX); uncontr. (as in Hom., Hdt.), ὀστέα, Luk.24:39; ὀστέων, Mat.23:27,…

ostrakinos.notes: ὀστράκινος -η, -ον (ὄστρακον, an earthen vessel or potsherd), [in LXX chiefly for חֶרֶשׂ ;] made of clay, earthen: 2Co.4:7, 2Ti.2:20.† (AS)

othone.notes: ὀθόνη, -ης, ἡ (of Semitic origin, cf. Heb. אֵטוּן, yarn) __1. fine linen (Hom., al.). __2. Later, a sheet or sail: Act.10:11 11:5.† (AS)

othonion.notes: ὀθόνιον, -ου, τό (dimin. of ὀθόνη, which see), [in LXX: Jdg.14:13 (סָדִין), Hos.2:5, 9 (פִּשְׁתֶּה)* ;] a piece of fine linen, a linen cloth; Luk.24:12 (WH, R, mg., om.), Jhn.19:40 20:5-7.† (AS)

otion.notes: ὠτίον, -ου, τό, dimin. of οὖς, [in LXX for אֹזֶן ;] an ear: Mat.26:51, Luk.22:51, Jhn.18:26.† (AS)

oudamos.notes: οὐδαμῶς (οὐδαμός, not even one), adv., [in LXX: 2-4Mac.8* ;] in no wise, by no means: Mat.2:6 (OT).† (AS)

oudeis.notes: οὐδείς, -δεμία, -δέν (also in WH, txt., the Hellenistic forms -θείς, -θέν, Luk.22:35 23:14, Act.15:9 19:27 26:26, 1Co.13:2, 2Co.11:8; cf. BL, §6, 7; M, Pr., 56n, Thackeray, Gr., 58), related to μηδείς as οὐ to μή,…

oudepo.notes: οὐδέπω adv., [in LXX: Exo.9:30 (טֶרֶם)* ;] not yet: Luk.23:53, Jhn.7:39 19:41 20:9, Act.8:16.† (AS)

oupo.notes: οὔπω neg. adv. of time, not yet: Mat.24:6, Mrk.13:7, Jhn.2:4 3:24 6:17, al.; with neg., Mrk.11:2, Luk.23:53; interrog., Mat.16:9, Mrk.4:40 8:17, 21 (AS)

oura.notes: οὐρά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX for זָנָב ;] a tail: Rev.9:10, 19 12:4.† (AS)

ouranios.notes: οὐράνιος, -ον (cl. usually -α, -ον), [in LXX: Deu.28:12 A (שָׁמַיִם), etc. ;] of or in heaven, heavenly: Mat.5:48 6:14, 26 6:32 15:13 18:35 23:9, Luk.2:13, Act.26:19.† (AS)

ouranos.notes: οὐρανός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for שָׁמַיִם (hence, often pl., οἱ οὐ., see infr.) ;] heaven; __1. of the vault or firmament of heaven, the sky and the aerial regions above the earth: opposite to ἡ γῆ, Heb.1:10,…

ouranothen.notes: οὐρανόθεν (οὐρανός), adv., [in LXX: 4Ma.4:10 * ;] from heaven: Act.14:17 26:13.† (AS)

ous.notes: οὖς genitive, ὠτός, τό, [in LXX chiefly for אֹזֶן ;] the ear: Mat.13:16, Mrk.7:33, Luk.22:50, 1Co.2:9 12:16, 1Pe.3:12; ἐν τ. ὠσί, Luk.4:21; εἰς τ. ὦτα ἀκούεσθαι, Act.7:57; γίνεσθαι, Luk.1:44; εἰσέρχεσθαι, Jas.5:4;…

ousia.notes: οὐσία, -ας, ἡ (οὖσα, fem. part. of εἰμί), [in LXX: Tob.14:13, 3Ma.3:28 * ;] substance, property: Luk.15:12-13.† (AS)

oxos.notes: ὄξος, -εος (-ους), τό (ὄξύς), [in LXX: Num.6:3, Rut.2:14, Psa.69:21, Pro.25:20 (חֹמֶץ)* ;] sour wine (posca, vin-Deu pays), the ordinary drink of labourers and common soldiers: Mat.27:48, Mrk.15:36, Luk.23:36,…

oxus.notes: ὄξύς, -εῖα, -ύ [in LXX: Psa.57:4, al. (חַד), Amo.2:15 (קַל), Isa.5:28 (שָׁנַן), etc. ;] __1. sharp: Rev.1:16 2:12 14:14, 17-18 19:15. __2. Of motion, swift: Rom.3:15 (LXX ταχινός).† (AS)

ozias.notes: Ὀζείας (Rec. Ὀζίας), -ου, ὁ (Heb. עֻזִּיָּה), Uzziah: Mat.18:1-35 9:1-38.† (AS)

ozo.notes: ὄζω [in LXX: Exo.8:14 (בָּאַשׁ)* ;] to smell (i.e. emit a smell): Jhn.11:39.† (AS)

pachuno.notes: παχύνω (παχύς, thick) [in LXX: Deu.32:15, Isa.6:10 (שָׁמֵן), al. ;] to thicken, fatten; pass., to grow fat. Metaphorical, to make dull or stupid (τ. ψυχάς, Rut.); pass., to wax gross: ἡ καρδία„ Mat.13:15, Act.28:27"…

pagideuo.notes: παγιδεύω (παγίς), [in LXX: 1Ki.28:9 (נָקַשׁ hith.), Ecc.9:12 (יָקֹשׁ pu.)* ;] to ensnare: metaphorically, with accusative, ἐν λόγῳ, Mat.22:15 (not elsewhere).† (AS)

pagis.notes: παγίς, -ίδος, ή (πήγνυμι), [in LXX for פַּח, מוֹקֵשׁ, רֶשֶׁת, etc. ;] poët. (Aristoph., al.) and late for πάγη, a trap, snare; metaphorically (as also in cl.): Luk.21:35, Rom.11:9" (LXX) 1Ti.3:7 6:9, 2Ti.2:26.† (AS)

paidagogos.notes: παιδαγωγός, -oῦ, ὁ (παῖς, ἄγω), a guide, guardian, trainer of boys, a tutor (disting. from διδάσκαλος, Xen., Lac., 3, 1), usually a trusty slave: opposite to πατήρ, 1Co.4:15; figuratively, of the Law, π. εἰς Χριστόν,…

paidarion.notes: παιδάριον, -ου, τό dimin. of παῖς, [in LXX chiefly for נַעַר ;] a little boy, a lad (in late and colloq. Gk. the word seems to be used with greater latitude and even to lose its dimin. force entirely; cf. Tob.6:2-3,…

paideia.notes: παιδεία (-ία, Τ), -ας, ἡ (παιδεύω), [in LXX chiefly for מוּסָר ;] __1. the rearing of a child (Æsch.). __2. training, learning, instruction (Plat., al.): Eph.6:4, 2Ti.3:16. __3. As in LXX (Pro.3:11 15:5, al.),…

paideuo.notes: παιδεύω (παίς), [in LXX chiefly for יָסַר ;] __1. as in cl., to train children, hence, generally, to teach, instruct: Act.7:22 22:3, 1Ti.1:20, Tit.2:12. __2. As in LXX (Psa.6:2, Pro.19:18, Wis.3:5, al., and for prob.…

paideutes.notes: παιδευτής, -οῦ, ὁ (παιδεύω), [in LXX: Hos.5:2 (מוּסָר), Sir.37:19, 4Ma.5:34 4Mac 9:6 * ;] __1. prop., a teacher, instructor: Rom.2:20. __2. a corrector, one who disciplines (cf. Ho, l.with) : Heb.12:9.† (AS)

paidion.notes: παιδίον, -ου, τό dimin, of παῖς, [in LXX chiefly for יֶלֶד, also for נַעַר, בֵּן, etc., frequently in To in ref. to full-grown youth ;] a young child, a little one: Mat.2:8-9, 11, ff., Luk.1:59, 66 1:76, 80 2:17, 27…

paidiothen.notes: παιδιόθεν (παιδίον), adv., [in LXX: Gen.47:3 * ;] = cl. ἐκ παιδός, παιδίον, from childhood: Mrk.9:21.† (AS)

paidiske.notes: παιδίσκη, -ης, ἡ dimin, of παῖς, [in LXX for שִׁפְחָה, אָמָה, etc. ;] __1. a young girl, a maiden (נַעֲרָה, Rut.4:12). __2. Colloq., a young female slave, a maid-servant (see Kennedy, Sources, 40 f.; Deiss., LAE,…

paio.notes: παίω [in LXX chiefly for נָכָה hi. ;] to strike, smite: with the hand or fist, Mat.26:68, Luk.22:64; with a sword, Mrk.14:47, Jhn.18:10; of a reptile, to sting: Rev.9:5.† (AS)

pais.notes: παῖς, genitive, παιδός, ὁ, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for עֶבֶד, also for נַעַר, נַעֲרָה, etc. ;] __1. a child, boy, youth, maiden: ὁ π., Mat.17:18, Luk.2:43 9:42, Act.20:12; ἡ π. Luk.8:51, 54 (on the artic. fern, of address,…

paizo.notes: παίζω (παῖς), [in LXX: Gen.21:9 26:8, Exo.32:6 (צָחַק pi.); and more frequently (Jdg.16:25, al.) for (שָׂחַק pi. ;] prop., to play as a child, hence, generally, to play (as with singing and dancing) : 1Co.10:7 (LXX)…

palai.notes: πάλαι adv. of time, [in LXX: Isa.48:5, 7 (אָז), etc. ;] long ago, of old, in time past (denoting past time absolutely, as πρότερον relatively) : Mat.11:21, Luk.10:13, Heb.1:1, Ju 4; as adj., with art., 2Pe.1:9; of…

palaioo.notes: παλαιόω, -ῶ (παλαιός), [in LXX chiefly for בָּלָה, pi., in pass. for בִּי qal. ;] to make or declare old: Heb.8:13; pass., to become old: of things worn out by time and use (cf. Jhn.9:13, Isa.50:9, al., and see:…

palaios.notes: παλαιός, -ά, -όν (πάλαι), [in LXX: Lev.25:22 26:10, Sng.7:13 (14) (יָשֵׁן), יָשֵׁן), Jos.9:4-5, Jer.38:11 (בָּלֶה), etc. ;] old, ancient; opposite to νέος, καινός: οἶνος, Luk.5:3; διαθήκη, 2Co.3:14; ἐντολή, 1Jn.2:7;…

pale.notes: πάλη, -ης, ἡ (πάλλω, to sway) wrestling, hence, generally, fight, contest: figuratively, of the spiritual combat of Christians, Eph.6:12.† (AS)

palin.notes: πάλιν, adv., [in LXX for שׁוּב, etc. ;] __1. of place, back, backwards (LS, see word). __2. Of time, again, once more: Mat.4:8, Mrk.2:13, Luk.23:20, Jhn.1:35 (and freq.), Act.17:32, Rom.11:23, Gal.1:9, Heb.1:6, al;…

palingenesia.notes: παλινγενεσία (Rec. παλιγγ-), -ας, ἡ (πάλιν, γενεσις), new birth, renewal, restoration, regeneration; __(a) of persons (Plut., Phil., al.), of spiritual regeneration, διὰ λουτροῦ παλινγενεστίας (genitive attr., see…

pamphulia.notes: Παμφυλία, -ας, ἡ Pamphylia, a province of Asia Minor: Act.2:10 13:13 14:24 15:38 27:5.† (AS)

pamplethei.notes: πανπληθεί (Rec. παμπλ-, see WH, App., 150), adv. (πᾶς, πλῆθος), = cl. παμπληθές, with the whole multitude, all together: Luk.23:18.† (AS)

pandocheion.notes: παν-δοχεῖον (-δοκίον, Τ), -ου, τό (πανδοχεύς), late form of Att.. πανδοκεῖον, an inn (khân, caravanserai): Luk.10:34.† (AS)

pandocheus.notes: παν-δοχεύς, -έως, ὁ (πᾶς, δέχομαι), late form of the Att.. πανδοκεύς (T, in l), an innkeeper, host: Luk.10:35.† (AS)

paneguris.notes: πανήγυρις, -εως, ἡ (πᾶς + ἄγυρας = ἀγορά), [in LXX: Hos.2:11(13) 9:5, Eze.46:11 (מוֹעֵד), Amo.5:21 (עֲצָרָה) * ;] prop., a national festal assembly in honour of a god; hence, generally, any festal assembly: Heb.12:23…

panoiki.notes: πανοικεί (Rec., LTr., -κί), adv. (πᾶς, οἶκος), a word rejected by strict Atticists, though found once in Plat. (Eryx., 392c); [in LXX: Exo.1:1 (בַּיִת), 3Ma.3:27 (where A in each case reads -κίᾳ, the Attic form, cf.…

panoplia.notes: πανοπλία, -ας, ἡ (πᾶς, ὅπλον) [in LXX: 2Ki.2:21 (חֲלִיצָה), Job.39:20, Jdth.14:3, Wis.5:17, Sir.46:6, 1-2; 4Mac.6* ;] full armour: Luk.11:22; metaphorically (cf. Wis, l.with), τ. θεοῦ, Eph.6:11, 13 † (AS)

panourgia.notes: πανουργία, -ας, ἡ (πανοῦργος), [in LXX: Jos.9:4, Pro.1:4 8:5 (עׇרְמָה), Num.24:22, Sir.19:25 21:22 31:10 (Sir.34:10) (in all cases in good or indifferent sense)* ;] cleverness, in cl. nearly always in bad sense,…

panourgos.notes: πανοῦργος, -ον [in LXX chiefly for עָרוּם ;] ready to do anything; __(a) in cl., chiefly in bad sense, knavish, crafty: 2Co.12:16; __(b) in good sense, skilful, clever (Pro.13:1 28:2).† (AS)

pantachou.notes: πανταχοῦ adv., [in LXX: Isa.42:22 (כָּלַם)* ;] everywhere: Mrk.1:28 16:20, Luk.9:6, Act.17:30 24:3 28:22, 1Co.4:17.† (AS)

pante.notes: πάντῃ (Rec. -τη), adv. (πᾶς), [in LXX: Sir.50:22, 3Ma.4:1 * ;] every way, entirely: Act.24:3.† (AS)

panteles.notes: παντελής, -ές (πᾶς, τέλος), [in LXX: 3Ma.7:16 A* ;] all-complete, entire, perfect; εἰς τ. παντελές, completely, utterly: Luk.13:11, Heb.7:25 (where perhaps in temp. sense, finally; see MM, xviii).† (AS)

pantokrator.notes: παντοκράτωρ, -ορος, ὁ (πᾶς, κρατέω), [in LXX: frequently in Job.5:8, 17, al. (שַׁדַּי), and for צָבָא, in the phrase θεός (κυρίος) π., 2Ki.5:10, al., and frequently in Am, Za, Ma; also in Wis.7:25, Sir.42:17 50:14,…

pantos.notes: πάντως adv. (πάς), [in LXX: 4Ki.5:11 (see Thackeray, Gr., 47), Tob.14:8, al. ;] altogether, by all means; __(a) without neg. (from Hdt. on) : 1Co.16:12; esp. in strong affirmations, surely, at all events: Luk.4:23,…

pantote.notes: πάντοτε adv. of time (πᾶς), [in LXX: Wis.11:21 19:18 * ;] in late writers (once in Arist.) for διαπαντός, ἑκάστοτε, at all times, always: Mat.26:11, Mrk.14:7, Luk.15:31, Jhn.6:34, Rom.1:10, and frequently in Paul.…

pantothen.notes: πάντοθεν adv. (πᾶς), [in LXX: Jer.48:31 (כֹּל), al. ;] from all sides: Mrk.1:45, Luk.19:43, Heb.9:4.† (AS)

paphos.notes: Πάφος, -ου, ἡ Paphos, a city in Cyprus: Act.13:6, 13 † (AS)

parabaino.notes: παρα-βαίνω [in LXX for סוּר, עָבַר, פָּרַר, שָׂטָה, etc. ;] __1. in Hom. (twice), to go by the side of, stand beside. __2. In Æsch., Herod., Thuc., al., to go past or pass over, chiefly metaphorically, to overstep,…

paraballo.notes: παρα-βάλλω [in LXX: Pro.2:2 4:20 5:1, 13 22:17 (נָטָה hi.), Rut.2:16 (שָׁלַל), 2Ma.14:38 (elsewhere as seel.4)* ;] __1. to throw to or beside, as fodder to horses (Hom., al.). __2. to lay beside, compare (Hdt.,…

parabasis.notes: παρά-βασις, -εως, ἡ (παραβαίνω), [in LXX: Psa.101:3 (שֵׂט), 4Ki.2:24 A, Wis.14:31, 2Ma.15:10 * ;] __1. a going aside, a deviation (Arist.). __2. In later writers, an overstepping; metaphorically, transgression…

parabates.notes: παρα-βάτης, -ου, ὁ (παραβαίνω), [in Sm.: Psa.17:4 139:19, Jer.6:28 * ;] __1. one who stands beside, the warrior who stands by the charioteer (of. παραβαίνω, 1). __2. a transgressor (Æsch., παρβάτης): Gal.2:18,…

parabiazomai.notes: παρα-βιάζομαι [in LXX: Gen.19:9, 4Ki.2:17 (פָּצַר), Deu.1:43 (זוּד hi.), 1Ki.28:23 (פָּרַץ) see1. 4 * ;] __1. prop., to force against nature or law. __2. to compel by force (Polyb.). __3. to constrain by entreaty…

parabole.notes: παραβολή, -ῆς, ἡ (παραβάλλω), [in LXX (cf. McNeile, Mt., 185): Num.23:7, Deu.28:37, Psa.44:14, Pro.1:6, Eze.12:22, al. (מָשַׁל), Sir.47:17 (חִידָה), al. mutt. in Sir, Wis.5:3 ;] __1. a placing beside, juxtaposition…

parabouleuomaiparaboleuomai.notes: παρα-βολεύομαι = cl. παραβάλλομαι (2Ma.14:38), to expose oneself to danger, hazard one's life: with dative ref., τ. ψυχῇ (see M, Pr., 64), Php.2:30 (Rec. παραβουλ-). Cited by Deiss., LAE, 84, 120.† παρα-βουλεύομαι,…

paracheimasia.notes: παρα-χειμασία, -ας, ἡ (παρα-χειμάζω) a wintering: Act.27:12 (Polyb., al.).† (AS)

paracheimazo.notes: παρα-χειμάζω to winter at a place: before εἰς, Act.27:12; ἐν, Act.28:11; πρὸς ὑμᾶς, 1Co.16:6; ἐκεῖ, Tit.3:12.† (AS)

parachrema.notes: παραχρῆμα adv. (= παρὰ τὸ χρῆμα), [in LXX: Num.6:9 12:4, Isa.29:5 30:13 (פִּתְאוֹם), etc. ;] on the spot, forthwith, instantly: Mat.21:19-20, Luk.1:64 4:39 5:25 8:44, 47 8:55 13:13 18:43 19:11 22:60, Act.3:7 5:10…

paradechomai.notes: παρα-δέχομαι [in LXX: Exo.23:1 (נָשָׂא), Pro.3:12 (רָצָה), 2Ma.4:22 R, 3Ma.7:12 * ;] to receive, admit; __(a) of things: Mrk.4:20, Act.15:4 16:21 22:18, 1Ti.5:19; __(b) of persons: Heb.12:6 (LXX).† (AS)

paradeigmatizo.notes: παρα-δειγματίζω (δείκνυμι), [in LXX: Num.25:4 (יָקַע hi.), Jer.13:22 (חָמַס ni.), Eze.28:17 (רַאֲוָה), Est.4:17, Dan LXX 2:5 * ;] to set forth as an example; in bad sense, to put to open shame: Heb.6:6 (Polyb.,…

paradeisos.notes: παράδεισος, -ου, ὁ (an Oriental word, first used by Xen. of the parks of Persian kings and nobles) [in LXX chiefly for גַּן, Gen.1:8 ff., al.; also for עֵדֶן, Isa.51:3; פַּרְדֵּס, Neh.2:8, Ecc.2:5 ;] __1. a park,…

paradiatribe.notes: παρα-δια-τριβή, -ῆς, ἡ useless wrangling: seel. in Rec. for διαπαρατριβή (which see), 1Ti.6:5.† δια-παρα-τριβή, -ῆς, ἡ (παρατριβή, friction, irritation) mutual irritation (Field, Notes, 211), wrangling: 1Ti.6:5 (Rec.…

paradidomi.notes: παρα-δίδωμι, [in LXX chiefly for נתן ;] correl. to παρδέχομαι, __1. to give or hand over to another: with accusative and dative, Mat.11:27 25:14, Luk.4:6, al.; of being delivered up to a course of teaching, pass.…

paradosis.notes: παρά-δοσις, -εως, ἡ (παραδίδωμι), [in LXX: Est.7:26 (אֵסוּר), Jer.32:4 (נָתַן ni.), Jer.34:2 * ;] __1. a handing down or over, transfer, transmission (Arist., Polyb., al., LXX). __2. tradition of doctrine (Plat.,…

paradoxos.notes: παράδοξος, -ον (παρά, δόξα), [in LXX: Jdth.13:13, Wis.5:2 16:17 19:5, Sir.43:25; 2-4 Mac 3 * ;] contrary to received opinion, incredible, marvellous: pl., Luk.5:26.† SYN.: see: δύναμις (AS)

paraginomai.notes: παρα-γίνομαι (Ion. and late for -γίγν-), [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא ;] __1. to be beside or at hand (Hom., Hdt., al.), hence, to stand by, sup­port (Æsch., Thuc., al.) : with dative of person(s), 2Ti.4:16. __2. to…

parago.notes: παρ-άγω [in LXX chiefly for עָבַר ;] __1. trans., to lead by, lead aside, lead into, lead forward, etc. __2. Intrans., __(a) to pass by: Mat.9:9, 27 20:30, Mrk.2:14 15:21, Jhn.8:59 R, txt., Jhn.9:1; before παρό, with…

paraineo.notes: παρ-αινέω, -ω [in LXX: 2Ma.7:25-26, 3Ma.5:17 3Mac 7:12 * ;] to exhort, advise: with accusative of person(s) and inf. (see Bl., § 72, 5; M, Pr., 205), Act.27:22; absol., before λέγων, Act.27:9.† (AS)

paraiteomai.notes: παρ-αιτέομαι, -οῦμαι [in LXX for בָּקַשׁ pi., etc. ;] __1. to beg of or from another: Mrk.15:6 (Rec. ὅνπερ ᾐτοῦντο, see Field, Notes, 43). __2. to deprecate; __(a) prop., with neg., to entreat that not (Thuc., al.):…

parakaleo.notes: παρακαλέω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for נחם ni., pi. ;] __1. to call to one, call for, summon: Act.28:20 (R, mg.; R, txt., entreat); hence (of the gods: Dem., Xen., al.), to invoke, call on, beseech, entreat: τ. πατέρα…

parakalupto.notes: παρα-καλύπτω [in LXX: Isa.44:8 (פָּחַד), Eze.22:26 (עָלַם hi.)* ;] to cover by hanging something beside, to hide: metaphorically (as Plat., al.), Luk.9:45.† (AS)

parakathizo.notes: παρα-καθίζω [in LXX: Job.2:13 (יָשַׁב)* ;] to set beside; act. for mid., to sit down beside: Luk.10:39, Rec. (for -έζομαι, which see).† (AS)

parakeimai.notes: παρά-κειμαι [in LXX: Jdt.3.2-3, Sir.30:18 34:16, Dan LXX Bel 1:14, 2,3 Mac.5* ;] to lie beside, be near, be present: Rom.7:18, 21.† (AS)

paraklesis.notes: παρά-κλησις, -εως, ἡ (παρακαλέω), [in LXX : Job.21:2, Psa.94:19, Hos.13:14, Nam.3:7, Isa.57:18 66:11, Jer.16:7 (נָחַם, its parts and derivatives), Jer.38:9 (תַּחֲנוּן), Isa.28:29 30:7, 1Ma.10:24 1Mac 12:9, 2Ma.7:24…

parakletos.notes: παρά-κλητος, -ον (παρακαλεω), [in Aq., Th.: Job.16:2 * ;] called to one's aid in a judicial cause (Dion. Cass.); hence, most frequently as subst., ὁ π., an advocate, pleader, intercessor (Dem., al.; so in Rabbinic…

parakoe.notes: παρ-ακοή, -ῆς, ἡ __1. a hearing amiss (Flat.) __2. Later, as following inattention, disobedience: Rom.5:19, 2Co.10:6, Heb.2:2 (cf. παρακούω, and see Tr., Syn., § lxvi).† (AS)

parakoloutheo.notes: παρ-ακολουθέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ma.8:11 2Mac 9:27 R* ;] with dative, to follow closely, accompany. Metaphorical, in various senses, __(a) to result: Mrk.16:17; __(b) to follow up, trace, investigate: Luk.1:3 (so…

parakouo.notes: παρ-ακούω [in LXX: Est.3:3, 8 (עָבַר, עָשָׂה, with neg.) Est.4:14 7:4 (חָרַשׁ hi.), Isa.65:12 (שָׁמַע, with neg.), I Est.4:11, Tob.3:4 * ;] __1. to overhear. __2. to hear amiss or imperfectly. __3. Later (as in LXX,…

parakupto.notes: παρα-κύπτω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁקַף ni., hi. ;] __1. to stoop sideways. __2. to stoop to look: Luk.24:12 (R, txt.), Jhn.20:5, 11, Metaphorical (see Hort, Ja., in l), to look into: before εἰς, Jas.1:25, 1Pe.1:12.† (AS)

paralambano.notes: παρα-λαμβάνω [in LXX chiefly for לָקַח, also for יָרַשׁ, etc. ;] __1. with accusative of thing(s), like παραδέχομαι, correl. to παραδίδωμι, to receive from another: Col.4:17, Heb.12:28; of the mind, 1Co.11:23 15:1…

paralego.notes: παρα-λέγω to lay beside; mid., __(a) to lie beside (Hom.); __(b) later, of sailors, to sail past: with accusative, Act.27:8, 13.† (AS)

paralios.notes: παρ-άλιος, -ον (in cl., chiefly -α, -ον), [in LXX for יָם, חוֹף, etc., with prep. ;] by the sea; ἡ π. (sc. χώρα, in LXX ἡ παραλία, Deu.1:7, Jos.9:1, Jdth.1:7, al.), the sea coast: Luk.6:17.† (AS)

parallage.notes: παρ-αλλαγή, -ῆς, ἡ (παραλλάσσω), [in LXX: 4Ki.9:20 (שִׁגָּעוֹן)* ;] in various senses (LS, see word), change: Jas.1:17.† (AS)

paralogizomai.notes: παρα-λογίζομαι [in LXX chiefly for רָמָה pi. ;] __1. to miscalculate. __2. to reason falsely, hence, to mislead: Col.2:4, Jas.1:22.† (AS)

paraluo.notes: παρα-λύω [in LXX: Jer.6:24, Jer 27:43, Eze.21:7 (רָפָה), etc. ;] __1. to loose from the side, set free. __2. to weaken, enfeeble; pass., to be en­feebled, esp. by a paralytic stroke: Luk.5:18, 24, Act.8:7 9:33,…

paralutikos.notes: παρα-λυτικός, -ή, -όν (παραλύω), paralytic: Mat.4:24 8:6 9:2, 6, Mrk.2:3-5, 9-10 Luk.5:24 (Rec., WH, mg.).† (AS)

parameno.notes: παρα-μένω [in LXX: Pro.12:7, Da TH Dan.11:17 (עָמַד), etc. ;] to remain beside or near: Heb.7:23, Jas.1:25; of remaining alive, 1Co.16:6 (WH, κατα-), Php.1:25 (cf. συμ-παραμένω).† (AS)

paramutheomai.notes: παρα-μυθέομαι, -οῦμαι [in LXX: 2Ma.15:9 (Sm. 8)* ;] __1. to en­courage, exhort. __2. to comfort, console: with accusative of person(s), Jhn.11:31, 1Th.2:11 5:14; id. before περί, Jhn.11:19.† SYN.: παρακαλέω (AS)

paramuthia.notes: παραμυθία, -ας, ἡ (παραμυθέομαι) [in LXX: Est.8:13, Wis.19:12 * ;] __1. encouragement, exhortation. __2. comfort, consolation: 1Co.14:3 .† SYN.: παράκλησις (AS)

paramuthion.notes: παραμύθιον, -ου, τό (παραμυθέομαι) [in LXX: Wis.3:18 * ;] __1. an exhortation, persuasion, encouragement: Php.2:1 (cf. Plat., Legg., vi, 773 E, al.; see Lft. and ICC, Phi., l.with). __2. assuagement, abatement,…

parangelia.notes: παρ-αγγελία, -ας, ἡ (παραγγέλλω), an instruction, charge, com­mand: Act.5:28 16:24, 1Th.4:2, 1Ti.1:5 1:18. (In Xen., Polyb., of a military order.)† (AS)

parangello.notes: παρ-αγγέλλω (παρά, ἀγγὲλλω), [in LXX for שָׁמַע pi., hi., etc. ;] __1. to transmit a message (Æsch., Eur.). __2. to order, command (Hdt., Xen., al.) : with inf., Act.15:5; with accusative of thing(s), 1Co.11:17,…

paranomeo.notes: παρα-νομέω, ῶ [in LXX for הָלַל, etc., chiefly in Pss and 2Mac ;] to be a παράνομος, to transgress the law: Act.23:3.† (AS)

paranomia.notes: παρανομία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Psa.37:7 (מְזִמָּה), etc. ;] law-breaking, transgression: 2Pe.2:16.† (AS)

paraphero.notes: παρα-φέρω [in LXX: 1Ki.21:13 (14) (הָלַל hithpo.), and as seel. 2* ;] __1. to bring to, set before, esp. of food (Hdt., al.). __2. to take or carry away: with accusative of thing(s), before ἀπό, Mrk.14:36, Luk.22:42;…

paraphroneo.notes: παρα-φρονέω (ιαρά, φρήν), [in LXX: Zec.7:11 (סָרַר)* ;] to be beside oneself, be deranged: 2Co.11:23.† (AS)

parapikraino.notes: παρα-πικραίνω [in LXX chiefly for מָרַד qal., hi., מְרִי, most frequently in Psa and Eze ;] to embitter, provoke: absol. (yet sc. τ. θεόν, cf. Psa.106:7, Eze.2:5-8, al., and with τὸν θ. added, Psa.5:4, Eze.20:21,…

parapikrasmos.notes: παρα-πικρασμός, -οῦ, ἁ (παραπικραίνω) [in LXX: Psa.95:8 (מְרִיבָה elsewhere rendered Λοιδόρησις, Exo.17:7; ἀντιλογία, Deu.33:8, al.; λοιδορία, Num.20:24)* ;] provocation: ἐν τῷ π. (Heb. at Meribah), Heb.3:8, 15"…

parapipto.notes: παρα-πίπτω [in LXX: Eze.14:13 15:8 18:24 20:27 (מָעַל), Eze.22:4 (אָשַׁם), Est.6:10 (נָפַל hi.), Wis.6:9 12:2, 2Ma.10:4 A* ;] __1. most frequently (Hdt., Thuc., al.), to fall in one's way, befall. __2. to fall into…

parapleo.notes: παρα-πλέω to sail by or past: with accusative, Act.20:16.† (AS)

paraplesion.notes: παρα-πλήσιος, -α, -ον (Hdt., Plat., al.; also -ος, -ον, Thuc., Polyb.), coming near, nearly resembling. Neut. -ον, adverbially, = ίως, in a way nearly resembling: with dative, Php.2:27.† (AS)

paraporeuomai.notes: παρα-πορεύομαι [in LXX chiefly for עָבַר ;] __1. to go beside, accom­pany. __2. to go past, pass by: Mat.27:39, Mrk.11:20 15:29; before διά, with genitive, Mrk.2:23 9:30 (WH, mg.).† (AS)

paraptoma.notes: παρά-πτωρμα, -τος, τό (παραπίπτω), [in LXX for מַעַל, פֶּשַׁע, etc. ;] __1. a false step, a blunder (Polyb.). __2. Ethically, a misdeed, trespass (LXX): Mat.6:14-15, Mrk.11:25-26, Rom.4:25 5:15 ff Rom.11:11-12,…

pararreo.notes: παρα-ρρέω [in LXX: Isa.44:4 (יָבַל), Pro.3:21 * ;] to flow by, drift away, slip away: Heb.2:1.† (AS)

parasemos.notes: παράσημος, -ον (σῆμα, a mark) [in LXX: 3Ma.2:29 * ;] __1. in cl., marked amiss, spurious, counterfeit. Later, __2. marked at the side, annotated (Plut.). __3. marked with a sign (LXX); as subst., a figure­head :…

paraskeuazo.notes: παρα-σκευάζω [in LXX: Jer.50:42 (עָרַךְ), etc. ;] to prepare, make ready: absol. (sc. τ. δεῖπνον, etc.; of. Hdt., ix, 82, 2Ma.2:27), Act.10:10; mid., to prepare, make preparations: 1Co.14:8 (cf. Je, l.with); pass.,…

paraskeue.notes: παρα-σκευή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Exo.35:24 39:42 (עֲבֹדָה, with seel., ἀποσκ-, κατασκ-), Jdth.2:17 4:5, 1Ma.9:35 א, 2Ma.15:21 * ;] __1. preparation. __2. equipment (Jth, ll with). __3. In Jewish usage, the day of…

parateino.notes: παρα-τείνω [in LXX: Num.23:28 (שָׁקַף ni.), etc. ;] to extend, pro­long: τ. λόγον, Act.20:7.† (AS)

pempo.notes: πέμπω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁלַח ;] to send; __(a) of persons: with accusative, absol., Mat.22:7, Luk.7:19, Jhn.1:22, 2Co.9:3, al.; ptcp. before verb., Mat.14:10, Act.19:31, al.; ptcp. before διά (= Heb. יָד;,…

pemptos.notes: πέμπτος, -η, -ον [in LXX for חֲמִישִׁי and cognate forms ;] fifth: Rev.6:9 9:1 16:10 21:20.† (AS)

penes.notes: πένης, -ητος, ὁ (πένομαι, to work for one's daily bread), [in LXX for אֶבְיוֹן, עָנִי, דַּל, etc. ;] one who works for his living, a labourer, a poor man: 2Co.9:9.† SYN.: πτωχός, properly a beggar and implying deeper…

penichros.notes: πενιχρός, -ά, -όν (πένομαι, see: πένης) [in LXX: Exo.22:25 (עָנִי), Pro.28:15 29:7 (דַּל) * ;] chiefly in Comic poets and late prose (but Plato, Rep., 578 A), = πένης, needy, poor: Luk.21:2 (for ex. in π., see MM,…

pentakischilioi.notes: πεντακισ-χίλιοι, -αι, -α five thousand: Mat.14:21 16:9, Mrk.6:44 8:19, Luk.9:14, Jhn.6:10.† (AS)

pente.notes: πέντε indecl., οἱ, αἱ, τά, five: Mat.14:17, al (AS)

pentekonta.notes: πεντήκοντα indecl., οἱ, αἱ, τά, fifty: Luk.7:41 16:6, Jhn.8:57 21:11, Act.13:20; ἀνὰ π., Luk.9:14; κατὰ π., Mrk.6:40.† (AS)

pentekoste.notes: πεντηκοστός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for חֲמִישִׁי, חֲמִישִׁי, Lev.25:10-11, 4Ki.15:23 15:27; 1Ma.4:1-61, 2Ma.14:4; ἡ π., Tob.2:1, 2Ma.12:32 * ;] fiftieth. As subst., ἡ π.; __(a) (sc. μερίς), at Athens, a tax of two per…

pentheo.notes: πενθέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for אָבַל ;] to mourn (for), lament; __(a) intrans. : Mat.5:4 (5) Mat.9:15, 1Co.5:2; π. καὶ κλαίειν, Mat.16:10, Luk.6:25, Jas.4:9, Rev.18:15, 19; before ἐπί, with accusative, Rev.18:11;…

penthera.notes: πενθερά, -ᾶς, ἡ (fem. of πενθερός), [in LXX for חֲמוֹת ;] a mother-in-law: Mat.8:14 10:35, Mrk.1:30, Luk.4:38 12:53.† (AS)

pentheros.notes: πενθερός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for חָם, Gen.38:13, al.; also for חָתַן, Jdg.1:16 A ;] a father-in-law: Jhn.18:13.† (AS)

penthos.notes: πένθος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for אֵבֶל ;] mourning: Jas.4:9, Rev.18:7-8 21:4.† (AS)

pepoithesis.notes: πεποίθησις, -εως, ἡ (πείθω), [in LXX: 4Ki.18:19 (בִּטָּחוֹן)* ;] confidence: 2Co.1:15 3:4 10:2, Eph.3:12; before εἰς, 2Co.8:22; ἐν, Php.3:4 (the word is condemned by the Atticists, see Rutherford, NPhr., 355).† (AS)

pera.notes: πἡρα, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Jdth.10:5 13:10, 15 * ;] a leathern pouch for victuals, etc., a wallet (Deiss. thinks an alms-bag, see LAE, 108 ff.): Mat.10:10, Mrk.6:8, Luk.9:3 10:4 22:35-36.† (AS)
