peras.notes: πέρας, -ατος, τό (πέρα, beyond), [in LXX chiefly for קֵץ, קָצָה and cognate forms ;] an end, limit, boundary; __(a) of space: chiefly in pl., τὰ π. τ. γῆς, Mat.12:42, Luk.11:31; τ. οἰκουμένης, Rom.10:18 (LXX); __(b)…

pergamos.notes: Πέργαμος, -ου, ἡ (so Xen., Paus., al., but -ον, τό in Strabo, Polyb., and most writers, also in Inscr.; in NT the termination is uncertain) Pergamum, a city of Mysia: Rev.1:11 2:12.† (AS)

perge.notes: Πέργη, -ης, ἡ Perga, a city of Pamphylia: Act.13:13-14 14:25.† (AS)

periago.notes: περι-άγω [in LXX: Amo.2:10 (הָלַךְ hi.); Isa.28:27, Eze.47:2 (סָבַב hi., ho.); Eze.37:2 46:21 (עָבַר hi), 2Ma.4:38 2Mac 6:10 * ;] __1. to lead about or around: with accusative of person(s), 1Co.9:5. __2. Intrans., to…

periaireo.notes: περι-αιρέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for סוּר hi. ;] to take away that which surrounds, take away, take off (τείχη, Hdt., Thuc.; χιτῶνα, Plat.; δακτύλιον, Gen.41:42, cf. σφρηγῖδα, Hdt., ii, 151) : τ. κάλυΜΜα, pass.,…

periastrapto.notes: περι-αστράπτω [in LXX: 4Ma.4:10 * ;] to flash around: with accusative, Act.9:3; before περί Act.22:6 (Eccl, and Byzant.).† (AS)

periballo.notes: περι-βάλλω [in LXX chiefly for כָּסָה pi., also for לָבַשׁ, עָטָה, etc. ;] to throw around or over, put on or over; __(a) of siege or defen­sive works: χάρακά σοι, Luk.19:43 (WH, mg.; παρεμβ-, WH, txt.); __(b) of…

periblepo.notes: περι-βλέπω [in LXX for שׁוּר, etc. ;] to look around (at). Mid., to look about one (at) : absol., Mrk.9:8 10:23; with inf., Mrk.5:32; with accusative of person(s), Mrk.3:5, 34, Luk.6:10; πάντα, Mrk.11:11.† (AS)

peribolaion.notes: περι-βόλαιον, -ου, τό (περιβάλλω), [in LXX chiefly for כְּסוּת, לְבוּשׁ and cognate forms ;] that which is thrown around, a covering, in NT, __(a) a mantle (Psa.102:27, Isa.59:17, al.) : Heb.1:12" (LXX) __(b) a veil:…

perichoros.notes: περί-χωρος, -ον [in LXX: chiefly (ἡ π.) for כִּכָּר ;] round about, neighbouring (Dem., Plut., al.). In LXX and NT, as subst., ἡ π. (sc. γῆ), the region round about: Mat.14:35, Mrk.1:28, Luk.4:14, 37 7:17 8:37,…

perideo.notes: περι-δέω [in LXX: Job.12:18 (אָסַר)* ;] to tie round, bind round: with accusative and dative, pass., Jhn.11:44.† (AS)

periecho.notes: περι-έχω, [in LXX for H661, H5362 hi., H6822 pi., etc. ;] __1. to surround, encompass: Luk.5:9 (cf. MM, xix). __2. to comprehend, include, contain, esp. of books: with accusative, Act.23:25, Rec.; accusative to a…

perierchomai.notes: περι-έρχομαι [in LXX chiefly for סָבַב ;] to go about (as an itinerant): Act.19:13 28:13 (περιελόντες, WH, R, mg.), Heb.11:37; τ. οἰκίας, from house to house: 1Ti.5:13.† (AS)

periergazomai.notes: περι-εργάζομαι [in LXX: Wis.8:5 א1, Sir.3:23 * ;] __1. to waste one's labour about a thing. __2. to be a busybody: 2Th.3:11 (cf. Plat., Apol., 19 B).† (AS)

periergos.notes: περίεργος, -ον __I. of persons; __1. over careful. __2. curious, meddling, a busybody: 1Ti.5:13. __II. Of things; __1. over-wrought. __2. super­fluous. __3. curious, uncanny; τὰ π., curious arts, magic: Act.19:19…

periistemi.notes: περι-ίστημι (Rec. ΐστημι) [in LXX: Jos.6:3 (סָבַב), 2Ki.13:31 (נָצַב ni.), 1Ki.4:16, Ep. Jer.37:1-21, Jdth.5:22, 2Ma.14:9 * ;] __1. to place around. __2. to stand around: Jhn.11:42; with accusative of person(s),…

perikalupto.notes: περι-καλύπτω [in LXX for כָּסָה pi., שָׁבַץ pu., etc. ;] to cover around, cover up or over. with accusative, Mrk.14:65, Luk.22:64; pass., before χρυσίῳ, Heb.9:4.† (AS)

perikatharma.notes: περι-κάθαρμα, -τος, τό (περικαθαίρω, to purify on all sides or completely, Deu.18:10, Jos.5:4, 4Ma.1:29*), [in LXX: Pro.21:18 (כֹּפֶר)* ;] __1. a victim, expiation (Pr, l.with). __2. refuse, rubbish: pl., 1Co.4:13…

perikeimai.notes: περί-κειμαι [in LXX: Ep. Je 24, 58, 4Ma.12:3 * ;] __1. to lie round about: μύλος, λίθος (RV, were hanged about), Mrk.9:42, Luk.17:2; νεφος μαρτύρων, Heb.12:1. __2. to have around one, be clothed with: Act.28:20;…

perikephalaia.notes: περι-κεφαλαία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX for כּוֹבַע, קוֹבַע ;] a helmet: figuratively, 1Th.5:8; with genitive explic., π. τ. σωτηρίου, Eph.6:17 (cf. Isa.59:17).† (AS)

perikrupto.notes: περι-κρύπτω to conceal entirely, keep hidden: late 2 aor., περιέκρυβσν (but see BL, § 17; Soph., Lex., see word περικρύβω), Luk.1:24.† (AS)

perikukloo.notes: περι-κυκλόω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for סָבַב ;] to encircle, encompass: of a besieged city, Luk.19:43.† (AS)

perilampo.notes: περι-λάμπω to shine around: with accusative, Luk.2:9, Act.26:13.† (AS)

perileipomai.notes: περι-λείπομαι depon. mid, and pass., [in LXX: 2Ch.34:21 A (שָׁאַר ni.), 2Ma.1:31 2Mac 8:14, 4Ma.12:6 4Mac 13:18 * ;] to be left remaining, remain over, survive: 1Th.4:15 4:17† (AS)

perilupos.notes: περί-λυπος, -ον [in LXX: Psa.42:5, 11, I Est.8:69, al. ;] very sad, deeply grieved: Mat.26:38, Mrk.6:26 14:34, Luk.18:23.† (AS)

perimeno.notes: περι-μένω [in LXX: Gen.49:18 (קָוָה pi.), Wis.8:12 * ;] to wait for: with acce., Act.1:4.† (AS)

perioche.notes: περι-οχή, -ῆς, ἡ (περιέχω), [in LXX for מָצוּד, מָצוֹר, etc. ;] __1. compass, circumference. __2. a portion circumscribed, a section: Act.8:32.† (AS)

perioikeo.notes: περι-οικέω, -ῶ to dwell round about: with accusative, Luk.1:65.† (AS)

perioikos.notes: περί-οικος, -ον [in LXX for כִּכָּר, etc. ;] dwelling around; as subst., ὁ π., a neighbour: Luk.1:58 (cf. πλησίον).† (AS)

periousios.notes: περιούσιος, -ον (περίειμι, to be over and above), [in LXX: Exo.19:5 23:22, Deu.7:6 14:2 26:18 (סְגֻלָּה, עַם)* ;] one's own, of one's own possession: λαὸς π. (cf. LXX, ll. with), Tit.2:14.† (AS)

peripateo.notes: περι-πατέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for הָלַךְ pi., hith. ;] to walk: absol., Mat.9:5, Mrk.5:42, Luk.5:23, Jhn.1:36; with pred., γυμνός, Rev.16:15; ἐπάνω Luk.11:44; διά, with genitive, Rev.21:24; ἐν, Mrk.11:27 12:38,…

peripeiro.notes: περι-πείρω to put on a spit, hence, to pierce: metaphorically, ἑαυτὸν . . . ὀδύναις, 1Ti.6:10.† (AS)

periphero.notes: περι-φέρω [in LXX: Ecc.7:8 (7) (הָלַל po.), al. ;] to carry about: Mrk.6:55, 2Co.4:10. Pass., metaphorically: Eph.4:14.† (AS)

periphroneo.notes: περι-φρονέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 4Ma.6:9 4Mac 14:1 * ;] __1. to examine on all sides, consider carefully (Aristoph.). __2. = ὑπερφρονέω, to have thouphts beyond, to despise (Thuc., Plut., al.; 4Mac, ll. with) : Tit.2:15.† (AS)

peripipto.notes: περι-πίπτω [in LXX: Rut.2:3, 2Ki.1:6 (קָרָה), Pro.11:5 (נָפַל), Dan LXX 2:9, 2Ma.6:13 2Mac 9:7 2Mac 9:21 2Mac 10:4 * ;] __1. to fall around. __2. to fall in with, light upon, come across: with dative, λῃσταῖς,…

peripoieo.notes: περι-ποιέω, -ῶ [in LXX for pi., hi., etc. ;] to make to remain over, preserve. Mid. __(a) to keep or save for oneself: τ. ψυχήν (cf. Xen., Cyr., iv, 4, 10; Arist., Pol., v, 11, 30), Luk.17:33; __(b) to get or gain…

peripoiesis.notes: περι-ποίησις, -εως, ἡ (περιποιέω), [in LXX: 2Ch.4:13 (12) (מִחְיָה) Mal.3:17 (סְגֻלָּה), Hag.2:10 (9) * ;] __1. preservation: Heb.10:39 (2 Ch, l.with). __2. acquisition, obtaining: 1Th.5:9, 2Th.2:14 (and so perh.…

peripsema.notes: περί-ψημα, -τος, τό (περιψράω, to wipe off all round), [in LXX: Tob.5:18 * ;] that which is wiped off, offscouring: metaphorically (assoc. with περικάθαρμα, which see), 1Co.4:13 (and so prob., To, l.with, as EV; but…

perirregnumi.notes: περι-ρήγνυμι (Rec. -ρρ-, see supr.) [in LXX: 2Ma.4:38 * ;] to break or tear off all around: frequently of garments, τ. ἰμάτια, Act.16:22.† (AS)

perispao.notes: περι-σπάω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for עָנָה ;] to draw around, draw off or away. Metaphorical, to distract: pass., Luk.10:40.† (AS)

perisseia.notes: περισσεία, -ας, ἡ (περισσευω), [in LXX: Ecc.12:1-14 (יִתְרוֹן and cogn. forms, Ecc.1:3, al.) * ;] __1. abundance, superfluity: Rom.5:17, 2Co.8:2 10:15, Jas.1:21 (so also Inscr., see LAE, 80). __2. (a) superiority…

perisseuma.notes: περίσσευμα, -τος, τό (περισσεύω) [in LXX: Ecc.2:15 * ;] that which is over and above, superfluity, abundance: opposite to ὑστέρημα (which see), 2Co.8:13-14; pl., Mrk.8:8; metaphorically, π. τ. καρδίας, Mat.12:34,…

perisseuo.notes: περισσεύω (περισσός), [in LXX: 1Ki.2:33 (מַרְבִּית), 1Ki.2:36 Ecc.3:19 (יָתַר ni., יוׄתֵר), Tob.4:16, Sir.10:27 11:12 19:24, Sir 30:38 (Sir.33:29), 1Ma.3:30 * ;] __I. Prop., intrans., __1. of things, to be over and…

perissos.notes: περισσός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for יֶתֶר and cogn. forms ;] __1. more than sufficient, over and above, abundant: Jhn.10:10, 2Co.9:1; with genitive (a popular substitute for πλειών, Bl., § 11, 3.4), Mat.5:37; id. with…

peristera.notes: περιστερά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for יוֹנָה ;] a dove: Mat.3:16 10:16 21:12, Mrk.1:10 11:15, Luk.2:24 3:22, Jhn.1:32 2:14, 16† (AS)

peritemno.notes: περι-τέμνω [in LXX chiefly for מוּל ;] (Ion., Epic. and late writers), to cut around (Hdt.), hence, to circumcise (π. τὰ αἰδοῖα, Hdt.) : Luk.1:59 2:21, Jhn.7:22, Act.7:8 15:5 16:3 21:21. Pass. and mid., to be…

peritithemi.notes: περι-τίθημι [in LXX for נָתַן, שׂוּם, etc. ;] to place or put around: with accusative and dative, Mat.21:33 27:48, Mrk.12:1 15:36, Jhn.19:29; of garments, etc., to put on: Mat.27:28 Mrk.15:17 (Sir.6:31).…

peritome.notes: περι-τομή, -ῆς, ἡ (περιτέμνω), [in LXX: Gen.17:13, Exo.4:25-26 (מוּל ni., מוּלָה), Jer.11:16 * ;] circumcision; __(a) of the rite itself : Jhn.7:22-23 Act.7:8, Rom.4:11, Gal.5:11, Php.3:5; __(b) of the state of…

peritrecho.notes: περι-τρέχω [in LXX: Amo.8:12, Jer.5:1 (שׁוּט pi.) * ;] to run about: with accusative loc., Mrk.6:55.† (AS)

peritrepo.notes: περι-τρέπω [in LXX: Wis.5:23 * ;] to turn about, turn: with accusative and, dative, Act.26:24 (cf. FlJ, Ant., ix, 4, 4).† (AS)

perix.notes: πέριξ adv., [in Al.: Lev.13:33 * ;] round about: αἱ π. πολεῖς, Act.5:16.† (AS)

perizonnumi.notes: περι-ζώννυμι and -ύω (see Veitch, see word), [in LXX chiefly for חָגַר, also for אָזַר, etc. ;] to gird (with dupl, accusative, as ὁ περιζωννύων με δύναμιν, Psa.18:32): pass., αἱ ὀσφύες περιεζωσμέναι, Luk.12:35; π.…

perpereuomai.notes: περπερεύοραι (περπερος, vainglorious) to boast or vaunt oneself: 1Co.13:4 (elsewhere. only in Antonin.; see Abbott, Essays, 87).† (AS)

persis.notes: 1. Persian , (Aeschulus Tragicus), etc. 2. as Substantive, (.] (entry γῆ); Persis, Persia , now Farsistan , (Herdotus Historicus) 3. (entry γυνή); a Persian woman , (Xenophon Historicus) 4. (entry χλαῖνα); a Persian…

perusi.notes: πέρυσι adv. (πέρας), last year, a year ago: ἀπὸ π. (as in π. see Deiss., BS, 221; LAE, 70), 2Co.8:10 9:2.† (AS)

peteinos.notes: πετεινός, -ή, -όν (πέτομαι), [in LXX chiefly for עוֹף ;] in trag., Ion. and late writers, winged, flying; as subst., τὸ π., a winged fowl, a bird: pl., Mat.6:26 8:20 13:4, 32, Mrk.4:4, 32, Luk.8:5 9:58 12:24 13:19,…

petomai.notes: πέτομαι [in LXX chiefly for עוּף ;] to fly: Rev.4:7 8:13 12:14 14:6 19:17 (Rec. πετάομαι, which see).† πετάομαι, [in LXX: Deu.4:17 * ;] false form of ποτάομαι, poët., frequentat. of πέτομαι (Aristoph., al.), Rec. for…

petra.notes: πέτρα, -ας, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for סֶלַע, צוּר ;] a rock, i.e. a mass of live rock as distinct from πέτρος, a detached stone or boulder : Mat.7:24-25 27:51, 60 Mrk.15:46, Luk.6:48 8:6, 13; of a hollow rock, a cave,…

petrodes.notes: πετρώδης, -ες (πέτρα, εἶδος), rock-like, rocky, stony: τὸ, τὰ π., of shallow soil with underlying rock, Mat.13:5, 20, Mrk.4:5, 16.† (AS)

petros.notes: Πέτρος, -ου, ὁ (i.e. a stone, see: πέτρα, Κηφᾶς), Simon Peter, the Apostle: Mat.4:18 10:2, Mrk.3:16, Luk.5:8, Jhn.1:41, 43, al. (AS)

peze.notes: πεζός, -ή, -όν (πούς), [in LXX for רַגְלִי ;] __1. on foot: Mat.14:13 (WH, mg., R, txt.). __2. Opp. to going by sea, by land: Mat.14:13 (WH, mg., R, mg.). __3. As adv., πεζῇ (sc. ὁδῷ), on foot or by land: Mt 1413…

pezeuo.notes: πεζεύω (πεζός) to travel on foot or by land: Act.20:13.† (AS)

phagein.notes: φάγομαιHellenistic for cl. ἔδομαι, see: ἐσθίω. ἐσθίω, and (poet, and late prose) ἔσθω, [in LXX chiefly for אכל ;] to eat; __(a) absol.: Mat.14:20, 21 Mrk.6:31, Jhn.4:31, al.; ἐν τ. φαγεῖν (on this aor. form, see M,…

phagos.notes: φάγος, -ου, ὁ (φαγεῖν, see: ἐσθίω) a glutton: Mat.11:19, Luk.7:34.† (AS)

phaino.notes: φαίνω [in LXX for אוֹר hi., רָאָה ni., etc. ;] __I. Act., __1. to bring to light, cause to appear (so most frequently in cl.). __2. Absol., to give light, shine (Hom., Plat., al.): Jhn.1:5 5:35, 2Pe.1:19, 1Jn.2:8,…

phalek.notes: Φάλεκ (T, Rec. Φαλέκ, L, mg., Φάλεγ), ὁ indecl. (Heb. D, Ge 1116), Peleg: Luk.3:35.† (AS)

phaneroo.notes: φανερόω, -ώ [in LXX: Jer.40:6 (33:6) (גָּלָה pi.) * ;] to make visible, clear, manifest or known: with accusative of thing(s), Jhn.2:11 17:6, Rom.1:19, 1Co.4:5, 2Co.2:14 11:6, Col.4:4, Tit.1:3; pass., Mrk.4:22,…

phaneros.notes: φανερός, -ά, -όν (φαίνομαι) [in LXX: Deu.29:29 (28) (גָּלָה ni.), Pro.14:4, Sir.6:22, al. ;] open to sight, visible, manifest: Gal.5:19; before ἐν, Rom.1:19, 1Jn.3:10; with dative of person(s), Act.4:16 7:13,…

phanerosis.notes: φανέρωσις, -εως, ἡ (φανερόω), [in LXX as seel. for δήλωσις אוּרִים), Lev.8:8, Cod. Ven. (Thayer, see word) * ;] manifestation: 1Co.12:7, 2Co.4:2.† (AS)

phanos.notes: φανός, -οῦ, ὁ (φαίνω), a torch or lantern (see Rutherford, NPhr., 131 f.): Jhn.18:3.† SYN.: see: λαμπάς (AS)

phanouel.notes: Φανουήλ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. פְּנוּאֵל), Phanuel: Luk.2:36.† (AS)

phantasia.notes: φαντασία, -ας, ἡ (φαντάζω), [in LXX: Zec.10:1 (חֲזִיז), חֲזִיז), Hab.2:18-19 3:10, Wis.18:17 * ;] __1. as philos. term, __(a) imagination; __(b) = φάντασμα (Plat., Arist.). __2. In later writers (Polyb., al.), show,…

phantasma.notes: φάντασμα, -τος, τό (φαντάζω), [in LXX: Job.20:8 A (חִזָּיוֹן), Isa.28:7 A, Wis.17:15 * ;] = φάσμα, an appearance, apparition (Æsch., al.): Mat.14:26, Mrk.6:49 (see DCG, i, 111b).† (AS)

phantazo.notes: φαντάζω (φαίνω) [in LXX: Wis.6:16, Sir.31:5 (34:5)* ;] to make visible. In cl. used in pass. only, = φαίνομαι, to become visible, appear: ptcp., Heb.12:21.† (AS)

pharagx.notes: φάραγξ, -αγγος, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for נַחַל, also for גַּיְא, etc. ;] a chasm, ravine: Luk.3:5 (LXX) (see DB, iv, 845 f.).† (AS)

pharao.notes: Φαραώ, ὁ indecl. (in FlJ, Ant., viii, 6, 2, Φαραών, -ῶνος), (Heb. פַּרְעֹה), Pharaoh, the general title of the kings of Egypt: Act.7:13, 21, Rom.9:17, Heb.11:24; Φ. βασιλεὺς Αἰγύπτου, Act.7:10.† (AS)

phares.notes: Φαρές, ὁ indecl. (Heb. פֶּרֶץ, Gen.38:29), Peres: Mat.1:3, Luk.3:33.† (AS)

pharisaios.notes: Φαρισαῖος, -ου, ὁ (Aram. פְּרִישָׁא; see Dalman, Gr., 157.n, Words, 2.n), a Pharisee: Mat.23:26, Php.3:5; usually in pl., Mat.9:11, Mrk.2:18, al.; Φ. κ. γραμματεῖς, Mat.5:20, Mrk.2:16, Luk.5:21, al.; Φ. κ.…

pharmakeia.notes: φαρμακία (Rec. -εία), -ας, ἡ (φαρμακεύω, to administer drugs) poët. and late prose form of φαρμακεία, [in LXX: Exo.7:11, 22 8:7, 18 (3, 14) (לָט, חַרְטֹם), Isa.47:9, 12 (כֶּשֶׁף) Wis.12:4 18:13 * ;] __1. generally,…

pharmakos.notes: φαρμακός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for כָּשַׁף, etc. ;] devoted to magical arts. As subst., ὁ φ. = φαρμακεύς, a magician, sorcerer: Rev.21:8 22:15.† (AS)

phasis.notes: φάσις, -εως, ἡ (φαίνω), [in LXX: Est.4:17, Da TH Su 1:55, 4Ma.15:25 א1 * ;] information, esp. against fraud or other crime: Act.21:31.† (AS)

phasko.notes: φάσκω [in LXX: Gen.26:20 (אָמַר), Dan LXX Bel 1:8, 2Ma.14:27 2Mac 14:32, 3Ma.3:7 * ;] to affirm, assert: with accusative and inf., Act.24:9 25:19; with inf. and nom., Rom.1:22.† (AS)

phatne.notes: φάτνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for אֵבוּס, etc. ;] a manger: Luk.2:7, 12 2:16 13:15 (see DB, iii, 234a, DCG, ii, 111a),† (AS)

phaulos.notes: φαῦλος, -η, -ον [in LXX: Pro.22:8 (עֶוֶל) Pro.29:9 (אֱוִיל), etc. ;] slight, worthless, of no account, both of persons and things in various shades of meaning (see LS, see word), in NT, as frequently in cl., always…

pheidomai.notes: φείδομαι [in LXX for חָמַל, חוּס, חָשַׂךְ, etc. ;] to spare: 2Co.13:2; with genitive of person(s), Act.20:29, Rom.8:32 11:21, 1Co.7:28, 2Co.1:23, 2Pe.2:4-5; with inf., to forbear, 2Co.12:6.† (AS)

phelix.notes: φῆλιξ (L, Φή-), -ικος, ὁ Felix, procurator of Judæa: Act.23:24, 26 24:3, 22 24:24-25, 27 25:14.† (AS)

phelonesphailones.notes: φελόνης (Rec. φαιλ-), -ου, ὁ by metath. for παινόλης (also φενόλης, φαινόλιον; Lat. pænula) a cloak (see DCG, i, 338; on the idea that the meaning here is book-cover, see CGT, in l; Milligan, NTD, 20; Field, Notes,…

pheme.notes: φήμη, -ης, ἡ (φημί) [in LXX: Pro.16:2 (15:30) (שְׁמוּעָה), 2Ma.4:39, 3Ma.3:2, 4Ma.4:22 * ;] a saying or report: Mat.9:26, Luk.4:14.† (AS)

phemi.notes: φημί [in LXX chiefly for נָאַם, also for אָמַר; frequently in 2-4 Mac ;] to declare, say: frequently in quoting the words of another, Mat.13:29 26:61, Luk.7:40 22:58, Jhn.1:23, al.; interjected into the recorded…

phengos.notes: φέγγος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for נֹגַהּ ;] light, brightness, usually with genitive of something that shines or reflects: of the moon, Mat.24:29, Mrk.13:24; of a lamp, Luk.11:33 (WH, RV, φῶς).† SYN.: αὐγή, q.v (AS)

phero.notes: φέρω [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא. hi., also for נָשָׂא, etc. ;] to bear; __1. to bear, carry: with accusative, Luk.23:26 24:1, Jhn.19:39, Heb.1:3 (see Westc., in l); pass., Act.2:2 27:15, 17 Heb.6:1, 2Pe.1:17-18; of…

phestos.notes: φῆστος, -ου, ὁ (Porcius), Festus, procurator of Judæa: Act.24:27 25:1 ff. Act.26:24-25, 32.† (AS)

pheugo.notes: φεύγω [in LXX chiefly for נוּס, also for בָּרַח, etc. ;] to flee from or away, take flight: absol., Mat.8:33 26:56, Mrk.5:14 14:50, Luk.8:34, Jhn.10:12, Act.7:29; before εἰς, Mat.2:13 10:23 24:16 (WH, txt.),…

phiale.notes: φιάλη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for מִזְרָק ;] a shallow bowl (= Lat. patera), used for pouring libations, etc.: Rev.5:8 15:7 16:1-17 17:1 21:9.† (AS)

philadelpheia.notes: Φιλαδελφία (Rec. -έλφεια), -ας, ἡ Philadelphia, a city of Lydia: Rev.1:11 3:7.† (AS)

philadelphia.notes: φιλαδελφία, -ας, ἡ (φιλάδελφος), [in LXX: 4Ma.13:23 4Mac 13:26 4Mac 14:1 * ;] the love of brothers, brotherly love: of Christians' mutual love as brethren (see: ἀδελφός), Rom.12:10, 1Th.4:9, Heb.13:1, 1Pe.1:22,…

philadelphos.notes: φιλ-άδελφος, -ον [in LXX: 2Ma.15:14, 4Ma.13:21 4Mac 15:10 * ;] loving one's brother, loving like a brother (Soph., Xen., al.): of Christians' love for one another (EV, loving as brethren), 1Pe.3:8.† (AS)

philagathos.notes: φιλ-άγαθος, -ον [in LXX: Wis.7:22 * ;] loving that which is good, loving goodness: Tit.1:8 (Arist., Polyb., a l.).† (AS)

philandros.notes: φίλανδρος, -ον __1. loving men (Æsch.). __2. Of a wife, loving her husband (frequently in epitaphs, see LS, see word, Deiss., BS, 255): Tit.2:4.† (AS)

philanthropia.notes: φιλανθρωπία, -ας, ἡ (φιλάνθρωπος, humane), [in LXX: Est.8:13, 2Ma.6:22 2Mac 14:9, 3Ma.3:15 3Mac 3:18 * ;] humanity, kindness (see Field, Notes, 147 f.): Act.28:2, Tit.3:4 (cf. also DCG, ii, 356 ff.).† (AS)

philanthropos.notes: φιλανθρώπως adv., [in LXX: 2Ma.9:27, 3Ma.3:20 * ;] humanely, kindly: Act.27:3.† (AS)

philarguria.notes: φιλαργυρία, -ας, ἡ (φιλάργυρος), [in LXX: 4Ma.1:26 4Mac 2:15 א1 * ;] love of money, avarice: 1Ti.6:1.† SYN.: πλεονεξία, covetousness (see Tr., Syn., § xxiv) (AS)

philarguros.notes: φιλ-άργυρος, -ον [in LXX: 4Ma.2:8 * ;] loving money, avaricious: Luk.16:14, 2Ti.3:2.† (AS)

philautos.notes: φίλ-αυτος, -ον loving oneself (Arist.); in bad sense (ib.), selfish: 2Ti.3:2.† SYN.: αὐθάδης (see Tr., Syn., § xciii) (AS)

philedonos.notes: φιλήδονος, -ον (φίλος, ἡδονή), loving pleasure: 2Ti.3:4 (Polyb., Plut. al.).† (AS)

philema.notes: φίλημα, -τος, τό (φιλέω), [in LXX: Pro.27:6, Sng.1:2 (נְשִׁיקָה) * ;] a kiss: Luk.7:45 22:48; as a token of Christian brotherhood, φ. ἅγιον, Rom.16:16, 1Co.16:20, 2Co.13:12, 1Th.5:26; φ. ἀγάπης, 1Pe.5:14 (see Lft.,…

phileo.notes: φιλέω, -ῶ (φίλος), [in LXX: Gen.27:4, 9, al. (אָהַב), Gen.27:27, al. (נָשַׁק), La 1:2 (רֵעַ) Wis.8:2, al. ;] __1. to love (with the love of emotion and friendship, Lat, amare; v, SYN.): with accusative of person(s),…

philia.notes: φιλία, -ας, ἡ (φίλος), [in LXX chiefly for אַהֲבָה ;] friendship: with genitive obj., Jas.4:4.† (AS)

philippesios.notes: φιλιππήσιος, -ου, ὁ (for other forms in use, see Lft., in l) a Philippian: Php.4:15.† (AS)

philippoi.notes: φίλιπποι, -ων, οἱ (on the pl., see WM, § 27, 3) Philippi: Act.16:12 20:6, Php.1:1, 1Th.2:2.† (AS)

philippos.notes: φίλιππος, -ου, ὁ Philip; __1. the husband of Herodias: Mat.14:3, Mrk.6:17. __2. The tetrarch: Mat.16:13, Mrk.8:27, Luk.3:1. __3. The apostle: Mat.10:3, Mrk.3:18, Luk.6:14, Jhn.1:44-49 6:5, 7 12:21-22 14:8-9,…

philologos.notes: 1. fond of speaking , (Plato Philosophus) 2. fond of learning, literary , Lat. studiosus, (Aristotle Philosopher) (ML)

philoneikia.notes: φιλονεικία, -ας, ἡ (φιλόνεικος), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:4, 4Ma.1:26 4Mac 8:26 * ;] love of strife, rivalry, emulation (Plat., al.), but mostly in bad sense (Thuc., al.), contentiousness, contention: Luk.22:24 (but see Field,…

philophronos.notes: φιλοφρόνως (φιλόφρων) adv., [in LXX: 2Ma.3:9, 4Ma.8:5 * ;] kindly, with friendliness: Act.28:7.† (AS)

philoproteuo.notes: φιλο-πρωτιύω (φιλόπρωτος, Plut., al.) to strive to be first: III Jn 9 (eccl.).† (AS)

philos.notes: φίλος, -η, -ον [in LXX chiefly for רֵעַ, אָהַב ;] __1. pass., beloved, dear (Hom., Eur., al.). __2. Act., loving, friendly (in cl. less frequently and only in poets): Act.19:31. As subst., a friend; __(a) masc., ὁ…

philosophia.notes: Φιλο-σοφία, -ας, ἡ (φιλόσοφος), [in LXX: 4Ma.1:1 4Mac 5:10 4Mac 5:22 4Mac 7:9 4Mac 7:21 * ;] the love and pursuit of wisdom; hence, philosophy, investi­gation of truth and nature: of the so-called philosophy of false…

philosophos.notes: φιλό-σοφος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Dan LXX 1:20 (אַשָּׁף), 4Ma.1:1 4Mac 5:35 4Mac 7:7 * ;] a philosopher: Act.17:18.† (AS)

philostorgos.notes: φιλόστοργος, -ον (στοργή, family affection), [in LXX: 4Ma.15:13 * ;] tenderly loving, affectionate (Xen., Plut., al.): of Christians, Rom.12:10.† (AS)

philoteknos.notes: φιλότεκνος, -ον (φίλος, τέκνον), [in LXX: 4Ma.15:4-6 * ;] loving one's children (Hdt., Arist., Plut., al.): of women, joined with φίλανδρος, which see, Tit.2:4.† (AS)

philotimeomai.notes: φιλοτιμέομαι, -οῦμαι (φίλος, τιμή), [in LXX: 4Ma.1:35 A ;] to love or seek after honour, hence, to be ambitious, emulous (Plat., Plut., al.): with inf., Rom.15:20, 2Co.5:9, 1Th.4:11.† (AS)

philoxenia.notes: φιλο-ξενία, -ας, ἡ (φιλόξενος), love of strangers, hospitality: Rom.12:13, Heb.13:2.† (AS)

phimoo.notes: φιμόω, -ῶ (φιμός, a muzzle), [in LXX: Deu.25:4 (חָסַם), Dan LXX Su 1:61, 4Ma.1:35 א R * ;] to muzzle: 1Co.9:9, 1Ti.5:18" (LXX) ; metaphorically, to put to silence: with accusative of person(s), Mat.22:34, 1Pe.2:15;…

phlogizo.notes: φλογίζω (φλόξ), [in LXX: Exo.9:24 (לָקַח hith.), Num.21:14 (†), Psa.97:3 (לָהַט pi.), Da TH Dan.3:27(94) (חָרַךְ ithp.), Sir.3:30, 1Ma.3:5 * ;] to set on fire, burn, burn up: figuratively, Jas.3:6 (on the meaning of…

phlox.notes: φλόξ genitive, φλογός, [in LXX chiefly for לַהַב, לֶהָבָה ;] a flame: Luk.16:24; φ, πυρός, Act.7:30, 2Th.1:8, Heb.1:7" (LXX), Rev.1:14 2:18 19:12.† (AS)

phluareo.notes: φλυαρέω, -ῶ (φλύαρος), to talk nonsense: with accusative of person(s), ἡμᾶς (EV, prating against us), III Jn 10.† (AS)

phluaros.notes: φλύαρος, -ον (φλύω, to babble), [in LXX: 4Ma.5:10 * ;] babbling, garrulous: 1Ti.5:13 (EV, tattlers; of things, φιλοσοφία, 4Mac, l.with).† (AS)

phobeo.notes: φοβέέω, ῶ (φόβος) [in LXX chiefly for מִן יָרֵא ;] __1. in Hem., to put to flight. Pass., to be put to flight, to flee affrighted __2. to terrify, frighten (Wis 179; Hdt. and Att..). Pass. (so always in NT; cf. M,…

phoberos.notes: φοβερός, -ά, -όν (φοβέω), [in LXX chiefly for יָרֵא, also for דְּחַל, אָיֹם ;] fearful, whether act, or pass.; __1. act., = δεινός, causing fear, terrible (LXX): Heb.10:27, 31 12:21 __2. Pass., = δειλός, feeling…

phobetron.notes: φόβητρον (LTr., WH, -θρον), -ου, τό (φοβέω), [in LXX: Isa.19:17 (חָגָא) * ;] that which causes fright, a terror: pl. (as always, exc.Isa, l.with), Luk.21:11 (Hipp., Plat.).† (AS)

phobos.notes: φόβος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for יִרְאָה, also for פַּחַד, אֵימָה, etc. ;] __1. in Hom., flight. __2. That which causes flight, fear, dread, terror: Luk.1:12, Act.5:5, 1Ti.5:20. 1Jn.4:18, al.; cogn. accusative,…

phoibe.notes: φοίβη, -ης, ἡ Phæbe, a deaconess of Cenchræa: Rom.16:1.† (AS)

phoinike.notes: φοινίκη, -ης, ἡ Phænicia: Act.11:19 15:3 21:2 (see DB, iii, 856b, 857a.n).† (AS)

phoinix.notes: Φοῖνιξ (on the accent, see Bl., § 4, 2; WM, § 6, lc), -ικος, ὁ, [in LXX for תָּמָר תִּמֹּר ;] the date palm, palm: τὰ βαΐα τῶν φ., Jhn.12:13; of palm branches, φοίνικες (as Arist., 2Ma.10:7, al.), Rev.7:9.† (AS)

pholeos.notes: φωλεός, -οῦ ὁ a hole, den, lair: Mat.8:20, Luk.9:58.† (AS)

phone.notes: φωνή, -ῆς, ἡ, [in LXX chiefly and very frequently for קוֹל ;] a voice; __(a) prop., of persons, Mat.2:18 (LXX), al.; φ. αἴρειν (ἐπαίρειν), Luk.17:13, Act.2:14, al.; φ. μεγάλῃ εἰπεῖν (λέγειν, φωνεῖν, etc.), Luk.8:28,…

phoneo.notes: φωνέω, -ῶ (φωνή), [in LXX for קָרָא (Jer.17:11, al.), etc. ;] __I. Intrans. __1. Of persons, to call out, cry out, speak aloud: Luk.8:8; φωνῇ μεγάλῃ, Mrk.1:26, Act.16:28; ἐφώνησε (φωνῇ μεγάλῇ) λέγων, Luk.8:54,…

phoneuo.notes: φονεύω (φονεύς), [in LXX chiefly for רָצַח ;] to kill, murder. absol., Mat.5:21, Jas.4:2; μὴ (οὐ) φονεύσῃς (-εις), Mat.5:21 19:18, Mrk.10:19, Luk.18:20, Rom.13:9, Jas.2:11 (all from Exo.20:13-14); with accusative,…

phoneus.notes: φονεύς, -έως, ὁ (φόνος) [in LXX: Wis.12:5 * ;] a murderer: Mat.22:7 Act.7:52 28:4, 1Pe.4:15, Rev.21:8 22:15; ἀνὴρ φ., Act.3:14.† SYN.: ἀνθρωποκτόνος (AS)

phonos.notes: φόνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for דָּם, פֶּה, etc. ;] murder, slaughter: Mrk.15:7, Luk.23:19, 25, Act.9:1, Rom.1:29; φ. μαχαίρης (cf. Exo.17:13, al.), Heb.11:37; pl., Mat.15:19, Mrk.7:21, Rev.9:21.† (AS)

phoreo.notes: φορέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Pro.16:23 (יָסַף hi.), Sir.11:5, al. ;] frequent. of φέρω, denoting repeated or habitual action (cf. Tr., Syn., § lviii), most commonly used of clothing, weapons, etc., to bear constantly, wear:…

phoron.notes: φόρον, -ου, τό (Lat. forum): Ἀππίου Φ., see: Ἄππιος (AS)

phoros.notes: φόρος, .-ου, ὁ (φέρω), [in LXX chiefly for מַס, also for מִדָּה, etc. ;] tribute paid by a subject nation (cf. 1Ma.10:33): φ. δοῦναι (1Ma.8:4 1Mac 8:7), Luk.20:22 23:2; ἀποδοῦναι, Rom.13:7; τελεῖν, Rom.13:6 (Hdt.,…

phortion.notes: φορτίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of φόρτος), [in LXX chiefly for מַשָּׂא ;] a burden, load: of the cargo of a ship (Hdt., al.), Act.27:10; metaphorically, Mat.11:30 23:4, Luk.11:46, Gal.6:5.† SYN.: βάρος (which see), ὄγκος (AS)

phortizo.notes: φορτίζω (φόρτος), [in LXX: Eze.16:33 (שָׁחַד) * ;] to load: with dupl. accusative, Luk.11:46; pass., to be laden: metaphorically (EV, heavy laden), Mat.11:28.† (AS)

phortounatos.notes: φορτούνατος (Rec. Φουρ-, see Mayser, 116 f.), -ου, ὁ (Lat.) Fortunatus: 1Co.16:17 (see Lft., Cl. Ro., 1Co., 59).† (AS)

phos.notes: φῶς (Att.. contr. from φάος; φάω), genitive, φωτός, τό, [in LXX chiefly for אוֹר ;] light (opposite to τὸ σκότος, ἡ σκοτία): Mat.17:2, 5, Jhn.11:9-10, 2Co.4:6; ἡλίου, Rev.22:5; of a lamp, Luk.8:16 11:33, Jhn.5:35,…

phosphoros.notes: φωσ-φόρος, -ον (φῶς, φέρω), light-bringing; as subst., ὁ φ., the morning star (Plat., al.; cf. ἐωσφόρος, Isa.14:12, Job.3:9, Psa.110:3): metaphorically, 2Pe.1:19 (see Mayor, in l).† (AS)

phoster.notes: φωστήρ, -ῆρος, ὁ (φῶς), [in LXX: Gen.1:14, 16 (מָאוֹר), Dan LXX 12:3 (זֹהַר), I Est.8:79, Wis.13:2, Sir.43:7 * ;] a luminary, light: Php.2:15, Rev.21:11.† SYN.: φέγγος, φῶς (AS)

photeinos.notes: φωτεινός (WH, φωτινός), ή, όν (φῶς), [in LXX: Sir.17:31 23:19 * ;] bright, light: νεφέλη, Mat.17:5; opposite to σκοτεινός, Mat.6:22, Luk.11:34, 36.† (AS)

photismos.notes: φωτισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (φωτίζω) [in LXX: Job.3:9, Psa.27:1 44:3 78:14 139:11 (אוֹר), Psa.89:8 (מָאוֹר) * ;] illumination, light: metaphorically, 1Co.4:4 4:6.† (AS)

photizo.notes: φωτίζω (φῶς) [in LXX for אוֹר hi, יָרָה hi., etc. ;] __1. intrans., to shine, give light (Arist., Plut., a1.): before ἐπί, Rev.22:5. __2. Trans. __(a) to illumine, enlighten (Diod., Plut., al.): with accusative,…

phragellion.notes: φραγέλλιον, -ου, τό (Lat. flagellum), a scourge: Jhn.2:15.† (AS)

phragelloo.notes: φραγελλόω, -ῶ (φραγέλλιον, which see), to scourge: with accusative, Mat.27:26, Mrk.15:15 (eccl.).† (AS)

phragmos.notes: φραγμός, -oῦ, ὁ (φράσσω), [in LXX chiefly for גָּדֵר, גְּדֵרָה, also for גְּדֵרָה, etc. ;] __1. prop., a fencing in (Soph., OT, 1387). __2. = φράγμα, a fence: Mat.21:33, Mrk.12:1, Luk.14:23. Metaphorical, μεσότοιχον…

phrasso.notes: φράσσω [in LXX: Job.38:8 (סָכַךְ hi.), Hos.2:6(8) (שׂוּךְ), Pro.21:13 (אָטַם), etc. ;] to fence in, stop, close: στόματα λεόντων, Heb.11:33; στόμα, metaphorically, Rom.3:19; pass., καύχησις, 2Co.11:10.† (AS)

phrazo.notes: φράζω [in LXX: Job.6:24 (בִּין hi.) Job.12:8 (יָרָה hi.), Dan LXX 2:4 (חָוָה pa.) * ;] to show forth, tell, declare, explain: Mat.13:36 (WH, RV, διασάφησον) Mat.15:15.† (AS)

phrear.notes: φρέαρ, -ατος, τό [in LXX chiefly for בְּאֵר ;] a well: Luk.14:5, Sng.4:11-12; φ. τῆς ἀβύσσου, Rev.9:1-2.† (AS)

phren.notes: φρήν genitive, φρενός, ἡ, [in LXX most frequently in Pr (Pro.6:32, al.) and chiefly for לֵב; also 3Ma.4:16 3Mac 5:47 ;] chiefly in Hom. and Trag., but also in Plat., al., both sing. and pl.; __1. in physical sense,…

phrenapatao.notes: φρεν-απατάω, -ῶ (φρεναπάτης), to deceive one's mind (Lft., deceive by fancies, see Ga., l.with): with accusative of person(s), Gal.6:3.† (AS)

phrenapates.notes: φρεναπάτης, -ου, ὁ (φρήν, ἀπάτη), a deceiver (B1., deceiver of his own mind, § 28, 5.2, where see ref. to π.): Tit.1:10 (eccl.).† (AS)

phrisso.notes: φρίσσω (Att.. -ττω, and so 4Ma.14:9 4Mac 17:7) [in LXX: Job.4:15 ( pi.), etc. ;] __1. to be rough, bristle. __2. to shiver, shudder, tremble, from fear: Jas.2:19 (see Hort, in l).† (AS)

phronema.notes: φρόνημα, -τος, τό (φρονέω), [in LXX: 2Ma.7:21 2Mac 13:9 * ;] that which is in the mind (the content of φρονεῖν, ICC, Ro., 88), the thought: Rom.8:6-7, 27.† (AS)

phroneo.notes: φρονέω, -ῶ (φρήν), [in LXX: Deu.32:29, Zec.9:2 (חָכָם), Psa.94:8 (שָׂכַל hi.), Isa.44:18 (בִּין) Isa.44:28, Est.8:13, Wis.1:1 14:30, 1Ma.10:20, 2Ma.9:12; 2Ma.14:8 2Mac 14:26 * ;] __1. to have understanding (Hon.,…

phronesis.notes: φρόνησις, -εως, ἡ (φρονέω), [in LXX for בִּינָה, תָּבוּן, חׇכְמָה, etc. ;] understanding, practical wisdom, prudence: Luk.1:17, Eph.1:8.† SYN.: see: σοφία, and cf. Lft., Notes, 317 (AS)

phronimos.notes: φρόνιμος, -ον (φρονέω), [in LXX for בִּין, חָכָם etc. ;] practically wise, sensible, prudent: Mat.10:16 24:45, Luk.12:42, 1Co.10:15; opposite to μωρός, Mat.7:24 25:2, 4 25:8-9, 1Co.4:10; to ἄφρων, 2Co.11:19; φ. παρ᾽…

phrontizo.notes: φροντίζω (φροντίς, thought), [in LXX: 1Ki.9:5 (דָּאַג), Psa.40:17 (חָשַׁב), al. ;] to give heed, take thought (in cl. usually absol., with accusative, with genitive), with inf., Tit.3:8 (see Bl., § 69, 4; M, Pr., 206…

phroureo.notes: φρουρέω, -ῶ (φρουρός, a guard), [in LXX: I Est.4:56, Jdth.3:6, Wis.17:16, 1Ma.11:3 * ;] to guard, keep under guard, protect or keep by guarding: 2Co.11:32; metaphorically, Gal.3:23, Php.4:7, 1Pe.1:5.† (AS)

phruassomai.notes: φρυάσσω (so Ps, l.with, NT; elsewhere depon., φρυάσσομαι, Att.. -ττ-), [in LXX: Psa.2:1 (רָגַשׁ), 2Ma.7:34 R, 3Ma.2:2 * ;] prop., of horses, to neigh, whinny and prance (Plut., al.). Metaphorical, to be wanton,…

phruganon.notes: φρύγανον, -ου, τό (φρύγω, to parch), [in LXX chiefly for קַשׁ, also for חָרוּל, etc. ;] a dry stick: pl., brushwood: Act.28:3.† (AS)

phrugia.notes: φρυγία, -ας, ἡ (prop., the adj., Φρυγίος, -α, -ον; sc. γῆ, χώρα), Phrygia, a region of Asia Minor: Act.2:10; Φ. καὶ Γαλατικὴ χώρα (Γ. χ. κ. Φ.), Act.16:6 18:23 (on these phrases see DB, i, 89 f.; CGT, Gal., xxii…

phthano.notes: φθάνω [in LXX for נָגַע hi., מְטָא, etc. ;] __1. to come before another, anticipate (cl.): with accusative of person(s), 1Th.4:15. __2. In late writers and MGr. (see Kennedy, Sources, 156; Lft., Notes, 35), to come,…

phthartos.notes: φθαρτός, -ή, -όν (φθείρω) [in LXX: Lev.22:25 (מִשְׁחָת), Isa.54:17 A א3, Wis.9:15 14:8, 2Ma.7:16 * ;] perishable, corruptible: ἄνθρωπος, Rom.1:23 (opposite to ἄφθαρτος θεός); στέφανος, 1Co.9:25 (opposite to αρτος);…

phtheiro.notes: φθείρω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁחַת hi., pi., also for חָבַל, etc. ;] to destroy, corrupt, spoil (on the varied usage and distinctive meaning of the word, see Mayor on 2Pe, App., 175 ff.): with accusative, 1Co.3:17…

phthengomai.notes: φθέγγομαι [in LXX for עָנָה, נָבַע hi., etc. ;] of men or animals, to utter a sound or voice: absol., Act.4:18; before ἐν φωνῇ, 2Pe.2:16; with accusative, ὑπέρογκα, 2Pe.2:18.† (AS)

phthinoporinos.notes: φθιν-οπωρινός, -ή, -όν (φθινόπωρον, late autumn), autumnal: δένδρα φ., autumn trees (said to be without fruit therefore at a time when fruit might be expected; see Mayor's elaborate note, Ep. Ju., 55-59, and reff.…

phthoneo.notes: φθονέω, -ῶ (φθόνος), [in LXX: Tob.4:7, 16 AB * ;] to envy: with dative (L, txt., Tr., mg., WH, mg., accusative), as in cl., Gal.5:26.† (AS)

phthongos.notes: φθόγγος, -ου, ὁ (φθέγγομαι), [in LXX: Psa.19:4 (קַו), Wis.19:18 * ;] a sound: Rom.10:18 (LXX) 1Co.14:7.† (AS)

phthonos.notes: φθόνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Wis.2:24 6:23, 1Ma.8:16, 3Ma.6:7 * ;] envy: Rom.1:29, Gal.5:21, 1Ti.6:4, Tit.3:3, 1Pe.2:1; διὰ φθόνον, Mat.27:18, Mrk.15:10, Php.1:15; πρὸς φθόνον ἐπιποθεῖ τ. πνεῦμα (on the meaning, see R,…

phthora.notes: φθορά, -ᾶς, ἡ (φθείρω) [in LXX for שַׁחַת, חֶבֶל, etc. ;] destruction, corruption, decay (see Mayor on 2Pe, App.,175 ff.): Rom.8:21, 1Co.15:42, Col.2:22, 2Pe.2:12; opposite to ζωὴ αἰώνιος, Gal.6:8; by meton., of that…

phuge.notes: φυγή, -ῆς, ἡ (φεύγω), [in LXX chiefly for מָנוֹס and cogn. forms ;] flight: Mat.24:20.† (AS)

phulake.notes: φυλακή, -ῆς, ἡ (φυλάσσω) [in LXX chiefly for מִשְׁמֶרֶת and cogn. forms, also for כֶּלֶא etc. ;] __(a) actively, a guarding, guard, watch (Hom., Plat., Xen., al.): cogn. accusative, φυλάσσειν φυλακάς, to keep watch,…

phulakizo.notes: φυλακίζω [in LXX: Wis.18:4 * ;] to imprison: Act.22:19.† (AS)

phulakterion.notes: φυλακτήριον, -ου, τό __1. an outpost, fortification (Thuc., al.). __2. a safeguard (Plat.). __3. an amulet (Plut., al.); in NT for the Talmudic תְּפִלִּין, a prayer fillet, a phylactery, a small strip of parchment on…

phulakterios.notes: φύλαξ, -ακος, ὁ (φυλάσσω), [in LXX for שָׁמַר, צוּר ;] a guard, keeper: Act.5:23 12:6, 19.† (AS)

phulasso.notes: φυλάσσω [in LXX chiefly and very frequently for שָׁמַר, also for נָצַר, etc. ;] to guard, watch; __(a) to guard or watch: with cogn. accusative, φυλακάς, Luk.2:8; with accusative of person(s), Act.12:4 28:16; pass.,…

phule.notes: φυλή, ῆς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for; מַטֶּה, also for שֵׁבֶט, מִשְׁפָּחָה, etc. ;] a body of men united by kinship or habitation, a clan or tribe: of the tribes of Israel, Mat.19:28, Luk.2:36 22:30, Act.13:21, Rom.11:1,…

phullon.notes: φύλλον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for עָלֶה ;] a leaf: Mat.21:19 24:32, Mrk.11:13 13:28, Rev.22:2.† (AS)

phuo.notes: φύω [in LXX: Pro.26:9, Eze.37:8 (עָלָה), etc. ;] __1. trans., to bring forth, produce (of men, to beget); pass., to spring up, grow: Luk.8:6, 8. __2. Intrans., in 2 aor., pf., plpf., and rarely in present (see LS,…

phurama.notes: φύραμα, -τος, τό (φυράω, to mix), [in LXX: Exo.8:3 (Exo.7:28) Exo.12:34 (מִשְׁאֶרֶת), מִשְׁאֶרֶת), Num.15:20-21 (עֲרִיסָה) * ;] that which is mixed or kneaded, a lump: of dough, Rom.11:16, 1Co.5:6-7, Gal.5:9; of…

phusikos.notes: φυσικός, -ή, -όν (ψύσις), natural __(a) produced by nature, innate (Xen., Arist., al.); __(b) according to nature (Arist., Diod., al.): opposite to παρὰ φύσιν, Rom.1:26-27; __(with) governed by mere natural instinct…

phusioo.notes: φυσιόω, -ῶ (φῦσα, bellows), = cl. φυσάω, to puff or blow up, inflate. Metaphorical, to puff up, make proud: 1Co.8:1. Pass., to be puffed up with pride: 1Co.4:18-19 5:2 13:4; before ὑπό, Col.2:18; before ὑπὲρ . . .…

phusiosis.notes: φυσίωσις, -εως, ἡ (φυσιόω), a puffing up, swelling with pride: pl., 2Co.12:20.† (AS)

phusis.notes: φύσις, -εως, ἡ (φύω) [in LXX: Wis.7:20 13:1 19:20 אΑ, 3Ma.3:29, 4Ma.1:20 4Mac 5:7-8 4Mac 5:25 4Mac 13:27 4Mac 15:13 4Mac 15:25 4Mac 16:3 * ;] nature, i.e., __(a) the nature (natural powers or constitution) of a…

phuteia.notes: φυτεία, -ας, ἡ (φυτεύω), [in LXX: 4Ki.19:29 (נָטַע), Mic.1:6, Eze.17:7 (מַטָּע) * ;] __1. a planting (Xen., al.; LXX). __2. = φύτευμα, that which is planted, a plant (Inscr.): Mat.15:13.† (AS)

phuteuo.notes: φυτεύω (φυτόν, a plant, φύω), [in LXX chiefly for נָטַע, also for שָׁתַל, etc. ;] to plant: Luk.17:28, 1Co.3:6-8; with accusative, φυτείαν, Mat.15:13; ἀμπελῶνα, Mat.21:33, Mrk.12:1, Luk.20:9, 1Co.9:7; pass., before…

piazo.notes: πιάζω (cf. MGr. πιάνω; see Kennedy, Sources, 155), Doric and late Att.. for πιέζω in its later senses [in LXX: Sng.2:15 (אָחַז), Sir.23:21 * ;] __1. to lay hold of: Act.3:7 (Theocr.). __2. to take, capture,…

piezo.notes: πιέζω [in LXX : Mic.6:15 (דָּרַךְ)* ;] __1. to press, press down or to­gether: Luk.6:38 (cl). __2. Later, to seize (see: πιάζω).† (AS)

pikraino.notes: πικραίνω (πικρός), [in LXX for מָרַד, קָצַף, etc. ;] to make bitter: Rev.10:9; pass., Rev.8:11 10:10; metaphorically, to embitter (LXX) : pass., before πρός, Col.3:19.† (AS)

pikria.notes: πικρία, -ας, ἡ (πικρός), [in LXX chiefly for מַר and cognate forms ;] bitterness; __(a) of taste (Arist., Jer.15:17, al.); __(b) metaphorically, of temper, character, etc.: Rom.3:14 (LXX), Eph.4:31; ῥίζα πικρίας,…

pikros.notes: πικρός, -ά, -όν (poët. in cl.), [in LXX chiefly for מַר ;] __1. sharp, pointed. __2. sharp to the senses; of taste, bitter: opposite to γλυκύ, Jas.3:11; metaphorically, harsh, bitter: Jas.3:14 (AS)

pilatos.notes: Πειλᾶτος (Rec. Πιλάτος, Tr., -ᾶτος, see WH, App., 155), -ου, ὁ, Pontius Pilate: Mat.27:2, Mrk.15:1, Luk.3:1, Jhn.18:29, Act.3:13, 1Ti.6:13, al (AS)

pimpremi.notes: πίμπρημι (in cl. prose, rare in the simple form), [in LXX: Num.5:21-22, 27 (צָבֶה) * ;] __1. to blow, burn. __2. Later, to cause to swell; mid., of parts of the body, to become swollen (LXX) : Act.28:6 (cf.…

pinakidion.notes: πινακίδιον, -ου, τό (dimin. of πινακίς, which see), [in Sm.: Eze.9:2 * ;] a writing tablet: Luk.1:63.† (AS)

pinax.notes: πίναξ, -ακος, ὁ [in LXX: 4Ma.17:7 R * ;] prop., a board, plank; hence, of various at wooden articles; __(a) a tablet; __(b) a disc, a dish: Mat.14:8, 11, Mrk.6:25, 28 Luk.11:39.† (AS)

pino.notes: πίνω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁתָה ;] to drink: absol., Luk.2:19, Jhn.4:7, al.; with accusative of thing(s), Mat.6:26, 31 Mrk.14:25, al.; of habitual use, Luk.1:15, Rom.14:21, al.; by meton., τὸ ποτήριον, 1Co.10:21,…

piotes.notes: πιότης, -ητος, ἡ (πίων, fat), [in LXX chiefly for דֶּשֶׁן ;] fatness: Rom.11:17.† (AS)

piprasko.notes: πιπράσκω [in LXX chiefly for מָכַר ni. ;] to sell: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.13:46 (on this pf., see Bl., § 59, 5), Act.2:45 4:34 5:4; with genitive (of price), Mat.26:9, Mrk.14:5, Jhn.12:5; with accusative of…

pipto.notes: πίπτω [in LXX chiefly for נָפַל ;] to fall; __1. of descent, to fall, fall down or from: before ἐπί, with accusative loc., Mat.10:29, al.; εἰς, Mat.15:14, al.; ἐς μέσῳ, with genitive, Luk.8:7; παρὰ τ. ὁδόν, Mat.13:4,…

pisidia.notes: Πισιδία, -ας, ι Pisidia, a region of Asia Minor: Act.14:24 (AS)

pisteuo.notes: πιστεύω, [in LXX chiefly for אמן hi. ;] __1. intrans., to have faith (in), to believe; in cl., with accusative, dative, in NT also with prep, (on the significance of the various constructions, see M, Pr., 67f.; Vau.…

pistikos.notes: πιστικός, -ή, -όν (πίστις), __1. having the gift of persuasion (Plat., Gorg., 455A). __2. __(a) of persons, faithful, trusty (Plut.); __(b) of things, trustworthy, genuine: νάρδος π., Mrk.14:3, Jhn.12:3. † (AS)

pistis.notes: πίστις, -εως, ἡ (πείθω), [in LXX chiefly for אֱמוּנָה ;] __1. in active sense, faith, belief, trust, confidence, in NT always of religious faith in God or Christ or spiritual things: Mat.8:10, Luk.5:20, Act.14:9,…

pistoo.notes: πιστόω, -ῶ (πιστός), [in LXX chiefly for אָמֵן ni. ;] to make trustworthy (Thuc., 3Ki.1:36), hence, to establish (1Ch.17:14). Pass. and mid., to be assured of: with accusative of thing(s), 2Ti.3:14.† (AS)

pistos.notes: πιστός, -ή, -όν (πείθω), [in LXX chiefly for אָמַן ;] __I. Pass., to be trusted or believed; __1. of persons, trusty, faithful : Mat.24:45 25:21, 23 Luk.12:42, Act.16:15, 1Co.4:2 4:17 7:25, Eph.1:1 6:21, Col.1:2, 7…

pithanologia.notes: πιθανολογία, -ας, ἡ (πιθανός, persuasive, plausible), in cl. (Plat), the use of probable arguments, as opposite to demonstration (ἀπόδειξις); hence, persuasive speech: Col.2:4 (see ICC, in l).† (AS)

planao.notes: πλανάω, -ῶ (πλάνη), [in LXX chiefly for תָּעָה ;] to cause to wander, lead astray. Pass., to go astray, wander: Mat.18:12-13 Heb.11:38, 1Pe.2:25 (cf. Isa.53:6). Metaphorical, to lead astray, deceive: with accusative…

plane.notes: πλάνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Pro.14:8 (מִרְמָה), Wis.1:12, al ;] a wandering. Metaphorical, a going astray, an error (in NT always with respect to morals or religion): Mat.27:64, Rom.1:27, Eph.4:14, 1Th.2:3, 2Th.2:11,…

planetes.notes: πλανήτης, -ου, ὁ (πλανάω), [in LXX: Hos.9:17 (נָדַד) * ;] = πλάνης, a wanderer: ἀστέρες π. (cl. planets), wandering stars, Ju 13 (WH, mg., -τες).† (AS)

planos.notes: πλάνος, -ον [in LXX: Job.19:4 (מְשׁוּגָה), Jer.23:32 * ;] __1. wandering. __2. leading astray, deceiving: πνεύματα π., 1Ti.4:1. As subst., ὁ π., a deceiver, impostor: Mat.27:63, 2Co.6:8 6:1-18 2Jn.1:7.† (AS)

plasma.notes: πλάσμα, -τος, τό (πλάσσω), [in LXX chiefly for יֵצֶר ;] that which is moulded or formed : Rom.9:20 (LXX).† (AS)

plasso.notes: πλάσσω [in LXX chiefly for יָצַר ;] to form, mould: Rom.9:26 (LXX), 1Ti.2:13.† (AS)

plastos.notes: πλαστός, -ή, -όν (πλάσσω), __1. formed, moulded (Hes., Plat., al.). __2. Metaphorical, made up, fabricated, feigned (Hdt., Xen., al.): 2Pe.2:3.† (AS)

plateia.notes: πλατεία, -ας, ἡ, see: πλατύς. πλατύς, -εῖα, -ύ [in LXX for רָחָב ;] broad: Mat.7:13. As subst., ἡ π. (sc. ὁδός), [in LXX chiefly for רָחָב ;] a street: Mat.6:5 12:19" (LXX), Mrk.6:56, WH, mg., Luk.10:10 13:26 14:21,…

platos.notes: πλάτος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for רֹחַב ;] breadth: Eph.3:18, Rev.21:16; τ. π. τῆς γῆς (Hab.1:6, מֶרְחָב), Rev.20:9.† (AS)

platuno.notes: πλατύνω (πλατύς), [in LXX chiefly for רָחַב hi. ;] to make broad, enlarge, extend: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.23:5. Metaphorical (cf. Psa.119:32, al.), pass., ἡ καρδία, 2Co.6:11; ὑμεῖς, 2Co.6:13.† (AS)

platus.notes: πλατύς, -εῖα, -ύ [in LXX for רָחָב ;] broad: Mat.7:13. As subst., ἡ π. (sc. ὁδός), [in LXX chiefly for רָחָב ;] a street: Mat.6:5 12:19" (LXX), Mrk.6:56, WH, mg., Luk.10:10 13:26 14:21, Act.5:15, Rev.11:8 21:21…

plax.notes: πλάξ, -ακός, ἡ [in LXX for לוּחַ ;] anything flat and broad. __1. a plain (poët.). __2. In late writers (Luc., al.), a flat stone, a tablet: 2Co.3:3, Heb.9:4.† (AS)

plege.notes: πληγή, ῆς, ἡ (πλήσσω), [in LXX chiefly for מַכָּה, also for מַגֵּפָה etc. ;] __1. a blow, stripe, wound: pl., Luk.10:30 12:48, Act.16:23, 33, 2Co.6:5 11:23; ἡ π. τ. θανάτου (RV, death-stroke), Rev.13:3, 12; τ.…

plegma.notes: πλέγμα, -τος, τό (πλέκω), [in Aq., Th.: Isa.28:5 * ;] what is woven or twisted (as basket-work, nets, etc.); a braiding (sc. τριχῶν, cf. 1Pe.3:3) : pl., 1Ti.2:9.† (AS)

pleionpleion.notes: πλέον, see: πολύς. πολύς, πολλή, πολύ, [in LXX chiefly for רַב and cognate forms ;] __1. as adj., much, many, great, of number, space, degree, value, time, etc.: ἀριθμός, Act.11:21; ὄχλος, Mrk.5:24; θερισμός,…

pleistos.notes: πλεῖστος, πλείων, see: πολύς. πολύς, πολλή, πολύ, [in LXX chiefly for רַב and cognate forms ;] __1. as adj., much, many, great, of number, space, degree, value, time, etc.: ἀριθμός, Act.11:21; ὄχλος, Mrk.5:24;…

pleko.notes: πλέκω [in LXX: Exo.28:14 (עֲבֹת), Isa.28:5 (צְפִירָה)* ;] to plait, twist, weave: Mat.27:29, Mrk.15:17, Jhn.19:2.† (AS)

plektes.notes: πλήκτης, -ου, ὁ (πλήσσω), [in Sm.: Psa.35:15 * ;] a striker, brawler: 1Ti.3:3, Tit.1:7 (Arist., Plut., al.).† (AS)

plemmura.notes: πλήμμυρα, -ης (for Att.. -ας, see Bl., § 7, 1) (πλήθω), [in LXX: π. γένεσθαι, Job.40:18 (23) (נָהָר עָשַׁק) * ;] a flood (of sea or river) : Luk.6:48.† (AS)

pleo.notes: πλέω [in LXX, before εἰς, Jon.1.3 (בּוֹא), I Est.4:23, Isa.42:10; before ἐπί, 4Ma.7:3; with accusative (poët.), Sir.43:24, 1Ma.13:29 (cf. Act.27:2, Rec.) * ;] to sail: Luk.8:23, Act.27:24; before εἰς, Act.21:3 27:2,…

pleonazo.notes: πλεονάζω (πλέον), [in LXX for עֲדַף, רָבָה, etc. ;] __I. Intrans. __1. Of persons; __(a) to abound in (Arist.); __(b) to superabound: 2Co.8:15 (LXX). __2. Of things, to abound, superabound: Rom.5:20 6:1, 2Co.4:15,…

pleonekteo.notes: πλεονεκτέω, -ῶ (πλεονέκτης, which see), [in LXX: Hab.2:9, Eze.22:27 (בָּצַע), Jdg.4:11 B* ;] __1. intrans., to have more, to have an advantage (cl., with genitive of person(s)). __2. Trans., in late writers (see M,…

pleonektes.notes: πλεονέκτης, -ου, ὁ (πλέον, ἔχω), [in LXX: Sir.14:9 * ;] = ὁ θέλων πλέον ἔχειν (see MM, xx), one desirous of having more, covetous: 1Co.5:10-11 6:10 Eph.5:5.† (AS)

pleonexia.notes: πλεονεξία, -ας, ἡ (πλεονέκτης), [in LXX for בֶּצַע ;] the character and conduct of a πλεονέκτης. __1. advantage. __2. desire for advantage, grasping, aggression, cupidity, covetousness: Luk.12:15, Rom.1:29, 2Co.9:5,…

pleres.notes: πλήρης, -ες [in LXX chiefly for מָלֵא ;] __1. full, filled: Mat.14:20 15:37; with genitive of thing(s), Mrk.8:19; λέπρας, Luk.5:12; metaphorically, of the soul: πνεύματος ἀγίου, Luk.4:1, Act.6:3 7:55 11:24; πίστεως,…

pleroma.notes: πλήρωμα, -τος, τό (πληρόω), [in LXX for מְלֹא ;] the result of the action involved in πληρόω (Lft., Col., 255 if.; AR, Eph., 255 ff.), hence, __1. in passive sense, that which has been completed, complement,…

pleroo.notes: πληρόω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for מָלֵא ;] __1. to fill, make full, fill to the full, with accusative; __(a) of things : pass. (σαψήνη, φάραγξ), Mat.13:48, Luk.3:5" (LXX) ; figuratively, Mat.23:32; but chiefly of…

plerophoreo.notes: πληροφορέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Ecc.8:11 (מָלֵא) * ;] __1. to bring in full measure, hence, to fulfil, accomplish: Luk.1:1, 2Ti.4:5 4:17. __2. to persuade, assure or satisfy fully (so in π.; see Deiss., LAE, 82 f.; M, Th.,…

plerophoria.notes: πληροφορία, -ας, ἡ (πληροφορέω), full assurance, confidence: 1Th.1:5; τ. συνέσεως, Col.2:2; τ. ἐλπίδος, Heb.6:11; πίστεως, Heb.10:22 (cf. Lft. on Col., l.with; M, Th., 9).† (AS)

plesion.notes: πλησίος, -α, -ον (πέλας, near), near, close by, neighbouring. As adv., πλησίον = πέλας, near: with genitive, Jhn.4:5; with art., ὁ π., one's neigh­bour [in LXX chiefly for רֵעַ ]: Luk.10:29, 36, Act.7:27, Rom.13:10…

plesmone.notes: πλησμονή, -ῆς, ἡ (πίμπλημι), [in LXX chiefly for שָׂבֵעַ and cognate forms ;] a filling up, satiety: πρὸς π. σαρκός (RV, against the indulgence of the flesh; but ICC, in l, for the full satisfaction of the flesh, op.…

plesso.notes: πλήσσω [in LXX chiefly for נָכָה hi., ho. ;] to strike, smite: pass., Rev.8:12 (cf. ἐπι, πλήσσω).† (AS)

plethopimplemi.notes: πίμπλημι [in LXX chiefly for מָלֵא, also for שָׂבַע ;] trans. form in present and imperfect of πλήθω (intrans. in these tenses), which supplies the other tense forms; to fill: with accusative, Luk.5:7; with…

plethos.notes: πλῆθος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for רֹב, also for הָמוֹן, etc. ;] __1. a great number, a multitude; __(a) of things : ἰχθύων, Luk.5:6, Jhn.21:6; φρυγάνων, Act.28:3; ἁμαρτιίῶν, Jas.5:20, 1Pe.4:8; τ. πλήθει, in…

plethuno.notes: πληθύνω (causal of πληθύω, to be full, πληθύς, Ion. for πλῆθος), [in LXX chiefly for רָבָה hi. ;] __1. trans., to increase, multiply: 2Co.9:10 6:14" (LXX) ; pass., to be increased, to multiply: Mat.24:12, Act.6:7…

pleura.notes: πλευρά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX (frequently pl. as in Hom.) chiefly for צֵלָע ;] the side: Mat.27:49 ([[WH]] R, mg.), Jhn.19:34 20:20, 25 20:27 Act.12:7.† (AS)

ploiarion.notes: πλοιάριον, -ου, τό (dimin. of πλοῖον), a boat: Mrk.3:9, Luk.5:2 (πλοῖα, WH, txt., R), Jhn.6:22-23 (πλοῖα, WH) Jhn.6:24 21:8.† (AS)

ploion.notes: πλοῖον, -ον, τό (πλέω), [in LXX chiefly for אֳנִיָּה ;] a boat, also (= obsolete ναῦς) a ship: Mat.4:21-22, and frequently in Gosp. and Ac, Jas.3:4, Rev.8:9 18:19. (AS)

ploos.notes: πλόος, πλούς genitive, -όου, -oῦ (and in late writers also πλοός, like νοός from νοῦς), ὁ (πλέω), [in LXX: Wis.14:1 * ;] a voyage: Act.21:7 27:9-10.† (AS)

plousios.notes: πλούσιος, -α, -ον (πλοῦτος), [in LXX chiefly for עָשִׁיר ;] rich, wealthy: Mat.27:57, Luk.12:16 14:12 16:1, 19 18:23 19:2. Substantively, ὁ π., Luk.16:21-22, Jas.1:10-11; oἱ π., Luk.6:24 21:1, 1Ti.6:17, Jas.2:6 5:1,…

plouteo.notes: πλουτέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for עָשַׁר hi. ;] to be rich, aor., to become rich: Luk.1:53, 1Ti.6:9; before ἀπό (Sir.11:18), Rev.18:15; ἐκ, Rev.18:3, 19; ἐν (= cl., with genitive, dative, accusative, cf. πλούσιος),…

ploutizo.notes: πλουτίζω (πλοῦτος), [in LXX chiefly for עָשַׁר hi. ;] to make rich, enrich: with accusative of person(s), pass., 2Co.9:11. Metaphorical, of spiritual riches, with accusative: of person(s), 2Co.6:10; before ἐν, pass.,…

ploutos.notes: πλοῦτος, -ου, ὁ and (in 2Co.8:2, Eph.1:17 2:7 3:8, 16, Php.4:19, Col.1:27 2:2; see WH, App., 158; M, Pr., 60) τό, [in LXX chiefly for עֹשֶׁר ;] riches, wealth: of external possessions, Mat.13:22, Mrk.4:19, Luk.8:14,…

pluno.notes: πλύνω [in LXX chiefly for כָּבַס pi. ;] to wash (inanimate objects, esp. clothing): τ. δίκτυα, Luk.5:2. Fig., τ. στολάς, Rev.7:14 22:14 (cf. Psa.51:4, 9 and see: ἀπο-πλύνω).† SYN.: see: λούω (AS)

pneo.notes: πνέω [in LXX: Psa.147:7 (18) (נָשַׁב hi.), Isa.40:24 (נָשַׁף), Sir.43:16, 20, Ep. Je 61, 2Ma.9:7 * ;] to breathe, blow: of the wind, Mat.7:25, 27, Luk.12:55, Jhn.3:8 6:18, Rev.7:1; ηῇ πνεούσῃ (sc. αὔρᾳ), Act.27:40…

pneuma.notes: πνεῦμα, -τος, τό, [in LXX chiefly and very frequently for רוּחַ ;] __1. of air in motion; __(a) wind: Jhn.3:8; pl., Heb.1:7 (LXX); __(b) breath: π. ζωῆς, Rev.11:11; π. τοῦ στόματος, figuratively, 2Th.2:8 (cf. Ps 32…

pneumatikos.notes: πνευματικός, -ή, -όν (πνεῦμα), __1. of or caused by the wind, air or breath (Arist., al.). __2. spiritual (opposite to σωματικός, Plut.); __(a) of created beings : τὰ π. (RV, the spiritual hosts), Eph.6:12; of that…

pnigo.notes: πνίγω [in LXX: 1Ki.16:14-15 (בָּעַת pi.) * ;] to choke: with accusative, Mat.13:7 (WH, mg.); imperfect (coniative), Mat.18:28; of drowning, pass., Mrk.5:13 (cf. ἀπο-, ἐπι-, συν-πνίγω).† (AS)

pnoe.notes: πνοή, -ῆς, ή (πνέω), [in LXX chiefly for נְשָׁמָה ;] __1. a blowing, blast, wind: Act.2:2. __2. a breathing, breath: Act.17:25 (Gen.2:7, al.).† (AS)

poiema.notes: ποίημα, -τος, τό (ποιέω), [in LXX chiefly for מַעֲשֶׂה (frequently in Ec) ;] that which is made or done, a work: Rom.1:20, Eph.2:10.† (AS)

poiesis.notes: ποίησις, -εως, ἡ (ποιέω) [in LXX chiefly for מַעֲשֶׂה and cognate forms ;] __1. a making (Hdt., Thuc., al.). __2. a doing (Sir.19:18 51:19): Jas.1:25.† (AS)

poietes.notes: ποιητής, -οῦ ὁ (ποιέω), [in LXX: 1Ma.2:67 * ;] in cl., __1. a maker, author. __2. Esp., a poet: Act.17:28. Later, __3. a doer: τ. νόμου, Rom.2:13, Jas.4:11 (1Mac, l.with); ἔργου, Jas.1:25; λόγου, ib. Jas.1:22-23.† (AS)

poikilos.notes: ποικίλος, -η, -ον [in LXX for נָקֹד, רִקְמָה, etc. ;] many-coloured, variegated. Metaphorical, various, manifold: Mat.4:24, Mrk.1:34, Luk.4:40, 2Ti.3:6, Tit.3:3, Heb.2:4 13:9, Jas.1:2, 1Pe.1:6 4:10.† (AS)

poimaino.notes: ποιμαίνω (ποιμήν), [in LXX chiefly for רָעָה ;] to act as shepherd, tend flocks: Luk.17:7; with accusative, ποιμνην, 1Co.9:7. Metaphorical, to tend, shepherd, govern: with accusative, Mat.2:6 (LXX), Jhn.21:16,…

poimen.notes: ποιμήν, -ίνος, ὁ [in LXX for רָעָה ;] a shepherd: Mat.9:36 25:32 26:31" (LXX), Mrk.6:34 14:27, Luk.2:8, 15 2:18, 20 Jhn.10:2, 12 Metaphorical, of Christ; Jhn.10:11, 14 10:16 Heb.13:20, 1Pe.2:25; of Christian pastors,…

poimne.notes: ποίμνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Gen.32:16 (17) (עֵדֶר), Zec.13:7 A * ;] a flock, prop., of sheep: Mat.26:31, Luk.2:8, 1Co.9:7. Metaphorical, of Christ's followers, Jhn.10:16.† (AS)

poimnion.notes: ποίμνιον, -ου, τό, = ποίμνη, which see [in LXX chiefly for צֹאן, also for עֵדֶר, etc. ;] a flock, prop., of sheep. Metaphorical, of Christians: Luk.12:32, Act.20:23, 28, 1Pe.5:3; τ. θεοῦ, ib. 1Pe.5:2.† (AS)

poios.notes: ποῖος, -α, -ον interrog. pronom. adj. (corresponding to the demonstr. τοῖος and the relat. οἷος), [in LXX chiefly for זֶה אֵי־ ;] of what quality or sort: absol., n. pl., Luk.24:19; with subst.; __(a) prop., in…

polemeo.notes: πολεμέω, -ῶ (πόλεμος), [in LXX chiefly for לָחַם ni. ;] to make war, fight: Rev.12:7 b Rev.19:11; before μετά (2Ki.21:15 and see M, Pr., 106, 247), Rev.2:16 12:7 a Rev.13:4 17:14; hyperb., of private quarrels,…

polemos.notes: πόλεμος, -ον, ό [in LXX chiefly for מִלְחָמָה ;] __1. war: Mat.24:6, Mrk.13:7, Luk.14:31 21:9, Heb.11:34; π. ποιεῖν, before μετά, with genitive (cf. πολεμέω), Rev.11:7 12:17 13:7 19:19. __2. = μάχη, a fight, battle:…

poleo.notes: πωλέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for מָכַר ;] __1. to exchange or barter. __2. to sell: Luk.17:28, Rev.13:17; οἱ πωλοῦντες, Mat.21:12 25:9, Mrk.11:15, Luk.19:45; with accusative of thing(s), Mat.13:44 19:21 21:12, Mrk.10:21…

polis.notes: πόλις, -εως, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly and very frequently for עִיר ;] a city: Mat.2:23, Mrk.1:45, Luk.4:29, Jhn.4:8, al. mult.; opposite to κῶμαι (κ. καὶ ἀγροί), Mat.9:35 10:11, Mrk.6:56, Luk.8:1 13:22; with nom. propr. in…

politarches.notes: πολιτάρχης, -ου, ὁ = πολίαρχος (Pind., Eur.), the ruler of a city, a politarch: Act.17:6, 8 (see MM, xx).† (AS)

politeia.notes: πολιτεία, -ας, ἡ (πολιτεύω), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:11 2Mac 6:23 2Mac 8:17 2Mac 13:14, 3Ma.3:21 3Mac 3:23, 4Ma.3:20 4Mac 8:7 4Mac 17:9 * ;] __1. citizenship: Act.22:28 (3 Mac, ll. with, Hdt., Xen., a1.). __2. government,…

polites.notes: πολίτης, -ου, ὁ (πόλις), [in LXX chiefly for רֵעַ ;] __1. a citizen: with genitive loc., Luk.15:15, Act.21:39. __2. a fellow-citizen: with genitive of person(s), Luk.19:14, Heb.8:11" (LXX) .† (AS)

politeuma.notes: πολίτευμα, -τος, τό (πολιτεύω), [in LXX: 2Ma.12:7 * ;] __1. an act of administration. __2. a form of government. __3. = πολιτεία, __(a) citizenship : Php.3:20 (R, txt.); __(b) community, commonwealth : Phi, l.with…

politeuo.notes: πολιτεύω more frequently as depon., -ομαι, and so in LXX and NT (πολίτης), [in LXX: Est.8:13, 2Ma.6:1 2Mac 11:25, 3Ma.3:4 3Mac 3:1-30; 4Ma.4:1-26 * ;] to be a citizen, live as a citizen: metaphorically, of conduct as…

pollaplasion.notes: πολλαπλασίων, -ον (πόλυς), = πολλαπλάσιος, many times more: Mat.19:29, Luk.18:30.† ἑπταπλασίων, -ον, genitive, -ονος, [in LXX for שִׁבְעָתַיִם, Psa.79:12, al. ;] sevenfold: Luk.18:30 (WH, mg., for πολλαπλ- WH, txt.,…

polos.notes: πῶλος, -ου, ὁ (in cl. also ) [in LXX chiefly for עַיִר ;] a foal, colt, prop., of a horse, then the young of other animals; in NT of the colt of an ass: Mat.21:2, 5" (LXX), Mat.21:7, Mrk.11:2, 4-5 11:7, Luk.19:30, 33…

polulogia.notes: πολυλογία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Pro.10:19 (דָּבָר רֹב)* ;] much speaking, loquacity: Mat.6:7.† (AS)

polumeros.notes: πολυμερῶς adv. (πολύς, μέρος), in many parts or portions: Heb.1:1 (Plut., al.).† (AS)

polupoikilos.notes: πολυ-ποίκιλος, -ον __1. much variegated, of greatly differing colours (Eur.). __2. manifold: Eph.3:10.† (AS)

polus.notes: πολύς, πολλή, πολύ, [in LXX chiefly for רַב and cognate forms ;] __1. as adj., much, many, great, of number, space, degree, value, time, etc.: ἀριθμός, Act.11:21; ὄχλος, Mrk.5:24; θερισμός, Mat.9:37; χόρτος,…

poluteles.notes: πολυτελής, -ές (πολύς, τέλος) [in LXX for יָקָר, etc. ;] very costly, very precious, of great value: Mrk.14:3, 1Ti.2:9; metaphorically, 1Pe.3:4.† (AS)

polutimos.notes: πολύτιμος, -ον (πολύς, τιμή) __1. much revered (Menand.). __2. very costly, very precious: Mat.13:46 26:7 (βαρύτιμος, WH), Jhn.12:3; comparat., 1Pe.1:7.† (AS)

polutropos.notes: πολυτρόπως adv. (πολύτροπος, 1. much turning. 2. manifold ), [in LXX: 4Ma.3:21 A * ;] in many ways or manners: Heb.1:1 (Philo).† (AS)

poma.notes: πόμα, -τος, τό (πίνω), late form of Att.. πῶμα, [in LXX.: Psa.102:9 (שִׁקּוּי), al. 4 * ;] drink: 1Co.10:4, Heb.9:10.† (AS)

poneria.notes: πονηρία, -ας, ἡ (πονηρός), [in LXX chiefly for רַע ;] iniquity, wickedness: Mat.22:18, Luk.11:39, Rom.1:29, Eph.6:12; pl. (see Bl., § 32, 6; WM, 220; Swete, Mk., 153), Mrk.7:22, Act.3:26; κακία καὶ π., 1Co.5:8.†…

poneros.notes: πονηρός, -ά, -όν (πονέω, to toil), [in LXX chiefly for רַע ;] __1. __(a) of persons, oppressed by toils (Hes.); __(b) of things, toilsome, painful (καιρός, Sir.51:12): Eph.5:16 6:13 Rev.16:2. __2. bad, worthless;…

ponos.notes: πόνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for עָמָל, etc. ;] __1. labour, toil: Col.4:13 __2. The consequence of toil, distress, suffering, pain (Xen., al., LXX): Rev.16:10-11 21:4.† SYN.: see: κόπος (AS)

pontikos.notes: from Pontus, Pontic , (Iliad by Homer) δένδρεον, probably, the bird-cherry , (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)

pontios.notes: Πόντιος, -ου, ὁ Pontius, the prænomen of Pilate (see: Πειλᾶτος), Mat.27:2 (Rec., WH, mg.), Luk.3:1, Act.4:27, 1Ti.6:13.† (AS)

pontos.notes: Πόντος, -ου, ὁ Pontus, a region of Asia Minor, bordering on the πόντος Εὔξεινος: Act.2:9, 1Pe.1:1.† (AS)

popote.notes: πώ-ποτε adv., ever yet: Luk.19:30, Jhn.1:18 5:37 6:35 8:33, 1Jn.4:12.† (AS)

poreia.notes: πορεία, -ας, ἡ (πορεύω), [in LXX chiefly for הֲלִיכָה and cogn. forms ;] __1. a journey: Luk.13:22. __2. a going: metaphorically ("the rich man perishes while he is still on the move," Hort., in l), Jas.1:11.† (AS)

poreuo.notes: πορεύω, (πόρος, a ford, a passage), [in LXX chiefly for הלךְ ;] in cl. (the act. becomes obsolete in late Gk.; see M, Pr., 162), to cause to go over, carry, convey. Mid. (always in LXX and NT), ־ομαι, to go, proceed,…

porismos.notes: πορισμός, -ου, ὁ (πορίζω, to procure), [in LXX: Wis.13:19 14:2 * ;] __1. a providing. __2. a means of gain: 1Ti.6:5-6 (Polyb., al.).† (AS)

porkios.notes: Πόρκιος, -ου, ὁ Porcius, prænomen of Porcius Festus (see: Φῆστος): Act.24:27.† (AS)

porne.notes: πόρνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for זָנָה ;] a prostitute, harlot: Mat.21:31-32, Luk.15:30, 1Co.6:15-16 Heb.11:31, Jas.2:25. Metaphorical (see Swete, Ap., 180 f.), of Babylon (i.e. Rome): Rev.17:1, 5 17:15-16 19:2.† (AS)

porneia.notes: πορνεία, -ας, ἡ (πορνεύω), [in LXX for תַּזְנוּת and cogn. forms ;] fornication: Act.15:20, 29 21:25, 1Co.5:1 6:13 6:18, 2Co.12:21, Gal.5:19, Eph.5:3, Col.3:5, 1Th.4:3 (Lft., Notes, 53), Rev.9:21; pl. (see WM, 220;…

porneuo.notes: πορνεύω [in LXX for זָנָה ;] __1. to prostitute the body for hire. __2. to commit fornication: Mrk.10:19 (WH, mg), 1Co.6:18 10:8, Rev.2:14, 20. Metaphorical (as in LXX: 1Ch.5:25, Jer.3:6, Hos.9:1, al.), of idolatry…

pornos.notes: πόρνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Sir.23:16-18 * ;] __1. a male prostitute (Xen., al.). __2. a fornicator: 1Co.5:9-11 6:9, Eph.5:5, 1Ti.1:10, Heb.12:15 13:4, Rev.21:8 22:15.† (AS)

poroo.notes: πωρόω, -ῶ (πῶρος, 1. a stone. 2. a callus) [in LXX: Job.17:7 B(כָּהָה), Pro.10:20 A * ;] to petrify, harden, form a callus. Metaphorical, π. τ. καρδίαν, Jhn.12:40. Pass., Rom.11:7; τ. νοήματα, 2Co.3:14; ἡ καρδία,…

porosis.notes: πώρωσις, -εως, ἡ (πωρόω), a covering with a callus, a hardening: metaphorically, Mrk.3:6, Rom.11:2, Eph.4:18.† (AS)

porphura.notes: πορφύρα, -ας, ἡ [in LXX for אַרְגָּמָן, אַרְגְּוָן ;] __1. the purple fish (cf. 1Ma.4:23). __2. purple dye. __3. Later, = πορφυρίς, a purple gar­ment: Mrk.15:17, 20, Luk.16:19, Rev.18:12.† (AS)

porphureos.notes: πορφύρεος, -α, -ον contr., -οῦς, -ᾶ, -οῦν (ποπφύρα), [in LXX for אַרְגָּמָן ;] purple: Jhn.19:2, 5; as subst., πορφυροῦν (sc. ἱμάτιον), Rev.17:4 18:6.† (AS)

porphuropolis.notes: πορφυρόπωλις, -ιδος, ἡ a seller of purple fabrics: Act.16:14.† (AS)

porro.notes: πόρρω adv. (in older Attic, πρόσω), [in LXX for רָחוֹק, etc. ;] far off: Mat.15:8, Mrk.7:6" (LXX), Luk.14:32; comparat., πορρώτερον (-ρω, T), Luk.24:28.† (AS)

porrothen.notes: πόρρωθεν adv. (τόρρω, which see), [in LXX for רָחוֹק and cogn. forms ;] from afar: Luk.17:12, Heb.11:13.† (AS)

portheo.notes: πορθέω collat. form (in cl. chiefly poët.) of πέρθω, [in LXX: 4Ma.4:23 4Mac 11:4 * ;] to destroy, ravage: Act.9:21, Gal.1:13, 23.† (AS)

posakis.notes: ποσάκις interrog. num. adv., how often: Mat.18:21 23:37, Luk.13:34.† (AS)

posis.notes: πόσις, -εως, ἡ (πίνω), [in LXX: Da, LXX TH Dan.1:10 (מִשְׁתֶּה) * ;] __1. prop., drinking: Rom.14:17, Col.2:16 (see Lft.; ICC, in l). __2. = πόμα, drink: Jhn.6:55 (cf. βρῶσις).† (AS)

potamophoretos.notes: ποταμο-φόρητος, -ον carried away by a stream: Rev.12:15 (for two exx. in π., see MM, xxi).† (AS)

potamos.notes: ποταμός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for נָהָר, יְאֹר ;] a river, stream, torrent: Mat.3:5 7:25, 27, Mrk.1:1-45 :, Luk.6:48-49, Act.16:13, 2Co.11:26, Rev.8:10 9:14 12:15-16 16:4, 12 21:1-2. Fig., pl., π. ὕδατος ζῶντος,…

potapos.notes: ποταπός (late form of cl. ποδ-), -ή, -όν, [in LXX: Dan LXX Su 1:54* ;] __1. (= ποδαπός) from what country? __2. In late writers, = ποῖος, of what sort?: Mat.8:27, Mrk.13:1, Luk.1:29 7:39, 2Pe.3:11, 1Jn.3:1.† (AS)

poterion.notes: ποτήριον, -ου, τό dimin. of ποτήρ (πίνω), [in LXX chiefly for כּוֹס:] a wine cup: Mat.23:25-26 26:27, Mrk.7:4 14:23, Luk.11:39 22:17, 20 a (WH, R, mg., cm.), 1Co.11:25, Rev.17:4; πίνειν ἐκ τοῦ π., 1Co.11:28; with…

poteros.notes: πότερος, -α, -ον which of two. Neut., adverbially, πότερον, whether: Jhn.7:17.† (AS)

pothen.notes: πόθεν adv., whence; __(a) of place: Mat.15:33, Luk.13:25, 27, Jhn.3:8 6:5 8:14 9:29-30 19:9, Rev.7:13; metaphorically, of condition, Rev.2:5; __(b) of origin: Mat.13:27, 54 13:56 21:25, Mrk.6:2, Luk.20:7, Jhn.2:9,…

potioloi.notes: Ποτίολοι, -ων, οἱ Puteoli (mod. Pozzuoli), a city on the Bay of Naples: Act.28:13.† (AS)

potizo.notes: ποτίζω (πότος), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁקָה hi. ;] to give to drink: with accusative of person(s), Mat.25:35, 37 25:42 27:48, Mrk.15:36, Luk.13:15, Rom.12:20; with dupl. accusative, Mat.10:42, Mrk.9:41; fig., γάλα,…

potos.notes: πότος, -ου, ὁ (πίνω), [in LXX chiefly for מִשְׁתֶּה ;] a drinking bout, carousal: 1Pe.4:3.† SYN.: see: κραιπάλη (AS)

pou.notes: πού enclitic particle, __1. anywhere, somewhere: Heb.2:6 4:4. __2. in some degree, perhaps, about: Act.27:29 (T, μήπου), Rom.4:19; δή που (T, δήπου), surely: Heb.2:16.† (AS)

poudes.notes: Πούδης (in π., genitive -εντος, see Zorell, see word), ὁ (Latin) Pudens: 2Ti.4:21.† (AS)

pous.notes: πούς, ποδός, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for רֶגֶל ;] a foot, both of men and beasts: Mat.4:6 (LXX), Mrk.9:45, Luk.1:79, Jhn.11:44, Act.7:5, al.; ὑπο τοὺς π., Rom.16:20, 1Co.15:25 15:27 Eph.1:22, Heb.2:8; ὑποκάτω τῶν π.,…

pragma.notes: πρᾶγμα, -τος, τύ (πράσσω), [in LXX chiefly for דָּבָר ;] __1. that which has been done, a deed, act: Luk.1:1, Act.5:4, 2Co.7:11, Heb.6:18. __2. That which is being done (like Lat. res), hence, a thing, matter,…

pragmateia.notes: πραγματία (Rec. -εία, as in cl.), -ας, ἡ (πραγματεύομαι) [in LXX for חֲשַׁל, etc. ;] __1. careful application, hard work. __2. business, occupation: pl., 2Ti.2:4.† (AS)

pragmateuomai.notes: πραγματεύομαι (πρᾶγμα), [in LXX: 3Ki.10:22 B, 3Ki.9:19 A (חָשַׁק), Dan LXX 8:27 (מְלָאכָה ־אֵת עָשָׂה) * ;] __1. to busy oneself. __2. to be engaged in business, esp. to trade: Luk.19:13 (cf. δια-πραγματεύομαι).† (AS)

praitorion.notes: πραιτώριον, -ου, τό (Lat. prætorium) __1. headquarters in a Roman camp, __2. The palace or official residence of the Governor of a province: Mat.27:27, Mrk.15:16 (see Swete, in l), Jhn.18:28, 33 19:9; τ. π. τ.…

praktor.notes: πράκτωρ, -ορος, ὁ (πράσσω), [in LXX: Isa.3:12 (נָגַשׁ) * ;] __1. (poët.) one who does or accomplishes. __2. In Athens, one who exacts payment, a collector; hence, generally (frequently in π., see Deiss., BS, 154), a…

prasia.notes: πρασιά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX: Sir.24:31 * ;] a garden-bed; metaphorically, of ranks or orderly groups of persons: Mrk.6:40.† (AS)

prasso.notes: πράσσω (Att.. -ττω, and so Act.17:7 Rec.; cf. M, Pr., 25, 45), [in LXX chiefly for עָשָׂה, פָּעַל ;] = Lat agere, as ποιέω (which see) = facere, __1. to do, practise, be engaged in: Act.19:19, 36, 1Co.9:17; τ. ἴδια…

praus.notes: πραΰς, -εῖα, -ΰ, and πρᾷος (Or πρᾶος, ν. Bl., § 3, 3), -α, -ον (see LS, Thayer, see word), [in LXX (always -ΰς, exc. 2Ma.15:12, -ᾷος, see Thackeray, Gr., 180 f.) for עָנָו, עָנִי ;] gentle, meek: Mat.5:5 11:29 21:5"…

prautes.notes: πραΰτης (Rec. -ότης, exc. Ja, 1Pe, ll. with, where πρᾳύτης), -ητος, ἡ, late form of πραότης, [in LXX: Psa.45:4 90:10 132:1 (עַנְוָה, עֱנוּת, Est.5:1, Sir.6:1-37 * ;] gentleness, meekness: 1Co.4:21, 2Co.10:1, Gal.5:23…

praxis.notes: πρᾶξις, -εως, ἡ (πράσσω), [in LXX: 2Ch.13:22 27:7 28:26 (דֶּרֶךְ), Job.24:5 A (פֹּעַל), Pro.13:13, Wis.9:11, al. ;] __1. a doing, deed, act: Mat.16:27; pl., π. ἀποσπτόλων, Ac, title; in late writers especially of…

prenes.notes: πρηνής, -ές (in Att.. also πρανής), [in LXX: Wis.4:19, 3Ma.5:43 3Mac 5:50 3Mac 6:23 * ;] headlong, prone: Act.1:18.† (AS)

prepo.notes: πρέπω [in LXX: Psa.93:5 (נָאָה pi.), etc. ;] __1. to be clearly seen. __2. to resemble. __3. to be fitting or becoming, to suit: with dative, Heb.7:26, 1Ti.2:10, Tit.2:1; impers., with dative, Eph.5:3; id. before…

presbeia.notes: πρεσβεία, -ας, ἡ (πρεσβεύω), [in LXX: 2Ma.4:11 * ;] __1. age, seniority. __2. rank, dignity. __3. an embassy; by meton., of the am­bassadors, embassy: Luk.14:32 19:14.† (AS)

presbeuo.notes: πρεσβεύω (πρέσβυς), __1. to be the elder, to take precedence. __2. to be an ambassador (see: πρεσβύτης): 2Co.5:20, Eph.6:20.† (AS)

presbuterion.notes: πρεσβυτέριον, -ου, τό (πρεσβύτερος) [in LXX: Da TH Su 1:50 A * ;] a body of elders, presbytery: of the Sanhedrin (cf. συνέδριον), Luk.22:66, Act.22:5; of Christian presbyters, 1Ti.4:14.† (AS)

presbuteros.notes: πρέσβυς, -εως, ὁ poët. form of πρεσβύτης (which see), [in LXX (= πρεσβευτής, an ambassador): Num.21:20 (21), al. (מֲלְאָךְ), Isa.13:8 57:9 (צִיר), 1Ma.9:70, al. ;] an old man. Compar., πρεσβύτερος, -α, -ον, [in LXX…

presbutes.notes: πρεσβύτης, -ου prose form of πρέσβυς, which see, [in LXX chiefly for זָקֵן, Gen.25:8, al.; also (= πρεσβευτής), 2Ch.32:31 Bl (לוּץ), 1Ma.14:22 S, al. (see Thackeray, Gr., 97) ;] __1. an old man: Luk.1:18, Tit.2:2,…

presbutis.notes: πρεσβῦτις, -ιδος, ἡ fem, of πρεσβύτης, [in LXX: 4Ma.16:14 * ;] an aged woman: Tit.2:3.† (AS)

priska.notes: Πρίσκα, -ας, ἡ (Lat.), and Πρίσκιλλα, -ης, ἡ, Prisca: Rom_16:3, 1Co_16:19, 2Ti_4:19; Priscilla: Act_18:2; Act_18:18; Act_18:26; the wife of Aquila, vs. Ἀκύλας.† (AS)

priskilla.notes: Πρίσκιλλα, see: Πρίσκα. Πρίσκα, -ας, ἡ (Lat.), and Πρίσκιλλα, -ης, ἡ, Prisca: Rom_16:3, 1Co_16:19, 2Ti_4:19; Priscilla: Act_18:2; Act_18:18; Act_18:26; the wife of Aquila, vs. Ἀκύλας.† (AS)

prizo.notes: πρίζω = πρίω, [in LXX: Amo.1:3, Da TH Su 1:59 * ;] to saw, saw asunder (= π. δίχα, Thuc., iv, 100): pass, Heb.11:37 (cf. δια-πρίω).† (AS)

proago.notes: προ-άγω [in LXX: 1Ki.17:16 (נָגַשׁ), Wis.19:11, al. ;] __1. prop. trans., to lead on, lead forth or forward: with accusative of person(s), Act.16:30 17:5; of bringing forth to trial, Act.12:6 (WH, txt., προσάγ-);…

proaireo.notes: προ-αιρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: for חָשַׁק, etc. ;] to bring forth or forward. Most frequently in mid., to take by choice, prefer, propose: 2Co.9:7.† (AS)

proaitiaomai.notes: προ-αιτιάομαι, -ῶμαι to accuse or charge beforehand: Rom.3:9 (not elsewhere) .† (AS)

proakouo.notes: προ-ακούω to hear beforehand: Col.1:5 (see Lft., in l).† (AS)

proaulion.notes: προ-αύλιον, -ου, τό (πρό, αὐλή), a porch, vestibule: Mrk.14:68.† (AS)

probaino.notes: προ-βαίνω [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא ;] to go forwards, go on, advance: Mat.4:21, Mrk.1:19. Metaphorical, of age (Gen.18:11, al., Lys., Diod., al.), ἐν τ. ἡμέραις, Luk.1:7, 18 2:36.† (AS)

proballo.notes: προ-βάλλω [in LXX for חוּד, מָרַק, etc. ;] __1. to throw before. __2. to put forward: with accusative, Act.19:33; of trees, to put forth, produce (sc. φύλλα), Luk.21:30.† (AS)

probatikos.notes: προβατικός, -ή, -όν (πρόβατον) [in LXX (π. πύλη): Neh.3:1, 32 12:39 (צֹאן)* ;] of sheep: ἡ π. (sc. πύλη, see supr.), the sheep-gate, Jhn.5:2.† (AS)

probaton.notes: πρόβατον, -ου, τό (προβαίνω), [in LXX chiefly for צֹאן, also for שֶׂה, more rarely for כֶּבֶשׂ (כֶּשֶׂב), רָחֵל ;] __1. in Hom., Hdt., cattle, esp. of small cattle, sheep and goats. __2. In NT, as in Attic writers…

probibazo.notes: προ-βιβάζω causal of προβαίνω, [in LXX: Exo.35:34 (יָרָה hi.), Deu.6:7 (שָׁנַן pi.) * ;] to lead forward, lead on; metaphorically, to induce, incite, urge: Mat.14:8.† (AS)

problepo.notes: προ-βλέπω [in LXX: Psa.37:13 (רָאָה)* ;] to foresee: mid., Heb.11:40 (see Bl., § 24, 55, 1).† (AS)

procheirizo.notes: προ-χειρίζω (πρόχειρος, at hand) [in LXX: Exo.4:13 (שָׁלַח), Jos.3:12 (לָקַח), Dan LXX 3:22, 2Ma.3:7 2Mac 8:9 2Mac 14:12 A * ;] __1. to put into the hand, deliver up: pass., Act.3:20. __2. More frequently as depon.,…

procheirotoneo.notes: προ-χειρο-τονέω, -ῶ (see χειροτονέω) to choose or appoint beforehand: Act.10:41 (Plat., al.).† (AS)

prodelos.notes: πρό-δηλος, -ον [in LXX: Jdth.8:29, 2Ma.3:17 2Mac 14:39 * ;] __1. evident beforehand. __2. clearly evident: 1Ti.5:24-25 Heb.7:14.† (AS)

prodidomi.notes: προ-δίδωμι [in LXX: Eze.16:34 A (נָתַן), 4Ma.4:1, al. ;] __1. to give before, give first: Rom.11:35 (Job.41:2 (11) LXX, al.). __2. to betray (4Mac, l.with).† (AS)

prodotes.notes: προ-δότης, -ου, ὁ (προδίδωμι) [in LXX: 2Ma.5:15 2Mac 10:13 2Mac 10:22, 3Ma.3:24 * ;] a betrayer, traitor: Luk.6:16, Act.7:52, 2Ti.3:4.† (AS)

prodromos.notes: πρό-δρομος, -ον (προτρέχω) [in LXX: Num.13:21 (20), Isa.28:4 (בִּכּוּר), Wis.12:8 * ;] running forward, going in advance. As subst., ὁ π., an advance guard, forerunner: Heb.6:20.† (AS)

proecho.notes: προ-έχω [in LXX: Job.27:6 A (חָזַק hi.) * ;] __1. Trans., to hold before; mid., to hold something before oneself (Hdt.), hence, metaphorically, to excuse oneself: Rom.3:9, R, mg. (but see Field, Notes, 152 f.; Lft.,…

proegeomai.notes: προ-ηγέσμαι [in LXX: Deu.20:9 (בְּרוֹשׁ), Pro.17:14 (פָּנִים), 2Ma.4:40, al. ;] __1. to go before as leader (in cl., with genitive, dative): Rom.12:10 (Chrys., Vg., al.; see ICC, in l). __2. In a sense not elsewhere…

proelpizo.notes: προ-ελπίζω to hope before: before ἐν, Eph.1:12.† (AS)

proenarchomai.notes: προ-ενάρχομαι to begin before: 2Co.8:6; with accusative, τὸ θέλειν, 2Co.8:10 (not elsewhere).† (AS)

proepangello.notes: προ-επ-αγγέλλω to announce before. Mid., to promise before: with accusative of thing(s), Rom.1:2, 2Co.9:5 (Dio. Cass.).† (AS)

proepo.notes: προ-εῖπον 2 aor. from unused Pres. (see: εἶπον, and pf., -είρηκα (3Ma.6:35 *), pass., -είρημαι (2Ma.2:32, 3Ma.1:26, al.), __1. to say before: Gal.1:9, 1Th.4:6 (on the form -αμεν, see WH, App., 164), Heb.4:7; before…

proerchomai.notes: προ-έρχομαι [in LXX: Gen.33:3, 14 R (עָבַר), Jdth.2:19, Sir.35:10 (Sir.32:10) al ;] __1. to go forward, go on, advance: before μικρόν, Mat.26:39 and Mrk.14:35 (WH, mg., προσελθών): ῥύμην μίαν (cf. Plat., Rep., i,…

proetoimazo.notes: προ-ετοιμάζω [in LXX: Isa.28:24 B, Wis.9:8 * ;] to prepare before: with accusative of thing(s), Rom.9:23 (οἱς for ἅ by attraction), Eph.2:10.† (AS)

proeuangelizomai.notes: προ-ευαγγελίζομαι to announce glad tidings beforehand: Gal.3:8.† (AS)

proginomai.notes: προ-γίνομαι [in LXX: Wis.19:13 אA, 2Ma.14:3 2Mac 15:8 * ;] to happen before: pf. pass. ptcp., Rom.3:25.† (AS)

proginosko.notes: προ-γινώσκω [in LXX: Wis.6:13 8:8 18:6 * ;] to know beforehand, foreknow: 2Pe.3:17; with accusative of person(s), Act.26:5; of the Divine foreknowledge, Rom.8:29 11:2, 1Pe.1:20.† (AS)

prognosis.notes: πρό-γνωσις, -εως, ἡ (προγινώσκω) [in LXX: Jdth.9:6 11:19 * ;] fore­knowledge: Act.2:23, 1Pe.1:2.† (AS)

progonos.notes: πρό-γονος, -ον (προγίνομαι), [in LXX: Est.4:17, Sir.8:4, al. ;] __1. born before. __2. As subst., in pl., οἱ π., ancestors, forefathers: 2Ti.1:3; of living parents (so Plat.), 1Ti.5:4.† (AS)

prographo.notes: προ-γράφω [in LXX: Dan LXX 3:3 cod., 1Ma.10:36 * ;] __1. to write before: Rom.15:4, Eph.3:3, Ju 4. __2. to write in public, placard, proclaim (Dem., Plut., al.): Gal.3:1 (Lft., in l). __3. = ζωγραφέω, to pourtray,…

proi.notes: πρωί (Rec. πρωΐ), adv. (πρό), [in LXX chiefly for בֹּקֶר ;] in the morning, early: Mat.16:3 (R, txt.) Mat.21:18, Mrk.1:35 11:20 13:35 15:1, Jhn.18:28 20:1; λίαν π., Mrk.16:2; ἅμα π., Mat.20:1; ἀπὸ π. (cf. ἀπὸ…

proia.notes: πρώιος (Rec. -ϊος), -α, -ον (πρωί), [in LXX for בֹּקֶר בַּ לַּ ;] at early morn, early; as subst., ἡ π. (sc. ὥρα, cf. ἡ ὥ. ἡ π., 3Ma.5:24), early morning: Mat.27:1, Jhn.21:4.† (AS)

proimos.notes: πρώϊμος, see: πρόϊμος . πρόϊμος (Rec. πρώ-, of which προ- is a late form; see Bl., § 6, 4; WH, App., 152), -ον, [in LXX: Deu.11:14 (יוֹרֶה), etc. ;] = the more common πρώιος (which see), early: of rain (as most…

proinos.notes: πρωινός (Rec. -ϊνος, see WH, App., ή, -όν (πρωί), [in LXX chiefly for בֹּקֶר ;] = cl. πρώιος, at early morn, early: ἀστήρ, Rev.2:28 22:16.† (AS)

proistemi.notes: προ-ΐστημι [in LXX: 2Ki.13:17, Pro.23:5 26:17, Isa.43:24, Amo.6:10 (no proper Heb. equiv.), Dan LXX Bel 1:7, 1Ma.5:19, 4Ma.11:27 * ;] __1. trans. in fut., 1 aor., and mid. 1 aor., to put before, set over (Plat.,…

prokaleo.notes: προ-καλέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ma.8:11 A * ;] to call forth. Most frequently in mid., __(a) to challenge; hence, to provoke: Gal.5:26; __(b) to invite (2Mac, l.with).† (AS)

prokatangello.notes: προ-κατ-αγγέλλω to announce beforehand: with accusative and inf., Act.3:18; before περί, Act.7:52.† (AS)

prokeimai.notes: πρό-κειμαι [in LXX: Exo.39:18 (36), Num.4:7 (פָּנִים), etc. ;] used as pass. of προτίθημι; __1. to be set before one, to be set forth: Heb.6:18 12:1-2, Ju 7. __2. to present oneself, be present: 2Co.8:12 (see Mey.,…

prokerusso.notes: προ-κηρύσσω __1. to proclaim by herald. __2. Of one who acts as a herald, to proclaim: Act.13:24.† (AS)

prokope.notes: προ-κοπή, -ῆς, ἡ (προκόπτω), [in LXX: Sir.51:17, 2Ma.8:8 * ;] progress (prop., on a journey, then generally): Php.1:12, 25, 1Ti.4:15 (condemned by Atticists, see Rutherford, NPhr., 158).† (AS)

prokopto.notes: προ-κόπτω [in Sm.: Psa.45:5 * ;] to cut forward a way, for­ward, advance, in cl. trans. with neut. adj., as οὐδὲν π., τὰ πολλὰ π. In late writers (Polyb., al.), wholly intrans., to advance, progress: of time,…

prokrima.notes: πρό-κριμα, -τος, τό (cl. προκρίνειν, 1. to prefer. 2. to judge beforehand), pre-judging, prejudice: 1Ti.5:21 (see Cremer, 378).† (AS)

prokuroomai.notes: προ-κυρόω, -ῶ to establish or confirm beforehand: Gal.3:17.† (AS)

prolambano.notes: προ-λαμβάνω [in LXX: Wis.17:11 א2, Wis.17:17 א2B * ;] __1. to take beforehand: with accusative, 1Co.11:21 (but see infr.). __2. to be beforehand, anticipate (in cl., with accusative, genitive or dative): with inf. (=…

prolego.notes: προ-λέγω [in LXX: Isa.41:26 (נָגַד hi.) * ;] __1. to tell or say before­hand: 2Co.13:2, Gal.5:21, 1Th.3:4 (R, txt.; cf. προεῖπον, but see infr.). __2. to declare, tell plainly: 2Co, Ga, 1Th, ll. with (R, mg.; see MM,…

promarturomai.notes: προ-μαρτύρομαι to protest beforehand (cf. μαρτύρομαι, and V. Hort, in l): 1Pe.1:11 (elsewhere only in Theod. Met., xiv/AD.).† (AS)

promeletao.notes: to practise beforehand : with infinitive, (Xenophon Historicus) (ML)

promerimnao.notes: προ-μεριμνάω, -ῶ to be anxious beforehand: Mrk.13:11.† (AS)

pronoeo.notes: προ-μοέω, -ῶ and depon. -έομαι, οῦμαι, [in LXX: Dan LXX 11:37 (בִּין), Wis.6:7, al. ;] __1. to foresee. __2. to provide (RV, take thought for): with accusative of thing(s), καλά, Rom.12:17 (-ούμενοι), 2Co.8:21…

pronoia.notes: πρόνοια, -ας, ἡ (πρόνοος, careful) [in LXX: Dan LXX 6:18 (19) Wis.14:3 17:2, 4Mac.6:1-35 * ;] foresight, forethought: Act.24:3; π. ποιεῖσθαι, with genitive (Dem., 546, 6), make provision for, show care for:…

proorao.notes: προ-οράω, -ῶ [in LXX: Psa.16:8) (שָׁוָה pi.), I Est.5:63 A * ;] to see before (as to place or time): with accusative, Act.21:29. Mid., with accusative, before ἐνώπιόν μου, Act.2:25 (LXX).† (AS)

proorizo.notes: προ-ορίζω to predetermine, foreordain: with accusative, Rom.8:30; id. and inf., Act.4:28; id. before εἰς, 1Co.2:7, Eph.1:5; with dupl. accusative, Rom.8:26; pass., Eph.1:11.† (AS)

propascho.notes: to suffer first or beforehand , (Herdotus Historicus), etc.: to be ill-treated before , ὑπό τινος (Thucydides):—;also, ἀγαθὸν πρ. (Xenophon Historicus) (ML)

propempo.notes: προ-πέμπω [in LXX: I Est.4:47, Jdth.10:15, Wis.19:2, 1Ma.12:4, 2Ma.6:23 * ;] __1. to send before, send forth. __2. to set forward on a journey, escort: with accusative of person(s), 1Co.16:11, Tit.3:13, 3Jn.6; before…

propetes.notes: προπετής, -ές (προπίπτω), [in LXX: Pro.10:14 (אֱוִיל) Pro.13:3 (פָּשַׂק) Sir.9:18 * ;] falling forwards, headlong. Metaphorical, precipitate, rash, reck­less: of persons, 2Ti.3:4; of things, Act.19:36.† (AS)

prophasis.notes: πρό-φασις, -εως, ἡ (φήμι), [in LXX: Psa.141:4 (עֲלִילָה), ??עֲלִילָה), ??Dan.6:4-5(5-6) (עִלָּה) Pro.18:1, Hos.10:4 * ;] a pretence, pretext: Mat.23:1 (WM, R, txt., om.), Mrk.12:40, Luk.20:47 Jhn.15:22, Act.27:30,…

prophero.notes: προφέρω [in LXX: Pro.10:13 (מָצָא ni.), Tob.9:5, al. ;] to bring forth: with accusative of thing(s), before ἐκ, Luk.6:45.† (AS)

propheteia.notes: προφητεία, -ας, ἡ (προψητεύω) [in LXX for נְבוּאָה, חָזוֹן ;] the gift (and its exercise) of interpreting the Divine will and purpose, prophecy, prophesying: of OT prophecy, Mat.13:14, 2Pe.1:20-21; of NT prophecy,…

prophetes.notes: προφήτης, -ου, ὁ [πρόφημι, to speak forth), [in LXX chiefly for נָבִיא ;] one who acts as an interpreter or forth-teller of the Divine will (see Lft., Notes, 83f.; Tr., Syn., §vi), a prophet; __1. in cl. (Æsch.,…

propheteuo.notes: προφητεύω (προφήτης), [in LXX chiefly for נָבָא ni., hith. ;] to be a προφήτης (which see), to prophesy: in the primary sense of telling forth the Divine counsels, Mat.7:22 26:68, Mrk.14:65, Luk.1:67 22:64, Act.19:6,…

prophetis.notes: προφῆτις, -ιδος, ἡ fem, of προφήτης, [in LXX: Exo.15:20, Jdg.4:4, 4Ki.22:14, 2Ch.34:22, Isa.8:3 (נְבִיאָה) * ;] a prophetess: Luk.2:36, Rev.2:20.† (AS)

prophthano.notes: προ-φθάνω [in LXX chiefly for קָדַם pi. ;] with ptcp. (as in cl., but more frequently the simple φθάνω, which see), to anticipate: Mat.17:25.† (AS)

proporeuo.notes: προ-πορεύω [in LXX chiefly for הָלַךְ, also for עָבַר, etc. ;] to make to go before. Pass. and mid., to go before: Luk.1:76, Act.7:40" (LXX) ,† (AS)

prora.notes: πρῷρα (Rec. incorrectly πρώρα, see Bl., § 3, 3; LS, see word), -ης (for Att.. -ας, see Bl., § 7, 1; Mayser, l 2), ἡ, the forward part of a ship, the prow: Act.27:30; opposite to πρύμνα, ib. Act.27:41.† (AS)

prosabbaton.notes: προ-σάββατον, -ου, τό [in LXX: Psa.92:1-15 title. א (שַׁבָּת) שַׁבָּת) Psa.93:1-5 title., Jdth.8:6 * ;] the day before the Sabbath: Mrk.15:42 (L, Tr., txt., πρὸς σ.).† (AS)

prosago.notes: προσ-άγω [in LXX chiefly for = קָרַב hi., also for נָגַשׁ etc. ;] __1. trans., to bring or lead: with accusative and dative, Act.16:20; metaphorically, τ. θεῷ, 1Pe.3:18; before ὧδε, Luk.9:41; pass., with dative,…

prosagoge.notes: προσ-αγωγή, -ῆς, ἡ (προσάγω), __1. a bringing to. __2. approach, access (see Lft., Notes, 284; MM, xxi): Rom.5:2, Eph.2:18 3:12 (but see Ellic., Eph., 59 f., where the transitive sense "introduction " is advocated).†…

prosagoreuo.notes: προσ-αγορεύω [in LXX: Deu.23:6 (7) (דָּרַשׁ), ?Est.10:11 (יָדָה hith.), Wis.14:22, 1Ma.14:40, 2Ma.1:36 2Mac 4:7 2Mac 10:9 2Mac 14:37 * ;] to address, greet, salute; hence, to call by name, address, style: with dupl.…

prosaiteo.notes: προσ-αιτέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Job.27:14 * ;] __1. to ask besides. __2. to continue asking; hence, to importune, beg, ask alms: Jhn.9:8.† (AS)

prosanabaino.notes: προσ-ανα-βαίνω [in LXX: Exo.19:23, al. (עָלָה) ;] __1. to go up besides. __2. to go up higher: Luk.14:10.† (AS)

prosanalisko.notes: προσ-αναλίσκω to spend besides: Luk.8:43 (WH, R, mg., om.).† (AS)

prosanapleroo.notes: προσ-αναπληρόω, -ῶ [in LXX: Wis.19:4 * ;] to fill up by adding to, to supply fully: 2Co.9:12 11:9.† (AS)

prosanatithemi.notes: προσ-ανα-τίθημι to lay on or offer besides; mid., __(a) to lay on oneself in addition, undertake besides; __(b) with genitive of person(s), of giving or obtaining information, to consult, communicate: Gal.1:16 2:6…

prosapeileo.notes: προσ-απειλέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Sir.13:3 א * ;] to threaten further: Act.4:21.† (AS)
