proschusis.notes: πρόσ-χυσις, -εως, ἡ a pouring or sprinkling upon: Heb.11:28.† (AS)

prosdapanao.notes: προσδαπανάω, -ῶ to spend besides: with accusative, Luk.10:35.† SYN.: προσαναλίσκω (AS)

prosdechomai.notes: προσ-δέχομαι [in LXX chiefly for רָצָה ;] __1. to receive to oneself, receive favourably, admit, accept: with accusative of person(s), Luk.15:2, Rom.16:2, Php.2:29; with accusative of thing(s), Act.24:15 (R, mg.),…

prosdeo.notes: προσ-δέομαι [in LXX: Pro.12:9 חָסֵר חָסֵר Sir.6:1-37 * ;] to want further, need in addition: Act.17:25.† (AS)

prosdokao.notes: προσ-δοκάω, -ῶ (the simple verb exists only in the forms δοκέω, -εύω) [in LXX: Psa.104:27 (שָׂבַר pi.), etc. ;] to await, expect: Mat.24:50, Luk.3:15 12:46, Act.27:33 28:6; with accusative of person(s), Mat.11:3,…

prosdokia.notes: προσδοκία, -ας, ἡ (ιροσδοκάω), [in LXX: Gen.49:10 (יִקָּהָה), Psa.119:116 (שֵׂבֶר) Wis.17:13, Sir.40:2, al. ;] expectation: with genitive obj., Luk.21:26; with genitive subjc., Act.12:11.† (AS)

proseao.notes: to suffer to go further , τινά (New Testament) (ML)

prosecho.notes: προσ-έχω [in LXX for קָשַׁב hi., שָׁמַר ni., etc. ;] __1. to turn to, bring to (frequently ναῦν, expressed or understood, to bring to port, land; Hdt., al.). __2. τ. νοῦν, before dative, to turn one's mind to, attend…

prosedreuo.notes: προσεδρεύω (πρόσεδρος, sitting near), [in LXX: 1Ma.11:40 * ;] __1. to sit near. __2. to attend regularly: with dative, 1Co.9:13, Rec. (see: παρεδρεύω).† παρ-εδρεύω [in LXX: Pro.1:21 8:3 * ;] to sit constantly beside,…

proseloo.notes: προσ-ηλόω -ῶ [in LXX: 3Ma.4:9 * ;] to nail to: with accusative and dative, figuratively, Col.2:14.† (AS)

proselutos.notes: προσήλυτος, -ον (προοελαύνω), [in LXX for גֵּר ;] __1. one who has arrived, a stranger. __2. Of converts to Judaism, a proselyte (see DB, see word): Mat.23:15, Act.2:10 6:5 13:43.† (AS)

proserchomai.notes: προσ-έρχομαι [in LXX for קָרַב, נָגַשׁ, etc. ;] to approach, draw near: absol., Mat.4:11, Luk.9:42, al.; with infin., Mat.24:1, al.; with dative loc., Heb.12:18, 22; dative of person(s), Mat.5:1, and freq.,…

prosergazomai.notes: προσ-εργάζομαι __1. to work or do service besides (Hdt., Plut.). __2. to gain besides, by working or trading: Luk.19:16 (Xen.).† (AS)

proseuche.notes: προσ-ευχή, -ῆς, ἡ (προσεύχομαι), [in LXX chiefly for תְּפִלָּה ;] __1. prayer to God: Mat.17:21 (WH, R, txt., om.) Mat.21:22, Mrk.9:29, Luk.22:45, Act.3:1 6:4 10:31, Rom.12:12, 1Co.7:5, Col.4:2; pl., Act.2:42 10:4,…

proseuchomai.notes: προσ-εύχομαι [in LXX chiefly for פָּלַל hith. ;] to pray (always of prayer to God, or in cl., to gods): absol., Mat.6:5-7, 9 14:23 19:13 26:36, 39 26:41, 44, Mrk.1:35 6:46 11:24-25 13:33 (WH, E, txt., om.) Mrk.14:32,…

proskairos.notes: πρόσ-καιρος, -ον [in LXX: 4Ma.15:2 4Mac 15:8 4Mac 15:23 * ;] __1. in season. __2. for a season, temporary, transient: 2Co.4:18, Heb.11:25; of plants, short-lived: Mat.13:21, Mrk.4:17.† (AS)

proskaleo.notes: προσ-καλέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for קָרָא ;] to call to. Mid., to call to oneself (see M, Pr., 157): with accusative of person(s), Mat.10:1, Mrk.3:13 6:7, Luk.7:19, Act.5:40, Jas.5:14, al. Metaphorical, of the Divine…

proskartereo.notes: προσ-καρτερέω, -ῶ (καρτερός, strong, stedfast) [in LXX: Num.13:21 (חָזַק hith.), Tob.5:8 א, Da Th Su 1:6* ;] to attend constantly, continue stedfastly, adhere to, wait on: with dative of person(s), Mrk.3:9, Act.8:13…

proskephalaion.notes: προσ-κεφάλαιον, -ου, τό [in LXX: Eze.13:18, 20 (כֶּסֶת), I Est.3:8 * ;] a pillow, cushion: Mrk.4:38.† (AS)

proskleroo.notes: προσ-κληρόω, -ῶ to allot to, assign to by lot; pass. (but perh. as nid., EV, consorted with, so Syr.): Act.17:4 (for exx., v, Cremer, 749).† (AS)

proskollao.notes: προσ-κολλάω, -ῶ, [in LXX chiefly for דָּבַק ;] to glue to; pass., reflexive, to stick to, cleave to (Plat.): metaphorically (with dative, Jos.23:8, Sir.6:34 al.), before πρός, Mrk.10:7 (R, txt.), Eph.5:31 (LXX).† (AS)

proskomma.notes: πρόσ-κομμα, -τος, τό (προσκόπτω), [in LXX: Exo.23:33 34:12 (מוֹקֵשׁ), Isa.8:14 (נֶגֶף), Jdth.8:22, Sir.17:25, al. ;] __(a) a stumble, stumbling: λίθος προσκόμματος (= נֶגֶף אֶבֶן, Isa, l.with), figuratively,…

proskope.notes: προσκοπή, -ῆς, ή (προσκόπτω), [in Gr. Ven.: Pro.16:18 (כִּשָּׁלוֹן) * ;] an occasion of stumbling, offence: 2Co.6:3.† (AS)

proskopto.notes: προσ-κόπτω [in LXX for נָגַף, כָּשַׁל ni., etc. ;] __1. trans., to strike (e.g. hand or foot) against: with accusative before πρός, figuratively, Mat.4:6, Luk.4:11, (LXX) __2. Intrans., to stumble: absol. (Tob.11:9,…

proskulio.notes: προσ-κυλίω to roll up, roll to: with accusative and dative, Mat.27:60; accusative before ἐπί, Mrk.15:46.† (AS)

proskuneo.notes: προσ-κυνέω, -ῶ (κυνέω, to kiss), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁחָה hith. ;] to make obeisance, do reverence to, worship; __(a) prop. (as in cl., of the gods: Hdt., Æsch., Plat., al.), of God, Christ and supra-mundane…

proslaleo.notes: προσ-λαλέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Exo.4:16 AB2 (דָבַר pi.), Wis.13:17 * ;] to speak to: Act.28:20; with dative of person(s), Act.13:43.† (AS)

proslambano.notes: προσ-λαμβάνω [in LXX: Psa.18:16 (מָשָׁה hi.), Psa.73:24 (לָקַח), etc. ;] __1. to take in addition. __2. to take to oneself, take, receive; in NT always mid., -ομαι; __(a) of things: of food, with accusative,…

proslepsis.notes: πρόσ-λημψις (Rec. -ληψις, as in Att..), -εως, ἡ (προσλαμβάνω), __1. an assumption (Plat.). __2. a receiving (cf. προσλαμβάνω): Rom.11:15,† (AS)

prosmeno.notes: προσ-μένω [in LXX: Jdg.3:25 A (חוּל hi.), Tob.2:2 א, Wis.3:9, 3Ma.7:17 * ;] __1. to wait longer, continue, remain still: Act.18:18; before ἐν, 1Ti.1:3. __2. C. dative, to remain with: Mat.15:32, Mrk.8:32.…

prosochthizo.notes: προσ-οχθίζω [in LXX: Lev.26:15, al. (גָּעַל); Lev.18:25, al. (קוֹא), Psa.95:10, Eze.36:31 (קוּט), Sir.6:25, al. (other writers use ὀχθέω, rarely -ίζω) ;] to be angry with: with dative, Heb.3:10 (LXX), Heb.3:17.† (AS)

prosopheilo.notes: to owe besides or still , (Thucydides): absolute, προσοφείλοντας ἡμᾶς ἐνέγραψεν (Demosthenes Orator):—;Pass. to be still owing, be still due , (Thucydides); so, ἡ ἔχθρη ἡ προσοφειλομένη ἐς Ἀθηναίους ἐκ τῶν Αἰγινητέων…

prosopolepsia.notes: προσωπολημψία (Rec. -ληψία), -ας, ἡ (προσωπολήμπτης) respect of persons: Rom.2:11, Eph.6:9, Col.3:25, Jas.2:1 (see Mayor, in l).† (AS)

prosopolepteo.notes: προσωπολημπτέω (Rec. -ληπτ-), -ῶ (-λήμπτης, which see), to have respect of persons: Jas.2:9.† (AS)

prosopoleptes.notes: προσωπο-λήμπτης (Rec. -λήπτης, see Bl., § 6, 8), -ου, ὁ (πρόσωπον λαμβάνειν, see: πρόσωπον), a respecter of persons: Act.10:34 (on this group of cognate forms, see Mayor, da., 78 f.; and cf. Thackeray, Gr., 44).† (AS)

prosopon.notes: πρόσωπον, -ου, τό (πρός, ὤψ), [in LXX chiefly and very frequently for פָּנִים ;] __1. prop., of persons (so always in cl.); __(a) the face, countenance: Mat.6:16-17, Mrk.14:65, Luk.9:29, 2Co.3:7, Rev.4:7, al.; τὸ π.…

prosormizo.notes: προσ-ορμίζω (ὅρμος, an anchorage), to bring a ship to anchor at; usually in mid., to come to anchor near, and so pass. in late writers (Ael., Dio Cass.): Mrk.6:53.† (AS)

prospegnumi.notes: προσ-πήγνυμι to fasten to: absol., to crucify, Act.2:23.† (AS)

prosphagion.notes: προσφάγιον, -ου, τό (φαγεῖν), Hellenistic for ὄψον ὀψάριον) a relish or dainty (esp, cooked fish), to be eaten with bread: Jhn.21:5 (have ye taken any fish, Field, Notes, 109; Abbott, Essays, 105; cf. M, Pr., 170n;…

prosphatos.notes: πρόσφατος, -ον (on the derivation, see Boisacq, see word and cf. -ως), [in LXX: Num.6:3 (לַח), Deu.32:17, Ecc.1:9 (חָדָשׁ), Psa.81:9 (זוּר), Sir.9:10 * ;] __1. originally, freshly slain. __2. Generally (from Æsch.…

prosphero.notes: προσ-φέρω [in LXX chiefly for קָרַב hi. ;] __1. to bring to, lead to: with accusative and dative of person(s), Mat.4:24 8:16 9:2, 32 14:35 17:16, Mrk.2:4 (WH, R, txt.; sc. αὐτόν) Mrk.10:13, Luk.18:15 23:14; pass.,…

prosphiles.notes: προσφιλής, -ές (φιλέω), [in LXX: Est.5:1, Sir.4:7 20:13 * ;] __(a) of persons, in both act, and pass. sense (LXX, ll. with); __(b) of things, pleas­ing, agreeable (EV, lovely): Php.4:8.† (AS)

prosphoneo.notes: προσ-φωνέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 1Es.2:21 Est.6:6 Est.6:22, 2Ma.15:15 * ;] __1. to address, call to; in cl., with accusative of person(s), with dupl. accusative (cf. 2Mac, l.with); absol. (Hom., Od., v, 159, al.): Act.21:40…

prosphora.notes: προσ-φορά, -ᾶς, ἡ (προσφέρω), [in LXX: 3Ki.7:48 (פָּנִים) Psa.40:6 (מִנְחָה), I Est.5:52, Sir.14:11, al. ;] __1. a bringing to, offering (Plat., al.). __2. (Less frequently in cl.), a present, an offering; in NT, of…

prospipto.notes: προσ-πίπτω [in LXX for נָפַל, נָגַע hi., כָּרַע ;] __1. to fall upon, strike against: with dative, of wind, Mat.7:25. __2. to fall down at one's feet, fall prostrate before: absol., before πρός, Mrk.7:25; with dative…

prospoieo.notes: προσ-ποιέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 1Ki.21:13 (14) (הָלַל hithpo.), Job.19:14, (Sir 34:30 (Sir.31:30)), Dan LXX Su 1:11 * ;] to make over to, add or attach to. Mid., to take to oneself, claim; hence, to pretend; with inf. (cf.…

prosporeuomai.notes: προσ-πορεύομαι [in LXX for קָרַב, נָגַשׁ, etc. ;] to come near, approach: with dative of person(s), Mrk.10:35.† (AS)

prospsauo.notes: προσ-ψαύω in poët. and late writers, to touch: with dative, Luk.11:46.† (AS)

prosregnumi.notes: προσ-ρήγνυμι [in Aq.: Psa.2:9 * ;] to break against, dash against; __(a) trans. (παιδία πέτραις, FlJ, Ant., ix, 4, 6); __(b) intrans., with dative: Luk.6:48-49 (cf. προσπίπτω).† (AS)

prostasso.notes: προσ-τάσσω [in LXX chiefly for צָוָה pi. ;] __1. with accusative of person(s), to place at, to attach to. __2. to give a command, enjoin, appoint: Luk.5:14; with dative of person(s), Mat.1:24; with accusative of…

prostatis.notes: προστάτις, -ιδος, ἡ (fem, of προοτάτης), a patroness, protectress: Rom.16:2.† (AS)

prostithemi.notes: προσ-τίθημι [in LXX chiefly for יָסַף hi., also for אָסַף ni., etc. ;] __1. to put to. __2. to add, join to, give in addition: with accusative before ἐπί, Mat.6:27, Luk.3:20 12:25; ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτό, Act.2:47; with…

prostrecho.notes: προσ-τρέχω [in LXX for רוּץ ;] to run to: Mrk.9:15 10:17, Act.8:30.† (AS)

proteino.notes: προ-τείνω, [in LXX: 2Ma_7:1-42, 3Ma_2:1 * ;] to stretch out, stretch forth: of preparations for scourging (see Field, Notes, 136 f.), Act.22:25.† (AS)

proteron.notes: before; (adv.) before, formerly, an earlier time former, prior, Eph. 4:22; before, former, prior, Eph. 4:22; before, formerly, Jn. 6:62

proteuo.notes: πρωτεύω (πρῶτος) [in LXX: Est.5:11, 2Ma.6:18 2Mac 13:15 * ;] to be first, pre-eminent, have the first place: Col.1:18.† (AS)

prothesis.notes: πρό-θεσις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX: Exo.40:4, 23 (עֵרֶךְ), 1Ch.9:32, al. (מַעֲרֶכֶת), 2Ch.4:19 (פָּנִים), 1Ma.1:22, 2Ma.3:8, al. ;] __1. a setting forth (Plat., Plut., al.): οἱ ἄρτοι τῆς π. (cf. LXX, ll. with, elsewhere…

prothesmia.notes: προ-θέσμιος, -α, -ον [in Sm.: Job.28:3, Dan.9:26 * ;] appointed before­hand. In Attic law, as subst. (so always in cl.), ἡ π. (sc. ἡμέρα), a day appointed beforehand, a previously appointed time: Gal.4:2.† (AS)

prothumia.notes: προθυμία, -ας, ἡ (πρόθυμος), [in LXX: Sir.45:23 * ;] eagerness, willingness, readiness: Act.17:11, 2Co.8:11-12 8:19 9:2.† (AS)

prothumos.notes: πρόθυμος, -ον [in LXX: 1Ch.28:21, 2Ch.29:31 (נָדִיב), Hab.1:8 (חוּשׁ), al. ;] willing, ready: Mat.26:41, Mrk.14:38; neut., τὸ π. = ἡ προθυμία (Thuc., al., 3Ma.5:26), οὕτως τὸ κατ᾽ ἐμὲ (= cl. τὸ ἐμὸν) π., Rom.1:15…

protithemi.notes: προ-τίθημι [in LXX: Exo.40:4, 23, Lev.24:8 (עָרַךְ) Psa.54:3 (שׂוּם), etc. ;] __1. to set before, set forth publicly; so also in mid.: with accusative of person(s), Rom.3:25 (for a suggested alt, rend., see MM,…

protokathedria.notes: πρωτοκαθεδρία, -ας, ἡ (πρῶιος, καθέδρα), the chief seat: Mat.23:6, Mrk.12:39 (see Swete, in l), Luk.11:43 20:46.† (AS)

protoklisia.notes: πρωτο-κλυτία, -ας, ἡ the chief place at table (see Swete, Mk., l.with): Mat.23:6, Mrk.12:39, Luk.14:7-8 20:46.† (AS)

proton.notes: πρῶτον, πρῶτος, see: πρότερος A. Compar., πρότερος, -α, -ον, [in LXX for רִאשׁוֹן ,לִפָנִים, etc. ;] before, of time, place, rank, etc.; in NT always of Time, before, former: Eph.4:22. Adverbially, πρότερον, before,…

protos.notes: B. Superl., πρῶτος, -η, -ον, [in LXX chiefly for רִאשׁוֹן, also for אֶחַד, etc. ;] first, __1. of Time or Place; __(a) absol., as subst., ὁ π.., Luk.14:18, Jhn.19:32, 1Co.14:30; ὁ π. καὶ ὁ ἔσχατος, Rev.1:17 2:8…

protostates.notes: πρωτοστάτης, -ου, ὁ (πρῶτος, ἵστημι) [in LXX: Job.15:24 AB * ;] prop., of soldiers, one who stands first, one in the front rank (Thuc., Xen.); hence, metaphorically, a leader: Act.24:5.† (AS)

prototokia.notes: πρωτοτόκια, -ων, τά (πρωτότοκος) [in LXX (with seel. -εῖα, -εία): Gen.25:31 ff. Gen.27:36, Deu.21:17, 1Ch.5:1 (בְּכוֹרָה) * ;] the rights of the first-born, birthright (= cl. ἡ πρεσβεία): Heb.12:16.† (AS)

prototokos.notes: πρωτότοκος, -ον (πρῶτος, τίκτω) [in LXX chiefly for בְּכוֹר ;] first-born: Luk.2:7; pl., Heb.11:28. Metaphorical, of the priority of Christ (originally perh. a Messianic title, cf. Psa.89:28, Heb.1:6; see ICC on…

protrecho.notes: προ-τρέχω [in LXX: 1Ki.8:11 (פָּנִים רוּץ), Job.41:13 (14) A (דּוּץ), Tob.11:3 (before ἔμπροσθεν), 1Ma.16:21 * ;] __1. to run forward. __2. to run on, run in advance: Jhn.20:4; before εἰς τὸ ἔμπροσθεν, Luk.19:4.† (AS)

protrepo.notes: προ-τρέπω [in LXX: Wis.14:18, 2Ma.11:7, 4Ma.12:7 4Mac 15:12 4Mac 16:13 * ;] to urge forwards, exhort, persuade. Mid., in same sense (as also in cl.): Act.18:27.† (AS)

prouparcho.notes: προ-ϋπ-άρχω [in LXX: Job 42:18* ;] __1. to be beforehand in. __2. to be before or previously: with ptcp., Luk.23:12, Act.8:9.† (AS)

prumna.notes: πρύμνα, -ης, ἡ prop. fem. of adj. πρύμνο, -η, -ον, (sc. ναῦς), the hindmost part of a ship, the stern: Mrk.4:38, Act.27:29; opposite to πρῷρα, Act.27:41.† (AS)

psallo.notes: ψάλλω [in LXX chiefly for זָמַר pi. (Jdg.5:3, Psa.7:17, al.), also for נָגַן pi. (1Ki.16:16 ff, al.) ;] __1. to pull, twitch, twang (as a bowstring, etc.; Æsch., Eur., al.), hence, __2. absol., __(a) to play a…

psalmos.notes: ψαλμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ψάλλω), [in LXX chiefly for מִזְמוֹר ;] __1. a striking, twitching with the fingers (Eur., al.), hence, a striking of musical strings (Æsch., a1.), and hence in later writers, __2. a sacred song sung…

pselaphao.notes: ψηλαφάω, -ῶ (ψάω, to touch), [in LXX for מוּשׁ, מָשַׁשׁ pi., etc. ;] __1. to feel or grope about; with accusative, to feel about for, search after: metaphorically, of seeking God, Act.17:27. __2. to feel, touch,…

psephizo.notes: ψηφίζω (ψῆφος), [in LXX: 3Ki.3:8 8:5 A (סָפַר ni..) * ;] to count (prop., with pebbles), reckon, calculate: τ. δαπάνην, Luk.14:28; τ. ἀριθμόν (i.e. calculate the number's meaning), Rev.13:18 (in cl. chiefly mid., to…

psephos.notes: ψῆφος, -ου, ἡ (ψάω, to rub) [in LXX: Exo.4:25 (צֹר), La 3:16 (חָצָץ), Ecc.7:26 (25) (צֹר), 4Ki.12:4 (5) A, Sir.18:10, 4Ma.15:26 * ;] __1. a small smooth stone, a pebble: ψ. λευκή (for suggestions as to the meaning,…

pseudadelphos.notes: ψευδ-άδελφος, -ου, ὁ a false brother: of professing Christians, 2Co.11:26, Gal.2:4.† (AS)

pseudes.notes: ψευδής, -ές (ψεύδομαι), [in LXX for שֶׁקֶר, שָׁוְא, כָּזָב; etc. ;] lying, false, untrue (of persons and things): Rev.2:2; μάρτυρες, Act.6:13; as subst., ὁ ψ., a liar: Rev.21:8.† (AS)

pseudochristos.notes: ψευδό-χριστος, -ου ὁ a false Christ or Messiah, "a pretender to the Messianic office" (Swete, Mk., 309; cf. also Tr., Syn., § xxx): Mat.24:24, Mrk.13:22 (cf. ἀντίχριστος).† (AS)

pseudologos.notes: ψευδο-λόγος, -ον (ψευδής, λέγω), speaking falsely, lying: 1Ti.4:2 (Aristoph., Polyb., al.).† (AS)

pseudomai.notes: ψεύδω to deceive by lies; more frequently in the depon. mid, form ψεύδομαι (so always in NT), [in LXX chiefly for כָּחַשׁ pi. ;] __1. absol., to lie: Heb.6:18, 1Jn.1:6, Rev.3:9; οὐ ψεύδομαι, Rom.9:1, 2Co.11:31,…

pseudomartur.notes: ψευδο-μάρτυς, -υρος, ὁ [in LXX: Dan LXX Su 1:60-61 Bl * ;] a false witness: Mat.26:60; with genitive obj. (see WM, § 30, 1a), 1Co.15:15.† (AS)

pseudomartureo.notes: ψευδο-μαρτυρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Exo.20:16, Deu.5:20 (17) (עָנָה), Da TH Su 1:61 AB2R (see ψευδομάρτυς) * ;] to bear false witness: Mat.19:18, Mrk.10:19, Luk.18:20" (LXX) ; before κατά, with genitive of person(s),…

pseudonumos.notes: ψευδώνυμος, -ον (ψευδής, ὄνομα) under a false name, falsely called: 1Ti.6:20 (Æsch., Plut., a1.).† (AS)

pseudoprophetes.notes: ψευδο-προφήτης, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Zec.13:2, Jer.1:9 (Jer.6:13, al.) (נָבִיא)* ;] a false prophet (= cl. ψευδόμαντις): Mat.7:15 24:11, 24, Mrk.13:22 (see Swete, in l), Luk.6:26, Act.13:6, 2Pe.2:1, 1Jn.4:1, Rev.16:13…

pseudos.notes: ψεῦδος, -εος (-ους), τό [in LXX chiefly for שֶׁקֶר, also for כַּחַשׁ, כָּזָב ;] a falsehood, untruth, lie: Rev.14:15; opposite to ἡ ἀλήθεια, Jhn.8:44, Rom.1:25, Eph.4:25, 2Th.2:11, 1Jn.2:21; to τὸ ἀληθές, 1Jn.2:27;…

pseusma.notes: ψεῦσμα, -τος, τό (ψεύδω), [in Aq., Th.: Job.34:6, Pro.23:3; in Sm.: Job.13:4, al. ;] a lie, falsehood: Rom.3:7 (Plat., Plut. al.).† (AS)

psichionpsix.notes: ψιχίον, -ου, τό dimin. of ψίξ, a crumb: Mat.15:27, Mrk.7:28.† (AS)

psithurismos.notes: ψιθυρισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (ψιθυρίζω, to whisper), [in LXX: Ecc.10:11 (לַחַשׁ) * ;] a whispering; __(a) of secret slander, 2Co.12:20 (Plut.); __(b) of a murmured enchantment, Ec, l.with† (AS)

psithuristes.notes: ψιθυριστής, -οῦ, ὁ (see supr.), a whisperer (as epithet of Hermes, Dem., 1358, 6): in bad sense, Rom.1:30.† Syn.: καταλάλος, q.v (AS)

psocho.notes: ψώχω (ψώω, collat. form of ψάω, to rub) to rub: Luk.6:1.† (AS)

psomion.notes: ψωμίον, -ου, τό dimin. of ψωμός, a fragment, morsel: Jhn.13:26-27, 30.† (AS)

psomizo.notes: ψωμίζω (ψωμός, a morsel), [in LXX chiefly for אָכַל hi. ;] to feed with morsels (as children or the sick; Hippocr.), hence, generally, in late writers, to feed, nourish: with accusative of person(s), Rom.12:20 (LXX);…

psuche.notes: ψυχή, -ῆς, ἡ, [in LXX very frequently for נֶפֶשׁ, sometimes for לֵבַב ,לֵב, etc. ;] __1. breath (Lat. anima), breath of life, life (Hom., al.; in Arist., of the vital principle): Mat.6:25, Mrk.3:4 10:45, Luk.12:22,…

psuchikos.notes: ψυχικός, -ή, -όν (ψυχή) [in LXX: 4Ma.1:32 * ;] of the ψυχή (as the lower part of the immaterial in man), EV, natural: opposite to πνευματικός, 1Co.2:14 15:44 15:46; πνεῦμα μὴ ἔχοντες (EV, sensual; R, mg., natural or…

psucho.notes: ψύχω [in LXX for קוּר, שָׁטַח ;] to breathe, blow; hence, to make cool. Pass., to grow cool: metaphorically, Mat.24:12.† (AS)

psuchos.notes: ψύχος (LT, ψῦ-, as in cl.), -εος (-ους), τό (ψύχω), [in LXX: Gen.8:22 (קֹר), Job.37:9, Psa.147:17 (קָרָה), Zec.14:6, Dan LXX TH 3:67,69 * ;] cold: Jhn.18:18, Act.28:2, 2Co.11:27.† (AS)

psuchros.notes: ψυχρός, -ά, -όν (ψύχω), [in LXX: Pro.25:25 (קַר), Sir.43:20, 4Ma.11:26 * ;] cold: sc. ὕδωρ (cf. Theogn., 263; Hdt., ii, 37), Mat.10:42; metaphorically, of indifferent persons, Rev.3:15-16 † (AS)

ptaio.notes: πταίω [in LXX chiefly for נָגַף ni. ;] __1. trans., to cause to stumble (1Ki.4:3, cf. Deiss., BS, 681). __2. Intrans., to stumble. Metaphorical, in moral sense, Rom.11:11, Jas.2:10 3:2, 2Pe.1:10.† (AS)

ptenos.notes: πτηνός, -ή, -όν (πέτομαι), [in Aq.: Job.5:7 * ;] winged; as subst., π., birds: 1Co.15:39.† (AS)

pterna.notes: πτέρνα, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for עָקֵב ;] the heel: figuratively, ἐπαίρειν τὴν π. ἐπί, Jhn.13:18 (LXX).† (AS)

pterugion.notes: πτερύγιον, -ου, -τό (dimin. of πτέρυξ), [in LXX chiefly for כָּנָף ;] __1. a little wing. __2. Anything like a wing, as a turret, battlement: τ. ἱεροῦ, Mat.4:5, Luk.4:9.† (AS)

pterux.notes: πτέρυξ, -υγος, ἡ (πέτομαι), [in LXX chiefly for כָּנָף ;] a wing: of birds, Mat.23:37, Luk.13:34, Rev.12:14; of creatures seen in a vision, Rev.4:8 9:9.† (AS)

ptocheia.notes: πτωχεία, -ας, ἡ (πτωχεύω), [in LXX chiefly for עֳנִי ;] beggary, destitution: 2Co.8:2 8:9, Rev.2:9.† (AS)

ptocheuo.notes: πτωχεύω (πτωχός), [in LXX: Jdg.6:6, Psa.79:8 (דָּלַל); Jdg.14:15 A, Pro.23:21 (יָרַשׁ); Psa.34:10 (רוּשׁ), Tob.4:21 * ;] __1. to be a beggar, to beg (so chiefly in cl.). __2. to be poor as a beggar, to be destitute,…

ptochos.notes: πτωχός, -ή, -όν (πτώσσω, to crouch, cower), [in LXX for עָנִי, דַּל, רָשׁ, etc. ;] of one who crouches and cowers, hence, __1. as subst., a beggar: Luk.14:13, 21 16:20, 22. __2. As adj., __(a) prop., beggarly:…

ptoeo.notes: πτοέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for חָתַת ni. ;] to terrify. Pass., to be terrified: Luk.21:9 24:37 (WH, mg., θροηθέντες).† (AS)

ptoesis.notes: πτόησις, -εως, ἡ (πτοέω) [in LXX: Pro.3:25 (פַּחַד), Sir 50:4 א1, 1Ma.3:25 R * ;] a fluttering, excitement, caused by any emotion, but esp. by fear, hence, terror: φοβεῖσθαι πτόησιν, to be afraid with (cogn.…

ptolemais.notes: Πτολεμαΐς, -ΐδος, ἡ Ptolemais, a maritime city of Phœnicea: Act.21:7.† (AS)

ptoma.notes: πτῶμα -τος, τό (πίπτω), [in LXX: Jdg.14:8 (מַפֶּלֶת), Job.16:15 (14) (פֶּרֶץ), Isa.51:19 (שֹׁד), Jdth.8:19, Wis.4:18, al. ;] __1. a fall, metaphorically, a misfortune, calamity (Trag., Plat., Polyb., al.; LXX). __2.…

ptosis.notes: πτῶσις, -εως, ἡ (πίπτω), [in LXX chiefly for נֶגֶף, מַפֶּלֶת, and cogn. forms ;] a falling, fall (Plat., Plut., al.): Mat.7:27; metaphorically, Luk.2:34.† (AS)

ptuo.notes: πτύω [in LXX: Num.12:14 (יָרָק), Sir.28:12 * ;] to spit: Mrk.7:33 8:23, Jhn.9:6 (cf. ἐκ-, ἐμ-πτύω).† (AS)

ptuon.notes: πτύον, -ου, τό [in Sm.: Isa.30:24 * ;] a winnowing shovel or fan: Mat.3:12, Luk.3:17.† (AS)

pturomai.notes: πτύρομαι depon., to be startled, frightened: Php.1:28. (The active πτύρω is also found in some late writers.) † (AS)

ptusso.notes: πτύσσω to fold; of a scroll, to roll up: βιβλίον, Luk.4:20 (cf. ἀνα-πτύσσω).† (AS)

pugme.notes: πυγμή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Exo.21:18, Isa.58:4 (אֶגְרֹף) * ;] the fist: πυγμῇ νίψασθαι τ. χῖρας (Τ, πυκνά; Vg., Goth., Copt., crebro), to wash the hands with the fist (diligently, R, txt.; up to the elbow, R, mg.; the…

puknos.notes: πυκιός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Eze.31:3 A (חֹרֶשׁ), 3Ma.1:28 3Mac 4:10, 4Ma.12:12 * ;] __1. close, compact, solid. __2. frequent: 1Ti.5:23. Neut. pl., πυκνά, as adv., much, often: Mrk.7:3 Τ (see: πυγμή), Luk.5:33.…

pule.notes: πύλη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly and very frequently for שַׁעַר sometimes for דֶּלֶת, פֶּתַח ;] a gate: Luk.7:12, Act.9:24 12:10 16:13, Heb.13:12; ἡ Ὡραία Π. τ. ἱεροῦ, Act.3:10. Metaphorical, Mat.7:13-14; πύλαι ᾅδου…

pulon.notes: πυλών, -ῶνος, ὁ (πύλη), [in LXX chiefly for פֶּתַח, שַׁעַר ;] __1. the porch or vestibule of a house or palace: Mat.26:71, Luk.16:20, Act.10:17 12:13-14 __2. The gate-way or gate-tower of a walled town: Act.14:13,…

punthanomai.notes: πυνθώ᾽ομαι [in LXX for דָּרַשׁ ;] __1. to inquire: with accusative of thing(s), Jhn.4:42, Act.23:20; before quæst. indir., Mat.2:4, Luk.15:25 18:36, Act.10:18 21:33; quæst. dir., Act.4:7 10:20 23:10; before παρά,…

pur.notes: πῦρ genitive, πυρός, τό, [in LXX chiefly and very frequently for אֵשׁ ;] fire: Mrk.9:22, Luk.3:9, Jhn.15:6, Act.2:10, 1Co.3:13, Jas.3:5, Rev.8:5, al; π. καὶ θεῖον, Luk.17:29; κατακαίειν (ἐν) π., Mat.13:40, Rev.17:16…

pura.notes: πυρά, -ᾶς, ἡ (πῦρ) [in LXX: Jdth.7:5, Wis.17:6, al. ;] a fire: Act.28:2-3.† (AS)

puresso.notes: πυρέσσω (πῦρ) to be ill of a fever: Mat.8:14, Mrk.1:3.† (AS)

puretos.notes: πυρετός, -οῦ, ὁ (πῦρ) [in LXX: Deu.28:22 (קַדַּחַת) * ;] a fever: Mat.8:15, Mrk.1:31, Luk.4:39, Jhn.4:52, Act.28:8; π. μέγας, a high fever: Luk.4:38 (on the technical phrase here, see MM, xxii).† (AS)

purgos.notes: πύργος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for מִגְדָּל ;] a tower: Luk.13:4; of a watch-tower in a vineyard (Isa.5:2): Mat.21:33, Mrk.12:1, and prob., Luk.14:28.† (AS)

purinos.notes: πύρινος, -η, -ον [in LXX: Eze.28:14, 16 (אֵשׁ), Sir.48:9 * ;] fiery: Rev.9:17.† (AS)

puroo.notes: πυρόω, -ῶ (πῦρ) [in LXX chiefly for צָרַף ;] to set on fire, burn up. In NT always pass., __1. to be set on fire, to burn: Eph.6:16, 2Pe.3:12; ptcp., glowing, Rev.1:15. Metaphorical, of grief or indignation,…

purosis.notes: πύρωσις, -εως, ἡ (πυρόω), [in LXX: Pro.27:21 (כּוּר), Amo.4:9 (שְׁדֵפָה) * ;] __1. a burning: Rev.18:9, 18. __2. a refining or trial by fire: metaphorically, 1Pe.4:12.† (AS)

purrazo.notes: πυρράζω (πυρρός), [in LXX, πυρρίζω: Lev.13:19, 42 ff. Lev.14:37 (אֲדַמְדָּם) * ;] to be fiery red: Mat.16:2-3.† (AS)

purros.notes: πυρρός -ά, -όν (πῦρ) [in LXX for אֱדֹם ;] fiery red: Rev.6:4 12:3.† Πύρρος, -ου, ὁ, Pyrrhus: Act.20:4.† (AS)

puthon.notes: πύθων, -ωνος, ὁ __1. in cl., Python, a serpent slain by Apollo, who is hence surnamed the Pythian. __2. In Plut. (ii, 414 E), a name given to ventriloquist soothsayers (ἐγγαστρίμυθοι; cf. Lev.19:31 20:6, 27,…

rhaab.notes: Ῥαάβ (and Ραχάβ, Mat.1:5; Ραχάβη, -ης, in FlJ), ἡ, indecl. (Heb. רָחָב) Rahab (LXX, Jos.2:1, al.): Heb.11:31, Jas.2:25.† (AS)

rhabbi.notes: ῥαββεί (Rec. -βί, see WH, App., 155) (Heb. and Aram. רַב, my master; see Dalman, Words, 327, 331 ff.), a title of respectful address to Jewish teachers, Rabbi: Mat.23:7-8; of John, Jhn.3:26; of Christ, Mat.26:25, 49,…

rhabboni.notes: ῥαββουνεί (Rec. -βονί, see: ῥαββεί) (Aram. רַבּוֺנִי, later, רִבּוֺנִי my master; on the Greek vocalization and the relation of the word to ῥαββεί, see Dalman, Words, 324, 340; Gr., 140; DB, iv, 190) Rabboni:…

rhabdizo.notes: ῥαβδίζω (ῥάβδος), [in LXX: Jdg.6:11 Rut.2:17 (חָבַט) * ;] to beat with a rod: Act.16:22, 2Co.11:25.† (AS)

rhabdos.notes: ῥάβδος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for מַטֶּה (e.g. Gen.47:31, MT, מַטֶּה, bed), also for מַקֵּל, שֵׁבֶט, etc. ;] a staff, rod: Heb.9:4, Rev.11:1; in particular, __(a) a staff, such as is used on a journey: Mat.10:10,…

rhabdouchos.notes: ῥαβδοῦχος, -ου, ό (ῥαβδος, ἔχω), one who carries a rod or staff of office; __(a) an umpire or judge (Plat.); __(b) in late writers, a Roman lictor: Act.16:38.† (AS)

rhachab.notes: A woman living before Israel's Monarchy, first mentioned at Jos.2.1; mother of: Boaz (H1162)(?) § Rahab, "spacious, broad" Rachab, (Proper name), indeclinable, Mt. 1:5*

rhachel.notes: Ῥαχήλ, ἡ indecl. (Heb. רָחֵל), Rachel: Mat.2:18 (LXX).† (AS)

rhadiourgema.notes: ῥᾳδιούργημα, -τος, τό (ῥᾳδιουργέω, to act recklessly or wrongly) __1. a reckless act. __2. crime, villany: Act.18:14.† (AS)

rhadiourgia.notes: ῥᾳδιουργία, -ας, ἡ (see: ῥᾳδιούργημα), __1. ease in doing, facility (Xen.). __2. easiness, laziness (Xen.). __3. recklessness, wickedness (of lewdness, Xen.; fraud, Plut.; in π., of theft, MM, xxii): Act.13:10.† (AS)

rhagau.notes: Ῥαγαύ (Rec. -αῦ), ὁ, indecl. (LXX for Heb. רְעוּ, Gen.11:18, al.), Reu: Luk.3:35.† (AS)

rhaka.notes: ῥακά (T, ῥαχά), usually taken to represent the Aram. רֵיקָא, a shortened form of H7386, " empty," as vocalized in the Galilæan dialect; an expression of contempt, raca: Mat.5:22 (cf. DB, iv, 191 f.; and for other…

rhakos.notes: ῥάκος, -ους, τό [in LXX: Isa.64:6 (5) (בֶּגֶד), Jer.38:11 (סְחָבָה), Est.4:17 * ;] __1. a ragged garment (Hom.). __2. a rag, remnant, piece of cloth: Mat.9:16, Mrk.2:21.† (AS)

rhama.notes: Ῥαμά (Rec. -ᾶ), ἡ, indecl. (Heb. רָמָה), Ramah: Mat.2:18 (LXX).† (AS)

rhantismos.notes: ῥαντισμός, -ῦo, ὁ (ῥαντίζω), [in LXX for נִדָּה ;] sprinkling: of the ceremonial sprinkling of blood for purification, Heb.12:24, 1Pe.1:2 (see Hort, Pe., 23 ff.).† (AS)

rhantizo.notes: ῥαντίζω [in LXX: Lev.6:27 (20), 4Ki.9:33 (נָזָה), Psa.51:7 (חָטָא pi.) * ;] = cl., ῥαίνω, to sprinkle, besprinkle: with accusative, Heb.9:13, 19; id. and dative, Heb.9:21; pass., Rev.19:13, WH; of cleansing by…

rhaphis.notes: ῥαφίς, -ίδος, ἡ (ῥάπτω, to sew), a needle: Mat.19:24, Mrk.10:25 (cf. βελόνη).† (AS)

rhapisma.notes: a stroke, a slap on the face , (Lucian) (ML)

rhapizo.notes: ῥαπίζω (ῥαπίς, a rod), [in LXX: Jdg.16:25, Heb.11:4, I Est.4:11 * ;] __1. prop., to strike with a rod. __2. In late writers, to strike (the face) with the palm of the hand: Mat.26:67; with accusative of person(s),…

rhebekka.notes: Ῥεβέκκα, -ας (see Bl., § 7, 2), ἡ, (Heb. רִבְקָה), Rebecca: Rom.9:10.† (AS)

rheda.notes: ῥέδη (see WH, App., 151), -ης, ἡ (a Gallic word), a chariot: Rev.18:13.† (AS)

rhegion.notes: Ῥήγιον, -ου, τό Rhegium, a town in Sicily: Act.28:13.† (AS)

rhegma.notes: ῥῆγμα, -τος, τό (ῥήγνυμι), [in LXX: 3Ki.11:30-31 12:24, 4Ki.2:12 (קֶרַע), Amo.6:12 (11) A (בְּקִיעַ) * ;] __1. cl. (and so in LXX), a fracture; then by meton., that which is torn. __2. In NT, ruin: Luk.6:49.† (AS)

rhegnumi.notes: ῥήγνυμι and (Mrk.9:18) ῥήσσω, [in LXX chiefly for בָּקַע, also for קָרַע, etc. ;] __1. to rend, break asunder: Mat.7:6, Mrk.2:22, Luk.5:37; pass., Mat.9:17. __2. Of the voice, with accusative, φωνήν, etc. (Hdt.,…

rhema.notes: ῥῆμα, -τος, τό [in LXX chiefly for דָּבָר, also for פֶּה, and Aram. פִּתְגָּם, etc. ;] __1. prop., of that which is said or spoken, __(a) a word: Mat.27:14, 2Co.12:4; pl., τὰ ῥ., of speech, discourse, Luk.7:1,…

rhemphanrhaiphan.notes: Ῥεμφάν, Ῥεφάς, see: Ῥομφά. Ῥομφά (-άν, T; Ῥεφάν, LTr.; Ῥεμφάν, Rec.; see WH, App., 92), [in LXX: Amo.5:26 (Ῥαιμφάν or Ῥεφάν, Heb. כִּיּוּן)* ;] Rompha, Rephan (RV), one of the names of Seb, the Egyptian Saturn:…

rheo.notes: ῥέω [in LXX chiefly for זוּב ;] to flow: Jhn.7:38 (cf. παραρρέω).† (AS)

rhetor.notes: ῥήτωρ, -ορος, ὁ a public speaker, an orator: Act.24:1.† (AS)

rhetos.notes: ῥητῶς adv. (ῥητός, stated, specified), in stated terms, expressly: 1Ti.4:1.† (AS)

rhipe.notes: ῥιπή, ῆς, ἡ (ῥίπτω), poët. in cl., any rapid movement such as the throw or flight of a javelin, the rush of wind or flame, the flapping of wings, the twinkling of lights; ῥ. ὀφθαλμοῦ, the twinkling of an eye:…

rhipizo.notes: ῥιπίζω (ῥιπίς, a fan) [in LXX: Dan LXX 2:35 (נְשָׂא) * ;] primarily, to fan a fire, hence generally, to make a breeze (Plut., al.). In pass. (cf. Philo, de inter. round., 24), to be tossed or blown by the wind: of…

rhipteo.notes: ῥιπτέω, see: ῥίπτω. ῥίπτω and (Act.22:23) ῥιπτέω (strengthened form; see Veitch, see word ῥίπτω, fin.), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁלַךְ hi. ;] __1. to throw, cast, hurl: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.27:5, Act.27:19,…

rhipto.notes: ῥίπτω and (Act.22:23) ῥιπτέω (strengthened form; see Veitch, see word ῥίπτω, fin.), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁלַךְ hi. ;] __1. to throw, cast, hurl: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.27:5, Act.27:19, 29; with accusative…

rhiza.notes: ῥίζα, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for שָׁרַשׁ ;] a root: Mat.3:10, Luk.3:9; ἐκ ῥιζῶν, Mrk.11:20; ῥ. ἔχειν, Mat.13:6, Mrk.4:6; id. before ἐν ἑαυτῷ, figuratively, Mat.13:21, Mrk.4:17, Luk.8:13. Metaphorical (as in various…

rhizoo.notes: ῥιζόω, -ῶ (ῥίζα), [in LXX: Isa.40:24, Jer.12:2 (שָׁרַשׁ), Sir.3:28 24:12 * ;] to cause to take root. Metaphorical, to plant, fix firmly, establish: pass. (EV, rooted), ἐν ἀγάπῃ, Eph.3:18(17); ἐν Χριστῷ, Col.2:7 (cf.…

rhoboam.notes: Ῥοβοάμ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. רְחַבְעָם), Rehoboam: Mat.1:7.† (AS)

rhodos.notes: the isle of Rhodes , (Iliad by Homer), etc. (ML)

rhoizedon.notes: ῥοιζηδόν adv. (ῥοῖζος, the whistling of an arrow), with rushing sound (as of roaring flames): 2Pe.3:10.† (AS)

rhomphaia.notes: ῥομφαία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for חֶרֶב ;] a large broad sword, used by the Thracians (see DB, iv, 634); then generally (in LXX used interchangeably with μάχαιρα, which see), a sword: Rev.1:16 2:12, 16 6:8 19:15,…

rhonnumi.notes: ῥώννυμι [in LXX: 2Ma.9:20 2Mac 11:21 2Mac 11:28 2Mac 11:33 2Mac 11:1-38; 3Ma.6:1-41 * ;] to strengthen; most frequently in pf., ἔρρωμαι, to put forth strength, be strong, hence, often in imperat., ἔρρωσο, ἔρρωσθε,…

rhouben.notes: Ῥουβήν, ὁ indecl., (Heb. רְאוּבֵן), Reuben: Rev.7:5.† (AS)

rhouth.notes: Ῥoύθ, ἡ indecl. (Heb. רוּת), Ruth: Mat.15:1-39.† (AS)

rhume.notes: ῥύμη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Isa.15:3 (רְחֹב), Pro.31:23 א, Tob.13:18, Sir.9:7 א1* ;] __1. in cl., the force, rush, swing, of a moving body; esp. of a charge of soldiers. __2. In late Greek (as in Macedonian, see Kennedy,…

rhuomai.notes: ῥύομαι [in LXX chiefly for נָצַל hi., also for גָּאַל, פָּלַט pi., etc. ;] to draw to oneself, hence, to rescue, deliver: with accusative of person(s), Mat.27:43, 2Pe.2:7; id. before ἀπό, Mat.6:13, Luk.11:4 (Rec., R,…

rhuparia.notes: ῥυπαρία, -ας, ἡ (ῥυπαρός) filthiness: metaphorically, of moral defile­ment, Jas.1:21.† (AS)

rhuparos.notes: ῥυπαρός, -ά, -όν (ῥύπος), [in LXX: Zec.3:4-5, (3-4) (צוֹא)* ;] filthy, dirty: of old, shabby clothing (Za, l.with), Jas.2:2; metaphorically, of moral defilement, Rev.22:11.† (AS)

rhupoo.notes: ῥυπόω, -ῶ (ῥύπος), to make filthy: Rev.22:11 Rec. (AV, tr. as = ῥυπάω, to be filthy).† (AS)

rhupos.notes: ῥύπος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Isa.4:4 (צוֹאָה), Job.14:4 (טָמֵא), etc. ;] dirt, filth: 1Pe.3:21.† (AS)

rhusis.notes: ῥύσις, -εως, ἡ (ῥέω), [in LXX chiefly for זוֹב ;] a flowing, issue: τ. αἵματος, Mrk.5:25, Luk.8:43-44.† (AS)

rhutis.notes: a fold or pucker in the face, a wrinkle , Lat. ruga, (Aristophanes Comicus) (ML)

sabachthani.notes: σαβαχθανεί (Rec. -νί; ζαφθανεί, Mt, l.with, WH, mg.) (Aram. שְׁבַקְתַּנִי), sabachthani, i.e. thou host forsaken me: Mat.27:46, Mrk.15:34 (Psa.22:2, Heb. עָזַב).† ζαφθανεί (cf. Heb. עָזַב), zaphthanei: Mat.27:46 (WH,…

sabaoth.notes: σαβαώθ indecl., [in LXX for צָבָא, chiefly in Isaiah (in other places, the phrase is also rendered by κύριος παντοκράτωρ, κ. τῶν δυνάμεων; see DB, iii, 137 f.) ;] Sabaoth, i.e. hosts or armies (see DB, l.with):…

sabbatismos.notes: σαββατισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (σαββατίζω, to keep the sabbath, Exo.16:30, al.), a keeping sabbath, a sabbath rest: metaphorically, as in Mishna (Zorell, see word), Heb.4:9.† (AS)

sabbaton.notes: σάββατον, -ου, τό (Aram. שַׁבָּתָא, transliterated σάββατα, and this being mistaken for a pl., the sing. σάββατον was formed from it), and σάββατα, -ων, τά [in LXX for שַׁבָּת, שַׁבָּתוֹן ;] __1. the seventh day of…

sadok.notes: Σαδώκ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. צָדוֹק, see supr., see word Σαδδουκαῖος) Sadoc, Zadok: Mat.1:14.† (AS)

sagene.notes: σαγήνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for חֵרֶם ;] a drag-net, seine: Mat.13:47.† SYN.: ἀμφίβληστρον (which see), δίκτυον (AS)

saino.notes: σαίνω __1. prop. (Hom., al.), of dogs, to wag the tail, fawn. __2. Metaphorical, of persons, with accusative, to fawn upon, flatter, beguile: pass., 1Th.3:3 (for conjectural emendations of the text, see ICC, and M,…

sakkos.notes: σάκκος also written σάκος, -ου, ὁ (cf. Heb. שַׂק, which it renders in LXX), __1. a coarse cloth, sackcloth, usually made of hair: Rev.6:12. __2. Anything made of sackcloth; __(a) a sack (Gen.42:25, al.); __(b) a…

sala.notes: Σαλά, ὁ indecl. (Heb. שֶׁלַח), Sala, Shalah: Luk.3:32 (R, txt., Σαλμών, which see), Luk.3:35.† (AS)

salamis.notes: Σαλαμίς, -ῖνσς, ἡ Salamis, the chief city of Cyprus: Act.13:5.† (AS)

salathiel.notes: Σαλαθιήλ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. שְׁאַלְתִּיאֵל), Salathiel: Mat.1:12, Luk.3:27.† (AS)

saleim.notes: Σαλείμ, τό indecl., Salim: Jhn.3:23 (see Westc., in l; DB, iii, 354).† (AS)

salem.notes: Σαλήμ, ἡ (Heb. שָׁלֵם Gen.14:18), Salem: Heb.7:1-2 (LXX).† (AS)

saleuo.notes: σαλεύω (σάλος), [in LXX for מוֹט, זוּעַ, etc. ;] prop., of the action of wind, storm, etc., to agitate, shake: of a reed, Mat.11:7, Luk.7:24; a house, Luk.6:48, Act.4:31 16:26; the earth, Heb.12:26; the heavenly…

salmon.notes: Σαλμών, ὁ indecl. (Heb. שַׂלְמוֹן), Salmon: Mat.1:4-5, Luk.3:32 (Σαλά, WH, R, mg.).† (AS)

salmone.notes: Σαλμώνη, -ης, ἡ Salmone, a promontory of Crete: Act.27:7.† (AS)

salos.notes: σάλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for מוֹט, etc. ;] in poets and late prose, a tossing, as of an earthquake; esp. the tossing, the rolling swell of the sea: Luk.21:25.† (AS)

salpigx.notes: σάλπιγξ, -ιγγος, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for שׁוֹפָר, חֲצֹצְרָה ;] a trumpet, used in war and in religious ceremonies: 1Co.14:8, Heb.12:19, Rev.1:10 4:1 8:2, 6 8:11 9:14 By meton., a trumpet blast: μετὰ σ. μεγάλης,…

salpistes.notes: σαλπιστής (so Inscr. and late writers for Mt. σαλπιγκτής), οῦ, ὁ, a trumpeter: Rev.18:22.† (AS)

salpizo.notes: σαλπίζω [in LXX chiefly for תָּקַע ;] to sound a trumpet: Rev.8:6 ff. Rev.9:1, 13 10:7 11:15; metaphorically, Mat.6:2. Impers., the trumpet sounds: 1Co.15:52.† (AS)

samareia.notes: Σαμαρία (Rec. -άρεια), -ας, ἡ (Heb. שֹׁמְרוֹן; Aram. שָׁמְרַיִן), Samaria; __(a) the city: Act.8:5 (and perhaps also Act.8:9, 14); __(b) the region: Luk.17:11, Jhn.4:4-5, 7, Act.1:8 8:1 (and perhaps also Act.8:9, 14)…

samareites.notes: Σαμαρείτης (-ίτης, Τ; see WH, App., 154; BI., § 3, 4), -ον, ό, a Samaritan, i.e. an inhabitant of the city or the region of Samaria, in NT always the latter (see: Σαμαρία): Mat.10:5, Luk.9:52 10:33 17:16, Jhn.4:9,…

samareitis.notes: Σαμαρεῖτις (-ῖτις, T, see supr.), -ιδος, ἡ __1. in FlJ (B.J., i, 21, 2, al.), the region of Samaria. __2. a Samaritan woman: Jhn.4:9.† (AS)

samos.notes: Σάμος, -ου, ἡ Samos, an island in the Ægean: Act.20:15.† (AS)

samothrake.notes: Σαμοθρᾴκη (-θράκη, BE), -ης, ἡ Samothrace, an island in the Ægean: Act.16:11.† (AS)

samouel.notes: Σαμουήλ, ὁ indecl. (in FlJ, Σαμούηλος; Heb. שְׁמוּאֵל), Samuel (1Ki.1:20, al.): Act.3:24 13:20, Heb.11:32.† (AS)

sampson.notes: Σαμψών, ὁ indecl. (Heb. שִׁמְשׁוֹן), Samson (Jdg.13:1-25 ff.): Heb.11:32.† (AS)

sandalion.notes: σανδάλιον, -ου, τό (dimin. of σάνδαλον, prob. Persian), [in LXX: Jos.9:5, Isa.20:2 (נָעַל, elsewhere rendered ὑπόδημα, which see), Jdth.10:4 16:9 * ;] a sandal: Mrk.6:9, Act.12:8.† (AS)

sanis.notes: σανίς, -ίδος, ἡ [in LXX: Sng.8:9, Eze.27:5 (לוּחַ) * ;] a board, plank: Act.27:44 (AS)

saoul.notes: Σαούλ, ὁ indecl. (in FlJ, Σάουλος; Heb. שָׁאוּל) Saul; __(a) the King of Israel: Act.13:21; __(b) the Jewish name of the Apostle Paul, used in the indecl. form only in address (cf. Σαῦλος): Act.9:4, 17 22:7, 13…

sappheire.notes: Σαπφείρα, -ης (see B1., § 3, 4), ἡ (Aram. שַׁפִּירָא) Sapphia: Act.5:1.† (AS)

sappheiros.notes: σάπφειρος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX for סַפִּיר ;] sapphire (perh. = lapis lazuli): Rev.21:19.† (AS)

sapros.notes: σαπρός, -ά -όν [in Sm.: Lev.27:14, 33 * ;] rotten, corrupt, bad, worth­less: of trees and fruit (opposite to ἀγαθός, καλός), Mat.7:17-18 12:33 Luk.6:43; of fish, Mat.13:48. Metaphorical, in moral sense: λόγος σ.,…

sardeis.notes: Σάρδεις, -εων, αἱ Sardis, the chief city of Lydia: Rev.1:11 3:1, 4 (AS)

sardion.notes: σάρδιον, -ον, τ [in LXX for אֹדֶם, Exo.28:17, al.; also for שֹׁהַם ;] the sardian stone, sard (of which carnelian is one variety): Rev.4:3 21:20.† (AS)

sardonux.notes: σαρδόνυξ (L, σαρδιόνυξ), -υχος, ὁ (σάρδιον, ὄνυξ), sardonyx, a stone marked by the red of the sard and the white of the onyx: Rev.21:20.† (AS)

sarepta.notes: Σάρεπτα, -ων (Ob 20), τά (Heb. צָרְפַת), Sarepta, a city of Sidon: Luk.4:26.† (AS)

sargane.notes: σαργάνη, -ης, ἡ __1. a plaited rope (Æsch.). __2. a hamper, a basket made of ropes: 2Co.11:33.† (AS)

sarkikos.notes: σαρκικός, -ή, -όν (σάρξ) (seel. for σάρκινος, Arist., H.A., x, 2, 7) = Lat. carnalis, i.e. __(a) associated with or pertaining to the flesh, fleshly, carnal: Rom.15:27, 1Co.9:11; __(b) in a more ethical sense, of the…

sarkinos.notes: σάρκινος, -η, -ον (σάρξ), [in LXX: 2Ch.32:8, Eze.11:19 36:26 (בָּשָׂר), Est.4:17, Pro.24:23 (Pro.29:27) * ;] = Lat. carneus (the termination -ινος denoting the substance or material of a thing; see Tr., Syn., §…

saron.notes: Σάρων, -ωνος (accusative -ῶνα, WH), ὁ (Heb. שָׁרוֹן), the plain of Sharon: Act.9:35.† (AS)

saroo.notes: σαρόω, -ῶ late form of σαίρω, to sweep: with accusative, Luk.15:8; pass., Mat.12:44, Luk.11:25.† (AS)

sarouch.notes: Σερούχ (Rec. Σαρ-), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. שְׂרוּג), Serug: Luk.3:35.† (AS)

sarra.notes: Σάρρα, -ας, ἡ (Heb. שָׂרָה, Gen.17:15), Sarah: Rom.4:19 9:9, Heb.11:11, 1Pe.3:6.† (AS)

sarx.notes: σάρξ, σαρκός, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for בָּשָׂר ;] flesh; __1. as in cl. generally, __(a) prop., of the soft substance of the animal body: 1Co.15:39, 2Co.12:7, Gal.6:13, al.; σ. καὶ αἷμα, 1Co.5:50; σ. καὶ ὀστέα,…

satanas.notes: Σατανᾶς, -ᾶ (so also Sir 21:30, but in 3Ki.11:14, σατάν indecl.; Heb. שָׂטָן; Aram. סָטָנָא, whence the inflected Gk. form), ό, Satan (i.e. the adversary, as in LXX, ll. with): Mat.4:10 12:26, Mrk.1:13 3:23, 26 4:15…

saton.notes: σάτον, -ου, τό (Aram. סָאתָא = Heb. סְאָה), [in LXX: Hag.2:17 (16)* ;] a Hebrew measure (= about a peck and a half): Mat.13:33, Luk.13:21.† (AS)

saulos.notes: Σαῦλος, -ου, ὁ (Hellenized form of Σαούλ, which see), Saul, the Jewish name of the Apostle Paul: Act.7:58 8:1, 3 9:1, 8 9:11, 22 9:24 11:25, 30 12:25 13:1-2, 7 13:9.† (AS)

sbennumi.notes: σβέννυμι [in LXX chiefly for כָּבָה ;] of fire or things on fire, to quench: with accusative, Mat.12:20, Eph.6:16, Heb.11:34; pass., Mat.25:8, Mrk.9:48, [44, 46]. Metaphorical (cf. Sng.8:7, 4Ma.16:4): τὸ πνεῦμα,…

schedon.notes: σχέδον adv. (ἔχω), [in LXX: 2Ma.5:2, 3Ma.5:14 3Mac 5:45 * ;] __1. of place, near. __2. Of degree, almost, nearly: Act.13:44 19:26, Heb.9:22.† (AS)

schema.notes: σχῆμα, -τος, τό (ἔχω), [in LXX: Isa.3:17 (פֹּת) * ;] figure, fashion: 1Co.7:31, Php.2:8.† SYN.: see: μορφή (AS)

schisma.notes: σχίσμα, -τος, τό (σχίζω), a rent (Arist., al.): Mat.9:16, Mrk.2:21. Metaphorical, a dissension, division: Jhn.7:43 9:16 10:19, 1Co.1:10 11:18 12:25.† SYN.: see: αἵρεσις (AS)

schizo.notes: σχίζω [in LXX chiefly for בָּקַע ;] to cleave, rend: Mat.27:51, Mrk.1:10 15:38, Luk.5:36 23:45, Jhn.19:24 21:11; metaphorically, in pass., to be divided into factions, Act.14:4 23:7.† (AS)

schoinion.notes: σχοινίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of σχοῖνος, a rush), [in LXX chiefly for a rope (prop., one made of rushes): Jhn.2:15, Act.27:32.† (AS)

scholazo.notes: σχολάζω (σχολή), [in LXX: Exo.5:8, 17, Psa.46:10 (רָפָה ni., hi.) * ;] to be at leisure, hence, to have time or opportunity for, to devote oneself to, be occupied in: 1Co.7:5; of things, to be unoccupied, empty…

schole.notes: σχολή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Gen.33:14 (κατὰ σ., אַט), Pro.28:19, Sir.38:24 * ;] __1. leisure. __2. Later (from Plato on), __(a) that for which leisure is employed, a disputation, lecture; __(b) the place where lectures…

sebasma.notes: σέβασμα, -τος, τό (σεβάζομαι), [in LXX: Wis.14:20 15:17, Da TH Bel 1:27 * ;] an object of worship: Act.17:23, 2Th.2:4.† (AS)

sebastos.notes: σεβαστός, -ή, -όν (σεβάζομαι) __1. reverend, august. __2. In late writers, with reference to the Roman Imperial name; __(a) ὁ Σ., Augustus, i.e. the Roman Emperor: Act.25:21, 25; __(b) Augustan: σπείρα σ., Act.27:1…

sebazomai.notes: σεβάζομαι (σέβας, reverential awe), [in Aq.: Hos.10:5 * ;] __1. to fear (Hom). __2. In later writers, = σέβομαι, to worship: Rom.1:25 (AS)

sebomai.notes: σέβω [in LXX (chiefly for יָרֵא, Jos.4:24, al.) and NT always mid., σέβομαι, exc. 4Ma.5:24 ;] to worship: with accusative of person(s), Mat.15:9, Mrk.7:7, Act.18:13 19:27; σεβόμενος τ. θεόν, Act.16:14 18:7; σεβόμενοι…

seio.notes: σείω [in LXX chiefly for רָעַשׁ ;] to shake, move to and fro: τ. γῆν, Heb.12:26 (LXX); pass., ἡ γῆ, Mat.27:51 (LXX); συκῆ, Rev.6:13. Metaphorical, to agitate, stir up, with fear or some other emotion: Mat.21:10 28:4…

seira.notes: σειρά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX: Jdg.16:13-14, 19 (מַחְלָפָה), Pro.5:22 (חֶבֶל) * ;] __1. (a) a cord; __(b) a chain (cf. Pr., l.with): σειραὶ ζόφου, 2Pe.2:4, Rec., R, mg. (see: σειρός). __2. a lock of hair (Jg, ll. with).† (AS)

seismos.notes: σεισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (σείω), [in LXX chiefly for רַעַשׁ ;] a shaking, commotion, as a tempest at sea: Mat.8:24; esp. an earthquake: Mat.24:7 27:54 28:2, Mrk.13:8, Luk.21:11, Act.16:26, Rev.6:12 8:5 11:13, 19 16:18.† (AS)

selene.notes: σελήνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for יָרֵחַ (Gen.37:9, al.), also for לְבָנָה; (Sng.6:9(10), ?Isa.24:23 30:26) ;] the moon: Mat.24:29, Mrk.13:24, Luk.21:25, Act.2:20" (LXX), 1Co.15:41, Rev.6:12 8:12 12:1 21:23.† (AS)

seleniazomai.notes: σεληνιάζω (σελήνη), act, in Manetho (Caren., 4, 81), in NT depon. -ομαι, to be moonstruck, i.e. epileptic (epilepsy being supposed to be influenced by the moon): Mat.4:24 17:15.† (AS)

seleukeia.notes: Σελευκία (Rec. -εύκεια), Seleucia, a city of Syria: Act.13:4.† (AS)

sem.notes: Σήμ, ὁ indecl. (Heb. שֵׁם), Shem: Luk.3:36.† (AS)

semaino.notes: σημαίνω (σῆμα, a sign), [in LXX for רוּעַ hi., תָּקַע, etc. ;] to give a sign, signify, indicate: with accusative of thing(s), Act.25:27, Rev.1:1 (cf. MM, xxii); with accusative and inf., Act.11:28; before quæst.…

semei.notes: Σεμεείν (Rec. Σεμεΐ), ὁ, indecl., Semein: Luk.3:26.† (AS)

semeion.notes: σημεῖον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for אוֹת ;] a sign, mark, token; __(a) of that which distinguishes a person or thing from others: Mat.26:48 Luk.2:12, 2Th.3:17 (cf. Deiss., LAE, 153.2); before genitive epexeg.,…

semeioo.notes: σημειόω, -ῶ (σημεῖον), [in LXX: Psa.4:6 (נָשָׂא)* ;] to mark, note. Mid., to note for oneself: 2Th.3:14 (frequently in π.; see ICC, M, Th., in l).† (AS)

semeron.notes: σήμερον (Att.. τήμερον), adv., [in LXX for יוֹם ;] to-day: Mat.6:11, Luk.4:21, Act.4:9, al.; opposite to αὔριον, Mat.6:30, Luk.12:28, Jas.4:13; χθὲς καὶ σ. καὶ εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας, Heb.13:8; ἡ σ. ἡμέρα, Act.20:20; ἕως…

semidalis.notes: σεμίδαλις, -εως, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for סֹלֶת ;] fine wheaten flour: Rev.18:13.† (AS)

semnos.notes: σεμνός, -ή, -όν (σέβομαι), [in LXX: Pro.6:8 8:6 15:26, 2Ma.6:11 2Mac 6:28 2Mac 8:15, 4Ma.5:36 4Mac 7:15 4Mac 17:5 * ;] __1. reverend, august, venerable, in cl. of the gods and also of human beings. __2. grave,…

semnotes.notes: σεμνότης, -ητος, ἡ (σεμνός), [in LXX: 2Ma.3:12 * ;] gravity: 1Ti.2:2 3:4 (Vg. eastitas, cf. Soph., Lex., and Zorell, see word), Tit.2:7.† (AS)

sepo.notes: σήπω [in LXX; Psa.38:5 (מָקַק ni.), Job.33:21 (כָּלָה) Job.40:7, Sir.14:19, al. ;] to make corrupt: 2 pf. act, with mid, sense (see M, Pr., 154), σέσηπεν, has perished, become corrupted, Jas.5:2.† (AS)

sergios.notes: Σέργιος, -ου, ὁ Sergius, surnamed Paulus: Act.13:7.† (AS)

serikos.notes: σιρικός (by assimilation of vowels, for σηρικός; see Mayser, 150; WH, Notes, 151), -ή, -όν (οἱ Σῆρες, a people of India from whom the ancients got the first silk), silk, silken; as subst., τὸ σ., silken fabric, silk:…

ses.notes: σής σητός (late genitive for cl. σεός), ὁ, [in LXX: Job.4:19, Isa.50:9 (עָשׁ), Isa.51:8 (סָס), al. ;] a moth, clothes moth: Mat.6:19-20, Luk.12:33.† (AS)

seth.notes: Σήθ, ὁ (Heb. שֵׁת), indecl., Seth (Gen.4:25): Luk.3:38.† (AS)

setobrotos.notes: σητό-βρωτος, -ον (οής, βιβρώσκω), [in LXX: Job.13:28 (אָכַל עָשׁ) * ;] moth-eaten: Jas.5:2.† (AS)

siagon.notes: σιαγών, -όνος, ἡ [in LXX for לְחִי ;] the jawbone, jaw, cheek: Mat.5:39, Luk.6:29 (cf. MM, xxii).† (AS)

sidereos.notes: σιδήρεος, -α, -ον (-οῦς, -ᾶ -οῦν) (σίδηρος), [in LXX chiefly for בַּרְזֶל ;] of iron: Act.12:10, Rev.2:27 9:9 12:5 19:15.† (AS)

sideros.notes: σίδηρος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for בַּרְזֶל ;] iron: Rev.18:12.† (AS)

sidon.notes: Σιδών, -ῶnος, ἡ (Heb. צִידוֹן), Sidon, a maritime city of Phœnicia: Mat.11:21-22 15:21, Mrk.3:8 7:24, 31, Luk.6:17 10:13-14, Act.27:3.† (AS)

sidonios.notes: Σιδώνιος, -α, -ον (Σιδών), of Sidon, Sidonian; __(a) the region: sc. χώρα, Luk.4:26; __(b) the people: Act.12:20.† (AS)

sigao.notes: σιγάω, -ῶ (σιγή), [in LXX for חָרַשׁ, חָשָׁה, etc. ;] __1. intrans., to be silent, keep silence: Luk.9:36 18:39 20:26, Act.12:17 15:12-13, 1Co.14:28 14:30 14:34. __2. Trans., to keep secret; pass., to be kept secret:…

sige.notes: σιγή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: Wis.18:14, 3Ma.3:23 * ;] silence: Act.21:40, Rev.8:1.† (AS)

sikarios.notes: σικάριος, -ου, ὁ (Lat.; sica, a dagger carried under their clothing by the Sicarii), a bandit, assassin, one of the Sicarii (FlJ, B.J., ii, 17, 6, al.): Act.21:38.† (AS)

sikera.notes: σίκερα, τό indecl. (Aram. שִׁכְרָא), [in LXX for שֵׁכָר (also rendered μέθυσμα, Jdg.13:4, Mic.2:11), Lev.10:9, al. ;] fermented liquor, strong drink: Luk.1:15.† (AS)

silas.notes: Σίλας, -α (accusative to BL, § 29, W-Schm., 74, -ᾶς, -ᾶ), ὁ (Aram. שׁאִילָא; see Dalman, Gr., 157.5), Silas (called also Σιλουανός, which see): Act.15:22, 27 15:32, 34 15:40 16:19, 25 16:29 17:4, 10 17:14-15 18:5 † (AS)

siloam.notes: Σιλωάμ (indecl., but in FlJ, genitive, ᾶ, B.J., ii, 16, 2), ὁ (Heb. שִׁלֹחַ), Siloam (see DB, iii, 515 f.): Luk.13:4, Jhn.9:7, 11.† (AS)

silouanos.notes: Σιλουανός (in MSS also Σιλβανός, a form frequently in π.), -οῦ, ὁ, Silvanus, latinized name of Silas: 2Co.1:19, 1Th.1:1, 2Th.1:1, 1Pe.5:12.† (AS)

simikinthion.notes: σιμικίνθιον (also written σημι-), -ου, τό (Lat. semicinctium), a work­man's apron: Act.19:12.† (AS)

simon.notes: Σίμων, -ωνος, ὁ a Greek name (transliterated סִימוֺן in Heb.) used as a substitute for Συμεών (which see), Simon __1. Simon Peter: Mat.17:25, Mrk.1:29, al. __2. Simon the Zealot (see: ζηλωτής, Καναναῖος): Mat.10:4,…

sina.notes: Σινά (-ᾶ, Rec.), indecl. (but τὸ Σιναῖον, FlJ, Ant., iii, 5, 1; τὸ Σιναῖον ὄρος, ib. ii, 12, 1), τό (Heb. סִינַי), Sinai (Exo.19:11, al.): Act.7:30, 38, Gal.4:24-25† (AS)

sinapi.notes: σίναπι, -εως, τό (prob. Egyptian), = Attic τὸ νᾶπυ mustard: Mat.13:31 17:20, Mrk.4:31, Luk.13:19 17:6.† (AS)

sindon.notes: σινδών, -όνος, ἡ [in LXX: Jdg.14:12-13 A, Pro.31:24 (סָדִין), 1Ma.10:64 A * ;] fine linen cloth; __(a) as used for swathing dead bodies (cf. Hdt., ii, 86): Mat.27:59, Mrk.15:46, Luk.23:53; __(b) a garment or wrap of…

siniazo.notes: σινιάω (σίνιον, a sieve), = σήθω, to sift, winnow: figuratively, Luk.22:31.† (AS)

sion.notes: Σιών indecl., in NT anarth., but in LXX when used of the city of Jerusalem, ἡ Σ. (Heb. צִיּוֹן), Zion; __1. the mountain: in typical sense, of the Church, Heb.12:22; of heaven, Rev.14:1. __2. The city, i.e.…

siopao.notes: σιωπάω, -ῶ (σιωπή, silence) [in LXX for חָשָׁה, חָרַשׁ hi., etc. ;] to be silent or still, keep silence: Mat.20:31 26:63, Mrk.3:4 9:34 10:48 14:61, Luk.19:4, Act.18:9; of one dumb, Luk.1:20 (cf. 4Ma.10:18); addressed…

siteutos.notes: σιτευτός, -ή, -όν (σιτεύω, to feed, fatten), [in LXX: 3Ki.4:23 (אָבַס), etc. ;] fattened: Luk.15:23, 27 15:30.† (AS)

sitistos.notes: σιτιστός, -ή, -όν (σιτίζω, to fatten) [in Sm.: Psa.22:13, Jer.46:21 * ;] = σιτευτός, fattened; as subst., pl., τὰ σ., fatlings: Mat.22:4.† (AS)

sitometrion.notes: σιτομέτριον, -ου, τό (σιτομετρέω, Gen.47:12, 14 *, = Attic τὸν σῖτον μετρέω), a measured portion of food: Luk.12:42 (for exx. in π., see Deiss., BS, 158; LAE, 103.1).† (AS)

sitos.notes: σῖτος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for דָּגָן ;] wheat, corn: Mat.3:12 13:25, 29-30 Mrk.4:28, Luk.3:17 12:18 16:7 22:32, Jhn.12:24, Act.27:38, 1Co.15:37, Rev.6:6 18:13.† σιτίοι, -ου, τό (dimin. of σῖτος), [in LXX:…

skandalizo.notes: σκανδαλίζω (σκάνδαλον), [in LXX: Dan LXX 11:41 (כָּשַׁל ni.), Sir.9:5 (Sir.35:15 32:15) (יָקֹשׁ ho.) Sir.23:8 (also in Aq., Sm., Th., and in Psa Sol Psa.16:7; not elsewhere, except NT and eccl.)* ;] prop., to put a…

skandalon.notes: σκάνδαλον, -ου, τό (late form of the rare word σκανδάληθρον, see LS, see word) [in LXX chiefly for מוֹקֵשׁ, מִכְשׁוֹל ;] prop., the bait-stick of a trap, a snare, stumbling-block (Lev.19:14, Jdth.5:1): figuratively,…

skaphe.notes: σκάφη, -ης, ἡ (σκάπτω), [in LXX: Dan LXX Bel 1:32, TH Bel 1:33 * ;] anything scooped out, esp. a light boat, skiff: Act.27:16, 30 27:32.† (AS)

skapto.notes: σκάπτω [in LXX: Isa.5:6 (עֲדַר ni.)* ;] to dig: Luk.6:48 13:8 16:3.† (AS)

skelos.notes: σκέλος, -ους, τό [in LXX for רֶגֶל, etc. ;] the leg from the hip downwards: ?Jhn.19:3-33 † (AS)

skene.notes: σκηνή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for אֹהֶל, also for מִשְׁכָּן, סֻכָּה, etc. ;] a tent, booth, tabernacle: Mat.17:4, Mrk.9:5, Luk.9:33, Heb.11:9; αἱ αἰώνιοι σ., Luk.16:9; of the Mosaic tabernacle, Heb.8:5 9:2-3, 6 9:8,…

skenoma.notes: σκήνωμα, -τος, τό (σκηνόω), [in LXX chiefly for אֹהֶל ;] a tent, tabernacle (cf. σκηνή, σκῆνος): of the temple as God's dwelling (Psa.132:5, al.), Act.7:46. Metaphorical, of the body: 2Pe.1:13-14.† (AS)

skenoo.notes: σκηνόω, -ῶ (σκηνή), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁכַן ;] to have one's tabernacle, to dwell (in π. of temporary dwelling, see MM, Exp., xxii): before ἐπί, with accusative of person(s), Rev.7:15; before ἐν, with dative of…

skenopegia.notes: σκηνοπηγία, -ας, ἡ (σκηνή, πήγνυηι), [in LXX for סֻכָּה, Deu.16:16 31:10, Zec.14:16, 18-19; I Est.5:51, 1Ma.10:21, 2Ma.1:9 2Mac 1:18 * ;] prop., the setting up of tents or dwellings (Arist.); in LXX and NT, ἡ σ., ἡ…

skenopoios.notes: σκηνοποιός, -όν (σκηνή, ποιέω), making tents; as subst., ὁ σ., a tent-maker: Act.18:3.† (AS)

skenos.notes: σκῆνος, -ους, τό [in LXX: Wis.9:15 * ;] = σκηνή, a tent, tabernacle (C.1., 3071). Metaphorical, of the body as the tabernacle of the soul: 2Co.5:1 5:4.† (AS)

skepasma.notes: σκέπασμα, -τος, τό (σκεπάζω, to cover), a covering, esp. clothing: 1Ti.6:8.† (AS)

skeue.notes: σκευή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX: 3Ma.5:45 R * ;] equipment, tackle: Act.27:19.† (AS)

skeuos.notes: σκεῦος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for כְּלִי ;] a vessel, implement (for exx. in various senses, see MM, xxii): Mrk.11:16, Luk.8:16, Jhn.19:29, Act.10:11, 16 11:5, Rom.9:21, Rev.18:12; pl., 2Ti.2:20, Rev.2:27; τὰ σ.…

skia.notes: σκιά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for צֵל, 4Ki.20:9 ff., Psa.57:1, al.; but σ. and σ. θανάτου are also used for צַלְמָוֶת, Job.3:5, Psa.3:4, Isa.9:2 (1) (see ICC, in l), al. ;] __1. shadow, shade, caused by interception…

skirtao.notes: σκιρτάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Gen.25:22 (רָצַץ hithpo.), Psa.114:4, 6 (רָקַד), etc. ;] to leap: Luk.1:41, 44 6:23.† (AS)

sklerokardia.notes: σκληρο-καρδία, -ας, ἡ (σκληρός, καρδία), [in LXX: Deu.10:16, Jer.4:4 (לֵבָב עׇרְלָה), Sir.16:10 (cf. καρδία σκληρά, Sir.3:26-27) * ;] hardness of heart: Mat.19:8, Mrk.10:5 16:14.† (AS)

skleros.notes: σκληρός, -ά, -όν (σκέλλω, to dry), [in LXX chiefly for קָשָׁה ;] hard to the touch, rough, harsh, (opposite to μαλακός); metaphorically, in various uses; __(a) of men, hard, stern, severe: Mat.25:24; __(b) of things,…

sklerotes.notes: σκληρότης, -ητος, ἡ (σκληρός), [in LXX: Deu.9:27 (קְשִׁי), etc. ;] hardness; metaphorically, of stubbornness: Rom.2:5.† (AS)

sklerotrachelos.notes: σκληρο-τράχηλος, -ον (σκληρός, τράχηλος), [in LXX: Exo.33:3, 5 (עֹרֶף ־קָשֶׁה), etc. ;] stiff-necked: metaphorically, Act.7:51.† (AS)

skleruno.notes: σκληρύνω (σκληρός), [in LXX chiefly for קָשָׁה hi., also for חָזַק ;] to harden. Metaphorical, to harden, make stubborn: with accusative of person(s), Rom.9:18; T. καρδίας (Psa.95:8), Heb.3:8, 15 4:7. Pass., to…

skolekobrotos.notes: σκωληκό-βρωτος, -ον (σκώληξ, βιβρώσκω), eaten of worms: Act.12:23 (cf. 2Ma.9:9, and see MM, xxiii) † (AS)

skolex.notes: σκώληξ, -ηκος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for תּוֹלָע and cogn. forms ;] a worm: of the kind which preys upon dead bodies, metaphorically, of fut. punishment, Mrk.9:48 (LXX, Isa.66:24; cf. Sir.7:17, Jdth.16:17).† (AS)

skolios.notes: σκολιός, -ά, -όν [in LXX: Deu.32:5 (עִקֵּשׁ), Pro.28:18 (σ. ὁδοῖς πορεύεσθαι, for: דֶּרֶךְ עָקַשׁ), Isa.40:4 (עָקֹב), etc. ;] curved, bent, winding (opposite to ὀρθός, εὐθύς): Luk.3:5 (LXX). Metaphorical, crooked,…

skolops.notes: σκόλοψ, -οπος, ὁ [in LXX: Num.33:55 (שֵׂךְ), Hos.2:6 (8) (סִיר), Eze.28:24 (סִלּוֹן), Sir.43:19 * ;] anything pointed, esp. __1. in cl., a stake __2. In Hellenistic vernacular, a thorn (cf. LXX, ll. with): σ. τῇ…

skopeo.notes: ακοπέω, -ῶ (σκοπός), [in LXX: Est.8:13, 2Ma.4:5 * ;] to look at, behold, watch, contemplate. Metaphorical, to look to, consider: with accusative of thing(s), 2Co.4:13, Php.2:4; with accusative of person(s),…

skopos.notes: σκοπός, -οῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for צָפָה ;] __1. a watcher, watchman (Eze.3:17, al.). __2. a mark on which to fix the eye (Wis.5:12, 21, al.). Metaphorical, of an aim or object: Php.3:14.† (AS)

skorpios.notes: σκορπίος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for עַקְרָב ;] a scorpion: Luk.10:19 11:12, Rev.9:3, 5 9:10 † (AS)

skorpizo.notes: σκορπίζω [in LXX for פּוּץ hi., etc. ;] in vernac. and in Ion, and late writers for σκεδάννυμι (see MM, xxiii; Rutherford, NPhr., 295), to scatter: ὁ λύκος, Jhn.10:12; ὁ μὴ συνάγων μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ σκορπίζει, Mat.12:30,…

skoteinos.notes: σκοτεινός (WH. -τινός), -ή, -όν (σκότος), [in LXX chiefly for חֹשֶׁךְ and cogn. forms ;] dark: opposite to φωτεινός, Mat.6:23, Luk.11:34, 36.† (AS)

skotia.notes: σκοτία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Job.28:3 (אֹפֶל), Mic.3:6 (חָשַׁךְ), Isa.16:3 * ;] in late writers = σκότος, darkness: Jhn.6:17 20:1. Metaphorical, __(a) of secrecy (opposite to ἐν τ. φωτί): Mat.10:27, Luk.12:3; __(b) of…

skotizo.notes: σκοτίζω (σκότος), [in LXX for חָשַׁךְ ;] to darken; in NT always pass., -ομαι, to be darkened: of the heavenly bodies, Mat.24:29, Mrk.13:24, Luk.23:45 (Rec., WH, mg.), Rev.8:12. Metaphorical, of the mind, Rom.1:21…

skotoo.notes: σκοτόω, -ῶ (σκότος), [in LXX for חָשַׁךְ, קָדַר, שָׁחַר ;] to darken: Rev.9:2 16:10. Metaphorical, of the mind! pass., Eph.4:18 (cf. σκοτίζω).† (AS)

skotos.notes: σκότος, -ου, ὁ the more usual cl. form (cf.σ., τό), darkness: Heb.12:18, Rec.† (AS)

skubalon.notes: σκύβαλον, -ου, τό [in LXX: Sir.27:4 * ;] refuse, esp. dung (see MM, ii, iii, xxiii): Php.3:8.† (AS)

skullo.notes: σκύλλω __1. in cl., to skin, flay, rend (Æsch., Anthol.). __2. In Hellenistic writers, to vex, trouble, annoy: with accusative of person(s), Mrk.5:35, Luk.8:49; pass., ἐσκυλμένοι, distressed, Mat.9:36; mid., to…

skulon.notes: σκῦλον (Rec. σκύλον), -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for שָׁלָל ;] mostly in pl., σκίλα, arms stripped from a foe, spoils: Luk.11:22.† (AS)

skuthes.notes: Σκύθης, -ου, ὁ Scythian, an inhabitant of Scythia, i.e. Russia and Siberia, a synonym with the Greeks for the wildest of barbarians: Col.3:11.† (AS)

skuthropos.notes: σκυθρωπός, -όν (also -ή, -όν) (σκυθρός, sullen, + ὤψ), [in LXX: Gen.40:7 (רַע), etc. ;] of a gloomy countenance: Mat.6:16, Luk.24:17.† (AS)

smaragdinos.notes: σμαράγδινος (σμάραγδος, which see), __1. of emerald (Luc., V.H., ii, 11). __2. As in π. (Deiss., BS, 267), emerald-green: Rev.4:3 (for the construction, see Swete, in l; Zorell, see word).† (AS)

smaragdos.notes: σμάραγδος, -ου, ἡ (and in late writers also ὁ), [in LXX: Exo.28:9 (שֹׁהַם) Exo.36:17 (Exo.39:10) (בָּרֶקֶת), Eze.28:13 (יַהֲלֹם), etc. ;] emerald or other trans parent green stone (LS, see word; DB, iv, 620; Swete,…

smurna.notes: σμύρνα (in some MSS, ζμ-; see Bl., § 3, 9), -ης, ἡ, [in LXX for מֹר ;] myrrh, a resinous gum used as an unguent and for em­balming: Mat.2:11, Jhn.19:39.† (AS)

smurnizo.notes: σμυρνίζω (σιύρνα), __1. intrans., to be like myrrh (Diosc., i, 79). __2. Trans., to mingle or drug with myrrh: pass., Mrk.15:23.† (AS)

solomonsalomon.notes: Σολομών, -ῶνος (so prop., but Rec. has frequently -ῶν, -ῶντος, as also WH in Act.3:11 5:12; in Act.7:47 T has Σαλωμών, as LXX freq., indecl.; see B1., § 10, 1; Tdf., Prol., 104, 119; WH, App., 158), ὁ (Heb.…

soma.notes: σῶμα, -τος, τό, [in LXX for גְּוִיָּה ,בָּשָׂר, etc., and for Aram. נְבֵלָה ;] a body. __1. Prop., of the human body, __(a) as always in Hom. (opposite to δέμας), of the dead body: Mat.27:58, 59 Mrk.15:43, al.; __(b)…

somatikos.notes: σωματικός, -ή, -όν (σῶμα), [in LXX: 4Ma.1:32 4Mac 3:1 * ;] __(a) of or for the body, bodily: γυμνασία, 1Ti.4:8; __(b) (opposite to ἀσώματος) bodily, corporeal: εἶδος, Luk.3:22.† (AS)

sophia.notes: σοφία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for חׇכְמָה ;] skill, intelligence, wisdom, ranging from knowledge of the arts and matters of daily life to mental excellence in its highest and fullest sense; __(a) of human wisdom:…

sophizo.notes: σοφίζω (σοφός), [in LXX chiefly for חָכָם ;] to make wise, instruct: with accusative of person(s), 2Ti.3:15 (cf. Psa.19:8 (Psa.119:98). As depon., -ομαι; __(a) intrans., to become wise (I2Ki.4:27 (3Ki.5:11),…

sophos.notes: σοφός, -ή, -όν [in LXX chiefly for חָכָם ;] skilled, clever, wise, whether in handicraft, the affairs of life, the sciences or learning: Rom.16:19, 1Co.3:10; of the learned, Rom.1:14, 22, 1Co.1:19-20 1:26-27 3:18-20;…

sophron.notes: σώφρων, -ον [in LXX: 4Ma.9:1-32 * ;] __(a) of sound mind, sane, sensible; __(b) self-controlled, sober-minded: 1Ti.3:2, Tit.1:8 2:2, 5 (Arist.).† (AS)

sophroneo.notes: σωφρονέω, -ῶ (σώφρων), __(a) to be of sound mind or in one's right mind: Mrk.5:15, Luk.8:35; opposite to ἐκστῆναι, 2Co.5:13 (Hdt.); __(b) to be temperate, discreet, self-controlled (opposite to μαίνεσθαι, ὑβρίζειν,…

sophronismos.notes: σωφρονισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (σωφρονίζω), __(a) an admonishing (F1J, Plut., al.); __(b) self-control, self-discipline: 2Ti.1:7 (on the reflexive meaning here, see Ellic., in l).† (AS)

sophronizo.notes: σωφρονίζω [in Aq.: Isa.38:16 * ;] to make σώφρων, recall one to his senses, control (RV, train): with accusative of person(s), Tit.2:4.† (AS)

sophronos.notes: σωφρόνως adv., [in LXX: Wis.9:11 * ;] with sound mind, prudently, soberly: Tit.2:12.† (AS)

sophrosune.notes: σωφροσύνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Est.3:13, Wis.8:7, 2Ma.4:37, 4Ma.6:1-35 * ;] __(a) soundness of mind, good sense, sanity (opposite to μανία, Xen.): Act.26:25; __(b) self-control, sobriety: 1Ti.2:9 (see Tr., Syn., § xx),…

soreuo.notes: σωρεύω [in LXX: Pro.25:22 (חָתָה), Jdth.15:11 * ;] __(a) to heap on: with accusative before ἐπί with accusative, Rom.12:20 (LXX) (Arist., al.); __(b) to heap with: with accusative and dative, metaphorically,…

soros.notes: σορός, -οῦ, ἡ [in LXX: Gen.50:26 (אָרוֹן), Job.21:32 A * ;] __(a) a cinerary urn (Hom.); __(b) a coffin (Hdt., i, 68, 3, a1.): Luk.7:14.† (AS)

sos.notes: σός, -ή, -όν possess. prop. of second of person(s), thy, thine: Mat.7:3, al.; as subst., οἱ σοί, thy kinsfolk, friends, Mrk.5:19; τὸ σόν, what is thine, Mat.20:14 25:25; τὰ σά, thy goods, Luk.6:30 (AS)

sosthenes.notes: Σωσθένης, -ου, ὁ Sosthenes; __(a) a Jewish ruler: Act.18:7; __(b) a Christian: 1Co.1:1.† (AS)

soter.notes: σωτήρ, -ῆρος, ὁ (σώζω), [in LXX for יָשַׁע and cognate forms ;] saviour, deliverer, preserver, a frequently epithet of kings in the Ptolemaic and Rom. periods (Deiss., BS, 83; LAE, 368 f.; MM, xxiv), in NT, __(a) of…

soteria.notes: σωτηρία, -ας, ἡ (σωτήρ), [in LXX for יֶשַׁע, יְשׁוּעָה, תְּשׁוּעָה, פְּלֵיטָה, etc. ;] deliverance, preservation, salvation, safety (Lat. salus): Act.7:25 27:34, Heb.11:7; ἐξ ἐχθρῶν, Luk.1:71. In NT esp. of Messianic…

soterion.notes: σωτήριος, -ον (σωτήρ), [in LXX for יְשׁוּעָה, שֶׁלֶם, etc. ;] saving, bringing salvation, in NT always in spiritual sense (see: σώζω, σωτηρία): ἡ χάρις ἡ σ., Tit.2:1. Neut., -τὸ σ., as subst. (cl.), salvation:…

soudarion.notes: σουδάριον, -ου, τό(Lat.), handkerchief, kerchief: Luk.19:20, Act.19:12; used as a head covering for the dead, Jhn.11:44 20:7 (cf. Deiss., BS, 223).† (AS)

sousanna.notes: Σουσάννα, -ης (cf. Dan LXX TH Su, inscr., 2, 7, al.) Susanna: Luk.8:3.† (AS)

sozo.notes: σώζω (on the more accurate σῴζω, see WH, Intr., §410; Bl., §3, 1-3), [in LXX chiefly for ישׁע hi., also for מלט ni., נצל ni., etc. ;] to save from peril, injury or suffering: Mat.8:25, Mrk.13:20, Luk.23:35, al.; τ.…

spania.notes: Σπανία, -ας, ἡ (= cl. Ἑσπερία or Ἰβηρία; late writers adopted the Roman name, Ἱσπανία (1Ma.8:3) or Σ. as here), Spain: Rom.15:24, 28.† (AS)

spao.notes: σπάω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁלַף, ;] in cl. poët. for ἕλκω; mostly used in mid. (cf. M, Pr., 157), to draw: μάχαιραν, Mrk.14:47, Act.16:27,† (AS)

sparasso.notes: σπαράσσω [in LXX: 2Ki.22:8 B (גָּעַשׁ hith.), Jhn.4:19 (הָמָה) Dan LXX 8:7 (שָׁלַךְ hi.), 3Ma.4:6 * ;] __1. to tear, rend, mangle. __2. to convulse: Mrk.1:26 (see Swete, in l) Mrk.9:26, Luk.9:39 (cf. συν-σπαράσσω).† (AS)

sparganoo.notes: σπαργανόω, -ῶ (σπάργανον, a swathing-band) [in LXX: Job.38:9, Eze.16:4 (חָתַל pu.)* ;] to swathe, wrap in swaddling-clothes: Luk.2:7, 12 (Hipp., Arist., Plut.).† (AS)

spatalao.notes: σπαταλάω, -ῶ (σπατάλη, wantonness, luxury, Sir.27:13 *), [in LXX: Eze.16:49 (שָׁקַט hi.), Sir.21:15 * ;] to live riotously: 1Ti.5:6, Jas.5:5 (Polyb.).† SYN.: στρηνιάω, τρυφάω (see Tr., Syn., § liv) (AS)

speira.notes: σπείρα, -ης (on this form of genitive, see M, Pr., 38, 48; Bl., § 7, 1; Mayser, 12; Deiss., BS, 186), [in LXX: Jdth.14:11, 2Ma.8:23 2Mac 12:20 2Mac 12:22 * ;] __1. (= Lat. spira) anything wound or rolled up, a coil.…

speiro.notes: σπείρω [in LXX chiefly for זָרַע ;] to sow (seed): absol., Mat.6:26 13:3-4, 18 25:24, 26, Mrk.4:3-4, Luk.8:5 12:24, Jhn.4:36, 2Co.9:10; with accusative of thing(s), Mat.13:24, 27 13:37, 39, Mrk.4:32, Luk.8:5,…

spekoulator.notes: σπεκουλάτωρ, -ορος (Rec. -ωρος), ὁ (Lat. speculator), __1. prop., a spy or scout. __2. An executioner: Mrk.6:27 (see Swete, in l).† (AS)

spelaion.notes: σπήλαιον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for מְעָרָה ;] a cave, cavern: Jhn.11:38, Heb.11:38, Heb.6:15; σ. (EV, den) λῃστῶν (Jhn.7:11), Mat.21:13, Mrk.11:17, Luk.19:46.† (AS)

spendo.notes: σπένδω [in LXX chiefly for נָסַךְ hi. ;] to pour out as a drink-offering, make a libation; pass., figuratively, σπένδομαι, I am poured out or offered as a libation (in the shedding of my life-blood): Php.2:17 (see…

sperma.notes: σπέρμα, -τος, τό (σπείρω) [in LXX chiefly for זֶרַע ;] seed; __(a) of plants: Mat.13:24, 27 13:37-38, 2Co.9:10; pl., Mat.13:32, Mrk.4:31, 1Co.15:33; metaphorically, of an escaping remnant (שָׂרִיד, Isa.1:9; cf.…

spermologos.notes: σπερμολόγος, -ον (σπέρμα, λέγω) a seed picker; __(a) prop., of birds (Arist., Aristoph, al.); __(b) in Attic slang, of an idler who lives on scraps picked up in the agora; hence, as subst., ὁ σ., an idle babbler:…

speudo.notes: σπεύδω [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi., also for בָּהַל pi., hi., etc. ;] __1. most frequently intrans., to hasten: with inf. (as frequently in cl.), Act.20:16; ptcp. with indic., Luk.2:16; with imperat., Luk.19:5-6;…

sphage.notes: σφαγή, -ῆς, ἡ (σφάζω), [in LXX for טָבַח, הֲרֵגָה, etc. ;] slaughter: Act.8:32 (LXX); πρόβατα σφαγῆς, Rom.8:36 (LXX); ἡμέρα σφαγῆς, Jas.5:5.† (AS)

sphagion.notes: σφάγιον, -ου, τό (σφαγή), [in LXX: Amo.5:25 (זֶבַח), Eze.21:10, 15 21:28 (טֶבַח), Lev.22:23 (נְדָבָה) * ;] a victim for slaughter: Act.7:42 (LXX).† (AS)

sphazo.notes: σφάζω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁחַט ;] to slay, slaughter (esp. of victims for sacrifice): ἀρνίον, Rev.5:6, 12 13:8; of persons, 1Jn.3:12, Rev.5:9 6:4, 9 18:24; ἐσφαγμένη εἰς θάνατον (RV, smitten unto death), Rev.13:3…

sphodra.notes: σφόδρα adv. (prop. neut. of σφοδρός, excessive, violent) [in LXX chiefly for מְאֹד ;] very, very much, exceedingly: with verbs, Mat.17:6, 23 18:31 19:25 26:22 27:54, Act.6:7; with adjectives, Mat.2:10, Mrk.16:4,…

sphragis.notes: σφραγίς, -ῖδος, ἡ [in LXX: Exo.28:11, al. (חוֹתָם) Exo.35:22 (חָח), Sir.17:22, al. ;] __1. a seal, signet: Rev.7:2. __2. The impression of a seal or signet, a seal (on its various purposes, see: σφραγίζω): on a book…

sphragizo.notes: σφραγίζω [in LXX chiefly for חָתַם (Deu.32:34, al.), also for סָתַם (Da TH Dan.8:26) ;] to seal, __(a) for security: Mat.27:66, Rev.20:3; τ. καρπὸν τοῦτον, figuratively (cf. Deiss., BS, 238 f.; MM, xxiv; DB, iv,…

sphuron.notes: σφυρόν, -οῦ, τό, [in LXX: Job.41:21 (לָהַט) * ;] the ankle: Act.3:7, Rec. (T, WH, σφυδρόν; see Bl., § 6, 8).† (AS)

spilas.notes: σπιλάς, -άδος, ἡ (on the gender in Ju, l.with, see Mayor, Ju., 41), __1. poët. in cl., a rock or reef over which the sea dashes (Polyb., FlJ, al.). Metaphorical, of men whose conduct causes danger to others, Ju 12,…

spiloo.notes: σπιλόω [in LXX: Wis.15:4 * ;] to stain, spot, defile: with accusative, Jas.3:6, Ju 23.† (AS)

spilos.notes: σπίλος (Rec. σπῖλος), -ου, ὁ __1. a rock, cliff (Arist., al.). __2. In late writers = Att.. κηλίς (see Rutherford, NPhr., 87 f.), a spot, stain; metaphorically, __(a) of moral blemish: Eph.5:27 __(b) of riotous and…

splagchnizo.notes: σπλαγχνίζομαι (σπλάγχνον), [in LXX (act., -ίζω): Pro.17:5 A, 2Ma.6:8 (= -εύω) * ;] to be moved as to the σπλάγχνα (which see), hence, to feel pity or compassion: absol., Luk.10:33 15:20; ptcp., σπλαγχνισθείς, with…

splagchnon.notes: σπλάγχνον, -ου, τό mostly (in NT always) in pl., σπλάγχνα, -ων, τά, [in LXX: Pro.12:10 (רַחַם, elsewhere rendered by οἰκτιρμοί, Psa.25:6 40:12 and by ἔλεος, Isa.47:6), Pro.26:22 (בֶּטֶן), Wis.10:5, Sir.30:7, 2Ma.9:5…

spodos.notes: σποδός, -oῦ, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for אֵפֶר ;] ashes: Heb.9:13; ἐν σάκκῳ καὶ σ. (Isa.58:5, Jhn.3:6, Dan LXX 9:3, al.), Mat.11:21, Luk.10:13.† (AS)

spongos.notes: σπόγγος, -ου, ὁ a sponge: Mat.27:48, Mrk.15:36, Jhn.19:29.† (AS)

spora.notes: σπορά, -ᾶς, ἡ (σπείρω), [in LXX: 4Ki.19:29 (זָרַע), 1Ma.10:30 * ;] __1. a sowing. __2. seedtime. __3. seed sown (of human offspring, Soph., Tr., 316, 420): 1Pe.1:23 (cf. σπέρμα, and see Milligan, NTD, 105 f.).† (AS)

sporimos.notes: σπόριμος, -ον (σπείρω) [in LXX: Gen.1:29, Lev.11:37 (זָרַע), Sir 40:22 א1 * ;] fit for sowing, sown; as subst., τὰ σ., corn-fields: Mat.12:1, Mrk.2:23, Luk.6:1.† (AS)

sporos.notes: σπόρος, -ου, ὁ (σπείρω), [in LXX chiefly for זֶרַע ;] = σπορά, __1. sowing or seedtime. __2. seed sown (so usually in late Gk.): Mrk.4:26-27, Luk.8:5, 11, 2Co.9:10 a (LTr.); metaphorically, of almsgiving, 2Co.9:10…

spoudaios.notes: σπουδαῖος, -α, -ον (σπουδή), [in LXX: Eze.41:25 * ;] in haste; hence, zealous, eager, diligent, earnest: before ἐν, 2Co.8:22; compar., -οτεροσς 2Co.8:17 (here in superl. sense, see Bl., § 44, 3) 2Co.8:22.† (AS)

spoudazo.notes: σπουδάζω (σπουδή), [in LXX chiefly for בָּהַל ;] to make haste; hence, to be zealous or eager, to give diligence: with inf. (see M, Pr., 205 f.), Gal.2:10, Eph.4:3, 1Th.2:17, 2Ti.2:15 4:9 4:21, Tit.3:12, Heb.4:11,…

spoude.notes: σπουδή, -ῆς, ἡ (σπεύδω), [in LXX chiefly for בָּהַל, its parts and derivatives ;] __1. haste, speed: μετὰ σ. (Wis.19:2), Mrk.6:25, Luk.1:39. __2. zeal, diligence, earnestness: Rom.12:11, 2Co.7:11-12 8:7-8; ἐν σ.,…

spuris.notes: σφυρίς (T, Rec. σπυρίς, see WH, App., 148; 131., § 6, 7), -ίδος, ἡ, a flexible mat-basket for carrying provisions: Mat.15:37 16:10, Mrk.8:8, 20 Act.9:25 (see: κόφινος).† (AS)

stachus.notes: στάχυς, -υος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for שִׁבֹּל ;] an ear of corn: Mat.12:1, Mrk.2:23 4:28, Luk.6:1.† (AS)

stadios.notes: στάδιον, -ου, τό pl., στάδια (Jhn.6:19, T), and metapl., στάδιοι (see B1., 9, 1), [in LXX: Dan LXX 4:9, Sus 1:37, 2Ma.11:5 2Mac 12:9 ff. * ;] a stadium, i.e. __1. a measure of length = 600 Greek feet or 1⁄8 of a…

stamnos.notes: στάρνος, -ου (ὁ and), ἡ [in LXX: Exo.16:33 (צִנְצֶנֶת), 3Ki.12:24 14:3 (בַּקְבֻּק), Dan LXX Bel, 1:32 * ;] prop., an earthen jar for racking off wine, hence, generally, a jar: Heb.9:4.† (AS)

staphule.notes: σταφυλή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for עֵנָב ;] a bunch of grapes: Mat.7:16, Luk.6:44, Rev.14:18. "σ. is properly the ripe grape-cluster as opposite to ὄμφαξ, cf. Gen.40:10, Job.15:33; as contrasted with βότρυς, it describes…

stasis.notes: στάσις, -εως, ἡ (ἵστημι), [in LXX chiefly for עָמַד, its parts and derivatives, also for רִיב (Pr 1714), etc. (see Deiss., BS, 158 f.) ;] __1. a standing, place, status: σ. ἔχειν (Polyb., v, 5, 3; and cf. Lat. locum…

stater.notes: στατήρ, -ῆρος, ὁ [in Aq., Sm., for מִשְׁקָל ;] a stater; __(a) a weight; __(b) a coin (used by late writers of the Greek τετράδραχμον): Mat.17:27 26:16, WH, mg.† (AS)

stauroo.notes: σταυρόω, -ῶ (σταυρός), [in LXX: Est.7:9 (תָּלָה), Est.8:13 * ;] __1. to fence with pales, impalisade (Thuc.). __2. In late writers (Polyb., FlJ; but ἀνασταυρόω is more common) to crucify: with accusative of…

stauros.notes: σταυρός, -oῦ, ὁ __1. an upright pale or stake (Hom., Hdt., Thuc., al.) __2. In late writers (Diod., Plut., al.) of the Roman instrument of crucifixion, the Cross: of the Cross on which Christ suffered, Mat.27:32, 40…

stege.notes: στέγη, -ης, ἡ (στέγω) [in LXX: Gen.8:13 (מְכַסֶּה), etc. ;] a roof: Mrk.2:4; of entering a house, εἰσέρχεσθαι ὑπὸ τὴν σ., Mat.8:8, Luk.7:6 (cf. MM, xxiii).† (AS)

stego.notes: στέγω (cf. Lat. tego), [in LXX: Sir.8:17 * ;] __1. prop., to cover closely, to protect by covering, esp. to keep water in or out (Soph., Plat., al.). __2. to cover, keep secret, conceal (Sir, l.with, Polyb., al.):…

steira.notes: στεῖρος, -ον also -α, -ον, [in LXX for עָקָר ;] barren: of a woman, Luk.1:7, 36 23:29, Gal.4:27" (LXX) .† (AS)

steko.notes: στήκω late Pres., formed from perf., ἕστηκα (see B1., § 17; WH, App., 169; Kennedy, Sources, 158; M, Pr., 238; MM, xxiii), [in LXX: Exo.14:13 A, Jdg.16:26 B, 3Ki.8:11 B * ;] = ἵστημι, to stand: Mrk.3:31 11:25,…

stello.notes: στέλλω [in LXX (mid.): Mal.2:5 (חָתַת ni.), Pro.31:24 (26), Wis.7:14 14:1, 2Ma.5:1, 3Ma.1:19 3Mac 4:11 * ;] __1. prop., to set, place, arrange, fit out; hence, mid., to set oneself for, prepare (Wis, xi Mac, ll.…

stemma.notes: στέμμα, -τος, τό (στέφω, to put around, enwreath) a wreath, garland: as used in sacrifices, Act.14:13.† (AS)

stenagmos.notes: στεναγμός, -οῦ, ὁ (στενάζω), [in LXX for אֲנָחָה, אֲנָקָה, נְאָקָה, etc. ;] a groaning: Act.7:34 (LXX κραυγή), Rom.8:26.† (AS)

stenazo.notes: στενάζω [in LXX for אָנַח ni., אָנַק, etc. ;] to groan ("the word denotes feeling which is internal and unexpressed," Mayor, Ja., 162): Mrk.7:34, 2Co.5:2 5:4, Heb.13:17, Jas.5:9 (EV, murmur); ἐν ἑαυτοῖς, Rom.8:23…

stenochoreo.notes: στενο-χωρέω, -ῶ (στενός + χῶρος, space) [in LXX: Jos.17:15 (אוּץ), Jdg.16:16 (אָלַץ pi.), Isa.28:19 (20) (קָצַר) Isa.49:19 (צָרַר), 4Ma.11:11 * ;] __1. to be straitened (cf. Isa.49:19); metaphorically, to be anxious…

stenochoria.notes: στενοχωρία, -ας, ἡ (see supr.) [in LXX for צוֹק, etc. ;] narrowness of space, want of room (Thuc., al.). Metaphorical (Ken., Polyb., Deu.28:53, Wis.5:3, al.), difficulty, distress: θλίψις καὶ (ἢ) σ., Rom.2:9 8:35…

stenos.notes: στενός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for צַר, etc. ;] narrow: fig (v MM, xxiii), Mat.7:13-14, Luk.13:24.† (AS)

stephanoo.notes: στεφανόω, -ῶ (στέφανος), [in LXX for עָטַר ;] with accusative of person(s), to crown: of a victor, 2Ti.2:5. Metaphorical, δόξῃ κ. τιμῇ, Heb.2:7, 9" (LXX) .† (AS)

stephanos.notes: στέφανος, -ου, ὁ (στέφω, to encircle), [in LXX chiefly for עֲטָרָה ;] __1. that which surrounds or encompasses (as a wall, a crowd: Hom., al.). __2. a crown, i.e. the wreath, garland or chaplet given as a prize for…

stereoma.notes: στερέωμα, -τος, τό (στερεόω), [in LXX: Gen.1:6 ff., Psa.19:1 150:1, Eze.1:22-26 10:1 13:5, Dan LXX, TH Dan.3:56, Dan.12:3 (רָקִיעַ), Deu.33:26 (שַׁחַק), Psa.18:2 71:3 (סֶלַע), Est.9:29, I Est.8:78 (82), Sir.43:1, 8,…

stereoo.notes: στερεόω, -ῶ (στερεός), [in LXX: Isa.42:5 (רָקַע), Jer.5:3 (חָזַק pi.), etc. ;] __(a) to make firm or solid; __(b) to strengthen, make strong; with accusative of person(s), Act.3:16; τ. βάσεις, pass., Act.3:7.…

stereos.notes: στερεός, -ά, -όν [in LXX: Num.8:4 (מִקְשָׁה); σ. πέτρα, Isa.5:28 (צַר), etc. ;] hard, firm, solid: τροψή, Heb.5:12, 14; θεμέλιος, figuratively, 2Ti.2:19. Metaphorical, __(a) in bad sense, hard, cruel (Hom., al.);…

sterigmos.notes: στηριγμός, -οῦ, ὁ (στηρίζω), [in Sm.: Isa.3:1 * ;] __1. a setting firmly, supporting. __2. fixedness, firmness, steadfastness: 2Pe.3:17.† (AS)

sterizo.notes: στηρίζω [in LXX for סָמַךְ, שׂוּם, etc. ;] to fix, set fast, make fast: Luk.16:26; τ. πρόσωτον (Eze.6:2 13:17, al.; see Dalman, Words, 30 f.), Luk.9:51. Metaphorical, to confirm, establish: with, accusative,…

stethos.notes: στῆθος, -ους, τό [in LXX: Exo.28:23, 26 (29-30) (לֵב), etc. ;] the breast: Jhn.13:25 21:10, Rev.15:6; as a sign of penitence, τύπτειν (εἰς) τὸ σ., Luk.18:13 23:48,† (AS)

sthenoo.notes: σθενόω, -ῶ (σθένος, strength), to strengthen: with accusative, 1Pe.5:10 (Hesych.).† (AS)

stigma.notes: στίγμα -τος, τό (στίζω, to prick), [in LXX: Sng.1:11 (נְקֻדָּה) * ;] a tattoed mark or brand: τὰ σ. τοῦ Ἰησοῦ, Gal.6:17 (see Lft., in l; Deiss., BS, 349; LAE, 303; MM, xxiii).† (AS)

stigme.notes: στιγμή, -ῆς, ἡ (στίζω, to prick), [in LXX: Isa.29:5 (פֶּתַע), 2Ma.9:11 * ;] a prick, a point; metaphorically, σ. χρόνου, a moment: Luk.4:5.† (AS)

stoa.notes: στοά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX: Eze.40:18 (רִצְפָּה), etc. ;] a portico: Jhn.5:2; used of the covered colonnade in the Temple (EV, porch), Jhn.10:23 Act.3:11 5:12 † (AS)

stoibasstibas.notes: στοιβάς, see: στιβάς. στιβάς, -άδος, ἡ, [in Aq.: Eze.46:23 (טִירוֺת) * ;] a litter of leaves or rushes: pl., Mrk.11:8 (Rec. στοιβ-).† (AS)

stoicheion.notes: στοιχεῖον, -ου, τό [in LXX: Wis.7:17 19:18, 4Ma.12:13 * ;] prop., one of a row (στοῖχος) or series, hence, __1. the shadow-line of a dial (Aristoph.). __2. an elementary sound or letter of the alphabet (Anth., Plut.,…

stoicheo.notes: στοιχέω, -ῶ (στοῖχος, a row), [in LXX: Ecc.11:6 (כָּשֵׁר) * ;] to be in rows (of waves, plants, etc., as well as of men), to walk in line (esp. of marching in file to battle; Xen., Cyr., vi, 3, 34, al.).…

stole.notes: στολή, -ῆς, ἡ (στέλλω), [in LXX chiefly for בֶּגֶד, also for לְבוּשׁ, etc. ;] __1 1, an equipment, an armament (Æsch.). __2. Equipment in clothes, apparel, esp. flowing raiment, a festal robe: (cf. Jon.3:6, Est.8:15,…

stoma.notes: στόμα, -τος, τό [in LXX chiefly for פֻּם ;] the mouth: of man, Mat.15:11, Jhn.19:29, Act.11:8, al.; of animals, Mat.17:27, 2Ti.4:17 (fig.), Heb.11:33, Jas.3:3, al.; figuratively, of inanimate things (ποταμοῦ, Hom.),…

stomachos.notes: στόμαχος, -ου, ὁ (στόμα), prop., a mouth, an opening; __(a) in early Gk. writers, the throat; __(b) of the opening of the stomach (Arist.); __(with) in later writers (Plut., al.), the stomach: 1Ti.5:23.† (AS)

strategos.notes: στρατηγός, -οῦ, ὁ (στραός, ἄγω), [in LXX chiefly for סָגָן (always in pl.), שַׂר ;] __1. a military commander, a general (Hdt., al.). __2. A civic commander, a governor, magistrate (Hdt., Xen., al.): Act.16:20, 22…

strateia.notes: στρατεία (on the orthogr., see Deiss., BS, 181 f.), -ας, ἡ (οτρατεύω), [in LXX chiefly for צָבָא ;] an expedition, a campaign, warfare: metaphorically, 2Co.10:4 (-τιά, T), 1Ti.1:18.† (AS)

strateuma.notes: στράτευμα, -τος, τό (στρατεύω), [in LXX: 1Ma.9:34, 2Ma.5:24 2Mac 8:21 2Mac 12:38 2Mac 13:13; pl., Jdth.11:8, 4Ma.5:1 * ;] __1. = στρατεία (Hdt., al.). __2. an army, a host: pl., Mat.22:7, Rev.9:16 19:14, 19; =…

strateuo.notes: στρατεύω and depon., -ομαι, so always in NT (στρατός, an encamped army), [in LXX for מההּ hith., יָצָא, צָבָא; metaphorically, 4Ma.9:23 ;] used of the general, to make war, do battle, and (chiefly) of the soldiers…

stratia.notes: στρατιά, -ᾶς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for צָבָא ;] __1. = στρατός, an army, a host: of angels (3Ki.22:19, al.), Luk.2:13; of the stars of heaven (Jer.8:2, 2Ch.33:3), Act.7:42. __2. As sometimes in cl. (poët.), = στρατεία:…

stratiotes.notes: στρατιώτης, -ου, ὁ [in LXX; 2Ki.23:8 B1, 2Ma.5:12 2Mac 14:39, III Mac 3:12; 3Ma.3:4 Mac3 * ;] a soldier: Mat.8:9, Mrk.15:16, Jhn.19:2, Act.10:7, al.; metaphorically, σ. Χριστοῦ Ἰησ., 2Ti.2:3.† (AS)

stratologeo.notes: στρατολογέω, -ῶ (στρατός, λέγω), to levy a troop, enlist soldiers: 2Ti.2:4 (Diod., Plut., al.).† (AS)

stratopedon.notes: στρατό-πεδον, -ου, τό (στρατός + πέδον, a plain), [in LXX: Jer.34:1 (חַיִל), 2Ma.8:12, al. ;] __(a) a military camp; __(b) an army: Luk.21:20.† (AS)

strebloo.notes: στρεβλόω, -ῶ (στρέφω), [in LXX: 2Ki.22:27 (פָּתַל hithp.), 3Ma.4:14, 4Ma.9:17 4Mac 12:4 4Mac 12:11 4Mac 15:14 * ;] to twist, torture. Metaphorical (cf. 2Ki, l.with), to twist or pervert language: 2Pe.3:16.† (AS)

streniao.notes: στρηνιάω, ῶ (στρῆνος, which see), [in Sm.: Isa.61:6 (אָכַל) * ;] a word which first appears in the middle comedy (Rutherford, NPhr., 475 f.), to run riot, wax wanton: Rev.18:7-9 (cf. κατα-στρηνιάω).† SYN.: σπαταλάω…

strenos.notes: στρῆνος, -ους, τό (cf. Lat. strenuus), [in LXX: 4Ki.19:28 (שַׁאֲנָן)* ;] insolent luxury, wantonness: Rev.18:3 (a late word, first found in a Comic poet, B.C. 300, see Kennedy, Sources, 41, cf. στρηνιάω).† (AS)

strepho.notes: στρέφω [in LXX chiefly for הָפַךְ, also for סָבַב, etc. ;] to turn: with accusative of thing(s) dative of person(s), Mat.5:39; = ἀποσ-, to bring back, Mat.27:3 (cf. Isa.38:8); reflexively (WM, § 38, 1), to turn…

stronnumi.notes: στρωννύω or στρώννυμι (seeBl., § 23, 1; Veitch, see word), [in LXX for יַצַע hoph., etc. ;] to spread: ἱμάτια ἐν τ. ὁδῷ (εἰς τ. ὁδόν), Mat.21:8, Mrk.11:8; of making a bed, στρῶσον (sc. τ. κλίνην) σεαυτῷ, Act.9:34; of…

strouthion.notes: στρουθίον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for צִפּוֹר ;] dimin. of στρουθός a sparrow: Mat.10:29, 31, Luk.12:6-7.† (AS)

stugetos.notes: στυγητός, -όν (στυγέω, to hate), hated, hateful: Tit.3:3.† (AS)

stugnazo.notes: στυγνάζω (στυγνός, sombre, gloomy, sullen, Isa.5:17, Wis.17:5, Dan LXX 2:12*), [in LXX: Eze.27:35 28:19 32:10 (שָׁמֵם)* ;] to have a sombre, gloomy appearance: of the human countenance (RV, his countenance fell),…

stulos.notes: στύλος (T, στῦλος, as in cl.), -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for עַמּוּד, also for קֶרֶשׁ, etc. ;] a pillar, regarded especially as a support: σ. πυρός, Rev.10:1; σ. ἐν τ. ναῷ, figuratively, Rev.3:12, Metaphorical, Gal.2:9;…

suchar.notes: Συχάρ (Ε, Σιχάρ), indecl., ἡ Sychar, a town of Samaria: Jhn.4:5.† (AS)
