suchem.notes: Συχέμ indecl. (Heb. שְׁכֶם), Shechem; __1. the son of Hamor (Gen.33:19): Act.7:16 b, Rec. __2. A city of Samaria (AV, Sychem; in LXX also sometimes Σίκιμα, -ων, as in FlJ): Act.7:16 a,b.† (AS)

sugchairo.notes: συν-χαίρω (Rec. συγ-), [in LXX: Gen.21:6 (צָחַק), 3Ma.1:8 R * ;] __1. to rejoice with: with dative of person(s), Luk.1:58 15:6, 9, Php.2:17-18, EV; with dative of thing(s), 1Co.12:26 13:6. __2. to congratulate…

sugcheo.notes: συν-χέω (Rec. συγ-), [in LXX for בָּלַל, רָגַז, etc. ;] to pour together, commingle, confuse; metaphorically, confound, throw into confusion, stir up trouble: Act.21:27 (cf. συν-χύννω).† (AS)

sugchraomai.notes: συν-χράομαι (Rec. συγ-), -ῶμαι, [in Al.: 1Ki.30:19 * ;] to use together with. Metaphorical, with dative of person(s), to associate with: Jhn.4:9 (Polyb., a1.).† (AS)

sugchusis.notes: σύγχυσις, -εως, ἡ (συγχέω), [in LXX: Gen.11:9 (בָּבֶל), 1Ki.5:6 5:12(11) 1Ki.14:20 (מְהוּמָה) * ;] confusion: of a popular uproar, Act.19:29.† (AS)

sugkakopatheo.notes: συν-κακοπαθέω (Rec. συγκ-), -ῶ, to bear evil treatment along with, take one's share of ill-treatment: 2Ti.2:3; with dative commod., τ. εὐαγγελίῳ, 2Ti.2:18.† (AS)

sugkakoucheomai.notes: συν-κακουχέομαι (Rec. συγκ-), -οῦμαι, pass. to endure adversity with: with dative of person(s), Heb.11:25. Not elsewhere.† (AS)

sugkaleo.notes: συν-καλέω (Rec. συγκ-), -ῶ, [in LXX for קָרָא ;] to call together: with accusative, Mrk.15:16, Luk.15:6, 9 (seel. -εῖται, see B1., § 55, 1), Act.5:21. Mid., to call together to oneself: Luk.9:1 (and seel. in…

sugkalupto.notes: συν-καλύπτω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX chiefly for כָּסָה pi. ;] to veil or cover completely: with accusative, pass., Luk.12:2.† (AS)

sugkampto.notes: συν-κάμπτω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Psa.68:10, 23 (מעד hi.), etc. ;] to bend completely, bend together: τ. νῶτον, figuratively, Rom.11:10 (LXX).† (AS)

sugkatabaino.notes: συν-κατα-βαίνω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Psa.49:17 (יָרַד), Wis.10:13, Dan LXX TH 3:49 * ;] to go down with: Act.25:5. In late writers also metaphorically (Wis, l.with), to condescend, on WH. see Rutherford, NPhr., 485…

sugkatapsephizomai.notes: συν-κατα-ψηφίζω (Rec. συγκ-), __1. to condemn with or together (Plut., Themist., 21). __2. to vote one a place among: pass., Act.1:26. Not elsewhere .† (AS)

sugkatathesis.notes: συν-κατά-θεσις (Rec συγκ-), -εως, ἡ (συνκατατίθημι) concord, agreement: 2Co.6:16 (Polyb., Plut., al.).† (AS)

sugkatatithemi.notes: συν-κατα-τίθημι (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Exo.23:1 (שִׁית יָד), Exo.23:32 (בְּרִית), Da TH Su 1:20 * ;] to deposit together. Mid., to deposit one's vote with, hence, to agree with, assent to: with dative, Luk.23:51.† (AS)

sugkathemai.notes: συν-κάθημαι (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Psa.101:6 (יָשַׁב) * ;] to sit together or with: with dative, Act.26:30; before μετά, with genitive, Mrk.14:54.† (AS)

sugkathizo.notes: περι-καθ-ίζω [in LXX for חָנָה, לָחַם ni., etc. ;] __1. to invest, besiege (Diod., 4Ki.6:24, 1Ma.11:61, al.). __2. to sit around: Luk.22:55, L, txt. † (AS)

sugkerannumi.notes: συν-κεράννυμι (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Dan LXX 2:43 (עֲרַב ithpa.), 2Ma.15:39 * ;] to mix or blend together, compound: with accusative, 1Co.12:24; ptcp. pf. pass., Heb.4:2, T, WH, mg., R, mg.; id., of persons, with…

sugkineo.notes: συν-κινέω (Rec. συγκ-), -ῶ, to move together. Metaphorical, to excite, stir up: τ. λαόν, Act.6:12 (Arist., Polyb., al.).† (AS)

sugkleio.notes: συν-κλείω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX chiefly for סגר ;] to shut together, enclose, shut in on all sides: of a catch of fish, Luk.5:6; metaphorically (Psa.31:9, al.), Rom.11:32, Gal.3:22-23.† (AS)

sugkleronomos.notes: συν-κληρο-νόμος (Rec. συγκ-), -ου, ὁ a co-inheritor, fellow-heir: Rom.8:17, Eph.3:6; with genitive of thing(s), Heb.11:9, 1Pe.3:7 (for exx., see Deiss., LAE, 88 f.).† (AS)

sugkoinoneo.notes: συν-κοινωνέω (Rec. συγκ-), -ῶ, to have fellowship with or in (in cl., with genitive of thing(s), dative of person(s)): with dative of thing(s), Eph.5:11, Rev.18:4; id. with genitive of person(s), Php.4:14.† (AS)

sugkoinonos.notes: συν-κοινωνός (Rec. συγκ-), όν, partaking jointly of: with genitive of thing(s), Rom.11:17, 1Co.9:23; id. with genitive of person(s), Php.1:7; before ἐν, Rev.1:9.† (AS)

sugkomizo.notes: συν-κομίζω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Job.5:26 (עָלָה) * ;] __1. to bring together, collect (Hdt., Xen., al.). __2. to take up a body for burial (Soph., Aj., 1048): Act.8:2 (see Field, Notes, 116).† (AS)

sugkrino.notes: συν-κρίνω (Rec. συγκ-) [in LXX chiefly for פָּתַר ;] __1. to compound, combine: 1Co.2:13, R, mg.1 (Lft., Notes, 180 f.). __2. In Arist. and later writers (Wis.7:29, a1.) = παραβάλλω, to compare: 1Co, l.with, R, txt.…

sugkupto.notes: συν-κύπτω (Rec. συγκ-), [in LXX: Job.9:27 (עָזַב), Sir.12:11 19:26 * ;] __1. to bend forwards (Arist., al.). __2. to be bowed down: Luk.13:11.† (AS)

sugkuria.notes: συγκυρία, -ας, ἡ (συγκυρέω, to happen), [in Sm.: 1Ki.6:9 (מִקְרֶה) * ;] (more frequently in late writers, συγκύρησις, -ημα), chance, coincidence: κατὰ σ. (see MM, xxiii), Luk.10:31 (Hippocr., Eccl.).† (AS)

sukaminos.notes: συκάμινος, -ου, ἡ [in LXX for שִׁקְמָה (pl.) ;] the mulberry tree (Lat. morus; cf. μόρον, 1Ma.6:34 *), the sycamine: Luk.17:6. (In LXX, σ. appears to represent the συκόμορος (see: συκομορέα), but St. Luke…

suke.notes: συκῆ (contr. fr. συκέα), -ῆς, ἡ (σῦκον), [in LXX for תְּאֵן ;] a fig-tree: Mat.21:19-21 24:32, Mrk.11:13, 20-21 13:28, Luk.13:6-7 21:29, Jhn.1:49, 51, Jas.3:12, Rev.6:13.† (AS)

sukomoraia.notes: συκο-μορέα (Rec. -μωραία, L, -μωρέα), -ας, ἡ, more commonly (so Aq., Sm., Psa.78:47, Isa.9:10) συκόμορος (σῦκον + μόρον, the black mulberry), a fig-mulberry, a sycamore: Luk.19:4 (cf. συκάμινος).† (AS)

sukon.notes: σῦκον, -ου, τό [in LXX for תְּאֵן ] a fig: Mat.7:16, Mrk.11:13, Luk.6:44 Jas.3:12.† (AS)

sukophanteo.notes: συκοφαντέω, -ῶ (σῦκον, φαίνω), [in LXX: Gen.43:18 (גָּלַל hithpo.), Lev.19:11 (שָׁקַר pi.), Job.35:9, Psa.119:122, Pro.14:31 2:16 28:3, Ecc.4:1 (עָשַׁק) ;] to act the συκοφάντης (on conjectures as to the origin of…

sulagogeo.notes: συλαγωγέω, -ῶ (σύλη, booty + ἄγω), to carry off as spoil, lead captive (θυγατέρα, Heliod., Aeth., 10, 35): metaphorically, Col.2:8.† (AS)

sulao.notes: συλάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Ep. Jer.1:18 * ;] with accusative of person(s), to strip, plunder, spoil: 2Co.11:8.† (AS)

sullaleo.notes: to talk or converse with another, (New Testament) (ML)

sullambano.notes: συλ-λαμβάνω [in LXX for תָּפַשׂ, הָרָה, etc. ;] __1. with accusative, to bring together, collect. __2. to seize, take: with accusative of person(s), as a prisoner, Mat.26:55, Mrk.14:48, Luk.22:54, Jhn.18:12, Act.1:16…

sullego.notes: συλ-λέγω [in LXX chiefly for לָקַט ;] to bring together, collect, gather up: with accusative of thing(s), Mat.13:28-30; pass., Mat.13:40; with accusative, before ἀπό, Mat.7:16; id. before ἐκ, Mat.13:41, Luk.6:44;…

sullogizomai.notes: συλ-λογίζομαι [in LXX chiefly for חָשַׁב pi. ;] __1. to compute. __2. to reason: Luk.20:5.† (AS)

sullupeo.notes: συν-λυπέω (Rec. συλλ-), -ῶ, [in LXX: Psa.69:20, Isa.51:19 (נוּד) * ;] to make to grieve with (Arist.). Pass., to be moved to grief by sympathy: before ἐπί, with dative, Mrk.3:5.† (AS)

sumbaino.notes: συμ-βαίνω [in LXX for עָשָׂה, קָרָא, קָרָה, etc. ;] __1. to stand with the feet together. __2. to come together, come to terms. __3. Of events, to come to pass, happen: with dative of person(s), Mrk.10:32, Act.3:10…

sumballo.notes: συν-βάλλω (Rec. συμ-), [in LXX: 2Ch.25:19 (גָּרָה hith.), Isa.46:6 (זוּל), Jer.43:3 (סוּת hi.), Wis.5:8, al. ;] to throw together, hence, __(a) of speech (before λόγους, Eur., al.), to discuss, confer (sc. λόγους):…

sumbasileuo.notes: συμ-βουλεύω [in LXX chiefly for יָעַץ ;] __1. to advise, counsel: with dative of person(s), Jhn.18:14; id. before inf., Rev.3:18. __2. Mid., to take counsel, consult: before ἵνα, Mat.26:4 ("reciprocal middle," see M,…

sumbibazo.notes: συν-βιβάζω (Rec. συμ-), [in LXX: Exo.4:12, 15, Lev.10:11, Jdg.13:8, Psa.32:8 (יָרָה hi.); Exo.18:16, Deu.4:9, Isa.40:13 (יָדַע hi.), Isa.40:14 (בִּין hi.), Da TH Dan.9:22 (שָׂכַל hi.) * ;] __1. to join or knit…

sumbouleuo.notes: 1. to advise, counsel , Lat. consulere alicui, with dative pers. et infinitive, to advise one to do a thing, (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 2. without the infinitive, ς. τινί τι (Herdotus Historicus); ς. τι to recommend…

sumboulion.notes: συμβούλιον, -ου, τό (σύμβουλος), [in LXX: 4Ma.17:17 א * ;] a word of the Græco-Roman period (of. Lat. consilium, and see Deiss., BS, 238) = cl. συμβουλία (Arist., Xen.), __1. counsel: σ. λαμβάνειν, Mat.12:14 22:15…

sumboulos.notes: σύμβουλος, -ου, ὁ (σύν, βουλή), [in LXX for יָעַץ and cogn. forms ;] a counsellor, adviser: Rom.11:34 (LXX).† (AS)

sumeon.notes: Συμεών, ὁ indecl., (Heb. שִׁמְעוֹן), Simeon (so always AV; RV in Luk.2:25, 34 only), Symeon, a Heb. name for which the Gk. Σίμων, which see, was also used; __1. the son of Jacob (Gen.29:33): Rev.7:7. __2. An ancestor…

summartureo.notes: συν-μαρτυρέω (Rec. συμμ-), -ῶ, to bear witness with: Rom.2:15; with dative, Rom.8:16; before ὅτι, Rom.9:1.† (AS)

summathetes.notes: συν-μαθητής (Rec. συμμ-), -οῦ, ὁ a fellow-disciple: Jhn.11:16 (on the use of συν- in such compounds as this, see Rutherford, NPhr., 255 f.).† (AS)

summerizo.notes: συν-μερίζω (Rec. συμμ-), [in LXX: Pro.29:24 א2 (חָלַק) * ;] to distri­bute in shares. Mid., to have a share in: 1Co.9:13 (Diod., al.).† (AS)

summetochos.notes: συν-μέτοχος (Rec. συμμ-), -ον, partaking together with; as subst., ὁ σ., a joint partaker (with dative of person(s) and genitive of thing(s), FlJ, BJ, i, 24, 6): with genitive of thing(s), Eph.3:6; with genitive of…

summimetes.notes: συν-μιμητής (Rec. συμμ-), -οῦ, ὁ a fellow-imitator: with genitive obj., Php.3:17 (see ICC, in l). Not elsewhere.† (AS)

summorphoomai.notes: Pass. to be conformed to , τινι (New Testament) (ML)

summorphos.notes: σύμ-μορφος, -ον (σύν, μορφή), __1. absol., similar (Luc.). __2. con­formed to: with dative, Php.3:21; with genitive, Rom.8:29 (see B1., § 36, 11; 37, 6).† (AS)

sumparaginomai.notes: συν-παρα-γίνομαι (Rec. συμπ-), [in LXX: Psa.83:8 (לָוָה ni.) * ;] __1. to come up or be present together: before ἐπί, Luk.23:48. __2. to come up to assist (Thuc., al.; 2Ti.4:16, Rec.).† (AS)

sumparakaleo.notes: συν-παρα-καλέω (Rec. συμπ-), -ῶ, to call upon or exhort together (Plat.). Pass., to be strengthened or comforted with and among: Rom.1:12.† (AS)

sumparalambano.notes: συν-παρα-λαμβάνω (Rec. συμπ-), [in LXX: Gen.19:17 (סָפָה ni.), Job.1:4 (שָׁלַח ni), 3Ma.1:1 * ;] to take along with: as a companion, with accusative, Act.12:25 15:37-38 (on the tenses, see M, Pr., 130), Gal.2:1.† (AS)

sumpareimi.notes: συν-πάρειμι (Rec. σνμπ-), [in LXX: Tob.12:12 ΑΒ, Pro.8:27 (?), Wis.9:10 * ;] to be present together or with: with dative of person(s), Act.25:24.† (AS)

sumpascho.notes: συν-πάσχω (Rec. συμπ-), [in Al.: 1Ki.22:8 * ;] __1. to suffer together or with: Rom.8:17, 1Co.12:28. __2. to sympathize with (Plat., Arist.).† (AS)

sumpatheo.notes: συν-παθέω (Rec. συμπ-), -ῶ, [in LXX: 4Ma.5:25 AR 4Ma.13:23 * ;] to have a fellow feeling for or with, sympathize with or in: with dative of thing(s), Heb.4:15; dative of person(s), Heb.10:34 (Arist., Plut., al.).† (AS)

sumpathes.notes: συμπαθής, -ές (σύν, πάσχω) [in LXX: Job.29:25 A (אָבֵל), 4Ma.5:25 א, 4Ma.13:23 4Mac 15:4 * ;] __1. affected by like feelings, sympathetic: 1Pe.3:8. __2. exciting sympathy (Jb, l.with; Dion. H.).† (AS)

sumpempo.notes: συν-πέμπω (Rec. συμπ-) to send together with: with accusative and dative, 2Co.8:22; id. before μετά, with genitive, 2Co.8:18.† (AS)

sumperilambano.notes: συν-περι-λαμβάνω (Rec. συμπ-), __1. to enclose (Eze.5:3 (צוּר) *). __2. to embrace: with accusative of person(s), Act.20:10.† (AS)

sumphemi.notes: σύν φημι (Rec. συμ-), to consent, confess: with dative, before ὅτι, Rom.7:16.† (AS)

sumphero.notes: συμ-φέρω [in LXX: Jer.33:14 (טוֹב), Pro.19:10 (נָאוֶה), Sir.30:19, al. ;] __1. trans. to bring together: with accusative of thing(s), Act.19:19. __2. Intrans., and mostly impers., συμφέρει, it is expedient,…

sumphoneo.notes: συμ-φωνέω, -ῶ, [in LXX: Gen.14:3 (H2266) 2Ki.12:8 (9) (H225 ni.), Isa.7:2 (H5117), 4Ma.14:6 * ;] prop., to agree in sound, be in harmony (Plat., Arist.). Metaphorical, __(a) to agree with, agree together: Luk.5:36;…

sumphonesis.notes: συμ-φώνησις, -εως, ἡ (συμφωνέω), concord, agreement: before πρός, with accusative of person(s), 2Co.6:15.† (AS)

sumphonia.notes: συμφωνία, -ας, ἡ (σύμφωνος), [in LXX: Dan LXX TH Dan.3:5, 15, TH Dan.3:7, 10 (סוּמְפּוֹנְיָה) 4Ma.14:3 A * ;] __1. symphony, music: Luk.15:25 __2. a musical instrument (Polyb., al.; Da, ll. with; v MM, xxiv).† (AS)

sumphonos.notes: σύμφωνος, -ον (ούν, φωνή), [in LXX: Ecc.7:14 (עֻמָּה), 4Ma.7:7 4Mac 14:7 * ;] agreeing in sound. Metaphorical, harmonious, agreeing: ἐκ συμφώνου (for exx. from π., see Deiss., BS, 255), by agreement, 1Co.7:5.† (AS)

sumphuletes.notes: συμ-φυλέτης, -ου, ὁ (σύν, φυλή; cf. σύμφυλος, Aq.: Zec.13:7; and see Rutherford, NPhr., 255 f.) a fellow-tribesman, fellow-countryman: 1Th.2:14 (see M, Th., in l, and Intr. liii). Not found elsewhere.† (AS)

sumphuo.notes: συν-φύω (Rec. συμ-), [in LXX: Wis.13:13 * ;] to cause to grow together. Pass., to grow together or with (Plat. al.): Luk.8:7.† (AS)

sumphutos.notes: σύμ-φυτος, -ον (συμφύω, to make to grow together) [in LXX: Zec.11:2 (בָּצַר), Est.7:7 Est.7:8 א2, Amo.9:13, 3Ma.3:22 * ;] __1. congenital, innate (Plat., al.; 3Mac, l.with). __2. grown along with, united with: τ.…

sumpino.notes: συμπόσιον, -ου, τό (συμπίνω), [in LXX: Est.7:7 (מִשְׁתֶּה יַיִן) Est.4:17, 1Ma.16:16, 2Ma.2:27, 3Ma.4:16 3Mac 5:36 3Mac 6:33; σ. οἴνου, Sir 34:31 (31:31) Sir.35:5 (32:5), Sir.49:1 * ;] __1. a drinking party __2. By…

sumpleroo.notes: συν-πληρόω (Rec. συμπ-), -ῶ, [in LXX: Jer.25:12 (נָפַל) * ;] __1. to fill up completely (Thuc., of manning ships): of a ship filling with water, and by meton., of those on board, Luk.8:23. __2. to complete, fulfil:…

sumpnigo.notes: συν-πνίγω (Rec. συμπ-), to choke: of thronging by a crowd, Luk.8:42. Fig., of seed, τ. λόγον, Mat.13:22, Mrk.4:7, 19, Luk.8:14.† (AS)

sumpolites.notes: συν-πολίτης (Rec. σνμπ-), -ου, ὁ a fellow-citizen: pl., σ. τ. ἁγίων, opposite to ξένοι κ. πάροικοι, Eph.2:19 (Eur.; C.I., 6446; condemned by Atticists; see Rutherford, NPhr., 255 f.).† (AS)

sumporeuomai.notes: συν-πορεύομαι (Rec. σνμπ-), [in LXX chiefly for הָלַךְ ;] __1. to journey together (Plat., al.): with dative of person(s), Luk.7:11 14:25 24:15. __2. to come together: Mrk.10:1 (Plut., al.).† (AS)

sumposion.notes: 1. a drinking-party, symposium , (Theognis Elegiacus), etc. 2. the room in which such parties were given, drinking-room , (Lucian) (ML)

sumpsephizo.notes: συμ-ψηφίζω [in LXX: Jer.29:20 (Jer.49:20) A * ;] to reckon together, count up: Act.19:19.† (AS)

sumpsuchos.notes: σύν-ψυχος (Rec. συμ-), -ον (ψυχή), of one mind: Php.2:2.† (AS)

sunago.notes: συν-άγω [in LXX chiefly for אָסַף, also for קָבַץ, etc. (cf. Kennedy, Sources, 128) ;] to gather or bring together: of things, Jhn.6:12-13; before εἰς, Mat.3:12, al.; ἐκεῖ, Luk.12:18; ποῦ, Luk.12:17; συναγαγὼν πάντα…

sunagoge.notes: συν-αγωγή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for עֵדָה, also for קָהָל, etc. ;] prop., a bringing together; __1. of things, __(a) a gathering in of harvest; __(b) a collection of money. __2. Of persons, __(a) a collecting,…

sunagonizomai.notes: συν-αγωνίζομαι to strive together with, to help (prop., of sharing in a contest): with dative of person(s) and of thing(s), Rom.15:30.† (AS)

sunaichmalotos.notes: συν-αιχμάλωτος, -ου, ὁ a fellow prisoner (prop., of a captive in war, see Lft., Col., 234): Rom.16:7, Col.4:10, Phm 23.† (AS)

sunairo.notes: συν-αίρω [in LXX: Exo.23:5 (עָזַב) * ;] to take up together: σ. λόγον (of which there are several exx. in π.; see Deiss., LAE, 118 f.; MM, i, xxiv; M, Pr., 160), to settle accounts, Mat.18:23-24; before μετά, C.…

sunakoloutheo.notes: συν-ακολουθέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 2Ma.2:4 2Mac 2:6 * ;] to follow along with, accompany: with dative of person(s), Mrk.14:51, Luk.23:49; before μετά, with genitive of person(s), Mrk.5:37.† (AS)

sunalizo.notes: συν-αλίζω [in Al.: Psa.141:4 * ;] to assemble with: Act.1:4 (EV, mg., eating with, but on this rendering and on the force of the Pres. ptcp. here, see Field, Notes, 110).† (AS)

sunanabaino.notes: συν-ανα-βαίνω [in LXX for עָלָה ;] to go up with: with dative of person(s), before εἰς, with accusative loc., Mrk.15:41, Act.13:31.† (AS)

sunanakeimai.notes: συν-ανά-κειμαι [in LXX: 3Ma.5:39 * ;] to recline with or together at table: Mat.14:9, Mrk.6:22, Luk.7:49 14:15; with dative of person(s), Mat.9:10, Mrk.2:15, Luk.14:10.† (AS)

sunanamignumi.notes: συν-ανα-μίγνυμι (accusative to Bl., 8, -μείγνυμι), [in LXX: Hos.7:8 A ( hithpo.), Eze.20:18 A * ;] to mix up together; pass., reflex, and metaphorically, to associate with (Plut., Ath., al.): 1Co.5:9 5:11, 2Th.3:14…

sunanapauomai.notes: συν-ανα-παύομαι [in LXX: Isa.11:6 (רָבַץ) * ;] with dative of person(s), to lie down to rest with, sleep with. Metaphorical, to be refreshed in spirit with: Rom.15:32 (L, om.).† (AS)

sunantao.notes: συν-αντάω, -ῶ [in LXX for פָּגַע, פָּגַשׁ, קָרָא, קָרָה, etc. ;] to meet with: with dative of person(s), Luk.9:18, 37 22:10, Act.10:25, Heb.7:1, 10. Metaphorical, of events, to happen, befall (Ding. L., Plut., al.):…

sunantilambanomai.notes: συν-αντι-λαμβάνομαι [in LXX: Gen.30:8 R (†), Exo.18:22, Num.11:17 (נָשָׂא), Psa.89:21 (כּוּן ni.) * ;] to take hold with at the side for assistance; hence, to take a share in, help in bearing, and generally, help…

sunapago.notes: συν-απ-άγω [in LXX: Exo.14:6 (לָקַח) * ;] to lead away with or together (Ex, l.with). Pass., metaphorically (as συμπεριφέρομαι, see LS, see word), to be carried away with: with dative, Gal.2:13, 2Pe.3:17; of…

sunapollumi.notes: συν-απ-όλλυμι [in LXX chiefly for סָפָה ;] to destroy with or together. Pass., to perish together (with): with dative of person(s), Heb.11:31.† (AS)

sunapostello.notes: συν-απο-στέλλω [in LXX: Exo.33:2, 12 (שָׁלַח), I Est.5:2 * ;] to send along with: with accusative, 2Co.12:18 (cf. MM, xxiv).† (AS)

sunapothnesko.notes: συν-απο-θνήσκω [in LXX: Sir.19:10 * ;] to die with or together: Mrk.14:31, 2Co.7:3 (see Meyer, in l), 2Ti.2:11.† (AS)

sunarmologeo.notes: συν-αρμολογέω, -ῶ (ἁρμός, λέγω), = cl. συναρμόζω, to fit or frame together: of the parts of a building, Eph.2:21; of the members of the body, Eph.4:16 (Eccl.).† (AS)

sunarpazo.notes: συν-αρπάζω [in LXX: Pro.6:25 (לָקַח), 2Ma.3:27 2Mac 4:41, 4Ma.5:4 * ;] "perfective " of ἁοπάζω (see M, Pr., 113), __(a) to seize and carry away (so most commonly) : Act.27:15; __(b) to seize and hold: Luk.8:29,…

sunathleo.notes: συν-αθλέω, -ῶ = συναγωνίζομαι: with dative commod., Php.1:27; with dative of person(s), before ἐν, Php.4:3.† (AS)

sunathroizo.notes: συν-αθροίζω [in LXX chiefly for קָבַץ ;] to gather together, assemble: with accusative of person(s), Act.19:25; pass., Act.12:12.† ἀθροίζω (ἀθρόος, assembled in crowds, MM, VGT, see word; θρόος, a noise, tumult), [in…

sunauxano.notes: συν-αυξάνω [in LXX: 2Ma.4:4, 4Ma.13:27 * ;] to cause to increase or grow together. Pass., to grow together: Mat.13:30 (Xen., al.).† (AS)

sundeo.notes: συν-δέω [in LXX for שָׁבַץ, etc. ;] __(a) to bind together; __(b) to bind together with: pass., Heb.13:3.† (AS)

sundesmos.notes: σύν-δεσμος, -ου, ὁ (συνδέω), [in LXX for קֶשֶׁר, קְטַר, etc. ;] that which binds together, a bond: of the ligaments of the body, Col.2:19. Metaphorical, σ. ἀδικίας, Act.8:23; τ. τελειότητος, Col.3:14; τ. εἰρήνης,…

sundoulos.notes: σύν-δουλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Est.4:7, 9 4:17, 23 5:3, 6 6:6, 13 (כְּנָת) * ;] a fellow-servant: Mat.18:28-33 24:49; of servants of the same divine Lord, Col.1:7 4:7, Rev.6:11; so of angels, Rev.19:10 22:9.† (AS)

sundoxazo.notes: συν-δοξάζω __1. to join in approving (Arist.). __2. In NT only, to glorify together: pass., Rom.8:17.† (AS)

sundrome.notes: συνδρομή, -ῆς, ἡ (συντρέχω), [in LXX: Jdth.10:18, 3Ma.3:8 * ;] concourse, esp. of a riotous gathering: Act.21:30 (Arist., Polyb., al.).† (AS)

sunecho.notes: συν-έχω [in LXX for עָצָר, חָבַר, etc. ;] __1. to hold together (τ. συνέχον τ. πάντα, Wis.1:7): of closing the ears, Act.7:57 (τ. στόμα, Isa.52:15); to hem in, press on every side: Luk.8:45 19:43. __2. to hold fast;…

sunedomai.notes: συν-ήδομαι to rejoice together; with dative, to rejoice with or in: τ. νόμῳ τ. θεοῦ, Rom.7:22 (see Lft., Notes, 304).† (AS)

sunedrion.notes: συνέδριον, -ου, τό (σύν + ἕδρα, a seat) [in LXX: Pro.11:13 15:22, Jer.15:17 (סוֹד), Psa.26:4 (מַת), Pro.22:10 (דִּין) Pro.26:26 (קָהָל); Pro.24:7 27:22 31:23, 2Ma.14:5, 4Ma.17:17 * ;] __1. a council (Plat., Xen.,…

sunegeiro.notes: συν-εγείρω [in LXX: Exo.23:5 B2 (עָזַב), Isa.14:9 (עוּר pil.), 4Ma.2:14 * ;] to raise together: metaphorically, of the Christian's mystical resur­rection with Christ, Eph.2:6; pass., Col.2:12 3:1.† (AS)

suneidesis.notes: συν-είδησις, -εως, ἡ (συνεῖδον), [in LXX: Ecc.10:20 (מַדָּע), Wis.17:11, Sir.42:18 א * ;] __1. consciousness: with genitive obj., Heb.10:2, 1Pe.2:19. __2. In ethical sense, innate discernment, self-judging…

suneido.notes: συν-εῖδον irreg. aorist of συνοράω (see: εἶδον), [in LXX: 1Ma.4:21 א, 2Ma.2:24 2Mac 4:4 2Mac 4:41 R 2Ma.5:17 2Mac 7:4 2Mac 7:20 2Mac 8:8, 3Ma.2:8 A * ;] __1. to see together or at the same time (Arist., Xen.). __2.…

suneimi.notes: σύν-ειμι (εἰμί to be), [in LXX: Jer.3:20 (רֵעַ), al. ;] to be with: Luk.9:18 (WH, mg., συνήντησαν), Act.22:11.† (AS)

suneiserchomai.notes: συν-εισ-έρχομαι [in LXX: Job.22:4 (עִם בּוֹא), etc. ;] to enter together: with dative of person(s), before εἰς, with accusative loc., Jhn.6:22 18:15.† (AS)

sunekdemos.notes: συν-έκδημος, -ου, ὁ (ἔκδημος, abroad), a fellow-traveller: Act.19:29, 2Co.8:1.† (AS)

suneklektos.notes: συν-εκ-λεκτός, -ή, -όν chosen together with, co-elect: ἡ ἐν Βαβυλῶνι συνεκλεκτή (on the meaning, see ICC, in l), 1Pe.5:13.† (AS)

sunelauno.notes: συν-ελαύνω [in LXX: 2Ma.4:26 2Mac 4:42 2Mac 5:5 * ;] to drive together, force together: εἰς εἰρήνην, Act.7:26, Rec. (WH, RV, συνήλλασσεν).† (AS)

sunelikiotes.notes: συν-ηλικιώτης (ἡλικία), = συνῆλιζ (Æsch.), one of the same age, an equal in age: Gal.1:14.† (AS)

sunephistemi.notes: συν-εφ-ίστημι to place over. Pass., __1. to stand over (Thuc.). __2. to rise together: before κατά, with genitive of person(s) (against), Act.16:22.† (AS)

sunepimartureo.notes: συν-επι-μαρτυρέω, -ῶ to join in attesting, bear witness together with: Heb.2:4 (Arist., Polyb., a1.).† (AS)

sunepomai.notes: συν-έπομαι [in LXX: 2Ma.15:2, 3Ma.5:48 R 3Ma.6:21 * ;] to follow with, accompany: with dative of person(s), Act.20:4.† (AS)

sunerchomai.notes: συν-έρχομαι [in LXX for הָלַךְ, בּוֹא, אָסַף ni., etc. ;] __1. (cl.) to come together, assemble: Mrk.3:20 14:53, T, WH, txt., Act.1:6 2:6 10:27 16:13 19:32 21:22 22:30 28:17, 1Co.14:26; before εἰς, with accusative…

sunergeo.notes: συνεργέω, -ῶ [in LXX: I Est.7:2, 1Ma.12:1 * ;] __1. prop., to work together (with): absol., Mrk.16:20, 1Co.16:16, 2Co.6:1; with dative, Jas.2:22; dative commod., Rom.8:28, T, R, txt. (but see infr.). __2. In…

sunergos.notes: συνεργός, -όν [in LXX: 2Ma.8:7 Mac 14:5 * ;] a fellow-worker: with genitive of person(s), Rom.16:3, 9 16:21, Php.2:25 4:3, 1Th.3:2, Rec., Phm 1 24; θεοῦ (cf. Lft., Notes, 41, 188), 1Co.3:9 (pl.), 1Th.3:2 [WH] R, mg.…

sunesis.notes: σύνεσις, -εως, ἡ (συνίημι), [in LXX for בִּינָה and cogn. forms, מַשְׂכִּית, דַּעַת, חׇכְמָה, etc. ;] __1. a running or flowing together (Hom.). __2. (a) understanding: Luk.2:47, 1Co.1:19" (LXX), Eph.3:4, Col.1:9…

sunesthio.notes: συν-εσθίω [in LXX: Gen.43:32, Exo.18:12 (אָכַל), 4Ki.12:17 (בָּרָה), Psa.101:5 * ;] to eat with one: with dative of person(s), Luk.15:2, Act.10:41 11:3, 1Co.5:11; before μετά, with genitive of person(s), Gal.2:12.† (AS)

sunetheia.notes: συνήθεια, -ας, ἡ (ἦθος), [in LXX: 4Ma.2:12 4Mac 6:13 4Mac 13:22 4Mac 13:27 * ;] __1 1 intimacy. __2. habit, custom: Jhn.18:39, 1Co.11:16; with genitive obj. (cf. Æschin., 23, 37, and see MM, xxiv), habitual use,…

sunetos.notes: συνετός, ή, -όν (συνίημι), [in LXX for בִּין, חָכָם, etc. ;] intelligent, sagacious, understanding: Mat.11:25, Luk.10:21, Act.13:7, 1Co.1:19" (LXX) .† (AS)

suneudokeo.notes: συν-ευ-δοκέω, -ῶ [in LXX: 1Ma.1:57, 1Ma.4:28 א1, 2Ma.11:24 2Mac 11:35 * ;] to join in approving, consent, agree to or with: absol., Act.22:20; with dative of person(s), Rom.1:32; with dative of thing(s), Luk.11:48,…

suneuocheomai.notes: συν-ευωχέω, -ῶ to entertain together. Pass., to fare sumptuously or feast together or with: Ju 12; with dative of person(s), 2Pe.2:13 (Arist., Luc., al.).† (AS)

sungeneia.notes: συγγένεια, -ας, ἡ (συγγενής), [in LXX chiefly for מִשְׁפָּחָה ;] __1. kinship. __2. By meton., kinsfolk, kindred: Luk.1:61, Act.7:3, 14 † (AS)

sungenes.notes: σνγγενής, -ές (dative pl., -εῦσιν, Mrk.6:4, Luk.2:44, as though from -εύς, see Swete, Mk., l.with; Bl., § 8, 6; cf. 1Ma.10:89 A א2 *;) (σύν, γένος), [in LXX for דּוֹד, דּוֹדָה, מִשְׁפָּחָה ;] __1. congenital,…

sungnome.notes: συν-γνώμη (Rec. συγγ-), -ης, ἡ (συγγιγνώσκω, (a) to agree with; (b) to pardon), [in LXX: Sir prol.14 (B1N1 cm.) 313, 2Mac 14:20* ;] __1. confession. __2. fellow feeling; hence, concession, allowance: 1Co.7:6 (see…

suniemi.notes: συν-ίημι and συνίω (see Bl., § 23, 7), [in LXX chiefly for בִּין hi., שָׂכַל hi. ;] __1. to bring or set together. __2. Metaphorical, to perceive, understand: Mat.13:13-15 (LXX), Mat.13:19 15:10, Mrk.4:9 (WH, mg.),…

sunistemisunistao.notes: συν-ίστημι [in LXX for צָוָה pi., קָהַל ni., etc.; 1Ma.12:43, 2Ma.4:24, 3Ma.1:19 (and frequently in these books) ;] __1. trans., __(a) to commend, recommend: with accusative of person(s), 2Co.3:1 6:4 10:12 10:18; id.…

sunoche.notes: συν-οχή, -ῆς, ή (συνέχω), [in LXX: Jdg.2:3 (?), Job.30:3 (שׁוֹא), Job.38:28 A; Mic.5:1 (4:14), Jer.52:5 (מָצוֹר) * ;] __1. a holding or being held together. __2. a narrow place (Hom., al.). Metaphorical, straits,…

sunodeuo.notes: συν-οδεύω [in LXX: Zec.8:21 א1 (הָלַךְ), Tob.5:16 א, Wis.6:23 * ;] to journey with: with dative of person(s), Act.9:7 (Plut., al.).† (AS)

sunodia.notes: συνοδία, -ας, ἡ [in LXX: Neh.7:5, 64 (יָחַשׂ) * ;] __1. a journey in company (Plut., al.). __2. By meton., κ company of travellers, a caravan: Luk.2:44.† (AS)

sunodino.notes: συν-ωδίνω to be in travail together (Arist.): metapb. (Eur.), Rom.8:22.† (AS)

sunoikeo.notes: συν-οικέω, -ῶ [in LXX for בַּעַל etc. ;] to dwell together: of man and wife (Hdt., al.), with dative of person(s), 1Pe.3:7,† (AS)

sunoikodomeo.notes: συν-οίκοδομέω, -ῶ [in LXX: I Est.5:68 * ;] __1. to build together or with (with dative of person(s), 1Esdras, l.with). __2. Of various materials, to build up together (Thuc., al.): metaphorically, of Christians,…

sunomileo.notes: συν-ομιλέω, -ῶ to converse with (absol., FlJ, Bl, v, 13, 1; before μετά, Cebes, 13): with dative of person(s), Act.10:27.† (AS)

sunomoreo.notes: συν-ομορέω, -ῶ to border on: with dative, Act.18:7 (Byz.).† (AS)

sunomosia.notes: συνωμοσία, -ας, ή (συνόμνυμι, to conspire), [in Sm.: Eze.22:25 * ;] a conspiracy: Act.23:13.† (AS)

suntasso.notes: συν-τάσσω [in LXX chiefly for צָוָה pi. ;] to prescribe, ordain, arrange: with dative of person(s), Mat.21:6 26:19 27:10" (LXX) .† (AS)

sunteleia.notes: συντέλεια, -ας, ἡ (συντελέω), [in LXX chiefly for כָּלָה and (in Da) for קוּם ;] __1. in cl., a joint payment or contribution for public service; hence, generally, joint action (Plat.). __2. In late writers (Polyb.,…

sunteleo.notes: συν-τελέω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for כָּלָה pi., also for עָשָׂה, תָּמַם, etc. ;] "perfective" of τελέω (cf. M, Pr., 118), __1. to complete, finish, bring to an end: Luk.4:13; pass., Luk.4:2, Jhn.2:3, WH, mg.,…

suntemno.notes: συν-τέμνω [in LXX chiefly for חָרַץ ;] __1. to cut in pieces. __2. to cut down, cut short: metapb., λόγον, Rom.9:28 (LXX) (Hdt., Plat., Fur., al.).† (AS)

suntereo.notes: συν-τηρέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Eze.18:19 (שָׁמַר), Da TH Dan.7:28 A (נְטַר), Sir.13:12 and freq., Tob.1:11, 2Ma.12:42, al. ;] "perfective" of τηρέω (see M, Pr., 113, 116), to preserve, keep safe, keep close: with accusative…

sunthapto.notes: συν-θάπτω to bury together with: in symbolical sense, of baptism, σὐτῷ (with Christ), Rom.6:4, Col.2:12.† (AS)

sunthlao.notes: συν-θλάω, -ῶ [in LXX for מָחַץ etc. ;] to crush together, crush: Mat.21:44 ([WH], R, mg., om.), Luk.20:18.† (AS)

sunthlibo.notes: συν-θλίβω [in LXX: Ecc.12:6 א A (רָצַץ), Sir.34:14 (31:14), 1Ma.15:14 A * ;] to press together, press on all sides: Mrk.5:24, 31.† (AS)

sunthrupto.notes: συν-θρύπτω to break in pieces, crush: metaphorically, τ. καρδίαν, Act.21:13.† (AS)

suntithemi.notes: συν-τίθημι [in LXX: 1Ki.22:13 (קָשַׁר), etc. ;] to place or put together, in various senses. Mid., __(a) to observe, perceive; __(b) to determine, agree, covenant: with inf., Luk.22:5; before τοῦ, with inf.,…

suntomos.notes: συν-τόμως, adv. (συντέμνω) [in LXX: Pro.13:23 23:28, 3Ma.5:25 * ;] concisely, briefly: Act.24:4 (for a similar ex., see MM, xxiv).† (AS)

suntrecho.notes: συν-τρέχω [in LXX: Psa.50:18 (עִם רָצָה), עִם רָצָה), 2Ma.2:1-32, Jdt.4.1-15 * ;] to run together or with: before ἐκεῖ, Mrk.6:33; πρόσ, with accusative, Act.3:11; metaphorically, 1Pe.4:4.† (AS)

suntribo.notes: συν-τρίβω [in LXX chiefly for שָׁבַר ;] to shatter, break in pieces: Mat.12:20 (LXX), Mrk.5:4 14:3, Jhn.19:36" (LXX), Rev.2:27; of persons and parts of the body, to break, crush, bruise: Luk.9:39; figuratively,…

suntrimma.notes: σύν-τριμμα, -τος, τό (συντρίβω), [in LXX (Lev.21:19, al.) chiefly for שֶׁבֶר; also for שֹׁד (Isa.59:7), etc. ;] a fracture (Arist.; Le, l.with, al.). Metaphorical, calamity, destruction (Isa, l.with, Wis.3:3, al.):…

suntrophos.notes: σύν-τροφος, -ου, ὁ (συντρέφομαι, to be brought up together) [in LXX: 3Ki.12:24, 1Ma.1:6 R, 2Ma.9:29 * ;] __1. prop., one nourished or brought up with, a foster-brother: Act.13:1 EV. __2. In Hellenistic usage, as a…

suntugchano.notes: συν-τυγχάνω [in LXX: 2Ma.8:14 * ;] to meet with, fall in with: with dative of person(s), Luk.8:19.† (AS)

sunupokrinomai.notes: συν-υπο-κρίνομαι to play a part with, dissemble with: with dative, Gal.2:13 (Polyb.).† (AS)

sunupourgeo.notes: συν-υπουργέω, -ῶ to help together, join in serving: with dative instr., 2Co.1:11.† (AS)

surakousai.notes: ionic Συρήκουσαι, doric Συράκοσαι, and Σῦράκοσσαι, Syracuse , (Herdotus Historicus), etc. (ML)

suro.notes: σύρω [in LXX: 4Ki.17:13 (סָחַב), 4Ma.6:1, al. ;] to draw, drag: with accusative of thing(s), Jhn.21:8, Rev.12:4; with accusative of person(s), of taking to trial or punish­ment, Act.8:3; ἔξω τ. πόλεως, Act.14:19; ἐπὶ…

surophoinissa.notes: Συροφοινίκισσα (Rec. -φοίνισσα), -ης, ἡ a Syropænician woman: Mrk.7:26, WH, txt., RV (cf. ἡ Φοινίκη Συρία, Diod., 19, 93).† (AS)

suros.notes: Σύρος, -ου, ὁ, fem. Σύρα a Syrian: Luk.4:27, Mrk.7:26, WH, mg.† (AS)

surtis.notes: Σύρτις (L, σῦρτις), -εως, accusative -ιν, ἡ, Syrtis, the name of two large sand-banks on the Libyan coast: Act.27:17.† (AS)

suschematizo.notes: συν-σχηματιζω (Rec. συσ-), (σχῆμα), to conform to (Arist.). Pass., to be conformed to, conform oneself to: Rom.12:2, 1Pe.1:14.† (AS)

susparasso.notes: συ-σπαράσσω to convulse completely: with accusative of person(s), Mrk.9:20, Luk.9:42 (cf. σπαράσσω).† (AS)

sussemon.notes: σύσ-σημον (T, συν-), -ου, τό (σῆμα), [in LXX: Jdg.20:38, 40 (מַשְׂאֵת), Isa.5:26 49:22 62:10 (נֵס) * ;] a fixed sign or signal: Mrk.14:44. (Strab., Plut., al.; condemned by Atticists, see Rutherford, NPhr., 492 f.) †…

sussomos.notes: σύν-σωμος (Rec. συσσ-), -ον (σῶμα), of the same body: Eph.3:6 (Eccl.).† (AS)

sustasiastes.notes: στασιαστής (στασιάζω, to stir up sedition, Jdth.7:15, 2Ma.4:30 2Mac 14:6 *) = Att.. στασιώτης, a rebel, revolutionist, one who stirs up sedition: Mrk.15:7 (FlJ, al.).† συ-στασιαστής, -οῦ, ὁ, a fellow-rioter:…

sustatikos.notes: συ-στατικός (Tr. συν-), -ή, -όν (συνίστημι), __1. for putting together, constructive. __2. for bringing together, introductory, commendatory: ἐπιστολή (Arist., Diog. L., al.; and see MM, xxiv), 2Co.3:1.† (AS)

sustauroomai.notes: συν-σταυρόω (Rec. συσ-), -ῶ, to crucify together with: pass., with dative, Jhn.19:32; id. before σύν, Mat.27:44, Mrk.15:32. Metaphorical, of the mystical death of the Christian with Christ: Rom.6:6, Gal.2:20…

sustello.notes: συν-στέλλω (Rec. συσ-), [in LXX: Jdg.8:28 11:33 (כָּנַע ni.), Sir.4:31, al. ;] __1. to draw together, contract, shorten: of time, 1Co.7:29. __2. to wrap up: of enshrouding a body for burial (Eur.), with accusative,…

sustenazo.notes: συν-στενάζω (Rec. συσ-), to groan together or with: Rom.8:22 (with dative of person(s), Eur., Ion., 935).† (AS)

sustoicheo.notes: συν-στοιχέω (Rec. συσ-), -ῶ, to stand in the same rank or line (Polyb). Metaphorical, to correspond to: Gal.4:25 (see Lft., in l).† (AS)

sustratiotes.notes: συν-στρατιώτης (Rec. συσ-), -ου, ὁ a fellow-soldier (Plat., Xen., al.). Metaphorical, of fellowship in Christian service: Php.2:25, Phm 2 .† (AS)

sustrepho.notes: συ-στέφω [in LXX chiefly for קָשַׁר ;] __1. to twist together: with accusative of thing(s), Act.28:3. __2. Pass., of persons, to gather themselves together: Mat.17:22 (WH, R, mg.).† (AS)

sustrophe.notes: συ-στροφή, -ῆς, ἡ (συστρέφω), [in LXX: Rom.4:19 13:12 (צָרַר); σ. ποιεῖσθαι, Amo.7:10 (קָשַׁר), etc. ;] __1. a twisting together (Plat.). __2. a concourse, a riotous gathering (Polyb.): Act.19:40; σ. ποιεῖσθαι (Am,…

suzao.notes: συν-ζάω (Rec. συζ-), to live with: opposite to συναποθανεῖν, 2Co.7:3; of life in union with Christ, here and hereafter, Rom.6:8, 2Ti.2:11.† (AS)

suzeteo.notes: συν-ζητέω (Rec. συζ-), -ῶ, [in LXX: Neh.2:4 AB1 (בָּקַשׁ pi.) * ;] __1. to search or examine together (Plat.). __2. In NT (and π., see MM, xxiii; also in MGr., see Kennedy, Sources, 155), to discuss, dispute:…

suzetetes.notes: συν-ζητητής (Rec. συζ-), -οῦ, ἁ (συνζητέω), a disputer, disputant: 1Co.1:20.† (AS)

suzeugnumi.notes: συν-ζεύγνυμι (Rec. συζ-), [in LXX: Eze.1:11, 23 (חָבַר) * ;] to yoke to­gether: metaphorically, of union in wedlock, Mat.19:6, Mrk.10:9.† (AS)

suzoopoieo.notes: συν-ζωο-ποιέω (Rec. συζ-), ῶ-, to make alive or quicken together with; metaphorically, of the spiritual life: τ. Χριστῷ, Eph.2:5; σὺν τ. Χ., Col.2:13.† (AS)

suzugos.notes: σύν-ζυγος (Rec. ουζ-), or as WH, mg., Σύνζυγος, -ον (συνζεύγνυμι), [in Aq., Eze.23:21 * ;] a yoke fellow. Prob., as proper name, Σ. γνήσιε, genuinely Synzygus, S. properly so-called, Php.4:3 (see ICC, Lft., in l; MM,…

tabernai.notes: ταβέρνη (Lat. taberna), see: Τρεῖς Ταβέρναι. Τρεῖς Ταβέρναι (see: ταβέρνη), Three Taverns (Lit. Tres Tabernae) a halting place on the Appian Way: Act.28:15 (see DB, iv, 690).† (AS)

tabitha.notes: Ταβειθά (Rec. Ταβιθά), ἡ (Aram. טַבְיְתָא or טְבִיתָא), Tabitha: Act.9:36, 40 (cf. Δορκάς).† (AS)

tacha.notes: τάχα (ταχύς), adv., [in LXX: Wis.13:6 14:19 * ;] __(a) (chiefly poët.), quickly, presently; __(b) perhaps: Rom.5:7, Phm 15.† (AS)

tacheos.notes: ταχέως (ταχύς) adv., [in LXX chiefly for מְהֵרָה and cognate forms ;] quickly, hastily: Luk.14:21 16:6, Jhn.11:31, 1Co.4:19, Php.2:19, 24, 2Ti.4:9; with suggestion of rashness, Gal.1:6, 2Th.2:2, 1Ti.5:22.† (AS)

tachinos.notes: ταχινός, -ή, -όν [in LXX: Pro.1:16, Hab.1:6, Isa.59:7 (מָהַר), Wis.13:2, Sir.11:22 18:26 * ;] poët. and late for ταχύς, swift: of swift approach, 2Pe.1:14 2:1.† (AS)

tachion.notes: τάχειον, see: ταχύ. ταχύ (neut. of ταχύς), adv., [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi. ;] quickly, speedily, forthwith: Mat.5:25 28:7-8, Mrk.9:39, Luk.15:22, Jhn.11:29, Rev.2:16 3:11 11:14 22:7, 12 22:2 o. Compar., τάχειον…

tachista.notes: τάχιστα, see: ταχύ. ταχύ (neut. of ταχύς), adv., [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi. ;] quickly, speedily, forthwith: Mat.5:25 28:7-8, Mrk.9:39, Luk.15:22, Jhn.11:29, Rev.2:16 3:11 11:14 22:7, 12 22:2 o. Compar., τάχειον…

tachos.notes: τάχος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for parts and derivatives of מָהַר ;] swiftness, speed. Adverbially, ἐν τ. (ταχέως), quickly, speedily, soon: Luk.18:8, Act.12:7 22:18 25:4, Rom.16:20, 1Ti.3:14, Rev.1:1 22:6.† (AS)

tachu.notes: ταχύ (neut. of ταχύς), adv., [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi. ;] quickly, speedily, forthwith: Mat.5:25 28:7-8, Mrk.9:39, Luk.15:22, Jhn.11:29, Rev.2:16 3:11 11:14 22:7, 12 22:2 o. Compar., τάχειον (T, Rec. τάχιον), =…

tachus.notes: ταχύς, -εῖα, -ύ [in LXX chiefly for מָהַר pi. ;] quick, swift, speedy: opposite to βραδύς, Ja 119.† (AS)

tagma.notes: τάγμα, -τος, τό (τάσσω), [in LXX chiefly for דֶּגֶל, also for חַי, רַגְלִי ;] that which has been arranged or placed in order; esp. as military term, a company, troop, division, rank: metaphorically, 1Co.15:23 (see…

taktos.notes: τακτός, -ή, -όν (τάσσω), [in LXX: Job.12:5 (שַׁאֲנָן) * ;] ordered, fixed, stated: ἡμέρα, Act.12:21,† (AS)

talaiporeo.notes: ταλαιπωρέω, -ῶ (ταλαίπωρος), [in LXX Chiefly for שָׁדַד pu. ;] __1. to do hard labour, suffer hardship or distress: Jas.4:9. __2. In cl. occasionally trans., to weary, distress (so Psa.17:9, Isa.33:1).† (AS)

talaiporia.notes: ταλαιπωρία, -ας, ἡ (ταλαίπωρος) [in LXX chiefly for שֹׁד ;] __1. hard work (Hippocr.). __2. hardship, suffering, distress: Rom.3:16 (LXX) pl., Jas.5:1 (see B1., § 32, 6; WM, 220; Swete, Mk., 153). (Hdt., Thuc., al.;…

talaiporos.notes: ταλαίωρος, -ον [in LXX: Psa.137:8 (שָׁדַד), Isa.33:1 (ποιεῖν τ., שָׁדַד), Tob.13:10, Wis.3:11, al. ;] distressed, miserable, wretched: Rom.7:24, Rev.3:17.† (AS)

talantiaios.notes: ταλαντιαῖος, -α, -ον (τάλαντον), __(a) worth a talent; __(b) of a talent's weight: Rev.16:21.† (AS)

talanton.notes: τάλαντομ, -ον, τ [in LXX for כִּכָּר ;] __1. a balance (Hom.). __2. that which is weighed, a talent; __(a) a talent in weight (in Horn. always of gold); __(b) a sum of money, whether gold or silver, equivalent to a…

talitha.notes: ταλειθά (Rec. ταλιθά), (Aram. טְלִיתָא, see Dalman, Gr., 150), talitha, i.e. maiden: Mrk.5:41.† (AS)

tameion.notes: ταμεῖον (late syncopated form of cl. ταμιεῖον; see M, Pr., 44 f.; Bl., § 6, 5; Thackeray, Gr., 63 ff.; Deiss., BS, 182 f.), -ον, τό, [in LXX chiefly for חֶדֶר ;] __1. a treasury (Thuc., al). __2. a store-chamber…

tapeinoo.notes: ταπεινόω, -ῶ (ταπεινός), [in LXX chiefly for עָנָה, also for שָׁפֵל, כָּנַע, etc. ;] to make low: ὄρος (βουνόν), Luk.3:5 (LXX). Metaphorical, to humble, abase: Mat.18:4 23:12, Luk.14:11 18:14, 2Co.11:7 12:21,…

tapeinophrosune.notes: ταπεινοφροσύνη, -ης, ἡ (ταπεινόφρων) lowliness of mind, humility: Act.20:19, Eph.4:2, Php.2:3, Col.3:12, 1Pe.5:5; of a false humility, Col.2:18, 23 (rare outside of NT, but found in bad sense in FlJ, BJ, iv, 9, 2;…

tapeinos.notes: ταπεινός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for שָׁפָל, עָנִי, etc. ;] low-lying; metaphorically, __(a) lowly, of low degree, brought low: Jas.1:9, 2Co.7:6 (cf. Sir.25:23); οἱ τ., opposite to δυνάσται, Luk.1:52; τ. ταπεινοῖς (neut.,…

tapeinosis.notes: ταπείνωσις, -εως, ἡ (ταπεινόω), [in LXX chiefly for עֳנִי ;] abasement, humiliation, low estate: Luk.1:48, Act.8:33" (LXX), Php.3:21, Jas.1:10 (Plat., Arist., and later writers).† (AS)

taphe.notes: ταφή, -ῆς, ἡ (θάπτω), [in LXX chiefly for קָבַר and cognate forms ;] burial: Mat.27:7 (Hdt., al.; for other meanings, see LS, see word, Deiss., BS, 355 f., MM xxiv).† (AS)

taphos.notes: τάφος, -ου, ὁ (θάντω), [in LXX chiefly for קֶבֶר ;] __1. a burial (Hom., al.). __2. a grave, tomb (Res., Hdt., al.): Mat.23:27, 29 27:61, 64 27:66 28:1, Rom.3:13" (LXX) .† (AS)

tarachos.notes: τάραχος, -ου, ὁ (ταράσσω), [in LXX for מְהוּמָה, etc. ;] later form of ταραχή (Xen., al.; see Thackeray, Gr., 159): Act.12:18 19:23.† (AS)

tarasso.notes: ταράσσω [in LXX for בָּהַל, רָגַז, etc., forty-six words in all ;] to disturb, trouble, stir up: primarily in physical sense (Hom., Eur.): τ. ὕδωρ, Jhn.1:5 [4], Jhn.1:7. Metaphorical, of the mind (Æsch., Plat., al.),…

tarsos.notes: Ταρσός, -oῦ Tarsus, a city of Cilicia: Act.9:30 11:25 22:3.† (AS)

tartaroo.notes: ταρταρόω, -ῶ (Τάρταρος, a Greek name for the underworld, esp. the abode of the damned), to cast into hell: 2Pe.2:4 (see Mayor, in l).† (AS)

tasso.notes: τάσσω [in LXX chiefly for שׂוּם, also for צָוָה pi., נָתַן, etc. ;] primarily, in military sense, then generally, to draw up in order, arrange in place, assign, appoint, order: with dative (accusative) and inf. (Bl.,…

tauros.notes: ταῦρος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for שׁוֹר ;] a bull: Mat.22:4, Act.14:13, Heb.9:13 10:4.† (AS)

taxis.notes: τάξις, -εως, ἡ (τάσσω), [in LXX: Job.38:12 (מָקוֹם), Psa.110:4 (דִּבְרָה), 2Ma.9:18, al. ;] __1. an arranging, hence, in military sense, disposition of an army, battle array (Thuc., al.). __2. arrangement, order…

techne.notes: τέχνη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX for חׇכְמָה, מַעֲשֶׂה, עֲבֹדָה ;] art, craft, trade: Act.17:29 18:3, Rev.18:22 (WH, R, mg., om.).† (AS)

technites.notes: τεχνίτης, -ου, ὁ (τέχνη), [in LXX for חָרָשׁ, etc. ;] a craftsman, artificer: Act.19:24, 38, Rev.18:22; of God (Wis.13:1), Heb.11:10.† SYN.: δημιουργός, which see, and cf. Tr., Syn., § cv (AS)

teichos.notes: τεῖχος, -ους, τό [in LXX chiefly for חוֹמָה ;] a wall, esp. that about a town: Act.9:25, 2Co.11:33, Heb.11:30, Rev.21:12-19 † (AS)

tekmerion.notes: τεκμήριον, -ου, τό (τέκμαρ, a mark, sign), [in LXX: Wis.5:4 19:13, 3Ma.3:24 * ;] a sure sign, a positive proof: Act.1:1-26 (for exx., see MM, xxiv).† SYN.: ἔνδειγμα, q.v (AS)

teknion.notes: τεκνίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of τέκνον), a little child: as a term of endearment, in voc. pl., Jhn.13:33, Gal.4:19, 1Jn.2:1 Jn 2:12 Jn 2:28 Jn 3:7 Jn 3:13 Jn 4:4 Jn 5:21.† (AS)

teknogoneo.notes: τεκνογονέω, -ῶ to beget or bear children: 1Ti.5:14 (Anthol.).† (AS)

teknon.notes: τέκνον, -ου, τό (τίκτω), [in LXX chiefly for בֵּן, also for יֶלֶד, etc. ;] that which is begotten, born (cf. Scottish bairn), a child of either sex: Mrk.13:12, Luk.1:7, Act.7:5; pl., Mat.7:11, Mrk.7:27, Luk.1:17,…

teknotropheo.notes: τεκνο-τροφέω, -ῶ to rear young (of bees, Arist.), to bring up children: 1Ti.5:10.† (AS)

teko.notes: τήκω [in LXX for מָסַס ni., מָקַק ni., מוּג ni., etc. ;] trans., to melt, melt down; pass., to melt, melt away: 2Pe.3:12.† (AS)

tekton.notes: τέκτων, -ονος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for חָרָשׁ ;] an artificer in wood, stone or metal, but esp. a carpenter (see MM, xxiv): Mat.13:55, Mrk.6:3.† (AS)

telaugos.notes: τηλαυγῶς adv. (τῆλε, afar, + αὐγή, radiance), poët. and in late prose, at a distance clearly: Mrk.8:25 (W, mg., δηλαυγῶς).† (AS)

teleioo.notes: τελειόω, -ῶ (τέλειος), [in LXX: Exo.29:9, Lev.4:5, al. (τ. χεῖρ מָלֵא pi.), 2Ki.22:26, al. (תָּמַם), Wis.4:13, al. (cf. Westc., He., 64) ;] __1. bring to an end, finish, accomplish, fulfil: Jhn.4:34, Act.20:24; of…

teleios.notes: τέλειος, -α, -ον (τέλος), [in LXX chiefly for שָׁלֵם, תָּמִים and cogn. forms ;] having reached its end, finished, mature, complete, perfect; __1. of persons, primarily of physical development, __(a) full-grown,…

teleiosis.notes: τελείωσις, -εως, ἡ (τελειόω), [in LXX chiefly for מִלֻּא, Exo.29:22, al. ;] fulfilment, completion, perfection: Luk.1:45, Heb.7:11.† (AS)

teleiotes.notes: τελειότης, -ητος, ἡ (τέλειος), [in LXX: Jdg.9:16, 19 (תָּמִים), Pro.11:3 (תֻּמָּה), Wis.6:15 12:17, Jer.2:2 * ;] perfection, completeness: Col.3:14, Heb.6:1 (AS)

teleo.notes: τελέω, -ῶ (τέλος), [in LXX for כָּלָה, pi., etc. ;] __1. to bring to an end, complete, finish: τ. δρόμον, 2Ti.4:7; τ. λόγους, Mat.7:28 19:1 26:1; τ. παραβολάς, Mat.13:53; τ. πόλεις, Mat.10:23; pass., Rev.15:8 20:3, 5…

telesphoreo.notes: τελεσφορέω, -ῶ (τέλος, φέρω) [in LXX: 4Ma.13:20 * ;] __(a) of plants, to bring fruit to perfection (Theophr.): Luk.8:14 __(b) of females, to bear perfect offspring (Artemid.; 4Mac, l.with).† (AS)

teleutao.notes: τελευτάω, -ῶ (τελευτή), [in LXX chiefly for מוּת ;] __1. trans., to complete, finish; esp. τ. τ. αἰῶνα, τ. βίον, to complete life, to die (Æsch., Hdt., al.). __2. Intrans., to come to an end, hence, to die (Hdt.,…

teleute.notes: τελευτή, -ῆς, ἡ (τελέω), [in LXX chiefly for מוּת, מָוֶת ;] __1. a finishing. __2. an end: τ. βίου (Hdt., al.): also without βίου, the end of life, death (Hdt., Plat., al.): Mat.2:15.† (AS)

telones.notes: τελώνης, -ου, ὁ (τέλος, ὠνέομαι) __1. a farmer of taxes (Let. publicanus). __2. A subordinate of the former, who collected taxes or tolls in a particular district, a tax-gatherer (EV, publican): Mat.5:46 10:3,…

telonion.notes: τελώνιον, -ου, τό (τελώνης), a custom house (so in MGr.), tollhouse, place of toll: Mat.9:9, Mrk.2:14, Luk.5:27.† (AS)

telos.notes: τέλος, -ους, τό [in LXX for קֵץ, etc.; εἰς τὸ τ., chiefly for נֶצַח and cognate forms ;] __1. end: most frequently of the termination or limit of an act or state (in NT also of the end of a period of time, cl.…

tephroo.notes: τεφρόω, -ῶ (τέφρα, ashes; Wis.2:3, al.), to burn to ashes: 2Pe.2:6.† (AS)

teras.notes: τέρας, -ατος, τό [in LXX chiefly for מוֹפֵת ;] a wonder, marvel: in NT always pl., τ. κ. σημεῖα, Mat.24:24, al. (see: σημεῖον) (AS)

tereo.notes: τηρέω, -ῶ [in LXX for שָׁמַר, נָצַר, etc. ;] __1. to watch over, guard, keep, preserve: Mat.27:36, Act.16:23, al.; ἑαυτόν, 2Co.11:9, 1Ti.5:22, Jas.1:27, Ju 21; before εἰς, Jhn.12:7, Act.25:21, 1Pe.1:4, 2Pe.2:4 2:9…

teresis.notes: τήρησις, -εως, ἡ (τηρέω), [in LXX: Wis.6:18, (Sir.32:23), 1Ma.5:18, 2Ma.3:40, 3Ma.5:44 * ;] __1. a watching, hence, imprisonment, ward (see Deiss., BS, 267): Act.4:3 5:18. __2. a keeping: τ. ἐντολῶν (cf. Sir, Wis,…

tessarakonta.notes: τεσσεράκοντα (Rec. τεσσαρ-, v, WH, App., 150; M, Pr., 45 f.; Thackeray, Gr., 62 f., 73 f.), οἱ, αἱ, τά, indecl., forty: Mat.4:2, Mrk.1:13, Luk.4:2, Jhn.2:20, Act.1:3, al (AS)

tessarakontaetes.notes: τεσσερακονταετίς (Τ, -έτης; Rec. τεσσαρ-, see supr.), -ές of forty years, forty years old: Act.7:23 13:18.† (AS)

tessares.notes: τέσσαρες (and Ion. and late -ερεν, and late accusative -εν; see WH, App., 150; M, Pr., 36, 45 f.), οἱ, αἱ, -αρα, τά, genitive, -ων, four: Mat.24:31, Mrk.2:3, Luk.2:37, Jhn.11:17 19:23, Act.10:11, Rev.4:4, 6, al (AS)

tetartaios.notes: τεταρταῖος, -α, -ον (τέταρτος), [in LXX: 2Ki.3:4 A (רְבִיעִי) * ;] of or on the fourth day: τ. εἶναι (Hdt., τ. γενέσθαι), to be four days dead, Jhn.11:39.† (AS)

tetartos.notes: τέταρτος, -η, -ον [in LXX chiefly for רְבִיעִי ;] fourth: Mat.14:25, Mrk.6:48, Act.10:30, Rev.4:7 6:7-8 8:12 16:8 21:19.† (AS)

tetradion.notes: τετράδιον, ου, τό a quarternion, a group of four: στρατιωτῶν, Act.12:4 (Philo). (AS)

tetragonos.notes: τετράγωνος, -ον (τετρα-, see supr., + γωνία), [in LXX for רָבַע and cogn. forms ;] square: Rev.21:16 (Hdt., Plat., al.).† (AS)

tetrakischilioi.notes: τετρακισ-χίλιοι, -αι, -α four thousand: Mat.15:38 16:10, Mrk.8:9, 20, Act.21:38.† (AS)

tetramenos.notes: τετράμηνος, -ον [in LXX (neut.): Jdg.19:2 A Jdg.20:47 A (עַרְבָּעָה חֳדָשִׁים ) * ;] of four months, four months; as subst., τ. (sc. ὥρα, but neut. in Rec.), Jhn.4:35 (Thuc., al.).† (AS)

tetrapous.notes: τετρά-πους, -ουν [in LXX chiefly for בְּהֵמָה ;] four-footed: of beasts, neut. pl., Act.10:12 11:6, Rom.1:23.† (AS)

tetrarcheo.notes: τετρααρχέω (Rec. τετραρχ-; see WH, App., 145), -ῶ (τετραάρχης), to be tetrarch: with genitive, Luk.3:1 (FlJ, BJ, iii, 10, 7).† (AS)

tetrarches.notes: τετραάρχης (Rec. τετράρχης, see supr.), -ου, ὁ (τετρα- in corp. = τέτορα, Doric for τέσσαρα, + ἄρχω), a tetrarch, i.e. __(a) prop., the governor of a fourth part of a region (Strab.); __(b) any petty ruler (Plut.);…

thaddaios.notes: Θαδδαῖος, -ου, ὁ (Aram. תּדִאַי, תַּדַּי), Thaddæus: Mat.10:3, Mrk.3:18 (WH, mg., Λεββαῖος, which see).† (AS)

thalassa.notes: θάλασσα, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for יָם ;] the sea: Mat.23:15, Mrk.11:23, Luk.21:25, Rom.9:27, Heb.11:12, Jas.1:6, Ju 13, Rev.7:1, al.; τὸ πέλαγος τῆς θ. (see Tr., ut infr.), Mat.18:6; ὁ οὐρανὸς κ. ἡ γῆ κ. ἡ θ., of…

thalpo.notes: θάλπω [in LXX for סָכַן, etc. ;] to heat, warm; metaphorically, __(a) to inflame (Trag.); __(b) to foster, cherish: Eph.5:29, 1Th.2:7.† (AS)

thamar.notes: Θάμαρ (Tr., Θαμάρ), ἡ (Heb. תָּמָר, Gen.38:6), Tamar: Mat.1:3.† (AS)

thambeo.notes: θαμβέω, -ῶ (θάμβος) [in LXX for בָּעַת ni., pi., etc. ;] __1. to be amazed. __2. In late Gk., to astonish, terrify (2Ki.22:5): pass., Mrk.1:27 10:32; before ἐπί with dative of thing(s), Mrk.10:24.† (AS)

thambos.notes: θάμβος, -ους, τό [in LXX for פַּחַד, etc. ;] amazement: Luk.4:36 5:9, Act.3:10.† (AS)

thanatephoros.notes: θανατη-φόρος, -ον (θάνατος, φέρω), [in LXX: Num.18:22 (מוּת), Job.33:23, 4Ma.8:18 4Mac 8:26 4Mac 15:26 * ;] deadly, death-bringing: Jas.3:8.† (AS)

thanatoo.notes: θανατόω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for מוּת hi., ho., also for הָרַג, etc. ;] to put to death: with accusative, Mat.10:21 26:59 27:1, Mrk.13:12 14:55, Luk.21:16, 2Co.6:9, 1Pe.3:18; pass., Rom.8:36. Metaphorical: Rom.8:13;…

thanatos.notes: θάνατος, -ου, ὁ (θνήσκω), [in LXX chiefly for מָוֶת ,מוּת, sometimes דֶּבֶר ;] death; __1. of the death of the body, whether natural or violent: Jhn.11:13, Php.2:27, Heb.7:23, al; opposite to ζωή, Rom.8:38, Php.1:20;…

thapto.notes: θάπτω [in LXX chiefly for קָבַר ;] to bury: with accusative, Mat.8:21-22 14:12, Luk.9:59-60, Act.5:6, 9-10; pass., Luk.16:22, Act.2:29, 1Co.15:4.† (AS)

thara.notes: Θαρά (Rec. Θάρα), ὁ, indecl. (Heb. תֶּרַח), Terah (Gen.11:24-28, 31-32): Luk.3:34.† (AS)

tharreo.notes: θαρρέω, -ῶ (later form of θαρσέω), [in LXX: Pro.1:21, Bar.4:21, 27, from LXX Bar.6:16, (17), 4Ma.13:11 4Mac 17:4 * ;] to be of good cheer or courage, to be confident: 2Co.5:6 5:8, Heb.13:6; τ. πεποιθήσει, 2Co.10:2;…

tharseo.notes: θαρσέω, -ῶ (see: θαρρέω), [in LXX chiefly for יָרֵא, with neg. ;] to be of good courage: imperat., θάραει, -εῖτε, Mat.9:2, 22 14:27, Mrk.6:50 10:49, Jhn.16:33, Act.23:11.† (AS)

tharsos.notes: 1. courage, boldness , (Homer), attic; θ. τινός courage to do a thing, (Aeschulus Tragicus) 2. that which gives courage , θάρση grounds of confidence , (Euripides) 3. in bad sense, audacity , (Iliad by Homer):…

thauma.notes: θαῦμα, -τος, τό [in LXX: Job.17:8 18:20 20:8 21:5 (שַׂעַר, etc.)* ;] __1. a wonder: 2Co.11:14. __2. wonder: Rev.17:6.† (AS)

thaumasios.notes: θαυμάσιος, -α, -ον (θαῦμα), [in LXX chiefly for פָּלָא ni. (n. pl. = פָּלָא Jos.3:5 ;) wonderful; n. pl., wonders: Mat.21:15.† (AS)

thaumastos.notes: θαυμαστός, -ή, -όν (θαυμάζω), [in LXX for פָּלָא and cogn. forms (Psa.118:22-23 119:129, Isa.25:1, al.), יָרֵא ni. (Exo.15:11, Psa.65:5, al.), אַדִּיר (Psa.8:1, 9 93:4), etc. ;] wonderful, marvellous: Mat.21:42 (LXX)…

thaumazo.notes: θαυμάζω [in LXX for נָשָׂא, etc. ;] to marvel, wonder, wonder at: absol., Mat.8:10, 27 9:33 15:31 21:20 22:22 27:14, Mrk.5:20 15:5, Luk.1:21 (R, txt.; ICC, in l, but see infr.), Luk.1:63 8:25 11:14 24:41, Jhn.5:20…

thea.notes: θεά, -ᾶς, ἡ (fem. of θεός, which see), a goddess: Act.19:27.† (AS)

theaomai.notes: θεάομαι, -ῶμαι [in LXX; 2Ch.22:6 (רָאָה), Tob.2:2 13:6, 14, Jdth.15:8, 2Ma.2:4 2Mac 3:36, 3Ma.5:47 * ;] to behold, look upon, contemplate, view (in early writers with a sense of wondering), in NT apparently always in…

theatrizo.notes: θεατρίζω (θέατρον), to make a spectacle of, expose to contempt (eccl.; ἐκθ-, Polyb.): pass., Heb.10:33.† (AS)

theatron.notes: θέατρον, -ον, τό (θεάομαι), __1. a theatre (used also as a place of assembly): Act.19:29, 31. __2. Collective for οἱ θεαταί, the spectators. __3. = θέα, θέαμα, a spectacle, show: metaphorically, 1Co.4:9.† (AS)

theios.notes: θεῖος, -εία, -εῖον (θεός), [in LXX: Exo.31:3 35:31, Job.27:3 33:4, Pro.2:17 (אֵל, אֱלֹהִים, אֱלוֹהַּ), Sir.6:35, 1-37 II Mal.3:1-40, 3Ma.1:1-29, 4Mac.25 * ;] divine: δύναμις, 2Pe.1:3; φύσις (for parallel in π., see…

theiotes.notes: θειότης, -ητος, ἡ (θεῖος) [in LXX: Wis.18:9 * ;] divine nature, divinity: Rom.1:20 (for ex. from π., see MM, Exp., xv).† SYN.: θεότης, deity, godhead, divine personality. θει. = divine nature and properties, a…

theke.notes: Θήκη, -ης, ἡ (τίθημι), [in LXX: Exo.25:27 (בַּיִת), Isa.6:13 3:26 * ;] a receptacle, chest, case: of the sheath of a sword, Jhn.18:11.† (AS)

thelazo.notes: θηλάζω (θηλή, a breast), [in LXX chiefly for יָנַק hi. ;] __1. of the mother, to suckle: Mat.24:19, Mrk.13:17, Luk.21:23. __2. Of the young, to suck: Mat.21:16; μαστούς, Luk.11:27 (cf. Job.3:12, Sng.8:1, al.).† (AS)

thelema.notes: θέλημα, -τος, τό (θέλω), [in LXX chiefly for חָפֵץ, also for רָצוֹן, etc. ;] will, __(a) objectively = that which is willed: Mat.18:14, Luk.12:47, Jhn.5:30, 1Co.7:37, 1Th.5:18, 2Ti.2:26, Heb.10:10, Rev.4:11; θ. τ.…

thelesis.notes: θέλησις, -εως, ἡ (θέλω) [in LXX: 2Ch.15:15, Pro.8:35 (רָצוֹן), Eze.18:23 (חֵפֶץ), LXX Dan.11:45 (צְבִי), Tob.12:18, Wis.16:25, 2Ma.12:16, 3Ma.2:26 * ;] in colloq. and MGr. = τὸ θέλειν, will: Heb.2:4.† (AS)

thelo.notes: θέλω (the strengthened form ἐθέλω is found in Hom., and is the more frequently in Attic; see Rutherford, NPhr., 415f.), [in LXX for חפץ ,אבה; with neg., מאן pi., etc. ;] to will, be willing, wish, desire (more…

thelus.notes: θῆλυς, -εια, -υ [in LXX chiefly for נְקֵבָה ;] female: as subst., ἡ θ., a female, a woman: Rom.1:26-27; τὸ θ.., Mat.19:4, Mrk.10:6, Gal.3:28.† (AS)

themelioo.notes: θεμελιόω, -ῶ [in LXX chiefly for יָסַד ;] to lay the foundation of, to found: with accusative, τ. γῆν, Heb.1:10 (LXX); pass., Mat.7:25, Luk.6:48; metaphorically, 1Pe.5:10 (R, mg., settle); pass., Eph.3:18, Col.1:23.†…

themelios.notes: θεμέλιος, -ον (τίθημι), [in LXX for אַרְמוֹן, מוֹסָד, etc. ;] of or for a foundation; as subst., ὁ θ. (sc. λίθος), a foundation stone, foundation: Luk.6:48-49 14:29 (but see Deiss., BS, 123); pl., οἰ θ. (cl.),…

theodidaktos.notes: θεο-δίδακτος, -ον taught of God (cf. διδακτοὶ θεοῦ, Jhn.6:45): 1Th.4:9.† (AS)

theomachos.notes: θεομάχος, -ον [in Sm.: Job.26:5, Pro.9:18 21:16 * ;] fighting against God: Act.5:39.† (AS)

theoreo.notes: θεωρέω, -ῶ (θεωρός, a spectator, θεάομαι) [in LXX chiefly for רָאָה, חָזָה ;] (present and imperfect only, exc. Jhn.7:3 (fut.), Mat.28:1, Luk.23:48, Jhn.8:51, Rev.11:12 (aor.); El., § 24); __1. (cl.), to look at,…

theoria.notes: θεωρία, -ας, ἡ (see: θεωρέω), [in LXX: Dan LXX 5:7, 2Ma.5:26 2Mac 15:12, 3Ma.5:24 * ;] __1. a viewing. __2. = θεώρημα, a spectacle, sight: Luk.23:48 (both senses in cl.).† (AS)

theos.notes: θεός, -οῦ, ὁ, ἡ (Act.19:37 only; see M, Pr., 60, 244), late voc., θεέ (Mat.27:46; cf. Deu.3:24, al.), [in LXX chiefly for אֱלֹהִים, also for אֵל and other cognate forms, יהוה, etc. ;] a god or deity, God. __1. In…

theosebeia.notes: θεοσέβεια, -ας, ἡ (θεοσεβής), [in LXX Gen.20:11 (אֱלֹהִים יִרְאָה), Job.28:28 (אֲדֹנָי id.), Sir.1:24, Bar.5:4, 4Ma.7:6 4Mac 7:22, א 4Ma.15:28 א 4Ma.17:15 * ;] fear of God, godliness: 1Ti.2:10.† (AS)

theosebes.notes: θεοσεβής, -ές (θεός, σέβομαι), [in LXX for אֱלֹהִים יָרֵא, Exo.18:21, al. ;] God-fearing, godly: Jhn.9:31.† SYN.: εὐσεβής (which see), θρῆσκος (AS)

theostuges.notes: θεοστυγής, -ές (θεός, στυγέω), __1. passive, as frequently in cl., hateful to God: Rom.1:30 (R, txt., cf. Lit., Notes, 256). __2. Active, hating God: lb. (R, mg., cf. ICC, in l).† (AS)

theotes.notes: θεότης, -ητος, ἡ deity, Godhead: Col.2:9† SYN.: θειότης, q.v (AS)

thera.notes: θήρα, -ας, ἡ [in LXX for צַיִד, טָרַף, רֶשֶׁת, and cogn. forms ;] __1. a hunting, chase (Hat., Xen., al.). __2. prey, game (c1.). __3. As in Psa.35:8 = רֶשֶׁת, a net: Rom.11:9 (but see Thayer, see word).† (AS)

therapeia.notes: θεραπεία, -ας, ἡ (θεραπεύω) [in LXX for עֶבֶד Gen.45:16 (cf. Est.5:2), etc. ;] __1. service. __2. healing: Luk.9:11 (Field, Notes, 60), Rev.22:2. __3. Collective, household attendants, servants: Luk.12:42 (LXX, ll.…

therapeuo.notes: θεραπεύω [in LXX for יָשַׁב, etc. ;] __1. to do service, serve: with accusative of person(s), pass., Act.17:25. __2. As medical term, to treat (MM, Exp., xv), cure, heal: Mat.12:10, Mrk.6:5, Luk.6:7, al.; with…

therapon.notes: θεράπων, -οντος, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for עֶבֶד ;] an attendant, servant: Heb.3:5 (LXX) SYN.: see: διάκονος (AS)

thereuo.notes: θηρεύω (θήρα), [in LXX for צוּד, etc. ;] to hunt, ensnare, catch: metaphorically, Luk.11:54.† (AS)

theriomacheo.notes: θηριομαχέω, -ῶ (θηρίον, μάχομαι), to fight with wild beasts (Diod., al.): 1Co.15:32.† (AS)

therion.notes: θηρίον, -ου, τό (dimin. of θήρ), [in LXX chiefly for חַי ;] a wild beast, beast: Mrk.1:13, Act.11:6 28:4-5, Tit.1:12, Heb.12:20, Jas.3:7, Rev.6:8; of Antichrist, Rev.11:7, 13-19.† (AS)

therismos.notes: θερισμός, -οῦ, ὁ (θερίζω), [in LXX chiefly for קָצִיר ;] harvest; __(a) the act: Jhn.4:35; __(b) the time: fig., Mat.13:30, 39, Mrk.4:29 __(with) the crop: figuratively, Mat.9:37-38, Luk.10:2, Rev.14:15.† (AS)

theristes.notes: θεριστής, -οῦ, ὁ (θερίζω), [in LXX: Dan LXX Bel 1:32, Th Bel 1:33* ;] a reaper: Mat.13:30, 39† (AS)

therizo.notes: θερίζω (θέρος), [in LXX chiefly for קָצַר ;] to reap: Mat.6:26, Luk.12:24, Jas.5:4; figuratively, Mat.25:24, 26, Luk.19:21-22, Jhn.4:36-38, 2Co.9:6, Gal.6:7, 9, Rev.14:15; with accusative, τ. σαρκικά, 1Co.9:11;…

thermaino.notes: θερμαίνω (θερμός), [in LXX chiefly for חָמַם ;] to warm, heat; mid., to warm oneself: Mrk.14:54, 67, Jhn.18:18, 25 Jas.2:16.† (AS)

therme.notes: θέρμη, -ης, ἡ [in LXX: Job.6:17, Psa.19:6, Ecc.4:11 (חָמַם and derivatives), Sir.38:28 * ;] heat: Act.28:3.† (AS)

theros.notes: θέρος, -ους, τό (θέρω, to heat), [in LXX chiefly for קַיִץ ;] summer: Mat.24:32, Mrk.13:28, Luk.21:30.† (AS)

thesaurizo.notes: θησαυρίζω [in LXX for צָפַן, etc. ;] to lay up, store up: of riches, Jas.5:3; id. with dative of person(s), Luk.12:21, 2Co.12:14; with accusative of thing(s), 1Co.16:2; θησαυροὺς ἑαυτῷ, Mat.6:19-20; pass., 2Pe.3:7;…

thesauros.notes: θησαυρός, -οῦ, ὁ (τίθημι), [in LXX chiefly for אוֹצָר ;] __1. a place of safe keeping; __(a) a casket: Mat.2:11; __(b) a treasury (1Ma.3:29 and frequently in cl.); __(with) a storehouse (Neh.13:12, Deu.28:12, al.):…

thessalonike.notes: θεσσαλονίκη, -ης, ἡ Thessalonica, a city of Macedonia: Act.17:1, 11 17:13, Php.4:16, 2Ti.4:10.† (AS)

theudas.notes: Θευδᾶς (perh. contr. from Θεόδωρος, but see MM, Exp., xv), -ᾶ, ὁ Theudas: Act.5:36.† (AS)

thingano.notes: θιγγάνω [in LXX for נָגַע, Exo.19:12 * ;] __1. to touch, handle: Col.2:21, Heb.12:20" (LXX) . __2. to injure (like Heb. נָגַע, and as in Eur., Iph. Aul., 1351): with genitive, Heb.11:28.† SYN.: see: ἅπτω (AS)

thlibo.notes: θλίβω [in LXX chiefly (ὁ θλίβων, ) for צוּר ;] to press: with accusative of person(s), Mrk.3:9; ὁδὸς τεθλιμμένη, a narrow (compressed) way, Mat.7:14. Metaphorical (as frequently in LXX), to oppress, afflict,…

thlipsis.notes: θλίψις (LTr., θλῖψις), -εως, ἡ (θλίβω), [in LXX for צָרָה, etc. ;] pressure (Arist.). In LXX and NT metaphorically, tribulation, affliction, distress: Mat.24:9, 21 24:29, Mrk.13:19, 24, Jhn.16:21, Act.7:11 11:19,…

thnesko.notes: θνήσκω [in LXX chiefly for מוּת ;] to die; pf. (M, Pr., 114), to be dead: Mat.2:20, Mrk.15:44, Luk.7:12 8:49, Jhn.11:44 19:33, Act.14:19 25:19; metaphorically, of spiritual death, 1Ti.5:6 (cf. ἀπο-, συν-απο-θνήσκω).†…

thnetos.notes: θνητός, -ή, -όν (θνήσκω), [in LXX: Pro.3:13 20:24 (אָדָם), Job.30:23 (חַי), Isa.51:12 (מוּת), Wis.9:14 15:17, 2Ma.9:12, 3Ma.3:29 * ;] subject to death, mortal: Rom.6:12 8:11, 1Co.15:53-54, 2Co.4:11 5:4.† (AS)

thomas.notes: θωμᾶς, -ᾶ, ὁ (Heb.: תָּאוֹם, a twin; cf. δίδυμος), Thomas the Apostle: Mat.10:3, Mrk.3:18, Luk.6:15, Jhn.11:16 14:5 20:24-28 21:2, Act.1:13.† (AS)

thorax.notes: θώραξ, -ακσς, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for שִׁרְיוֹן and cogn. forms ;] a breastplate: Rev.9:9, 17; θ. τ. δικαιοσύνης, Eph.6:14 (cf. Isa.59:17, Wis.5:18-19); θ. πίστεως, 1Th.5:8.† (AS)

thorubeo.notes: θορυβέω, -ῶ (θόρυβος), [in LXX: Nam.2:3 (רָעַל ho.), Dan LXX 8:17 (בָּעַת ni.), Jdg.3:26, Wis.18:19, Sir.40:6 * ;] __1. to make a noise or uproar: mid., of loud and ostentatious lamentation, Mat.9:23, Mrk.5:39,…

thorubos.notes: θόρυβος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX for הָמוֹן, etc. ;] a noise, uproar, tumult, as of an excited mob: Mat.26:5 27:24, Mrk.14:2, Act.20:1 21:34 24:18; of mourners, = ὄχλος θορυβούμενος, Mrk.5:38.† (AS)

thrauo.notes: θραύω [in LXX for 1,21 (Deu.28:33, Isa.42:4 58:6), etc. ;] to break in pieces, shatter; metaphorically, to break down: Luk.4:18 (LXX).† (AS)

thremma.notes: θρέμμα, -τος, τό (τρέφω) __1. a nurseling, esp. of animals (Eur., Plat., al.). __2. cattle: Jhn.4:12.† (AS)

threneo.notes: θρηνέω, -ῶ (θρῆνος), [in LXX chiefly for יָלַל hi., also for קַיִן pit., etc. ;] __1. intrans., to lament, wail: Mat.11:17, Luk.7:32, Jhn.16:20. __2. Trans., to bewail: with accusative of person(s), Luk.23:27.† SYN.:…

threskeia.notes: θρησκεία (-κία, T), -ας, ἡ (θρῆσκος), [in LXX: Wis.14:18, 27, Sir.22:5 A, 4Ma.5:6 4Mac 5:13 * ;] religion in its external aspect (MM, Exp., xv), worship: Act.26:5, Jas.1:26-27; θ. τῶν ἀγγέλων, Col.2:18.† SYN.: see:…

threskos.notes: θρῆσκος (-κός, WH), -ον, ό religious, careful of the outward forms of divine service (see previous word)): Jas.1:26.† SYN.: εὐσεβής, θεοσεβής, δεισιδαίμων (see Tr., Syn., § xlviii) (AS)

thriambeuo.notes: θριαμβεύω (θρίαμβος, 1. a festal hymn to Bacchus. 2. The Roman triumphus), __1. to triumph (and rarely, with accusative, to triumph over; so perh. Col.2:15, but see infr.). __2. to lead in triumph: with accusative of…

thrix.notes: θρίξ, τριχός, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for שֵׂעָר ;] hair; __(a) of the head: Mat.3:4 5:36 10:30, Luk.7:38, 44 12:7 21:18, Jhn.11:2 12:3, Act.27:34, 1Pe.3:3, Rev.1:14; __(b) of animals: Mrk.1:6, Rev.9:8.† (AS)

throeo.notes: θροέω, -ῶ (θρόος, a noise, tumult), [in LXX: Sng.5:4 (הָמָה * ;] in cl., __1. to cry aloud, make an outcry. __2. to utter aloud. __3. In NT (and LXX), pass., to be troubled, as by an alarm: Mat.24:6, Mrk.13:7,…

thrombos.notes: θρόμβος, -ου, ὁ (τρέφω, in primary sense to thicken), a lump, a clot of blood (DCG, ii, 685b): αἵματος, Luk.22:44 (WH, R, mg. omit the passage, see WH, App., 64 ff.).† (AS)

thronos.notes: θρόνος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for כִּסֵּא, Exo.11:5, al. ;] in Hom., a seat, chair. Later, a throne, chair of state, seat of authority; of kings: metaphorically, of God, Mat.5:34, Act.7:49" (LXX), Rev.1:4, al.; by…

thuateira.notes: Θυάτειρα, -ων, τά (-ας, ἡ, Rev.1:11 L; cf. WH, App., 156) Thyatira, a city of Lydia: Act.16:14, Rev.1:11 2:18, 24.† (AS)

thuella.notes: θύελλα, -ης, ἡ (θύω), [in LXX Deu.4:11 5:22 (עֲרָפֶל), Exo.10:22 * ;] (poët. in cl.), a hurricane, cyclone, whirlwind: Heb.12:18.† SYN.: λαίλαψ (which see) (AS)

thugater.notes: θυγάτηρ, -τρός, ἡ (for use of vocat., cf. M, Pr., 71; WH, App, 158), [in LXX for בַּת (Gen.5:4, al.), exc. Jdg.21:14 B, 2Ch.21:17 (אִשָּׁה ;] daughter: Mat.9:18 10:35, 37 14:6 15:22, 28, Mrk.5:35 6:22 7:26, 29,…

thugatrion.notes: θυγάτριον, -ου, τό (dimin. of θυγάτηρ), a little daughter, a term of endearment used in late Gk. (cf. B1., § 27, 4): Mrk.5:23 7:25.† (AS)

thuinos.notes: θύινος (usually θύϊνος, as Rec.), -η, -ον (θυία, θύα, an African aromatic tree, with ornamentally veined wood of varying colour, = Lat. citrinus), [in Sm.: 3Ki.10:11 (אַלְמֻגִּים)* ;] thyme: ξύλον, Rev.18:12…

thumiama.notes: θυμίαμα, -τος, τό (θυμιάω), [in LXX chiefly for קְטֹרֶת ;] fragrant stuff for burning, incense: ἡ ὥρα τοῦ θ., Luk.1:10; θυσιαστήριον τοῦ θ. (Exo.30:27, al.), Luk.1:11; pl., Rev.5:8 8:3-4 18:13.† (AS)

thumiao.notes: θυμιάω, -ῶ (θύω), [in LXX (with -άζω) chiefly for קָטַר pi., hi. ;] to burn incense: Luk.1:9.† (AS)

thumiaterion.notes: θυμιατήριον, -ου, τό (θυμιάω), [in LXX: 2Ch.26:19, Eze.8:11 (קְטֹרֶת), 4Ma.7:11 * ;] __1. as in cl. (Hdt., iv, 162; Thuc., vi, 46) and LXX, a censer: Heb.9:4 (but see infr., and cf. Westc., in l). __2. As in Philo.,…

thumomacheo.notes: θυμομαέω, -ῶ (θυμός, μάχομαι), to fight desperately, have a hot quarrel: with dative of person(s), Act.12:20.† (AS)

thumoo.notes: θυμόω, -ῶ (θυμός) [in LXX (chiefly in pass.) for חָרָה, אַף חָרָה hith., etc. ;] (no act. in Attic.); pass. (and mid.), to be wroth or very angry: Mat.2:16.† (AS)

thumos.notes: θυμός, -οῦ, ὁ (θύω), [in LXX most frequently for אַף, also for חֱמָא, חָרוֹן, etc., 30 words in all ;] passion, hot anger, wrath: Heb.11:27; ὀργὴ καὶ θ., Rom.2:8, cf. Col.3:8; πικρία κ. θ., Eph.4:31; πλησθῆναι,…

thuo.notes: θύω [in LXX chiefly for זָבַח, also for שָׁחַט, etc. ;] as in c1.; __1. to offer first fruits to a god. __2. to sacrifice by slaying a victim, offer sacrifice: Act.14:13; with dative of person(s), Act.14:18; id. with…

thura.notes: θύρα, -ας, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for פֶּתַח, also for דַּל, דֶּלֶת, etc. ;] a door: κλείειν (ἀποκ-) τὴν θ., Mat.6:6, Luk.13:25; pass., Mat.25:10, Luk.11:7, Jhn.20:19, 26, Act.21:30; ἀνοίγειν, Act.5:19; pass.,…

thureos.notes: θυρεός, -οῦ, ὁ (θύρα), [in LXX for מָגֵן, צִנָּה ;] __1. in Hom., a door-stone. __2. In late Gk. (Polyb., Plut.), the scutum, a large oblong shield: θ. τ. πίστεως, Eph.6:16 (cf. Wis.5:20, where ὁσιότης is likened to…

thuris.notes: θυρίς, -ίδος, ἡ (dim, of θύρα), [in LXX chiefly for חַלּוֹן ;] a window: Act.20:9, 2Co.11:33.† (AS)

thuroros.notes: θυρωρός, -οῦ, ὁ, ἡ (θύρα, + οὖρος, a guardian) [in LXX: 4Ki.7:11 (שׁוֹעֵר), Eze.44:11 (פְּקֻדָּה), 2Ki.4:6 4:1 II Est.7:1-28 * ;] a door-keeper, porter: ὁ, Mrk.13:34, Jhn.10:3; ἡ, Jhn.18:16-17† (AS)

thusia.notes: θυσία, -ας, ἡ (θύω) [in LXX chiefly for מִנְחָה, זָבַח ;] __1. actively, an offering, sacrifice (Hdt., al.) __2. Objectively, that which is offered, a sacrifice: Mat.9:13 12:7" (LXX), Mrk.9:49 (WH, mg.), Eph.5:2,…

thusiasterion.notes: θυσιαστήριον, -ου, τό (θυσιάζω, to sacrifice), [in LXX (where the word first appears) very freq., nearly always for מַדְבַּח ;] an altar: __(a) generally, Jas.2:21; pl., Rom.11:3 (LXX); metaphorically, Heb.13:10 (see…

tiberias.notes: Τιβεριάς, -άδος, ἡ (Τιβέριος), Tiberias, a city of Galilee: Jhn.6:23, Jhn.6:1 21:1 (cf Γεννησσαρέτ, Γαλιλαία).† (AS)

tiberios.notes: Τιβέριος, -ου, ὁ, the Emperor Tiberius: Luk.3:1.† (AS)

tikto.notes: τίκτω [in LXX chiefly for יָלַד ;] prop., of parents, to beget, bring forth, but esp. the latter, and so always in NT: absol., Luk.1:57 2:6, Jhn.16:21, Gal.4:27" (LXX) (see M, Pr., 127), Rev.12:2, 4; υἱόν, Mat.1:21,…

tillo.notes: τίλλω [in LXX: Est.9:3, Isa.18:7, Dan LXX 7:4 (מָרַט) * ;] to pluck, pluck off: στάχυας, Mat.12:1, Mrk.2:23, Luk.6:1.† (AS)

timaios.notes: Τιμαῖος, -ου, ὁ (Aram. טִימַי (?), see Zorell, see word), Timæus: Mrk.10:46.† (AS)

timao.notes: τιμάω, -ῶ (τιμή), [in LXX for כָּבַד pi., עָרַךְ hi., etc. ;] __1. to fix the value, price: with accusative of person(s), of Christ, Mat.27:9 (LXX). __2. to honour: with accusative of person(s), Mat.15:4 (LXX),…

time.notes: τιμή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX for עֵרֶךְ, יְקָר, etc. ;] a valuing, hence, objectively; __1. a price paid or received: with genitive of person(s), Mat.27:9; with genitive of thing(s), Act.5:2-3; pl., Act.4:34 19:19; τ.…

timios.notes: τίμιος, -α, -ον (τιμή), [in LXX chiefly for יָקָר ;] valued, hence, __(a) precious, costly, highly valued: primarily, of money value, λίθος, Rev.17:4 18:12, 16 21:19; pl., 1Co.3:12; compar., -ώτεφος, 1Pe.1:7, Rec.;…

timiotes.notes: τιμιότης, -ητος, ἡ (τίμιος), preciousness, worth: Rev.18:19 (cf. Hort on τιμή, 1Pe.2:7).† (AS)

timoreo.notes: τιμωρέω, -ῶ (τιμή + οὖρος, a guardian) [in LXX: Eze.5:17 14:15 (שָׁכֹל pi), Wis.12:20 18:8, al. ;] __1. to help. __2. to avenge; mid., to avenge oneself on, punish (Hdt., Eur., al): act, in this sense, Act.22:5 26:11…

timoria.notes: τιμωρία, -ας, ἡ (τιμωρέω), [in LXX: Pro.24:22 (פִּיד), etc. ;] __1. help, assistance. __2. vengeance, punishment: Heb.10:29.† SYN.: κόλασις, which see, and cf. MM, xxiv (AS)

timotheos.notes: Τιμόθεος, -ου, ὁ Timothy: Act.17:14-15 18:5, Rom.16:21, al. (AS)

tino.notes: τίνω [in LXX: Pro.27:12 (τ. ζημίαν, for עָנַשׁ ni.), etc. ;] to pay: t. δίκην (cf. Pr, l.with), to pay penalty, 2Th.1:9 (see M, Th., in l).† (AS)

tis.notes: τις, neut., τι, genitive, τινός, enclitic indefinite pron., related to interrog. τίς as πού, πως, ποτέ to ποῦ, πῶς, πότε. __I. As subst., __1. one, a certain one: Luk.9:49, Jhn.11:1, Act.5:25, al.; pl., τίνες,…

tithemi.notes: τίθημι, [in LXX for שִׁית ,נתן ,שׂוּם, etc. ;] __1. causative of κεῖμαι, __(a) to place, lay, set: Luk.6:48, Rom.9:33, al.; of laying the dead to rest, Mrk.15:47, Luk.23:55, Jhn.11:34, Act.7:16, al.; before ἐπί, with…

titlos.notes: τίτλος, -ου, ὁ (Lat. titulus), [in Aq., Sm., Th.: Jer.21:4 * ;] a title, inscription: Jhn.19:19-20 (in Christian Inscr., epitaph, see MM. xxiv).† (AS)

titos.notes: Τίτος, -ου, ὁ Titus; __1. St. Paul's disciple and companion: 2Co.2:13 7:6 7:13-14 8:6 8:16 8:23 12:18, Gal.2:1, 3, 2Ti.4:10, Tit.1:4, subscr., Rec. (Τῖτος). __2. One surnamed Justus: Act.18:7, RV (T, WH, Τίτιος).† (AS)

toichos.notes: τοῖχος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for קִיר ;] a wall, esp. of a house: figuratively, Act.23:3 (cf. τεῖχος).† (AS)

tokos.notes: τόκος, -ου, ὁ (τίκτω), [in LXX for נֶשֶׁךְ, etc. ;] __(a) a bringing forth, birth; __(b) offspring. Metaphorical, of the produce of money lent out, interest, usury (cf. Merch. of Venice, I, iii, "a breed of barren…

tolmao.notes: τολμάω, -ῶ [in LXX: Job.15:12 (לָקַח), Est.1:18 7:5 (לֵב מָלֵא) Jdth.14:13, 2Ma.4:2, 3Ma.3:21 R, 4Ma.8:18 * ;] to have courage, to venture, dare, be bold: absol, 2Co.11:21; before ἐπί, 2Co.10:2; with inf. (Bl., § 69,…

tolmeroteron.notes: τολμηρῶς adv., (τολμηρός, bold, daring), boldly: compar., -ότερως (T, Rec. -ότερον), Rom.15:15.† (AS)

tolmetes.notes: τολμητής, -οῦ, ὁ (τολμάω), a bold, daring man: 2Pe.2:10 (Thuc.).† (AS)

topazion.notes: τοπάζιον, -ου, τό (and τόπαζος, ὁ), [in LXX: Exo.28:17 35:17 (Exo.39:10), Job.28:19, Eze.28:13 (פִּטְדָה), Psa.119:127 (פָּז)* ;] topaz: Rev.21:20 (see Swete, in l).† (AS)

topos.notes: τόπος, -ου, ὁ, [in LXX chiefly for מָקוֹם ;] place: Luk.4:37 10:1, 32 Jhn.5:13 6:10, Act.12:17, 1Co.1:2, al.; τ. ἅγιος (cf. Isa.60:13), Mat.24:15; ἔρημος, Mat.14:13, al.; πεδινός, Luk.6:17; ἄνυδρος (pl.), Mat.12:43,…

tosoutos.notes: τοσοῦτος, -αύτη, -οῦτο (Heb.7:22) and (elsewhere, as usually in Attic) -οῦτον, correlat. of ὅσος, of quantity, size, number, so great, so much, pl., so many: Mat.8:10 Luk.7:9, Heb.12:1, Rev.18:7, 16; of time, so…

tote.notes: τότε, demonstr. adv. of time, correlat. of ὅτε, then, at that time; __(a) of concurrent events: Mat.2:17 3:5 and freq., Rom.6:21; before ptcp., Mat.2:16, Gal.4:8; opposite to νῦν, Gal.4:29, Heb.12:26; ὁ τ. κόσμος,…

tounantion.notes: τοὐναντίον by crasis for τὸ ἐνανίον, neut. accusative, used adverbially, on the contrary, contrariwise: 2Co.2:7, Gal.2:7, 1Pe.3:9.† (AS)

tounoma.notes: τοὔνομα by crasis for τὸ ὄνομα, accusative absol., by name: Mat.27:57.† (AS)

toxon.notes: τόξον, -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for קֶשֶׁת ;] a bow: Rev.6:2.† (AS)

trachelizo.notes: τραχηλίζω (τράχηλος), prop., of wrestlers, to take by the throat, hence (Philo), to prostrate, overthrow. Metaphorical, but in what sense and from what age it is doubtful (see Westc., Rendall, on He, l.with; DB, iii,…

trachelos.notes: τράχηλος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for צַוַּאר, also forעֹרֶף, etc. ;] the neck: Mat.18:6, Mrk.9:42, Luk.17:2; ἐπιπεσεῖν ἐπὶ τὸν τ., to embrace (Gen.46:29), Luk.15:20, Act.20:37. Metaphorical, ὑποθεῖναι τὸν ἑαυτοῦ τ.…

trachonitis.notes: Τραχωνῖτις, -ιδος, ἡ (τραχύς) Trachonitis, a rough region S. of Damascus (in FlJ, sometimes ἡ Τ., sometimes ό Τράχων): ἡ Τ. χώρα, Luk.3:1.† (AS)

trachus.notes: τραχύς, -εῖα, -ύ [in LXX: Isa.40:4 (רֶכֶס), etc. ;] rough: ὁδοί (Is, l.with), Luk.3:5 (LXX); τοποί (i.e. rocky), Act.27:29.† (AS)

tragos.notes: τράγος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX chiefly for עַתּוּד, also for צָפִיר, תַּיִשׁ ;] a he-goat: Heb.9:12-13, 19 10:4.† (AS)

trapeza.notes: τράπεζα, -ης, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for שֻׁלְחָן, also for לֶחֶם, etc. ;] __1. a table, dining-table: Mat.15:27, Mrk.7:28, Luk.16:21 19:23 22:21, 30; of the table of shewbread, Heb.9:2. By meton., of food provided (see…

trapezites.notes: τραπεζείτης (Rec. -ζίτης, as in cl.), -ου, ὁ (τράπεζα), a money­changer, banker: Mat.25:27 (Dem., Plut., al.).† (AS)

trauma.notes: τραῦμα, -τος, τό [in LXX for פָּצַע, חָלַל, etc. ;] a wowed: Luk.10:34.† (AS)

traumatizo.notes: τραυματίζω (τραῦμα), [in LXX chiefly for חָלַל ;] to wound: Luk.20:12, Act.19:16.† (AS)

trecho.notes: τρέχω [in LXX chiefly for רוּץ ;] to run: Mrk.5:6, Jhn.20:2, 4, 1Co.9:24 9:26; with inf., Mat.28:8; before ἐπί, Luk.24:12 [T [WH] R, mg., om.]; εἰς, Rev.9:9; ptcp., δραμών, with indic., Mat.27:48, Mrk.15:36,…

treis.notes: τρεῖς, οἱ, αἱ, τρία, τά three: Mat.12:40, al.; μετὰ τ. ἡμέρας = τῇ τρίτῃ ἡ., Mrk.10:34, al. (cf. Field, Notes, 11 ff.) Τρεῖς Ταβέρναι (see: ταβέρνη), Three Taverns (Lit. Tres Tabernae) a halting place on the Appian…

tremo.notes: τρέμω to tremble, esp. with fear: Mrk.5:33, Luk.8:47; with ptcp. (in cl more frequently with inf.), 2Pe.2:10.† (AS)

trepho.notes: τρέφω [in LXX for חָיָה hi., גָּדַל pi., אָכַל hi., etc. ;] __1. to make to grow, bring up, rear (cl.; 1Ma.3:33 1Mac 11:39): Luk.4:16 (T, WH, mg., ἀνατ-) __2. to nourish, feed: Mat.6:26 25:37, Luk.12:24, Act.12:20,…

triakonta.notes: τριάκοντα, ἱο, αἱ, τά indecl. (τρεῖς), thirty: Mat.13:8, al. (AS)

tribolos.notes: τρίβολος, -ου, ὁ [in LXX: Gen.3:18, Hos.10:8 (דַּרְדַּר), 2Ki.12:31 (חָרִיץ), Pro.22:5 (צֵן)* ;] a thistle: Mat.7:16, Heb.6:8.† (AS)

tribos.notes: τρίβος, -ου, -ἡ (τρίβω), [in LXX for מְסִלָּה, נָתִיב, etc. ;] a beaten track, a path: Mat.3:3, Mrk.1:3, Luk.3:4" (LXX) .† (AS)

trichinos.notes: τρίχινος, -η, -ον (θρίξ), [in LXX: Zec.13:4 (שֵׂעָר), Exo.26:7 * ;] of hair: στόκος, Rev.6:12 (Xen., Plat., al.).† (AS)

trietia.notes: τριετία, -ας, ἡ (τρεῖς, ἔτος), a period of three years: Act.20:31.† (AS)

trimenos.notes: τρίμηνος, -ον (τρεῖς, μήν) [in LXX (neut.): Gen.38:24, 4Ki.23:31 B 4Ki.24:8, 2Ch.36:2 36:9 (חֹדֶשׁ שָׁלוֹשׁ) * ;] of three months; as subst., τὸ τ. (= cl. ἡ τ.), a space of three months (Polyb., al.; LXX):…

tristegos.notes: τρίστεγος, -ον (τρεῖς, στέγη) [in Sm.: Gen.6:17(16), Eze.42:6 * ;] of three stories: τὸ τ. (sc. ὄκημα), the third story, Act.20:9 (Dion., FlJ, al.).† (AS)

tritos.notes: τρίτος, -η, -ον the third: Mat.22:26, Mrk.12:21 15:25, Luk.24:21, Act.2:15, al.; τῇ τ. ἡμέρᾳ (i.e. the next day but one: Xen., al.; see Field, Notes, 11 ff.), Mat.16:21, Luk.24:46, al.; substantively, accusative…

trizo.notes: τρίζω [in Aq.: Amo.2:13; Sm.: Isa.38:14 * ;] __1. prop., of sounds by animals, to cry, chirp, etc. __2. Of other sounds, e.g., trans., τ. ὀδόντας, to gnash or grind the teeth: Mrk.9:18.† (AS)

troas.notes: Τρῳάς (Rec. Τρω-), -άδος, ἡ Troas, a city near the Hellespont: Act.16:8, 11 20:5-6, 2Co.2:12, 2Ti.4:13.† (AS)

trochia.notes: τροχιά, -ᾶς, ἡ (τροχός), [in LXX: Pro.2:15 4:11, 26-27 5:6, 21 (מַעְגָּל), Eze.27:19 A* ;] the track of a wheel, hence, a track, path: figuratively, Heb.12:13 (LXX) † (AS)

trochos.notes: τροχός, -οῦ, τό (τρέχω), [in LXX chiefly for אוֹפָן ;] a wheel: Jas.3:6 (see Mayor, Hort, in l).† (AS)

trogo.notes: τρώγω __1. prop., of animals, to gnaw, munch, crunch (Hom., al.). __2. Of men, to eat raw food, as vegetables, nuts, etc. (Hdt., al.). __3. In late vernacular, simply to eat (= ἐσθίω): Mat.24:38, Jhn.6:54, 56-58…

tromos.notes: τρόμος, -ου, ὁ (τρέμω), [in LXX for רַעַד, רַעַד, פַּחַד, etc. ;] trembling, quaking, esp. from fear: Mrk.16:8; φόβος κ. τ. (as in Gen.9:2, Exo.15:16, Deu.2:25, Isa.19:16, al.), 1Co.2:3, 2Co.7:15, Eph.6:5, Php.2:12.†…

trope.notes: τροπή, -ῆς, ἡ (τρέπω), [in LXX: Deu.33:14, Job.38:33, Wis.7:18, al. ;] a turning: esp. of the revolution of heavenly bodies, figuratively, Jas.1:17 (see: ἀποσκίασμα).† (AS)

trophe.notes: τροφή, -ῆς, ἡ (τρέφω), [in LXX for לֶחֶם, מָזוֹן, אֹכֶל, etc. ;] nourishment, food: Mat.3:4 6:25 10:10 24:45, Luk.12:23, Jhn.4:8, Act.2:46 9:19 14:17 27:33-34, 36 27:38 Jas.2:15. Metaphorical: Heb.5:12, 14.† (AS)

trophos.notes: τροφός, -oῦ, ἡ (τρέφω), [in LXX: Gen.35:8, 4Ki.11:2, 2Ch.22:11 Isa.49:23 (יָנַק) * ;] a nurse: 1Th.2:7.† (AS)

tropophoreo.notes: τροπο-φορέω, -ῶ [in LXX: Deu.1:31 B.l (נָשָׂא) * ;] __1. to bear another's manners (R, txt., suffered he their manners): with accusative of person(s), Act.13:18 (Rec., WH, R, txt.; ἐτροφο-, T, R, mg.). __2. =…

tropos.notes: τρόπος, -ου, ὁ (τρέπω), [in LXX chiefly (ὃν τ.) for אֲשֶׁר ;] __1. a way, manner, fashion: Mat.23:37, Luk.13:34, Act.1:11 7:28, 2Ti.3:8, Ju 7; καθ᾽ ὃν τ., Act.15:11 27:25 (ef. MM, xxv); κατὰ πάντα τ., Rom.3:2; κατὰ…

trublion.notes: τρύβλιον (Rec. τρυβλίον), -ου, τό [in LXX chiefly for קְעָרָה ;] a bowl, dish: Mat.26:23, Mrk.14:20 (Hippocr., Aristoph., Pint., al.).† (AS)

trugao.notes: τρυγάω, -ῶ [in LXX for קָצַר, בָּצַר, etc. ;] to gather in: with accusative, __(a) of the fruit, Luk.6:44, Rev.14:18; __(b) of that from which it is gathered, Rev.14:19.† (AS)

trugon.notes: τρυγών, -όνος, ἡ (τρύζω, to murmur, coo), [in LXX for תּוֹר ;] a turtle-dove: Luk.2:24.† (AS)

trumalia.notes: τρυμαλιά, -ᾶς, ἡ (τρύω, to wear away), [in LXX: Jer.13:4 (נָקִיק), etc. ;] = τρύμη, a hole: τ. ῥαφίδος, eye of a needle, Mrk.10:25.† SYN.: τρῆμα, τρύπημα (AS)

trupema.notes: τρύπημα, -τος, τό (τρυπάω, to bore) a hole: τ. ῥαφιδος, eye of a needle, Mat.19:24 (WH, txt., τρῆμα).† SYN.: τρῆμα, τρυμαλιά (AS)

truphaina.notes: Τρύφαινα, -ης, ἡ Tryphæna: Rom.16:12 (see Lft., Phi., 175 f.; MM, xxv).† (AS)

truphao.notes: τρυφάω, -ῶ (τρυφή), [in LXX: Neh.9:25 (עָדַן hithp.), Isa.66:11 (עָנַג hithp.), Sir.14:4 * ;] to live daintily, luxuriously, to fare sumptuously: Jas.5:5 (cf. ἐν-τρυφάω).† SYN.: σπαταλάω (which see), στρηνιάω (AS)

truphe.notes: τρυφή, -ῆς, ἡ [in LXX chiefly for עָדַן ;] softness, daintiness, luxuriousness: Luk.7:25, 2Pe.2:13.† (AS)

truphosa.notes: Τρυφῶσα, -ης, ἡ, ς, ἡ Tryphosa: Rom.16:12 (see Lft., Phi., 175 f.).† (AS)

tuchikos.notes: τύχικος (T, Rec., Τυχικός), -ου, ὁ Tychicus: Act.20:4, Eph.6:21, Col.4:7, 2Ti.4:12, Tit.3:12.† (AS)

tugchano.notes: τυγχάνω [in LXX: Deu.19:5 (מָצָא), Job.3:21 7:2 17:1, Pro.30:23 (without definite Heb. equiv.), Wis.15:19, 1Ma.11:42, 3Ma.3:7, al. ;] __I. Trans. __1. to hit (opposite to ἁμαρτάνω, to miss the mark: Hom., Xen., al.).…

tumpanizo.notes: τυμπανίζω (τύμπανον, a kettle-drum), [in LXX: 1Ki.21:13 (14) (17 pi?) * ;] __1. to beat a drum. __2. to torture by beating, beat to death (cf. Westc. on He, l.with): pass., Heb.11:35.† (AS)

tuphloo.notes: τυφλόω, -ῶ (τυφλός), [in LXX: Isa.42:19 (עִוֵּר), Tob.7:7 א, Wis.2:21 א * ;] to blind, make blind: metaphorically, Jhn.12:40 (LXX, καμμύω), 2Co.4:4, 1Jn.2:11.† (AS)

tuphlos.notes: τυφλός, -ή, -όν [in LXX for עִוֵּר ;] blind: as subst., ὁ τ., Mat.9:27, Mrk.8:22, Luk.4:18" (LXX), Jhn.5:3, al. Metaphorical: Mat.15:14 23:16-26, Jhn.9:39-41, Rom.2:19, 2Pe.1:9, Rev.3:17. (AS)

tupho.notes: τύφω (τῦφος, smoke), to raise a smoke; pass., to smoke: Mat.12:20 (LXX, καπνιζόμενον).† (AS)

tuphonikos.notes: τυφωνικός, -ή, -όν (τῦφων, a hurricane, typhoon) tempestuous: Act.27:14.† (AS)

tuphoo.notes: τυφόω, -ῶ (τῦφος, smoke; metaphorically, conceit), prop., to wrap in smoke; used only metaphorically, to puff up, becloud with pride: pass., 1Ti.3:6 6:4, 2Ti.3:4 (Dem., Arist., al.).† (AS)

tupos.notes: τύπος, -ου, ὁ (τύπτω), [in LXX: Exo.25:39 (40) (תַּבְנִית), Amo.5:26, 3Ma.3:30, 4Ma.6:19 * ;] __1. the mark of a blow: τῶν ἥλων, Jhn.20:25. __2. An impression, impress, the stamp made by a die; hence, a figure,…

tupto.notes: τύπτω [in LXX chiefly for hi. ;] to strike, smite, beat: with accusative, Mat.24:49, Mrk.15:19, Luk.12:45, Act.18:17 21:32 23:2-3; of mourners, τὰ στήθη, Luk.18:13 23:48; ἐπὶ τ. σιαγόνα, Luk.6:29; εἰς τ. κεφαλήν,…

turannos.notes: 1. an absolute sovereign , unlimited by law or constitution, (Herdotus Historicus), etc.: not applied to old hereditary sovereignties βασιλεῖαι); such as those of (Homer) or of Sparta; for the term rather regards the…

turos.notes: Τύρος, -ου, ἡ Tyre, a maritime city of Phænicia: Mrk.7:31, Act.21:3, 7; Τ. κ, Σιδών, Mat.11:21-22 15:21, Mrk.3:8 7:24, Luk.6:17 10:13-14.† (AS)

uhios.notes: υἱός, -οῦ, ὁ, [in LXX very frequently and nearly always for בֵּן, Gen.4:17, al.; for בַּר, Dan LXX TH 7:13, al.; etc. ;], a son; __1. in the ordinary sense: Mat.10:37, Mrk.9:17, Luk.1:13, al. mult.; omitted with the…

uhiothesia.notes: υἱοθεσία, -ας, ἡ (cf. the cl. phrases, υἱὸν τίθεσθαι, θετὸς υἱός), frequently in Inscr. (see Deiss., BS, 239), adoption of a son (or daughter); metaphorically, of God's relation established __(a) with Israel:…
